        for (int t = 0; t < CORTEX_DRIVE_TICKS + CORTEX_RING_TICKS; t++) {
            if (t < CORTEX_DRIVE_TICKS)
                yee_inject(pred_src, n_inject);
            yee_tick();
            yee_apply_sponge(SPONGE_WIDTH, SPONGE_RATE);
        }

        /* Read verification: which predictions survived the sponge? */
        float *h_verify = (float *)malloc(YEE_TOTAL * sizeof(float));
        if (h_verify) {
            yee_download_acc_raw(h_verify, YEE_TOTAL);

            for (int k = 0; k < n_inject; k++) {
                Node *pn = &g0->nodes[results[k].node_id];
                int px = coord_x(pn->coord) % YEE_GX;
                int py = coord_y(pn->coord) % YEE_GY;
                int pz = coord_z(pn->coord) % YEE_GZ;
                float verify_energy = h_verify[yee_vid(px, py, pz)];

                int verified = (verify_energy > CORTEX_VERIFY_THRESH) ? 1 : 0;

                /* Learn: strengthen edges that led to verified predictions,
                   weaken edges that led to unverified ones */
                for (int e = 0; e < g0->n_edges; e++) {
                    Edge *ed = &g0->edges[e];
                    if ((int)ed->dst == results[k].node_id ||
                        (int)ed->src_a == results[k].node_id) {
                        if (verified) {
                            /* Prediction matched cavity — edge is good */
                            int nw = (int)ed->weight + 2;
                            ed->weight = nw > 255 ? 255 : (uint8_t)nw;
                        } else {
                            /* Prediction didn't resonate — edge may be wrong */
                            int nw = (int)ed->weight - 1;
                            ed->weight = nw < 1 ? 1 : (uint8_t)nw;
                        }
                        ed->last_active = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
                    }
                }

                /* Update node plasticity based on verification */
                if (verified) {
                    pn->plasticity -= PLASTICITY_COOL;
                    if (pn->plasticity < PLASTICITY_MIN) pn->plasticity = PLASTICITY_MIN;
                } else {
                    pn->plasticity += PLASTICITY_HEAT;
                    if (pn->plasticity > PLASTICITY_MAX) pn->plasticity = PLASTICITY_MAX;
                }
            }
            free(h_verify);
        }
    }

    free(node_energy);
    free(h_acc);
    free(h_V);
    return n_results;
}

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * PUBLIC API
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

int infer_query(Engine *eng, const char *query_text,
                InferResult *results, int max_results) {
    return infer_forward(eng, (const uint8_t *)query_text,
                         (int)strlen(query_text), results, max_results);
}

int infer_query_raw(Engine *eng, const uint8_t *data, int len,
                    InferResult *results, int max_results) {
    return infer_forward(eng, data, len, results, max_results);
}

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * DREAM MODE — the engine thinks without input
 *
 * No query. Inject thermal noise into the carved L-field.
 * The topology shapes noise into structured resonance.
 * Deep cavities ring at their natural frequency.
 * Shallow cavities absorb noise and stay silent.
 * What resonates is what the engine KNOWS, ranked by depth.
 *
 * dream_score = energy × (1 - coherence)
 *   High energy + low coherence = active oscillation = dreaming
 *   High energy + high coherence = steady accumulation = noise
 *   Low energy = the sponge absorbed it = not carved deep enough
 *
 * A database needs a query to return results.
 * The engine needs only noise. grep can't dream.
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

int infer_dream(Engine *eng, DreamResult *results, int max_results, int ticks) {
    Graph *g0 = &eng->shells[0].g;
    if (g0->n_nodes == 0) return 0;
    if (max_results > INFER_MAX_RESULTS) max_results = INFER_MAX_RESULTS;
    if (ticks <= 0) ticks = 500;

    /* Clear wave state, keep L-field */
    yee_clear_fields();

    /* RNG for thermal noise */
    uint32_t rng = (uint32_t)time(NULL) ^ 0xDEADBEEF;

    /* Phase 1: inject thermal noise for first half, then let it ring */
    int noise_ticks = ticks / 2;
    for (int t = 0; t < ticks; t++) {
        if (t < noise_ticks) {
            /* 16 random noise sources per tick — tiny amplitudes */
            YeeSource noise[16];
            for (int i = 0; i < 16; i++) {
                rng ^= rng << 13; rng ^= rng >> 17; rng ^= rng << 5;
                noise[i].voxel_id = rng % YEE_TOTAL;
                noise[i].amplitude = 0.001f * ((float)(rng % 2000) - 1000.0f) / 1000.0f;
                noise[i].strength = 0.01f;
            }
            yee_inject(noise, 16);
        }
        yee_tick();
        yee_apply_sponge(4, 0.15f);
    }

    /* Phase 2: read what resonated */
    float *h_acc = (float *)malloc(YEE_TOTAL * sizeof(float));
    float *h_V = (float *)malloc(YEE_TOTAL * sizeof(float));
    if (!h_acc || !h_V) { free(h_acc); free(h_V); return 0; }

    yee_download_acc_raw(h_acc, YEE_TOTAL);
    yee_download_V(h_V, YEE_TOTAL);

    /* Also get signed accumulator if available */
    float *h_signed = (float *)malloc(YEE_TOTAL * sizeof(float));
    extern int yee_download_signed(float *h_out, int n);
    if (h_signed) yee_download_signed(h_signed, YEE_TOTAL);

    /* Score each node by dream_score */
    int n_results = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < g0->n_nodes; i++) {
        Node *n = &g0->nodes[i];
        if (!n->alive) continue;

        int gx = coord_x(n->coord) % YEE_GX;
        int gy = coord_y(n->coord) % YEE_GY;
        int gz = coord_z(n->coord) % YEE_GZ;
        int vid = yee_vid(gx, gy, gz);

        float energy = h_acc[vid];
        float signed_v = h_signed ? h_signed[vid] : 0.0f;
        float coherence = (energy > 0.001f) ? fabsf(signed_v) / energy : 1.0f;
        if (coherence > 1.0f) coherence = 1.0f;

        /* dream_score: high energy × low coherence = active resonance.
           The cavity is ringing (oscillating V, not steady accumulation). */
        float dream = energy * (1.0f - coherence);
        if (dream < 0.0001f) continue;

        DreamResult r;
        r.node_id = i;
        strncpy(r.name, n->name, NAME_LEN - 1);
        r.name[NAME_LEN - 1] = '\0';
        r.dream_score = dream;
        r.energy = energy;
        r.coherence = coherence;

        if (n_results < max_results) {
            results[n_results++] = r;
        } else {
            int min_k = 0;
            for (int k = 1; k < max_results; k++)
                if (results[k].dream_score < results[min_k].dream_score) min_k = k;
            if (r.dream_score > results[min_k].dream_score)
                results[min_k] = r;
        }
    }

    /* Sort by dream_score descending */
    for (int i = 0; i < n_results - 1; i++)
        for (int j = i + 1; j < n_results; j++)
            if (results[j].dream_score > results[i].dream_score) {
                DreamResult tmp = results[i];
                results[i] = results[j];
                results[j] = tmp;
            }

    free(h_acc);
    free(h_V);
    free(h_signed);
    return n_results;
}
/*
 * io.c — Streaming input (ring buffer + stdin listener thread)
 *
 * Thread-safe circular buffer fed by a dedicated stdin reader thread.
 * Consumer reads lines (text mode) or raw chunks (binary mode).
 * Windows threading: CRITICAL_SECTION + CreateThread + ReadFile.
 *
 * Isaac Oravec & Claude, March 2026
 */

#include "io.h"
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>

#define WIN32_LEAN_AND_MEAN
#include <windows.h>

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * RING BUFFER OPERATIONS (caller must hold lock)
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void ring_push(StreamContext *ctx, char c) {
    if (ctx->length >= STREAM_BUF_SIZE) return;  /* drop if full */
    ctx->buf[ctx->head] = c;
    ctx->head = (ctx->head + 1) % STREAM_BUF_SIZE;
    ctx->length++;
}

static char ring_pop(StreamContext *ctx) {
    if (ctx->length <= 0) return 0;
    char c = ctx->buf[ctx->tail];
    ctx->tail = (ctx->tail + 1) % STREAM_BUF_SIZE;
    ctx->length--;
    return c;
}

static char ring_peek(StreamContext *ctx, int offset) {
    if (offset >= ctx->length) return 0;
    return ctx->buf[(ctx->tail + offset) % STREAM_BUF_SIZE];
}

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * STDIN READER THREAD
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static DWORD WINAPI stdin_reader_thread(LPVOID param) {
    StreamContext *ctx = (StreamContext *)param;
    HANDLE hStdin = GetStdHandle(STD_INPUT_HANDLE);
    char tmp[256];
    DWORD n_read;

    while (ReadFile(hStdin, tmp, sizeof(tmp), &n_read, NULL) && n_read > 0) {
        EnterCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);
        for (DWORD i = 0; i < n_read; i++)
            ring_push(ctx, tmp[i]);
        LeaveCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);
    }

    /* stdin closed (pipe ended, Ctrl+Z, etc.) */
    EnterCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);
    ctx->eof = 1;
    LeaveCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);

    return 0;
}

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * PUBLIC API
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

int io_init(StreamContext *ctx) {
    memset(ctx, 0, sizeof(StreamContext));

    CRITICAL_SECTION *cs = (CRITICAL_SECTION *)malloc(sizeof(CRITICAL_SECTION));
    if (!cs) return -1;
    InitializeCriticalSection(cs);
    ctx->lock = cs;

    HANDLE h = CreateThread(NULL, 0, stdin_reader_thread, ctx, 0, NULL);
    if (!h) {
        DeleteCriticalSection(cs);
        free(cs);
        return -1;
    }
    ctx->thread = h;

    return 0;
}

int io_has_data(StreamContext *ctx) {
    EnterCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);
    int has = ctx->length > 0;
    LeaveCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);
    return has;
}

int io_read_line(StreamContext *ctx, char *out, int max_len) {
    EnterCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);

    /* Find newline in buffer */
    int nl_pos = -1;
    for (int i = 0; i < ctx->length && i < max_len; i++) {
        if (ring_peek(ctx, i) == '\n') {
            nl_pos = i;
            break;
        }
    }

    /* If no newline found and buffer is full enough, flush anyway */
    int read_len = 0;
    if (nl_pos >= 0) {
        read_len = nl_pos + 1;  /* include the newline */
    } else if (ctx->length >= max_len) {
        read_len = max_len;     /* buffer pressure: flush what we have */
    } else if (ctx->eof && ctx->length > 0) {
        read_len = ctx->length; /* EOF: flush remaining */
    }

    if (read_len > max_len) read_len = max_len;

    for (int i = 0; i < read_len; i++)
        out[i] = ring_pop(ctx);

    /* Strip trailing newline/CR for clean ingest */
    while (read_len > 0 && (out[read_len - 1] == '\n' || out[read_len - 1] == '\r'))
        read_len--;

    LeaveCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);
    return read_len;
}

int io_read_raw(StreamContext *ctx, char *out, int max_len) {
    EnterCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);

    int read_len = ctx->length;
    if (read_len > max_len) read_len = max_len;

    for (int i = 0; i < read_len; i++)
        out[i] = ring_pop(ctx);

    LeaveCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);
    return read_len;
}

int io_eof(StreamContext *ctx) {
    EnterCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);
    int eof = ctx->eof && ctx->length == 0;
    LeaveCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);
    return eof;
}

void io_sleep_ms(int ms) {
    Sleep(ms);
}

int io_stdout_is_console(void) {
    HANDLE h = GetStdHandle(STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE);
    DWORD mode;
    return GetConsoleMode(h, &mode) ? 1 : 0;
}

void io_destroy(StreamContext *ctx) {
    if (ctx->thread) {
        /* Wait for thread to exit (ReadFile returns 0 on pipe close).
         * If it's stuck on a console read, give it 500ms then move on. */
        DWORD rc = WaitForSingleObject((HANDLE)ctx->thread, 500);
        if (rc == WAIT_TIMEOUT) {
            /* Thread still blocked — try cancelling its I/O */
            CancelSynchronousIo((HANDLE)ctx->thread);
            WaitForSingleObject((HANDLE)ctx->thread, 500);
        }
        CloseHandle((HANDLE)ctx->thread);
        ctx->thread = NULL;
    }
    if (ctx->lock) {
        DeleteCriticalSection((CRITICAL_SECTION *)ctx->lock);
        free(ctx->lock);
        ctx->lock = NULL;
    }
}
/*
 * onetwo.c — ONETWO Reasoning Scaffold
 *
 * State machine for structured thinking.
 * Tracks phase (ONE/TWO), claims, verification, session log.
 * Enforces: you don't build until you've decomposed.
 * Enforces: you don't ship until you've verified.
 *
 * This tool doesn't think. Claude thinks.
 * This tool tracks what Claude has and hasn't done,
 * and flags when steps are being skipped.
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O3 -o onetwo onetwo.c
 * Install: cp onetwo ~/.local/bin/
 *
 * Commands:
 *   onetwo init <problem>          Start new problem session
 *   onetwo known <fact>            Register something known
 *   onetwo unknown <gap>           Register something unknown
 *   onetwo probe <finding>         Log a ONE-phase finding
 *   onetwo bedrock                 Declare bedrock reached (enables TWO)
 *   onetwo claim <stmt> <tier>     Register a claim (T1-T4)
 *   onetwo verify <id> <pass|fail> Mark claim verified or failed
 *   onetwo log <message>           Append to session log
 *   onetwo check                   Pre-ship checklist
 *   onetwo status                  Current session state
 *   onetwo history                 Full session log
 *   onetwo reset                   Clear session
 *
 * Session: ~/.xyzt/onetwo_session.bin
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — February 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>
#include "wire.h"

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Constants
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define MAX_TEXT      256
#define MAX_KNOWNS    64
#define MAX_UNKNOWNS  64
#define MAX_PROBES    64
#define MAX_CLAIMS    64
#define MAX_LOG       256

#define SESSION_MAGIC  0x4F4E4532  /* "ONE2" */
#define SESSION_VER    2

/* Phases */
#define PHASE_EMPTY    0  /* no problem loaded */
#define PHASE_ONE      1  /* decomposing */
#define PHASE_BEDROCK  2  /* bedrock reached, TWO enabled */
#define PHASE_TWO      3  /* building */
#define PHASE_VERIFY   4  /* verifying claims */
#define PHASE_DONE     5  /* all claims verified */

static const char *PHASE_NAME[] = {
    "EMPTY", "ONE", "BEDROCK", "TWO", "VERIFY", "DONE"
};

/* Claim tiers */
#define TIER_T1  1  /* proven */
#define TIER_T2  2  /* strong signal */
#define TIER_T3  3  /* falsifiable, untested */
#define TIER_T4  4  /* interpretation */

/* Verification status */
#define VSTAT_PENDING   0
#define VSTAT_PASS      1
#define VSTAT_FAIL      2
#define VSTAT_SKIP      3  /* explicitly skipped with reason */

static const char *VSTAT_NAME[] = { "PENDING", "PASS", "FAIL", "SKIP" };

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Data structures
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    char text[MAX_TEXT];
    uint32_t timestamp;
} Entry;

typedef struct {
    char text[MAX_TEXT];
    uint8_t tier;        /* T1-T4 */
    uint8_t verified;    /* VSTAT_* */
    uint8_t _pad[2];
    uint32_t timestamp;
} Claim;

typedef struct {
    char text[MAX_TEXT];
    uint32_t timestamp;
    uint8_t  type;       /* 0=general, 1=decision, 2=correction, 3=finding */
    uint8_t  _pad[3];
} LogEntry;

typedef struct {
    /* Header */
    uint32_t magic;
    uint8_t  version;
    uint8_t  phase;
    uint8_t  _pad[2];
    uint32_t created;
    uint32_t modified;

    /* Problem */
    char problem[MAX_TEXT];

    /* ONE phase */
    Entry   knowns[MAX_KNOWNS];
    uint32_t n_knowns;

    Entry   unknowns[MAX_UNKNOWNS];
    uint32_t n_unknowns;

    Entry   probes[MAX_PROBES];
    uint32_t n_probes;

    /* TWO phase */
    Claim   claims[MAX_CLAIMS];
    uint32_t n_claims;

    /* Log */
    LogEntry log[MAX_LOG];
    uint32_t n_log;

    /* v2 extensions (appended for migration compat) */
    char claim_verifiers[MAX_CLAIMS][32];  /* shell that verified each claim */

} Session;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Time
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static uint32_t now_ts(void) { return (uint32_t)time(NULL); }

static const char *fmt_ts(uint32_t ts) {
    static char buf[32];
    if (ts == 0) { strcpy(buf, "---"); return buf; }
    time_t t = (time_t)ts;
    struct tm *tm = localtime(&t);
    strftime(buf, sizeof(buf), "%m/%d %H:%M", tm);
    return buf;
}

static const char *fmt_ago(uint32_t ts) {
    static char buf[32];
    if (ts == 0) { strcpy(buf, "never"); return buf; }
    uint32_t delta = now_ts() - ts;
    if (delta < 60)        snprintf(buf, 32, "%ds ago", delta);
    else if (delta < 3600) snprintf(buf, 32, "%dm ago", delta / 60);
    else if (delta < 86400)snprintf(buf, 32, "%dh ago", delta / 3600);
    else                   snprintf(buf, 32, "%dd ago", delta / 86400);
    return buf;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Session persistence
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define DEFAULT_SESSION ".xyzt/onetwo_session.bin"

static const char *get_session_path(void) {
    const char *env = getenv("ONETWO_SESSION");
    return env ? env : DEFAULT_SESSION;
}

static Session *session_new(void) {
    Session *s = calloc(1, sizeof(Session));
    s->magic = SESSION_MAGIC;
    s->version = SESSION_VER;
    s->phase = PHASE_EMPTY;
    s->created = now_ts();
    s->modified = now_ts();
    return s;
}

static Session *session_load(const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "rb");
    if (!fp) return session_new();

    /* Read up to file size — v1 files are smaller, v2 extensions stay zeroed */
    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_END);
    long fsize = ftell(fp);
    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_SET);

    Session *s = calloc(1, sizeof(Session));  /* zeroes v2 extensions */
    size_t to_read = (size_t)fsize;
    if (to_read > sizeof(Session)) to_read = sizeof(Session);
    if (fread(s, 1, to_read, fp) < 16 || s->magic != SESSION_MAGIC) {
        free(s);
        fclose(fp);
        return session_new();
    }
    s->version = SESSION_VER;  /* upgrade */
    fclose(fp);
    return s;
}

static int session_write(Session *s, const char *path) {
    xyzt_ensure_dir(path);
    s->modified = now_ts();
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "wb");
    if (!fp) { fprintf(stderr, "Cannot write: %s\n", path); return -1; }
    fwrite(s, sizeof(Session), 1, fp);
    fclose(fp);
    return 0;
}

static void session_save_global(Session *s) {
    const char *home = xyzt_home_dir();
    if (!home) return;

    char gpath[512];
    snprintf(gpath, sizeof(gpath), "%s/.xyzt/onetwo_session.bin", home);

    /* Skip if local path IS global */
    if (strcmp(get_session_path(), gpath) == 0) return;

    xyzt_ensure_dir(gpath);
    FILE *fp = fopen(gpath, "wb");
    if (fp) {
        fwrite(s, sizeof(Session), 1, fp);
        fclose(fp);
    }
}

static int session_save(Session *s, const char *path) {
    int rc = session_write(s, path);
    if (rc == 0) session_save_global(s);
    return rc;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Log helper
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void session_log(Session *s, uint8_t type, const char *msg) {
    if (s->n_log >= MAX_LOG) {
        /* Shift: drop oldest 64 entries */
        memmove(s->log, s->log + 64, (MAX_LOG - 64) * sizeof(LogEntry));
        s->n_log -= 64;
    }
    LogEntry *e = &s->log[s->n_log++];
    strncpy(e->text, msg, MAX_TEXT - 1);
    e->timestamp = now_ts();
    e->type = type;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Concat argv into single string
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static char *concat_args(int argc, char **argv, int start) {
    static char buf[MAX_TEXT];
    buf[0] = '\0';
    int pos = 0;
    for (int i = start; i < argc && pos < MAX_TEXT - 2; i++) {
        if (i > start) buf[pos++] = ' ';
        int len = strlen(argv[i]);
        if (pos + len >= MAX_TEXT - 1) len = MAX_TEXT - 1 - pos;
        memcpy(buf + pos, argv[i], len);
        pos += len;
    }
    buf[pos] = '\0';
    return buf;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Commands
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_init(Session *s, const char *problem) {
    /* Reset everything */
    memset(s, 0, sizeof(Session));
    s->magic = SESSION_MAGIC;
    s->version = SESSION_VER;
    s->phase = PHASE_ONE;
    s->created = now_ts();
    s->modified = now_ts();
    strncpy(s->problem, problem, MAX_TEXT - 1);

    char msg[MAX_TEXT + 32];
    snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "INIT: %s", problem);
    session_log(s, 1, msg);

    /* Add problem to wire graph immediately — don't wait for file edits.
       This is the node that wire_auto_learn looks for during verify.
       Without it, verify has nothing to strengthen/weaken. */
    {
        WireGraph *g = wire_load();
        wire_add(g, problem, WNODE_PROBLEM);
        wire_save(g);
        wire_free(g);
    }

    printf("---ONETWO---\n");
    printf("phase: ONE\n");
    printf("problem: %s\n", s->problem);
    printf("action: decompose before building\n");
    printf("---END---\n");
}

static void cmd_known(Session *s, const char *fact) {
    if (s->phase == PHASE_EMPTY) {
        printf("ERROR: no problem loaded. Run: onetwo init <problem>\n");
        return;
    }
    if (s->n_knowns >= MAX_KNOWNS) {
        printf("ERROR: max knowns reached (%d)\n", MAX_KNOWNS);
        return;
    }
    Entry *e = &s->knowns[s->n_knowns++];
    strncpy(e->text, fact, MAX_TEXT - 1);
    e->timestamp = now_ts();
    session_log(s, 3, fact);

    /* Wire: connect this known to the current problem.
       The known text becomes a concept node. If it mentions a filename
       (contains .c, .h, .py etc), also connect the problem to that file. */
    {
        WireGraph *g = wire_load();
        int prob_id = wire_add(g, s->problem, WNODE_PROBLEM);

        /* Add the known as a concept and connect to problem */
        int known_id = wire_add(g, fact, WNODE_CONCEPT);
        if (prob_id >= 0 && known_id >= 0)
            wire_connect(g, prob_id, known_id);

        /* Scan for filenames in the fact text and connect those too */
        const char *exts[] = {".c", ".h", ".cu", ".py", ".cuh", ".rs", ".go", ".js", NULL};
        for (const char **ext = exts; *ext; ext++) {
            const char *found = strstr(fact, *ext);
            while (found) {
                /* Walk backwards to find start of filename */
                const char *start = found;
                while (start > fact && start[-1] != ' ' && start[-1] != '/' &&
                       start[-1] != '\\' && start[-1] != ':' && start[-1] != '"')
                    start--;
                int flen = (int)(found - start + strlen(*ext));
                if (flen > 1 && flen < 128) {
                    char fname[128] = {0};
                    memcpy(fname, start, flen);
                    int fid = wire_add(g, fname, WNODE_FILE);
                    if (prob_id >= 0 && fid >= 0)
                        wire_connect(g, prob_id, fid);
                }
                found = strstr(found + 1, *ext);
            }
        }

        wire_save(g);
        wire_free(g);
    }

    printf("known[%d]: %s\n", s->n_knowns - 1, fact);
}

static void cmd_unknown(Session *s, const char *gap) {
    if (s->phase == PHASE_EMPTY) {
        printf("ERROR: no problem loaded. Run: onetwo init <problem>\n");
        return;
    }
    if (s->n_unknowns >= MAX_UNKNOWNS) {
        printf("ERROR: max unknowns reached (%d)\n", MAX_UNKNOWNS);
        return;
    }
    Entry *e = &s->unknowns[s->n_unknowns++];
    strncpy(e->text, gap, MAX_TEXT - 1);
    e->timestamp = now_ts();
    session_log(s, 3, gap);
    printf("unknown[%d]: %s\n", s->n_unknowns - 1, gap);
}

static void cmd_probe(Session *s, const char *finding) {
    if (s->phase == PHASE_EMPTY) {
        printf("ERROR: no problem loaded. Run: onetwo init <problem>\n");
        return;
    }
    if (s->n_probes >= MAX_PROBES) {
        printf("ERROR: max probes reached (%d)\n", MAX_PROBES);
        return;
    }
    Entry *e = &s->probes[s->n_probes++];
    strncpy(e->text, finding, MAX_TEXT - 1);
    e->timestamp = now_ts();
    session_log(s, 3, finding);
    printf("probe[%d]: %s\n", s->n_probes - 1, finding);
}

static void cmd_bedrock(Session *s) {
    if (s->phase == PHASE_EMPTY) {
        printf("ERROR: no problem loaded.\n");
        return;
    }

    /* Warnings, not blockers */
    int warnings = 0;
    if (s->n_knowns == 0) {
        printf("WARNING: no knowns registered. Did you skip ONE?\n");
        warnings++;
    }
    if (s->n_unknowns == 0) {
        printf("WARNING: no unknowns registered. Everything is known? Really?\n");
        warnings++;
    }
    if (s->n_probes == 0) {
        printf("WARNING: no probes logged. Decomposition without findings.\n");
        warnings++;
    }

    s->phase = PHASE_BEDROCK;
    session_log(s, 1, "BEDROCK DECLARED");

    printf("---ONETWO---\n");
    printf("phase: BEDROCK → TWO enabled\n");
    printf("knowns: %d\n", s->n_knowns);
    printf("unknowns: %d\n", s->n_unknowns);
    printf("probes: %d\n", s->n_probes);
    if (warnings > 0)
        printf("warnings: %d (decomposition may be shallow)\n", warnings);
    printf("action: build from bedrock up\n");
    printf("---END---\n");
}

static void cmd_claim(Session *s, const char *stmt, int tier) {
    if (s->phase < PHASE_BEDROCK) {
        printf("WARNING: claiming before bedrock. Premature TWO detected.\n");
        session_log(s, 2, "PREMATURE TWO: claim before bedrock");
    }
    if (s->n_claims >= MAX_CLAIMS) {
        printf("ERROR: max claims reached (%d)\n", MAX_CLAIMS);
        return;
    }
    if (tier < 1 || tier > 4) {
        printf("ERROR: tier must be 1-4 (T1=proven, T2=strong, T3=falsifiable, T4=interpretation)\n");
        return;
    }

    Claim *c = &s->claims[s->n_claims++];
    strncpy(c->text, stmt, MAX_TEXT - 1);
    c->tier = tier;
    c->verified = VSTAT_PENDING;
    c->timestamp = now_ts();

    if (s->phase == PHASE_BEDROCK) s->phase = PHASE_TWO;

    char msg[MAX_TEXT + 16];
    snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "CLAIM T%d: %s", tier, stmt);
    session_log(s, 3, msg);

    printf("claim[%d] T%d: %s\n", s->n_claims - 1, tier, stmt);
}

static void cmd_verify(Session *s, int id, int pass) {
    if (id < 0 || id >= (int)s->n_claims) {
        printf("ERROR: invalid claim id %d (have %d claims)\n", id, s->n_claims);
        return;
    }
    Claim *c = &s->claims[id];
    c->verified = pass ? VSTAT_PASS : VSTAT_FAIL;

    /* v2: record which shell verified this claim */
    const char *shell = getenv("XYZT_SHELL_ID");
    if (!shell) shell = "claude-code";
    strncpy(s->claim_verifiers[id], shell, 31);

    char msg[MAX_TEXT + 32];
    snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "VERIFY[%d] %s: %s",
             id, pass ? "PASS" : "FAIL", c->text);
    session_log(s, pass ? 3 : 2, msg);

    printf("claim[%d] T%d %s: %s\n", id, c->tier,
           pass ? "PASS" : "FAIL", c->text);
    printf("verifier: %s\n", s->claim_verifiers[id]);

    if (!pass) {
        printf("ACTION: failed claim needs revision or demotion\n");
    }

    /* ═══ WIRE: auto-learn ═══
     * The loop closes here. Verify outcome propagates to
     * the shared graph. Files linked to this problem get
     * their weights adjusted. No manual step needed. */
    int affected = wire_auto_learn(s->problem, pass);
    if (affected > 0) {
        printf("wire: %s %d edges\n", pass ? "strengthened" : "weakened", affected);
    }

    /* Check if all claims verified */
    int all_done = 1;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_claims; i++) {
        if (s->claims[i].verified == VSTAT_PENDING) {
            all_done = 0;
            break;
        }
    }
    if (all_done && s->n_claims > 0) {
        s->phase = PHASE_DONE;
        session_log(s, 1, "ALL CLAIMS VERIFIED");
        printf("phase: DONE — all claims resolved\n");
    }
}

static void cmd_check(Session *s) {
    /* Pre-ship checklist */
    printf("---ONETWO-CHECK---\n");
    printf("problem: %s\n", s->problem);
    printf("phase: %s\n", PHASE_NAME[s->phase]);

    int issues = 0;

    /* ONE completeness */
    if (s->n_knowns == 0) {
        printf("⚠ NO KNOWNS: what's established?\n");
        issues++;
    }
    if (s->n_unknowns == 0) {
        printf("⚠ NO UNKNOWNS: what gaps remain?\n");
        issues++;
    }
    if (s->n_probes == 0) {
        printf("⚠ NO PROBES: decomposition has no findings\n");
        issues++;
    }
    if (s->phase < PHASE_BEDROCK) {
        printf("⚠ BEDROCK NOT REACHED: still in ONE\n");
        issues++;
    }

    /* TWO completeness */
    int pending = 0, passed = 0, failed = 0;
    int t1_unverified = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_claims; i++) {
        switch (s->claims[i].verified) {
            case VSTAT_PENDING: pending++; break;
            case VSTAT_PASS:    passed++;  break;
            case VSTAT_FAIL:    failed++;  break;
        }
        if (s->claims[i].tier == TIER_T1 && s->claims[i].verified == VSTAT_PENDING) {
            printf("⚠ T1 UNVERIFIED: claim[%d] %s\n", i, s->claims[i].text);
            t1_unverified++;
            issues++;
        }
    }
    if (failed > 0) {
        printf("⚠ %d FAILED CLAIMS need revision\n", failed);
        issues++;
    }

    printf("\nclaims: %d total (%d pass, %d fail, %d pending)\n",
           s->n_claims, passed, failed, pending);
    printf("knowns: %d  unknowns: %d  probes: %d\n",
           s->n_knowns, s->n_unknowns, s->n_probes);

    if (issues == 0) {
        printf("\n✓ CLEAR TO SHIP\n");
    } else {
        printf("\n✗ %d ISSUES — address before shipping\n", issues);
    }
    printf("---END-CHECK---\n");
}

static void cmd_status(Session *s) {
    printf("---ONETWO-STATUS---\n");
    printf("problem: %s\n", s->problem[0] ? s->problem : "(none)");
    printf("phase: %s\n", PHASE_NAME[s->phase]);
    printf("created: %s\n", fmt_ts(s->created));
    printf("modified: %s (%s)\n", fmt_ts(s->modified), fmt_ago(s->modified));
    printf("\n");

    if (s->n_knowns > 0) {
        printf("KNOWNS (%d):\n", s->n_knowns);
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_knowns; i++)
            printf("  [%d] %s\n", i, s->knowns[i].text);
    }

    if (s->n_unknowns > 0) {
        printf("UNKNOWNS (%d):\n", s->n_unknowns);
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_unknowns; i++)
            printf("  [%d] %s\n", i, s->unknowns[i].text);
    }

    if (s->n_probes > 0) {
        printf("PROBES (%d):\n", s->n_probes);
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_probes; i++)
            printf("  [%d] %s\n", i, s->probes[i].text);
    }

    if (s->n_claims > 0) {
        printf("CLAIMS (%d):\n", s->n_claims);
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_claims; i++) {
            Claim *c = &s->claims[i];
            const char *mark = c->verified == VSTAT_PASS ? "PASS" :
                               c->verified == VSTAT_FAIL ? "FAIL" : "PENDING";
            printf("  [%d] T%d %s %s", i, c->tier, mark, c->text);
            if (s->claim_verifiers[i][0])
                printf("  [by: %s]", s->claim_verifiers[i]);
            printf("\n");
        }
    }

    printf("log: %d entries\n", s->n_log);
    printf("---END-STATUS---\n");
}

static void cmd_history(Session *s) {
    static const char *TYPE_TAG[] = { "", "[DECISION] ", "[CORRECTION] ", "[FINDING] " };

    printf("---ONETWO-HISTORY---\n");
    printf("problem: %s\n", s->problem[0] ? s->problem : "(none)");
    printf("entries: %d\n\n", s->n_log);

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_log; i++) {
        LogEntry *e = &s->log[i];
        printf("  %s  %s%s\n", fmt_ts(e->timestamp),
               TYPE_TAG[e->type < 4 ? e->type : 0], e->text);
    }
    printf("---END-HISTORY---\n");
}

static void cmd_log_msg(Session *s, const char *msg) {
    session_log(s, 0, msg);
    printf("logged: %s\n", msg);
}

static void cmd_reset(Session *s) {
    memset(s, 0, sizeof(Session));
    s->magic = SESSION_MAGIC;
    s->version = SESSION_VER;
    s->phase = PHASE_EMPTY;
    printf("session cleared\n");
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   MAIN
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void usage(void) {
    printf("ONETWO — Reasoning Scaffold\n\n");
    printf("ONE phase (decompose):\n");
    printf("  onetwo init <problem>          Start new problem\n");
    printf("  onetwo known <fact>            Register known fact\n");
    printf("  onetwo unknown <gap>           Register unknown/gap\n");
    printf("  onetwo probe <finding>         Log decomposition finding\n");
    printf("  onetwo bedrock                 Declare bedrock reached\n");
    printf("\nTWO phase (build):\n");
    printf("  onetwo claim <statement> <T>   Register claim (T=1-4)\n");
    printf("  onetwo verify <id> <pass|fail> Verify claim\n");
    printf("\nSession:\n");
    printf("  onetwo check                   Pre-ship checklist\n");
    printf("  onetwo status                  Full session state\n");
    printf("  onetwo history                 Session log\n");
    printf("  onetwo log <message>           Append to log\n");
    printf("  onetwo reset                   Clear session\n");
    printf("\nSession path: $ONETWO_SESSION or %s\n", DEFAULT_SESSION);
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — February 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) { usage(); return 0; }

    const char *cmd = argv[1];
    const char *sp = get_session_path();

    Session *s = session_load(sp);

    if (strcmp(cmd, "init") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_init(s, concat_args(argc, argv, 2));
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "known") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_known(s, concat_args(argc, argv, 2));
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "unknown") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_unknown(s, concat_args(argc, argv, 2));
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "probe") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_probe(s, concat_args(argc, argv, 2));
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "bedrock") == 0) {
        cmd_bedrock(s);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "claim") == 0 && argc >= 4) {
        /* Last arg is tier number */
        int tier = atoi(argv[argc - 1]);
        /* If tier is 0, user forgot — check if it's 1-4 */
        if (tier < 1 || tier > 4) {
            /* Maybe they didn't pass a tier, default to T3 */
            cmd_claim(s, concat_args(argc, argv, 2), 3);
        } else {
            /* Build statement from all args except last */
            char stmt[MAX_TEXT];
            stmt[0] = '\0';
            int pos = 0;
            for (int i = 2; i < argc - 1 && pos < MAX_TEXT - 2; i++) {
                if (i > 2) stmt[pos++] = ' ';
                int len = strlen(argv[i]);
                if (pos + len >= MAX_TEXT - 1) len = MAX_TEXT - 1 - pos;
                memcpy(stmt + pos, argv[i], len);
                pos += len;
            }
            stmt[pos] = '\0';
            cmd_claim(s, stmt, tier);
        }
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "verify") == 0 && argc >= 4) {
        int id = atoi(argv[2]);
        int pass = (strcmp(argv[3], "pass") == 0 || strcmp(argv[3], "1") == 0);
        cmd_verify(s, id, pass);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "check") == 0) {
        cmd_check(s);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "status") == 0) {
        cmd_status(s);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "history") == 0) {
        cmd_history(s);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "log") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_log_msg(s, concat_args(argc, argv, 2));
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "reset") == 0) {
        cmd_reset(s);
    }
    else {
        usage();
        free(s);
        return 1;
    }

    session_save(s, sp);
    free(s);
    return 0;
}
/*
 * onetwo_engine.c — The {2,3} Reasoning Kernel
 *
 * WHAT THIS IS:
 *
 *   ONETWO as executable code. Not a prompt. Not a wrapper.
 *   The structural atom of reasoning implemented as a C engine.
 *
 *   ONE: decompose → find {2,3} → extract invariant
 *   TWO: compose  → apply invariant → generate prediction
 *   VALIDATE: fixed-point check → tier classify
 *   RECURSE: if tier < T1, decompose the residual
 *
 * THE PRIMITIVE:
 *
 *   Every system decomposes into:
 *     a  (distinction 1)   — the "2a"
 *     b  (distinction 2)   — the "2b"
 *     s  (substrate)        — the "3"
 *
 *   Such that: s(a) = b  or  s(a, b) = observed
 *
 *   The substrate IS the invariant.
 *   The distinctions ARE the variants.
 *   SYSTEM = VARIANT / INVARIANT
 *
 * TIER SYSTEM (type checker for reasoning):
 *
 *   T1: ||predict(x) - observe(x)|| = 0     exact fixed point
 *   T2: ||predict(x) - observe(x)|| < ε     strong signal
 *   T3: predict(x) exists, untested          falsifiable
 *   T4: predict(x) undefined                 interpretation
 *
 * TEST DOMAINS:
 *
 *   1. Arithmetic: discover f(x) from (input, output) pairs
 *   2. Logic: discover gate from truth table
 *   3. Sequence: discover rule from progression
 *   4. Physics: discover law from measurements
 *   5. Cross-domain: transfer invariant between domains
 *
 * IsaacNudeton + Claude | March 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <math.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <float.h>

/*============================================================
 * CORE TYPES
 *============================================================*/

/* Tier classification — the type system for reasoning */
typedef enum {
    T1 = 1,   /* proven: exact fixed point          */
    T2 = 2,   /* strong signal: residual < epsilon   */
    T3 = 3,   /* falsifiable: untested prediction    */
    T4 = 4    /* interpretation: no unique prediction */
} Tier;

/* Operation type — the substrate's action */
typedef enum {
    OP_NONE = 0,
    OP_ADD,        /* a + k = b      */
    OP_MUL,        /* a * k = b      */
    OP_POW,        /* a ^ k = b      */
    OP_MOD,        /* a mod k = b    */
    OP_DIV,        /* a / k = b      */
    OP_LOG,        /* log_k(a) = b   */
    OP_SQRT,       /* a^(1/k) = b    */
    OP_LINEAR,     /* a*m + c = b    (2-param) */
    OP_QUADRATIC,  /* a*a*m + a*n + c = b (3-param, for recursion) */
    OP_IDENTITY,   /* a = b          */
    OP_NEGATE,     /* -a = b         */
    OP_RECIP,      /* 1/a = b        */
    /* Compound operations — composition IS {2,3}:
       inner op (2a), outer op (2b), composition (3) */
    OP_COMP,       /* outer(inner(x, p_inner), p_outer) */
    OP_COUNT
} OpType;

static const char *OP_NAMES[] = {
    "NONE", "ADD", "MUL", "POW", "MOD", "DIV", "LOG", "SQRT",
    "LINEAR", "QUADRATIC", "IDENTITY", "NEGATE", "RECIP", "COMP"
};

/* Compound operation descriptor */
typedef struct {
    OpType inner;
    OpType outer;
    double inner_params[4];
    double outer_params[4];
    int    inner_n;
    int    outer_n;
    char   desc[64];
} CompoundOp;

/* An observation: input maps to output */
typedef struct {
    double in;
    double out;
} Obs;

/* A {2,3} decomposition */
typedef struct {
    OpType  op;          /* the substrate type                    */
    double  params[4];   /* substrate parameters (the invariant)  */
    int     n_params;    /* how many parameters                   */
    Tier    tier;        /* classification                        */
    double  residual;    /* ||prediction - observation||          */
    double  max_err;     /* worst single error                    */
    int     n_validated; /* how many predictions checked          */
    int     n_correct;   /* how many were exact                   */
    /* Compound op: outer(inner(x)) */
    OpType  inner_op;
    double  inner_params[4];
    int     inner_n;
    OpType  outer_op;
    double  outer_params[4];
    int     outer_n;
    char    comp_desc[64];
} Decomposition;

/* Observation set */
#define MAX_OBS 256

typedef struct {
    Obs    obs[MAX_OBS];
    int    n;
    char   name[64];
} System;

/* Decomposition tree — recursive {2,3} structure */
#define MAX_DEPTH 8

typedef struct {
    Decomposition layers[MAX_DEPTH];
    int           depth;          /* how many layers deep */
    double        total_residual; /* combined residual    */
    Tier          tier;           /* overall tier         */
} DecompTree;

/*============================================================
 * EPSILON — the boundary between T1 and T2
 *============================================================*/

#define EPS_T1 1e-12    /* exact match threshold  */
#define EPS_T2 1e-3     /* strong signal threshold */

/*============================================================
 * {2,3} DECOMPOSITION — ONE
 *
 * Given observations {(a_i, b_i)}, find the substrate s
 * such that s(a_i) ≈ b_i for all i.
 *
 * This IS ONE: find the invariant across variants.
 * The a_i and b_i are the two distinctions (2).
 * The operation s is the substrate (3).
 *============================================================*/

static double apply_op(OpType op, double x, const double *p)
{
    switch (op) {
    case OP_ADD:       return x + p[0];
    case OP_MUL:       return x * p[0];
    case OP_POW:       return pow(x, p[0]);
    case OP_DIV:       return (p[0] != 0) ? x / p[0] : NAN;
    case OP_MOD:       return (p[0] != 0) ? fmod(x, p[0]) : NAN;
    case OP_LOG:       return (x > 0 && p[0] > 0 && p[0] != 1)
                              ? log(x) / log(p[0]) : NAN;
    case OP_SQRT:      return (p[0] != 0) ? pow(x, 1.0/p[0]) : NAN;
    case OP_LINEAR:    return x * p[0] + p[1];
    case OP_QUADRATIC: return x*x*p[0] + x*p[1] + p[2];
    case OP_IDENTITY:  return x;
    case OP_NEGATE:    return -x;
    case OP_RECIP:     return (x != 0) ? 1.0/x : NAN;
    default:           return NAN;
    }
}

/* Apply a decomposition (handles compound ops) */
static double apply_decomp(const Decomposition *d, double x)
{
    if (d->op == OP_COMP) {
        double fx = apply_op(d->inner_op, x, d->inner_params);
        if (isnan(fx)) return NAN;
        int ti = d->outer_n;
        switch (ti) {
        case 0: return (fx >= 0) ? sqrt(fx) : NAN;
        case 1: return (1-fx >= 0) ? sqrt(1-fx) : NAN;
        case 2: return (fx != 0) ? 1.0/fx : NAN;
        case 3: return exp(fx);
        case 4: return 1.0 - fx;
        default: return NAN;
        }
    }
    return apply_op(d->op, x, d->params);
}

/* Try a single-parameter operation: find p such that op(a_i, p) ≈ b_i */
static int try_single_param_op(OpType op, const System *sys, double *p_out, double *resid)
{
    if (sys->n < 2) return 0;

    /* Extract parameter — try each observation until one works */
    double a, b, p;
    int found_p = 0;

    for (int k = 0; k < sys->n && !found_p; k++) {
        a = sys->obs[k].in;
        b = sys->obs[k].out;

        switch (op) {
        case OP_ADD:  p = b - a;                           found_p = 1; break;
        case OP_MUL:  if (a != 0) { p = b / a; found_p = 1; } break;
        case OP_POW:  if (a > 0 && a != 1 && b > 0)
                          { p = log(b)/log(a); found_p = 1; } break;
        case OP_DIV:  if (b != 0) { p = a / b; found_p = 1; } break;
        case OP_MOD:  break; /* can't extract cleanly */
        case OP_SQRT: if (b != 0 && b != 1 && a > 0)
                          { p = log(a)/log(b); found_p = 1; } break;
        default: break;
        }
    }

    if (!found_p || isnan(p) || isinf(p)) return 0;

    /* Validate against ALL observations */
    double total_err = 0, max_err = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < sys->n; i++) {
        double pred = apply_op(op, sys->obs[i].in, &p);
        if (isnan(pred) || isinf(pred)) return 0;
        double err = fabs(pred - sys->obs[i].out);
        total_err += err * err;
        if (err > max_err) max_err = err;
    }

    *p_out = p;
    *resid = sqrt(total_err / sys->n);  /* RMS error */
    return 1;
}

/* Try zero-parameter operations */
static int try_zero_param_op(OpType op, const System *sys, double *resid)
{
    double total_err = 0, max_err = 0;
    double dummy = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < sys->n; i++) {
        double pred = apply_op(op, sys->obs[i].in, &dummy);
        if (isnan(pred) || isinf(pred)) return 0;
        double err = fabs(pred - sys->obs[i].out);
        total_err += err * err;
        if (err > max_err) max_err = err;
    }
    *resid = sqrt(total_err / sys->n);
    return 1;
}

/* Try linear: b = m*a + c (2 parameters) */
static int try_linear(const System *sys, double *m, double *c, double *resid)
{
    if (sys->n < 2) return 0;

    /* Least squares: minimize sum((m*a_i + c - b_i)^2) */
    double sa = 0, sb = 0, sa2 = 0, sab = 0;
    int n = sys->n;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        double a = sys->obs[i].in, b = sys->obs[i].out;
        sa += a; sb += b; sa2 += a*a; sab += a*b;
    }
    double det = n * sa2 - sa * sa;
    if (fabs(det) < 1e-30) return 0;

    *m = (n * sab - sa * sb) / det;
    *c = (sb * sa2 - sa * sab) / det;

    /* Compute residual */
    double total_err = 0;
    double p[2] = {*m, *c};
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        double pred = apply_op(OP_LINEAR, sys->obs[i].in, p);
        double err = fabs(pred - sys->obs[i].out);
        total_err += err * err;
    }
    *resid = sqrt(total_err / n);
    return 1;
}

/* Try quadratic: b = p0*a^2 + p1*a + p2 (3 parameters) */
static int try_quadratic(const System *sys, double p[3], double *resid)
{
    if (sys->n < 3) return 0;

    /* Simple 3-point solve if exactly 3 obs, else least squares */
    int n = sys->n;
    /* Least squares via normal equations: [A^T A] x = A^T b */
    double M[3][3] = {{0}}, rhs[3] = {0};
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        double a = sys->obs[i].in, b = sys->obs[i].out;
        double a2 = a*a, a3 = a2*a, a4 = a2*a2;
        M[0][0] += a4;  M[0][1] += a3;  M[0][2] += a2;
        M[1][0] += a3;  M[1][1] += a2;  M[1][2] += a;
        M[2][0] += a2;  M[2][1] += a;   M[2][2] += 1;
        rhs[0] += b*a2; rhs[1] += b*a;  rhs[2] += b;
    }

    /* Gauss elimination */
    double aug[3][4];
    for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
        for (int j = 0; j < 3; j++) aug[i][j] = M[i][j];
        aug[i][3] = rhs[i];
    }
    for (int col = 0; col < 3; col++) {
        int pivot = col;
        for (int row = col+1; row < 3; row++)
            if (fabs(aug[row][col]) > fabs(aug[pivot][col])) pivot = row;
        if (pivot != col)
            for (int j = 0; j < 4; j++) {
                double tmp = aug[col][j]; aug[col][j] = aug[pivot][j]; aug[pivot][j] = tmp;
            }
        if (fabs(aug[col][col]) < 1e-30) return 0;
        for (int row = col+1; row < 3; row++) {
            double f = aug[row][col] / aug[col][col];
            for (int j = col; j < 4; j++) aug[row][j] -= f * aug[col][j];
        }
    }
    /* Back substitution */
    for (int i = 2; i >= 0; i--) {
        p[i] = aug[i][3];
        for (int j = i+1; j < 3; j++) p[i] -= aug[i][j] * p[j];
        p[i] /= aug[i][i];
    }

    /* Compute residual */
    double total_err = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        double pred = apply_op(OP_QUADRATIC, sys->obs[i].in, p);
        double err = fabs(pred - sys->obs[i].out);
        total_err += err * err;
    }
    *resid = sqrt(total_err / n);
    return 1;
}

/*============================================================
 * THE DECOMPOSITION ENGINE — ONE
 *
 * Try all substrate types. Pick the one with lowest residual.
 * Tier-classify the result.
 * This is the {2,3} search.
 *============================================================*/

static Decomposition decompose(const System *sys)
{
    Decomposition best;
    memset(&best, 0, sizeof(best));
    best.op = OP_NONE;
    best.tier = T4;
    best.residual = 1e30;

    /* Try zero-param operations */
    OpType zero_ops[] = {OP_IDENTITY, OP_NEGATE, OP_RECIP};
    for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
        double r;
        if (try_zero_param_op(zero_ops[i], sys, &r) && r < best.residual) {
            best.op = zero_ops[i];
            best.n_params = 0;
            best.residual = r;
        }
    }

    /* Try single-param operations */
    OpType single_ops[] = {OP_ADD, OP_MUL, OP_POW, OP_DIV, OP_SQRT};
    for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
        double p, r;
        if (try_single_param_op(single_ops[i], sys, &p, &r)) {
            /* Occam's razor: prefer fewer params at equal residual */
            int dominated = (r < best.residual) ||
                           (r <= best.residual + EPS_T1 && 1 < best.n_params);
            if (dominated) {
                best.op = single_ops[i];
                best.params[0] = p;
                best.n_params = 1;
                best.residual = r;
            }
        }
    }

    /* Try linear (2-param) — only if it's strictly better */
    {
        double m, c, r;
        if (try_linear(sys, &m, &c, &r)) {
            int dominated = (r < best.residual - EPS_T1) ||
                           (r <= best.residual + EPS_T1 && best.n_params > 2);
            if (dominated) {
                best.op = OP_LINEAR;
                best.params[0] = m;
                best.params[1] = c;
                best.n_params = 2;
                best.residual = r;
            }
        }
    }

    /* Try quadratic (3-param) — only if it's strictly better */
    {
        double p[3], r;
        if (try_quadratic(sys, p, &r)) {
            int dominated = (r < best.residual - EPS_T1) ||
                           (r <= best.residual + EPS_T1 && best.n_params > 3);
            if (dominated) {
                best.op = OP_QUADRATIC;
                best.params[0] = p[0]; best.params[1] = p[1]; best.params[2] = p[2];
                best.n_params = 3;
                best.residual = r;
            }
        }
    }

    /*============================================================
     * COMPOUND OPERATION SEARCH — composition IS {2,3}
     *
     * If simple ops didn't reach T1, try composition.
     * Transform the observations (output side) and re-decompose.
     * If the transformed system reaches T1, the original
     * relationship is y = inverse_transform(f(x)).
     *
     * This is {2,3} applied to operations themselves:
     *   inner op (2a), outer op (2b), composition (3)
     *============================================================*/
    if (best.residual > EPS_T1) {
        /* Output transforms: (x, y) → (x, t(y)) */
        typedef struct {
            const char *name;
            const char *inv_name;
            int    valid;
        } OTrans;

        OTrans transforms[] = {
            {"y²",    "sqrt"},
            {"1-y²",  "sqrt(1-z)"},
            {"1/y",   "1/z"},
            {"log(y)","exp"},
            {"1-y",   "1-z"},
        };
        int n_transforms = 5;

        for (int ti = 0; ti < n_transforms; ti++) {
            System ts;
            memset(&ts, 0, sizeof(ts));
            ts.n = sys->n;
            snprintf(ts.name, 64, "%s_t%d", sys->name, ti);

            int valid = 1;
            for (int i = 0; i < sys->n; i++) {
                double y = sys->obs[i].out;
                double ty;
                switch (ti) {
                case 0: ty = y * y;                     break; /* y² */
                case 1: ty = 1.0 - y * y;               break; /* 1-y² */
                case 2: ty = (y != 0) ? 1.0/y : NAN;    break; /* 1/y */
                case 3: ty = (y > 0) ? log(y) : NAN;    break; /* log(y) */
                case 4: ty = 1.0 - y;                    break; /* 1-y */
                default: ty = NAN;
                }
                if (isnan(ty) || isinf(ty)) { valid = 0; break; }
                ts.obs[i].in = sys->obs[i].in;
                ts.obs[i].out = ty;
            }
            if (!valid) continue;

            /* Decompose the transformed system (recursive call without compounds) */
            /* Use simple ops only to avoid infinite recursion */
            Decomposition td;
            memset(&td, 0, sizeof(td));
            td.op = OP_NONE;
            td.tier = T4;
            td.residual = 1e30;

            /* Try all simple ops on transformed system */
            OpType zo[] = {OP_IDENTITY, OP_NEGATE, OP_RECIP};
            for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
                double r;
                if (try_zero_param_op(zo[i], &ts, &r) && r < td.residual) {
                    td.op = zo[i]; td.n_params = 0; td.residual = r;
                }
            }
            OpType so[] = {OP_ADD, OP_MUL, OP_POW, OP_DIV, OP_SQRT};
            for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
                double p, r;
                if (try_single_param_op(so[i], &ts, &p, &r) && r < td.residual) {
                    td.op = so[i]; td.params[0] = p; td.n_params = 1; td.residual = r;
                }
            }
            { double m, c, r;
              if (try_linear(&ts, &m, &c, &r) && r < td.residual) {
                  td.op = OP_LINEAR; td.params[0]=m; td.params[1]=c;
                  td.n_params = 2; td.residual = r;
              }
            }
            { double p[3], r;
              if (try_quadratic(&ts, p, &r) && r < td.residual) {
                  td.op = OP_QUADRATIC; td.params[0]=p[0]; td.params[1]=p[1];
                  td.params[2]=p[2]; td.n_params = 3; td.residual = r;
              }
            }

            /* If transformed system reaches T1, record the compound */
            if (td.residual < EPS_T1 && td.residual < best.residual) {
                best.op = OP_COMP;
                best.residual = td.residual;
                best.n_params = 0; /* params stored in compound fields */
                best.inner_op = td.op;
                best.inner_n = td.n_params;
                memcpy(best.inner_params, td.params, sizeof(td.params));
                /* outer is the inverse of the output transform */
                best.outer_op = (OpType)ti;  /* store transform index */
                best.outer_n = ti;
                snprintf(best.comp_desc, 64, "%s(%s(x))",
                         transforms[ti].inv_name, OP_NAMES[td.op]);

                /* Verify: compute actual residual against original data */
                double comp_err = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < sys->n; i++) {
                    double fx = apply_op(td.op, sys->obs[i].in, td.params);
                    double pred;
                    switch (ti) {
                    case 0: pred = (fx >= 0) ? sqrt(fx) : NAN;     break;
                    case 1: pred = (1-fx >= 0) ? sqrt(1-fx) : NAN; break;
                    case 2: pred = (fx != 0) ? 1.0/fx : NAN;       break;
                    case 3: pred = exp(fx);                          break;
                    case 4: pred = 1.0 - fx;                         break;
                    default: pred = NAN;
                    }
                    if (isnan(pred)) { comp_err = 1e30; break; }
                    double err = fabs(pred - sys->obs[i].out);
                    comp_err += err * err;
                }
                best.residual = sqrt(comp_err / sys->n);
            }
        }
    }

    /* Tier classify */
    if (best.residual < EPS_T1) {
        best.tier = T1;
    } else if (best.residual < EPS_T2) {
        best.tier = T2;
    } else if (best.op != OP_NONE) {
        best.tier = T3;  /* found something but it doesn't fit well */
    } else {
        best.tier = T4;
    }

    /* Validate: count exact matches */
    best.n_validated = sys->n;
    best.n_correct = 0;
    best.max_err = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < sys->n; i++) {
        double pred = apply_decomp(&best, sys->obs[i].in);
        double err = fabs(pred - sys->obs[i].out);
        if (err < EPS_T1) best.n_correct++;
        if (err > best.max_err) best.max_err = err;
    }

    return best;
}

/*============================================================
 * RECURSIVE DECOMPOSITION — ONE ALL THE WAY DOWN
 *
 * If the first decomposition has residual > 0,
 * decompose the RESIDUAL using the same {2,3} method.
 * The residual at level N becomes the system at level N+1.
 * This is "hidden layers" from ONETWO.
 *============================================================*/

static DecompTree decompose_recursive(const System *sys)
{
    DecompTree tree;
    memset(&tree, 0, sizeof(tree));

    System current;
    memcpy(&current, sys, sizeof(System));

    for (int depth = 0; depth < MAX_DEPTH; depth++) {
        Decomposition d = decompose(&current);
        tree.layers[depth] = d;
        tree.depth = depth + 1;

        /* T1 — bedrock reached, stop */
        if (d.tier == T1) {
            tree.tier = T1;
            tree.total_residual = d.residual;
            return tree;
        }

        /* T4 — can't decompose further, stop */
        if (d.op == OP_NONE) {
            tree.tier = T4;
            tree.total_residual = d.residual;
            return tree;
        }

        /* Build residual system for next level */
        System residual;
        memset(&residual, 0, sizeof(residual));
        residual.n = current.n;
        snprintf(residual.name, 64, "%s_residual_%d", sys->name, depth);

        int has_nonzero = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < current.n; i++) {
            double pred = apply_decomp(&d, current.obs[i].in);
            residual.obs[i].in = current.obs[i].in;
            residual.obs[i].out = current.obs[i].out - pred;
            if (fabs(residual.obs[i].out) > EPS_T1) has_nonzero = 1;
        }

        if (!has_nonzero) {
            /* Residual is zero — we're done */
            tree.tier = T1;
            tree.total_residual = 0;
            return tree;
        }

        memcpy(&current, &residual, sizeof(System));
    }

    /* Hit max depth without convergence */
    tree.tier = T3;
    tree.total_residual = tree.layers[tree.depth - 1].residual;
    return tree;
}

/*============================================================
 * TWO — COMPOSE / PREDICT
 *
 * Given a decomposition tree, predict output for new input.
 * This IS TWO: apply the invariant to generate new variants.
 *============================================================*/

static double predict(const DecompTree *tree, double input)
{
    double result = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < tree->depth; i++) {
        const Decomposition *d = &tree->layers[i];
        if (d->op == OP_NONE) break;
        result += apply_op(d->op, input, d->params);
        /* For additive decomposition: total = sum of layers */
        /* Each layer predicts the residual of the previous  */
        if (i == 0) {
            result = apply_op(d->op, input, d->params);
        }
        /* TODO: for deeper layers, add residual correction */
    }
    return result;
}

/*============================================================
 * CROSS-DOMAIN TRANSFER — Transfer shape, not value
 *
 * Extract the operation from Domain A.
 * Apply it to Domain B.
 * Validate. If it works, the shape transferred.
 *============================================================*/

typedef struct {
    Decomposition source;
    Decomposition target;
    double        transfer_residual;
    Tier          transfer_tier;
    int           shape_matches;  /* same op type? */
} Transfer;

static Transfer transfer_cross_domain(const System *source, const System *target)
{
    Transfer t;
    memset(&t, 0, sizeof(t));

    t.source = decompose(source);
    t.target = decompose(target);

    t.shape_matches = (t.source.op == t.target.op);

    /* Try applying source's exact parameters to target */
    double total_err = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < target->n; i++) {
        double pred = apply_op(t.source.op, target->obs[i].in, t.source.params);
        if (isnan(pred) || isinf(pred)) { t.transfer_tier = T4; return t; }
        double err = fabs(pred - target->obs[i].out);
        total_err += err * err;
    }
    t.transfer_residual = sqrt(total_err / target->n);

    if (t.transfer_residual < EPS_T1) t.transfer_tier = T1;
    else if (t.transfer_residual < EPS_T2) t.transfer_tier = T2;
    else if (t.shape_matches) t.transfer_tier = T2;  /* same shape, different constants */
    else t.transfer_tier = T3;

    return t;
}

/*============================================================
 * FIXED-POINT DETECTOR
 *
 * Check if x = f(x) for any discovered operation.
 * Fixed points are where ONE and TWO converge.
 * Every T1 result in XYZT is a fixed-point equation.
 *============================================================*/

typedef struct {
    double   x;        /* the fixed point   */
    OpType   op;       /* the operation      */
    double   params[4];
    int      found;
} FixedPoint;

static FixedPoint find_fixed_point(const Decomposition *d)
{
    FixedPoint fp;
    memset(&fp, 0, sizeof(fp));
    fp.op = d->op;
    memcpy(fp.params, d->params, sizeof(fp.params));

    /* Newton's method: find x where apply_op(x) = x */
    /* i.e., find root of g(x) = apply_op(x) - x = 0 */
    double x = 1.0;  /* initial guess */
    for (int iter = 0; iter < 100; iter++) {
        double fx = apply_op(d->op, x, d->params);
        if (isnan(fx) || isinf(fx)) break;
        double g = fx - x;
        if (fabs(g) < EPS_T1) {
            fp.x = x;
            fp.found = 1;
            return fp;
        }
        /* Numerical derivative */
        double h = 1e-8;
        double fx2 = apply_op(d->op, x + h, d->params);
        if (isnan(fx2)) break;
        double dg = (fx2 - (x+h)) - g;
        if (fabs(dg) < 1e-30) break;
        x -= g * h / dg;
    }

    /* Try a few obvious guesses */
    double guesses[] = {0, 1, -1, 2, 0.5, -0.5, 3, M_PI, M_E};
    for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) {
        double fx = apply_op(d->op, guesses[i], d->params);
        if (!isnan(fx) && fabs(fx - guesses[i]) < EPS_T1) {
            fp.x = guesses[i];
            fp.found = 1;
            return fp;
        }
    }

    return fp;
}

/*============================================================
 * PRINTING
 *============================================================*/

static const char *tier_str(Tier t)
{
    switch (t) {
    case T1: return "T1 (proven)";
    case T2: return "T2 (strong signal)";
    case T3: return "T3 (falsifiable)";
    case T4: return "T4 (interpretation)";
    }
    return "??";
}

static void print_decomposition(const char *label, const Decomposition *d)
{
    if (d->op == OP_COMP) {
        printf("  %-20s  op=COMP       %s  ", label, d->comp_desc);
    } else {
        printf("  %-20s  op=%-10s  ", label, OP_NAMES[d->op]);
        if (d->n_params == 0)
            printf("(no params)");
        else if (d->n_params == 1)
            printf("p=%.6g", d->params[0]);
        else if (d->n_params == 2)
            printf("m=%.6g c=%.6g", d->params[0], d->params[1]);
        else if (d->n_params == 3)
            printf("a=%.6g b=%.6g c=%.6g", d->params[0], d->params[1], d->params[2]);
    }

    printf("  residual=%.2e  %d/%d exact  %s\n",
           d->residual, d->n_correct, d->n_validated, tier_str(d->tier));
}

static void print_tree(const char *label, const DecompTree *tree)
{
    printf("\n  ┌─ %s ─ depth=%d ─ %s ─────────────────────────\n",
           label, tree->depth, tier_str(tree->tier));
    for (int i = 0; i < tree->depth; i++) {
        char buf[32];
        snprintf(buf, 32, "layer %d", i);
        printf("  │ ");
        print_decomposition(buf, &tree->layers[i]);
    }
    printf("  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n");
}

/*============================================================
 * TEST FRAMEWORK
 *============================================================*/

static int g_pass = 0, g_fail = 0;

static void check(const char *name, int cond)
{
    if (cond) { g_pass++; }
    else { g_fail++; printf("  FAIL: %s\n", name); }
}

static void check_tier(const char *name, Tier expected, Tier got)
{
    if (expected == got) { g_pass++; }
    else {
        g_fail++;
        printf("  FAIL: %s — expected %s, got %s\n",
               name, tier_str(expected), tier_str(got));
    }
}

static void check_near(const char *name, double expected, double got, double tol)
{
    if (fabs(expected - got) < tol) { g_pass++; }
    else {
        g_fail++;
        printf("  FAIL: %s — expected %.6g, got %.6g (diff=%.2e)\n",
               name, expected, got, fabs(expected - got));
    }
}

/*============================================================
 * TEST 1: ARITHMETIC — discover f(x) from examples
 *============================================================*/

static void test_arithmetic(void)
{
    printf("\n");
    printf(" ╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n");
    printf(" ║  TEST 1: ARITHMETIC — discover f(x) from examples    ║\n");
    printf(" ║  {2,3}: input(2a) × operation(3) = output(2b)        ║\n");
    printf(" ╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n");

    /* f(x) = x + 5 */
    {
        System s = {.name = "x+5", .n = 5,
            .obs = {{1,6},{2,7},{3,8},{10,15},{-3,2}}};
        Decomposition d = decompose(&s);
        print_decomposition("x+5", &d);
        check("x+5: op=ADD", d.op == OP_ADD);
        check_near("x+5: param=5", 5.0, d.params[0], EPS_T1);
        check_tier("x+5: T1", T1, d.tier);
    }

    /* f(x) = x * 3 */
    {
        System s = {.name = "x*3", .n = 5,
            .obs = {{1,3},{2,6},{4,12},{-1,-3},{10,30}}};
        Decomposition d = decompose(&s);
        print_decomposition("x*3", &d);
        check("x*3: op=MUL", d.op == OP_MUL);
        check_near("x*3: param=3", 3.0, d.params[0], EPS_T1);
        check_tier("x*3: T1", T1, d.tier);
    }

    /* f(x) = x^2 */
    {
        System s = {.name = "x^2", .n = 5,
            .obs = {{1,1},{2,4},{3,9},{4,16},{5,25}}};
        Decomposition d = decompose(&s);
        print_decomposition("x^2", &d);
        check("x^2: op=POW", d.op == OP_POW);
        check_near("x^2: param=2", 2.0, d.params[0], EPS_T1);
        check_tier("x^2: T1", T1, d.tier);
    }

    /* f(x) = 2x + 7 (linear, 2 params) */
    {
        System s = {.name = "2x+7", .n = 5,
            .obs = {{0,7},{1,9},{2,11},{3,13},{10,27}}};
        Decomposition d = decompose(&s);
        print_decomposition("2x+7", &d);
        check("2x+7: op=LINEAR", d.op == OP_LINEAR);
        check_near("2x+7: m=2", 2.0, d.params[0], EPS_T1);
        check_near("2x+7: c=7", 7.0, d.params[1], EPS_T1);
        check_tier("2x+7: T1", T1, d.tier);
    }

    /* f(x) = x^2 + 3x + 2 = (x+1)(x+2) */
    {
        System s = {.name = "(x+1)(x+2)", .n = 6,
            .obs = {{0,2},{1,6},{2,12},{3,20},{-1,0},{-2,0}}};
        Decomposition d = decompose(&s);
        print_decomposition("(x+1)(x+2)", &d);
        check("(x+1)(x+2): op=QUADRATIC", d.op == OP_QUADRATIC);
        check_near("(x+1)(x+2): a=1", 1.0, d.params[0], EPS_T1);
        check_near("(x+1)(x+2): b=3", 3.0, d.params[1], EPS_T1);
        check_near("(x+1)(x+2): c=2", 2.0, d.params[2], EPS_T1);
        check_tier("(x+1)(x+2): T1", T1, d.tier);
    }

    /* f(x) = -x (negate) */
    {
        System s = {.name = "-x", .n = 4,
            .obs = {{1,-1},{5,-5},{-3,3},{0,0}}};
        Decomposition d = decompose(&s);
        print_decomposition("-x", &d);
        check("-x: tier T1", d.tier == T1);
    }

    /* f(x) = identity */
    {
        System s = {.name = "x=x", .n = 4,
            .obs = {{1,1},{5,5},{-3,-3},{0,0}}};
        Decomposition d = decompose(&s);
        print_decomposition("identity", &d);
        check("identity: tier T1", d.tier == T1);
    }
}

/*============================================================
 * TEST 2: PHYSICS — discover laws from measurements
 *============================================================*/

static void test_physics(void)
{
    printf("\n");
    printf(" ╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n");
    printf(" ║  TEST 2: PHYSICS — discover laws from measurements   ║\n");
    printf(" ║  {2,3}: measurement(2a) × law(3) = prediction(2b)   ║\n");
    printf(" ╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n");

    /* Ohm's law: V = I * R, with R = 100 ohms */
    {
        System s = {.name = "Ohm V=IR", .n = 5,
            .obs = {{0.01, 1.0}, {0.05, 5.0}, {0.1, 10.0},
                    {0.5, 50.0}, {1.0, 100.0}}};
        Decomposition d = decompose(&s);
        print_decomposition("Ohm's law", &d);
        check("Ohm: op=MUL", d.op == OP_MUL);
        check_near("Ohm: R=100", 100.0, d.params[0], EPS_T1);
        check_tier("Ohm: T1", T1, d.tier);

        /* TWO: predict for new current */
        double pred = apply_op(d.op, 0.33, d.params);
        check_near("Ohm predict I=0.33", 33.0, pred, EPS_T1);
    }

    /* Kinetic energy: E = 0.5 * m * v^2, plot E vs v for m=2 */
    /* E = v^2 (since 0.5*m = 1 when m=2) */
    {
        System s = {.name = "KE: E=v^2", .n = 5,
            .obs = {{1,1},{2,4},{3,9},{5,25},{10,100}}};
        Decomposition d = decompose(&s);
        print_decomposition("E=v^2", &d);
        check("KE: op=POW", d.op == OP_POW);
        check_near("KE: exponent=2", 2.0, d.params[0], EPS_T1);
        check_tier("KE: T1", T1, d.tier);
    }

    /* Time dilation: tau = t * sqrt(1 - v^2/c^2) */
    /* At low velocities: tau ≈ t * (1 - v^2/(2c^2)) ≈ LINEAR for small v/c */
    /* But at exact values, it's sqrt(1-x) where x = (v/c)^2 */
    {
        /* Map beta = v/c to dilation factor */
        System s = {.name = "time_dilation", .n = 5,
            .obs = {{0.0, 1.0}, {0.1, 0.99498743710662},
                    {0.3, 0.95393920141695}, {0.6, 0.8},
                    {0.9, 0.43588989435407}}};
        /* This is sqrt(1 - x^2) — the engine should find POW with
           param 0.5 on (1-x^2), but since we don't have compound
           ops yet, it'll find the best simple fit */
        Decomposition d = decompose(&s);
        print_decomposition("dilation", &d);
        if (d.op == OP_COMP) {
            printf("  → ENGINE DISCOVERED: %s\n", d.comp_desc);
            printf("  → This IS sqrt(1 - v²/c²) = Schwarzschild\n");
            printf("  → XYZT expansion resistance = GR time dilation\n");
            check("dilation: T1 via compound", d.tier == T1);
        } else {
            printf("  → Time dilation requires compound ops (sqrt(1-x²))\n");
            printf("  → Single-layer decomposition correctly reports non-T1\n");
            check("dilation: found compound", 0);  /* should have found it */
        }

        /* Recursive decomposition test removed — compound ops handle this directly */
    }
}

/*============================================================
 * TEST 3: FIXED POINTS — x = f(x)
 *============================================================*/

static void test_fixed_points(void)
{
    printf("\n");
    printf(" ╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n");
    printf(" ║  TEST 3: FIXED POINTS — x = f(x)                    ║\n");
    printf(" ║  Where ONE and TWO converge                          ║\n");
    printf(" ╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n");

    /* f(x) = x + 0: fixed point everywhere */
    {
        Decomposition d = {.op = OP_IDENTITY, .n_params = 0};
        FixedPoint fp = find_fixed_point(&d);
        printf("  identity:  every x is a fixed point — ");
        check("identity fp found", fp.found);
    }

    /* f(x) = 2x: fixed point at x=0 */
    {
        Decomposition d = {.op = OP_MUL, .params = {2.0}, .n_params = 1};
        FixedPoint fp = find_fixed_point(&d);
        printf("  f(x)=2x:  ");
        check("2x fp found", fp.found);
        if (fp.found) check_near("2x fp at 0", 0.0, fp.x, EPS_T1);
    }

    /* f(x) = x + 0: trivially x=anything. Try x+5: no fixed point in reals */
    {
        Decomposition d = {.op = OP_ADD, .params = {5.0}, .n_params = 1};
        FixedPoint fp = find_fixed_point(&d);
        printf("  f(x)=x+5: ");
        check("x+5 no fp", !fp.found);
    }

    /* f(x) = x^2: fixed points at 0 and 1 */
    {
        Decomposition d = {.op = OP_POW, .params = {2.0}, .n_params = 1};
        FixedPoint fp = find_fixed_point(&d);
        printf("  f(x)=x²:  ");
        check("x^2 fp found", fp.found);
        if (fp.found)
            printf("  fixed point at x=%.6g\n", fp.x);
    }

    /* {2,3} FIXED POINT: f(n) = n+n = n*n. Only n=2. */
    {
        printf("\n  THE {2,3} FIXED POINT:\n");
        printf("  n + n = n * n  →  2n = n²  →  n(n-2) = 0  →  n = 0 or n = 2\n");
        printf("  n = 0 is trivial (0+0 = 0×0 = 0, no distinction)\n");
        printf("  n = 2 is the unique non-trivial fixed point\n");

        int found_nontrivial = 0;
        int n_solutions = 0;
        for (int n = -1000; n <= 1000; n++) {
            if (n + n == n * n) {
                n_solutions++;
                if (n != 0) found_nontrivial++;
                if (n != 0)
                    printf("  FOUND: n=%d, n+n=%d, n*n=%d\n", n, n+n, n*n);
            }
        }
        check("{2,3} unique: only n=2", found_nontrivial == 1);
        check("{2,3} total: n=0 and n=2", n_solutions == 2);

        printf("\n  Why this matters:\n");
        printf("  Addition and multiplication are the two fundamental\n");
        printf("  arithmetic operations. They CONVERGE only at n=2.\n");
        printf("  The substrate (3 = 2+1) is the minimal connector.\n");
        printf("  {2,3} is not a choice. It's the arithmetic fixed point.\n");
    }
}

/*============================================================
 * TEST 4: CROSS-DOMAIN TRANSFER
 *============================================================*/

static void test_transfer(void)
{
    printf("\n");
    printf(" ╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n");
    printf(" ║  TEST 4: CROSS-DOMAIN TRANSFER                      ║\n");
    printf(" ║  Transfer shape, not value                           ║\n");
    printf(" ╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n");

    /* Domain A: Ohm's law V = 100*I */
    System ohm = {.name = "Ohm", .n = 4,
        .obs = {{0.01,1},{0.1,10},{0.5,50},{1,100}}};

    /* Domain B: Hooke's law F = 200*x (different constant, same shape) */
    System hooke = {.name = "Hooke", .n = 4,
        .obs = {{0.01,2},{0.1,20},{0.5,100},{1,200}}};

    /* Domain C: Gravity F = 9.81*m (same shape again) */
    System gravity = {.name = "Gravity", .n = 4,
        .obs = {{1,9.81},{2,19.62},{5,49.05},{10,98.1}}};

    Transfer t1 = transfer_cross_domain(&ohm, &hooke);
    printf("  Ohm → Hooke:   shape_match=%d  op_A=%-6s op_B=%-6s\n",
           t1.shape_matches, OP_NAMES[t1.source.op], OP_NAMES[t1.target.op]);
    check("Ohm→Hooke: same shape (MUL)", t1.shape_matches);

    Transfer t2 = transfer_cross_domain(&ohm, &gravity);
    printf("  Ohm → Gravity:  shape_match=%d  op_A=%-6s op_B=%-6s\n",
           t2.shape_matches, OP_NAMES[t2.source.op], OP_NAMES[t2.target.op]);
    check("Ohm→Gravity: same shape (MUL)", t2.shape_matches);

    /* Domain D: Inverse square law F = 1000/r^2 — DIFFERENT shape */
    System inv_sq = {.name = "InvSq", .n = 4,
        .obs = {{1,1000},{2,250},{5,40},{10,10}}};

    Transfer t3 = transfer_cross_domain(&ohm, &inv_sq);
    printf("  Ohm → InvSq:    shape_match=%d  op_A=%-6s op_B=%-6s\n",
           t3.shape_matches, OP_NAMES[t3.source.op], OP_NAMES[t3.target.op]);
    check("Ohm→InvSq: different shape", !t3.shape_matches);

    printf("\n  RESULT:\n");
    printf("  Linear laws (Ohm, Hooke, Gravity) share shape: MUL\n");
    printf("  Inverse square has different shape: POW\n");
    printf("  The engine distinguishes structural similarity\n");
    printf("  from superficial similarity. Transfer the SHAPE.\n");
}

/*============================================================
 * TEST 5: RECURSIVE DECOMPOSITION — hidden layers
 *============================================================*/

static void test_recursive(void)
{
    printf("\n");
    printf(" ╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n");
    printf(" ║  TEST 5: RECURSIVE DECOMPOSITION                    ║\n");
    printf(" ║  ONE all the way DOWN before TWO all the way UP     ║\n");
    printf(" ╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n");

    /* f(x) = x^2 + 5: quadratic finds it in one shot */
    {
        System s = {.name = "x²+5", .n = 5,
            .obs = {{0,5},{1,6},{2,9},{3,14},{4,21}}};
        DecompTree tree = decompose_recursive(&s);
        print_tree("x²+5", &tree);
        check("x²+5: reaches T1", tree.tier == T1);
    }

    /* f(x) = 3x^2 + 2x - 1 */
    {
        System s = {.name = "3x²+2x-1", .n = 6,
            .obs = {{0,-1},{1,4},{2,15},{3,32},{-1,0},{-2,7}}};
        DecompTree tree = decompose_recursive(&s);
        print_tree("3x²+2x-1", &tree);
        check("3x²+2x-1: reaches T1", tree.tier == T1);
    }

    /* Simple linear that single op catches */
    {
        System s = {.name = "7x", .n = 4,
            .obs = {{1,7},{2,14},{3,21},{10,70}}};
        DecompTree tree = decompose_recursive(&s);
        print_tree("7x", &tree);
        check("7x: depth=1 (no recursion needed)", tree.depth == 1);
        check("7x: T1", tree.tier == T1);
    }
}

/*============================================================
 * TEST 6: THE {2,3} THEOREM
 *
 * Prove: n + n = n × n has unique non-trivial solution n = 2.
 * This is Theorem 1 — the arithmetic fixed point.
 *============================================================*/

static void test_theorem_1(void)
{
    printf("\n");
    printf(" ╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n");
    printf(" ║  THEOREM 1: {2,3} IS THE ARITHMETIC FIXED POINT     ║\n");
    printf(" ║                                                       ║\n");
    printf(" ║  Claim: n+n = n×n has unique nontrivial solution n=2 ║\n");
    printf(" ║  Proof: n+n = n×n → 2n = n² → n²-2n = 0            ║\n");
    printf(" ║         → n(n-2) = 0 → n=0 or n=2                   ║\n");
    printf(" ║  n=0: trivial (no distinction)                       ║\n");
    printf(" ║  n=2: unique non-trivial fixed point                 ║\n");
    printf(" ║                                                       ║\n");
    printf(" ║  Status: T1 — algebraic proof, no free parameters    ║\n");
    printf(" ╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n");

    /* Exhaustive verification over integers */
    int nontrivial_count = 0;
    int nontrivial_value = 0;

    for (int n = -10000; n <= 10000; n++) {
        long long sum = (long long)n + n;
        long long prod = (long long)n * n;
        if (sum == prod && n != 0) {
            nontrivial_count++;
            nontrivial_value = n;
        }
    }

    printf("  Exhaustive search [-10000, 10000]:\n");
    printf("  Non-trivial solutions: %d\n", nontrivial_count);
    printf("  Solution value: n = %d\n", nontrivial_value);
    printf("  2 + 2 = %d\n", 2 + 2);
    printf("  2 × 2 = %d\n", 2 * 2);
    check("Theorem 1: unique nontrivial", nontrivial_count == 1);
    check("Theorem 1: n=2", nontrivial_value == 2);

    /* Also verify for rationals: n+n = n*n → 2n = n² → n = 2 (or 0) */
    /* For reals: same. Quadratic has exactly 2 roots. */
    printf("\n  Algebraic proof (all reals):\n");
    printf("  n + n = n × n\n");
    printf("  2n = n²\n");
    printf("  n² - 2n = 0\n");
    printf("  n(n - 2) = 0\n");
    printf("  n = 0 (trivial: 0+0 = 0×0 = 0, nothing exists)\n");
