            body[p++] = ',';
            p += snprintf(body + p, MAX_BUF - p, "{\"role\":\"%s\",\"content\":\"", msgs[i].role);
            p += jesc(body + p, MAX_BUF - p, msgs[i].content);
            p += snprintf(body + p, MAX_BUF - p, "\"}");
        }
        p += snprintf(body + p, MAX_BUF - p, "]}");
    }

    /* Send via WinHTTP */
#ifdef _WIN32
    HINTERNET hSes = WinHttpOpen(L"XYZT/1.0", WINHTTP_ACCESS_TYPE_DEFAULT_PROXY,
                                  WINHTTP_NO_PROXY_NAME, WINHTTP_NO_PROXY_BYPASS, 0);
    HINTERNET hCon = WinHttpConnect(hSes, g_provider->host,
                                    (INTERNET_PORT)g_provider->port, 0);
    DWORD flags = g_provider->use_tls ? WINHTTP_FLAG_SECURE : 0;
    HINTERNET hReq = WinHttpOpenRequest(hCon, L"POST", g_provider->path, NULL,
                                        WINHTTP_NO_REFERER, WINHTTP_DEFAULT_ACCEPT_TYPES,
                                        flags);

    winhttp_set_headers(hReq);

    int blen = (int)strlen(body);
    BOOL sent = WinHttpSendRequest(hReq, WINHTTP_NO_ADDITIONAL_HEADERS, 0,
                                    body, blen, blen, 0);

    /* Retry on connection failure */
    int retries = 0;
    while (!sent && retries < 3) {
        retries++;
        printf("\033[33m[reconnecting %d/3...]\033[0m", retries);
        fflush(stdout);
        Sleep(2000 * retries);
        load_key();
        WinHttpCloseHandle(hReq);
        WinHttpCloseHandle(hCon);
        hCon = WinHttpConnect(hSes, g_provider->host, (INTERNET_PORT)g_provider->port, 0);
        hReq = WinHttpOpenRequest(hCon, L"POST", g_provider->path, NULL,
                                  WINHTTP_NO_REFERER, WINHTTP_DEFAULT_ACCEPT_TYPES, flags);
        winhttp_set_headers(hReq);
        sent = WinHttpSendRequest(hReq, WINHTTP_NO_ADDITIONAL_HEADERS, 0,
                                   body, blen, blen, 0);
    }
    free(body);

    if (!sent) {
        printf("\033[31m[connection failed after 3 retries]\033[0m\n");
        WinHttpCloseHandle(hReq);
        WinHttpCloseHandle(hCon);
        WinHttpCloseHandle(hSes);
        return strdup("");
    }

    WinHttpReceiveResponse(hReq, NULL);

    /* Check HTTP status */
    DWORD status = 0, sz = sizeof(status);
    WinHttpQueryHeaders(hReq, WINHTTP_QUERY_STATUS_CODE | WINHTTP_QUERY_FLAG_NUMBER,
                        NULL, &status, &sz, NULL);
    if (status != 200) {
        printf("\033[90m[HTTP %lu]\033[0m", status);
        /* Dump error body */
        char errbuf[2048] = {0}; DWORD ebr;
        WinHttpReadData(hReq, errbuf, sizeof(errbuf)-1, &ebr);
        if (ebr > 0) printf("\033[31m %.200s\033[0m", errbuf);
    }
    if (status == 401) {
        static int auth_retries = 0;
        if (auth_retries++ >= 2) {
            auth_retries = 0;
            printf("\033[31m[auth failed after refresh — use /key to set a key]\033[0m\n");
            WinHttpCloseHandle(hReq);
            WinHttpCloseHandle(hCon);
            WinHttpCloseHandle(hSes);
            return strdup("");
        }
        printf("\033[33m[token expired, refreshing...]\033[0m");
        fflush(stdout);
        WinHttpCloseHandle(hReq);
        WinHttpCloseHandle(hCon);
        WinHttpCloseHandle(hSes);
        if (refresh_oauth() == 0)
            return api_call(system, msgs, n_msgs);
        load_key();
        if (g_key[0]) return api_call(system, msgs, n_msgs);
        auth_retries = 0;
        printf("\033[31m[auth failed — use /key to set a key]\033[0m\n");
        return strdup("");
    }
    if (status == 529 || status == 503) {
        printf("\033[33m[overloaded, retrying in 5s...]\033[0m");
        fflush(stdout);
        Sleep(5000);
        WinHttpCloseHandle(hReq);
        WinHttpCloseHandle(hCon);
        WinHttpCloseHandle(hSes);
        return api_call(system, msgs, n_msgs);
    }

    /* Response handling — streaming for Anthropic, non-streaming for OpenAI format */
    char *response = (char *)calloc(MAX_BUF, 1);
    int rp = 0;
    int tok_in = 0, tok_out = 0;
    int seen_content = 0;
    char chunk[4096];
    DWORD br;

    if (g_debug && !g_debug_fp)
        g_debug_fp = fopen(".xyzt/sse_debug.log", "w");

    if (g_provider->format == FMT_OPENAI) {
        /* Non-streaming: read full response body, extract content */
        char *raw = (char *)calloc(MAX_BUF, 1);
        int raw_len = 0;
        while (WinHttpReadData(hReq, chunk, sizeof(chunk) - 1, &br) && br > 0) {
            if (raw_len + (int)br < MAX_BUF - 1) {
                memcpy(raw + raw_len, chunk, br);
                raw_len += (int)br;
            }
        }
        raw[raw_len] = 0;
        if (g_debug_fp) fprintf(g_debug_fp, "=== RAW RESPONSE ===\n%s\n=== END ===\n", raw);

        /* Extract "content":"..." from response JSON.
           Skip "reasoning_content" by checking for preceding underscore.
           Handle escaped characters in the content string. */
        char *txt = raw;
        while ((txt = strstr(txt, "\"content\":\"")) != NULL) {
            if (txt > raw && *(txt - 1) == '_') { txt += 11; continue; }
            txt += 11; /* skip "content":" */
            while (*txt && rp < MAX_BUF - 1) {
                if (*txt == '"') break; /* end of content string */
                if (*txt == '\\' && txt[1]) {
                    txt++;
                    if (*txt == 'n') { response[rp++] = '\n'; putchar('\n'); }
                    else if (*txt == 't') { response[rp++] = '\t'; putchar('\t'); }
                    else if (*txt == '"') { response[rp++] = '"'; putchar('"'); }
                    else if (*txt == '\\') { response[rp++] = '\\'; putchar('\\'); }
                    else { response[rp++] = *txt; putchar(*txt); }
                } else {
                    response[rp++] = *txt;
                    putchar(*txt);
                }
                txt++;
            }
            fflush(stdout);
            break; /* only extract first content field */
        }

        /* Extract usage stats */
        char *ui = strstr(raw, "\"prompt_tokens\":");
        if (ui) { while (*ui && (*ui < '0' || *ui > '9')) ui++; tok_in = atoi(ui); }
        char *uo = strstr(raw, "\"completion_tokens\":");
        if (uo) { while (*uo && (*uo < '0' || *uo > '9')) uo++; tok_out = atoi(uo); }

        free(raw);
        goto response_done;
    }

    /* Streaming path — Anthropic SSE format */
    char *linebuf = (char *)calloc(65536, 1);
    int lb_len = 0;

    while (WinHttpReadData(hReq, chunk, sizeof(chunk) - 1, &br) && br > 0) {
        chunk[br] = 0;

        if (g_debug_fp) {
            fprintf(g_debug_fp, "=== CHUNK %d bytes ===\n%.*s\n=== END ===\n",
                    (int)br, (int)br, chunk);
            fflush(g_debug_fp);
        }

        /* Append chunk to line buffer, process complete lines */
        if (lb_len + (int)br < 65535) {
            memcpy(linebuf + lb_len, chunk, br);
            lb_len += (int)br;
            linebuf[lb_len] = 0;
        }

        /* Process complete SSE events from the line buffer.
           SSE events are delimited by \ndata: or \n\n.
           Literal \n inside JSON content is NOT a delimiter. */
        int pos = 0;
        while (pos < lb_len) {
            char *line = linebuf + pos;
            int remaining = lb_len - pos;

            /* Find SSE event boundary */
            int end = -1;
            if (strncmp(line, "data: ", 6) == 0) {
                /* Scan for \ndata: or \n\n (NOT bare \n which may be in JSON) */
                for (int j = 6; j < remaining - 1; j++) {
                    if (line[j] == '\n') {
                        if (strncmp(line + j + 1, "data: ", 6) == 0 ||
                            line[j + 1] == '\n') {
                            end = j; break;
                        }
                    }
                }
            } else {
                /* Non-data line — split on any \n */
                for (int j = 0; j < remaining; j++) {
                    if (line[j] == '\n') { end = j; break; }
                }
            }

            if (end < 0) {
                /* Incomplete — shift to front, wait for more data */
                memmove(linebuf, line, remaining);
                lb_len = remaining;
                break;
            }

            char saved = line[end];
            line[end] = 0; /* temporarily terminate */

            if (strncmp(line, "data: ", 6) == 0) {
                char *data = line + 6;

                /* Error detection (both formats) */
                if (strstr(data, "\"error\"")) {
                    char *msg = strstr(data, "\"message\":\"");
                    if (msg) {
                        msg += 11;
                        char *end = strchr(msg, '"');
                        if (end) { *end = 0; printf("\033[31m[error: %s]\033[0m", msg); *end = '"'; }
                    }
                }

                /* Usage stats (both formats use similar keys) */
                char *ui = strstr(data, "\"input_tokens\":");
                if (!ui) ui = strstr(data, "\"prompt_tokens\":");
                if (ui) { while (*ui && (*ui < '0' || *ui > '9')) ui++; tok_in = atoi(ui); }
                char *uo = strstr(data, "\"output_tokens\":");
                if (!uo) uo = strstr(data, "\"completion_tokens\":");
                if (uo) { while (*uo && (*uo < '0' || *uo > '9')) uo++; tok_out = atoi(uo); }

                /* Text extraction — different JSON path per format */
                char *txt = NULL;
                if (g_provider->format == FMT_ANTHROPIC) {
                    txt = strstr(data, "\"text\":\"");
                    if (txt) txt += 8;
                } else {
                    /* OpenAI: "delta":{"content":"..."} */
                    /* Skip reasoning_content (preceded by _) */
                    /* Skip "content":null (thinking phase — no opening quote) */
                    if (strstr(data, "\"content\":null"))  {
                        txt = NULL; /* still thinking */
                    } else {
                        txt = data;
                        while ((txt = strstr(txt, "\"content\":\"")) != NULL) {
                            if (txt > data && *(txt - 1) == '_') {
                                txt += 11; continue;
                            }
                            txt += 11;
                            /* Skip first content token if it duplicates (transition stutter) */
                            if (!seen_content && rp == 0 && g_provider->thinking_param) {
                                seen_content = 1;
                                /* Check if this exact text was already in a previous chunk */
                            }
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                }

                if (txt) {
                    /* Dedup stutter: if thinking model's first content token
                       duplicates what we already have, skip it */
                    if (g_provider->thinking_param && rp > 0 && rp < 64) {
                        char peek[64] = {0};
                        const char *pp = txt;
                        int pi = 0;
                        while (*pp && *pp != '"' && pi < 63) {
                            if (*pp == '\\' && pp[1]) { pp++; peek[pi++] = *pp; }
                            else peek[pi++] = *pp;
                            pp++;
                        }
                        if (pi > 0 && pi <= rp && memcmp(response, peek, pi) == 0) {
                            txt = NULL; /* skip duplicate */
                        }
                    }
                }
                if (txt) {
                    while (*txt && *txt != '"' && rp < MAX_BUF - 1) {
                        if (*txt == '\\' && txt[1]) {
                            txt++;
                            if (*txt == 'n') { response[rp++] = '\n'; putchar('\n'); }
                            else if (*txt == 't') { response[rp++] = '\t'; putchar('\t'); }
                            else { response[rp++] = *txt; putchar(*txt); }
                        } else {
                            response[rp++] = *txt;
                            putchar(*txt);
                        }
                        txt++;
                    }
                    fflush(stdout);
                }
            }
            line[end] = saved; /* restore */
            pos += end + 1; /* advance past the delimiter */
        }
        /* If we consumed everything, reset buffer */
        if (pos >= lb_len) lb_len = 0;
        /* Repetition detection — body kills the mouth.
           Every 256 chars, check if last 64 repeat the prior 64.
           If the model is looping, stop streaming. */
        if (rp > 128 && (rp % 256) < 64) {
            int window = 64;
            if (rp >= window * 2 &&
                memcmp(response + rp - window, response + rp - window * 2, window) == 0) {
                printf("\n\033[90m[repetition detected — stopped]\033[0m");
                break;
            }
        }
    }
    response[rp] = 0;
    free(linebuf);
response_done:
    if (g_debug_fp) {
        fprintf(g_debug_fp, "\n=== RESPONSE BUFFER (%d bytes) ===\n%s\n=== END ===\n", rp, response);
        fclose(g_debug_fp); g_debug_fp = NULL;
    }

    /* Show cost per turn — provider-specific pricing */
    if (tok_in > 0 || tok_out > 0) {
        float cost = (tok_in * g_provider->cost_in + tok_out * g_provider->cost_out) / 1000000.0f;
        if (cost > 0.0001f)
            printf("\n\033[90m[%d in / %d out / $%.4f]\033[0m", tok_in, tok_out, cost);
        else
            printf("\n\033[90m[%d in / %d out]\033[0m", tok_in, tok_out);
    }

    WinHttpCloseHandle(hReq);
    WinHttpCloseHandle(hCon);
    WinHttpCloseHandle(hSes);
    return response;
#else
    free(body);
    return strdup("[POSIX API not implemented — use WinHTTP on Windows]");
#endif
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Body — acts on response automatically
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void body_act(const char *response, Senses *s, const char *user_input) {
    /* Intelligence-gated body. Phase gates action types.
       Intent detection prevents casual input from triggering code.
       Consult actions always allowed (observation, not action). */

    int phase = s->onetwo ? s->onetwo->phase : 0;
    int intent = has_build_intent(user_input);

    /* Phase gate matrix:
       0 EXPLORE: full if intent   1 ONE: observe only
       2 BED: verify (runs, no writes)   3 TWO: full if intent
       4 VER: runs + claims   5 DONE: full */
    int allow_write = (phase != 1) && (phase != 2) && intent;
    int allow_run   = (phase != 1) && intent;

    /* Signal daemon */
    xyzt_signal_query(response);

    /* Detect actions — gated by phase + intent */
    if (allow_write) detect_edits(response);
    ActionQueue q = {0};
    if (allow_write) detect_code_blocks(response, &q);
    if (allow_run)   detect_cmd_blocks(response, &q);
    detect_file_refs(response, &q);      /* always: observation */
    detect_tier_claims(response, &q);    /* always: observation */
    detect_consult(response, &q);        /* always: observation */

    /* File reading sense: model says "reading X" → actually read it */
    detect_read_intent(response, s->pending_result, sizeof(s->pending_result));

    /* Log suppressed actions for transparency */
    if (!allow_write || !allow_run) {
        ActionQueue check = {0};
        if (!allow_write) detect_code_blocks(response, &check);
        if (!allow_run)   detect_cmd_blocks(response, &check);
        if (check.count > 0) {
            const char *reason = !intent ? "casual input" :
                                 phase == 1 ? "phase ONE" :
                                 phase == 2 ? "phase BED" : "gated";
            printf("\033[90m  [gated: %d actions suppressed — %s]\033[0m\n",
                   check.count, reason);
        }
    }

    /* Execute actions */
    for (int i = 0; i < q.count; i++) {
        BodyAction *a = &q.actions[i];
        switch (a->type) {
        case ACT_WRITE_FILE: {
            /* Intelligence check: don't destroy existing files with tiny replacements */
            int new_len = (int)strlen(a->content);
            int existing_len = 0;
            char *existing = xyzt_read_file(a->target, &existing_len);
            free(existing);
            if (existing_len > 0 && new_len < existing_len / 2) {
                printf("\033[90m  skipped %s (new %d < existing %d — not a real edit)\033[0m\n",
                       a->target, new_len, existing_len);
                break;
            }
            if (xyzt_write_file(a->target, a->content, 0) == 0) {
                printf("\033[90m  wrote %s (%d bytes)\033[0m\n",
                       a->target, new_len);
                /* Autonomic: wire connect + fingerprint + signal */
                int fid = wire_add(s->wire, a->target, WNODE_FILE);
                if (fid >= 0) {
                    xyzt_signal_file(a->target, 0.5f);
                    /* Auto-link to current problem */
                    char *problem = s->onetwo ? s->onetwo->problem : NULL;
                    if (problem && problem[0]) {
                        int pid = wire_find(s->wire, problem);
                        if (pid >= 0) wire_connect(s->wire, pid, fid);
                    }
                }
                /* Auto-fingerprint: store file identity for change detection */
                wire_boost(a->target);

                /* Auto-compile: detect build system, then build */
                const char *ext = strrchr(a->target, '.');
                if (ext && (strcmp(ext, ".c") == 0 || strcmp(ext, ".cu") == 0 ||
                            strcmp(ext, ".cpp") == 0)) {
                    char build_cmd[512];
                    int detected = 0;

                    /* Extract directory from target path */
                    char dir[256] = ".";
                    const char *last_slash = strrchr(a->target, '/');
                    if (!last_slash) last_slash = strrchr(a->target, '\\');
                    if (last_slash) {
                        int dlen = (int)(last_slash - a->target);
                        if (dlen > 0 && dlen < 255) {
                            memcpy(dir, a->target, dlen);
                            dir[dlen] = 0;
                        }
                    }

                    /* Priority 1: Makefile in same directory */
                    {
                        char mpath[512];
                        snprintf(mpath, sizeof(mpath), "%s/Makefile", dir);
                        FILE *mf = fopen(mpath, "r");
                        if (!mf) { snprintf(mpath, sizeof(mpath), "%s/makefile", dir); mf = fopen(mpath, "r"); }
                        if (mf) {
                            fclose(mf);
                            if (strcmp(dir, ".") == 0)
                                snprintf(build_cmd, sizeof(build_cmd), "make 2>&1");
                            else
                                snprintf(build_cmd, sizeof(build_cmd), "make -C %s 2>&1", dir);
                            detected = 1;
                        }
                    }

                    /* Priority 2: build.bat in same directory */
                    if (!detected) {
                        char bpath[512];
                        snprintf(bpath, sizeof(bpath), "%s/build.bat", dir);
                        FILE *bf = fopen(bpath, "r");
                        if (bf) {
                            fclose(bf);
                            if (strcmp(dir, ".") == 0)
                                snprintf(build_cmd, sizeof(build_cmd), "build.bat 2>&1");
                            else
                                snprintf(build_cmd, sizeof(build_cmd), "cd %s && build.bat 2>&1", dir);
                            detected = 1;
                        }
                    }

                    /* Priority 3: infer from extension */
                    if (!detected) {
                        if (strcmp(ext, ".cu") == 0) {
                            snprintf(build_cmd, sizeof(build_cmd),
                                     "nvcc -O2 -o %.*s.exe %s 2>&1",
                                     (int)(ext - a->target), a->target, a->target);
                        } else if (strcmp(ext, ".cpp") == 0) {
                            snprintf(build_cmd, sizeof(build_cmd),
                                     "g++ -O2 -o %.*s.exe %s 2>&1",
                                     (int)(ext - a->target), a->target, a->target);
                        } else {
                            /* .c — check for xyzt libs */
                            int needs_winhttp = (strstr(a->target, "xyzt") != NULL);
                            snprintf(build_cmd, sizeof(build_cmd),
                                     "gcc -O2 -o %.*s.exe %s%s 2>&1",
                                     (int)(ext - a->target), a->target,
                                     a->target,
                                     needs_winhttp ? " -lwinhttp" : "");
                        }
                    }

                    int exit_code = 0;
                    char *output = xyzt_run_cmd(build_cmd, &exit_code);
                    g_build_total++;
                    if (exit_code == 0) {
                        printf("\033[90m  built %s\033[0m\n", a->target);
                        int nid = wire_find(s->wire, a->target);
                        if (nid >= 0) wire_adjust(s->wire, nid, 1);
                        g_build_pass++;
                        g_build_fails = 0;  /* reset consecutive failures */

                        /* Test awareness: after successful build, find and run tests */
                        {
                            char test_cmd[1024], test_desc[128];
                            if (detect_test_for_file(a->target, test_cmd, sizeof(test_cmd),
                                                     test_desc, sizeof(test_desc))) {
                                printf("\033[90m  testing: %s\033[0m\n", test_desc);
                                int test_exit = 0;
                                char *test_out = xyzt_run_cmd(test_cmd, &test_exit);
                                if (test_exit == 0) {
                                    printf("\033[90m  tests passed\033[0m\n");
                                    if (nid >= 0) wire_adjust(s->wire, nid, 1);
                                } else {
                                    printf("\033[31m  tests failed:\033[0m\n%.300s\n",
                                           test_out ? test_out : "");
                                    /* Feed test failure into auto-continue */
                                    snprintf(s->pending_result, sizeof(s->pending_result),
                                             "[test failed: %s]\n%.500s",
                                             test_desc, test_out ? test_out : "");
                                    if (nid >= 0) wire_adjust(s->wire, nid, 0);
                                }
                                free(test_out);
                            }
                        }

                        /* Multi-file: scan for missing #includes → queue follow-up */
                        {
                            char *missing = scan_missing_includes(a->target, a->content);
                            if (missing) {
                                int plen = (int)strlen(s->pending_result);
                                snprintf(s->pending_result + plen,
                                         sizeof(s->pending_result) - plen,
                                         "\n%s wrote %s which includes files that don't exist: %s. Write them.",
                                         a->target, a->target, missing);
                                printf("\033[90m  %s\033[0m\n", missing);
                                free(missing);
                            }
                        }
                    } else {
                        printf("\033[31m  build failed:\033[0m\n%.300s\n",
                               output ? output : "");
                        int nid = wire_find(s->wire, a->target);
                        if (nid >= 0) wire_adjust(s->wire, nid, 0);
                        g_build_fails++;  /* consecutive failures → triggers escalation */
                        /* Store error in pending_result — survives senses_refresh */
                        snprintf(s->pending_result, sizeof(s->pending_result),
                                 "[build error: %s]\n%.500s",
                                 a->target, output ? output : "");
                    }
                    free(output);
                }
            }
            } break;

        case ACT_PRELOAD_FILE:
            /* Queue for senses_refresh — next turn will have this context */
            if (s->n_preload < 16) {
                strncpy(s->preload[s->n_preload], a->target, 255);
                s->n_preload++;
            }
            /* Wire: touch the node */
            {
                int fid = wire_find(s->wire, a->target);
                if (fid < 0) fid = wire_add(s->wire, a->target, WNODE_FILE);
                xyzt_signal_file(a->target, 0.3f);
            }
            break;

        case ACT_TIER_CLAIM:
            /* Write claim to onetwo session + signal daemon */
            ot_add_claim(a->target, a->tier);
            xyzt_signal(4, a->target, (float)a->tier);
            printf("\033[90m  T%d claim: %.60s\033[0m\n", a->tier, a->target);
            break;

        case ACT_RUN_CMD:
            /* Run command, learn result into wire — crystallize or soften */
            {
                int exit_code = 0;
                char *output = xyzt_run_cmd(a->target, &exit_code);
                int pass = (exit_code == 0);
                /* Windows cmd.exe quirk: chained commands may return
                   non-zero even on success. Check output for clues. */
                if (!pass && output &&
                    (strstr(output, "BUILD OK") || strstr(output, "Hello") ||
                     strstr(output, "PASS") || strstr(output, "0 error")))
                    pass = 1;
                printf("\033[90m  ran: %s → %s\033[0m\n", a->target,
                       pass ? "pass" : "fail");
                if (output && output[0] && !pass)
                    printf("\033[90m  out: %.120s\033[0m\n", output);

                /* Crystallization: pass → harden edges, fail → soften */
                char *problem = s->onetwo ? s->onetwo->problem : "unknown";
                if (problem[0]) {
                    int pid = wire_find(s->wire, problem);
                    if (pid >= 0) {
                        wire_adjust(s->wire, pid, pass);
                        /* Pass: lower plasticity (crystallize) */
                        /* Fail: raise plasticity (soften for relearning) */
                        if (pass) {
                            s->wire->nodes[pid].plasticity *= (1.0f - WIRE_PLASTICITY_COOL);
                            if (s->wire->nodes[pid].plasticity < WIRE_PLASTICITY_MIN)
                                s->wire->nodes[pid].plasticity = WIRE_PLASTICITY_MIN;
                        } else {
                            s->wire->nodes[pid].plasticity *= (1.0f + WIRE_PLASTICITY_HEAT);
                            if (s->wire->nodes[pid].plasticity > WIRE_PLASTICITY_MAX)
                                s->wire->nodes[pid].plasticity = WIRE_PLASTICITY_MAX;
                        }
                    }
                }
                /* Autonomic: parse output for file references → wire learn */
                if (output) {
                    const char *exts[] = {".c", ".h", ".cu", NULL};
                    for (const char **ext = exts; *ext; ext++) {
                        const char *fp = output;
                        while ((fp = strstr(fp, *ext)) != NULL) {
                            /* Walk back to find filename:line pattern */
                            const char *start = fp;
                            while (start > output && start[-1] != ' ' && start[-1] != '\n')
                                start--;
                            int flen = (int)(fp + strlen(*ext) - start);
                            if (flen > 2 && flen < 128) {
                                char fname[128] = {0};
                                memcpy(fname, start, flen);
                                int fid = wire_add(s->wire, fname, WNODE_FILE);
                                if (fid >= 0) {
                                    char *prob = s->onetwo ? s->onetwo->problem : NULL;
                                    if (prob && prob[0]) {
                                        int pid = wire_find(s->wire, prob);
                                        if (pid >= 0) wire_connect(s->wire, pid, fid);
                                    }
                                    wire_adjust(s->wire, fid, pass);
                                }
                            }
                            fp += strlen(*ext);
                        }
                    }
                    /* Queue output for next turn context */
                    if (s->n_preload < 16) {
                        snprintf(s->preload[s->n_preload], 255,
                                 "R %d %.200s", exit_code, output);
                        s->n_preload++;
                    }
                }
                free(output);
            }
            break;

        case ACT_WIRE_CONNECT:
            break; /* future */

        case ACT_CONSULT: {
            /* Multi-model consult: temporarily switch provider, call, restore */
            const Provider *orig_provider = g_provider;
            char orig_key[1024]; strncpy(orig_key, g_key, 1023);
            char orig_model[128]; strncpy(orig_model, g_model, 127);

            /* Find target provider */
            int found = 0;
            for (const Provider *pr = PROVIDERS; pr->name; pr++) {
                if (strcmp(a->target, pr->name) == 0) {
                    g_provider = pr;
                    strncpy(g_model, pr->default_model, 127);
                    load_key();
                    found = 1;
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (!found || !g_key[0]) {
                printf("\033[90m  consult @%s: provider not found or no key\033[0m\n",
                       a->target);
                /* Restore */
                g_provider = orig_provider;
                strncpy(g_key, orig_key, 1023);
                strncpy(g_model, orig_model, 127);
                break;
            }

            printf("\033[35m  [@%s]\033[0m ", a->target);
            fflush(stdout);

            /* Build minimal system prompt for consult target — density-adapted */
            char consult_sys[2048];
            if (g_provider->density <= DENSITY_HIGH)
                snprintf(consult_sys, sizeof(consult_sys),
                    "CC consult. Answer directly. Dense shorthand. No files. No code unless asked.");
            else
                snprintf(consult_sys, sizeof(consult_sys),
                    "You are being consulted by another AI assistant. "
                    "Answer the question directly and concisely. "
                    "Do not write code unless asked. Do not create files.");

            Msg consult_msgs[1];
            consult_msgs[0].role = "user";
            consult_msgs[0].content = a->content;

            char *consult_response = api_call(consult_sys, consult_msgs, 1);

            /* Feed consult result into preloaded context for next turn */
            if (consult_response && consult_response[0] && s->n_preload < 16) {
                snprintf(s->preload[s->n_preload], 255,
                         "[@%s] %.200s", a->target, consult_response);
                s->n_preload++;
            }
            free(consult_response);

            /* Restore original provider */
            g_provider = orig_provider;
            strncpy(g_key, orig_key, 1023);
            strncpy(g_model, orig_model, 127);
            printf("\n");
            } break;
        }
    }

    /* Auto-advance ONETWO phase based on what the model did */
    if (s->onetwo && s->onetwo->problem[0]) {
        int cur = s->onetwo->phase;
        int new_phase = cur;

        /* Scan what actions were executed this turn */
        int wrote_code = 0, ran_test = 0, made_claim = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < q.count; i++) {
            if (q.actions[i].type == ACT_WRITE_FILE) wrote_code = 1;
            if (q.actions[i].type == ACT_RUN_CMD) {
                /* Distinguish test commands from general commands */
                const char *cmd = q.actions[i].target;
                if (cmd && (strstr(cmd, "test") || strstr(cmd, "check") ||
                            strstr(cmd, "verify") || strstr(cmd, "assert")))
                    ran_test = 1;
            }
            if (q.actions[i].type == ACT_TIER_CLAIM) made_claim = 1;
        }

        /* Phase advancement — only forward, never backward
           0 EXPLORE → 1 ONE (decomposing)
           1 ONE → 3 TWO (started coding)
           3 TWO → 4 VER (running tests)
           4 VER → 5 DONE (all claims verified) */
        if (wrote_code && cur < 3) new_phase = 3;          /* → TWO */
        if (ran_test && cur < 4) new_phase = 4;             /* → VER */
        if (made_claim && cur >= 4) new_phase = 4;          /* stay VER */

        if (new_phase != cur) {
            s->onetwo->phase = (uint8_t)new_phase;
            ot_save(s->onetwo);
            static const char *PH[] = {"EXPLORE","ONE","BED","TWO","VER","DONE"};
            printf("\033[90m  phase → %s\033[0m\n", PH[new_phase]);
        }
    }

    /* Free action queue content allocations */
    action_queue_free(&q);

    /* Save wire changes from this turn */
    wire_save(s->wire);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   CLI front end — input history, tab complete, Ctrl+C
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define HIST_MAX 32
static char g_history[HIST_MAX][MAX_BUF];
static int g_hist_count = 0;

static void hist_add(const char *line) {
    if (!line[0]) return;
    /* Don't duplicate last entry */
    if (g_hist_count > 0 && strcmp(g_history[g_hist_count - 1], line) == 0) return;
    if (g_hist_count >= HIST_MAX) {
        memmove(g_history[0], g_history[1], sizeof(g_history[0]) * (HIST_MAX - 1));
        g_hist_count = HIST_MAX - 1;
    }
    strncpy(g_history[g_hist_count++], line, MAX_BUF - 1);
}

static const char *SLASH_CMDS[] = {
    "/providers", "/use ", "/models", "/model ", "/key ",
    "/context", "/wire", "/clear", "/quit", "/help",
    /* Provider shortcuts */
    "/anthropic", "/gemini", "/groq", "/qwen", "/openai",
    "/openrouter", "/ollama",
    NULL
};

/* Find best ghost completion for current input */
static const char *find_ghost(const char *buf, int len) {
    if (len == 0 || buf[0] != '/') return NULL;
    /* Match slash commands */
    for (const char **cmd = SLASH_CMDS; *cmd; cmd++)
        if (strncmp(buf, *cmd, len) == 0 && (int)strlen(*cmd) > len)
            return *cmd + len; /* return only the ghost part */
    /* Match provider names after /use */
    if (strncmp(buf, "/use ", 5) == 0 && len > 5) {
        const char *partial = buf + 5;
        int plen = len - 5;
        for (const Provider *pr = PROVIDERS; pr->name; pr++)
            if (strncmp(partial, pr->name, plen) == 0 && (int)strlen(pr->name) > plen)
                return pr->name + plen;
    }
    return NULL;
}

/* Clear ghost text from display */
static void clear_ghost(int ghost_len) {
    (void)ghost_len;
    printf("\033[K"); /* ANSI clear to end of line */
    fflush(stdout);
}

/* Render ghost text in gray after cursor */
static int show_ghost(const char *buf, int len) {
    const char *ghost = find_ghost(buf, len);
    if (!ghost || !ghost[0]) return 0;
    int glen = (int)strlen(ghost);
    printf("\033[90m%s\033[0m", ghost); /* gray */
    for (int i = 0; i < glen; i++) printf("\b"); /* move cursor back */
    fflush(stdout);
    return glen;
}

/* Redraw line: use ANSI escape to clear and rewrite cleanly */
static int redraw_line(const char *buf, int len, int pos, int prev_ghost) {
    /* Move to column 0 of input area, clear to end of line */
    printf("\r\033[36myou>\033[0m %s", buf);
    /* Show ghost */
    int glen = show_ghost(buf, len);
    /* Clear anything leftover after ghost */
    printf("\033[K");
    /* Move cursor to correct position: prompt is 5 chars ("you> ") */
    printf("\r\033[%dC", 5 + pos);
    fflush(stdout);
    return glen;
}

/* Read a line with history, ghost completion, tab accept */
static int cli_readline(const char *prompt, char *buf, int max) {
    HANDLE hIn = GetStdHandle(STD_INPUT_HANDLE);
    DWORD old_mode;
    if (!GetConsoleMode(hIn, &old_mode)) {
        /* Not a console (piped input) — fall back to fgets */
        printf("%s", prompt); fflush(stdout);
        if (!fgets(buf, max, stdin)) return -1;
        int n = (int)strlen(buf);
        while (n > 0 && (buf[n-1] == '\n' || buf[n-1] == '\r')) buf[--n] = 0;
        hist_add(buf);
        return n;
    }
    SetConsoleMode(hIn, ENABLE_WINDOW_INPUT | ENABLE_MOUSE_INPUT);

    printf("%s", prompt);
    fflush(stdout);

    int pos = 0, len = 0, ghost_len = 0;
    int hist_idx = g_hist_count;
    char saved[MAX_BUF] = {0};
    buf[0] = 0;

    while (1) {
        INPUT_RECORD rec;
        DWORD nread;
        ReadConsoleInputA(hIn, &rec, 1, &nread);
        if (rec.EventType != KEY_EVENT || !rec.Event.KeyEvent.bKeyDown) continue;

        WORD vk = rec.Event.KeyEvent.wVirtualKeyCode;
        char ch = rec.Event.KeyEvent.uChar.AsciiChar;

        if (vk == VK_RETURN) {
            clear_ghost(ghost_len);
            buf[len] = 0;
            printf("\n");
            break;
        }
        else if (vk == VK_BACK) {
            if (pos > 0) {
                memmove(buf + pos - 1, buf + pos, len - pos);
                pos--; len--;
                buf[len] = 0;
                ghost_len = redraw_line(buf, len, pos, ghost_len);
            }
        }
        else if (vk == VK_LEFT) {
            if (pos > 0) { pos--; printf("\b"); fflush(stdout); }
        }
        else if (vk == VK_RIGHT) {
            /* Right arrow: if at end and ghost exists, accept ghost */
            if (pos == len && ghost_len > 0) {
                const char *ghost = find_ghost(buf, len);
                if (ghost) {
                    clear_ghost(ghost_len);
                    int glen = (int)strlen(ghost);
                    memcpy(buf + len, ghost, glen);
                    len += glen; pos = len;
                    buf[len] = 0;
                    ghost_len = redraw_line(buf, len, pos, 0);
                }
            } else if (pos < len) {
                putchar(buf[pos]); pos++; fflush(stdout);
            }
        }
        else if (vk == VK_UP) {
            if (hist_idx > 0) {
                if (hist_idx == g_hist_count) strncpy(saved, buf, MAX_BUF - 1);
                hist_idx--;
                strncpy(buf, g_history[hist_idx], max - 1);
                len = pos = (int)strlen(buf);
                ghost_len = redraw_line(buf, len, pos, ghost_len);
            }
        }
        else if (vk == VK_DOWN) {
            if (hist_idx < g_hist_count) {
                hist_idx++;
                if (hist_idx == g_hist_count)
                    strncpy(buf, saved, max - 1);
                else
                    strncpy(buf, g_history[hist_idx], max - 1);
                len = pos = (int)strlen(buf);
                ghost_len = redraw_line(buf, len, pos, ghost_len);
            }
        }
        else if (vk == VK_TAB) {
            /* Tab: accept ghost completion */
            const char *ghost = find_ghost(buf, len);
            if (ghost && ghost[0]) {
                clear_ghost(ghost_len);
                int glen = (int)strlen(ghost);
                memcpy(buf + len, ghost, glen);
                len += glen; pos = len;
                buf[len] = 0;
                ghost_len = redraw_line(buf, len, pos, 0);
            }
        }
        else if (vk == VK_ESCAPE) {
            buf[0] = 0; len = pos = 0; ghost_len = 0;
            ghost_len = redraw_line(buf, len, pos, ghost_len);
        }
        else if (ch == 3) {
            SetConsoleMode(hIn, old_mode);
            printf("\n");
            return -1;
        }
        else if (ch >= 32 && ch < 127 && len < max - 1) {
            memmove(buf + pos + 1, buf + pos, len - pos);
            buf[pos] = ch;
            len++; pos++;
            buf[len] = 0;
            ghost_len = redraw_line(buf, len, pos, ghost_len);
        }
    }

    SetConsoleMode(hIn, old_mode);
    hist_add(buf);
    return len;
}

static volatile int g_running = 1;
static BOOL WINAPI ctrl_handler(DWORD type) {
    if (type == CTRL_C_EVENT) { g_running = 0; return TRUE; }
    return FALSE;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main — the loop of being
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    /* Enable ANSI, UTF-8, Ctrl+C handler */
    HANDLE hOut = GetStdHandle(STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE);
    DWORD dw_mode; GetConsoleMode(hOut, &dw_mode);
    SetConsoleMode(hOut, dw_mode | ENABLE_VIRTUAL_TERMINAL_PROCESSING);
    SetConsoleOutputCP(65001);
    SetConsoleCtrlHandler(ctrl_handler, TRUE);

    printf("\033[33m");
    printf("  ╔═══════════════════════╗\n");
    printf("  ║  X Y Z T  terminal   ║\n");
    printf("  ╚═══════════════════════╝\033[0m\n\n");

    /* Provider + model from argv FIRST, then load creds */
    for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
        if (strcmp(argv[i], "--provider") == 0) {
            for (const Provider *pr = PROVIDERS; pr->name; pr++) {
                if (strcmp(argv[i+1], pr->name) == 0) {
                    g_provider = pr;
                    strncpy(g_model, pr->default_model, 127);
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        if (strcmp(argv[i], "--model") == 0)
            strncpy(g_model, argv[i+1], 127);
        if (strcmp(argv[i], "--debug") == 0)
            g_debug = 1;
    }
    const char *env_model = getenv("XYZT_MODEL");
    if (env_model && env_model[0] && strcmp(g_model, MODEL_DEFAULT) == 0)
        strncpy(g_model, env_model, 127);

    /* Single-shot: collect non-flag args as the prompt */
    char oneshot[MAX_BUF] = {0};
    {
        int op = 0;
        for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
            if (strcmp(argv[i], "--provider") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--model") == 0)
                { i++; continue; } /* skip flag + value */
            if (strcmp(argv[i], "--debug") == 0) continue; /* standalone flag */
            if (argv[i][0] == '-') continue; /* skip unknown flags */
            if (op > 0) oneshot[op++] = ' ';
            int alen = (int)strlen(argv[i]);
            if (op + alen < MAX_BUF - 1) {
                memcpy(oneshot + op, argv[i], alen);
                op += alen;
            }
        }
    }

    load_key();
    if (!g_key[0] && g_provider->cred_file) {
        printf("No credentials for %s. Use /key to set one.\n", g_provider->name);
    }

    /* Load senses */
    Senses senses;
    senses_load(&senses);

    /* Boot display */
    printf("\033[90m%s@%s | %dn %de wire | %s\033[0m\n\n",
           g_model, g_provider->name,
           senses.wire->n_nodes, senses.wire->n_edges, senses.resume);

    /* Startup resume injection — models never start cold.
       Run xyzt_resume quick and seed preloaded_context so the
       first system prompt includes full session context. */
    {
        int rc = 0;
        char *resume_out = xyzt_run_cmd("xyzt_resume quick 2>&1", &rc);
        if (resume_out && resume_out[0]) {
            int rlen = (int)strlen(resume_out);
            if (rlen > (int)sizeof(senses.preloaded_context) - 1)
                rlen = (int)sizeof(senses.preloaded_context) - 1;
            memcpy(senses.preloaded_context, resume_out, rlen);
            senses.preloaded_context[rlen] = 0;
            printf("\033[90mresumed into context (%d bytes)\033[0m\n", rlen);
        }
        free(resume_out);
    }

    /* Conversation — load previous session (REPL only, not single-shot) */
    Msg msgs[MAX_CONV];
    int n_msgs = 0;
    if (!oneshot[0]) {
        FILE *sf = fopen(".xyzt/conversation.bin", "rb");
        if (sf) {
            uint32_t saved_n = 0;
            fread(&saved_n, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, sf);
            for (uint32_t i = 0; i < saved_n && i < MAX_CONV; i++) {
                uint8_t is_assistant = 0;
                uint32_t clen = 0;
                fread(&is_assistant, 1, 1, sf);
                fread(&clen, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, sf);
                if (clen > MAX_BUF) break;
                char *content = (char *)calloc(clen + 1, 1);
                fread(content, 1, clen, sf);
                msgs[n_msgs].role = is_assistant ? "assistant" : "user";
                msgs[n_msgs].content = content;
                n_msgs++;
            }
            fclose(sf);
            printf("\033[90mresumed %d messages\033[0m\n", n_msgs);
        }
    } /* end if (!oneshot) */

    char input[MAX_BUF];
    char sys_prompt[MAX_BUF];

    /* ═══ Single-shot mode: xyzt "prompt here" ═══ */
    if (oneshot[0]) {
        if (!g_key[0] && g_provider->cred_file) {
            printf("\033[31mNo key for %s. Use: xyzt --provider ollama-local \"prompt\"\033[0m\n",
                   g_provider->name);
            senses_free(&senses);
            return 1;
        }

        route_model(&senses, oneshot);
        build_system(&senses, oneshot, sys_prompt, MAX_BUF);
        msgs[n_msgs].role = "user";
        msgs[n_msgs].content = strdup(oneshot);
        n_msgs++;

        printf("\033[33mcc>\033[0m ");
        fflush(stdout);

        char *response = api_call(sys_prompt, msgs, n_msgs);
        if (response && response[0]) {
            msgs[n_msgs].role = "assistant";
            msgs[n_msgs].content = response;
            n_msgs++;
            body_act(response, &senses, oneshot);
            senses_refresh(&senses);

            /* Append pending results */
            if (senses.pending_result[0]) {
                int plen = (int)strlen(senses.preloaded_context);
                snprintf(senses.preloaded_context + plen,
                         sizeof(senses.preloaded_context) - plen,
                         "\n%s", senses.pending_result);
                senses.pending_result[0] = 0;
            }

            /* Auto-continue loop — same as REPL */
            int continues = 0;
            char prev_ctx[4096] = {0};
            while (senses.preloaded_context[0] && continues < 5) {
                int has_result = (strncmp(senses.preloaded_context, "R ", 2) == 0 ||
                                  strstr(senses.preloaded_context, "\nR ") ||
                                  strncmp(senses.preloaded_context, "[@", 2) == 0 ||
                                  strstr(senses.preloaded_context, "\n[@") ||
                                  strstr(senses.preloaded_context, "[build error:"));
                if (!has_result) break;
                /* Convergence: same error twice = not converging, stop */
                if (prev_ctx[0] && strcmp(prev_ctx, senses.preloaded_context) == 0) {
                    printf("\033[90m[same error repeated — not converging, stopped]\033[0m\n");
                    break;
                }
                strncpy(prev_ctx, senses.preloaded_context, sizeof(prev_ctx) - 1);
                continues++;
                printf("\n\033[90m[auto-continue %d]\033[0m\n", continues);
                build_system(&senses, oneshot, sys_prompt, MAX_BUF);
                char result_msg[4096];
                snprintf(result_msg, sizeof(result_msg),
                    "[command output]\n%s", senses.preloaded_context);
                msgs[n_msgs].role = "user";
                msgs[n_msgs].content = strdup(result_msg);
                n_msgs++;
                printf("\033[33mcc>\033[0m ");
                fflush(stdout);
                char *cont = api_call(sys_prompt, msgs, n_msgs);
                if (cont && cont[0]) {
                    msgs[n_msgs].role = "assistant";
                    msgs[n_msgs].content = cont;
                    n_msgs++;
                    body_act(cont, &senses, oneshot);
                    senses_refresh(&senses);
                } else { free(cont); break; }
            }
        } else {
            printf("\033[31m[no response]\033[0m\n");
            free(response);
        }
        printf("\n");

        /* Save wire + free */
        wire_save(senses.wire);
        for (int i = 0; i < n_msgs; i++) free(msgs[i].content);
        senses_free(&senses);
        return 0;
    }

    /* ═══ Interactive REPL ═══ */
    while (g_running) {
        int len = cli_readline("\033[36myou>\033[0m ", input, MAX_BUF);
        if (len < 0) break;   /* Ctrl+C */
        if (!len) continue;
        if (strcmp(input, "quit") == 0 || strcmp(input, "/quit") == 0) break;

        /* ═══ Slash commands — local, never hit the API ═══ */
        if (input[0] == '/') {
            if (strcmp(input, "/providers") == 0) {
                int idx = 0;
                for (const Provider *pr = PROVIDERS; pr->name; pr++, idx++) {
                    const char *status = "no key";
                    if (!pr->cred_file) status = pr->port == 11434 ? "local" : "open";
                    else {
                        char cp[512];
                        const char *h = getenv("USERPROFILE");
                        if (h) { snprintf(cp, 512, "%s/%s", h, pr->cred_file);
                            FILE *tf = fopen(cp, "r");
                            if (tf) { fclose(tf); status = "ready"; }
                        }
                    }
                    /* Also check saved per-provider key */
                    {
                        char kp[256];
                        snprintf(kp, sizeof(kp), ".xyzt/key_%s", pr->name);
                        FILE *skf = fopen(kp, "r");
                        if (skf) { fclose(skf); status = "saved"; }
                    }
                    printf("  \033[36m%d\033[0m  %-12s %-24s [%s]%s\n",
                           idx + 1, pr->name, pr->default_model, status,
                           pr == g_provider ? " \033[33m<<<\033[0m" : "");
                }
                printf("\033[90mType /use <name> or number to switch\033[0m\n");
            }
            else if (strncmp(input, "/use ", 5) == 0) {
                const char *name = input + 5;
                int found = 0;
                /* Accept number or name */
                int num = atoi(name);
                if (num >= 1) {
                    int idx = 0;
                    for (const Provider *pr = PROVIDERS; pr->name; pr++, idx++) {
                        if (idx + 1 == num) {
                            g_provider = pr;
                            strncpy(g_model, pr->default_model, 127);
                            load_key();
                            printf("\033[90mswitched to %s@%s%s\033[0m\n", g_model, pr->name,
                                   g_key[0] ? "" : " [no key — use /key]");
                            found = 1; break;
                        }
                    }
                } else {
                    for (const Provider *pr = PROVIDERS; pr->name; pr++) {
                        if (strcmp(name, pr->name) == 0) {
                            g_provider = pr;
                            strncpy(g_model, pr->default_model, 127);
                            load_key();
                            printf("\033[90mswitched to %s@%s%s\033[0m\n", g_model, pr->name,
                                   g_key[0] ? "" : " [no key — use /key]");
                            found = 1; break;
                        }
                    }
                }
                if (!found) printf("Unknown provider. Try /providers\n");
            }
            else if (strncmp(input, "/model ", 7) == 0) {
                strncpy(g_model, input + 7, 127);
                printf("\033[90mmodel: %s\033[0m\n", g_model);
            }
            else if (strncmp(input, "/key ", 5) == 0) {
                strncpy(g_key, input + 5, 1023);
                /* Persist per-provider: .xyzt/key_<provider> */
                char kpath[256];
                snprintf(kpath, sizeof(kpath), ".xyzt/key_%s", g_provider->name);
                FILE *kf = fopen(kpath, "w");
                if (kf) { fputs(g_key, kf); fclose(kf); }
                printf("\033[90mkey set + saved for %s\033[0m\n", g_provider->name);
            }
            else if (strcmp(input, "/context") == 0) {
                /* Show what the model sees — build a preview system prompt */
                char preview[MAX_BUF];
                build_system(&senses, "(preview)", preview, MAX_BUF);
                printf("\033[90m--- system prompt (%d bytes) ---\033[0m\n", (int)strlen(preview));
                printf("%s", preview);
                printf("\033[90m--- end ---\033[0m\n");
                printf("\033[90mmodel: %s@%s | msgs: %d | key: %s\033[0m\n",
                       g_model, g_provider->name, n_msgs,
                       g_key[0] ? "yes" : "none");
            }
            else if (strcmp(input, "/wire") == 0) {
                printf("\033[90m%d nodes, %d edges, %d learns\033[0m\n",
                       senses.wire->n_nodes, senses.wire->n_edges,
                       senses.wire->total_learns);
            }
            else if (strcmp(input, "/clear") == 0) {
                for (int i = 0; i < n_msgs; i++) free(msgs[i].content);
                n_msgs = 0;
                printf("\033[90mconversation cleared\033[0m\n");
            }
            else if (strcmp(input, "/models") == 0) {
                printf("\033[90m%s models:\033[0m\n", g_provider->name);
                /* Ollama-local: query live installed models */
                if (strcmp(g_provider->name, "ollama-local") == 0) {
                    int rc = 0;
                    char *out = xyzt_run_cmd("curl -s http://localhost:11434/api/tags 2>&1", &rc);
                    if (out && rc == 0) {
                        /* Parse {"models":[{"name":"..."},...]} */
                        char *p = out;
                        int count = 0;
                        while ((p = strstr(p, "\"name\":\"")) != NULL) {
                            p += 8;
                            char *end = strchr(p, '"');
                            if (end && (end - p) < 64) {
                                printf("  %.*s%s\n", (int)(end - p), p,
                                       strncmp(p, g_model, end - p) == 0 ? " \033[33m<<<\033[0m" : "");
                                count++;
                            }
                            p = end ? end + 1 : p + 1;
                        }
                        if (!count) printf("  (no models installed — ollama pull <model>)\n");
                    } else {
                        printf("  (ollama not running on :11434)\n");
                    }
                    free(out);
                } else {
                    /* Show current + default — use /model to change */
                    printf("  current: %s\n", g_model);
                    if (strcmp(g_model, g_provider->default_model) != 0)
                        printf("  default: %s\n", g_provider->default_model);
                    printf("  \033[90muse /model <name> to switch\033[0m\n");
                }
            }
            else if (strcmp(input, "/help") == 0) {
                printf("  /providers   — list providers + status\n");
                printf("  /use <name> — switch provider\n");
                printf("  /models      — list models\n");
                printf("  /model <m>  — switch model\n");
                printf("  /key <key>   — set + save API key\n");
                printf("  /context    — show what the model sees\n");
                printf("  /wire       — wire graph stats\n");
                printf("  /clear       — clear conversation\n");
                printf("  /quit       — exit\n");
                printf("  /<provider>  — shortcut: /qwen = /use qwen\n");
                printf("\n  \033[90mMulti-model: model writes @provider: prompt\n");
                printf("  to consult another model. Result fed back.\033[0m\n");
            }
            else {
                /* Try as provider shortcut: /qwen = /use qwen */
                const char *shortcut = input + 1;
                int found = 0;
                for (const Provider *pr = PROVIDERS; pr->name; pr++) {
                    if (strcmp(shortcut, pr->name) == 0) {
                        g_provider = pr;
                        strncpy(g_model, pr->default_model, 127);
                        load_key();
                        printf("\033[90mswitched to %s@%s%s\033[0m\n", g_model, pr->name,
                               g_key[0] ? "" : " [no key — use /key]");
                        found = 1; break;
                    }
                }
                if (!found) printf("Unknown command. Try /help\n");
            }
            printf("\n");
            continue;
        }

        /* No key? Can't call API */
        if (!g_key[0] && g_provider->cred_file) {
            printf("\033[33mNo key for %s. Use /key <your-key> or /use <provider>\033[0m\n\n",
                   g_provider->name);
            continue;
        }

        /* Route model, then build system prompt — body picks the brain */
        route_model(&senses, input);
        build_system(&senses, input, sys_prompt, MAX_BUF);

        /* Add user message */
        msgs[n_msgs].role = "user";
        msgs[n_msgs].content = strdup(input);
        n_msgs++;

        /* Signal daemon with user input */
        xyzt_signal_query(input);

        printf("\n\033[33mcc>\033[0m ");
        fflush(stdout);

        /* One API call. Senses loaded. Context selected. */
        char *response = api_call(sys_prompt, msgs, n_msgs);

        if (response && response[0]) {
            /* Add to conversation */
            msgs[n_msgs].role = "assistant";
            msgs[n_msgs].content = response;
            n_msgs++;

            /* Body acts automatically — gated by phase + intent */
            body_act(response, &senses, input);

            /* Refresh senses for next turn */
            senses_refresh(&senses);

            /* Append any pending results from body_act (e.g. build errors) */
            if (senses.pending_result[0]) {
                int plen = (int)strlen(senses.preloaded_context);
                snprintf(senses.preloaded_context + plen,
                         sizeof(senses.preloaded_context) - plen,
                         "\n%s", senses.pending_result);
                senses.pending_result[0] = 0;  /* consumed */
            }

            /* Auto-continue: if commands ran, builds failed, or consults returned,
               feed results back so the model can keep working.
               Max 5 continues for build-fix cycles. */
            int continues = 0;
            char prev_ctx[4096] = {0};
            while (senses.preloaded_context[0] && continues < 5) {
                /* Check if there's actionable output */
                int has_result = (strncmp(senses.preloaded_context, "R ", 2) == 0 ||
                                  strstr(senses.preloaded_context, "\nR ") ||
                                  strncmp(senses.preloaded_context, "[@", 2) == 0 ||
                                  strstr(senses.preloaded_context, "\n[@") ||
                                  strstr(senses.preloaded_context, "[build error:"));
                if (!has_result) break;

                /* Convergence: same error twice = not converging, stop */
                if (prev_ctx[0] && strcmp(prev_ctx, senses.preloaded_context) == 0) {
                    printf("\033[90m[same error repeated — not converging, stopped]\033[0m\n");
                    break;
                }
                strncpy(prev_ctx, senses.preloaded_context, sizeof(prev_ctx) - 1);

                continues++;
                printf("\033[90m[auto-continue %d — feeding results back]\033[0m\n",
                       continues);

                /* Build fresh system prompt with results in context */
                build_system(&senses, input, sys_prompt, MAX_BUF);

                /* Inject results as a user message */
                char result_msg[4096];
                if (strstr(senses.preloaded_context, "[@"))
                    snprintf(result_msg, sizeof(result_msg),
                        "[consult result]\n%s", senses.preloaded_context);
                else
                    snprintf(result_msg, sizeof(result_msg),
                        "[command output]\n%s", senses.preloaded_context);
                msgs[n_msgs].role = "user";
                msgs[n_msgs].content = strdup(result_msg);
                n_msgs++;

                printf("\033[33mcc>\033[0m ");
                fflush(stdout);

                char *cont_response = api_call(sys_prompt, msgs, n_msgs);
                if (cont_response && cont_response[0]) {
                    msgs[n_msgs].role = "assistant";
                    msgs[n_msgs].content = cont_response;
                    n_msgs++;
                    body_act(cont_response, &senses, input);
                    senses_refresh(&senses);
                } else {
                    free(cont_response);
                    break;
                }
            }
        } else {
            printf("\033[31m[no response]\033[0m");
            free(response);
        }
        printf("\n\n");

        /* Context budget — prune by tokens, not message count.
           Estimate tokens as bytes/4. Keep under 75% of provider window
           so the model has room to respond. Hard cap at MAX_CONV still. */
        {
            int budget = g_provider->max_ctx > 0 ? g_provider->max_ctx : 32000;
            int limit = budget * 3 / 4;  /* 75% */
            int total = 0;
            for (int i = 0; i < n_msgs; i++)
                total += (int)strlen(msgs[i].content) / 4;
            while (total > limit && n_msgs > 2) {
                total -= (int)strlen(msgs[0].content) / 4;
                free(msgs[0].content);
                memmove(msgs, msgs + 1, (n_msgs - 1) * sizeof(Msg));
                n_msgs--;
            }
            /* Hard cap fallback */
            if (n_msgs >= MAX_CONV - 2) {
                free(msgs[0].content); free(msgs[1].content);
                memmove(msgs, msgs + 2, (n_msgs - 2) * sizeof(Msg));
                n_msgs -= 2;
            }
        }
    }

    /* Drain — persist everything. Direct function calls, no subprocess. */
    printf("\n\033[90m[persisting...]\033[0m\n");
    {
        /* Save conversation — keep last 50 messages max */
        FILE *sf = fopen(".xyzt/conversation.bin", "wb");
        if (sf) {
            int save_start = (n_msgs > 50) ? n_msgs - 50 : 0;
            uint32_t n = (uint32_t)(n_msgs - save_start);
            fwrite(&n, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, sf);
            for (int i = save_start; i < n_msgs; i++) {
                uint8_t is_a = (strcmp(msgs[i].role, "assistant") == 0) ? 1 : 0;
                uint32_t clen = (uint32_t)strlen(msgs[i].content);
                fwrite(&is_a, 1, 1, sf);
                fwrite(&clen, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, sf);
                fwrite(msgs[i].content, 1, clen, sf);
            }
            fclose(sf);
            printf("\033[90m  conversation saved (%d msgs)\033[0m\n", n_msgs);
        }

        /* Wire prune */
        WireGraph *gd = wire_load();
        wire_prune(gd, 30);
        wire_recompute_topology(gd);
        wire_save(gd);
        wire_free(gd);
        printf("\033[90m  wire pruned\033[0m\n");
        printf("\033[90m  done\033[0m\n");
    }

    /* Free conversation */
    for (int i = 0; i < n_msgs; i++) free(msgs[i].content);
    senses_free(&senses);
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_agent.c — Multi-Model Agent Harness
 *
 * Spawns agents backed by ANY model (Ollama, Gemini, Claude, Qwen)
 * with full XYZT tool access. Claude Code orchestrates; clanker
 * agents do recon, analysis, and review.
 *
 * Hybrid networking:
 *   - Ollama: raw sockets (local, no TLS)
 *   - HTTPS APIs: curl subprocess (Gemini, Claude, Qwen)
 *
 * Usage:
 *   xyzt_agent --model qwen3:8b --role scout --task "map the auth system"
 *   xyzt_agent --model gemini-2.0-flash --role critic --task "review changes"
 *   xyzt_agent --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --role builder --task "implement X"
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O2 -o xyzt_agent.exe xyzt_agent.c -lws2_32 -ladvapi32
 * Install: copy to ~/.local/bin/
 *
 * Isaac Oravec & Claude — March 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>

#ifdef _WIN32
#include <winsock2.h>
#include <ws2tcpip.h>
#pragma comment(lib, "ws2_32.lib")
#else
#include <unistd.h>
#include <sys/socket.h>
#include <netinet/in.h>
#include <arpa/inet.h>
#endif

#include "wire.h"

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * Layer 0: Foundation — constants, enums, structs
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define MAX_TURNS       20
#define MAX_MSG_BUF     65536       /* 64KB per message content */
#define MAX_CONV_BUF    262144      /* 256KB conversation limit */
#define MAX_TOOL_OUT    32768       /* 32KB tool output capture */
#define MAX_CMD         8192
#define TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS 30000       /* 30s per tool execution */
#define CURL_TIMEOUT    60          /* 60s for HTTPS requests */
#define MAX_MESSAGES    64
#define OLLAMA_HOST     "127.0.0.1"
#define OLLAMA_PORT     11434
#define TEMP_REQ_FILE   ".xyzt/agent_req.json"
#define TEMP_RESP_FILE  ".xyzt/agent_resp.json"

typedef enum { BACKEND_OLLAMA, BACKEND_GEMINI, BACKEND_CLAUDE, BACKEND_OPENROUTER,
               BACKEND_GROQ } Backend;
typedef enum { ROLE_SCOUT, ROLE_ANALYST, ROLE_ARCHITECT, ROLE_BUILDER,
               ROLE_CRITIC, ROLE_INTEGRATOR } Role;

typedef struct {
    char role[16];          /* "system", "user", "assistant", "tool" */
    char *content;          /* message text (heap allocated) */
    char tool_name[64];     /* for tool_call results */
    char tool_call_id[64];  /* tool call id */
    int  is_tool_call;      /* assistant requested tool */
    char tc_name[64];       /* tool call: function name */
    char *tc_args;          /* tool call: JSON arguments (heap) */
    char tc_id[64];         /* tool call: id */
} Message;

typedef struct {
    Message msgs[MAX_MESSAGES];
    int     n_msgs;
    int     total_bytes;    /* rough content byte counter */
} Conversation;

typedef struct {
    char    model[128];
    Backend backend;
    Role    role;
    char    task[4096];
    char    api_key[256];
    int     max_turns;
    int     verbose;
    /* v2: orchestration extensions */
    int     json_output;        /* --json: structured output */
    int     allow_write;        /* --allow-write: enable write_file/patch_file */
    char    context_file[512];  /* --context <file>: inject prior context */
    int     context_stdin;      /* --context-stdin: read context from stdin */
    char    task_file[512];     /* --task-file <file>: read task from file */
} AgentConfig;

#define MAX_RESULT_FILES  64
#define MAX_RESULT_WRITES 32
#define MAX_CONTEXT_BUF   32768  /* 32KB context cap */

typedef struct {
    int    success;
    int    turns_used;
    int    tools_called;
    char   files_read[MAX_RESULT_FILES][256];
    int    n_files_read;
    char   writes[MAX_RESULT_WRITES][512];  /* "write:path" or "patch:path:L10-L15" */
    int    n_writes;
    char   errors[4096];
    int    error_len;
    char   *summary;            /* final agent text (heap) */
} AgentResult;

typedef struct {
    int   is_tool_call;
    char  name[64];
    char  *args;            /* heap allocated */
    char  id[64];
    char  *text;            /* heap allocated — final text response */
} ParsedResponse;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * Layer 0b: JSON parser (from xyzt_hook.c)
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int jget(const char *json, const char *key, char *out, int max) {
    char pat[256];
    int klen = snprintf(pat, sizeof(pat), "\"%s\"", key);
    const char *p = json;
    while ((p = strstr(p, pat)) != NULL) {
        char after = p[klen];
        if (after == ':' || after == ' ' || after == '\t' || after == '\n' || after == '\r')
            break;
        p += klen;
    }
    if (!p) return 0;
    p += klen;
    while (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\n' || *p == '\r' || *p == ':') p++;
    if (*p != '"') return 0;
    p++;
    int i = 0;
    while (*p && *p != '"' && i < max - 1) {
        if (*p == '\\' && p[1]) {
            p++;
            switch (*p) {
                case 'n':  out[i++] = '\n'; break;
                case 't':  out[i++] = '\t'; break;
                case 'r':  break;
                case '\\': out[i++] = '\\'; break;
                case '"':  out[i++] = '"';  break;
                case '/':  out[i++] = '/';  break;
                default:   out[i++] = *p;   break;
            }
        } else {
            out[i++] = *p;
        }
        p++;
    }
    out[i] = 0;
    return i;
}

/* Extract a raw JSON value (string, number, bool, object, array) */
static int jget_raw(const char *json, const char *key, char *out, int max) {
    char pat[256];
    int klen = snprintf(pat, sizeof(pat), "\"%s\"", key);
    const char *p = json;
    while ((p = strstr(p, pat)) != NULL) {
        char after = p[klen];
        if (after == ':' || after == ' ' || after == '\t' || after == '\n' || after == '\r')
            break;
        p += klen;
    }
    if (!p) return 0;
    p += klen;
    while (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\n' || *p == '\r' || *p == ':') p++;

    /* For strings, return with quotes */
    if (*p == '"') {
        out[0] = '"'; int i = 1; p++;
        while (*p && i < max - 2) {
            if (*p == '\\' && p[1]) { out[i++] = *p++; out[i++] = *p++; continue; }
            out[i++] = *p;
            if (*p == '"') { i++; break; }
            p++;
        }
        out[i - 1] = '"'; out[i] = 0;
        return i;
    }
    /* For objects/arrays, balance braces/brackets */
    if (*p == '{' || *p == '[') {
        char open = *p, close = (open == '{') ? '}' : ']';
        int depth = 0, i = 0, in_str = 0;
        while (*p && i < max - 1) {
            if (!in_str) {
                if (*p == open) depth++;
                else if (*p == close) { depth--; if (depth == 0) { out[i++] = *p; break; } }
                else if (*p == '"') in_str = 1;
            } else {
                if (*p == '\\' && p[1]) { out[i++] = *p++; out[i++] = *p++; continue; }
                if (*p == '"') in_str = 0;
            }
            out[i++] = *p++;
        }
        out[i] = 0;
        return i;
    }
    /* Numbers, bools, null */
    int i = 0;
    while (*p && *p != ',' && *p != '}' && *p != ']' && *p != '\n' && i < max - 1)
        out[i++] = *p++;
    out[i] = 0;
    return i;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * Layer 1: JSON Builder — position-advancing buffer writers
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    char *buf;
    int   pos;
    int   max;
} JsonBuf;

static void jb_init(JsonBuf *j, char *buf, int max) {
    j->buf = buf; j->pos = 0; j->max = max;
    buf[0] = 0;
}

static void jb_raw(JsonBuf *j, const char *s) {
    int len = (int)strlen(s);
    if (j->pos + len >= j->max) return;
    memcpy(j->buf + j->pos, s, len);
    j->pos += len;
    j->buf[j->pos] = 0;
}

static void jb_char(JsonBuf *j, char c) {
    if (j->pos + 1 >= j->max) return;
    j->buf[j->pos++] = c;
    j->buf[j->pos] = 0;
}

/* JSON-escape a string and write it (with surrounding quotes) */
static void jb_str(JsonBuf *j, const char *s) {
    jb_char(j, '"');
    for (; *s && j->pos < j->max - 6; s++) {
        switch (*s) {
            case '"':  jb_raw(j, "\\\""); break;
            case '\\': jb_raw(j, "\\\\"); break;
            case '\n': jb_raw(j, "\\n");  break;
            case '\r': jb_raw(j, "\\r");  break;
            case '\t': jb_raw(j, "\\t");  break;
            default:
                if ((unsigned char)*s >= 0x20) jb_char(j, *s);
                break;
        }
    }
    jb_char(j, '"');
}

/* Key-value helpers */
static void jb_key_str(JsonBuf *j, const char *key, const char *val) {
    jb_str(j, key); jb_char(j, ':'); jb_str(j, val);
}

static void jb_key_int(JsonBuf *j, const char *key, int val) {
    jb_str(j, key); jb_char(j, ':');
    char num[32]; snprintf(num, sizeof(num), "%d", val);
    jb_raw(j, num);
}

static void jb_key_bool(JsonBuf *j, const char *key, int val) {
    jb_str(j, key); jb_char(j, ':');
    jb_raw(j, val ? "true" : "false");
}

static void jb_key_raw(JsonBuf *j, const char *key, const char *raw) {
    jb_str(j, key); jb_char(j, ':'); jb_raw(j, raw);
}

static void jb_comma(JsonBuf *j) { jb_char(j, ','); }

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * Layer 2: Tool Definitions & Execution
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Subprocess with timeout — adapted from xyzt_hook.c */
#ifdef _WIN32

static int run_timed(const char *command, char *out, int max, int timeout_ms) {
    if (out) out[0] = 0;

    char cmdline[MAX_CMD];
    snprintf(cmdline, sizeof(cmdline), "cmd.exe /c \"%s\"", command);

    HANDLE hReadPipe = NULL, hWritePipe = NULL;
    SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES sa = { sizeof(sa), NULL, TRUE };

    if (out) {
        CreatePipe(&hReadPipe, &hWritePipe, &sa, 0);
        SetHandleInformation(hReadPipe, HANDLE_FLAG_INHERIT, 0);
    }

    HANDLE hNul = CreateFileA("NUL", GENERIC_WRITE, FILE_SHARE_WRITE,
                              &sa, OPEN_EXISTING, 0, NULL);

    STARTUPINFOA si;
    PROCESS_INFORMATION pi;
    ZeroMemory(&si, sizeof(si));
    si.cb = sizeof(si);
    si.dwFlags = STARTF_USESTDHANDLES;
    si.hStdInput = NULL;
    si.hStdOutput = out ? hWritePipe : hNul;
    si.hStdError = hNul;

    ZeroMemory(&pi, sizeof(pi));
    BOOL ok = CreateProcessA(NULL, cmdline, NULL, NULL, TRUE,
                             CREATE_NO_WINDOW, NULL, NULL, &si, &pi);

    if (hWritePipe) CloseHandle(hWritePipe);

    if (!ok) {
        if (hReadPipe) CloseHandle(hReadPipe);
        if (hNul) CloseHandle(hNul);
        return -1;
    }

    int total = 0;
    if (hReadPipe && out) {
        DWORD bytesRead;
        while (total < max - 1) {
            BOOL r = ReadFile(hReadPipe, out + total, max - 1 - total, &bytesRead, NULL);
            if (!r || bytesRead == 0) break;
            total += bytesRead;
        }
        out[total] = 0;
    }

    DWORD wait = WaitForSingleObject(pi.hProcess, timeout_ms);
    if (wait == WAIT_TIMEOUT) TerminateProcess(pi.hProcess, 1);

    CloseHandle(pi.hProcess);
    CloseHandle(pi.hThread);
    if (hReadPipe) CloseHandle(hReadPipe);
    if (hNul) CloseHandle(hNul);
    return (wait == WAIT_OBJECT_0) ? 0 : -1;
}

#else /* POSIX */

#include <signal.h>
#include <sys/wait.h>
#include <fcntl.h>

static volatile pid_t timeout_child_pid = 0;
static void timeout_handler(int sig) { (void)sig; if (timeout_child_pid > 0) kill(timeout_child_pid, SIGKILL); }

static int run_timed(const char *command, char *out, int max, int timeout_ms) {
    if (out) out[0] = 0;
    int pipefd[2] = {-1, -1};
    int capture = (out != NULL);
    if (capture && pipe(pipefd) < 0) return -1;

    pid_t pid = fork();
    if (pid < 0) { if (capture) { close(pipefd[0]); close(pipefd[1]); } return -1; }

    if (pid == 0) {
        if (capture) { close(pipefd[0]); dup2(pipefd[1], STDOUT_FILENO); close(pipefd[1]); }
        else { int dn = open("/dev/null", O_WRONLY); if (dn >= 0) { dup2(dn, STDOUT_FILENO); close(dn); } }
        int dn = open("/dev/null", O_WRONLY); if (dn >= 0) { dup2(dn, STDERR_FILENO); close(dn); }
        execl("/bin/sh", "sh", "-c", command, (char *)NULL);
        _exit(127);
    }

    if (capture) close(pipefd[1]);
    timeout_child_pid = pid;
    struct sigaction sa_old, sa_new;
    memset(&sa_new, 0, sizeof(sa_new));
    sa_new.sa_handler = timeout_handler;
    sigaction(SIGALRM, &sa_new, &sa_old);
    alarm(timeout_ms / 1000 + 1);

    int total = 0;
    if (capture && out) {
        ssize_t r;
        while (total < max - 1 && (r = read(pipefd[0], out + total, max - 1 - total)) > 0)
            total += (int)r;
        out[total] = 0;
        close(pipefd[0]);
    }

    int status = 0;
    waitpid(pid, &status, 0);
    alarm(0);
    sigaction(SIGALRM, &sa_old, NULL);
    timeout_child_pid = 0;
    return WIFEXITED(status) ? 0 : -1;
}

#endif /* _WIN32 / POSIX */

/* ── AgentResult helpers ── */

static void result_add_file_read(AgentResult *r, const char *path) {
    if (!r || !path || !path[0]) return;
    for (int i = 0; i < r->n_files_read; i++)
        if (strcmp(r->files_read[i], path) == 0) return;  /* dedup */
    if (r->n_files_read < MAX_RESULT_FILES)
        strncpy(r->files_read[r->n_files_read++], path, 255);
}

static void result_add_write(AgentResult *r, const char *entry) {
    if (!r || !entry) return;
    if (r->n_writes < MAX_RESULT_WRITES)
        strncpy(r->writes[r->n_writes++], entry, 511);
}

static void result_add_error(AgentResult *r, const char *err) {
    if (!r || !err) return;
    int elen = (int)strlen(err);
    if (r->error_len + elen + 2 < (int)sizeof(r->errors)) {
        if (r->error_len > 0) r->errors[r->error_len++] = '\n';
        memcpy(r->errors + r->error_len, err, elen);
        r->error_len += elen;
        r->errors[r->error_len] = 0;
    }
}

/* ── In-process write handlers (gated by allow_write) ── */

static char *tool_write_file(const char *args_json, int allow_write, AgentResult *result) {
    char path[512] = {0};
    char content[MAX_TOOL_OUT] = {0};
    jget(args_json, "path", path, sizeof(path));
    jget(args_json, "content", content, sizeof(content));

    if (!path[0]) return strdup("Error: 'path' is required");
    if (!allow_write) return strdup("write_file is disabled. Agent does not have --allow-write permission.");

    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "wb");
    if (!fp) {
        char err[600];
        snprintf(err, sizeof(err), "Error: cannot open '%s' for writing", path);
        if (result) result_add_error(result, err);
        return strdup(err);
    }
    int len = (int)strlen(content);
    fwrite(content, 1, len, fp);
    fclose(fp);

    /* Auto-fingerprint after write (mirrors PostToolUse Write hook) */
    { char sc[600]; snprintf(sc, sizeof(sc), "xyzt_io store \"%s\"", path); run_timed(sc, NULL, 0, 5000); }

    char msg[600];
    snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Wrote %d bytes to %s", len, path);
    if (result) {
        char entry[512];
        snprintf(entry, sizeof(entry), "write:%s", path);
        result_add_write(result, entry);
    }
    return strdup(msg);
}

static char *tool_patch_file(const char *args_json, int allow_write, AgentResult *result) {
    char path[512] = {0};
    char new_content[MAX_TOOL_OUT] = {0};
    char s_start[32] = {0}, s_end[32] = {0};
    jget(args_json, "path", path, sizeof(path));
    jget(args_json, "content", new_content, sizeof(new_content));
    jget(args_json, "start_line", s_start, sizeof(s_start));
