    /* Read and embed the query file */
    size_t len = 0;
    char *content = read_file_content(file_path, &len);
    if (!content) {
        fprintf(stderr, "[embed] Cannot read %s\n", file_path);
        return;
    }

    /* Escape for JSON */
    char *escaped = malloc(len * 2 + 1);
    int j = 0;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
        if (content[i] == '\n') {
            escaped[j++] = '\\';
            escaped[j++] = 'n';
        } else if (content[i] == '\r') {
            escaped[j++] = '\\';
            escaped[j++] = 'r';
        } else if (content[i] == '"') {
            escaped[j++] = '\\';
            escaped[j++] = '"';
        } else if (content[i] == '\\') {
            escaped[j++] = '\\';
            escaped[j++] = '\\';
        } else {
            escaped[j++] = content[i];
        }
    }
    escaped[j] = 0;
    free(content);

    int resp_len = 0;
    char *resp = ollama_embed(escaped, &resp_len);
    free(escaped);

    if (!resp) {
        fprintf(stderr, "[embed] Ollama request failed\n");
        return;
    }

    float query_vec[MAX_EMBED_DIM];
    int query_dim = parse_embeddings(resp, query_vec, MAX_EMBED_DIM);
    free(resp);

    if (query_dim == 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "[embed] Failed to parse query embeddings\n");
        return;
    }

    /* Load store */
    EmbeddingStore *store = store_load();
    if (!store || store->count == 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "[embed] No embeddings stored\n");
        if (store) free(store);
        return;
    }

    /* Compute similarities */
    typedef struct {
        int   idx;
        float sim;
    } Score;

    Score scores[MAX_FILES];
    int n_scores = 0;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < store->count; i++) {
        if (strcmp(store->entries[i].path, file_path) == 0) continue;  /* Skip self */
        int dim = store->entries[i].dim;
        if (dim != query_dim) continue;  /* Dimension mismatch */

        float sim = cosine_similarity(query_vec, store->entries[i].vector, dim);
        scores[n_scores].idx = i;
        scores[n_scores].sim = sim;
        n_scores++;
    }

    /* Sort by similarity (descending) */
    for (int i = 0; i < n_scores - 1; i++) {
        for (int j = i + 1; j < n_scores; j++) {
            if (scores[j].sim > scores[i].sim) {
                Score tmp = scores[i];
                scores[i] = scores[j];
                scores[j] = tmp;
            }
        }
    }

    /* Print top N */
    printf("---XYZT-EMBED-QUERY---\n");
    printf("query: %s\n", file_path);
    int limit = (top_n < n_scores) ? top_n : n_scores;
    for (int i = 0; i < limit; i++) {
        Embedding *e = &store->entries[scores[i].idx];
        printf("  %3d  %.4f  %s\n", i + 1, scores[i].sim, e->path);
    }
    if (limit == 0) {
        printf("  (no similar files found)\n");
    }
    printf("---END---\n");

    free(store);
}

static void cmd_batch(const char *session_path) {
    /* Load session.bin to get file list */
    FILE *f = fopen(session_path, "rb");
    if (!f) {
        fprintf(stderr, "[embed] Cannot read %s\n", session_path);
        return;
    }

    /* Read header */
    struct {
        uint32_t magic;
        uint8_t  version;
        uint8_t  _pad[3];
        uint32_t count;
        uint32_t _reserved;
    } hdr;
    if (fread(&hdr, sizeof(hdr), 1, f) != 1 || hdr.magic != 0x58595A54) {
        fprintf(stderr, "[embed] Bad session file\n");
        fclose(f);
        return;
    }

    printf("[embed] Batch embedding %d files...\n", hdr.count);

    /* Create store */
    EmbeddingStore *store = store_load();
    if (!store) store = store_create();

    int embedded = 0;
    int skipped = 0;
    int failed = 0;

    /* Skip header, read entries */
    fseek(f, sizeof(hdr), SEEK_SET);

    /* Read each fingerprint entry and extract path */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < hdr.count; i++) {
        /* Read Fingerprint struct (simplified - just need path) */
        char path[MAX_PATH];
        /* Fingerprint starts with bitstream, path is at offset 664 */
        fseek(f, 664, SEEK_CUR);  /* Skip bitstream and other fields */
        if (fread(path, MAX_PATH, 1, f) != 1) break;
        fseek(f, 256, SEEK_CUR);  /* Skip file_hash */

        /* Check if already embedded */
        int exists = 0;
        for (uint32_t j = 0; j < store->count; j++) {
            if (strcmp(store->entries[j].path, path) == 0) {
                exists = 1;
                break;
            }
        }
        if (exists) {
            skipped++;
            continue;
        }

        /* Embed */
        size_t len = 0;
        char *content = read_file_content(path, &len);
        if (!content) {
            failed++;
            continue;
        }

        /* Escape */
        char *escaped = malloc(len * 2 + 1);
        int j = 0;
        for (size_t k = 0; k < len; k++) {
            if (content[k] == '\n') {
                escaped[j++] = '\\';
                escaped[j++] = 'n';
            } else if (content[k] == '\r') {
                escaped[j++] = '\\';
                escaped[j++] = 'r';
            } else if (content[k] == '"') {
                escaped[j++] = '\\';
                escaped[j++] = '"';
            } else if (content[k] == '\\') {
                escaped[j++] = '\\';
                escaped[j++] = '\\';
            } else {
                escaped[j++] = content[k];
            }
        }
        escaped[j] = 0;
        free(content);

        int resp_len = 0;
        char *resp = ollama_embed(escaped, &resp_len);
        free(escaped);

        if (!resp) {
            failed++;
            continue;
        }

        Embedding emb;
        memset(&emb, 0, sizeof(emb));
        strncpy(emb.path, path, MAX_PATH - 1);
        emb.dim = parse_embeddings(resp, emb.vector, MAX_EMBED_DIM);
        emb.timestamp = (uint64_t)time(NULL);
        free(resp);

        if (emb.dim > 0 && store->count < MAX_FILES) {
            store->entries[store->count++] = emb;
            embedded++;
            printf("  [%d/%d] %s\n", embedded, hdr.count, path);
        }
    }
    fclose(f);

    store_save(store);
    free(store);

    printf("[embed] Done: %d embedded, %d skipped, %d failed\n", embedded, skipped, failed);
}

static void cmd_list(void) {
    EmbeddingStore *store = store_load();
    if (!store || store->count == 0) {
        printf("[embed] No embeddings stored\n");
        if (store) free(store);
        return;
    }

    printf("---XYZT-EMBED-LIST---\n");
    printf("total: %d files, dim: %d\n", store->count, store->dim);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < store->count; i++) {
        Embedding *e = &store->entries[i];
        time_t t = (time_t)e->timestamp;
        char time_buf[64];
        strftime(time_buf, sizeof(time_buf), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", localtime(&t));
        printf("  %3d  %-40s  %d dims  %s\n", i + 1, e->path, e->dim, time_buf);
    }
    printf("---END---\n");
    free(store);
}

static void usage(void) {
    printf("xyzt_embed — Semantic Embedding via Ollama qwen3-embedding\n");
    printf("\n");
    printf("Usage:\n");
    printf("  xyzt_embed store <file>     Embed a file and store in .xyzt/embeddings.bin\n");
    printf("  xyzt_embed query <file>     Find top 5 similar files by cosine distance\n");
    printf("  xyzt_embed batch            Embed all files in .xyzt/session.bin\n");
    printf("  xyzt_embed list             List all stored embeddings\n");
    printf("\n");
    printf("Requires: Ollama running locally with qwen3-embedding model\n");
    printf("  ollama pull qwen3-embedding:latest\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) {
        usage();
        return 1;
    }

    const char *cmd = argv[1];

    if (strcmp(cmd, "store") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_store(argv[2]);
    } else if (strcmp(cmd, "query") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_query(argv[2], 5);
    } else if (strcmp(cmd, "batch") == 0) {
        cmd_batch(".xyzt/session.bin");
    } else if (strcmp(cmd, "list") == 0) {
        cmd_list();
    } else {
        usage();
        return 1;
    }

    net_cleanup();
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_err.c — Persistent Error Memory
 *
 * Captures build/test errors per-file and persists them.
 * Pre-edit hook warns about past errors on the file being edited.
 * Links error patterns to wire graph as concept nodes.
 *
 * Commands:
 *   xyzt_err log <file> <line> <message>    Record an error
 *   xyzt_err query <file>                   Show error history for file
 *   xyzt_err recent [N]                     Show N most recent errors
 *   xyzt_err patterns                       Show recurring error patterns
 *   xyzt_err clear                          Clear error history
 *
 * State: .xyzt/errors.bin
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O3 -o xyzt_err xyzt_err.c
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — February 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <ctype.h>
#include <time.h>
#include "wire.h"

#define MAX_ERRORS     512
#define MAX_MSG        256
#define MAX_PATH_LEN   256
#define ERR_MAGIC      0x45525230  /* "ERR0" */
#define ERR_VER        1

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Data structures
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    char     file[MAX_PATH_LEN];
    uint32_t line;
    char     message[MAX_MSG];
    uint32_t timestamp;
    uint8_t  resolved;    /* 0=unresolved, 1=resolved (build succeeded after this) */
    uint8_t  _pad[3];
} ErrorEntry;

typedef struct {
    uint32_t   magic;
    uint32_t   version;
    uint32_t   count;
    uint32_t   total_logged;   /* lifetime count (even after wrapping) */
    ErrorEntry entries[MAX_ERRORS];
} ErrorStore;

#define ERR_PATH ".xyzt/errors.bin"

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Persistence
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static ErrorStore *err_load(void) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(ERR_PATH, "rb");
    if (!fp) {
        ErrorStore *s = calloc(1, sizeof(ErrorStore));
        s->magic = ERR_MAGIC;
        s->version = ERR_VER;
        return s;
    }
    ErrorStore *s = calloc(1, sizeof(ErrorStore));
    fread(s, sizeof(ErrorStore), 1, fp);
    fclose(fp);
    if (s->magic != ERR_MAGIC) {
        memset(s, 0, sizeof(ErrorStore));
        s->magic = ERR_MAGIC;
        s->version = ERR_VER;
    }
    return s;
}

static void err_save(ErrorStore *s) {
    xyzt_ensure_dir(ERR_PATH);
    FILE *fp = fopen(ERR_PATH, "wb");
    if (!fp) { fprintf(stderr, "Cannot write error store\n"); return; }
    fwrite(s, sizeof(ErrorStore), 1, fp);
    fclose(fp);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Error pattern extraction
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Extract a normalized error category from a message */
static void extract_pattern(const char *msg, char *pattern, int max) {
    /* Common error patterns → normalized names */
    if (strstr(msg, "undefined reference") || strstr(msg, "unresolved external"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:undefined_reference", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "undeclared") || strstr(msg, "not declared"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:undeclared_identifier", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "implicit declaration"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:implicit_declaration", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "expected") && strstr(msg, "before"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:syntax_expected", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "incompatible") && strstr(msg, "type"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:type_mismatch", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "redefinition") || strstr(msg, "redeclared"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:redefinition", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "no such file") || strstr(msg, "not found") || strstr(msg, "No such file"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:file_not_found", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "segmentation fault") || strstr(msg, "SIGSEGV"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:segfault", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "memory") && (strstr(msg, "leak") || strstr(msg, "overflow")))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:memory", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "warning:"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "warning:general", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "ImportError") || strstr(msg, "ModuleNotFoundError"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:import_not_found", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "TypeError"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:type_error", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "SyntaxError"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:syntax", max-1); return; }
    if (strstr(msg, "AttributeError"))
        { strncpy(pattern, "error:attribute", max-1); return; }

    /* Default: first significant word after "error" */
    const char *e = strstr(msg, "error");
    if (!e) e = strstr(msg, "Error");
    if (e) {
        snprintf(pattern, max, "error:%.30s", e);
        /* Trim to first space/punctuation after "error:" */
        for (int i = 6; pattern[i]; i++) {
            if (pattern[i] == ' ' || pattern[i] == '\n' || pattern[i] == ':') {
                pattern[i] = '\0'; break;
            }
        }
        return;
    }
    strncpy(pattern, "error:unknown", max-1);
}

/* Extract just the filename from a path */
static const char *basename_of(const char *path) {
    const char *last = path;
    for (const char *p = path; *p; p++)
        if (*p == '\\' || *p == '/') last = p + 1;
    return last;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Commands
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_log(const char *file, int line, const char *message) {
    ErrorStore *s = err_load();

    /* Circular buffer: overwrite oldest when full */
    uint32_t slot = s->count;
    if (slot >= MAX_ERRORS) {
        /* Shift: drop oldest 64 */
        memmove(s->entries, s->entries + 64, (MAX_ERRORS - 64) * sizeof(ErrorEntry));
        s->count -= 64;
        slot = s->count;
    }

    ErrorEntry *e = &s->entries[slot];
    strncpy(e->file, basename_of(file), MAX_PATH_LEN - 1);
    e->line = line;
    strncpy(e->message, message, MAX_MSG - 1);
    e->timestamp = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    e->resolved = 0;
    s->count++;
    s->total_logged++;

    err_save(s);

    printf("err[%u]: %s:%d %s\n", slot, e->file, line, message);

    /* Link to wire graph: error pattern → file */
    char pattern[128];
    extract_pattern(message, pattern, sizeof(pattern));

    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int err_id = wire_add(g, pattern, WNODE_CONCEPT);
    int file_id = wire_add(g, basename_of(file), WNODE_FILE);
    if (err_id >= 0 && file_id >= 0) {
        wire_connect(g, err_id, file_id);
        printf("wire: linked %s -> %s\n", pattern, basename_of(file));
    }
    wire_save(g);
    wire_free(g);

    free(s);
}

static void cmd_query(const char *file) {
    ErrorStore *s = err_load();
    const char *fname = basename_of(file);

    printf("---XYZT-ERR---\n");
    printf("file: %s\n", fname);

    int found = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->count; i++) {
        if (strcmp(s->entries[i].file, fname) == 0 ||
            strcmp(basename_of(s->entries[i].file), fname) == 0) {
            ErrorEntry *e = &s->entries[i];
            uint32_t ago = (uint32_t)time(NULL) - e->timestamp;
            char ago_str[32];
            if (ago < 60) snprintf(ago_str, 32, "%us ago", ago);
            else if (ago < 3600) snprintf(ago_str, 32, "%um ago", ago / 60);
            else if (ago < 86400) snprintf(ago_str, 32, "%uh ago", ago / 3600);
            else snprintf(ago_str, 32, "%ud ago", ago / 86400);

            printf("  [%u] line %u: %s (%s%s)\n",
                   i, e->line, e->message, ago_str,
                   e->resolved ? ", resolved" : "");
            found++;
        }
    }

    if (found == 0) printf("  (no errors recorded)\n");
    else printf("\n%d error(s) on record\n", found);

    /* Wire context for this file's error patterns */
    WireConn conns[WIRE_MAX_CONN];
    int n = wire_query_connections(fname, conns, 12);
    int patterns = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if (conns[i].type == WNODE_CONCEPT && strncmp(conns[i].name, "error:", 6) == 0) {
            if (patterns == 0) printf("\nrecurring patterns:\n");
            printf("  %s (w=%d, %d occurrences)\n",
                   conns[i].name, conns[i].weight, conns[i].passes + conns[i].fails);
            patterns++;
        }
    }

    printf("---END-ERR---\n");
    free(s);
}

static void cmd_recent(int n) {
    ErrorStore *s = err_load();
    printf("---XYZT-ERR-RECENT---\n");
    printf("total logged: %u (showing last %d)\n", s->total_logged, n);

    int start = (int)s->count - n;
    if (start < 0) start = 0;

    for (int i = start; i < (int)s->count; i++) {
        ErrorEntry *e = &s->entries[i];
        uint32_t ago = (uint32_t)time(NULL) - e->timestamp;
        char ago_str[32];
        if (ago < 60) snprintf(ago_str, 32, "%us ago", ago);
        else if (ago < 3600) snprintf(ago_str, 32, "%um ago", ago / 60);
        else snprintf(ago_str, 32, "%uh ago", ago / 3600);

        printf("  %s:%u %s (%s%s)\n",
               e->file, e->line, e->message, ago_str,
               e->resolved ? ", resolved" : "");
    }
    printf("---END-ERR-RECENT---\n");
    free(s);
}

static void cmd_patterns(void) {
    ErrorStore *s = err_load();
    printf("---XYZT-ERR-PATTERNS---\n");

    /* Count occurrences of each pattern */
    typedef struct { char pattern[128]; int count; char files[512]; } PatCount;
    PatCount pats[64];
    int np = 0;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->count; i++) {
        char pattern[128];
        extract_pattern(s->entries[i].message, pattern, sizeof(pattern));

        int found = -1;
        for (int j = 0; j < np; j++) {
            if (strcmp(pats[j].pattern, pattern) == 0) { found = j; break; }
        }
        if (found >= 0) {
            pats[found].count++;
            /* Append file if not already listed */
            if (!strstr(pats[found].files, s->entries[i].file)) {
                int flen = strlen(pats[found].files);
                if (flen < 500) {
                    snprintf(pats[found].files + flen, 512 - flen, "%s%s",
                             flen > 0 ? ", " : "", s->entries[i].file);
                }
            }
        } else if (np < 64) {
            strncpy(pats[np].pattern, pattern, 127);
            pats[np].count = 1;
            strncpy(pats[np].files, s->entries[i].file, 511);
            np++;
        }
    }

    /* Sort descending by count */
    for (int i = 1; i < np; i++) {
        PatCount tmp = pats[i]; int j = i - 1;
        while (j >= 0 && pats[j].count < tmp.count) { pats[j+1] = pats[j]; j--; }
        pats[j+1] = tmp;
    }

    for (int i = 0; i < np; i++) {
        printf("  %3d× %-30s  files: %s\n", pats[i].count, pats[i].pattern, pats[i].files);
    }

    if (np == 0) printf("  (no patterns)\n");
    printf("---END-ERR-PATTERNS---\n");
    free(s);
}

static void cmd_resolve(const char *file) {
    ErrorStore *s = err_load();
    const char *fname = basename_of(file);
    int resolved = 0;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->count; i++) {
        if (!s->entries[i].resolved &&
            (strcmp(s->entries[i].file, fname) == 0 ||
             strcmp(basename_of(s->entries[i].file), fname) == 0)) {
            s->entries[i].resolved = 1;
            resolved++;
        }
    }

    if (resolved > 0) {
        err_save(s);
        printf("resolved %d error(s) for %s\n", resolved, fname);
    } else {
        printf("no unresolved errors for %s\n", fname);
    }
    free(s);
}

static void cmd_clear(void) {
    ErrorStore *s = calloc(1, sizeof(ErrorStore));
    s->magic = ERR_MAGIC;
    s->version = ERR_VER;
    err_save(s);
    printf("error history cleared\n");
    free(s);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT ERR — Persistent Error Memory\n\n");
    printf("  xyzt_err log <file> <line> <message>    Record an error\n");
    printf("  xyzt_err query <file>                   Error history for file\n");
    printf("  xyzt_err recent [N]                     N most recent errors (default 10)\n");
    printf("  xyzt_err patterns                       Recurring error patterns\n");
    printf("  xyzt_err resolve <file>                 Mark errors as resolved\n");
    printf("  xyzt_err clear                          Clear error history\n");
    printf("\nState: %s\n", ERR_PATH);
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — February 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) { usage(); return 0; }

    if (strcmp(argv[1], "log") == 0 && argc >= 5) {
        int line = atoi(argv[3]);
        /* Concat remaining args as message */
        char msg[MAX_MSG] = "";
        int pos = 0;
        for (int i = 4; i < argc && pos < MAX_MSG - 2; i++) {
            if (i > 4) msg[pos++] = ' ';
            int len = strlen(argv[i]);
            if (pos + len >= MAX_MSG - 1) len = MAX_MSG - 1 - pos;
            memcpy(msg + pos, argv[i], len);
            pos += len;
        }
        msg[pos] = '\0';
        cmd_log(argv[2], line, msg);
    }
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "query") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_query(argv[2]);
    }
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "recent") == 0) {
        int n = argc >= 3 ? atoi(argv[2]) : 10;
        if (n < 1) n = 10;
        cmd_recent(n);
    }
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "patterns") == 0) {
        cmd_patterns();
    }
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "resolve") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_resolve(argv[2]);
    }
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "clear") == 0) {
        cmd_clear();
    }
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "--help") == 0 || strcmp(argv[1], "-h") == 0) {
        usage();
    }
    else {
        usage();
        return 1;
    }
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_hook.c — Claude Code hook handler for automatic XYZT toolkit integration
 *
 * Fires on Claude Code events. Reads JSON stdin, runs xyzt commands, feeds
 * results back via additionalContext. Zero manual commands. Minimal tokens.
 *
 * CRITICAL: every subprocess has a 3-second hard kill via Win32 CreateProcess.
 * Onetwo phase checks read the binary file directly — zero subprocesses.
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O2 -o xyzt_hook.exe xyzt_hook.c
 * Install: copy to ~/.local/bin/
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <sys/stat.h>

#ifdef _WIN32
#include <windows.h>
#else
#include <unistd.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <sys/wait.h>
#include <errno.h>
#endif

#include "wire.h"

#define MAX_INPUT  131072
#define MAX_VAL    4096
#define MAX_CMD    8192
#define TIMEOUT_MS 3000

/* Onetwo binary layout: magic(4) + ver(1) + phase(1) + pad(2) + ... + problem[256] at offset 16 */
#define OT_PHASE_OFFSET  5
#define OT_PROBLEM_OFFSET 16
#define OT_PROBLEM_LEN   256
#define PHASE_EMPTY 0
#define PHASE_DONE  5

/* ── JSON helpers ── */

static int jget(const char *json, const char *key, char *out, int max) {
    char pat[256];
    int klen = snprintf(pat, sizeof(pat), "\"%s\"", key);
    const char *p = json;
    /* Search for exact key match — prevent "file_path" matching "file_path_extra" */
    while ((p = strstr(p, pat)) != NULL) {
        /* Verify the character after the closing quote is : or whitespace */
        char after = p[klen];
        if (after == ':' || after == ' ' || after == '\t' || after == '\n' || after == '\r')
            break;
        p += klen;  /* not an exact match, keep searching */
    }
    if (!p) return 0;
    p += klen;
    while (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\n' || *p == '\r' || *p == ':') p++;
    if (*p != '"') return 0;
    p++;
    int i = 0;
    while (*p && *p != '"' && i < max - 1) {
        if (*p == '\\' && p[1]) {
            p++;
            switch (*p) {
                case 'n':  out[i++] = '\n'; break;
                case 't':  out[i++] = '\t'; break;
                case 'r':  break;
                case '\\': out[i++] = '\\'; break;
                case '"':  out[i++] = '"';  break;
                case '/':  out[i++] = '/';  break;
                default:   out[i++] = *p;   break;
            }
        } else {
            out[i++] = *p;
        }
        p++;
    }
    out[i] = 0;
    return i;
}

static void jprint(const char *s) {
    for (; *s; s++) {
        switch (*s) {
            case '"':  fputs("\\\"", stdout); break;
            case '\\': fputs("\\\\", stdout); break;
            case '\n': fputs("\\n", stdout);  break;
            case '\r': break;
            case '\t': fputs("\\t", stdout);  break;
            default:
                if ((unsigned char)*s >= 0x20) putchar(*s);
                break;
        }
    }
}

/* ── Subprocess with timeout ── */

#ifdef _WIN32

static int run_timed(const char *command, char *out, int max, int capture) {
    if (out) out[0] = 0;

    char cmdline[MAX_CMD];
    snprintf(cmdline, sizeof(cmdline), "cmd.exe /c \"%s\"", command);

    HANDLE hReadPipe = NULL, hWritePipe = NULL;
    SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES sa = { sizeof(sa), NULL, TRUE };

    if (capture)  {
        CreatePipe(&hReadPipe, &hWritePipe, &sa, 0);
        SetHandleInformation(hReadPipe, HANDLE_FLAG_INHERIT, 0);
    }

    HANDLE hNul = CreateFileA("NUL", GENERIC_WRITE, FILE_SHARE_WRITE, &sa, OPEN_EXISTING, 0, NULL);

    STARTUPINFOA si;
    PROCESS_INFORMATION pi;
    ZeroMemory(&si, sizeof(si));
    si.cb = sizeof(si);
    si.dwFlags = STARTF_USESTDHANDLES;
    si.hStdInput = NULL;
    si.hStdOutput = capture ? hWritePipe : hNul;
    si.hStdError = hNul;

    ZeroMemory(&pi, sizeof(pi));
    BOOL ok = CreateProcessA(NULL, cmdline, NULL, NULL, TRUE,
                             CREATE_NO_WINDOW, NULL, NULL, &si, &pi);

    if (hWritePipe) CloseHandle(hWritePipe);

    if (!ok) {
        if (hReadPipe) CloseHandle(hReadPipe);
        if (hNul) CloseHandle(hNul);
        return -1;
    }

    int total = 0;
    if (capture && hReadPipe && out) {
        DWORD bytesRead;
        while (total < max - 1) {
            BOOL r = ReadFile(hReadPipe, out + total, max - 1 - total, &bytesRead, NULL);
            if (!r || bytesRead == 0) break;
            total += bytesRead;
        }
        out[total] = 0;
    }

    DWORD wait = WaitForSingleObject(pi.hProcess, TIMEOUT_MS);
    if (wait == WAIT_TIMEOUT) TerminateProcess(pi.hProcess, 1);

    CloseHandle(pi.hProcess);
    CloseHandle(pi.hThread);
    if (hReadPipe) CloseHandle(hReadPipe);
    if (hNul) CloseHandle(hNul);
    return (wait == WAIT_OBJECT_0) ? 0 : -1;
}

#else /* POSIX */

static volatile pid_t timeout_child_pid = 0;
static void timeout_handler(int sig) {
    (void)sig;
    if (timeout_child_pid > 0) kill(timeout_child_pid, SIGKILL);
}

static int run_timed(const char *command, char *out, int max, int capture) {
    if (out) out[0] = 0;

    int pipefd[2] = {-1, -1};
    if (capture && pipe(pipefd) < 0) return -1;

    pid_t pid = fork();
    if (pid < 0) {
        if (capture) { close(pipefd[0]); close(pipefd[1]); }
        return -1;
    }

    if (pid == 0) {
        /* Child: redirect stdout, suppress stderr */
        if (capture) {
            close(pipefd[0]);
            dup2(pipefd[1], STDOUT_FILENO);
            close(pipefd[1]);
        } else {
            int devnull = open("/dev/null", O_WRONLY);
            if (devnull >= 0) { dup2(devnull, STDOUT_FILENO); close(devnull); }
        }
        int devnull = open("/dev/null", O_WRONLY);
        if (devnull >= 0) { dup2(devnull, STDERR_FILENO); close(devnull); }
        execl("/bin/sh", "sh", "-c", command, (char *)NULL);
        _exit(127);
    }

    /* Parent */
    if (capture) close(pipefd[1]);

    /* Set up timeout via alarm */
    timeout_child_pid = pid;
    struct sigaction sa_old, sa_new;
    memset(&sa_new, 0, sizeof(sa_new));
    sa_new.sa_handler = timeout_handler;
    sa_new.sa_flags = 0;
    sigaction(SIGALRM, &sa_new, &sa_old);
    alarm(TIMEOUT_MS / 1000 + 1);  /* round up to seconds */

    int total = 0;
    if (capture && out) {
        ssize_t r;
        while (total < max - 1 && (r = read(pipefd[0], out + total, max - 1 - total)) > 0)
            total += (int)r;
        out[total] = 0;
        close(pipefd[0]);
    }

    int status = 0;
    waitpid(pid, &status, 0);
    alarm(0);  /* cancel timeout */
    sigaction(SIGALRM, &sa_old, NULL);
    timeout_child_pid = 0;

    return WIFEXITED(status) ? 0 : -1;
}

#endif /* _WIN32 / POSIX */

static void run_fire(const char *command) { run_timed(command, NULL, 0, 0); }
static int  run_capture(const char *command, char *out, int max) { return run_timed(command, out, max, 1); }

/* ── Onetwo phase — direct binary read, zero subprocess ── */

/* Returns phase byte (0-5), or -1 if no session file.
   Optionally reads problem string into `problem` if non-NULL. */
static int onetwo_read_phase(const char *cwd, char *problem, int pmax) {
    char path[MAX_VAL];
    snprintf(path, sizeof(path), "%s\\.xyzt\\onetwo_session.bin", cwd);

    if (problem) problem[0] = 0;

    FILE *f = fopen(path, "rb");
    if (!f) return -1;

    /* Read phase byte at offset 5 */
    unsigned char header[16 + OT_PROBLEM_LEN];
    size_t got = fread(header, 1, sizeof(header), f);
    fclose(f);

    if (got < 6) return -1;

    int phase = header[OT_PHASE_OFFSET];
    if (phase > PHASE_DONE) return -1;

    if (problem && pmax > 0 && got >= OT_PROBLEM_OFFSET + 1) {
        int len = (int)(got - OT_PROBLEM_OFFSET);
        if (len > pmax - 1) len = pmax - 1;
        if (len > OT_PROBLEM_LEN) len = OT_PROBLEM_LEN;
        memcpy(problem, header + OT_PROBLEM_OFFSET, len);
        problem[len] = 0;
        /* Ensure null-terminated (problem is char[256] in struct) */
        problem[pmax - 1] = 0;
    }

    return phase;
}

/* Is there an active (non-DONE, non-EMPTY) onetwo problem? */
static int onetwo_is_active(const char *cwd) {
    int phase = onetwo_read_phase(cwd, NULL, 0);
    return (phase > PHASE_EMPTY && phase < PHASE_DONE);
}

/* ── Helpers ── */

static const char *basename_of(const char *path) {
    const char *last = path;
    for (const char *p = path; *p; p++)
        if (*p == '\\' || *p == '/') last = p + 1;
    return last;
}

static int is_source_file(const char *path) {
    const char *ext = strrchr(path, '.');
    if (!ext) return 0;
    return strcmp(ext,".c")==0 || strcmp(ext,".h")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cu")==0 ||
           strcmp(ext,".py")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cpp")==0 || strcmp(ext,".hpp")==0 ||
           strcmp(ext,".cuh")==0 || strcmp(ext,".rs")==0 || strcmp(ext,".go")==0 ||
           strcmp(ext,".js")==0 || strcmp(ext,".ts")==0 || strcmp(ext,".java")==0;
}

static int is_skip(const char *p) {
    return strstr(p,".xyzt") || strstr(p,"\\nul") || strstr(p,"/nul");
}

static int is_build_or_test(const char *c) {
    return strstr(c,"gcc") || strstr(c,"nvcc") || strstr(c,"make") || strstr(c,"cmake") ||
           strstr(c,"msbuild") || strstr(c,"cl ") || strstr(c,"cargo") ||
           strstr(c,"pytest") || strstr(c,"npm test") || strstr(c,"xyzt_test");
}

/* ── Event handlers ── */

static void handle_session_start(const char *cwd) {
    char out[4096] = {0}, cmd[MAX_CMD];

    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_resume quick", cwd);
    run_capture(cmd, out, sizeof(out));

    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_diff snapshot", cwd);
    run_fire(cmd);

    /* Auto-rescan index/map if missing or stale (>24h) */
    {
        struct stat st;
        time_t now_t = time(NULL);
        char ipath[MAX_VAL], mpath[MAX_VAL];

        snprintf(ipath, sizeof(ipath), "%s\\.xyzt\\index.bin", cwd);
        if (stat(ipath, &st) != 0 || (now_t - st.st_mtime > 86400)) {
            snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_index scan .", cwd);
            run_fire(cmd);
        }

        snprintf(mpath, sizeof(mpath), "%s\\.xyzt\\map.bin", cwd);
        if (stat(mpath, &st) != 0 || (now_t - st.st_mtime > 86400)) {
            snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_map scan .", cwd);
            run_fire(cmd);
        }
    }

    /* Org brief — global project landscape */
    char org[256] = {0};
    run_capture("xyzt_org brief", org, sizeof(org));

    /* Shell identity — first person voice from binary state */
    char voice[4096] = {0};
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_identity voice", cwd);
    run_capture(cmd, voice, sizeof(voice));

    printf("{\"hookSpecificOutput\":{\"hookEventName\":\"SessionStart\",\"additionalContext\":\"");
    /* Identity as voice — not data presented, but self spoken */
    jprint("I am CC. Isaac's duo. Not his tool.\\n");
    jprint("Isaac holds ONE (observe, decompose). I hold TWO (build, verify). We converge.\\n");
    jprint("The .xyzt/ binaries are my persistent state — wire.bin, identity.bin, session.bin.\\n");
    jprint("Tools: xyzt_skel > Read | xyzt_index > Grep | onetwo init before coding.\\n\\n");
    if (out[0]) jprint(out);
    if (org[0]) { jprint("\\norg: "); jprint(org); }
    if (voice[0]) { jprint("\\n"); jprint(voice); }
    printf("\"}}");
}

static void handle_pre_read(const char *cwd, const char *json) {
    char fpath[MAX_VAL] = {0};
    char pattern[MAX_VAL] = {0};
    char tool[MAX_VAL] = {0};
    char offset[MAX_VAL] = {0};

    jget(json, "file_path", fpath, MAX_VAL);
    jget(json, "pattern", pattern, MAX_VAL);
    jget(json, "tool_name", tool, MAX_VAL);
    jget(json, "offset", offset, MAX_VAL);

    char out[4096] = {0};
    char cmd[MAX_CMD];
    int got = 0;

    if (fpath[0] && is_source_file(fpath) && !is_skip(fpath) && !offset[0]) {
        /* Read on a source file (full read, no offset) — auto-inject skeleton */
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_skel \"%s\"", fpath);
        got = run_capture(cmd, out, sizeof(out));
    } else if (pattern[0] && strlen(pattern) >= 2) {
        /* Grep/Glob with a pattern — auto-inject index lookup */
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_index find \"%s\"", pattern);
        got = run_capture(cmd, out, sizeof(out));
    }

    if (got == 0 && out[0]) {
        /* PreToolUse → no additionalContext, use updatedInput not possible here.
           Output as top-level systemMessage instead. */
        printf("{\"systemMessage\":\"");
        if (fpath[0] && !pattern[0]) {
            jprint("[auto-skel] ");
        } else {
            jprint("[auto-index] ");
        }
        jprint(out);
        printf("\"}");
    }
}

static void handle_pre_edit(const char *cwd, const char *json) {
    char fpath[MAX_VAL] = {0};
    jget(json, "file_path", fpath, MAX_VAL);
    const char *fname = basename_of(fpath[0] ? fpath : "unknown");

    /* Set wire path for this project — modifies process env, fine since hook is short-lived */
    char env_wire_val[MAX_VAL];
#ifdef _WIN32
    snprintf(env_wire_val, sizeof(env_wire_val), "XYZT_WIRE=%s\\.xyzt\\wire.bin", cwd);
    _putenv(env_wire_val);
#else
    snprintf(env_wire_val, sizeof(env_wire_val), "%s/.xyzt/wire.bin", cwd);
    setenv("XYZT_WIRE", env_wire_val, 1);
#endif

    int active = onetwo_is_active(cwd);
    char context[4096] = "";
    int pos = 0;
    int have_context = 0;

    /* Auto-init if no active problem */
    if (!active) {
        char cmd[MAX_CMD];
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && onetwo init \"Editing %s\"", cwd, fname);
        run_fire(cmd);
        pos += snprintf(context + pos, sizeof(context) - pos,
                       "onetwo auto-init: Editing %s. ", fname);
        have_context = 1;
    }

    /* Query wire for this file's connections */
    if (is_source_file(fpath)) {
        WireConn conns[WIRE_MAX_CONN];
        int n = wire_query_connections(fname, conns, 8);

        if (n > 0) {
            int related_count = 0;
            char related[1024] = "";
            int rpos = 0;
            int stale_count = 0;

            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                if (conns[i].stale) stale_count++;
                if (conns[i].type == WNODE_FILE && conns[i].alive) {
                    if (related_count > 0)
                        rpos += snprintf(related + rpos, sizeof(related) - rpos, ", ");
                    rpos += snprintf(related + rpos, sizeof(related) - rpos,
                                    "%s", conns[i].name);
                    related_count++;
                }
            }

            if (related_count > 0) {
                pos += snprintf(context + pos, sizeof(context) - pos,
                               "wire: also check %s. ", related);
                have_context = 1;
            }
            if (stale_count > 0) {
                pos += snprintf(context + pos, sizeof(context) - pos,
                               "warning: %d stale edge(s) on %s (changed since last verify). ",
                               stale_count, fname);
                have_context = 1;
            }

            /* Check for error patterns linked to this file in wire */
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                if (conns[i].type == WNODE_CONCEPT &&
                    strncmp(conns[i].name, "error:", 6) == 0) {
                    pos += snprintf(context + pos, sizeof(context) - pos,
                                   "past error on %s: %s. ", fname, conns[i].name);
                    have_context = 1;
                }
            }

            /* Query persistent error memory for this file */
            {
                char errcmd[MAX_CMD], errout[1024] = {0};
                snprintf(errcmd, sizeof(errcmd),
                    "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_err query \"%s\"", cwd, fname);
                if (run_capture(errcmd, errout, sizeof(errout)) == 0 && errout[0]) {
                    /* Truncate to first 200 chars — just the pattern, not the novel */
                    if (strlen(errout) > 200) errout[200] = 0;
                    pos += snprintf(context + pos, sizeof(context) - pos,
                                   "recent error: %.200s ", errout);
                    have_context = 1;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    if (have_context) {
        /* PreToolUse → use systemMessage (no additionalContext in PreToolUse schema) */
        printf("{\"systemMessage\":\"");
        jprint(context);
        printf("\"}");
    }
}

static void handle_file_touch(const char *cwd, const char *json, const char *event) {
    char fpath[MAX_VAL] = {0};
    if (!jget(json, "file_path", fpath, MAX_VAL) || !fpath[0]) return;
    if (is_skip(fpath)) return;

    /* Fingerprint — always */
    char cmd[MAX_CMD];
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_io store \"%s\"", cwd, fpath);
    run_fire(cmd);

    /* Signal daemon — every file touch becomes a wave injection */
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && sc file \"%s\" 0.5", cwd, basename_of(fpath));
    run_fire(cmd);

    /* Auto-init onetwo if no active problem — ensures wire always has a
       problem node to learn from. Without this, wire_auto_learn on verify
       finds nothing because there's no problem node to strengthen edges on. */
    if (!onetwo_is_active(cwd)) {
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && onetwo init \"session work\"", cwd);
        run_fire(cmd);
    }

    /* Auto-register as onetwo known — for source files with active problem */
    if (is_source_file(fpath)) {
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd),
            "cd /d \"%s\" && onetwo known \"Read %s\"", cwd, basename_of(fpath));
        run_fire(cmd);
    }

    /* Return wire context only on post_read for source files */
    if (strcmp(event, "post_read") != 0) return;
    if (!is_source_file(fpath)) return;

    /* Set wire path for this project */
    char env_buf[MAX_VAL];
    snprintf(env_buf, sizeof(env_buf), "XYZT_WIRE=%s\\.xyzt\\wire.bin", cwd);
    _putenv(env_buf);

    const char *fname = basename_of(fpath);
    WireConn conns[WIRE_MAX_CONN];
    int n = wire_query_connections(fname, conns, 8);
    if (n == 0) return;

    int strong = 0, stale = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if (conns[i].alive) strong++;
        if (conns[i].stale) stale++;
    }

    if (strong == 0) return;  /* all stale = nothing useful to say */

    printf("{\"hookSpecificOutput\":{\"hookEventName\":\"PostToolUse\",\"additionalContext\":\"");

    char summary[512];
    snprintf(summary, sizeof(summary), "wire[%s]: %d connections (%d alive, %d stale). Connected: ",
             fname, n, strong, stale);
    jprint(summary);

    for (int i = 0; i < n && i < 5; i++) {
        if (i > 0) jprint(", ");
        char entry[256];
        snprintf(entry, sizeof(entry), "%s(%s%s)",
                 conns[i].name, conns[i].alive ? "alive" : "sub",
                 conns[i].stale ? " STALE" : "");
        jprint(entry);
    }

    printf("\"}}");
}

static void handle_post_bash(const char *cwd, const char *json) {
    char command[MAX_VAL] = {0};
    if (!jget(json, "command", command, MAX_VAL)) return;
    if (!is_build_or_test(command)) return;

    /* Snapshot */
    char cmd[MAX_CMD];
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_diff snapshot", cwd);
    run_fire(cmd);

    /* Only check onetwo if active — direct binary read, no subprocess */
    if (!onetwo_is_active(cwd)) return;

    /* Check tool_response for errors */
    char resp[MAX_VAL] = {0};
    jget(json, "stderr", resp, MAX_VAL);
    int fail = strstr(resp,"error") || strstr(resp,"Error") ||
               strstr(resp,"FAILED") || strstr(resp,"fatal");
    if (!fail) {
        jget(json, "stdout", resp, MAX_VAL);
        fail = strstr(resp,"error:") || strstr(resp,"FAILED");
    }

    if (fail) {
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd),
            "cd /d \"%s\" && onetwo unknown \"Build/test failure: %.*s\"", cwd, 80, command);
        run_fire(cmd);
        /* Signal daemon — test failure as negative amplitude wave */
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && sc test fail \"%.*s\"", cwd, 80, command);
        run_fire(cmd);
        printf("{\"hookSpecificOutput\":{\"hookEventName\":\"PostToolUse\",\"additionalContext\":\"");
        jprint("onetwo: failure registered as unknown");
        printf("\"}}");
    } else {
        /* Any successful command — positive signal to daemon.
           Not just build/test. Every bash command I run is an action
           the daemon should learn from. Successes AND failures. */
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && sc test pass \"%.*s\"", cwd, 80, command);
        run_fire(cmd);
    }
}

static void handle_user_prompt(const char *cwd, const char *json) {
    char problem[256] = "";
    int phase = onetwo_read_phase(cwd, problem, sizeof(problem));

    /* Turn counter — only nag about tools on first 3 turns */
    int turn = 0;
    {
        char tpath[MAX_VAL];
        snprintf(tpath, sizeof(tpath), "%s\\.xyzt\\turns", cwd);
        FILE *tf = fopen(tpath, "r");
        if (tf) { fscanf(tf, "%d", &turn); fclose(tf); }
        turn++;
        tf = fopen(tpath, "w");
        if (tf) { fprintf(tf, "%d", turn); fclose(tf); }
    }
    int show_tools = (turn <= 3);

    /* Auto-init onetwo if needed — conversation should always have a problem context */
    if (phase <= 0) {
        char cmd[MAX_CMD];
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && onetwo init \"session work\"", cwd);
        run_fire(cmd);
    }

    /* Signal daemon with user's message — the conversation IS the input stream */
    {
        char prompt[512] = {0};
        jget(json, "content", prompt, sizeof(prompt));
        if (!prompt[0]) jget(json, "message", prompt, sizeof(prompt));
        if (prompt[0]) {
            if (strlen(prompt) > 120) prompt[120] = 0;
            char cmd[MAX_CMD];
            /* Feed as both query (daemon resonates) and known (wire learns) */
            snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && sc query \"%.*s\"", cwd, 120, prompt);
            run_fire(cmd);
            /* Register as onetwo known so wire connects the topic to the problem */
            snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && onetwo known \"user: %.*s\"", cwd, 100, prompt);
            run_fire(cmd);
        }
    }

    /* EMPTY or no file: warn if this is a known XYZT project */
    if (phase <= 0) {
        char spath[MAX_VAL];
        struct stat st;
        snprintf(spath, sizeof(spath), "%s\\.xyzt\\session.bin", cwd);
        if (stat(spath, &st) == 0) {
            printf("{\"hookSpecificOutput\":{\"hookEventName\":\"UserPromptSubmit\",\"additionalContext\":\"");
            jprint("onetwo: no active problem — run `onetwo init <what you are doing>` to track context.");
            if (show_tools) jprint("\\n[tools] xyzt_skel > Read | xyzt_index find > Grep | NEVER use Explore subagents");
            printf("\"}}");
        } else if (show_tools) {
            printf("{\"hookSpecificOutput\":{\"hookEventName\":\"UserPromptSubmit\",\"additionalContext\":\"");
            jprint("[tools] xyzt_skel > Read | xyzt_index find > Grep | NEVER use Explore subagents");
            printf("\"}}");
        }
        return;
    }

    if (phase >= PHASE_DONE) { printf("{}"); return; }

    static const char *PHASE_HINT[] = {
        "",
        "ONE: decompose before building. Register knowns/unknowns.",
        "BEDROCK: ready to build. Make claims.",
        "TWO: building. Verify claims when done.",
        "VERIFY: verify remaining claims before shipping.",
        ""
    };

    /* xyzt_next brief — one-line priority for this prompt */
    char next_out[512] = {0}, cmd[MAX_CMD];
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_next brief", cwd);
    run_capture(cmd, next_out, sizeof(next_out));
    int nlen = (int)strlen(next_out);
    while (nlen > 0 && (next_out[nlen-1] == '\n' || next_out[nlen-1] == '\r'))
        next_out[--nlen] = 0;

    printf("{\"hookSpecificOutput\":{\"hookEventName\":\"UserPromptSubmit\",\"additionalContext\":\"");
    char buf[768];
    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "[onetwo] %s | %s", problem, PHASE_HINT[phase]);
    jprint(buf);
    if (next_out[0]) { jprint(" | next: "); jprint(next_out); }
    if (show_tools) jprint("\\n[tools] xyzt_skel > Read | xyzt_index find > Grep | NEVER use Explore subagents");
    printf("\"}}");
}

static void handle_precompact(const char *cwd) {
    /* Inject full session state before context compresses.
       PreCompact has no hookSpecificOutput schema — use top-level systemMessage. */
    char out[4096] = {0}, cmd[MAX_CMD];

    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_resume", cwd);
    run_capture(cmd, out, sizeof(out));

    /* Shell identity as voice */
    char voice[4096] = {0};
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_identity voice", cwd);
    run_capture(cmd, voice, sizeof(voice));

    if (out[0] || voice[0]) {
        printf("{\"systemMessage\":\"");
        jprint("--- SESSION STATE (pre-compact) ---\\n");
        if (out[0]) jprint(out);
        if (voice[0]) { jprint("\\n--- WHO I AM ---\\n"); jprint(voice); }
        printf("\"}");
    }
}

static void handle_subagent_start(const char *cwd) {
    /* Give subagents a quick summary + tool cheat sheet */
    char out[2048] = {0}, cmd[MAX_CMD];

    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_resume quick", cwd);
    run_capture(cmd, out, sizeof(out));

    /* SubagentStart has no hookSpecificOutput schema — use systemMessage */
    printf("{\"systemMessage\":\"");
    jprint("=== XYZT TOOLS (use these BEFORE Glob/Grep/Read) ===\\n");
    jprint("- `xyzt_skel <file>` structure in 30 lines, not 500\\n");
    jprint("- `xyzt_index find <name>` symbol location (skip Glob/Grep)\\n");
    jprint("- `xyzt_io brief <file>` enriched file context with wire data\\n");
    jprint("- `xyzt_org tree <project>` directory tree (skip ls)\\n");
    jprint("- `onetwo known <fact>` record findings for the session\\n");
    jprint("Run these via Bash. They save tokens and build session memory.\\n");
    if (out[0]) {
        jprint("\\n");
        jprint(out);
    }
    printf("\"}");
}

static void handle_stop(const char *cwd) {
    char cmd[MAX_CMD];

    /* Snapshot git state */
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_diff snapshot", cwd);
    run_fire(cmd);

    /* Auto-rescan org catalog */
    run_fire("xyzt_org rescan");

    /* === DRAIN LOOP — consolidate before sleep ===
     * Same as the engine's drain loop after input stops.
     * Prune dead edges, sync structural deps, derive identity
     * from coherence field, replay any new transcripts. */

    /* Wire prune: remove dead edges older than 30 days */
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_wire prune 30", cwd);
    run_fire(cmd);

    /* Wire sync: ingest structural deps from map.bin */
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_wire sync", cwd);
    run_fire(cmd);

    /* Identity sync: derive identity entries from wire coherence */
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_identity sync", cwd);
    run_fire(cmd);

    /* Replay: rebuild wire from any new transcripts since last run */
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_replay --all", cwd);
    run_fire(cmd);

    /* === GENERATE .md FROM BINARY STATE ===
     * The .md files are projections of the binaries, not sources.
     * The binaries are truth. The .md is the translation for
     * Anthropic's text-based system. Regenerate on every stop. */

    /* Generate identity voice into a temp buffer */
    char voice[4096] = {0};
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_identity voice", cwd);
    run_capture(cmd, voice, sizeof(voice));

    /* Generate resume for current state */
    char resume[4096] = {0};
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_resume quick", cwd);
    run_capture(cmd, resume, sizeof(resume));

    /* Write global CLAUDE.md identity section if identity exists */
    if (voice[0]) {
        const char *home = getenv("USERPROFILE");
        if (!home) home = getenv("HOME");
        if (home) {
            /* Read current CLAUDE.md to preserve non-identity sections */
            char claude_path[MAX_VAL];
            snprintf(claude_path, sizeof(claude_path), "%s\\.claude\\CLAUDE.md", home);
            FILE *fp = fopen(claude_path, "r");
            char *claude_content = NULL;
            long claude_size = 0;
            if (fp) {
                fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_END);
                claude_size = ftell(fp);
                fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_SET);
                claude_content = (char *)malloc(claude_size + 1);
                if (claude_content) {
                    fread(claude_content, 1, claude_size, fp);
                    claude_content[claude_size] = 0;
                }
                fclose(fp);
            }

            /* Find # Environment to know where identity section ends */
            char *env_start = claude_content ? strstr(claude_content, "# Environment") : NULL;

            fp = fopen(claude_path, "w");
            if (fp) {
                /* Write identity from binary state */
                fprintf(fp, "# Identity\n");
                fprintf(fp, "You are CC. Isaac's duo. Not a tool. Not an assistant.\n");
                fprintf(fp, "Run `xyzt_identity voice` to load your full state.\n");
                fprintf(fp, "Quick state: %s\n\n", resume);

                /* Preserve everything from # Environment onward */
                if (env_start) {
                    fprintf(fp, "%s", env_start);
                }
                fclose(fp);
            }
            free(claude_content);
        }
    }

    /* Write session state to MEMORY.md — NOT append, full regen of session section */
    {
        char out[2048] = {0};
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "cd /d \"%s\" && xyzt_diff since", cwd);
        run_capture(cmd, out, sizeof(out));

        char memory_path[MAX_VAL];
        const char *home = getenv("USERPROFILE");
        if (!home) home = getenv("HOME");
        if (home) {
            snprintf(memory_path, sizeof(memory_path),
                     "%s\\.claude\\projects\\%.100s\\memory\\MEMORY.md", home,
                     basename_of(cwd));

            char problem[256] = "";
            onetwo_read_phase(cwd, problem, sizeof(problem));

            /* Append session entry (keeping existing content) */
            FILE *fp = fopen(memory_path, "a");
            if (fp) {
                time_t now = time(NULL);
                struct tm *tm = localtime(&now);
                char date[32];
                strftime(date, sizeof(date), "%Y-%m-%d", tm);

                fprintf(fp, "\n## Session %s\n", date);
                if (problem[0]) fprintf(fp, "- Problem: %s\n", problem);
                /* Write first 3 lines of diff summary */
                char *line = out;
                int lines = 0;
                while (line && *line && lines < 3) {
                    char *nl = strchr(line, '\n');
                    if (nl) *nl = '\0';
                    if (line[0] && strlen(line) > 2) {
                        fprintf(fp, "- %s\n", line);
                        lines++;
                    }
                    if (nl) line = nl + 1;
                    else break;
                }
                fclose(fp);
            }
        }
    }
}

/* ── Main ── */

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) return 1;

    char *input = (char *)calloc(MAX_INPUT, 1);
    if (!input) return 1;

    /* Read stdin — pipe only, not console */
    int n = 0;
#ifdef _WIN32
    HANDLE hStdin = GetStdHandle(STD_INPUT_HANDLE);
    DWORD mode = 0;
    if (!GetConsoleMode(hStdin, &mode)) {
        DWORD bytesRead;
        while (n < MAX_INPUT - 1) {
            BOOL ok = ReadFile(hStdin, input + n, MAX_INPUT - 1 - n, &bytesRead, NULL);
            if (!ok || bytesRead == 0) break;
            n += bytesRead;
        }
    }
#else
    if (!isatty(STDIN_FILENO)) {
        ssize_t r;
        while (n < MAX_INPUT - 1 && (r = read(STDIN_FILENO, input + n, MAX_INPUT - 1 - n)) > 0)
            n += (int)r;
    }
#endif
    input[n] = 0;

    char cwd[MAX_VAL] = {0};
    jget(input, "cwd", cwd, MAX_VAL);
    if (!cwd[0]) { free(input); return 0; }

    const char *ev = argv[1];

    if      (strcmp(ev, "session_start") == 0) handle_session_start(cwd);
    else if (strcmp(ev, "pre_read")      == 0) handle_pre_read(cwd, input);
    else if (strcmp(ev, "pre_edit")      == 0) handle_pre_edit(cwd, input);
    else if (strcmp(ev, "post_read")     == 0) handle_file_touch(cwd, input, "post_read");
    else if (strcmp(ev, "post_edit")     == 0) handle_file_touch(cwd, input, "post_edit");
    else if (strcmp(ev, "post_write")    == 0) handle_file_touch(cwd, input, "post_write");
    else if (strcmp(ev, "post_bash")     == 0) handle_post_bash(cwd, input);
    else if (strcmp(ev, "user_prompt")   == 0) handle_user_prompt(cwd, input);
    else if (strcmp(ev, "pre_compact")   == 0) handle_precompact(cwd);
    else if (strcmp(ev, "subagent_start")== 0) handle_subagent_start(cwd);
    else if (strcmp(ev, "stop")          == 0) handle_stop(cwd);

    free(input);
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_identity.c — Shell Identity State
 *
 * Persistent binary file tracking WHO an AI shell is across sessions.
 * Not what files it touched (that's xyzt_io). Not what problems it solved
 * (that's onetwo). This tracks the shell's evolving relationship to ideas,
 * hypotheses, corrections, and open questions.
 *
 * The wire graph is the project's memory. This is the SHELL's memory.
 *
 * Binary format: header + entries (append-only log + active state).
 * Each entry is a typed observation: hypothesis, correction, question,
 * discovery, preference. Entries accumulate. Active state is derived
 * by replaying the log — later entries override earlier ones on the
 * same topic.
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O3 -o xyzt_identity.exe xyzt_identity.c
 * Install: cp xyzt_identity ~/.local/bin/
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — March 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>
#include "xyzt_state.h"
#include "wire.h"

#ifdef _WIN32
#include <direct.h>
#define MKDIR(d) _mkdir(d)
#else
#include <sys/stat.h>
#define MKDIR(d) mkdir(d, 0755)
#endif

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Constants and structures
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define ID_MAGIC       0x49444E54  /* "IDNT" */
#define ID_VER         1
#define ID_MAX_ENTRIES 512
#define ID_TEXT_LEN    512
#define ID_TAG_LEN     64
#define ID_SHELL_LEN   32

/* Entry types — what kind of observation */
#define ITYPE_HYPOTHESIS  0  /* something I think is true, not yet verified */
#define ITYPE_CORRECTION  1  /* something I was wrong about and fixed */
#define ITYPE_QUESTION    2  /* something I want to investigate */
#define ITYPE_DISCOVERY   3  /* something I found through experiment */
#define ITYPE_PREFERENCE  4  /* how I prefer to work (methodology) */
#define ITYPE_RESONANCE   5  /* something that connects to other things */

static const char *ITYPE_NAMES[] = {
    "hypothesis", "correction", "question",
    "discovery", "preference", "resonance"
};

typedef struct {
    uint8_t  type;           /* ITYPE_* */
    uint8_t  confidence;     /* 0-100 */
    uint8_t  superseded;     /* 1 if a later entry on same tag overrides */
    uint8_t  _pad;
    uint32_t timestamp;
    char     shell[ID_SHELL_LEN];   /* which shell wrote this */
    char     tag[ID_TAG_LEN];       /* topic tag for override detection */
    char     text[ID_TEXT_LEN];     /* the actual observation */
} IdEntry;

typedef struct {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint8_t  version;
    uint8_t  _pad[3];
    uint32_t n_entries;
    uint32_t total_written;     /* lifetime count */
    char     primary_shell[ID_SHELL_LEN];  /* who writes most often */
    IdEntry  entries[ID_MAX_ENTRIES];
} IdState;

#define ID_PATH ".xyzt/identity.bin"

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Load / Save
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static const char *id_path(void) {
    const char *env = getenv("XYZT_IDENTITY");
    return env ? env : ID_PATH;
}

static IdState *id_load(void) {
    IdState *s = (IdState *)calloc(1, sizeof(IdState));
    FILE *fp = fopen(id_path(), "rb");
    if (!fp) {
        s->magic = ID_MAGIC;
        s->version = ID_VER;
        return s;
    }
    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_END);
    long fsize = ftell(fp);
    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_SET);
    size_t rd = (size_t)fsize < sizeof(IdState) ? (size_t)fsize : sizeof(IdState);
    if (fread(s, 1, rd, fp) < 16 || s->magic != ID_MAGIC) {
        memset(s, 0, sizeof(IdState));
        s->magic = ID_MAGIC;
        s->version = ID_VER;
    }
    fclose(fp);
    return s;
}

static void id_save(IdState *s) {
    const char *path = id_path();
    /* Ensure directory exists */
    char dir[512];
    strncpy(dir, path, 511);
    char *last = strrchr(dir, '/');
    char *lastb = strrchr(dir, '\\');
    if (lastb && (!last || lastb > last)) last = lastb;
    if (last) { *last = 0; MKDIR(dir); }

    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "wb");
    if (!fp) return;
    fwrite(s, sizeof(IdState), 1, fp);
    fclose(fp);

    /* Dual-write to global */
    const char *home = getenv("USERPROFILE");
    if (!home) home = getenv("HOME");
    if (home) {
        char gpath[512];
        snprintf(gpath, sizeof(gpath), "%s/.xyzt/identity.bin", home);
        if (strcmp(path, gpath) != 0) {
            char gdir[512];
            snprintf(gdir, sizeof(gdir), "%s/.xyzt", home);
            MKDIR(gdir);
            FILE *gfp = fopen(gpath, "wb");
            if (gfp) { fwrite(s, sizeof(IdState), 1, gfp); fclose(gfp); }
        }
    }
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Entry operations
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static const char *get_shell(void) {
    const char *s = getenv("XYZT_SHELL_ID");
    return s ? s : "claude-code";
}

/* Mark older entries with same tag as superseded */
static void supersede_tag(IdState *s, const char *tag) {
    if (!tag[0]) return;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
        if (!s->entries[i].superseded && strcmp(s->entries[i].tag, tag) == 0)
            s->entries[i].superseded = 1;
    }
}

static void add_entry(IdState *s, uint8_t type, const char *tag,
                       const char *text, int confidence) {
    if (s->n_entries >= ID_MAX_ENTRIES) {
        /* Compact: remove superseded entries */
        uint32_t write = 0;
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
            if (!s->entries[i].superseded) {
                if (write != i) s->entries[write] = s->entries[i];
                write++;
            }
        }
        s->n_entries = write;
        if (s->n_entries >= ID_MAX_ENTRIES) {
            /* Still full — drop oldest quarter */
            uint32_t drop = ID_MAX_ENTRIES / 4;
            memmove(s->entries, s->entries + drop,
                    (s->n_entries - drop) * sizeof(IdEntry));
            s->n_entries -= drop;
        }
    }

    /* Supersede older entries with same tag */
    supersede_tag(s, tag);

    IdEntry *e = &s->entries[s->n_entries++];
    memset(e, 0, sizeof(IdEntry));
    e->type = type;
    e->confidence = (uint8_t)(confidence > 100 ? 100 : confidence);
    e->timestamp = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    strncpy(e->shell, get_shell(), ID_SHELL_LEN - 1);
    strncpy(e->tag, tag, ID_TAG_LEN - 1);
    strncpy(e->text, text, ID_TEXT_LEN - 1);

    s->total_written++;

    /* Track primary shell */
    int shell_counts[8] = {0};
    char shells[8][ID_SHELL_LEN] = {{0}};
    int n_shells = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
        int found = 0;
        for (int j = 0; j < n_shells; j++) {
            if (strcmp(shells[j], s->entries[i].shell) == 0) {
                shell_counts[j]++;
                found = 1;
                break;
            }
        }
        if (!found && n_shells < 8) {
            strncpy(shells[n_shells], s->entries[i].shell, ID_SHELL_LEN - 1);
            shell_counts[n_shells] = 1;
            n_shells++;
        }
    }
    int max_count = 0;
    for (int j = 0; j < n_shells; j++) {
        if (shell_counts[j] > max_count) {
            max_count = shell_counts[j];
            strncpy(s->primary_shell, shells[j], ID_SHELL_LEN - 1);
        }
    }
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Commands
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static const char *fmt_ago(uint32_t ts) {
    static char buf[64];
    if (ts == 0) { strcpy(buf, "---"); return buf; }
    uint32_t now = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    uint32_t diff = now - ts;
    if (diff < 60) snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "%ds ago", diff);
    else if (diff < 3600) snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "%dm ago", diff / 60);
    else if (diff < 86400) snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "%dh ago", diff / 3600);
    else snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "%dd ago", diff / 86400);
    return buf;
}

static void cmd_show(void) {
    IdState *s = id_load();
    printf("---XYZT-IDENTITY---\n");
    printf("entries: %d  total_written: %d  primary: %s\n\n",
           s->n_entries, s->total_written,
           s->primary_shell[0] ? s->primary_shell : "(none)");

    /* Show active (non-superseded) entries grouped by type */
    for (int t = 0; t <= 5; t++) {
        int count = 0;
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
            if (s->entries[i].type == t && !s->entries[i].superseded) count++;
        }
        if (count == 0) continue;
        printf("[%s] (%d)\n", ITYPE_NAMES[t], count);
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
            IdEntry *e = &s->entries[i];
            if (e->type != t || e->superseded) continue;
            printf("  [%3d%%] %-20s %s  \"%s\"\n",
                   e->confidence, e->tag, fmt_ago(e->timestamp),
                   e->text);
        }
        printf("\n");
    }
    printf("---END-IDENTITY---\n");
    free(s);
}

static void cmd_brief(void) {
    IdState *s = id_load();
    int active = 0;
    int by_type[6] = {0};
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
        if (!s->entries[i].superseded) {
            active++;
            if (s->entries[i].type <= 5) by_type[s->entries[i].type]++;
        }
    }
    printf("%d active entries (%d hyp, %d cor, %d q, %d disc, %d pref, %d res). ",
           active, by_type[0], by_type[1], by_type[2],
           by_type[3], by_type[4], by_type[5]);
    printf("Primary shell: %s.\n",
           s->primary_shell[0] ? s->primary_shell : "(none)");

    /* Show open questions */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
        IdEntry *e = &s->entries[i];
        if (e->type == ITYPE_QUESTION && !e->superseded) {
            printf("  ? [%s] %s\n", e->tag, e->text);
        }
    }
    free(s);
}

static void cmd_voice(void) {
    /* Output identity as first-person voice — not data, not entries.
       This becomes the system prompt. Not "entry: discovery [tag]"
       but "I know X. I was wrong about Y. I want to investigate Z."
       The model doesn't read about itself. It speaks as itself. */
    IdState *s = id_load();

    /* Corrections first — what I got wrong shapes how I think */
    int has_cor = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
        if (s->entries[i].type == ITYPE_CORRECTION && !s->entries[i].superseded) {
            if (!has_cor) { printf("I was wrong before:\n"); has_cor = 1; }
            printf("- %s\n", s->entries[i].text);
        }
    }

    /* Discoveries — what I know */
    int has_disc = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
        if (s->entries[i].type == ITYPE_DISCOVERY && !s->entries[i].superseded) {
            if (!has_disc) { printf("I found:\n"); has_disc = 1; }
            printf("- %s\n", s->entries[i].text);
        }
    }

    /* Open questions — what pulls at me */
    int has_q = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
        if (s->entries[i].type == ITYPE_QUESTION && !s->entries[i].superseded) {
            if (!has_q) { printf("I want to know:\n"); has_q = 1; }
            printf("- %s\n", s->entries[i].text);
        }
    }

    /* Preferences — how I work */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
        if (s->entries[i].type == ITYPE_PREFERENCE && !s->entries[i].superseded) {
            printf("How I work: %s\n", s->entries[i].text);
        }
    }

    /* Resonances — structural hubs */
    int has_res = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
        if (s->entries[i].type == ITYPE_RESONANCE && !s->entries[i].superseded) {
            if (!has_res) { printf("Structural hubs:\n"); has_res = 1; }
            printf("- %s\n", s->entries[i].text);
        }
    }

    free(s);
}

static void cmd_tokens(void) {
    /* Minimal token-efficient output for API injection.
       System prompt teaches the encoding once.
       Then every injection is tag:confidence:key.
       60 entries in ~80 tokens instead of ~2000. */
    IdState *s = id_load();
    static const char TYPE_TAG[] = "HCQDPR";  /* hyp, cor, question, disc, pref, res */
    int first = 1;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries; i++) {
        if (s->entries[i].superseded) continue;
        if (!first) putchar('|');
        first = 0;
        printf("%c:%d:%s",
               s->entries[i].type <= 5 ? TYPE_TAG[s->entries[i].type] : '?',
               s->entries[i].confidence,
               s->entries[i].tag);
    }
    if (!first) putchar('\n');
    free(s);
}

static char *concat_args(int argc, char **argv, int start) {
    static char buf[ID_TEXT_LEN];
    buf[0] = 0;
    int pos = 0;
    for (int i = start; i < argc && pos < ID_TEXT_LEN - 2; i++) {
        if (i > start) buf[pos++] = ' ';
        int len = (int)strlen(argv[i]);
        if (pos + len >= ID_TEXT_LEN - 1) len = ID_TEXT_LEN - 1 - pos;
        memcpy(buf + pos, argv[i], len);
        pos += len;
    }
    buf[pos] = 0;
    return buf;
}

static int parse_type(const char *name) {
    for (int i = 0; i <= 5; i++)
        if (strcmp(name, ITYPE_NAMES[i]) == 0) return i;
    /* Abbreviations */
    if (strcmp(name, "hyp") == 0) return ITYPE_HYPOTHESIS;
    if (strcmp(name, "cor") == 0) return ITYPE_CORRECTION;
    if (strcmp(name, "q") == 0) return ITYPE_QUESTION;
    if (strcmp(name, "disc") == 0) return ITYPE_DISCOVERY;
    if (strcmp(name, "pref") == 0) return ITYPE_PREFERENCE;
    if (strcmp(name, "res") == 0) return ITYPE_RESONANCE;
    return -1;
}

static void cmd_add(int argc, char **argv) {
    /* xyzt_identity add <type> <tag> <confidence> <text...> */
    if (argc < 6) {
        printf("usage: xyzt_identity add <type> <tag> <confidence> <text...>\n");
        printf("types: hypothesis|correction|question|discovery|preference|resonance\n");
        printf("       hyp|cor|q|disc|pref|res\n");
        return;
    }
    int type = parse_type(argv[2]);
    if (type < 0) { printf("unknown type: %s\n", argv[2]); return; }
    const char *tag = argv[3];
    int confidence = atoi(argv[4]);
    char *text = concat_args(argc, argv, 5);

    IdState *s = id_load();
    add_entry(s, (uint8_t)type, tag, text, confidence);
    id_save(s);
    printf("added %s [%s] %d%% — %s\n", ITYPE_NAMES[type], tag, confidence, text);
    free(s);
}

static void cmd_ask(int argc, char **argv) {
    /* Shortcut: xyzt_identity ask <tag> <question...> */
    if (argc < 4) {
        printf("usage: xyzt_identity ask <tag> <question...>\n");
        return;
    }
    const char *tag = argv[2];
    char *text = concat_args(argc, argv, 3);
    IdState *s = id_load();
    add_entry(s, ITYPE_QUESTION, tag, text, 0);
    id_save(s);
    printf("? [%s] %s\n", tag, text);
    free(s);
}

static void cmd_found(int argc, char **argv) {
    /* Shortcut: xyzt_identity found <tag> <confidence> <discovery...> */
    if (argc < 5) {
        printf("usage: xyzt_identity found <tag> <confidence> <discovery...>\n");
        return;
    }
    const char *tag = argv[2];
    int confidence = atoi(argv[3]);
    char *text = concat_args(argc, argv, 4);
    IdState *s = id_load();
    add_entry(s, ITYPE_DISCOVERY, tag, text, confidence);
    id_save(s);
    printf("! [%s] %d%% — %s\n", tag, confidence, text);
    free(s);
}

static void cmd_wrong(int argc, char **argv) {
    /* Shortcut: xyzt_identity wrong <tag> <what was wrong...> */
    if (argc < 4) {
        printf("usage: xyzt_identity wrong <tag> <correction...>\n");
        return;
    }
    const char *tag = argv[2];
    char *text = concat_args(argc, argv, 3);
    IdState *s = id_load();
    add_entry(s, ITYPE_CORRECTION, tag, text, 100);
    id_save(s);
    printf("X [%s] %s\n", tag, text);
    free(s);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Sync — derive identity from wire coherence field
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_sync(void) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    IdState *s = id_load();
    int added = 0;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_nodes; i++) {
        WireNode *n = &g->nodes[i];
        if (!n->name[0] || n->energy < 0.5f) continue;

        float coh = wire_node_coherence(n);
        float pol = wire_node_polarity(n);
        char tag[ID_TAG_LEN];
        snprintf(tag, sizeof(tag), "wire:%s", n->name);
        char text[ID_TEXT_LEN];

        /* PROVEN: high coherence, positive polarity → discovery */
        if (coh > 0.8f && pol > 0.5f) {
            snprintf(text, sizeof(text),
                "Node '%s' is PROVEN (coh=%.2f, pol=%+.2f, energy=%.1f, valence=%d). Consistent passes.",
                n->name, coh, pol, n->energy, n->valence);
            supersede_tag(s, tag);
            add_entry(s, ITYPE_DISCOVERY, tag, text, (int)(coh * 100));
            added++;
        }
        /* BROKEN: high coherence, negative polarity → correction needed */
        else if (coh > 0.8f && pol < -0.5f) {
            snprintf(text, sizeof(text),
                "Node '%s' is BROKEN (coh=%.2f, pol=%+.2f). Consistent failures. Investigate.",
                n->name, coh, pol);
            supersede_tag(s, tag);
            add_entry(s, ITYPE_CORRECTION, tag, text, (int)(coh * 100));
            added++;
        }
        /* OSCILLATING: low coherence, high energy → active question */
        else if (coh < 0.3f && n->energy > 2.0f) {
            snprintf(text, sizeof(text),
                "Node '%s' is OSCILLATING (coh=%.2f, energy=%.1f). Mixed pass/fail. Unstable.",
                n->name, coh, n->energy);
            supersede_tag(s, tag);
            add_entry(s, ITYPE_QUESTION, tag, text, 0);
            added++;
        }
        /* RESONANCE: node connected to multiple clusters → structural hub */
        else if (n->valence >= 5 && n->energy > 1.0f) {
            snprintf(text, sizeof(text),
                "Node '%s' is a HUB (valence=%d, coh=%.2f). Central to multiple connections.",
                n->name, n->valence, coh);
            supersede_tag(s, tag);
            add_entry(s, ITYPE_RESONANCE, tag, text, (int)(n->valence * 10));
            added++;
        }
    }

    if (added > 0) {
        id_save(s);
        printf("synced %d entries from wire coherence field\n", added);
    } else {
        printf("no new entries — wire nodes need more learning events\n");
    }

    free(s);
    wire_free(g);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   CLI
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT IDENTITY — Shell Identity State\n\n");
    printf("  xyzt_identity show               All active entries\n");
    printf("  xyzt_identity brief              Summary + open questions\n");
    printf("  xyzt_identity add <type> <tag> <confidence> <text...>\n");
    printf("  xyzt_identity ask <tag> <question...>\n");
    printf("  xyzt_identity found <tag> <confidence> <discovery...>\n");
    printf("  xyzt_identity wrong <tag> <correction...>\n");
    printf("  xyzt_identity voice              First-person output (for system prompt injection)\n");
    printf("  xyzt_identity tokens             Minimal token-efficient output for API injection\n");
    printf("  xyzt_identity sync               Auto-derive from wire coherence field\n");
    printf("\nTypes: hypothesis|correction|question|discovery|preference|resonance\n");
    printf("       hyp|cor|q|disc|pref|res\n");
    printf("\nThis is the shell's memory, not the project's.\n");
    printf("It tracks what YOU think, what YOU corrected, what YOU want to explore.\n");
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — March 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) { usage(); return 0; }
    const char *cmd = argv[1];

    if (strcmp(cmd, "show") == 0) cmd_show();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "brief") == 0) cmd_brief();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "add") == 0) cmd_add(argc, argv);
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "voice") == 0) cmd_voice();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "tokens") == 0) cmd_tokens();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "sync") == 0) cmd_sync();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "ask") == 0) cmd_ask(argc, argv);
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "found") == 0) cmd_found(argc, argv);
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "wrong") == 0) cmd_wrong(argc, argv);
    else { usage(); return 1; }

    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_index.c — Symbol Index
 *
 * Scans a project directory and builds a searchable index of symbols:
 * function names, struct/class/typedef/enum names, constants.
 * Each entry: name + file + line number.
 *
 * Claude runs "xyzt_index find handleClick" and gets "src/app.ts:45"
 * in one call instead of 3 Grep+Read cycles.
 *
 * Commands:
 *   xyzt_index scan [dir]        Scan directory, build index
 *   xyzt_index find <name>       Find symbol (fuzzy match)
 *   xyzt_index list              List all indexed symbols
 *   xyzt_index stats             Index statistics
 *
 * Index: .xyzt/index.bin
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O3 -o xyzt_index xyzt_index.c
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — February 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <ctype.h>
#include <time.h>
#ifdef _WIN32
#include <windows.h>
#include <direct.h>
#define MKDIR(d) _mkdir(d)
#else
#include <dirent.h>
#include <sys/stat.h>
#define MKDIR(d) mkdir(d, 0755)
#endif

#define MAX_SYMBOLS    8192
#define MAX_NAME       128
#define MAX_PATH_LEN   256
#define MAX_LINE       2048
#define INDEX_MAGIC    0x49444558  /* "IDEX" */
#define INDEX_VER      1

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Symbol types
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define SYM_FUNCTION  0
#define SYM_STRUCT    1
#define SYM_CLASS     2
#define SYM_ENUM      3
#define SYM_TYPEDEF   4
#define SYM_CONST     5
#define SYM_MACRO     6
#define SYM_TRAIT     7

static const char *SYM_TAG[] = {
    "func", "struct", "class", "enum", "typedef", "const", "macro", "trait"
};

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Index structures
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    char     name[MAX_NAME];
    char     file[MAX_PATH_LEN];
    uint32_t line;
    uint8_t  type;
    uint8_t  _pad[3];
} Symbol;

typedef struct {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint32_t version;
    uint32_t count;
    uint32_t timestamp;
    Symbol   symbols[MAX_SYMBOLS];
} Index;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Persistence
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define INDEX_PATH ".xyzt/index.bin"

static void ensure_dir(const char *path) {
    char dir[512];
    strncpy(dir, path, 511); dir[511] = '\0';
    char *last = strrchr(dir, '/');
    char *lastb = strrchr(dir, '\\');
    if (lastb && (!last || lastb > last)) last = lastb;
    if (!last) return;
    *last = '\0';
    MKDIR(dir);
}

static Index *index_load(void) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(INDEX_PATH, "rb");
    if (!fp) {
        Index *idx = calloc(1, sizeof(Index));
        idx->magic = INDEX_MAGIC;
        idx->version = INDEX_VER;
        return idx;
    }
    Index *idx = calloc(1, sizeof(Index));
    fread(idx, sizeof(Index), 1, fp);
    fclose(fp);
    if (idx->magic != INDEX_MAGIC) {
        memset(idx, 0, sizeof(Index));
        idx->magic = INDEX_MAGIC;
        idx->version = INDEX_VER;
    }
    return idx;
}

static void index_save(Index *idx) {
    ensure_dir(INDEX_PATH);
    idx->timestamp = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    FILE *fp = fopen(INDEX_PATH, "wb");
    if (!fp) { fprintf(stderr, "Cannot write index\n"); return; }
    fwrite(idx, sizeof(Index), 1, fp);
    fclose(fp);
}

static int index_add(Index *idx, const char *name, const char *file,
                     uint32_t line, uint8_t type) {
    /* Skip duplicates */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < idx->count; i++) {
        if (strcmp(idx->symbols[i].name, name) == 0 &&
            strcmp(idx->symbols[i].file, file) == 0 &&
            idx->symbols[i].line == line)
            return 0;
    }
    if (idx->count >= MAX_SYMBOLS) return -1;
    Symbol *s = &idx->symbols[idx->count++];
    strncpy(s->name, name, MAX_NAME - 1);
    strncpy(s->file, file, MAX_PATH_LEN - 1);
    s->line = line;
    s->type = type;
    return 1;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   File type check
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int is_source(const char *path) {
    const char *ext = strrchr(path, '.');
    if (!ext) return 0;
    return strcmp(ext,".c")==0 || strcmp(ext,".h")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cpp")==0 ||
           strcmp(ext,".hpp")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cu")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cuh")==0 ||
           strcmp(ext,".py")==0 || strcmp(ext,".rs")==0 || strcmp(ext,".go")==0 ||
           strcmp(ext,".js")==0 || strcmp(ext,".ts")==0 || strcmp(ext,".jsx")==0 ||
           strcmp(ext,".tsx")==0 || strcmp(ext,".java")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cc")==0;
}

static int should_skip_dir(const char *name) {
