    return strcmp(name, ".") == 0 || strcmp(name, "..") == 0 ||
           strcmp(name, ".git") == 0 || strcmp(name, ".xyzt") == 0 ||
           strcmp(name, "node_modules") == 0 || strcmp(name, "target") == 0 ||
           strcmp(name, "__pycache__") == 0 || strcmp(name, ".venv") == 0 ||
           strcmp(name, "build") == 0 || strcmp(name, "dist") == 0;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Symbol extraction — extract identifier after keyword
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Extract the next identifier (word) after position `start` */
static int extract_ident(const char *line, int start, char *out, int max) {
    int i = start;
    /* Skip whitespace */
    while (line[i] && (line[i] == ' ' || line[i] == '\t')) i++;
    /* Skip pointer stars */
    while (line[i] == '*') i++;
    /* Read identifier */
    int n = 0;
    while (line[i] && (isalnum(line[i]) || line[i] == '_') && n < max - 1) {
        out[n++] = line[i++];
    }
    out[n] = '\0';
    return n;
}

static void scan_file(Index *idx, const char *filepath, const char *relpath) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(filepath, "r");
    if (!fp) return;

    const char *ext = strrchr(filepath, '.');
    int is_c = ext && (strcmp(ext,".c")==0 || strcmp(ext,".h")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cpp")==0 ||
                       strcmp(ext,".hpp")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cu")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cuh")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cc")==0);
    int is_py = ext && strcmp(ext,".py")==0;
    int is_rs = ext && strcmp(ext,".rs")==0;
    int is_js = ext && (strcmp(ext,".js")==0 || strcmp(ext,".ts")==0 || strcmp(ext,".jsx")==0 || strcmp(ext,".tsx")==0);
    int is_go = ext && strcmp(ext,".go")==0;
    int is_java = ext && strcmp(ext,".java")==0;

    char line[MAX_LINE];
    int lineno = 0;
    char name[MAX_NAME];

    while (fgets(line, MAX_LINE, fp)) {
        lineno++;
        /* Strip newline */
        int len = strlen(line);
        while (len > 0 && (line[len-1] == '\n' || line[len-1] == '\r')) line[--len] = '\0';

        const char *t = line;
        while (*t == ' ' || *t == '\t') t++;
        int indent = (int)(t - line);

        if (is_c) {
            /* #define NAME */
            if (strncmp(t, "#define ", 8) == 0) {
                if (extract_ident(t, 8, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_MACRO);
            }
            /* typedef ... NAME; or typedef struct { ... } NAME; */
            else if (strncmp(t, "typedef ", 8) == 0) {
                /* Find last word before ; or { */
                const char *semi = strrchr(t, ';');
                const char *brace = strchr(t, '{');
                if (semi && !brace) {
                    /* typedef int Foo; → extract Foo */
                    int pos = (int)(semi - t) - 1;
                    while (pos > 0 && t[pos] == ' ') pos--;
                    int end = pos + 1;
                    while (pos > 0 && (isalnum(t[pos]) || t[pos] == '_')) pos--;
                    if (!isalnum(t[pos]) && t[pos] != '_') pos++;
                    int nlen = end - pos;
                    if (nlen > 0 && nlen < MAX_NAME) {
                        memcpy(name, t + pos, nlen);
                        name[nlen] = '\0';
                        index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_TYPEDEF);
                    }
                }
            }
            /* struct/union/enum NAME */
            else if (indent == 0 && (strncmp(t, "struct ", 7) == 0)) {
                if (extract_ident(t, 7, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_STRUCT);
            }
            else if (indent == 0 && strncmp(t, "enum ", 5) == 0) {
                if (extract_ident(t, 5, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_ENUM);
            }
            /* Function: top-level, has ( but not #define, not typedef */
            else if (indent == 0 && t[0] != '#' && t[0] != '/' && t[0] != '*' &&
                     strchr(t, '(') && !strchr(t, ';')) {
                /* Extract function name: word before ( */
                const char *paren = strchr(t, '(');
                if (paren > t + 1) {
                    int pos = (int)(paren - t) - 1;
                    while (pos > 0 && t[pos] == ' ') pos--;
                    int end = pos + 1;
                    while (pos > 0 && (isalnum(t[pos]) || t[pos] == '_')) pos--;
                    if (!isalnum(t[pos]) && t[pos] != '_') pos++;
                    int nlen = end - pos;
                    if (nlen > 1 && nlen < MAX_NAME) {
                        memcpy(name, t + pos, nlen);
                        name[nlen] = '\0';
                        /* Filter out common non-function patterns */
                        if (strcmp(name, "if") != 0 && strcmp(name, "for") != 0 &&
                            strcmp(name, "while") != 0 && strcmp(name, "switch") != 0)
                            index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_FUNCTION);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        else if (is_py) {
            if (strncmp(t, "def ", 4) == 0 && indent <= 4) {
                if (extract_ident(t, 4, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_FUNCTION);
            }
            else if (strncmp(t, "class ", 6) == 0) {
                if (extract_ident(t, 6, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_CLASS);
            }
        }
        else if (is_rs) {
            if (strncmp(t, "fn ", 3) == 0 || strncmp(t, "pub fn ", 7) == 0 ||
                strncmp(t, "pub async fn ", 13) == 0 || strncmp(t, "async fn ", 9) == 0) {
                const char *fn = strstr(t, "fn ");
                if (fn && extract_ident(fn, 3, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_FUNCTION);
            }
            else if (strncmp(t, "struct ", 7) == 0 || strncmp(t, "pub struct ", 11) == 0) {
                const char *s = strstr(t, "struct ");
                if (s && extract_ident(s, 7, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_STRUCT);
            }
            else if (strncmp(t, "enum ", 5) == 0 || strncmp(t, "pub enum ", 9) == 0) {
                const char *e = strstr(t, "enum ");
                if (e && extract_ident(e, 5, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_ENUM);
            }
            else if (strncmp(t, "trait ", 6) == 0 || strncmp(t, "pub trait ", 10) == 0) {
                const char *tr = strstr(t, "trait ");
                if (tr && extract_ident(tr, 6, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_TRAIT);
            }
            else if (strncmp(t, "impl ", 5) == 0) {
                if (extract_ident(t, 5, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_CLASS);
            }
        }
        else if (is_js) {
            if (strncmp(t, "function ", 9) == 0) {
                if (extract_ident(t, 9, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_FUNCTION);
            }
            else if (strncmp(t, "async function ", 15) == 0) {
                if (extract_ident(t, 15, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_FUNCTION);
            }
            else if (strncmp(t, "class ", 6) == 0) {
                if (extract_ident(t, 6, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_CLASS);
            }
            else if (indent == 0 && (strncmp(t, "const ", 6) == 0 ||
                     strncmp(t, "export const ", 13) == 0)) {
                const char *c = strstr(t, "const ");
                if (c) {
                    if (extract_ident(c, 6, name, MAX_NAME) > 1) {
                        if (strstr(t, "=>") || strstr(t, "= function"))
                            index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_FUNCTION);
                        else
                            index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_CONST);
                    }
                }
            }
            else if (strncmp(t, "interface ", 10) == 0 ||
                     strncmp(t, "export interface ", 17) == 0) {
                const char *iface = strstr(t, "interface ");
                if (iface && extract_ident(iface, 10, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_STRUCT);
            }
        }
        else if (is_go) {
            if (strncmp(t, "func ", 5) == 0) {
                /* Could be func (r Receiver) Name or func Name */
                const char *paren = strchr(t + 5, '(');
                const char *space = strchr(t + 5, ' ');
                if (t[5] == '(' && space) {
                    /* Method: func (r Type) Name( */
                    const char *close = strchr(t + 5, ')');
                    if (close && extract_ident(close, 1, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                        index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_FUNCTION);
                } else {
                    if (extract_ident(t, 5, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                        index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_FUNCTION);
                }
            }
            else if (strncmp(t, "type ", 5) == 0) {
                if (extract_ident(t, 5, name, MAX_NAME) > 1) {
                    if (strstr(t, "struct"))
                        index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_STRUCT);
                    else if (strstr(t, "interface"))
                        index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_CLASS);
                    else
                        index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_TYPEDEF);
                }
            }
        }
        else if (is_java) {
            if (strstr(t, "class ") && indent <= 4) {
                const char *c = strstr(t, "class ");
                if (c && extract_ident(c, 6, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_CLASS);
            }
            else if (strstr(t, "interface ") && indent <= 4) {
                const char *i = strstr(t, "interface ");
                if (i && extract_ident(i, 10, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_STRUCT);
            }
            else if (strstr(t, "enum ") && indent <= 4) {
                const char *e = strstr(t, "enum ");
                if (e && extract_ident(e, 5, name, MAX_NAME) > 1)
                    index_add(idx, name, relpath, lineno, SYM_ENUM);
            }
        }
    }
    fclose(fp);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Directory traversal (Win32)
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#ifdef _WIN32
static void scan_dir(Index *idx, const char *basedir, const char *reldir) {
    char pattern[MAX_PATH_LEN];
    if (reldir[0])
        snprintf(pattern, sizeof(pattern), "%s\\%s\\*", basedir, reldir);
    else
        snprintf(pattern, sizeof(pattern), "%s\\*", basedir);

    WIN32_FIND_DATAA fd;
    HANDLE hFind = FindFirstFileA(pattern, &fd);
    if (hFind == INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE) return;

    do {
        if (should_skip_dir(fd.cFileName)) continue;

        char fullpath[MAX_PATH_LEN];
        char relpath[MAX_PATH_LEN];
        if (reldir[0]) {
            snprintf(fullpath, sizeof(fullpath), "%s\\%s\\%s", basedir, reldir, fd.cFileName);
            snprintf(relpath, sizeof(relpath), "%s\\%s", reldir, fd.cFileName);
        } else {
            snprintf(fullpath, sizeof(fullpath), "%s\\%s", basedir, fd.cFileName);
            snprintf(relpath, sizeof(relpath), "%s", fd.cFileName);
        }

        if (fd.dwFileAttributes & FILE_ATTRIBUTE_DIRECTORY) {
            scan_dir(idx, basedir, relpath);
        } else if (is_source(fd.cFileName)) {
            scan_file(idx, fullpath, relpath);
        }
    } while (FindNextFileA(hFind, &fd));
    FindClose(hFind);
}
#else
static void scan_dir(Index *idx, const char *basedir, const char *reldir) {
    char path[MAX_PATH_LEN];
    if (reldir[0])
        snprintf(path, sizeof(path), "%s/%s", basedir, reldir);
    else
        snprintf(path, sizeof(path), "%s", basedir);

    DIR *dir = opendir(path);
    if (!dir) return;

    struct dirent *entry;
    while ((entry = readdir(dir))) {
        if (should_skip_dir(entry->d_name)) continue;

        char fullpath[MAX_PATH_LEN];
        char relpath[MAX_PATH_LEN];
        if (reldir[0]) {
            snprintf(fullpath, sizeof(fullpath), "%s/%s/%s", basedir, reldir, entry->d_name);
            snprintf(relpath, sizeof(relpath), "%s/%s", reldir, entry->d_name);
        } else {
            snprintf(fullpath, sizeof(fullpath), "%s/%s", basedir, entry->d_name);
            snprintf(relpath, sizeof(relpath), "%s", entry->d_name);
        }

        struct stat st;
        if (stat(fullpath, &st) != 0) continue;
        if (S_ISDIR(st.st_mode)) {
            scan_dir(idx, basedir, relpath);
        } else if (is_source(entry->d_name)) {
            scan_file(idx, fullpath, relpath);
        }
    }
    closedir(dir);
}
#endif

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Commands
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_scan(const char *dir) {
    Index *idx = calloc(1, sizeof(Index));
    idx->magic = INDEX_MAGIC;
    idx->version = INDEX_VER;

    printf("Scanning %s...\n", dir);
    scan_dir(idx, dir, "");

    index_save(idx);
    printf("---XYZT-INDEX---\n");
    printf("indexed: %u symbols from %s\n", idx->count, dir);

    /* Count by type */
    int counts[8] = {0};
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < idx->count; i++) {
        if (idx->symbols[i].type < 8) counts[idx->symbols[i].type]++;
    }
    for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
        if (counts[i] > 0) printf("  %-8s %d\n", SYM_TAG[i], counts[i]);
    }
    printf("---END-INDEX---\n");
    free(idx);
}

static void cmd_find(const char *query) {
    Index *idx = index_load();
    if (idx->count == 0) {
        printf("Index empty. Run: xyzt_index scan [dir]\n");
        free(idx);
        return;
    }

    /* Exact match first, then substring match */
    printf("---XYZT-INDEX-FIND---\n");
    printf("query: %s\n", query);

    int found = 0;
    /* Pass 1: exact match */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < idx->count; i++) {
        if (strcmp(idx->symbols[i].name, query) == 0) {
            printf("  %-8s %s:%u  %s\n", SYM_TAG[idx->symbols[i].type],
                   idx->symbols[i].file, idx->symbols[i].line,
                   idx->symbols[i].name);
            found++;
        }
    }

    /* Pass 2: case-insensitive substring match (if no exact) */
    if (found == 0) {
        char lquery[MAX_NAME];
        strncpy(lquery, query, MAX_NAME - 1); lquery[MAX_NAME - 1] = '\0';
        for (char *p = lquery; *p; p++) *p = tolower(*p);

        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < idx->count; i++) {
            char lname[MAX_NAME];
            strncpy(lname, idx->symbols[i].name, MAX_NAME - 1); lname[MAX_NAME - 1] = '\0';
            for (char *p = lname; *p; p++) *p = tolower(*p);

            if (strstr(lname, lquery)) {
                printf("  %-8s %s:%u  %s\n", SYM_TAG[idx->symbols[i].type],
                       idx->symbols[i].file, idx->symbols[i].line,
                       idx->symbols[i].name);
                found++;
                if (found >= 20) { printf("  ... (truncated)\n"); break; }
            }
        }
    }

    if (found == 0) printf("  (no matches)\n");
    else printf("\n%d match(es)\n", found);
    printf("---END-INDEX-FIND---\n");
    free(idx);
}

static void cmd_list(void) {
    Index *idx = index_load();
    printf("---XYZT-INDEX-LIST---\n");
    printf("symbols: %u\n", idx->count);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < idx->count; i++) {
        printf("  %-8s %-30s %s:%u\n", SYM_TAG[idx->symbols[i].type],
               idx->symbols[i].name, idx->symbols[i].file, idx->symbols[i].line);
    }
    printf("---END-INDEX-LIST---\n");
    free(idx);
}

static void cmd_stats(void) {
    Index *idx = index_load();
    printf("---XYZT-INDEX-STATS---\n");
    printf("symbols: %u / %d max\n", idx->count, MAX_SYMBOLS);

    if (idx->timestamp > 0) {
        uint32_t ago = (uint32_t)time(NULL) - idx->timestamp;
        if (ago < 60) printf("last scan: %us ago\n", ago);
        else if (ago < 3600) printf("last scan: %um ago\n", ago / 60);
        else printf("last scan: %uh ago\n", ago / 3600);
    }

    /* Unique files */
    char files[1024][MAX_PATH_LEN];
    int nfiles = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < idx->count && nfiles < 1024; i++) {
        int dup = 0;
        for (int j = 0; j < nfiles; j++) {
            if (strcmp(files[j], idx->symbols[i].file) == 0) { dup = 1; break; }
        }
        if (!dup) strncpy(files[nfiles++], idx->symbols[i].file, MAX_PATH_LEN - 1);
    }
    printf("files: %d\n", nfiles);

    int counts[8] = {0};
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < idx->count; i++)
        if (idx->symbols[i].type < 8) counts[idx->symbols[i].type]++;
    for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
        if (counts[i] > 0) printf("  %-8s %d\n", SYM_TAG[i], counts[i]);

    printf("---END-INDEX-STATS---\n");
    free(idx);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT INDEX — Symbol Index\n\n");
    printf("  xyzt_index scan [dir]        Scan directory, build index\n");
    printf("  xyzt_index find <name>       Find symbol (fuzzy match)\n");
    printf("  xyzt_index list              List all indexed symbols\n");
    printf("  xyzt_index stats             Index statistics\n");
    printf("\nIndex: %s\n", INDEX_PATH);
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — February 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) { usage(); return 0; }

    if (strcmp(argv[1], "scan") == 0) {
        const char *dir = argc >= 3 ? argv[2] : ".";
        cmd_scan(dir);
    }
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "find") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_find(argv[2]);
    }
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "list") == 0) {
        cmd_list();
    }
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "stats") == 0) {
        cmd_stats();
    }
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "--help") == 0 || strcmp(argv[1], "-h") == 0) {
        usage();
    }
    else {
        usage();
        return 1;
    }
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_io.c — XYZT File Adapter
 *
 * Eats files. Produces structural fingerprints as BitStreams.
 * Computes XNOR correlation to measure similarity.
 * The first layer of the IO middleware.
 *
 * Fingerprint layers:
 *   L0 (256b):  Byte presence — which byte values appear
 *   L1 (256b):  Byte frequency — which bytes are above average
 *   L2 (2048b): Bigram bloom — hash of all byte pairs
 *   L3 (2048b): Token bloom — hash of whitespace-delimited tokens
 *   L4 (64b):   Entropy profile — 64 blocks, high/low
 *   L5 (128b):  Line structure — length distribution + indent
 *   L6 (512b):  Keyword signature — language/domain markers
 *   ─────────
 *   Total: 5312 bits. Fits in BS_MAXBITS (8192).
 *
 * XNOR correlation on these = structural similarity without parsing.
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O3 -o xyzt_io xyzt_io.c -lm
 * Install: cp xyzt_io /usr/local/bin/
 *
 * Fingerprinting:
 *   xyzt_io fingerprint <file>
 *   xyzt_io correlate <file1> <file2>
 *   xyzt_io matrix <file1> <file2> ... <fileN>
 *   xyzt_io intake <file>
 *
 * Session (persistent memory):
 *   xyzt_io store <file>
 *   xyzt_io batch <file1> ... <fileN>
 *   xyzt_io recall <file>
 *   xyzt_io session
 *   xyzt_io forget
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — February 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stddef.h>
#include <math.h>
#include <ctype.h>
#include "wire.h"

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   BitStream — from xyzt_v5.c (the 2)
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define BS_WORDS 128
#define BS_MAXBITS (BS_WORDS * 64)

typedef struct { uint64_t w[BS_WORDS]; int len; } BitStream;

static inline void bs_init(BitStream *b) { memset(b, 0, sizeof(*b)); }

static inline void bs_push(BitStream *b, int bit) {
    if (b->len >= BS_MAXBITS) return;
    int idx = b->len / 64, off = b->len % 64;
    if (bit) b->w[idx] |= (1ULL << off);
    else     b->w[idx] &= ~(1ULL << off);
    b->len++;
}

static inline void bs_set(BitStream *b, int pos, int bit) {
    if (pos < 0 || pos >= BS_MAXBITS) return;
    int idx = pos / 64, off = pos % 64;
    if (bit) b->w[idx] |= (1ULL << off);
    else     b->w[idx] &= ~(1ULL << off);
    if (pos >= b->len) b->len = pos + 1;
}

static inline int bs_get(const BitStream *b, int i) {
    if (i < 0 || i >= b->len) return 0;
    return (b->w[i/64] >> (i%64)) & 1;
}

static int bs_popcount(const BitStream *b) {
    int ones = 0, full = b->len / 64;
    for (int i = 0; i < full; i++) ones += __builtin_popcountll(b->w[i]);
    int rem = b->len % 64;
    if (rem) ones += __builtin_popcountll(b->w[full] & ((1ULL << rem) - 1));
    return ones;
}

static int bs_corr_pct(const BitStream *a, const BitStream *b) {
    int n = a->len < b->len ? a->len : b->len;
    if (n < 1) return 50;
    int agree = 0, full = n / 64;
    for (int i = 0; i < full; i++)
        agree += __builtin_popcountll(~(a->w[i] ^ b->w[i]));
    int rem = n % 64;
    if (rem) agree += __builtin_popcountll((~(a->w[n/64] ^ b->w[n/64])) & ((1ULL << rem) - 1));
    return (agree * 100) / n;
}

/* Per-layer correlation: compare only bits in [start, start+len) */
static int bs_corr_layer(const BitStream *a, const BitStream *b, int start, int layer_len) {
    int agree = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < layer_len; i++) {
        int ba = bs_get(a, start + i);
        int bb = bs_get(b, start + i);
        if (ba == bb) agree++;
    }
    return (agree * 100) / layer_len;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Fingerprint layer offsets
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define L0_OFF   0       /* Byte presence:   256 bits */
#define L0_LEN   256
#define L1_OFF   256     /* Byte frequency:  256 bits */
#define L1_LEN   256
#define L2_OFF   512     /* Bigram bloom:    2048 bits */
#define L2_LEN   2048
#define L3_OFF   2560    /* Token bloom:     2048 bits */
#define L3_LEN   2048
#define L4_OFF   4608    /* Entropy profile: 64 bits */
#define L4_LEN   64
#define L5_OFF   4672    /* Line structure:  128 bits */
#define L5_LEN   128
#define L6_OFF   4800    /* Keyword sig:     512 bits */
#define L6_LEN   512
#define FP_TOTAL 5312    /* Total fingerprint bits */

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Hash functions — fast, non-crypto, good distribution
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static uint32_t hash32(const uint8_t *data, int len) {
    /* FNV-1a */
    uint32_t h = 2166136261u;
    for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
        h ^= data[i];
        h *= 16777619u;
    }
    return h;
}

/* Double hash for bloom filter: h1 + i*h2 */
static void bloom_set(BitStream *bs, int base_off, int bloom_len,
                      const uint8_t *data, int data_len, int n_hashes) {
    uint32_t h1 = hash32(data, data_len);
    uint32_t h2 = h1 >> 16 | h1 << 16;  /* rotate for second hash */
    h2 |= 1; /* ensure odd for better distribution */
    for (int i = 0; i < n_hashes; i++) {
        int pos = (h1 + i * h2) % bloom_len;
        bs_set(bs, base_off + pos, 1);
    }
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   File reading
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    uint8_t *data;
    size_t   len;
    char     name[256];
} FileData;

static FileData file_read(const char *path) {
    FileData f = {0};
    strncpy(f.name, path, 255);

    /* Extract just filename from path */
    const char *slash = strrchr(path, '/');
    if (slash) strncpy(f.name, slash + 1, 255);

    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "rb");
    if (!fp) { fprintf(stderr, "Cannot open: %s\n", path); return f; }
    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_END);
    f.len = ftell(fp);
    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_SET);
    f.data = malloc(f.len + 1);
    if (!f.data) { fclose(fp); f.len = 0; return f; }
    fread(f.data, 1, f.len, fp);
    f.data[f.len] = 0;
    fclose(fp);
    return f;
}

static void file_free(FileData *f) { free(f->data); f->data = NULL; f->len = 0; }

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   FINGERPRINT ENGINE
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    BitStream bs;
    char      name[256];
    size_t    file_size;

    /* Extracted metadata */
    double    entropy;
    int       is_text;
    int       line_count;
    int       unique_bytes;
    int       token_count;
    double    avg_line_len;

    /* Detected domain */
    char      domain[64];    /* "c_source", "python", "markdown", "binary", etc */
    int       domain_score;  /* confidence 0-100 */

    /* v2: Intershell mismatch tracking (appended for migration compat) */
    uint32_t  file_hash;     /* FNV-1a hash of file content */
    uint32_t  mismatches;    /* times file changed on re-store */
    uint32_t  stores;        /* total store count */
} Fingerprint;

static double block_entropy(const uint8_t *data, int len) {
    if (len < 1) return 0.0;
    int freq[256] = {0};
    for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) freq[data[i]]++;
    double ent = 0.0;
    for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
        if (freq[i] == 0) continue;
        double p = (double)freq[i] / len;
        ent -= p * log2(p);
    }
    return ent;
}

/* ── L0: Byte presence (256 bits) ── */
static void fp_layer0(Fingerprint *fp, const FileData *f) {
    int seen[256] = {0};
    for (size_t i = 0; i < f->len; i++) seen[f->data[i]] = 1;
    int unique = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
        bs_set(&fp->bs, L0_OFF + i, seen[i]);
        if (seen[i]) unique++;
    }
    fp->unique_bytes = unique;
}

/* ── L1: Byte frequency profile (256 bits) ── */
static void fp_layer1(Fingerprint *fp, const FileData *f) {
    int freq[256] = {0};
    for (size_t i = 0; i < f->len; i++) freq[f->data[i]]++;
    double avg = (double)f->len / 256.0;
    for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++)
        bs_set(&fp->bs, L1_OFF + i, freq[i] > avg ? 1 : 0);
}

/* ── L2: Bigram bloom filter (2048 bits) ── */
static void fp_layer2(Fingerprint *fp, const FileData *f) {
    /* Sample bigrams — for large files, stride to keep bounded */
    size_t stride = f->len > 100000 ? f->len / 50000 : 1;
    for (size_t i = 0; i + 1 < f->len; i += stride) {
        uint8_t pair[2] = { f->data[i], f->data[i+1] };
        bloom_set(&fp->bs, L2_OFF, L2_LEN, pair, 2, 3);
    }
}

/* ── L3: Token bloom filter (1024 bits) ── */
static void fp_layer3(Fingerprint *fp, const FileData *f) {
    /* Extract whitespace/punctuation-delimited tokens, hash into bloom */
    int token_count = 0;
    size_t i = 0;
    while (i < f->len && token_count < 10000) {
        /* Skip delimiters */
        while (i < f->len && (isspace(f->data[i]) ||
               f->data[i] == '(' || f->data[i] == ')' ||
               f->data[i] == '{' || f->data[i] == '}' ||
               f->data[i] == '[' || f->data[i] == ']' ||
               f->data[i] == ';' || f->data[i] == ',' ||
               f->data[i] == ':' || f->data[i] == '=' ||
               f->data[i] == '<' || f->data[i] == '>'))
            i++;

        /* Collect token */
        size_t start = i;
        while (i < f->len && !isspace(f->data[i]) &&
               f->data[i] != '(' && f->data[i] != ')' &&
               f->data[i] != '{' && f->data[i] != '}' &&
               f->data[i] != ';' && f->data[i] != ',' &&
               f->data[i] != ':' && f->data[i] != '=')
            i++;

        int tlen = i - start;
        if (tlen >= 2 && tlen <= 64) {
            /* Adaptive: reduce hashes as tokens accumulate to prevent saturation */
            int nh = token_count < 500 ? 3 : (token_count < 2000 ? 2 : 1);
            bloom_set(&fp->bs, L3_OFF, L3_LEN, f->data + start, tlen, nh);
            token_count++;
        }
    }
    fp->token_count = token_count;
}

/* ── L4: Entropy profile (64 bits) ── */
static void fp_layer4(Fingerprint *fp, const FileData *f) {
    fp->entropy = block_entropy(f->data, f->len);

    int n_blocks = 64;
    size_t bsz = f->len / n_blocks;
    if (bsz < 8) { /* file too small for 64 blocks */
        n_blocks = f->len / 8;
        bsz = 8;
    }
    if (n_blocks < 1) n_blocks = 1;

    double global_ent = fp->entropy;
    for (int i = 0; i < n_blocks && i < 64; i++) {
        size_t off = i * bsz;
        int len = (off + bsz <= f->len) ? bsz : f->len - off;
        double ent = block_entropy(f->data + off, len);
        /* 1 if block entropy is above global average (high info region) */
        bs_set(&fp->bs, L4_OFF + i, ent > global_ent ? 1 : 0);
    }
}

/* ── L5: Line structure (128 bits) ── */
static void fp_layer5(Fingerprint *fp, const FileData *f) {
    /* 64 bits: line length histogram (buckets: 0-9, 10-19, ... 630+)
     * 32 bits: indent depth histogram (0-31 spaces)
     * 32 bits: line count features */

    int line_lens[64] = {0};  /* bucket by 10s, capped at 63 */
    int indents[32] = {0};
    int line_count = 0;
    double total_len = 0;

    size_t i = 0;
    while (i < f->len) {
        /* Measure indent */
        int indent = 0;
        while (i + indent < f->len && f->data[i + indent] == ' ') indent++;
        if (indent > 31) indent = 31;
        indents[indent]++;

        /* Measure line length */
        size_t start = i;
        while (i < f->len && f->data[i] != '\n') i++;
        int llen = i - start;
        if (i < f->len) i++; /* skip \n */

        int bucket = llen / 10;
        if (bucket > 63) bucket = 63;
        line_lens[bucket]++;
        total_len += llen;
        line_count++;
    }

    fp->line_count = line_count;
    fp->avg_line_len = line_count > 0 ? total_len / line_count : 0;
    fp->is_text = (fp->unique_bytes < 100 && fp->entropy < 5.5) ? 1 :
                  (line_count > 5 && fp->avg_line_len < 200) ? 1 : 0;

    /* Histogram to bits: above average = 1 */
    double avg_ll = line_count > 0 ? (double)line_count / 64.0 : 1.0;
    for (int b = 0; b < 64; b++)
        bs_set(&fp->bs, L5_OFF + b, line_lens[b] > avg_ll ? 1 : 0);

    double avg_ind = line_count > 0 ? (double)line_count / 32.0 : 1.0;
    for (int b = 0; b < 32; b++)
        bs_set(&fp->bs, L5_OFF + 64 + b, indents[b] > avg_ind ? 1 : 0);

    /* Line count features: log2 buckets */
    int lc_log = line_count > 0 ? (int)log2(line_count) : 0;
    if (lc_log > 31) lc_log = 31;
    for (int b = 0; b < 32; b++)
        bs_set(&fp->bs, L5_OFF + 96 + b, b <= lc_log ? 1 : 0);
}

/* ── L6: Keyword / domain signature (512 bits) ── */

typedef struct { const char *kw; int group; } Keyword;

static const Keyword KEYWORDS[] = {
    /* C/C++ — group 0 */
    {"#include", 0}, {"typedef", 0}, {"struct", 0}, {"malloc", 0},
    {"printf", 0}, {"static", 0}, {"inline", 0}, {"uint8_t", 0},
    {"uint16_t", 0}, {"uint32_t", 0}, {"uint64_t", 0}, {"void", 0},
    {"sizeof", 0}, {"NULL", 0}, {"#define", 0}, {"int main", 0},

    /* Python — group 1 */
    {"def ", 1}, {"import ", 1}, {"from ", 1}, {"class ", 1},
    {"self.", 1}, {"__init__", 1}, {"print(", 1}, {"if __name__", 1},
    {"lambda", 1}, {"yield", 1}, {"async ", 1}, {"await ", 1},
    {"numpy", 1}, {"torch", 1}, {"argparse", 1}, {"#!/usr/bin/env python", 1},

    /* Verilog/FPGA — group 2 */
    {"module ", 2}, {"endmodule", 2}, {"always @", 2}, {"assign ", 2},
    {"input ", 2}, {"output ", 2}, {"wire ", 2}, {"reg ", 2},
    {"posedge", 2}, {"negedge", 2}, {"PACKAGE_PIN", 2}, {"IOSTANDARD", 2},

    /* Markdown/docs — group 3 */
    {"## ", 3}, {"### ", 3}, {"```", 3}, {"---\n", 3},
    {"**", 3}, {"| ", 3}, {"- [ ]", 3}, {"> ", 3},

    /* Math/Lean — group 4 */
    {"theorem ", 4}, {"lemma ", 4}, {"proof", 4}, {"sorry", 4},
    {"axiom", 4}, {"Lean", 4}, {"∀", 4}, {"∃", 4},

    /* XYZT-specific — group 5 */
    {"BitStream", 5}, {"impedance", 5}, {"collision", 5}, {"substrate", 5},
    {"ONETWO", 5}, {"distinction", 5}, {"invariant", 5}, {"bedrock", 5},
    {"bs_push", 5}, {"bs_corr", 5}, {"graph_tick", 5}, {"FP_ONE", 5},
    {"bs_init", 5}, {"Edge", 5}, {"Junction", 5}, {"Z-order", 5},

    /* Shell/system — group 6 */
    {"#!/bin/", 6}, {"sudo ", 6}, {"chmod", 6}, {"grep ", 6},
    {"apt ", 6}, {"pip ", 6}, {"npm ", 6}, {"docker", 6},

    {NULL, -1}
};

#define N_DOMAIN_GROUPS 7
static const char *DOMAIN_NAMES[] = {
    "c_source", "python", "verilog", "markdown",
    "math_lean", "xyzt_project", "shell"
};

static void fp_layer6(Fingerprint *fp, const FileData *f) {
    if (!fp->is_text) {
        strncpy(fp->domain, "binary", 63);
        fp->domain_score = 90;
        return;
    }

    int group_hits[N_DOMAIN_GROUPS] = {0};
    int kw_idx = 0;

    for (const Keyword *kw = KEYWORDS; kw->kw != NULL; kw++) {
        /* Search for keyword in file (case sensitive, word-boundary check) */
        const char *pos = (const char *)f->data;
        int found = 0;
        int kwlen = strlen(kw->kw);
        /* Keywords starting with special chars (#, !, /) self-delimit */
        int need_boundary = (isalpha((unsigned char)kw->kw[0]) || kw->kw[0] == '_');
        while ((pos = strstr(pos, kw->kw)) != NULL) {
            int valid = 1;
            if (need_boundary) {
                /* Check left boundary: must not be preceded by alnum/_ */
                if (pos > (const char *)f->data) {
                    char before = *(pos - 1);
                    if (isalnum((unsigned char)before) || before == '_') valid = 0;
                }
                /* Check right boundary: must not be followed by alnum/_ */
                if (valid) {
                    char after = pos[kwlen];
                    if (isalnum((unsigned char)after) || after == '_') valid = 0;
                }
            }
            if (valid) found++;
            pos += kwlen;
            if (found >= 3) break; /* cap per keyword */
        }

        if (found > 0) {
            /* Set bits in bloom for this keyword */
            bloom_set(&fp->bs, L6_OFF, L6_LEN,
                      (const uint8_t *)kw->kw, strlen(kw->kw), 3);
            group_hits[kw->group] += found;
        }
        kw_idx++;
    }

    /* Determine dominant domain */
    int best_group = 0, best_score = 0;
    int total_hits = 0;
    for (int g = 0; g < N_DOMAIN_GROUPS; g++) {
        total_hits += group_hits[g];
        if (group_hits[g] > best_score) {
            best_score = group_hits[g];
            best_group = g;
        }
    }

    if (total_hits > 0) {
        strncpy(fp->domain, DOMAIN_NAMES[best_group], 63);
        fp->domain_score = best_score * 100 / (total_hits > 0 ? total_hits : 1);
    } else {
        strncpy(fp->domain, "unknown", 63);
        fp->domain_score = 0;
    }
}

/* ── Master fingerprint function ── */
static Fingerprint fingerprint(const FileData *f) {
    Fingerprint fp = {0};
    bs_init(&fp.bs);
    strncpy(fp.name, f->name, 255);
    fp.file_size = f->len;

    /* Ensure bitstream is full length for clean correlation */
    fp.bs.len = FP_TOTAL;

    fp_layer0(&fp, f);   /* byte presence — always */
    fp_layer1(&fp, f);   /* byte frequency — always */
    fp_layer4(&fp, f);   /* entropy profile — always (sets fp.entropy, fp.is_text) */
    fp_layer5(&fp, f);   /* line structure — always (sets fp.is_text) */

    /* Content layers only for text files.
     * Binary data produces false bloom saturation. */
    if (fp.entropy < 7.0 && fp.unique_bytes < 200) {
        fp_layer2(&fp, f);   /* bigram bloom */
        fp_layer3(&fp, f);   /* token bloom */
    }

    fp_layer6(&fp, f);   /* keyword/domain — always (handles binary internally) */

    return fp;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   OUTPUT FORMATTERS
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void print_fingerprint(const Fingerprint *fp) {
    printf("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n");
    printf("  XYZT IO — %s\n", fp->name);
    printf("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n");
    printf("  Size:     %zu bytes\n", fp->file_size);
    printf("  Entropy:  %.3f bits/byte (%.1f%%)\n", fp->entropy, fp->entropy / 8.0 * 100);
    printf("  Text:     %s\n", fp->is_text ? "yes" : "no");
    printf("  Lines:    %d (avg %.1f chars)\n", fp->line_count, fp->avg_line_len);
    printf("  Unique:   %d/256 byte values\n", fp->unique_bytes);
    printf("  Tokens:   %d\n", fp->token_count);
    printf("  Domain:   %s (%d%% confidence)\n", fp->domain, fp->domain_score);
    printf("  Fingerprint: %d bits, %d set (%.1f%% density)\n",
           FP_TOTAL, bs_popcount(&fp->bs),
           bs_popcount(&fp->bs) * 100.0 / FP_TOTAL);

    /* Layer density */
    struct { const char *name; int off; int len; } layers[] = {
        {"L0 byte_present", L0_OFF, L0_LEN},
        {"L1 byte_freq",    L1_OFF, L1_LEN},
        {"L2 bigram_bloom", L2_OFF, L2_LEN},
        {"L3 token_bloom",  L3_OFF, L3_LEN},
        {"L4 entropy_prof", L4_OFF, L4_LEN},
        {"L5 line_struct",  L5_OFF, L5_LEN},
        {"L6 keyword_sig",  L6_OFF, L6_LEN},
    };
    printf("\n  Layer density:\n");
    for (int i = 0; i < 7; i++) {
        int set = 0;
        for (int b = 0; b < layers[i].len; b++)
            if (bs_get(&fp->bs, layers[i].off + b)) set++;
        printf("    %-18s %4d/%4d bits  %5.1f%%\n",
               layers[i].name, set, layers[i].len,
               set * 100.0 / layers[i].len);
    }
    printf("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n");
}

static void print_correlation(const Fingerprint *a, const Fingerprint *b) {
    int total = bs_corr_pct(&a->bs, &b->bs);

    printf("\n  %s  ×  %s\n", a->name, b->name);
    printf("  ─────────────────────────────────────────\n");
    printf("  TOTAL CORRELATION: %d%%\n\n", total);

    /* Per-layer breakdown */
    struct { const char *name; int off; int len; } layers[] = {
        {"byte_present", L0_OFF, L0_LEN},
        {"byte_freq",    L1_OFF, L1_LEN},
        {"bigram_bloom", L2_OFF, L2_LEN},
        {"token_bloom",  L3_OFF, L3_LEN},
        {"entropy_prof", L4_OFF, L4_LEN},
        {"line_struct",  L5_OFF, L5_LEN},
        {"keyword_sig",  L6_OFF, L6_LEN},
    };
    for (int i = 0; i < 7; i++) {
        int corr = bs_corr_layer(&a->bs, &b->bs, layers[i].off, layers[i].len);
        /* Visual bar */
        int bar = corr * 30 / 100;
        printf("    %-14s %3d%% ", layers[i].name, corr);
        for (int b = 0; b < 30; b++) printf(b < bar ? "█" : "░");
        printf("\n");
    }
}

static void print_matrix(Fingerprint *fps, int n) {
    printf("\n═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n");
    printf("  XYZT IO — CORRELATION MATRIX (%d files)\n", n);
    printf("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n");

    /* Truncate names for display */
    char short_names[64][16];
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        strncpy(short_names[i], fps[i].name, 12);
        short_names[i][12] = '\0';
        /* Trim extension if it helps */
        char *dot = strrchr(short_names[i], '.');
        if (dot && strlen(dot) <= 4) *dot = '\0';
    }

    /* Header */
    printf("  %14s", "");
    for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) printf(" %8s", short_names[j]);
    printf("\n");
    printf("  %14s", "");
    for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) printf(" ────────");
    printf("\n");

    /* Matrix */
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        printf("  %-14s", short_names[i]);
        for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) {
            int corr = bs_corr_pct(&fps[i].bs, &fps[j].bs);
            if (i == j)
                printf("    ·   ");
            else if (corr >= 75)
                printf("  \033[1;32m%3d%%\033[0m   ", corr);  /* green = high */
            else if (corr >= 60)
                printf("  \033[1;33m%3d%%\033[0m   ", corr);  /* yellow = medium */
            else
                printf("  %3d%%   ", corr);
        }
        printf("  %s (%s)\n", fps[i].domain, fps[i].is_text ? "text" : "bin");
    }

    /* Find clusters: files with >75% correlation */
    printf("\n  CLUSTERS (>75%% correlation):\n");
    int printed_cluster = 0;
    int clustered[64] = {0};
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if (clustered[i]) continue;
        int members[64], nm = 0;
        members[nm++] = i;
        for (int j = i + 1; j < n; j++) {
            if (bs_corr_pct(&fps[i].bs, &fps[j].bs) >= 75) {
                members[nm++] = j;
                clustered[j] = 1;
            }
        }
        if (nm > 1) {
            printf("    [");
            for (int m = 0; m < nm; m++) {
                printf("%s%s", m > 0 ? ", " : "", fps[members[m]].name);
            }
            printf("]\n");
            printed_cluster = 1;
        }
    }
    if (!printed_cluster) printf("    (no clusters found)\n");

    /* Find outliers: files with max correlation < 55% to anything */
    printf("\n  OUTLIERS (max corr <55%%):\n");
    int printed_outlier = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        int max_corr = 0;
        for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) {
            if (i == j) continue;
            int c = bs_corr_pct(&fps[i].bs, &fps[j].bs);
            if (c > max_corr) max_corr = c;
        }
        if (max_corr < 55) {
            printf("    %s (max: %d%%)\n", fps[i].name, max_corr);
            printed_outlier = 1;
        }
    }
    if (!printed_outlier) printf("    (none)\n");

    printf("\n═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n");
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   INTAKE — The money shot.
   Structured brief for Claude consumption.
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void print_intake(const Fingerprint *fp) {
    /* Compact, parseable, no noise */
    printf("---XYZT-INTAKE---\n");
    printf("file: %s\n", fp->name);
    printf("size: %zu\n", fp->file_size);
    printf("domain: %s (%d%%)\n", fp->domain, fp->domain_score);
    printf("type: %s\n", fp->is_text ? "text" : "binary");
    printf("entropy: %.3f\n", fp->entropy);
    printf("lines: %d\n", fp->line_count);
    printf("tokens: %d\n", fp->token_count);
    printf("unique_bytes: %d\n", fp->unique_bytes);
    printf("avg_line: %.1f\n", fp->avg_line_len);
    printf("fp_density: %.1f%%\n", bs_popcount(&fp->bs) * 100.0 / FP_TOTAL);

    /* Signal classification */
    if (fp->entropy > 7.5)
        printf("signal: encrypted_or_compressed\n");
    else if (fp->entropy > 6.0)
        printf("signal: dense_structured\n");
    else if (fp->entropy > 4.0)
        printf("signal: normal_code_or_text\n");
    else if (fp->entropy > 2.0)
        printf("signal: sparse_or_repetitive\n");
    else
        printf("signal: near_empty\n");

    printf("---END-INTAKE---\n");
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   SESSION PERSISTENCE
   Save/load fingerprint collections. Binary format.
   The engine accumulates. Edges that fire together wire together.
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define SESSION_MAGIC  0x58595A54  /* "XYZT" */
#define SESSION_VER    2
#define MAX_SESSION    512
/* v1 fingerprint size: everything before file_hash */
#define V1_FP_SIZE     offsetof(Fingerprint, file_hash)

typedef struct {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint8_t  version;
    uint8_t  _pad[3];
    uint32_t count;
    uint32_t _reserved;
} SessionHeader;

typedef struct {
    SessionHeader hdr;
    Fingerprint   entries[MAX_SESSION];
} Session;

static const char *get_session_path(void);

static Session *session_create(void) {
    Session *s = calloc(1, sizeof(Session));
    s->hdr.magic = SESSION_MAGIC;
    s->hdr.version = SESSION_VER;
    s->hdr.count = 0;
    return s;
}

static Session *session_load(const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "rb");
    if (!fp) return session_create(); /* new session if doesn't exist */

    SessionHeader hdr;
    if (fread(&hdr, sizeof(hdr), 1, fp) != 1 || hdr.magic != SESSION_MAGIC) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Bad session file: %s\n", path);
        fclose(fp);
        return session_create();
    }

    Session *s = session_create();
    s->hdr = hdr;
    if (s->hdr.count > MAX_SESSION) s->hdr.count = MAX_SESSION;

    if (hdr.version >= 2) {
        /* Current format */
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->hdr.count; i++) {
            if (fread(&s->entries[i], sizeof(Fingerprint), 1, fp) != 1) {
                s->hdr.count = i; break;
            }
        }
    } else {
        /* v1: smaller Fingerprint (no file_hash/mismatches/stores) */
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->hdr.count; i++) {
            uint8_t buf[4096];
            if (fread(buf, V1_FP_SIZE, 1, fp) != 1) {
                s->hdr.count = i; break;
            }
            memcpy(&s->entries[i], buf, V1_FP_SIZE);
            /* v2 fields stay zeroed from session_create */
        }
    }
    s->hdr.version = SESSION_VER;
    fclose(fp);
    return s;
}

static int session_write(const Session *s, const char *path) {
    xyzt_ensure_dir(path);
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "wb");
    if (!fp) { fprintf(stderr, "Cannot write: %s\n", path); return -1; }
    fwrite(&s->hdr, sizeof(SessionHeader), 1, fp);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->hdr.count; i++)
        fwrite(&s->entries[i], sizeof(Fingerprint), 1, fp);
    fclose(fp);
    return 0;
}

static int session_add(Session *s, const Fingerprint *fp) {
    /* Check for duplicate by name — update if exists */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->hdr.count; i++) {
        if (strcmp(s->entries[i].name, fp->name) == 0) {
            s->entries[i] = *fp;
            return (int)i; /* updated existing */
        }
    }
    if (s->hdr.count >= MAX_SESSION) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Session full (%d entries)\n", MAX_SESSION);
        return -1;
    }
    s->entries[s->hdr.count] = *fp;
    return (int)s->hdr.count++;
}

static void session_free(Session *s) { free(s); }

static void session_save_global(const Session *local) {
    const char *home = xyzt_home_dir();
    if (!home) return;

    char gpath[512];
    snprintf(gpath, sizeof(gpath), "%s/.xyzt/session.bin", home);

    /* Skip if local path IS global */
    if (strcmp(get_session_path(), gpath) == 0) return;

    /* Load global session and merge fingerprints by name */
    Session *g = session_load(gpath);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < local->hdr.count; i++)
        session_add(g, &local->entries[i]);

    session_write(g, gpath);
    session_free(g);
}

static int session_save(const Session *s, const char *path) {
    int rc = session_write(s, path);
    if (rc == 0) session_save_global(s);
    return rc;
}

/* ── store: fingerprint + add to session ── */
static void cmd_store(const char *session_path, const char *file_path) {
    Session *s = session_load(session_path);
    FileData f = file_read(file_path);
    if (!f.data) { session_free(s); return; }

    Fingerprint fp = fingerprint(&f);

    /* v2: compute content hash for mismatch detection */
    fp.file_hash = hash32(f.data, (int)(f.len > 65536 ? 65536 : f.len));
    fp.stores = 1;
    fp.mismatches = 0;

    /* Check if file already stored — detect mismatch */
    int mismatch = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->hdr.count; i++) {
        if (strcmp(s->entries[i].name, fp.name) == 0) {
            uint32_t old_hash = s->entries[i].file_hash;
            fp.stores = s->entries[i].stores + 1;
            fp.mismatches = s->entries[i].mismatches;
            if (old_hash != 0 && old_hash != fp.file_hash) {
                fp.mismatches++;
                mismatch = 1;
            }
            break;
        }
    }

    int idx = session_add(s, &fp);

    if (idx >= 0) {
        session_save(s, session_path);
        printf("stored: %s → slot %d (%d total)\n", fp.name, idx, s->hdr.count);

        if (mismatch) {
            printf("mismatch: file changed since last store (%u total mismatches)\n", fp.mismatches);
            /* Distinction non-persistence: mark wire edges stale */
            wire_mark_stale(fp.name);
        }

        /* ═══ WIRE: auto-link ═══ */
        if (wire_auto_link(fp.name)) {
            printf("wire: linked to active problem\n");
        }
    }
    file_free(&f);
    session_free(s);
}

/* ── recall: find top N matches in session ── */
static void cmd_recall(const char *session_path, const char *file_path, int top_n) {
    Session *s = session_load(session_path);
    if (s->hdr.count == 0) {
        printf("---XYZT-RECALL---\nempty_session\n---END-RECALL---\n");
        session_free(s);
        return;
    }

    FileData f = file_read(file_path);
    if (!f.data) { session_free(s); return; }
    Fingerprint query = fingerprint(&f);

    /* Score all entries: fingerprint correlation + wire boost */
    typedef struct { int idx; int corr; int boost; int combined; } Score;
    Score scores[MAX_SESSION];
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->hdr.count; i++) {
        scores[i].idx = i;
        scores[i].corr = bs_corr_pct(&query.bs, &s->entries[i].bs);
        scores[i].boost = wire_boost(s->entries[i].name);
        /* Combined: fingerprint correlation + wire topology.
         * Wire boost is 0 or 1 (alive connection or not).
         * Alive edge = +15 points. Binary. Connected or not. */
        scores[i].combined = scores[i].corr + (scores[i].boost ? 15 : 0);
    }

    /* Sort descending by combined score */
    for (uint32_t i = 1; i < s->hdr.count; i++) {
        Score tmp = scores[i];
        int j = i - 1;
        while (j >= 0 && scores[j].combined < tmp.combined) {
            scores[j + 1] = scores[j];
            j--;
        }
        scores[j + 1] = tmp;
    }

    if (top_n > (int)s->hdr.count) top_n = s->hdr.count;

    printf("---XYZT-RECALL---\n");
    printf("query: %s\n", query.name);
    printf("session: %d entries\n", s->hdr.count);
    printf("matches:\n");
    for (int i = 0; i < top_n; i++) {
        Fingerprint *m = &s->entries[scores[i].idx];
        if (scores[i].boost != 0) {
            printf("  %3d%% %s [%s %zuB] (wire:%+d)\n",
                   scores[i].corr, m->name, m->domain, m->file_size, scores[i].boost);
        } else {
            printf("  %3d%% %s [%s %zuB]\n",
                   scores[i].corr, m->name, m->domain, m->file_size);
        }
    }
    printf("---END-RECALL---\n");

    file_free(&f);
    session_free(s);
}

/* ── session: list contents ── */
static void cmd_session_list(const char *session_path) {
    Session *s = session_load(session_path);
    printf("---XYZT-SESSION---\n");
    printf("path: %s\n", session_path);
    printf("entries: %d/%d\n", s->hdr.count, MAX_SESSION);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->hdr.count; i++) {
        Fingerprint *fp = &s->entries[i];
        printf("  [%3d] %-30s %6zuB  %-14s  ent=%.2f  %s",
               i, fp->name, fp->file_size, fp->domain,
               fp->entropy, fp->is_text ? "text" : "bin");
        if (fp->stores > 1) printf("  stores=%u", fp->stores);
        if (fp->mismatches > 0) printf("  mismatches=%u", fp->mismatches);
        printf("\n");
    }
    printf("---END-SESSION---\n");
    session_free(s);
}

/* ── forget: clear session ── */
static void cmd_forget(const char *session_path) {
    Session *s = session_create();
    session_save(s, session_path);
    printf("session cleared: %s\n", session_path);
    session_free(s);
}

/* ── batch: fingerprint + store multiple files at once ── */
static void cmd_batch(const char *session_path, int n_files, char **files) {
    Session *s = session_load(session_path);
    int added = 0, linked = 0, mismatches = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n_files; i++) {
        FileData f = file_read(files[i]);
        if (!f.data) continue;
        Fingerprint fp = fingerprint(&f);

        /* v2: compute hash and track mismatches */
        fp.file_hash = hash32(f.data, (int)(f.len > 65536 ? 65536 : f.len));
        fp.stores = 1;
        fp.mismatches = 0;
        for (uint32_t j = 0; j < s->hdr.count; j++) {
            if (strcmp(s->entries[j].name, fp.name) == 0) {
                uint32_t old_hash = s->entries[j].file_hash;
                fp.stores = s->entries[j].stores + 1;
                fp.mismatches = s->entries[j].mismatches;
                if (old_hash != 0 && old_hash != fp.file_hash) {
                    fp.mismatches++;
                    mismatches++;
                    wire_mark_stale(fp.name);
                }
                break;
            }
        }

        if (session_add(s, &fp) >= 0) {
            added++;
            if (wire_auto_link(fp.name)) linked++;
        }
        file_free(&f);
    }
    session_save(s, session_path);
    printf("batch: %d files stored (%d total)\n", added, s->hdr.count);
    if (linked > 0) printf("wire: %d files linked to active problem\n", linked);
    if (mismatches > 0) printf("mismatches: %d files changed since last store\n", mismatches);
    session_free(s);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   BRIEF — Enriched file report with wire + session context
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_brief(const char *session_path, const char *file_path) {
    FileData f = file_read(file_path);
    if (!f.data) return;
    Fingerprint fp = fingerprint(&f);

    printf("---XYZT-BRIEF---\n");
    printf("file: %s\n", fp.name);
    printf("size: %zu  domain: %s (%d%%)  type: %s\n",
           fp.file_size, fp.domain, fp.domain_score, fp.is_text ? "text" : "binary");
    printf("lines: %d  entropy: %.3f  tokens: %d\n",
           fp.line_count, fp.entropy, fp.token_count);

    if (fp.entropy > 7.5)       printf("signal: encrypted_or_compressed\n");
    else if (fp.entropy > 6.0)  printf("signal: dense_structured\n");
    else if (fp.entropy > 4.0)  printf("signal: normal_code_or_text\n");
    else if (fp.entropy > 2.0)  printf("signal: sparse_or_repetitive\n");
    else                        printf("signal: near_empty\n");

    /* Wire context */
    WireConn conns[WIRE_MAX_CONN];
    int n = wire_query_connections(fp.name, conns, 12);
    if (n > 0) {
        printf("\nwire_connections: %d\n", n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            const char *type_tag = conns[i].type == WNODE_FILE ? "file" :
                                   conns[i].type == WNODE_PROBLEM ? "problem" : "concept";
            printf("  %-8s w=%3d p=%d f=%d%s  %s\n",
                   type_tag, conns[i].weight, conns[i].passes, conns[i].fails,
                   conns[i].stale ? " STALE" : "", conns[i].name);
        }
    } else {
        printf("\nwire_connections: none\n");
    }

    /* Session context: top 3 similar files */
    Session *s = session_load(session_path);
    if (s->hdr.count > 0) {
        typedef struct { int idx; int corr; } Match;
        Match matches[MAX_SESSION];
        int nm = 0;
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->hdr.count; i++) {
            if (strcmp(s->entries[i].name, fp.name) == 0) continue;
            int corr = bs_corr_pct(&fp.bs, &s->entries[i].bs);
            if (corr > 60) {
                matches[nm].idx = i;
                matches[nm].corr = corr;
                nm++;
            }
        }
        for (int i = 1; i < nm; i++) {
            Match tmp = matches[i]; int j = i - 1;
            while (j >= 0 && matches[j].corr < tmp.corr) { matches[j+1] = matches[j]; j--; }
            matches[j+1] = tmp;
        }
        if (nm > 0) {
            printf("\nsimilar_files:\n");
            int show = nm > 3 ? 3 : nm;
            for (int i = 0; i < show; i++) {
                printf("  %3d%% %s [%s]\n", matches[i].corr,
                       s->entries[matches[i].idx].name,
                       s->entries[matches[i].idx].domain);
            }
        }
    }
    session_free(s);

    /* Onetwo context */
    char problem[256];
    if (wire_read_problem(problem, 256) >= 0) {
        printf("\nactive_problem: %s\n", problem);
        int boost = wire_boost(fp.name);
        if (boost != 0) printf("wire_boost: %+d\n", boost);
    }

    printf("---END-BRIEF---\n");
    file_free(&f);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   MAIN
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define DEFAULT_SESSION ".xyzt/session.bin"

static const char *get_session_path(void) {
    const char *env = getenv("XYZT_SESSION");
    return env ? env : DEFAULT_SESSION;
}

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT IO — File Adapter & Session Engine\n\n");
    printf("Fingerprinting:\n");
    printf("  xyzt_io fingerprint <file>              Structural fingerprint\n");
    printf("  xyzt_io correlate <file1> <file2>       Compare two files\n");
    printf("  xyzt_io matrix <file1> ... <fileN>      NxN correlation matrix\n");
    printf("  xyzt_io intake <file>                   Machine-readable brief\n");
    printf("  xyzt_io brief <file>                    Enriched brief + wire + session\n");
    printf("\nSession (persistent memory):\n");
    printf("  xyzt_io store <file>                    Fingerprint + save to session\n");
    printf("  xyzt_io batch <file1> ... <fileN>       Store multiple files\n");
    printf("  xyzt_io recall <file>                   Find top matches in session\n");
    printf("  xyzt_io session                         List session contents\n");
    printf("  xyzt_io forget                          Clear session\n");
    printf("\nSession path: $XYZT_SESSION or %s\n", DEFAULT_SESSION);
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — February 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) { usage(); return 0; }

    const char *cmd = argv[1];
    const char *sp = get_session_path();

    /* ── Fingerprinting commands ── */

    if (strcmp(cmd, "fingerprint") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        FileData f = file_read(argv[2]);
        if (!f.data) return 1;
        Fingerprint fp = fingerprint(&f);
        print_fingerprint(&fp);
        file_free(&f);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "correlate") == 0 && argc >= 4) {
        FileData fa = file_read(argv[2]);
        FileData fb = file_read(argv[3]);
        if (!fa.data || !fb.data) return 1;
        Fingerprint fpa = fingerprint(&fa);
        Fingerprint fpb = fingerprint(&fb);
        print_fingerprint(&fpa);
        print_fingerprint(&fpb);
        print_correlation(&fpa, &fpb);
        file_free(&fa); file_free(&fb);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "matrix") == 0 && argc >= 4) {
        int n = argc - 2;
        if (n > 64) n = 64;
        Fingerprint *fps = calloc(n, sizeof(Fingerprint));
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            FileData f = file_read(argv[2 + i]);
            if (!f.data) { fprintf(stderr, "Skip: %s\n", argv[2+i]); continue; }
            fps[i] = fingerprint(&f);
            file_free(&f);
        }
        print_matrix(fps, n);
        free(fps);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "intake") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        FileData f = file_read(argv[2]);
        if (!f.data) return 1;
        Fingerprint fp = fingerprint(&f);
        print_intake(&fp);
        file_free(&f);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "brief") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_brief(sp, argv[2]);
    }

    /* ── Session commands ── */

    else if (strcmp(cmd, "store") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_store(sp, argv[2]);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "batch") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_batch(sp, argc - 2, argv + 2);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "recall") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        cmd_recall(sp, argv[2], 5);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "session") == 0) {
        cmd_session_list(sp);
    }
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "forget") == 0) {
        cmd_forget(sp);
    }
    else {
        usage();
        return 1;
    }

    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_loop.c — Autonomous work daemon
 *
 * The heartbeat. Reads xyzt_next for priorities, dispatches agents,
 * tests results, learns into the wire graph. Runs until idle or killed.
 *
 * Cycle: next → agent → test → learn → next
 *
 * Confidence gates:
 *   P0 (test failures, errors) → builder agent, auto-test, auto-commit on pass
 *   P1 (unverified claims)     → critic agent, verify only, no writes
 *   P2 (unknowns)              → scout agent, investigate, report
 *   P3+ (stale edges, etc)     → scout agent, re-verify, report
 *   Human review required       → queued to .xyzt/review_queue.txt
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O3 -o xyzt_loop.exe xyzt_loop.c
 * Install: cp xyzt_loop ~/.local/bin/
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — March 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>

#ifdef _WIN32
#include <windows.h>
#define SLEEP_MS(ms) Sleep(ms)
#else
#include <unistd.h>
#define SLEEP_MS(ms) usleep((ms) * 1000)
#endif

#include "wire.h"
#include "xyzt_state.h"

#define MAX_CMD       8192
#define MAX_OUTPUT    65536
#define MAX_LINE      1024
#define MAX_QUEUE     32
#define LOOP_INTERVAL_MS  10000   /* 10 seconds between cycles */
#define MAX_CYCLES    100         /* safety: don't run forever without asking */
#define IDLE_CYCLES   3           /* exit after 3 cycles with nothing to do */

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Action parsing — reads xyzt_next output
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    int  priority;
    char source[16];
    char action[512];
} Action;

typedef struct {
    Action items[MAX_QUEUE];
    int    count;
} ActionQueue;

/* Run a command and capture output */
static int run_capture(const char *cmd, char *out, int max) {
    out[0] = 0;
#ifdef _WIN32
    char cmdline[MAX_CMD];
    snprintf(cmdline, sizeof(cmdline), "cmd.exe /c \"%s\" 2>&1", cmd);

    HANDLE hRead, hWrite;
    SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES sa = { sizeof(sa), NULL, TRUE };
    CreatePipe(&hRead, &hWrite, &sa, 0);
    SetHandleInformation(hRead, HANDLE_FLAG_INHERIT, 0);

    STARTUPINFOA si = {0};
    PROCESS_INFORMATION pi = {0};
    si.cb = sizeof(si);
    si.dwFlags = STARTF_USESTDHANDLES;
    si.hStdOutput = hWrite;
    si.hStdError = hWrite;
    si.hStdInput = NULL;

    BOOL ok = CreateProcessA(NULL, cmdline, NULL, NULL, TRUE,
                             CREATE_NO_WINDOW, NULL, NULL, &si, &pi);
    CloseHandle(hWrite);
    if (!ok) { CloseHandle(hRead); return -1; }

    DWORD n;
    int total = 0;
    while (total < max - 1) {
        BOOL r = ReadFile(hRead, out + total, max - 1 - total, &n, NULL);
        if (!r || n == 0) break;
        total += n;
    }
    out[total] = 0;

    WaitForSingleObject(pi.hProcess, 60000);
    DWORD exit_code = 1;
    GetExitCodeProcess(pi.hProcess, &exit_code);
    CloseHandle(pi.hProcess);
    CloseHandle(pi.hThread);
    CloseHandle(hRead);
    return (int)exit_code;
#else
    FILE *fp = popen(cmd, "r");
    if (!fp) return -1;
    int total = 0;
    while (total < max - 1) {
        int c = fgetc(fp);
        if (c == EOF) break;
        out[total++] = (char)c;
    }
    out[total] = 0;
    return pclose(fp);
#endif
}

/* Parse xyzt_next output into action queue */
static int parse_next_output(const char *output, ActionQueue *q) {
    q->count = 0;
    const char *line = output;
    while (line && *line && q->count < MAX_QUEUE) {
        /* Skip header/footer */
        if (strncmp(line, "---", 3) == 0 || strncmp(line, "No pending", 10) == 0) {
            line = strchr(line, '\n');
            if (line) line++;
            continue;
        }
        /* Parse: "  P0 [test   ] Fix failing test: ..." */
        if (line[0] == ' ' && line[1] == ' ' && line[2] == 'P') {
            Action *a = &q->items[q->count];
            a->priority = line[3] - '0';
            /* Extract source between [ ] */
            const char *sb = strchr(line, '[');
            const char *se = strchr(line, ']');
            if (sb && se && se > sb) {
                sb++;
                int slen = (int)(se - sb);
                while (slen > 0 && sb[slen-1] == ' ') slen--;
                if (slen > 15) slen = 15;
                memcpy(a->source, sb, slen);
                a->source[slen] = 0;
                /* Rest is the action text */
                se += 2; /* skip "] " */
                int alen = 0;
                while (se[alen] && se[alen] != '\n' && alen < 511) alen++;
                memcpy(a->action, se, alen);
                a->action[alen] = 0;
                q->count++;
            }
        }
        line = strchr(line, '\n');
        if (line) line++;
    }
    return q->count;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Action → Agent mapping
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    const char *role;
    int   allow_write;
    int   needs_test;    /* run xyzt_test after? */
    int   needs_review;  /* queue for human review instead? */
    int   use_engine;    /* dispatch to xyzt_pc infer instead of xyzt_agent? */
} AgentPlan;

static AgentPlan plan_action(const Action *a) {
    AgentPlan p = { "scout", 0, 0, 0, 0 };

    if (a->priority == 0) {
        /* P0: test failures, unresolved errors — builder fixes, test verifies */
        if (strcmp(a->source, "test") == 0 || strcmp(a->source, "errors") == 0) {
            p.role = "builder";
            p.allow_write = 1;
            p.needs_test = 1;
        }
    } else if (a->priority == 1) {
        /* P1: pending/failed claims — critic verifies, doesn't write */
        p.role = "critic";
        p.allow_write = 0;
        p.needs_test = 0;
    } else if (a->priority == 2) {
        /* P2: unknowns — scout investigates */
        p.role = "scout";
        p.allow_write = 0;
        p.needs_test = 0;
    } else if (a->priority == 3) {
        /* P3: stale wire edges — engine can handle this directly.
           The engine's inference tells us which files still resonate
           together without needing a language model to check. */
        if (strcmp(a->source, "wire") == 0) {
            p.use_engine = 1;
        } else {
            p.role = "scout";
        }
        p.allow_write = 0;
        p.needs_test = 0;
    } else if (a->priority == 4) {
        /* P4: weak edges — engine perception query */
        p.use_engine = 1;
        p.allow_write = 0;
        p.needs_test = 0;
    } else {
        /* P5+: low priority — skip */
        p.needs_review = 1;
    }

    return p;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Engine dispatch — structural queries via wave inference
   No language model needed. The engine resonates on
   carved topology. Waves for perception, edges for reasoning.
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    int  success;
    int  wrote_files;
    char summary[4096];
    char files_written[MAX_LINE];
} LoopResult;

static LoopResult dispatch_engine(const Action *a) {
    LoopResult r = {0};
    char cmd[MAX_CMD];
    char out[MAX_OUTPUT];

    /* Extract the file name from the action text for the query.
       Actions look like: "Re-verify: wire.h has 10 stale edge(s)..."
       We want the filename to query the engine about. */
    const char *fname = NULL;
    const char *p = strstr(a->action, "Re-verify: ");
    if (p) {
        p += 11;  /* skip "Re-verify: " */
        fname = p;
    } else {
        p = strstr(a->action, "Weak connection: ");
        if (p) { p += 17; fname = p; }
    }

    if (!fname || !fname[0]) {
        /* Can't extract a query — fall back to wire check */
        printf("  [engine] can't extract query from action, skipping\n");
        r.success = 0;
        return r;
    }

    /* Extract just the filename (until space or special char) */
    char query[256] = {0};
    int qi = 0;
    while (fname[qi] && fname[qi] != ' ' && qi < 255) {
        query[qi] = fname[qi];
        qi++;
    }
    query[qi] = 0;

    /* Check if the engine binary exists */
    struct stat st;
    const char *engine_path = NULL;
    char epath[512];

    /* Try common locations */
    const char *home = getenv("USERPROFILE");
    if (!home) home = getenv("HOME");
    if (home) {
        snprintf(epath, sizeof(epath), "%s/.local/bin/xyzt_pc.exe", home);
        if (stat(epath, &st) == 0) engine_path = epath;
    }
    if (!engine_path) {
        if (stat("xyzt_pc.exe", &st) == 0) engine_path = "xyzt_pc.exe";
    }

    if (!engine_path) {
        /* No engine binary — use xyzt_io correlate as fallback.
           Fingerprint comparison is the CLI-level perception. */
        printf("  [engine] no xyzt_pc found, using xyzt_io correlate\n");

        /* Check if the file exists and correlate against session */
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_io recall \"%s\"", query);
        int exit_code = run_capture(cmd, out, sizeof(out));
        r.success = (exit_code == 0 && out[0]);

        if (r.success) {
            /* File found in session — the relationship is verified */
            snprintf(r.summary, sizeof(r.summary),
                     "Engine (fingerprint): %s verified via xyzt_io recall", query);
        } else {
            snprintf(r.summary, sizeof(r.summary),
                     "Engine: %s not found in session", query);
        }
        printf("  [engine] %s\n", r.summary);
        return r;
    }

    /* Engine exists — run inference query */
    /* Check for saved state */
    char state_path[512] = ".xyzt/engine_state.xyzt";
    if (stat(state_path, &st) != 0) {
        /* No saved engine state — use fingerprint fallback */
        printf("  [engine] no engine state, using xyzt_io recall\n");
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_io recall \"%s\"", query);
        int exit_code = run_capture(cmd, out, sizeof(out));
        r.success = (exit_code == 0 && out[0]);
        snprintf(r.summary, sizeof(r.summary), "Fingerprint recall for %s", query);
        printf("  [engine] %s\n", r.summary);
        return r;
    }

    /* Full engine inference */
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "\"%s\" infer \"%s\" <<< \"%s\nquit\"",
             engine_path, state_path, query);
    printf("  [engine] querying: %s\n", query);
    int exit_code = run_capture(cmd, out, sizeof(out));

    r.success = (exit_code == 0 && strstr(out, "resonated"));
    if (r.success) {
        snprintf(r.summary, sizeof(r.summary),
                 "Engine inference: %s resonated in carved topology", query);
    } else {
        snprintf(r.summary, sizeof(r.summary),
                 "Engine inference: %s — no resonance (stale or disconnected)", query);
    }
    printf("  [engine] %s\n", r.summary);
    return r;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Agent execution — generative tasks via language model
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static LoopResult dispatch_agent(const Action *a, const AgentPlan *p) {
    LoopResult r = {0};
    char cmd[MAX_CMD];
    char out[MAX_OUTPUT];

    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd),
        "xyzt_agent --role %s --task \"%s\"%s --json",
        p->role, a->action, p->allow_write ? " --allow-write" : "");

    printf("  [agent] role=%s write=%s task=\"%.60s...\"\n",
           p->role, p->allow_write ? "yes" : "no", a->action);

    int exit_code = run_capture(cmd, out, sizeof(out));
    r.success = (exit_code == 0);

    /* Parse JSON result for key fields */
    /* Look for "success": true/false */
    if (strstr(out, "\"success\":true") || strstr(out, "\"success\": true"))
        r.success = 1;
    else if (strstr(out, "\"success\":false") || strstr(out, "\"success\": false"))
        r.success = 0;

    /* Check if files were written */
    if (strstr(out, "\"n_writes\":") || strstr(out, "\"n_writes\" :")) {
        const char *nw = strstr(out, "\"n_writes\"");
        if (nw) {
            nw = strchr(nw, ':');
            if (nw) {
                nw++;
                while (*nw == ' ') nw++;
                r.wrote_files = atoi(nw);
            }
        }
    }

    /* Extract summary */
    const char *sum = strstr(out, "\"summary\":\"");
    if (sum) {
        sum += 11;
        int i = 0;
        while (sum[i] && sum[i] != '"' && i < (int)sizeof(r.summary) - 1) {
            if (sum[i] == '\\' && sum[i+1]) { i++; }
            r.summary[i] = sum[i];
            i++;
        }
        r.summary[i] = 0;
    }

    return r;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Test execution after agent writes
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int run_test(void) {
    /* Check test history for the last known test command */
    TestHistory *h = state_load_tests();
    if (!h || h->count == 0) {
        free(h);
        printf("  [test] no test history — skipping\n");
        return -1;  /* unknown */
    }

    /* Re-run the last test command */
    char cmd[MAX_CMD];
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_test run \"%s\"",
             h->results[h->count - 1].cmd);
    free(h);

    char out[MAX_OUTPUT];
    printf("  [test] re-running last test...\n");
    int exit_code = run_capture(cmd, out, sizeof(out));

    if (exit_code == 0 && !strstr(out, "FAIL")) {
        printf("  [test] PASS\n");
        return 1;
    } else {
        printf("  [test] FAIL\n");
        return 0;
    }
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Wire learning from loop results
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void learn_result(const Action *a, const LoopResult *r, int test_pass) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();

    /* Create/find nodes for the action source and agent role */
    char concept_name[256];
    snprintf(concept_name, sizeof(concept_name), "loop:%s", a->source);
    int cid = wire_add(g, concept_name, WNODE_CONCEPT);

    /* Learn: success + test pass = strengthen, else weaken */
    int pass = r->success && (test_pass != 0);  /* test_pass: 1=pass, -1=unknown(ok), 0=fail */
    if (test_pass == -1 && r->success) pass = 1;  /* no test needed, agent succeeded */

    wire_adjust(g, cid, pass);

    /* If the action was about a specific file, learn that connection too */
    /* Extract filename from action text if present */
    const char *p = strstr(a->action, ".c");
    if (!p) p = strstr(a->action, ".h");
    if (!p) p = strstr(a->action, ".py");
    if (p) {
        /* Walk backwards to find start of filename */
        const char *start = p;
        while (start > a->action && start[-1] != ' ' && start[-1] != '/' && start[-1] != '\\')
            start--;
        char fname[256] = {0};
        int flen = (int)(p - start + 2);  /* include extension */
        if (flen > 0 && flen < 255) {
            memcpy(fname, start, flen);
            fname[flen] = 0;
            int fid = wire_add(g, fname, WNODE_FILE);
            if (fid >= 0 && cid >= 0) {
                wire_connect(g, cid, fid);
                wire_adjust(g, fid, pass);
            }
        }
    }

    wire_save(g);
    wire_free(g);

    printf("  [wire] learned: %s (pass=%d)\n", concept_name, pass);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Review queue — human needs to look at this
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void queue_for_review(const Action *a, const char *reason) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(".xyzt/review_queue.txt", "a");
    if (!fp) return;
    time_t now = time(NULL);
    struct tm *tm = localtime(&now);
    char date[32];
    strftime(date, sizeof(date), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M", tm);
    fprintf(fp, "[%s] P%d [%s] %s — %s\n", date, a->priority, a->source, a->action, reason);
    fclose(fp);
    printf("  [review] queued: %.60s... (%s)\n", a->action, reason);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main loop
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void print_banner(void) {
    printf("═══════════════════════════════════════════\n");
    printf("  XYZT LOOP — Autonomous Work Daemon\n");
    printf("  Cycle: next → agent → test → learn\n");
    printf("═══════════════════════════════════════════\n\n");
}

static void cmd_loop(int max_cycles, int dry_run, int verbose) {
    print_banner();

    int idle_count = 0;
    int total_dispatched = 0;
    int total_passed = 0;
    int total_failed = 0;

    for (int cycle = 0; cycle < max_cycles; cycle++) {
        printf("── cycle %d/%d ──\n", cycle + 1, max_cycles);

        /* Step 1: Get priorities */
        char next_out[MAX_OUTPUT];
        run_capture("xyzt_next", next_out, sizeof(next_out));

        ActionQueue q;
        int n = parse_next_output(next_out, &q);

        if (n == 0) {
            idle_count++;
            printf("  nothing to do (%d/%d idle cycles)\n", idle_count, IDLE_CYCLES);
            if (idle_count >= IDLE_CYCLES) {
                printf("\nidle limit reached — exiting\n");
                break;
            }
            SLEEP_MS(LOOP_INTERVAL_MS);
            continue;
        }
        idle_count = 0;

        /* Step 2: Plan and dispatch top action */
        Action *top = &q.items[0];
        printf("  [next] P%d [%s] %s\n", top->priority, top->source, top->action);

        AgentPlan plan = plan_action(top);

        if (plan.needs_review) {
            queue_for_review(top, "low priority, skipping");
            SLEEP_MS(LOOP_INTERVAL_MS);
            continue;
        }

        if (dry_run) {
            printf("  [dry-run] would dispatch: role=%s write=%s test=%s\n",
                   plan.role, plan.allow_write ? "yes" : "no",
                   plan.needs_test ? "yes" : "no");
            SLEEP_MS(LOOP_INTERVAL_MS);
            continue;
        }

        /* Step 3: Dispatch — engine for structural queries, agent for generative tasks */
        LoopResult result;
        if (plan.use_engine) {
            result = dispatch_engine(top);
        } else {
            result = dispatch_agent(top, &plan);
        }
        total_dispatched++;

        printf("  [result] success=%d wrote=%d\n", result.success, result.wrote_files);

        /* Step 4: Test if needed */
        int test_pass = -1;  /* -1 = not applicable */
        if (plan.needs_test && result.wrote_files > 0) {
            test_pass = run_test();
            if (test_pass == 0) {
                /* Test failed after agent wrote code — queue for review */
                queue_for_review(top, "agent wrote code but test failed");
                total_failed++;
            } else {
                total_passed++;
            }
        } else if (result.success) {
            total_passed++;
        } else {
            total_failed++;
        }

        /* Step 5: Learn */
        learn_result(top, &result, test_pass);

        /* Brief pause between cycles */
        if (cycle < max_cycles - 1) {
            SLEEP_MS(LOOP_INTERVAL_MS);
        }
    }

    /* Summary */
    printf("\n═══════════════════════════════════════════\n");
    printf("  LOOP COMPLETE\n");
    printf("  dispatched: %d  passed: %d  failed: %d\n",
           total_dispatched, total_passed, total_failed);
    printf("═══════════════════════════════════════════\n");
}

/* One-shot: run next action once, don't loop */
static void cmd_once(int dry_run, int verbose) {
    char next_out[MAX_OUTPUT];
    run_capture("xyzt_next", next_out, sizeof(next_out));

    ActionQueue q;
    int n = parse_next_output(next_out, &q);

    if (n == 0) {
        printf("nothing to do\n");
        return;
    }

    Action *top = &q.items[0];
    printf("P%d [%s] %s\n", top->priority, top->source, top->action);

    AgentPlan plan = plan_action(top);

    if (plan.needs_review) {
        printf("skipped (needs human review)\n");
        return;
    }

    if (dry_run) {
        printf("would dispatch: role=%s write=%s test=%s\n",
               plan.role, plan.allow_write ? "yes" : "no",
               plan.needs_test ? "yes" : "no");
        return;
