    }

    LoopResult result;
    if (plan.use_engine) {
        result = dispatch_engine(top);
    } else {
        result = dispatch_agent(top, &plan);
    }
    printf("success=%d wrote=%d\n", result.success, result.wrote_files);

    int test_pass = -1;
    if (plan.needs_test && result.wrote_files > 0) {
        test_pass = run_test();
    }

    learn_result(top, &result, test_pass);
}

/* Status: show what loop would do without doing it */
static void cmd_status(void) {
    char next_out[MAX_OUTPUT];
    run_capture("xyzt_next", next_out, sizeof(next_out));

    ActionQueue q;
    int n = parse_next_output(next_out, &q);

    printf("---XYZT-LOOP-STATUS---\n");
    if (n == 0) {
        printf("queue empty — nothing to do\n");
    } else {
        printf("%d action(s) queued:\n", n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            AgentPlan p = plan_action(&q.items[i]);
            printf("  P%d [%-7s] → %s%s%s%s | %.60s\n",
                   q.items[i].priority, q.items[i].source,
                   p.use_engine ? "ENGINE" : p.role,
                   p.allow_write ? " +write" : "",
                   p.needs_test ? " +test" : "",
                   p.use_engine ? " (wave inference)" : "",
                   q.items[i].action);
        }
    }

    /* Review queue */
    FILE *fp = fopen(".xyzt/review_queue.txt", "r");
    if (fp) {
        char line[512];
        int count = 0;
        while (fgets(line, sizeof(line), fp)) count++;
        fclose(fp);
        if (count > 0)
            printf("\n%d item(s) in review queue (.xyzt/review_queue.txt)\n", count);
    }
    printf("---END-LOOP-STATUS---\n");
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   CLI
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT LOOP — Autonomous Work Daemon\n\n");
    printf("  xyzt_loop run [N]       Run up to N cycles (default %d)\n", MAX_CYCLES);
    printf("  xyzt_loop once          Run one cycle then exit\n");
    printf("  xyzt_loop status        Show what would happen (dry)\n");
    printf("  xyzt_loop dry [N]       Dry run — plan but don't execute\n");
    printf("\nCycle: xyzt_next → pick top → agent → test → wire learn → repeat\n");
    printf("Exits after %d idle cycles or N total cycles.\n", IDLE_CYCLES);
    printf("Review items go to .xyzt/review_queue.txt\n");
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — March 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) { usage(); return 0; }
    const char *cmd = argv[1];

    if (strcmp(cmd, "run") == 0) {
        int n = (argc >= 3) ? atoi(argv[2]) : MAX_CYCLES;
        if (n <= 0) n = MAX_CYCLES;
        cmd_loop(n, 0, 0);
    } else if (strcmp(cmd, "once") == 0) {
        cmd_once(0, 0);
    } else if (strcmp(cmd, "status") == 0) {
        cmd_status();
    } else if (strcmp(cmd, "dry") == 0) {
        int n = (argc >= 3) ? atoi(argv[2]) : MAX_CYCLES;
        if (n <= 0) n = MAX_CYCLES;
        cmd_loop(n, 1, 0);
    } else {
        usage();
        return 1;
    }

    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_map.c — Project Dependency Graph
 *
 * Scans project directory, parses import/include statements,
 * builds dependency graph. Auto-links hub files to wire.
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — February 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <dirent.h>
#include "wire.h"

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Constants & structures
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define MAX_FILES 1024
#define MAX_DEPS  8192
#define PATH_LEN  256
#define MAP_MAGIC 0x4D415000  /* "MAP\0" */

typedef struct {
    char path[PATH_LEN];
    char lang[16];
    uint32_t lines;
    uint32_t deps_out;
    uint32_t deps_in;
} MapFile;

typedef struct {
    uint16_t src;
    uint16_t dst;
} MapDep;

typedef struct {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint32_t n_files;
    uint32_t n_deps;
    MapFile  files[MAX_FILES];
    MapDep   deps[MAX_DEPS];
} MapGraph;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Helpers
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int should_skip(const char *name) {
    const char *skip[] = {".git", "node_modules", "__pycache__", ".xyzt",
                          "build", "target", ".vs", ".vscode", NULL};
    for (int i = 0; skip[i]; i++)
        if (strcmp(name, skip[i]) == 0) return 1;
    return 0;
}

static const char *detect_lang(const char *path) {
    const char *dot = strrchr(path, '.');
    if (!dot) return NULL;
    if (strcmp(dot, ".c") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".h") == 0) return "c";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".cpp") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".hpp") == 0 ||
        strcmp(dot, ".cc") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".cxx") == 0) return "cpp";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".py") == 0) return "python";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".js") == 0) return "javascript";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".ts") == 0) return "typescript";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".tsx") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".jsx") == 0) return "javascript";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".rs") == 0) return "rust";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".go") == 0) return "go";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".v") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".sv") == 0) return "verilog";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".cu") == 0) return "cuda";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".java") == 0) return "java";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".md") == 0) return "markdown";
    return NULL;
}

static uint32_t count_lines(const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "r");
    if (!fp) return 0;
    uint32_t n = 0;
    int ch;
    while ((ch = fgetc(fp)) != EOF)
        if (ch == '\n') n++;
    fclose(fp);
    return n;
}

static int file_exists(const char *path) {
    struct stat st;
    return stat(path, &st) == 0;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Graph ops
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int map_find(MapGraph *g, const char *path) {
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_files; i++)
        if (strcmp(g->files[i].path, path) == 0) return (int)i;
    return -1;
}

static int map_add(MapGraph *g, const char *path, const char *lang) {
    int idx = map_find(g, path);
    if (idx >= 0) return idx;
    if (g->n_files >= MAX_FILES) return -1;
    MapFile *f = &g->files[g->n_files];
    strncpy(f->path, path, PATH_LEN - 1);
    strncpy(f->lang, lang ? lang : "unknown", 15);
    f->lines = count_lines(path);
    f->deps_out = 0;
    f->deps_in = 0;
    return (int)g->n_files++;
}

static void map_dep(MapGraph *g, int src, int dst) {
    if (src < 0 || dst < 0 || src == dst) return;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_deps; i++)
        if (g->deps[i].src == (uint16_t)src && g->deps[i].dst == (uint16_t)dst) return;
    if (g->n_deps >= MAX_DEPS) return;
    g->deps[g->n_deps].src = (uint16_t)src;
    g->deps[g->n_deps].dst = (uint16_t)dst;
    g->n_deps++;
    g->files[src].deps_out++;
    g->files[dst].deps_in++;
}

/* Resolve import path relative to source file's directory */
static void resolve_path(const char *source, const char *import, char *out, int maxlen) {
    char dir[PATH_LEN] = "";
    strncpy(dir, source, PATH_LEN - 1);
    char *slash = strrchr(dir, '/');
    char *bslash = strrchr(dir, '\\');
    if (bslash && (!slash || bslash > slash)) slash = bslash;
    if (slash) *(slash + 1) = '\0';
    else dir[0] = '\0';
    snprintf(out, maxlen, "%s%s", dir, import);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Import parsers (one per language family)
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void parse_c(MapGraph *g, int src, const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "r");
    if (!fp) return;
    char line[1024];
    while (fgets(line, sizeof(line), fp)) {
        char *inc = strstr(line, "#include");
        if (!inc) continue;
        char *q1 = strchr(inc, '"');
        if (!q1) continue;
        char *q2 = strchr(q1 + 1, '"');
        if (!q2) continue;
        int len = (int)(q2 - q1 - 1);
        if (len <= 0 || len >= PATH_LEN) continue;
        char import[PATH_LEN];
        strncpy(import, q1 + 1, len);
        import[len] = '\0';
        char resolved[PATH_LEN];
        resolve_path(path, import, resolved, PATH_LEN);
        int dst = map_find(g, resolved);
        if (dst < 0 && file_exists(resolved))
            dst = map_add(g, resolved, detect_lang(resolved));
        if (dst >= 0) map_dep(g, src, dst);
    }
    fclose(fp);
}

static void parse_python(MapGraph *g, int src, const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "r");
    if (!fp) return;
    char line[1024];
    while (fgets(line, sizeof(line), fp)) {
        char module[PATH_LEN] = "";
        if (strncmp(line, "from ", 5) == 0) {
            char *space = strchr(line + 5, ' ');
            if (space) {
                int len = (int)(space - (line + 5));
                if (len > 0 && len < PATH_LEN) { strncpy(module, line + 5, len); module[len] = '\0'; }
            }
        } else if (strncmp(line, "import ", 7) == 0) {
            char *end = line + 7;
            while (*end && *end != '\n' && *end != ' ' && *end != ',') end++;
            int len = (int)(end - (line + 7));
            if (len > 0 && len < PATH_LEN) { strncpy(module, line + 7, len); module[len] = '\0'; }
        }
        if (module[0] == '\0' || module[0] == '.') continue;
        for (char *p = module; *p; p++) if (*p == '.') *p = '/';
        char candidate[PATH_LEN];
        resolve_path(path, module, candidate, PATH_LEN - 4);
        strcat(candidate, ".py");
        int dst = map_find(g, candidate);
        if (dst < 0 && file_exists(candidate))
            dst = map_add(g, candidate, "python");
        if (dst >= 0) map_dep(g, src, dst);
    }
    fclose(fp);
}

static void parse_js(MapGraph *g, int src, const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "r");
    if (!fp) return;
    char line[1024];
    while (fgets(line, sizeof(line), fp)) {
        char *from = strstr(line, "from ");
        if (!from) from = strstr(line, "require(");
        if (!from) continue;
        char *q = strchr(from, '\'');
        char *dq = strchr(from, '"');
        if (dq && (!q || dq < q)) q = dq;
        if (!q) continue;
        char qc = *q;
        char *q2 = strchr(q + 1, qc);
        if (!q2) continue;
        int len = (int)(q2 - q - 1);
        if (len <= 0 || len >= PATH_LEN) continue;
        char import[PATH_LEN];
        strncpy(import, q + 1, len); import[len] = '\0';
        if (import[0] != '.' && import[0] != '/') continue;
        char resolved[PATH_LEN];
        resolve_path(path, import, resolved, PATH_LEN);
        const char *exts[] = {"", ".js", ".ts", ".tsx", ".jsx", NULL};
        for (int i = 0; exts[i]; i++) {
            char candidate[PATH_LEN];
            snprintf(candidate, PATH_LEN, "%s%s", resolved, exts[i]);
            int dst = map_find(g, candidate);
            if (dst < 0 && file_exists(candidate))
                dst = map_add(g, candidate, detect_lang(candidate));
            if (dst >= 0) { map_dep(g, src, dst); break; }
        }
    }
    fclose(fp);
}

static void parse_rust(MapGraph *g, int src, const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "r");
    if (!fp) return;
    char line[1024];
    while (fgets(line, sizeof(line), fp)) {
        if (strncmp(line, "mod ", 4) != 0) continue;
        char *semi = strchr(line + 4, ';');
        if (!semi) continue;
        int len = (int)(semi - (line + 4));
        while (len > 0 && (line[4 + len - 1] == ' ' || line[4 + len - 1] == '\t')) len--;
        if (len <= 0 || len >= PATH_LEN) continue;
        char module[PATH_LEN];
        strncpy(module, line + 4, len); module[len] = '\0';
        char candidate[PATH_LEN];
        resolve_path(path, module, candidate, PATH_LEN - 4);
        strcat(candidate, ".rs");
        int dst = map_find(g, candidate);
        if (dst < 0 && file_exists(candidate))
            dst = map_add(g, candidate, "rust");
        if (dst >= 0) map_dep(g, src, dst);
    }
    fclose(fp);
}

static void parse_verilog(MapGraph *g, int src, const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "r");
    if (!fp) return;
    char line[1024];
    while (fgets(line, sizeof(line), fp)) {
        char *inc = strstr(line, "`include");
        if (!inc) continue;
        char *q1 = strchr(inc, '"');
        if (!q1) continue;
        char *q2 = strchr(q1 + 1, '"');
        if (!q2) continue;
        int len = (int)(q2 - q1 - 1);
        if (len <= 0 || len >= PATH_LEN) continue;
        char import[PATH_LEN];
        strncpy(import, q1 + 1, len); import[len] = '\0';
        char resolved[PATH_LEN];
        resolve_path(path, import, resolved, PATH_LEN);
        int dst = map_find(g, resolved);
        if (dst < 0 && file_exists(resolved))
            dst = map_add(g, resolved, detect_lang(resolved));
        if (dst >= 0) map_dep(g, src, dst);
    }
    fclose(fp);
}

static void parse_imports(MapGraph *g, int idx) {
    const char *lang = g->files[idx].lang;
    if (strcmp(lang, "c") == 0 || strcmp(lang, "cpp") == 0 || strcmp(lang, "cuda") == 0)
        parse_c(g, idx, g->files[idx].path);
    else if (strcmp(lang, "python") == 0)
        parse_python(g, idx, g->files[idx].path);
    else if (strcmp(lang, "javascript") == 0 || strcmp(lang, "typescript") == 0)
        parse_js(g, idx, g->files[idx].path);
    else if (strcmp(lang, "rust") == 0)
        parse_rust(g, idx, g->files[idx].path);
    else if (strcmp(lang, "verilog") == 0)
        parse_verilog(g, idx, g->files[idx].path);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Directory scanner
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void scan_dir(MapGraph *g, const char *dir) {
    DIR *dp = opendir(dir);
    if (!dp) return;
    struct dirent *entry;
    while ((entry = readdir(dp)) != NULL) {
        if (entry->d_name[0] == '.') continue;
        if (should_skip(entry->d_name)) continue;
        char fullpath[PATH_LEN];
        snprintf(fullpath, PATH_LEN, "%s/%s", dir, entry->d_name);
        struct stat st;
        if (stat(fullpath, &st) != 0) continue;
        if (S_ISDIR(st.st_mode)) {
            scan_dir(g, fullpath);
        } else {
            const char *lang = detect_lang(entry->d_name);
            if (lang) map_add(g, fullpath, lang);
        }
    }
    closedir(dp);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Persistence
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define MAP_PATH ".xyzt/map.bin"

static void map_save(MapGraph *g) {
    xyzt_ensure_dir(MAP_PATH);
    FILE *fp = fopen(MAP_PATH, "wb");
    if (!fp) { fprintf(stderr, "Cannot write: %s\n", MAP_PATH); return; }
    fwrite(g, sizeof(MapGraph), 1, fp);
    fclose(fp);
}

static MapGraph *map_load(void) {
    MapGraph *g = calloc(1, sizeof(MapGraph));
    FILE *fp = fopen(MAP_PATH, "rb");
    if (!fp) return g;
    if (fread(g, sizeof(MapGraph), 1, fp) != 1 || g->magic != MAP_MAGIC) {
        memset(g, 0, sizeof(MapGraph));
    }
    fclose(fp);
    return g;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Union-find for clustering
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int uf_find(int *parent, int x) {
    while (parent[x] != x) { parent[x] = parent[parent[x]]; x = parent[x]; }
    return x;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Commands
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_scan(const char *dir) {
    MapGraph *g = calloc(1, sizeof(MapGraph));
    g->magic = MAP_MAGIC;

    scan_dir(g, dir);

    uint32_t n = g->n_files;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < n; i++)
        parse_imports(g, (int)i);

    /* Hub detection: above-average deps_in */
    uint32_t total_in = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_files; i++) total_in += g->files[i].deps_in;
    uint32_t avg_in = g->n_files > 0 ? total_in / g->n_files : 0;

    int linked = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_files; i++) {
        if (g->files[i].deps_in > avg_in)
            if (wire_auto_link(g->files[i].path)) linked++;
    }

    map_save(g);

    printf("---XYZT-MAP-SCAN---\n");
    printf("root: %s\n", dir);
    printf("files: %u\n", g->n_files);
    printf("deps: %u\n", g->n_deps);
    printf("hubs:");
    int hc = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_files; i++) {
        if (g->files[i].deps_in > avg_in) {
            printf(" %s(%u)", g->files[i].path, g->files[i].deps_in);
            hc++;
        }
    }
    if (hc == 0) printf(" (none)");
    printf("\n");
    if (linked > 0) printf("wire: %d hub files linked to active problem\n", linked);
    printf("---END-SCAN---\n");
    free(g);
}

static void cmd_show(void) {
    MapGraph *g = map_load();
    if (g->n_files == 0) { printf("No map data. Run: xyzt_map scan\n"); free(g); return; }
    printf("---XYZT-MAP---\n");
    printf("files: %u  deps: %u\n\n", g->n_files, g->n_deps);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_files; i++) {
        MapFile *f = &g->files[i];
        printf("  [%3u] %-40s %6u lines  %-12s  in=%u out=%u\n",
               i, f->path, f->lines, f->lang, f->deps_in, f->deps_out);
    }
    if (g->n_deps > 0) {
        printf("\ndependencies:\n");
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_deps; i++)
            printf("  %s -> %s\n", g->files[g->deps[i].src].path, g->files[g->deps[i].dst].path);
    }
    printf("---END-MAP---\n");
    free(g);
}

static void cmd_hub(void) {
    MapGraph *g = map_load();
    if (g->n_files == 0) { printf("No map data. Run: xyzt_map scan\n"); free(g); return; }
    /* Simple insertion sort by deps_in descending */
    int order[MAX_FILES];
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_files; i++) order[i] = (int)i;
    for (uint32_t i = 1; i < g->n_files; i++) {
        int tmp = order[i];
        int j = (int)i - 1;
        while (j >= 0 && g->files[order[j]].deps_in < g->files[tmp].deps_in) {
            order[j + 1] = order[j]; j--;
        }
        order[j + 1] = tmp;
    }
    printf("---XYZT-MAP-HUBS---\n");
    int shown = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_files && shown < 20; i++) {
        if (g->files[order[i]].deps_in == 0) break;
        MapFile *f = &g->files[order[i]];
        printf("  %3u dependents  %-40s  %s\n", f->deps_in, f->path, f->lang);
        shown++;
    }
    if (shown == 0) printf("  (no files with dependents)\n");
    printf("---END-HUBS---\n");
    free(g);
}

static void cmd_deps(const char *target) {
    MapGraph *g = map_load();
    if (g->n_files == 0) { printf("No map data. Run: xyzt_map scan\n"); free(g); return; }
    /* Exact match, then suffix match */
    int idx = map_find(g, target);
    if (idx < 0) {
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_files; i++) {
            char *base = strrchr(g->files[i].path, '/');
            if (!base) base = g->files[i].path; else base++;
            if (strcmp(base, target) == 0) { idx = (int)i; break; }
        }
    }
    if (idx < 0) { printf("Not found: %s\n", target); free(g); return; }
    MapFile *f = &g->files[idx];
    printf("---XYZT-MAP-DEPS---\n");
    printf("file: %s (%s, %u lines)\n\n", f->path, f->lang, f->lines);
    printf("imports (%u):\n", f->deps_out);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_deps; i++)
        if (g->deps[i].src == (uint16_t)idx)
            printf("  -> %s\n", g->files[g->deps[i].dst].path);
    printf("\nimported by (%u):\n", f->deps_in);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_deps; i++)
        if (g->deps[i].dst == (uint16_t)idx)
            printf("  <- %s\n", g->files[g->deps[i].src].path);
    printf("---END-DEPS---\n");
    free(g);
}

static void cmd_cluster(void) {
    MapGraph *g = map_load();
    if (g->n_files == 0) { printf("No map data. Run: xyzt_map scan\n"); free(g); return; }
    int parent[MAX_FILES];
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_files; i++) parent[i] = (int)i;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_deps; i++) {
        int a = uf_find(parent, g->deps[i].src);
        int b = uf_find(parent, g->deps[i].dst);
        if (a != b) parent[a] = b;
    }
    printf("---XYZT-MAP-CLUSTERS---\n");
    int shown[MAX_FILES] = {0};
    int cn = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_files; i++) {
        int root = uf_find(parent, (int)i);
        if (shown[root]) continue;
        shown[root] = 1;
        int count = 0;
        for (uint32_t j = 0; j < g->n_files; j++)
            if (uf_find(parent, (int)j) == root) count++;
        printf("cluster %d (%d files):\n", ++cn, count);
        for (uint32_t j = 0; j < g->n_files; j++)
            if (uf_find(parent, (int)j) == root)
                printf("  %s\n", g->files[j].path);
    }
    printf("---END-CLUSTERS---\n");
    free(g);
}

static void cmd_brief(void) {
    MapGraph *g = map_load();
    if (g->n_files == 0) { printf("---XYZT-MAP-BRIEF---\nempty\n---END-BRIEF---\n"); free(g); return; }
    typedef struct { char lang[16]; int count; uint32_t lines; } LC;
    LC langs[32]; int nl = 0;
    uint32_t total_lines = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_files; i++) {
        total_lines += g->files[i].lines;
        int found = 0;
        for (int j = 0; j < nl; j++) {
            if (strcmp(langs[j].lang, g->files[i].lang) == 0) { langs[j].count++; langs[j].lines += g->files[i].lines; found = 1; break; }
        }
        if (!found && nl < 32) { strncpy(langs[nl].lang, g->files[i].lang, 15); langs[nl].count = 1; langs[nl].lines = g->files[i].lines; nl++; }
    }
    int top_hub = -1; uint32_t top_in = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_files; i++)
        if (g->files[i].deps_in > top_in) { top_in = g->files[i].deps_in; top_hub = (int)i; }
    printf("---XYZT-MAP-BRIEF---\n");
    printf("files: %u\n", g->n_files);
    printf("lines: %u\n", total_lines);
    printf("deps: %u\n", g->n_deps);
    printf("languages:");
    for (int i = 0; i < nl; i++) printf(" %s(%d)", langs[i].lang, langs[i].count);
    printf("\n");
    if (top_hub >= 0) printf("hub: %s (%u dependents)\n", g->files[top_hub].path, top_in);
    printf("---END-BRIEF---\n");
    free(g);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT MAP — Project Dependency Graph\n\n");
    printf("  xyzt_map scan [dir]       Scan directory, build dep graph\n");
    printf("  xyzt_map show             Show stored dependency graph\n");
    printf("  xyzt_map hub              Show hub files (most dependents)\n");
    printf("  xyzt_map deps <file>      Show dependencies for a file\n");
    printf("  xyzt_map cluster          Show connected clusters\n");
    printf("  xyzt_map brief            Machine-readable project overview\n");
    printf("\nSession: .xyzt/map.bin\n");
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — February 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) { usage(); return 0; }
    const char *cmd = argv[1];
    if (strcmp(cmd, "scan") == 0)         cmd_scan(argc >= 3 ? argv[2] : ".");
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "show") == 0)    cmd_show();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "hub") == 0)     cmd_hub();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "deps") == 0 && argc >= 3) cmd_deps(argv[2]);
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "cluster") == 0) cmd_cluster();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "brief") == 0)   cmd_brief();
    else { usage(); return 1; }
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_mcp.c — MCP Server for XYZT Toolkit
 *
 * Exposes XYZT tools via Model Context Protocol (JSON-RPC over stdio).
 * Works with any MCP-compatible client: Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Qwen Code.
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O2 -o xyzt_mcp.exe xyzt_mcp.c
 * Install: copy to ~/.local/bin/
 *
 * Isaac Oravec & Claude — March 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>

#ifdef _WIN32
#include <windows.h>
#include <io.h>
#include <fcntl.h>
#else
#include <unistd.h>
#endif

#define MAX_INPUT   262144  /* 256KB max request */
#define MAX_OUTPUT  131072  /* 128KB max tool output */
#define MAX_CMD     8192

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * JSON helpers (minimal — just enough for MCP)
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int jget(const char *json, const char *key, char *out, int max) {
    char pat[256];
    int klen = snprintf(pat, sizeof(pat), "\"%s\"", key);
    const char *p = json;
    while ((p = strstr(p, pat)) != NULL) {
        char after = p[klen];
        if (after == ':' || after == ' ' || after == '\t' || after == '\n' || after == '\r')
            break;
        p += klen;
    }
    if (!p) return 0;
    p += klen;
    while (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\n' || *p == '\r' || *p == ':') p++;
    if (*p != '"') return 0;
    p++;
    int i = 0;
    while (*p && *p != '"' && i < max - 1) {
        if (*p == '\\' && p[1]) {
            p++;
            switch (*p) {
                case 'n':  out[i++] = '\n'; break;
                case 't':  out[i++] = '\t'; break;
                case 'r':  break;
                case '\\': out[i++] = '\\'; break;
                case '"':  out[i++] = '"';  break;
                case '/':  out[i++] = '/';  break;
                default:   out[i++] = *p;   break;
            }
        } else {
            out[i++] = *p;
        }
        p++;
    }
    out[i] = 0;
    return i;
}

/* Extract raw JSON value (number, bool, etc) */
static int jget_num(const char *json, const char *key) {
    char val[64] = {0};
    char pat[256];
    int klen = snprintf(pat, sizeof(pat), "\"%s\"", key);
    const char *p = json;
    while ((p = strstr(p, pat)) != NULL) {
        char after = p[klen];
        if (after == ':' || after == ' ' || after == '\t') break;
        p += klen;
    }
    if (!p) return 0;
    p += klen;
    while (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == ':') p++;
    int i = 0;
    while (*p && *p != ',' && *p != '}' && *p != ']' && i < 63)
        val[i++] = *p++;
    val[i] = 0;
    return atoi(val);
}

/* JSON-escape a string into buffer, return bytes written */
static int json_escape(const char *src, char *dst, int max) {
    int i = 0;
    for (; *src && i < max - 6; src++) {
        switch (*src) {
            case '"':  dst[i++] = '\\'; dst[i++] = '"';  break;
            case '\\': dst[i++] = '\\'; dst[i++] = '\\'; break;
            case '\n': dst[i++] = '\\'; dst[i++] = 'n';  break;
            case '\r': dst[i++] = '\\'; dst[i++] = 'r';  break;
            case '\t': dst[i++] = '\\'; dst[i++] = 't';  break;
            default:
                if ((unsigned char)*src >= 0x20) dst[i++] = *src;
                break;
        }
    }
    dst[i] = 0;
    return i;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * Tool Definitions
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    const char *name;
    const char *description;
    const char *schema; /* JSON input_schema */
} McpTool;

#define N_TOOLS (int)(sizeof(TOOLS) / sizeof(TOOLS[0]))

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * Tool Execution — map MCP tool name to shell command
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int run_tool(const char *command, char *out, int max) {
#ifdef _WIN32
    char cmdline[MAX_CMD];
    snprintf(cmdline, sizeof(cmdline), "cmd.exe /c \"%s\"", command);

    HANDLE hReadPipe = NULL, hWritePipe = NULL;
    SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES sa = { sizeof(sa), NULL, TRUE };
    CreatePipe(&hReadPipe, &hWritePipe, &sa, 0);
    SetHandleInformation(hReadPipe, HANDLE_FLAG_INHERIT, 0);

    STARTUPINFOA si;
    PROCESS_INFORMATION pi;
    ZeroMemory(&si, sizeof(si));
    si.cb = sizeof(si);
    si.dwFlags = STARTF_USESTDHANDLES;
    si.hStdInput = NULL;
    si.hStdOutput = hWritePipe;
    si.hStdError = hWritePipe;

    ZeroMemory(&pi, sizeof(pi));
    BOOL ok = CreateProcessA(NULL, cmdline, NULL, NULL, TRUE,
                             CREATE_NO_WINDOW, NULL, NULL, &si, &pi);
    CloseHandle(hWritePipe);

    if (!ok) { CloseHandle(hReadPipe); return -1; }

    int total = 0;
    DWORD bytesRead;
    while (total < max - 1) {
        BOOL r = ReadFile(hReadPipe, out + total, max - 1 - total, &bytesRead, NULL);
        if (!r || bytesRead == 0) break;
        total += bytesRead;
    }
    out[total] = 0;

    WaitForSingleObject(pi.hProcess, 30000);
    CloseHandle(pi.hProcess);
    CloseHandle(pi.hThread);
    CloseHandle(hReadPipe);
    return 0;
#else
    FILE *fp = popen(command, "r");
    if (!fp) return -1;
    int total = 0;
    while (total < max - 1) {
        int n = fread(out + total, 1, max - 1 - total, fp);
        if (n <= 0) break;
        total += n;
    }
    out[total] = 0;
    pclose(fp);
    return 0;
#endif
}

static char *execute_mcp_tool(const char *name, const char *args_json) {
    char cmd[MAX_CMD] = {0};
    char val[4096] = {0};

    if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_skel") == 0) {
        jget(args_json, "file", val, sizeof(val));
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_skel \"%s\"", val);
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_index_find") == 0) {
        jget(args_json, "name", val, sizeof(val));
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_index find \"%s\"", val);
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_index_scan") == 0) {
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_index scan");
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_index_list") == 0) {
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_index list");
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_io_brief") == 0) {
        jget(args_json, "file", val, sizeof(val));
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_io brief \"%s\"", val);
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_io_store") == 0) {
        jget(args_json, "file", val, sizeof(val));
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_io store \"%s\"", val);
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_org_tree") == 0) {
        jget(args_json, "project", val, sizeof(val));
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_org tree \"%s\"", val);
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_org_scan") == 0) {
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_org scan");
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_map_show") == 0) {
        jget(args_json, "file", val, sizeof(val));
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_map show \"%s\"", val);
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_map_hub") == 0) {
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_map hub");
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_wire_graph") == 0) {
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_wire graph");
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_wire_query") == 0) {
        jget(args_json, "name", val, sizeof(val));
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_wire query \"%s\"", val);
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_resume") == 0) {
        char quick[16] = {0};
        jget(args_json, "quick", quick, sizeof(quick));
        if (quick[0] == 't' || quick[0] == '1')
            snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_resume quick");
        else
            snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_resume");
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_next") == 0) {
        char brief[16] = {0};
        jget(args_json, "brief", brief, sizeof(brief));
        if (brief[0] == 't' || brief[0] == '1')
            snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_next brief");
        else
            snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_next");
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_test_run") == 0) {
        jget(args_json, "command", val, sizeof(val));
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_test run \"%s\"", val);
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_err_recent") == 0) {
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_err recent");
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_err_patterns") == 0) {
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_err patterns");
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_diff_status") == 0) {
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_diff status");
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "onetwo_init") == 0) {
        jget(args_json, "problem", val, sizeof(val));
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "onetwo init \"%s\"", val);
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "onetwo_known") == 0) {
        jget(args_json, "fact", val, sizeof(val));
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "onetwo known \"%s\"", val);
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "onetwo_unknown") == 0) {
        jget(args_json, "question", val, sizeof(val));
        snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "onetwo unknown \"%s\"", val);
    }
    else if (strcmp(name, "xyzt_agent_spawn") == 0) {
        char role[32] = {0}, task[4096] = {0}, aw[16] = {0}, jo[16] = {0};
        jget(args_json, "role", role, sizeof(role));
        jget(args_json, "task", task, sizeof(task));
        jget(args_json, "allow_write", aw, sizeof(aw));
        jget(args_json, "json_output", jo, sizeof(jo));
        int pos = snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_agent --role %s --task \"%s\"", role, task);
        if (aw[0] == 't' || aw[0] == '1')
            pos += snprintf(cmd + pos, sizeof(cmd) - pos, " --allow-write");
        if (jo[0] == 't' || jo[0] == '1')
            pos += snprintf(cmd + pos, sizeof(cmd) - pos, " --json");
    }
    else {
        char *err = malloc(128);
        snprintf(err, 128, "Unknown tool: %s", name);
        return err;
    }

    char *out = malloc(MAX_OUTPUT);
    if (!out) return NULL;
    out[0] = 0;

    if (run_tool(cmd, out, MAX_OUTPUT) < 0) {
        snprintf(out, MAX_OUTPUT, "Tool execution failed: %s", name);
    }

    return out;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * MCP Protocol — JSON-RPC over stdio
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void send_response(const char *json) {
    int len = (int)strlen(json);
    printf("Content-Length: %d\r\n\r\n%s", len, json);
    fflush(stdout);
}

static void handle_initialize(int id) {
    char resp[1024];
    snprintf(resp, sizeof(resp),
        "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":%d,\"result\":{"
        "\"protocolVersion\":\"2024-11-05\","
        "\"capabilities\":{\"tools\":{}},"
        "\"serverInfo\":{\"name\":\"xyzt-mcp\",\"version\":\"1.0.0\"}"
        "}}", id);
    send_response(resp);
}

static void handle_tools_list(int id) {
    /* Build tools list */
    char *buf = malloc(MAX_OUTPUT);
    if (!buf) return;
    int pos = snprintf(buf, MAX_OUTPUT,
        "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":%d,\"result\":{\"tools\":[", id);

    for (int i = 0; i < N_TOOLS; i++) {
        if (i > 0) buf[pos++] = ',';
        pos += snprintf(buf + pos, MAX_OUTPUT - pos,
            "{\"name\":\"%s\",\"description\":\"%s\",\"inputSchema\":%s}",
            TOOLS[i].name, TOOLS[i].description, TOOLS[i].schema);
    }
    pos += snprintf(buf + pos, MAX_OUTPUT - pos, "]}}");
    send_response(buf);
    free(buf);
}

static void handle_tools_call(int id, const char *input) {
    char name[128] = {0};
    jget(input, "name", name, sizeof(name));

    /* Find arguments — look for "arguments" key */
    const char *args_start = strstr(input, "\"arguments\"");
    char args[MAX_CMD] = "{}";
    if (args_start) {
        args_start += 11; /* skip "arguments" */
        while (*args_start == ' ' || *args_start == ':' || *args_start == '\t') args_start++;
        if (*args_start == '{') {
            int depth = 0, i = 0, in_str = 0;
            const char *p = args_start;
            while (*p && i < MAX_CMD - 1) {
                if (!in_str) {
                    if (*p == '{') depth++;
                    else if (*p == '}') { depth--; if (depth == 0) { args[i++] = *p; break; } }
                    else if (*p == '"') in_str = 1;
                } else {
                    if (*p == '\\' && p[1]) { args[i++] = *p++; args[i++] = *p++; continue; }
                    if (*p == '"') in_str = 0;
                }
                args[i++] = *p++;
            }
            args[i] = 0;
        }
    }

    char *result = execute_mcp_tool(name, args);
    if (!result) result = strdup("Tool returned no output");

    /* Escape result for JSON */
    char *escaped = malloc(MAX_OUTPUT * 2);
    if (!escaped) { free(result); return; }
    json_escape(result, escaped, MAX_OUTPUT * 2 - 1);
    free(result);

    /* Build response */
    char *buf = malloc(MAX_OUTPUT * 2 + 512);
    if (!buf) { free(escaped); return; }
    snprintf(buf, MAX_OUTPUT * 2 + 512,
        "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":%d,\"result\":{"
        "\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"%s\"}]"
        "}}", id, escaped);

    send_response(buf);
    free(escaped);
    free(buf);
}

/* Read a JSON-RPC message from stdin (Content-Length header framing) */
static char *read_message(void) {
    char header[512];
    int content_length = 0;

    /* Read headers until empty line */
    while (fgets(header, sizeof(header), stdin)) {
        if (header[0] == '\r' || header[0] == '\n') break;
        if (strncmp(header, "Content-Length:", 15) == 0)
            content_length = atoi(header + 15);
    }

    if (content_length <= 0 || content_length > MAX_INPUT) return NULL;

    char *body = malloc(content_length + 1);
    if (!body) return NULL;

    int total = 0;
    while (total < content_length) {
        int n = (int)fread(body + total, 1, content_length - total, stdin);
        if (n <= 0) break;
        total += n;
    }
    body[total] = 0;
    return body;
}

int main(void) {
#ifdef _WIN32
    _setmode(_fileno(stdin), _O_BINARY);
    _setmode(_fileno(stdout), _O_BINARY);
#endif

    while (1) {
        char *msg = read_message();
        if (!msg) break;

        char method[128] = {0};
        jget(msg, "method", method, sizeof(method));
        int id = jget_num(msg, "id");

        if (strcmp(method, "initialize") == 0) {
            handle_initialize(id);
        }
        else if (strcmp(method, "notifications/initialized") == 0) {
            /* No response needed */
        }
        else if (strcmp(method, "tools/list") == 0) {
            handle_tools_list(id);
        }
        else if (strcmp(method, "tools/call") == 0) {
            handle_tools_call(id, msg);
        }
        else {
            /* Unknown method — return error */
            char resp[512];
            snprintf(resp, sizeof(resp),
                "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":%d,\"error\":{"
                "\"code\":-32601,\"message\":\"Method not found: %s\"}}",
                id, method);
            send_response(resp);
        }

        free(msg);
    }

    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_next.c — What Next?
 *
 * Reads all .xyzt/ state and synthesizes a prioritized action list.
 * Read-only. Does not modify any state files.
 *
 * Priority scheme:
 *   P0: Test failures (fix first)
 *   P1: Pending onetwo claims (verify)
 *   P2: Unresolved unknowns (investigate)
 *   P3: Stale wire edges (re-verify after file changes)
 *   P4: Weak wire edges (strengthen or discard)
 *   P5: File mismatches (review)
 *   P6: Phase guidance (next step hint)
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O3 -o xyzt_next xyzt_next.c
 * Install: copy to ~/.local/bin/
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — February 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <time.h>
#include "wire.h"
#include "xyzt_state.h"

static const char *OT_PHASE[] = {"EMPTY", "ONE", "BEDROCK", "TWO", "VERIFY", "DONE"};

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Action list
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define MAX_ACTIONS 64

typedef struct {
    int  priority;
    char action[512];
    char source[16];
} Action;

static int action_cmp(const void *a, const void *b) {
    return ((const Action*)a)->priority - ((const Action*)b)->priority;
}

static const char *fmt_ago(uint32_t ts) {
    static char buf[32];
    if (ts == 0) { strcpy(buf, "never"); return buf; }
    uint32_t delta = (uint32_t)time(NULL) - ts;
    if (delta < 60)        snprintf(buf, 32, "%us ago", delta);
    else if (delta < 3600) snprintf(buf, 32, "%um ago", delta / 60);
    else if (delta < 86400)snprintf(buf, 32, "%uh ago", delta / 3600);
    else                   snprintf(buf, 32, "%ud ago", delta / 86400);
    return buf;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   State readers
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Loaders now come from xyzt_state.h: state_load_onetwo(), state_load_tests(), etc. */

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main action builder
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_next(void) {
    Action actions[MAX_ACTIONS];
    int n = 0;

    /* ── P0: Test failures ── */
    TestHistory *tests = state_load_tests();
    if (tests && tests->count > 0) {
        TestResult *last = &tests->results[tests->count - 1];
        if (last->exit_code != 0) {
            Action *a = &actions[n++];
            a->priority = 0;
            strncpy(a->source, "test", 15);
            if (last->n_errors > 0) {
                snprintf(a->action, sizeof(a->action),
                         "Fix failing test: %.80s (%u errors, %s)",
                         last->cmd, last->n_errors, fmt_ago(last->timestamp));
            } else {
                snprintf(a->action, sizeof(a->action),
                         "Fix failing test: %.80s (exit %d, %s)",
                         last->cmd, last->exit_code, fmt_ago(last->timestamp));
            }
        }
        free(tests);
    }

    /* ── P0.5: Unresolved errors from xyzt_err ── */
    ErrStore *errs = state_load_errors();
    if (errs && errs->count > 0) {
        int unresolved = 0;
        const char *latest_file = NULL;
        const char *latest_msg = NULL;
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < errs->count && i < ERR_MAX; i++) {
            if (!errs->entries[i].resolved) {
                unresolved++;
                latest_file = errs->entries[i].file;
                latest_msg = errs->entries[i].message;
            }
        }
        if (unresolved > 0 && n < MAX_ACTIONS) {
            Action *a = &actions[n++];
            a->priority = 0;
            strncpy(a->source, "errors", 15);
            if (unresolved == 1) {
                snprintf(a->action, sizeof(a->action),
                         "Unresolved error in %.80s: %.120s",
                         latest_file ? latest_file : "?", latest_msg ? latest_msg : "?");
            } else {
                snprintf(a->action, sizeof(a->action),
                         "%d unresolved errors (latest: %.80s — %.100s)",
                         unresolved, latest_file ? latest_file : "?", latest_msg ? latest_msg : "?");
            }
        }
        free(errs);
    }

    /* ── P1/P2: Onetwo claims and unknowns ── */
    OTSession *ot = state_load_onetwo();
    if (ot && ot->problem[0]) {
        /* Pending claims → P1 */
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < ot->n_claims && n < MAX_ACTIONS; i++) {
            if (ot->claims[i].verified == 0) {  /* PENDING */
                Action *a = &actions[n++];
                a->priority = 1;
                strncpy(a->source, "onetwo", 15);
                snprintf(a->action, sizeof(a->action),
                         "Verify claim[%d] T%d: %.200s",
                         i, ot->claims[i].tier, ot->claims[i].text);
            }
        }

        /* Failed claims → also P1 */
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < ot->n_claims && n < MAX_ACTIONS; i++) {
            if (ot->claims[i].verified == 2) {  /* FAIL */
                Action *a = &actions[n++];
                a->priority = 1;
                strncpy(a->source, "onetwo", 15);
                snprintf(a->action, sizeof(a->action),
                         "Revise failed claim[%d] T%d: %.200s",
                         i, ot->claims[i].tier, ot->claims[i].text);
            }
        }

        /* Unknowns → P2 */
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < ot->n_unknowns && n < MAX_ACTIONS; i++) {
            Action *a = &actions[n++];
            a->priority = 2;
            strncpy(a->source, "onetwo", 15);
            snprintf(a->action, sizeof(a->action),
                     "Investigate unknown[%d]: %.200s",
                     i, ot->unknowns[i].text);
        }

        /* P6: Phase guidance */
        if (n < MAX_ACTIONS) {
            static const char *PHASE_HINT[] = {
                "",
                "Phase ONE: decompose the problem. Register knowns, unknowns, probe findings.",
                "Phase BEDROCK: foundation established. Start making claims.",
                "Phase TWO: building. Make and verify claims.",
                "Phase VERIFY: verify all remaining claims before shipping.",
                "Phase DONE: all claims verified. Ready to ship."
            };
            int pi = ot->phase;
            if (pi >= 1 && pi <= 5) {
                Action *a = &actions[n++];
                a->priority = 6;
                strncpy(a->source, "onetwo", 15);
                snprintf(a->action, sizeof(a->action),
                         "%s (problem: %.150s)", PHASE_HINT[pi], ot->problem);
            }
        }
        free(ot);
    }

    /* ── P3/P4: Wire graph — stale and weak edges ── */
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    if (g->n_nodes > 0) {
        /* Collect file nodes with stale edges → P3 */
        for (uint32_t ni = 0; ni < g->n_nodes && n < MAX_ACTIONS; ni++) {
            if (g->nodes[ni].type != WNODE_FILE) continue;
            int stale = 0;
            for (uint32_t ei = 0; ei < g->n_edges; ei++) {
                if (((int)g->edges[ei].src == (int)ni || (int)g->edges[ei].dst == (int)ni)
                    && g->edges[ei].stale)
                    stale++;
            }
            if (stale > 0) {
                Action *a = &actions[n++];
                a->priority = 3;
                strncpy(a->source, "wire", 15);
                snprintf(a->action, sizeof(a->action),
                         "Re-verify: %s has %d stale edge(s) (changed since last verify)",
                         g->nodes[ni].name, stale);
            }
        }

        /* Dead edges (below WIRE_ALIVE) between file nodes → P4 */
        for (uint32_t ei = 0; ei < g->n_edges && n < MAX_ACTIONS; ei++) {
            WireEdge *e = &g->edges[ei];
            if (e->weight >= WIRE_ALIVE) continue;
            if (g->nodes[e->src].type != WNODE_FILE) continue;
            if (g->nodes[e->dst].type != WNODE_FILE) continue;
            Action *a = &actions[n++];
            a->priority = 4;
            strncpy(a->source, "wire", 15);
            snprintf(a->action, sizeof(a->action),
                     "Weak connection: %s <-> %s (w=%d, p=%d, f=%d)",
                     g->nodes[e->src].name, g->nodes[e->dst].name,
                     e->weight, e->passes, e->fails);
        }
    }
    wire_free(g);

    /* ── P5: File mismatches in session ── */
    {
        const char *spath = getenv("XYZT_SESSION");
        if (!spath) spath = ".xyzt/session.bin";
        FILE *fp = fopen(spath, "rb");
        if (fp) {
            IOHeader hdr;
            if (fread(&hdr, sizeof(IOHeader), 1, fp) == 1
                && hdr.magic == IO_MAGIC && hdr.count > 0) {
                size_t entry_size = (hdr.version >= 2) ? sizeof(IOFingerprint) : IO_V1_FP_SIZE;
                for (uint32_t i = 0; i < hdr.count && i < IO_MAX && n < MAX_ACTIONS; i++) {
                    IOFingerprint entry = {0};
                    if (fread(&entry, entry_size, 1, fp) != 1) break;
                    if (entry.mismatches > 0) {
                        Action *a = &actions[n++];
                        a->priority = 5;
                        strncpy(a->source, "session", 15);
                        snprintf(a->action, sizeof(a->action),
                                 "Review: %s changed %u time(s) since first store (%u stores total)",
                                 entry.name, entry.mismatches, entry.stores);
                    }
                }
            }
            fclose(fp);
        }
    }

    /* ── Sort and print ── */
    qsort(actions, n, sizeof(Action), action_cmp);

    printf("---XYZT-NEXT---\n");
    if (n == 0) {
        printf("No pending actions. All clear.\n");
    } else {
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            printf("  P%d [%-7s] %s\n", actions[i].priority, actions[i].source, actions[i].action);
        }
        printf("\n%d action(s) pending.\n", n);
    }
    printf("---END-NEXT---\n");
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Brief mode — single most important action
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_brief(void) {
    /* Check test failures first (P0) */
    TestHistory *tests = state_load_tests();
    if (tests && tests->count > 0) {
        TestResult *last = &tests->results[tests->count - 1];
        if (last->exit_code != 0) {
            printf("Fix failing test: %.80s (%u errors)\n",
                   last->cmd, last->n_errors);
            free(tests);
            return;
        }
        free(tests);
    }

    /* Check pending claims (P1) */
    OTSession *ot = state_load_onetwo();
    if (ot && ot->problem[0]) {
        int pending = 0;
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < ot->n_claims; i++)
            if (ot->claims[i].verified == 0) pending++;
        if (pending > 0) {
            printf("%d pending claim(s) to verify for: %s\n", pending, ot->problem);
            free(ot);
            return;
        }

        /* Check unknowns (P2) */
        if (ot->n_unknowns > 0) {
            printf("%d unknown(s) to investigate for: %s\n", ot->n_unknowns, ot->problem);
            free(ot);
            return;
        }
        free(ot);
    }

    /* Check stale wire edges (P3) */
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int stale_nodes = 0;
    for (uint32_t ni = 0; ni < g->n_nodes; ni++) {
        if (g->nodes[ni].type != WNODE_FILE) continue;
        for (uint32_t ei = 0; ei < g->n_edges; ei++) {
            if (((int)g->edges[ei].src == (int)ni || (int)g->edges[ei].dst == (int)ni)
                && g->edges[ei].stale) {
                stale_nodes++;
                break;
            }
        }
    }
    wire_free(g);
    if (stale_nodes > 0) {
        printf("%d file(s) with stale wire edges need re-verification.\n", stale_nodes);
        return;
    }

    printf("All clear. No pending actions.\n");
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT NEXT — What should you do next?\n\n");
    printf("  xyzt_next              Prioritized action list\n");
    printf("  xyzt_next brief        One-line top action\n");
    printf("\nReads: .xyzt/onetwo_session.bin, wire.bin, test_history.bin, session.bin\n");
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — February 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc >= 2 && (strcmp(argv[1], "help") == 0 || strcmp(argv[1], "--help") == 0 || strcmp(argv[1], "-h") == 0)) {
        usage();
        return 0;
    }
    if (argc >= 2 && strcmp(argv[1], "brief") == 0) {
        cmd_brief();
    } else {
        cmd_next();
    }
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_org.c — Cross-Project File Organizer
 *
 * Global catalog spanning multiple projects/directories.
 * Gives Isaac a dashboard of what's where, finds duplicates,
 * tracks changes, and gives Claude pre-computed project knowledge.
 *
 * Commands:
 *   xyzt_org scan <dir1> [dir2] ...   Catalog source/text files
 *   xyzt_org projects                  Project overview with stats
 *   xyzt_org dupes                     Find duplicates + name collisions
 *   xyzt_org tree [project]            File listing for a project
 *   xyzt_org changes                   New/changed/missing since last scan
 *   xyzt_org stats                     Overall numbers
 *   xyzt_org brief                     One-line summary
 *
 * Storage: ~/.xyzt/org.bin (global, not per-project)
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O3 -o xyzt_org.exe xyzt_org.c
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — February 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <sys/stat.h>
#include <windows.h>
#include "wire.h"

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Constants & structures
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define ORG_MAX       8192
#define ORG_PATH_LEN  256
#define ORG_PROJ_LEN  64
#define ORG_LANG_LEN  16
#define ORG_MAGIC     0x4F524700  /* "ORG\0" */
#define ORG_VER       1
#define MAX_ROOTS     8
#define MAX_DEPTH     20
#define HASH_BUF      65536

typedef struct {
    char     path[ORG_PATH_LEN];
    char     project[ORG_PROJ_LEN];
    char     lang[ORG_LANG_LEN];
    uint32_t file_hash;
    uint32_t file_size;
    uint32_t line_count;
    uint32_t scan_time;
    uint32_t mod_time;
    uint8_t  is_text;
    uint8_t  changed;
    uint8_t  is_new;
    uint8_t  _pad[17];
} OrgEntry;  /* 384 bytes */

typedef struct {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint8_t  version;
    uint8_t  _hpad[3];
    uint32_t count;
    uint32_t last_scan;
    char     scan_roots[MAX_ROOTS][ORG_PATH_LEN];
    uint32_t n_roots;
    uint32_t _reserved[3];
    OrgEntry entries[ORG_MAX];
} OrgCatalog;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Helpers
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static uint32_t fnv1a(const uint8_t *data, int len) {
    uint32_t h = 2166136261u;
    for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
        h ^= data[i];
        h *= 16777619u;
    }
    return h;
}

static const char *detect_lang(const char *path) {
    const char *dot = strrchr(path, '.');
    if (!dot) return NULL;
    if (strcmp(dot, ".c") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".h") == 0) return "c";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".cpp") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".hpp") == 0 ||
        strcmp(dot, ".cc") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".cxx") == 0) return "cpp";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".py") == 0) return "python";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".js") == 0) return "javascript";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".ts") == 0) return "typescript";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".tsx") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".jsx") == 0) return "javascript";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".rs") == 0) return "rust";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".go") == 0) return "go";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".v") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".sv") == 0) return "verilog";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".cu") == 0) return "cuda";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".java") == 0) return "java";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".md") == 0) return "markdown";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".json") == 0) return "json";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".yaml") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".yml") == 0) return "yaml";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".toml") == 0) return "toml";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".sh") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".bash") == 0) return "shell";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".bat") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".cmd") == 0) return "batch";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".ps1") == 0) return "powershell";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".css") == 0) return "css";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".html") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".htm") == 0) return "html";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".txt") == 0) return "text";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".cfg") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".ini") == 0) return "config";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".xml") == 0) return "xml";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".sql") == 0) return "sql";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".r") == 0 || strcmp(dot, ".R") == 0) return "r";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".lua") == 0) return "lua";
    if (strcmp(dot, ".zig") == 0) return "zig";
    return NULL;
}

static int should_skip(const char *name) {
    const char *skip[] = {".git", "node_modules", "__pycache__", ".xyzt",
                          "build", "target", ".vs", ".vscode", "dist",
                          ".venv", "venv", ".idea", ".claude",
                          "site-packages", "Lib", "lib", "vendor",
                          ".tox", ".mypy_cache", ".pytest_cache",
                          ".next", "coverage", "obj",
                          "oss-cad-suite", "deprecated",
                          ".cargo", ".rustup",
                          "prjxray-db", "prjxray",
                          "archive", "testdata", NULL};
    for (int i = 0; skip[i]; i++)
        if (_stricmp(name, skip[i]) == 0) return 1;
    /* Skip anything starting with .venv (catches .venv312, .venv311, etc.) */
    if (_strnicmp(name, ".venv", 5) == 0) return 1;
    if (_strnicmp(name, "venv", 4) == 0 && (name[4] == '\0' || name[4] >= '0')) return 1;
    return 0;
}

static const char *basename_of(const char *path) {
    const char *last = path;
    for (const char *p = path; *p; p++)
        if (*p == '\\' || *p == '/') last = p + 1;
    return last;
}

static void fmt_size(uint32_t bytes, char *out, int max) {
    if (bytes < 1024)
        snprintf(out, max, "%uB", bytes);
    else if (bytes < 1024 * 1024)
        snprintf(out, max, "%.1fKB", bytes / 1024.0);
    else
        snprintf(out, max, "%.1fMB", bytes / (1024.0 * 1024.0));
}

static void fmt_ago(uint32_t ts, char *out, int max) {
    if (ts == 0) { snprintf(out, max, "never"); return; }
    uint32_t ago = (uint32_t)time(NULL) - ts;
    if (ago < 60) snprintf(out, max, "%us ago", ago);
    else if (ago < 3600) snprintf(out, max, "%um ago", ago / 60);
    else if (ago < 86400) snprintf(out, max, "%uh ago", ago / 3600);
    else snprintf(out, max, "%ud ago", ago / 86400);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Catalog I/O
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void org_path(char *out, int max) {
    const char *home = xyzt_home_dir();
    if (home)
        snprintf(out, max, "%s/.xyzt/org.bin", home);
    else
        snprintf(out, max, ".xyzt/org.bin");
}

static OrgCatalog *org_load(void) {
    char path[512];
    org_path(path, sizeof(path));

    OrgCatalog *cat = (OrgCatalog *)calloc(1, sizeof(OrgCatalog));
    if (!cat) return NULL;
    cat->magic = ORG_MAGIC;
    cat->version = ORG_VER;

    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "rb");
    if (!fp) return cat;

    fread(cat, 1, sizeof(OrgCatalog), fp);
    fclose(fp);

    if (cat->magic != ORG_MAGIC) {
        memset(cat, 0, sizeof(OrgCatalog));
        cat->magic = ORG_MAGIC;
        cat->version = ORG_VER;
    }
    return cat;
}

static void org_save(OrgCatalog *cat) {
    char path[512];
    org_path(path, sizeof(path));
    xyzt_ensure_dir(path);

    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "wb");
    if (!fp) { fprintf(stderr, "Cannot write org catalog\n"); return; }
    fwrite(cat, sizeof(OrgCatalog), 1, fp);
    fclose(fp);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Scan engine
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static uint32_t hash_file(const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "rb");
    if (!fp) return 0;
    uint8_t buf[HASH_BUF];
    size_t n = fread(buf, 1, HASH_BUF, fp);
    fclose(fp);
    return fnv1a(buf, (int)n);
}

static uint32_t count_lines(const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "r");
    if (!fp) return 0;
    uint32_t n = 0;
    int ch;
    while ((ch = fgetc(fp)) != EOF)
        if (ch == '\n') n++;
    fclose(fp);
    return n;
}

static int is_text(const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "rb");
    if (!fp) return 0;
    uint8_t buf[4096];
    size_t n = fread(buf, 1, sizeof(buf), fp);
    fclose(fp);
    for (size_t i = 0; i < n; i++)
        if (buf[i] == 0) return 0;
    return 1;
}

static void extract_project(const char *fullpath, const char *root,
                             char *out, int max) {
    /* Skip root prefix */
    int rlen = (int)strlen(root);
    const char *rel = fullpath + rlen;
    while (*rel == '\\' || *rel == '/') rel++;

    /* Find first 2 path components as project name */
    int seps = 0;
    const char *end = NULL;
    for (const char *p = rel; *p; p++) {
        if (*p == '\\' || *p == '/') {
            seps++;
            if (seps == 2) { end = p; break; }
            if (!end) end = p;  /* Mark after first component */
        }
    }

    if (!end) {
        /* File directly under root — no subdirectory */
        snprintf(out, max, "(root)");
        return;
    }

    int len = (int)(end - rel);
    if (len >= max) len = max - 1;
    memcpy(out, rel, len);
    out[len] = '\0';

    /* Normalize separators to backslash for display */
    for (int i = 0; out[i]; i++)
        if (out[i] == '/') out[i] = '\\';
}

static int path_exists(OrgCatalog *cat, const char *path) {
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++)
        if (_stricmp(cat->entries[i].path, path) == 0) return 1;
    return 0;
}

static void scan_dir(OrgCatalog *cat, const char *dir, const char *root, int depth) {
    if (depth > MAX_DEPTH || cat->count >= ORG_MAX) return;

    char pattern[512];
    snprintf(pattern, sizeof(pattern), "%s\\*", dir);

    WIN32_FIND_DATAA fd;
    HANDLE hFind = FindFirstFileA(pattern, &fd);
    if (hFind == INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE) return;

    do {
        if (strcmp(fd.cFileName, ".") == 0 || strcmp(fd.cFileName, "..") == 0) continue;
        if (should_skip(fd.cFileName)) continue;

        char fullpath[512];
        snprintf(fullpath, sizeof(fullpath), "%s\\%s", dir, fd.cFileName);

        if (fd.dwFileAttributes & FILE_ATTRIBUTE_DIRECTORY) {
            scan_dir(cat, fullpath, root, depth + 1);
        } else {
            const char *lang = detect_lang(fd.cFileName);
            if (!lang) continue;                     /* Skip unrecognized extensions */
            if (cat->count >= ORG_MAX) break;
            if (path_exists(cat, fullpath)) continue; /* Dedup overlapping roots */

            /* Skip very large files (>2MB) */
            LARGE_INTEGER sz;
            sz.LowPart = fd.nFileSizeLow;
            sz.HighPart = fd.nFileSizeHigh;
            if (sz.QuadPart > 2 * 1024 * 1024) continue;

            OrgEntry *e = &cat->entries[cat->count];
            memset(e, 0, sizeof(OrgEntry));
            strncpy(e->path, fullpath, ORG_PATH_LEN - 1);
            strncpy(e->lang, lang, ORG_LANG_LEN - 1);
            extract_project(fullpath, root, e->project, ORG_PROJ_LEN);
            e->file_hash = hash_file(fullpath);
            e->file_size = (uint32_t)sz.QuadPart;
            e->line_count = count_lines(fullpath);
            e->scan_time = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
            e->is_text = is_text(fullpath);

            /* mod_time from FILETIME */
            FILETIME ft = fd.ftLastWriteTime;
            ULARGE_INTEGER ul;
            ul.LowPart = ft.dwLowDateTime;
            ul.HighPart = ft.dwHighDateTime;
            e->mod_time = (uint32_t)((ul.QuadPart - 116444736000000000ULL) / 10000000ULL);

            cat->count++;
        }
    } while (FindNextFileA(hFind, &fd));
    FindClose(hFind);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Commands
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_scan(int n_dirs, char **dirs) {
    OrgCatalog *cat = org_load();

    /* Save old entries for change detection */
    uint32_t old_count = cat->count;
    OrgEntry *old = NULL;
    if (old_count > 0) {
        old = (OrgEntry *)malloc(old_count * sizeof(OrgEntry));
        memcpy(old, cat->entries, old_count * sizeof(OrgEntry));
    }

    /* Reset catalog for fresh scan */
    cat->count = 0;
    cat->n_roots = 0;
    cat->last_scan = (uint32_t)time(NULL);

    /* Scan each root */
    for (int i = 0; i < n_dirs && i < MAX_ROOTS; i++) {
        /* Normalize: strip trailing separator */
        char root[ORG_PATH_LEN];
        strncpy(root, dirs[i], ORG_PATH_LEN - 1);
        root[ORG_PATH_LEN - 1] = '\0';
        int len = (int)strlen(root);
        while (len > 0 && (root[len-1] == '\\' || root[len-1] == '/'))
            root[--len] = '\0';

        strncpy(cat->scan_roots[i], root, ORG_PATH_LEN - 1);
        cat->n_roots++;
        scan_dir(cat, root, root, 0);
    }

    /* Change detection */
    int n_new = 0, n_changed = 0, n_missing = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
        int found = 0;
        for (uint32_t j = 0; j < old_count; j++) {
            if (_stricmp(cat->entries[i].path, old[j].path) == 0) {
                found = 1;
                if (cat->entries[i].file_hash != old[j].file_hash && old[j].file_hash != 0) {
                    cat->entries[i].changed = 1;
                    n_changed++;
                }
                break;
            }
        }
        if (!found) {
            cat->entries[i].is_new = 1;
            n_new++;
        }
    }

    /* Count missing (in old but not in new) */
    for (uint32_t j = 0; j < old_count; j++) {
        int found = 0;
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
            if (_stricmp(cat->entries[i].path, old[j].path) == 0) { found = 1; break; }
        }
        if (!found) n_missing++;
    }

    org_save(cat);

    /* Count unique projects */
    char projects[256][ORG_PROJ_LEN];
    int n_projects = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
        int dup = 0;
        for (int j = 0; j < n_projects; j++)
            if (strcmp(projects[j], cat->entries[i].project) == 0) { dup = 1; break; }
        if (!dup && n_projects < 256) {
            strncpy(projects[n_projects++], cat->entries[i].project, ORG_PROJ_LEN - 1);
        }
    }

    printf("---XYZT-ORG-SCAN---\n");
    printf("roots:");
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->n_roots; i++) printf(" %s", cat->scan_roots[i]);
    printf("\n");
    printf("entries: %u / %d\n", cat->count, ORG_MAX);
    printf("projects: %d\n", n_projects);
    if (old_count > 0)
        printf("new: %d | changed: %d | missing: %d\n", n_new, n_changed, n_missing);
    else
        printf("(first scan — all %u entries are new)\n", cat->count);
    printf("---END-SCAN---\n");

    if (old) free(old);
    free(cat);
}

static void cmd_projects(void) {
    OrgCatalog *cat = org_load();
    if (cat->count == 0) {
        printf("No catalog. Run: xyzt_org scan <dir>\n");
        free(cat); return;
    }

    /* Collect unique projects */
    typedef struct {
        char name[ORG_PROJ_LEN];
        int  file_count;
        uint32_t total_lines;
        uint32_t total_size;
        /* Language counts */
        char langs[32][ORG_LANG_LEN];
        int  lang_counts[32];
        int  n_langs;
    } ProjInfo;

    ProjInfo projs[256];
    int n_projs = 0;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
        OrgEntry *e = &cat->entries[i];
        int pi = -1;
        for (int j = 0; j < n_projs; j++) {
            if (strcmp(projs[j].name, e->project) == 0) { pi = j; break; }
        }
        if (pi < 0 && n_projs < 256) {
            pi = n_projs++;
            memset(&projs[pi], 0, sizeof(ProjInfo));
            strncpy(projs[pi].name, e->project, ORG_PROJ_LEN - 1);
        }
        if (pi < 0) continue;

        projs[pi].file_count++;
        projs[pi].total_lines += e->line_count;
        projs[pi].total_size += e->file_size;

        /* Track language */
        int li = -1;
        for (int j = 0; j < projs[pi].n_langs; j++) {
            if (strcmp(projs[pi].langs[j], e->lang) == 0) { li = j; break; }
        }
        if (li >= 0) {
            projs[pi].lang_counts[li]++;
        } else if (projs[pi].n_langs < 32) {
            li = projs[pi].n_langs++;
            strncpy(projs[pi].langs[li], e->lang, ORG_LANG_LEN - 1);
            projs[pi].lang_counts[li] = 1;
        }
    }

    /* Sort by total lines descending */
    for (int i = 1; i < n_projs; i++) {
        ProjInfo tmp = projs[i]; int j = i - 1;
        while (j >= 0 && projs[j].total_lines < tmp.total_lines) {
            projs[j+1] = projs[j]; j--;
        }
        projs[j+1] = tmp;
    }

    char ago[32];
    fmt_ago(cat->last_scan, ago, sizeof(ago));
    printf("---XYZT-ORG-PROJECTS---\n");
    printf("Last scan: %s | %d projects | %u files\n\n", ago, n_projs, cat->count);

    for (int i = 0; i < n_projs; i++) {
        ProjInfo *p = &projs[i];
        char sz[32];
        fmt_size(p->total_size, sz, sizeof(sz));

        printf("PROJECT: %s\n", p->name);
        printf("  %d files | %s | ", p->file_count, sz);

        /* Show language breakdown */
        for (int j = 0; j < p->n_langs; j++) {
            if (j > 0) printf(", ");
            printf("%d %s", p->lang_counts[j], p->langs[j]);
        }
        printf(" | %u lines\n", p->total_lines);

        if (i < n_projs - 1) printf("\n");
    }
    printf("---END-PROJECTS---\n");
    free(cat);
}

static void cmd_dupes(void) {
    OrgCatalog *cat = org_load();
    if (cat->count == 0) {
        printf("No catalog. Run: xyzt_org scan <dir>\n");
        free(cat); return;
    }

    uint8_t *visited = (uint8_t *)calloc(cat->count, 1);

    printf("---XYZT-ORG-DUPES---\n");

    /* Exact duplicates (same hash) */
    int exact_groups = 0;
    printf("IDENTICAL (same content):\n");
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
        if (visited[i] || cat->entries[i].file_hash == 0) continue;
        int group_size = 0;
        uint32_t members[64];
        members[group_size++] = i;

        for (uint32_t j = i + 1; j < cat->count; j++) {
            if (!visited[j] && cat->entries[j].file_hash == cat->entries[i].file_hash &&
                cat->entries[j].file_size == cat->entries[i].file_size) {
                if (group_size < 64) members[group_size++] = j;
            }
        }

        if (group_size > 1) {
            exact_groups++;
            printf("  %s -- %d copies (hash: 0x%08X)\n",
                   basename_of(cat->entries[i].path), group_size, cat->entries[i].file_hash);
            for (int k = 0; k < group_size; k++) {
                char sz[32];
                fmt_size(cat->entries[members[k]].file_size, sz, sizeof(sz));
                printf("    %s (%s)\n", cat->entries[members[k]].path, sz);
                visited[members[k]] = 1;
            }
        }
    }
    if (exact_groups == 0) printf("  (none)\n");

    /* Name collisions (same basename, different hash) */
    memset(visited, 0, cat->count);
    int name_groups = 0;
    printf("\nSAME NAME (different content):\n");
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
        if (visited[i]) continue;
        const char *base_i = basename_of(cat->entries[i].path);
        int group_size = 0;
        uint32_t members[64];
        members[group_size++] = i;
        int has_diff_hash = 0;

        for (uint32_t j = i + 1; j < cat->count; j++) {
            if (visited[j]) continue;
            if (_stricmp(basename_of(cat->entries[j].path), base_i) == 0) {
                if (group_size < 64) members[group_size++] = j;
                if (cat->entries[j].file_hash != cat->entries[i].file_hash)
                    has_diff_hash = 1;
            }
        }

        if (group_size > 1 && has_diff_hash) {
            name_groups++;
            printf("  %s -- %d copies\n", base_i, group_size);
            for (int k = 0; k < group_size; k++) {
                char sz[32];
                fmt_size(cat->entries[members[k]].file_size, sz, sizeof(sz));
                printf("    %s (%s, hash: 0x%08X)\n",
                       cat->entries[members[k]].path, sz, cat->entries[members[k]].file_hash);
                visited[members[k]] = 1;
            }
        }
    }
    if (name_groups == 0) printf("  (none)\n");

    printf("\nTotal: %d identical group(s), %d name-collision group(s)\n", exact_groups, name_groups);
    printf("---END-DUPES---\n");
    free(visited);
    free(cat);
}

static void cmd_tree(const char *project) {
    OrgCatalog *cat = org_load();
    if (cat->count == 0) {
        printf("No catalog. Run: xyzt_org scan <dir>\n");
        free(cat); return;
    }

    printf("---XYZT-ORG-TREE---\n");

    /* Collect matching entries */
    int shown = 0;
    uint32_t total_lines = 0;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
        OrgEntry *e = &cat->entries[i];
        if (project && _stricmp(e->project, project) != 0 &&
            strstr(e->project, project) == NULL) continue;

        if (shown == 0 && project) {
            printf("PROJECT: %s\n", project);
        }

        char sz[32];
        fmt_size(e->file_size, sz, sizeof(sz));
        printf("  %-40s %-12s %6u lines  %8s%s%s\n",
               basename_of(e->path), e->lang, e->line_count, sz,
               e->changed ? "  [CHANGED]" : "",
               e->is_new ? "  [NEW]" : "");
        total_lines += e->line_count;
        shown++;
    }

    if (shown == 0) {
        if (project) printf("No files found for project '%s'\n", project);
        else printf("Catalog is empty\n");
    } else {
        printf("\n%d file(s), %u total lines\n", shown, total_lines);
    }
    printf("---END-TREE---\n");
    free(cat);
}

static void cmd_changes(void) {
    OrgCatalog *cat = org_load();
    if (cat->count == 0) {
        printf("No catalog. Run: xyzt_org scan <dir>\n");
        free(cat); return;
    }

    char ago[32];
    fmt_ago(cat->last_scan, ago, sizeof(ago));
    printf("---XYZT-ORG-CHANGES---\n");
    printf("Since last scan (%s):\n\n", ago);

    int n_new = 0, n_changed = 0, n_missing = 0;

    /* New files */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
        if (cat->entries[i].is_new) {
            if (n_new == 0) printf("NEW:\n");
            printf("  %s\n", cat->entries[i].path);
            n_new++;
        }
    }

    /* Changed files */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
        if (cat->entries[i].changed) {
            if (n_changed == 0) printf("\nCHANGED:\n");
            printf("  %s\n", cat->entries[i].path);
            n_changed++;
        }
    }

    /* Missing files (in catalog but no longer on disk) */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
        struct stat st;
        if (stat(cat->entries[i].path, &st) != 0) {
            if (n_missing == 0) printf("\nMISSING:\n");
            printf("  %s\n", cat->entries[i].path);
            n_missing++;
        }
    }

    if (n_new == 0 && n_changed == 0 && n_missing == 0)
        printf("  (no changes)\n");
    else
        printf("\nSummary: %d new, %d changed, %d missing\n", n_new, n_changed, n_missing);

    printf("---END-CHANGES---\n");
    free(cat);
}

static void cmd_stats(void) {
    OrgCatalog *cat = org_load();
    if (cat->count == 0) {
        printf("No catalog. Run: xyzt_org scan <dir>\n");
        free(cat); return;
    }

    uint32_t total_lines = 0, total_size = 0;
    uint32_t largest_size = 0;
    int largest_idx = 0;
    int n_text = 0;

    /* Language counts */
    char langs[64][ORG_LANG_LEN];
    int  lang_counts[64];
    int  n_langs = 0;

    /* Project count */
    char projects[256][ORG_PROJ_LEN];
    int n_projects = 0;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
        OrgEntry *e = &cat->entries[i];
        total_lines += e->line_count;
        total_size += e->file_size;
        if (e->is_text) n_text++;
        if (e->file_size > largest_size) {
            largest_size = e->file_size;
            largest_idx = i;
        }

        /* Count languages */
        int li = -1;
        for (int j = 0; j < n_langs; j++)
            if (strcmp(langs[j], e->lang) == 0) { li = j; break; }
        if (li >= 0) lang_counts[li]++;
        else if (n_langs < 64) {
            strncpy(langs[n_langs], e->lang, ORG_LANG_LEN - 1);
            lang_counts[n_langs] = 1;
            n_langs++;
        }

        /* Count projects */
        int dup = 0;
        for (int j = 0; j < n_projects; j++)
            if (strcmp(projects[j], e->project) == 0) { dup = 1; break; }
        if (!dup && n_projects < 256)
            strncpy(projects[n_projects++], e->project, ORG_PROJ_LEN - 1);
    }

    char ago[32], sz[32];
    fmt_ago(cat->last_scan, ago, sizeof(ago));
    fmt_size(total_size, sz, sizeof(sz));

    printf("---XYZT-ORG-STATS---\n");
    printf("entries:    %u / %d\n", cat->count, ORG_MAX);
    printf("projects:   %d\n", n_projects);
    printf("last scan:  %s\n", ago);
    printf("total size: %s\n", sz);
    printf("total lines: %u\n", total_lines);
    printf("text files: %d | binary: %u\n", n_text, cat->count - n_text);
    printf("languages:  ");
    /* Sort languages by count descending */
    for (int i = 1; i < n_langs; i++) {
        char tl[ORG_LANG_LEN]; int tc;
        strcpy(tl, langs[i]); tc = lang_counts[i];
        int j = i - 1;
        while (j >= 0 && lang_counts[j] < tc) {
            strcpy(langs[j+1], langs[j]); lang_counts[j+1] = lang_counts[j]; j--;
        }
        strcpy(langs[j+1], tl); lang_counts[j+1] = tc;
    }
    for (int i = 0; i < n_langs; i++) {
        if (i > 0) printf(", ");
        printf("%s(%d)", langs[i], lang_counts[i]);
    }
    printf("\n");

    if (cat->count > 0) {
        char lsz[32];
        fmt_size(largest_size, lsz, sizeof(lsz));
        printf("largest:    %s (%s, %u lines)\n",
               cat->entries[largest_idx].path, lsz,
               cat->entries[largest_idx].line_count);
    }
    printf("---END-STATS---\n");
    free(cat);
}

static void cmd_brief(void) {
    OrgCatalog *cat = org_load();
    if (cat->count == 0) {
        printf("(no catalog)\n");
        free(cat); return;
    }

    uint32_t total_lines = 0;
    int n_new = 0, n_changed = 0;

    /* Count projects */
    char projects[256][ORG_PROJ_LEN];
    int n_projects = 0;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
        total_lines += cat->entries[i].line_count;
        if (cat->entries[i].is_new) n_new++;
        if (cat->entries[i].changed) n_changed++;
        int dup = 0;
        for (int j = 0; j < n_projects; j++)
            if (strcmp(projects[j], cat->entries[i].project) == 0) { dup = 1; break; }
        if (!dup && n_projects < 256)
            strncpy(projects[n_projects++], cat->entries[i].project, ORG_PROJ_LEN - 1);
    }

    /* Count duplicates quickly */
    int n_dupes = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < cat->count; i++) {
        if (cat->entries[i].file_hash == 0) continue;
        for (uint32_t j = i + 1; j < cat->count; j++) {
            if (cat->entries[j].file_hash == cat->entries[i].file_hash &&
                cat->entries[j].file_size == cat->entries[i].file_size) {
                n_dupes++; break;  /* Count groups, not pairs */
            }
        }
    }

    char lines_str[32];
    if (total_lines > 1000000)
        snprintf(lines_str, sizeof(lines_str), "%.1fM", total_lines / 1000000.0);
    else if (total_lines > 1000)
        snprintf(lines_str, sizeof(lines_str), "%.0fK", total_lines / 1000.0);
    else
        snprintf(lines_str, sizeof(lines_str), "%u", total_lines);

    printf("%d projects, %u files, %s lines",
           n_projects, cat->count, lines_str);
    if (n_dupes > 0) printf(", %d dupes", n_dupes);
    if (n_changed > 0) printf(", %d changed", n_changed);
    if (n_new > 0) printf(", %d new", n_new);
    printf("\n");

    free(cat);
}

static void cmd_rescan(void) {
    OrgCatalog *cat = org_load();
    if (cat->n_roots == 0) {
        printf("No scan roots stored. Run: xyzt_org scan <dir> first.\n");
        free(cat); return;
    }

    /* Copy roots before freeing catalog */
    static char root_bufs[MAX_ROOTS][ORG_PATH_LEN];
    char *roots[MAX_ROOTS];
    int n = (int)cat->n_roots;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        strncpy(root_bufs[i], cat->scan_roots[i], ORG_PATH_LEN - 1);
        root_bufs[i][ORG_PATH_LEN - 1] = '\0';
        roots[i] = root_bufs[i];
    }
    free(cat);

    cmd_scan(n, roots);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT ORG — Cross-Project File Organizer\n\n");
    printf("  xyzt_org scan <dir1> [dir2] ...   Catalog source/text files\n");
    printf("  xyzt_org rescan                    Re-scan stored roots\n");
    printf("  xyzt_org projects                  Project overview\n");
    printf("  xyzt_org dupes                     Find duplicates\n");
    printf("  xyzt_org tree [project]            File listing\n");
    printf("  xyzt_org changes                   New/changed/missing\n");
    printf("  xyzt_org stats                     Overall statistics\n");
    printf("  xyzt_org brief                     One-line summary\n");
    printf("\nCatalog: ~/.xyzt/org.bin (global)\n");
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — February 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) { usage(); return 0; }

    if (strcmp(argv[1], "scan") == 0 && argc >= 3)
        cmd_scan(argc - 2, argv + 2);
