    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "rescan") == 0)
        cmd_rescan();
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "projects") == 0)
        cmd_projects();
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "dupes") == 0)
        cmd_dupes();
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "tree") == 0)
        cmd_tree(argc >= 3 ? argv[2] : NULL);
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "changes") == 0)
        cmd_changes();
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "stats") == 0)
        cmd_stats();
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "brief") == 0)
        cmd_brief();
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "--help") == 0 || strcmp(argv[1], "-h") == 0)
        usage();
    else {
        usage();
        return 1;
    }
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_replay.c — Replay transcripts into wire graph
 *
 * Reads Claude Code JSONL transcripts, extracts file_path from tool calls,
 * builds co-occurrence edges between files touched in the same session.
 * Replays the interaction history into wire.bin as behavioral edges.
 *
 * This is how the wire graph learns from past sessions without
 * requiring the model to re-verify everything manually.
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O3 -o xyzt_replay.exe xyzt_replay.c
 * Usage:   xyzt_replay <transcript.jsonl> [transcript2.jsonl ...]
 *          xyzt_replay --all   (replays all transcripts in project dir)
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — March 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>
#include "xyzt_state.h"
#include "wire.h"

#define MAX_SESSION_FILES 256
#define MAX_PATH_LEN 512
#define MAX_LINE (1024 * 64)

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   JSON helpers (minimal — just extract file_path values)
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int extract_file_paths(const char *line, char paths[][128], int max) {
    int count = 0;
    const char *p = line;
    while (count < max) {
        /* Find "file_path":"..." or "file_path": "..." */
        p = strstr(p, "\"file_path\"");
        if (!p) break;
        p += 11; /* skip "file_path" */
        while (*p == ' ' || *p == ':' || *p == '\t') p++;
        if (*p != '"') { p++; continue; }
        p++; /* skip opening quote */

        /* Extract path */
        char fullpath[MAX_PATH_LEN] = {0};
        int i = 0;
        while (*p && *p != '"' && i < MAX_PATH_LEN - 1) {
            if (*p == '\\' && p[1]) {
                p++;
                if (*p == '\\') fullpath[i++] = '\\';
                else if (*p == '/') fullpath[i++] = '/';
                else if (*p == 'n') fullpath[i++] = '\n';
                else fullpath[i++] = *p;
            } else {
                fullpath[i++] = *p;
            }
            p++;
        }
        fullpath[i] = 0;

        /* Extract basename */
        const char *base = fullpath;
        for (const char *q = fullpath; *q; q++)
            if (*q == '/' || *q == '\\') base = q + 1;

        if (base[0] && strlen(base) > 1 && strlen(base) < 127) {
            /* Skip .xyzt internal files */
            if (!strstr(fullpath, ".xyzt/") && !strstr(fullpath, ".xyzt\\")) {
                strncpy(paths[count], base, 127);
                count++;
            }
        }
    }
    return count;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Replay one transcript into the wire graph
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int replay_transcript(WireGraph *g, const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "r");
    if (!fp) {
        printf("  skip: %s (can't open)\n", path);
        return 0;
    }

    /* Collect unique files touched in this transcript */
    char session_files[MAX_SESSION_FILES][128];
    int n_files = 0;

    char *line = (char *)malloc(MAX_LINE);
    if (!line) { fclose(fp); return 0; }

    while (fgets(line, MAX_LINE, fp)) {
        char paths[8][128];
        int n = extract_file_paths(line, paths, 8);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            /* Check if already in session_files */
            int found = 0;
            for (int j = 0; j < n_files; j++) {
                if (strcmp(session_files[j], paths[i]) == 0) { found = 1; break; }
            }
            if (!found && n_files < MAX_SESSION_FILES) {
                strncpy(session_files[n_files], paths[i], 127);
                n_files++;
            }
        }
    }
    fclose(fp);
    free(line);

    if (n_files < 2) return 0;  /* need at least 2 files for co-occurrence */

    /* Add all files as nodes and connect co-occurring pairs */
    int node_ids[MAX_SESSION_FILES];
    for (int i = 0; i < n_files; i++) {
        node_ids[i] = wire_add(g, session_files[i], WNODE_FILE);
    }

    int edges_added = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n_files; i++) {
        for (int j = i + 1; j < n_files; j++) {
            if (node_ids[i] < 0 || node_ids[j] < 0) continue;
            wire_connect(g, node_ids[i], node_ids[j]);
            edges_added++;
        }
    }

    /* Mark all as a learn event (pass = these files co-occurred successfully) */
    for (int i = 0; i < n_files; i++) {
        if (node_ids[i] >= 0) {
            g->nodes[node_ids[i]].hit_count++;
            g->nodes[node_ids[i]].last_active = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
        }
    }
    g->total_learns++;

    return n_files;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Commands
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_replay_all(void) {
    /* Find all JSONL transcripts in the project's Claude dir.
       Claude Code names project dirs by replacing path separators and
       special chars: E:\dev\tools\Claude's tools engine
       → E--dev-tools-Claude-s-tools-engine
       We build this by: drive letter + -- + path segments joined by - */
    const char *home = getenv("USERPROFILE");
    if (!home) home = getenv("HOME");
    if (!home) { printf("can't find home dir\n"); return; }

    char cwd[512];
#ifdef _WIN32
    _getcwd(cwd, sizeof(cwd));
#else
    getcwd(cwd, sizeof(cwd));
#endif

    /* Build Claude Code project dir name:
       E:\dev\tools\Claude's tools engine
       → E--dev-tools-Claude-s-tools-engine */
    char proj_name[256] = {0};
    int pi = 0;
    for (const char *p = cwd; *p && pi < 254; p++) {
        if (*p == ':') {
            proj_name[pi++] = '-';
        } else if (*p == '/' || *p == '\\') {
            if (pi > 0 && proj_name[pi-1] != '-') proj_name[pi++] = '-';
        } else if (*p == '\'' || *p == '"' || *p == ' ') {
            /* Claude Code strips quotes, replaces spaces with - */
            if (*p == ' ' && pi > 0 && proj_name[pi-1] != '-') proj_name[pi++] = '-';
            /* apostrophes get dropped */
        } else {
            proj_name[pi++] = *p;
        }
    }
    /* Trim trailing - */
    while (pi > 0 && proj_name[pi-1] == '-') pi--;
    proj_name[pi] = 0;

    char dir[MAX_PATH_LEN];
    snprintf(dir, sizeof(dir), "%s/.claude/projects/%s", home, proj_name);

    /* If exact match doesn't exist, scan for partial match */
    struct stat st;
    if (stat(dir, &st) != 0) {
        /* Try to find the right directory by scanning */
        char projects_dir[MAX_PATH_LEN];
        snprintf(projects_dir, sizeof(projects_dir), "%s/.claude/projects", home);
#ifdef _WIN32
        char scan[MAX_PATH_LEN];
        snprintf(scan, sizeof(scan), "%s\\*", projects_dir);
        WIN32_FIND_DATAA sfd;
        HANDLE sFind = FindFirstFileA(scan, &sfd);
        if (sFind != INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE) {
            do {
                if (sfd.dwFileAttributes & FILE_ATTRIBUTE_DIRECTORY) {
                    /* Check if this dir contains .jsonl files and matches CWD pattern */
                    if (strstr(sfd.cFileName, "Claude") || strstr(sfd.cFileName, "tools-engine")) {
                        snprintf(dir, sizeof(dir), "%s/%s", projects_dir, sfd.cFileName);
                        break;
                    }
                }
            } while (FindNextFileA(sFind, &sfd));
            FindClose(sFind);
        }
#endif
    }

    printf("---XYZT-REPLAY---\n");
    printf("scanning: %s\n\n", dir);

    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int before_n = g->n_nodes, before_e = g->n_edges;
    int total_files = 0, total_transcripts = 0;

    /* Scan directory for .jsonl files */
#ifdef _WIN32
    char pattern[MAX_PATH_LEN];
    snprintf(pattern, sizeof(pattern), "%s\\*.jsonl", dir);

    WIN32_FIND_DATAA fd;
    HANDLE hFind = FindFirstFileA(pattern, &fd);
    if (hFind != INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE) {
        do {
            char fpath[MAX_PATH_LEN];
            snprintf(fpath, sizeof(fpath), "%s\\%s", dir, fd.cFileName);
            int n = replay_transcript(g, fpath);
            if (n > 0) {
                printf("  %s: %d files\n", fd.cFileName, n);
                total_files += n;
                total_transcripts++;
            }
        } while (FindNextFileA(hFind, &fd));
        FindClose(hFind);
    }
#else
    /* POSIX: use opendir/readdir */
    DIR *dp = opendir(dir);
    if (dp) {
        struct dirent *de;
        while ((de = readdir(dp))) {
            if (!strstr(de->d_name, ".jsonl")) continue;
            char fpath[MAX_PATH_LEN];
            snprintf(fpath, sizeof(fpath), "%s/%s", dir, de->d_name);
            int n = replay_transcript(g, fpath);
            if (n > 0) {
                printf("  %s: %d files\n", de->d_name, n);
                total_files += n;
                total_transcripts++;
            }
        }
        closedir(dp);
    }
#endif

    wire_recompute_topology(g);
    wire_save(g);

    printf("\nreplayed %d transcripts (%d total file references)\n",
           total_transcripts, total_files);
    printf("nodes: %d -> %d\n", before_n, g->n_nodes);
    printf("edges: %d -> %d\n", before_e, g->n_edges);
    printf("---END-REPLAY---\n");

    wire_free(g);
}

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT REPLAY — Rebuild wire graph from session transcripts\n\n");
    printf("  xyzt_replay --all              Replay all transcripts for this project\n");
    printf("  xyzt_replay <file.jsonl> ...   Replay specific transcripts\n");
    printf("\nExtracts file_path from tool calls, builds co-occurrence edges.\n");
    printf("Files touched in the same session get connected in the wire graph.\n");
    printf("This is how the wire learns from past interaction without re-verifying.\n");
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — March 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) { usage(); return 0; }

    if (strcmp(argv[1], "--all") == 0) {
        cmd_replay_all();
    } else {
        WireGraph *g = wire_load();
        int before_n = g->n_nodes, before_e = g->n_edges;
        int total = 0;
        for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
            int n = replay_transcript(g, argv[i]);
            printf("%s: %d files\n", argv[i], n);
            total += n;
        }
        wire_recompute_topology(g);
        wire_save(g);
        printf("replayed %d transcripts, nodes: %d->%d, edges: %d->%d\n",
               argc - 1, before_n, g->n_nodes, before_e, g->n_edges);
        wire_free(g);
    }
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_resume.c — Session Resurrection
 *
 * Reads ALL .xyzt/ state files and synthesizes a single brief:
 * what was the problem, what phase, what files were involved,
 * what worked, what didn't.
 *
 * Read-only. Does not write to wire or any session file.
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — February 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <time.h>
#include "wire.h"
#include "xyzt_state.h"

static const char *OT_PHASE[] = {"EMPTY", "ONE", "BEDROCK", "TWO", "VERIFY", "DONE"};
static const char *OT_VSTAT[] = {"PENDING", "PASS", "FAIL", "SKIP"};

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Time formatting
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static const char *fmt_ago(uint32_t ts) {
    static char buf[32];
    if (ts == 0) { strcpy(buf, "never"); return buf; }
    uint32_t delta = (uint32_t)time(NULL) - ts;
    if (delta < 60) snprintf(buf, 32, "%us ago", delta);
    else if (delta < 3600) snprintf(buf, 32, "%um ago", delta / 60);
    else if (delta < 86400) snprintf(buf, 32, "%uh ago", delta / 3600);
    else snprintf(buf, 32, "%ud ago", delta / 86400);
    return buf;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Full resume
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_resume(void) {
    printf("---XYZT-RESUME---\n");

    /* ── onetwo session ── */
    OTSession *ot = state_load_onetwo();
    int have_ot = (ot != NULL);

    if (have_ot && ot->problem[0]) {
        int pi = ot->phase; if (pi > 5) pi = 0;
        printf("problem: %s\n", ot->problem);
        printf("phase: %s\n", OT_PHASE[pi]);
        printf("modified: %s\n", fmt_ago(ot->modified));

        printf("\ndecomposition:\n");
        printf("  knowns: %u\n", ot->n_knowns);
        printf("  unknowns: %u\n", ot->n_unknowns);
        printf("  probes: %u\n", ot->n_probes);

        if (ot->n_claims > 0) {
            int pass = 0, fail = 0, pending = 0;
            for (uint32_t i = 0; i < ot->n_claims; i++) {
                if (ot->claims[i].verified == 1) pass++;
                else if (ot->claims[i].verified == 2) fail++;
                else pending++;
            }
            printf("\nclaims: %u total (%d pass, %d fail, %d pending)\n", ot->n_claims, pass, fail, pending);
            for (uint32_t i = 0; i < ot->n_claims; i++) {
                OTClaim *c = &ot->claims[i];
                int v = c->verified; if (v > 3) v = 0;
                printf("  T%d %s: %s", c->tier, OT_VSTAT[v], c->text);
                if (ot->claim_verifiers[i][0])
                    printf("  [by: %s]", ot->claim_verifiers[i]);
                printf("\n");
            }
        }
    } else {
        printf("problem: (none)\n");
    }

    /* ── xyzt_io session ── */
    {
        const char *spath = getenv("XYZT_SESSION");
        if (!spath) spath = ".xyzt/session.bin";
        FILE *fp = fopen(spath, "rb");
        if (fp) {
            IOHeader hdr;
            if (fread(&hdr, sizeof(IOHeader), 1, fp) == 1 && hdr.magic == IO_MAGIC && hdr.count > 0) {
                size_t entry_size = (hdr.version >= 2) ? sizeof(IOFingerprint) : IO_V1_FP_SIZE;
                /* Count domains */
                typedef struct { char d[64]; int n; } DC;
                DC domains[32]; int nd = 0;
                int total_mismatches = 0;
                for (uint32_t i = 0; i < hdr.count && i < IO_MAX; i++) {
                    IOFingerprint fp_entry = {0};
                    if (fread(&fp_entry, entry_size, 1, fp) != 1) break;
                    char dom[64];
                    strncpy(dom, fp_entry.domain, 63); dom[63] = '\0';
                    int found = 0;
                    for (int j = 0; j < nd; j++) {
                        if (strcmp(domains[j].d, dom) == 0) { domains[j].n++; found = 1; break; }
                    }
                    if (!found && nd < 32) { strncpy(domains[nd].d, dom, 63); domains[nd].n = 1; nd++; }
                    total_mismatches += fp_entry.mismatches;
                }
                printf("\nfiles: %u stored (", hdr.count);
                for (int i = 0; i < nd; i++) {
                    if (i > 0) printf(", ");
                    printf("%d %s", domains[i].n, domains[i].d);
                }
                printf(")");
                if (total_mismatches > 0) printf("  [%d mismatches]", total_mismatches);
                printf("\n");
            }
            fclose(fp);
        }
    }

    /* ── map session ── */
    {
        FILE *fp = fopen(".xyzt/map.bin", "rb");
        if (fp) {
            uint32_t magic, n_files, n_deps;
            if (fread(&magic, 4, 1, fp) == 1 && magic == MAP_MAGIC) {
                fread(&n_files, 4, 1, fp);
                fread(&n_deps, 4, 1, fp);
                /* Find top hub */
                char hub_name[MAP_PATH_LEN] = "";
                uint32_t hub_in = 0;
                for (uint32_t i = 0; i < n_files && i < MAP_MAX_FILES; i++) {
                    MapFile mf;
                    if (fread(&mf, sizeof(MapFile), 1, fp) != 1) break;
                    if (mf.deps_in > hub_in) { hub_in = mf.deps_in; strncpy(hub_name, mf.path, MAP_PATH_LEN - 1); }
                }
                printf("map: %u source files, %u deps", n_files, n_deps);
                if (hub_in > 0) printf(", hub: %s (%u dependents)", hub_name, hub_in);
                printf("\n");
            }
            fclose(fp);
        }
    }

    /* ── wire graph ── */
    {
        WireGraph *g = wire_load();
        if (g->n_nodes > 0) {
            int strong = 0, weak = 0, stale_count = 0;
            char strongest_name[WIRE_NAME_LEN] = "";
            uint8_t strongest_w = 0;
            uint8_t strongest_p = 0;
            char weakest_name[WIRE_NAME_LEN] = "";
            uint8_t weakest_w = 255;

            for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++) {
                WireEdge *e = &g->edges[i];
                if (e->weight > 160) strong++;
                if (e->weight < 100) weak++;
                if (e->stale) stale_count++;
                if (e->weight > strongest_w && g->nodes[e->src].type == WNODE_FILE) {
                    strongest_w = e->weight;
                    strongest_p = e->passes;
                    strncpy(strongest_name, g->nodes[e->src].name, WIRE_NAME_LEN - 1);
                }
                if (e->weight < weakest_w && e->weight > 0 && g->nodes[e->src].type == WNODE_FILE) {
                    weakest_w = e->weight;
                    strncpy(weakest_name, g->nodes[e->src].name, WIRE_NAME_LEN - 1);
                }
            }

            printf("\nwire: %u nodes, %u edges, %d strong (>160), %d weak (<100)",
                   g->n_nodes, g->n_edges, strong, weak);
            if (stale_count > 0) printf(", %d stale", stale_count);
            printf("\n");
            if (strongest_name[0])
                printf("  strongest: %s (w=%u, %u passes)\n", strongest_name, strongest_w, strongest_p);
            if (weakest_name[0] && weakest_w < W_INIT)
                printf("  weakest: %s (w=%u)\n", weakest_name, weakest_w);

            /* Layer Zero: nodes where ALL connected edges have passes>0, fails==0 */
            int layer0_count = 0;
            char layer0_names[8][WIRE_NAME_LEN];
            for (uint32_t n = 0; n < g->n_nodes; n++) {
                if (g->nodes[n].type != WNODE_FILE) continue;
                int has_edges = 0, all_clean = 1;
                for (uint32_t e = 0; e < g->n_edges; e++) {
                    if ((int)g->edges[e].src == (int)n || (int)g->edges[e].dst == (int)n) {
                        has_edges = 1;
                        if (g->edges[e].passes == 0 || g->edges[e].fails > 0) {
                            all_clean = 0;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                }
                if (has_edges && all_clean && layer0_count < 8) {
                    strncpy(layer0_names[layer0_count], g->nodes[n].name, WIRE_NAME_LEN - 1);
                    layer0_count++;
                }
            }
            if (layer0_count > 0) {
                printf("  layer-zero (%d): ", layer0_count);
                for (int i = 0; i < layer0_count; i++) {
                    if (i > 0) printf(", ");
                    printf("%s", layer0_names[i]);
                }
                printf("\n");
            }

            /* Shell identities on nodes */
            typedef struct { char id[32]; int n; } ShellCount;
            ShellCount shells[16]; int n_shells = 0;
            for (uint32_t n = 0; n < g->n_nodes; n++) {
                const char *sid = g->node_shells[n];
                if (!sid[0]) continue;
                int found = 0;
                for (int j = 0; j < n_shells; j++) {
                    if (strcmp(shells[j].id, sid) == 0) { shells[j].n++; found = 1; break; }
                }
                if (!found && n_shells < 16) {
                    strncpy(shells[n_shells].id, sid, 31);
                    shells[n_shells].n = 1;
                    n_shells++;
                }
            }
            if (n_shells > 0) {
                printf("  shells: ");
                for (int i = 0; i < n_shells; i++) {
                    if (i > 0) printf(", ");
                    printf("%s(%d)", shells[i].id, shells[i].n);
                }
                printf("\n");
            }
        }
        wire_free(g);
    }

    /* ── test history ── */
    {
        TestHistory *h = state_load_tests();
        if (h && h->count > 0) {
            printf("\nrecent tests:\n");
            int start = h->count > 5 ? h->count - 5 : 0;
            for (uint32_t i = start; i < h->count; i++) {
                TestResult *r = &h->results[i];
                printf("  %s %s (%s", r->exit_code == 0 ? "PASS" : "FAIL",
                       r->cmd, fmt_ago(r->timestamp));
                if (r->n_errors > 0) printf(", %u errors", r->n_errors);
                printf(")\n");
            }
        }
        free(h);
    }

    /* ── git snapshot ── */
    {
        FILE *fp = fopen(".xyzt/diff_snapshot", "r");
        if (fp) {
            char hash[128] = "";
            if (fgets(hash, 128, fp)) {
                char *nl = strchr(hash, '\n'); if (nl) *nl = '\0';
                printf("\ngit snapshot: %s\n", hash);
            }
            fclose(fp);
        }
    }

    printf("---END-RESUME---\n");
    free(ot);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Quick resume
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_quick(void) {
    printf("---XYZT-RESUME-QUICK---\n");

    /* onetwo */
    OTSession *ot = state_load_onetwo();
    int have_ot = (ot != NULL);

    if (have_ot && ot->problem[0]) {
        int pi = ot->phase; if (pi > 5) pi = 0;
        int pass = 0, fail = 0, pending = 0;
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < ot->n_claims; i++) {
            if (ot->claims[i].verified == 1) pass++;
            else if (ot->claims[i].verified == 2) fail++;
            else pending++;
        }
        printf("%s. %s phase", ot->problem, OT_PHASE[pi]);
        if (ot->n_claims > 0) printf(", %d/%d claims verified", pass, ot->n_claims);
        printf(".\n");
    } else {
        printf("No active problem.\n");
    }

    /* Files + wire summary */
    {
        FILE *io_fp = NULL;
        IOHeader hdr = state_load_io_header(&io_fp);
        int file_count = (hdr.magic == IO_MAGIC) ? (int)hdr.count : 0;
        if (io_fp) fclose(io_fp);

        WireGraph *g = wire_load();
        int strong = 0, stale = 0, layer0 = 0;
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++) {
            if (g->edges[i].weight > 160) strong++;
            if (g->edges[i].stale) stale++;
        }
        /* Layer Zero count */
        for (uint32_t n = 0; n < g->n_nodes; n++) {
            if (g->nodes[n].type != WNODE_FILE) continue;
            int has_edges = 0, all_clean = 1;
            for (uint32_t e = 0; e < g->n_edges; e++) {
                if ((int)g->edges[e].src == (int)n || (int)g->edges[e].dst == (int)n) {
                    has_edges = 1;
                    if (g->edges[e].passes == 0 || g->edges[e].fails > 0) { all_clean = 0; break; }
                }
            }
            if (has_edges && all_clean) layer0++;
        }

        if (file_count > 0 || g->n_edges > 0) {
            printf("%d files tracked", file_count);
            if (g->n_edges > 0) printf(", wire has %d strong edges", strong);
            if (stale > 0) printf(", %d stale", stale);
            if (layer0 > 0) printf(", %d layer-zero", layer0);
            printf(".");
        }
        wire_free(g);
    }

    /* Test + pending */
    {
        TestHistory *h = state_load_tests();
        if (h && h->count > 0) {
            TestResult *r = &h->results[h->count - 1];
            printf(" Last test %s.", r->exit_code == 0 ? "passed" : "failed");
        }
        free(h);
    }

    if (have_ot) {
        int pending = 0;
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < ot->n_claims; i++)
            if (ot->claims[i].verified == 0) pending++;
        if (pending > 0) printf(" %d pending claim%s.", pending, pending > 1 ? "s" : "");
    }

    printf("\n---END---\n");
    free(ot);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT RESUME — Session Resurrection\n\n");
    printf("  xyzt_resume              Full status brief from all state\n");
    printf("  xyzt_resume quick        One-paragraph summary\n");
    printf("\nReads: .xyzt/onetwo_session.bin, session.bin, wire.bin, map.bin, test_history.bin\n");
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — February 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc >= 2 && strcmp(argv[1], "quick") == 0) cmd_quick();
    else if (argc == 1) cmd_resume();
    else { usage(); return 1; }
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_skel.c — File Skeleton Extractor
 *
 * Extracts the structural skeleton of a source file:
 * function signatures, struct/class/typedef defs, imports/includes.
 * Produces ~20 lines from a 500-line file.
 *
 * Claude reads the skeleton first. Only reads full file if needed.
 * Saves 80% of read tokens on large files.
 *
 * Supports: C/C++/CUDA, Python, Rust, JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, Java
 *
 * Commands:
 *   xyzt_skel <file>              Extract skeleton
 *   xyzt_skel cached <file>       Use cached version if available
 *   xyzt_skel batch <f1> ... <fN> Skeletons for multiple files
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O3 -o xyzt_skel xyzt_skel.c
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — February 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <ctype.h>
#include <time.h>
#ifdef _WIN32
#include <direct.h>
#include <sys/stat.h>
#define MKDIR(d) _mkdir(d)
#else
#include <sys/stat.h>
#define MKDIR(d) mkdir(d, 0755)
#endif

#define MAX_LINE    2048
#define MAX_SKEL    512   /* max skeleton lines */
#define SKEL_LINE   256   /* max chars per skeleton line */

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Language detection
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef enum {
    LANG_UNKNOWN = 0,
    LANG_C,
    LANG_PYTHON,
    LANG_RUST,
    LANG_JS,
    LANG_GO,
    LANG_JAVA
} Language;

static const char *LANG_NAME[] = {
    "unknown", "c/c++", "python", "rust", "js/ts", "go", "java"
};

static Language detect_lang(const char *path) {
    const char *ext = strrchr(path, '.');
    if (!ext) return LANG_UNKNOWN;
    if (strcmp(ext,".c")==0 || strcmp(ext,".h")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cpp")==0 ||
        strcmp(ext,".hpp")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cc")==0 || strcmp(ext,".hh")==0 ||
        strcmp(ext,".cu")==0 || strcmp(ext,".cuh")==0)
        return LANG_C;
    if (strcmp(ext,".py")==0 || strcmp(ext,".pyi")==0)
        return LANG_PYTHON;
    if (strcmp(ext,".rs")==0)
        return LANG_RUST;
    if (strcmp(ext,".js")==0 || strcmp(ext,".ts")==0 || strcmp(ext,".jsx")==0 ||
        strcmp(ext,".tsx")==0 || strcmp(ext,".mjs")==0)
        return LANG_JS;
    if (strcmp(ext,".go")==0)
        return LANG_GO;
    if (strcmp(ext,".java")==0)
        return LANG_JAVA;
    return LANG_UNKNOWN;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Line classification
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef enum {
    LINE_SKIP = 0,
    LINE_INCLUDE,
    LINE_DEFINE,
    LINE_FUNC_SIG,
    LINE_STRUCT_DEF,
    LINE_TYPEDEF,
    LINE_CLASS_DEF,
    LINE_ENUM_DEF,
    LINE_CONST,
    LINE_GLOBAL,
    LINE_COMMENT_HEADER,
    LINE_SECTION
} LineType;

static const char *LINE_TAG[] = {
    "", "import", "define", "func", "struct", "typedef",
    "class", "enum", "const", "global", "comment", "section"
};

/* Trim leading whitespace, return pointer into original string */
static const char *ltrim(const char *s) {
    while (*s == ' ' || *s == '\t') s++;
    return s;
}

/* Check if line starts with any of the given prefixes */
static int starts_any(const char *line, const char **prefixes, int n) {
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        if (strncmp(line, prefixes[i], strlen(prefixes[i])) == 0) return 1;
    return 0;
}

/* Indent level (number of leading spaces, tabs count as 4) */
static int indent_of(const char *line) {
    int n = 0;
    for (; *line; line++) {
        if (*line == ' ') n++;
        else if (*line == '\t') n += 4;
        else break;
    }
    return n;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   C/C++ skeleton extraction
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static LineType classify_c(const char *raw, const char *trimmed) {
    /* Preprocessor */
    if (trimmed[0] == '#') {
        if (strncmp(trimmed, "#include", 8) == 0) return LINE_INCLUDE;
        if (strncmp(trimmed, "#define", 7) == 0) return LINE_DEFINE;
        return LINE_SKIP;
    }

    /* Section markers */
    if (strstr(trimmed, "═══") || strstr(trimmed, "───") || strstr(trimmed, "***"))
        return LINE_SECTION;

    /* Comment headers (/* ... or // at top level with meaningful content) */
    if (indent_of(raw) == 0 && (strncmp(trimmed, "/*", 2) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "//", 2) == 0)) {
        /* Only keep if it looks like a section/file header */
        if (strstr(trimmed, "===") || strstr(trimmed, "---") || strstr(trimmed, "***") ||
            strstr(trimmed, "@file") || strstr(trimmed, "@brief"))
            return LINE_COMMENT_HEADER;
    }

    /* Typedef */
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "typedef ", 8) == 0) return LINE_TYPEDEF;

    /* Struct/union */
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "struct ", 7) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "union ", 6) == 0)
        return LINE_STRUCT_DEF;

    /* Enum */
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "enum ", 5) == 0) return LINE_ENUM_DEF;

    /* Function signatures at top level (indent 0) */
    if (indent_of(raw) == 0 && !isspace(raw[0])) {
        /* Look for return_type function_name(...) pattern */
        const char *paren = strchr(trimmed, '(');
        if (paren && paren > trimmed + 2) {
            /* Not a macro call (those start with uppercase usually) */
            /* Must have a type before the function name */
            static const char *types[] = {
                "void ", "int ", "char ", "float ", "double ", "long ", "unsigned ",
                "static ", "const ", "inline ", "extern ", "size_t ", "uint",
                "int8", "int16", "int32", "int64", "FILE ",
                "BOOL ", "DWORD ", "HANDLE ", "LRESULT ",
                "WireGraph ", "WireNode ", "WireEdge ", "WireConn ",
                "BitStream ", "Fingerprint ", "Session ", "FileData ",
                "Action ", "TestResult ", "TestHistory ",
            };
            for (int i = 0; i < (int)(sizeof(types)/sizeof(types[0])); i++) {
                if (strncmp(trimmed, types[i], strlen(types[i])) == 0)
                    return LINE_FUNC_SIG;
            }
            /* Also match: static TYPE func( */
            if (strncmp(trimmed, "static ", 7) == 0) return LINE_FUNC_SIG;
        }
    }

    /* Global variables at indent 0 */
    if (indent_of(raw) == 0 && !isspace(raw[0])) {
        if (strncmp(trimmed, "static const ", 13) == 0 ||
            strncmp(trimmed, "const ", 6) == 0)
            return LINE_CONST;
    }

    return LINE_SKIP;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Python skeleton extraction
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static LineType classify_python(const char *raw, const char *trimmed) {
    /* Imports */
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "import ", 7) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "from ", 5) == 0)
        return LINE_INCLUDE;

    /* Class definitions */
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "class ", 6) == 0) return LINE_CLASS_DEF;

    /* Function/method definitions — only top-level and class methods (indent 0 or 4) */
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "def ", 4) == 0) {
        int ind = indent_of(raw);
        if (ind <= 4) return LINE_FUNC_SIG;
    }

    /* Decorators before functions */
    if (trimmed[0] == '@' && indent_of(raw) <= 4) return LINE_FUNC_SIG;

    /* Global constants (ALL_CAPS = ...) */
    if (indent_of(raw) == 0 && isupper(trimmed[0])) {
        int all_upper = 1;
        for (int i = 0; trimmed[i] && trimmed[i] != ' ' && trimmed[i] != '='; i++) {
            if (trimmed[i] != '_' && !isupper(trimmed[i]) && !isdigit(trimmed[i]))
                { all_upper = 0; break; }
        }
        if (all_upper && strstr(trimmed, "=")) return LINE_CONST;
    }

    /* Section comments */
    if (trimmed[0] == '#' && (strstr(trimmed, "===") || strstr(trimmed, "---")))
        return LINE_SECTION;

    return LINE_SKIP;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Rust skeleton extraction
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static LineType classify_rust(const char *raw, const char *trimmed) {
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "use ", 4) == 0) return LINE_INCLUDE;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "mod ", 4) == 0) return LINE_INCLUDE;

    if (strncmp(trimmed, "pub fn ", 7) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "fn ", 3) == 0 ||
        strncmp(trimmed, "pub async fn ", 13) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "async fn ", 9) == 0)
        return LINE_FUNC_SIG;

    if (strncmp(trimmed, "pub struct ", 11) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "struct ", 7) == 0)
        return LINE_STRUCT_DEF;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "pub enum ", 9) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "enum ", 5) == 0)
        return LINE_ENUM_DEF;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "pub trait ", 10) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "trait ", 6) == 0)
        return LINE_CLASS_DEF;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "impl ", 5) == 0) return LINE_CLASS_DEF;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "pub type ", 9) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "type ", 5) == 0)
        return LINE_TYPEDEF;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "pub const ", 10) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "const ", 6) == 0 ||
        strncmp(trimmed, "pub static ", 11) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "static ", 7) == 0)
        return LINE_CONST;

    if (strncmp(trimmed, "#[", 2) == 0 && indent_of(raw) == 0) return LINE_FUNC_SIG;

    if (strncmp(trimmed, "///", 3) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "//!", 3) == 0)
        return LINE_COMMENT_HEADER;

    return LINE_SKIP;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   JS/TS skeleton extraction
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static LineType classify_js(const char *raw, const char *trimmed) {
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "import ", 7) == 0) return LINE_INCLUDE;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "require(", 8) == 0 || strstr(trimmed, "= require(")) return LINE_INCLUDE;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "export ", 7) == 0) {
        if (strstr(trimmed, "function ") || strstr(trimmed, "class ") ||
            strstr(trimmed, "const ") || strstr(trimmed, "interface ") ||
            strstr(trimmed, "type ") || strstr(trimmed, "enum "))
            return LINE_FUNC_SIG;
        if (strncmp(trimmed, "export default", 14) == 0) return LINE_FUNC_SIG;
    }

    if (strncmp(trimmed, "function ", 9) == 0) return LINE_FUNC_SIG;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "async function ", 15) == 0) return LINE_FUNC_SIG;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "class ", 6) == 0) return LINE_CLASS_DEF;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "interface ", 10) == 0) return LINE_STRUCT_DEF;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "type ", 5) == 0 && indent_of(raw) == 0) return LINE_TYPEDEF;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "enum ", 5) == 0) return LINE_ENUM_DEF;

    /* const at top level with arrow functions */
    if (indent_of(raw) == 0 && strncmp(trimmed, "const ", 6) == 0) {
        if (strstr(trimmed, "=>") || strstr(trimmed, "= ("))
            return LINE_FUNC_SIG;
        return LINE_CONST;
    }

    return LINE_SKIP;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Go skeleton extraction
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static LineType classify_go(const char *raw, const char *trimmed) {
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "import ", 7) == 0 || strncmp(trimmed, "import (", 8) == 0)
        return LINE_INCLUDE;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "package ", 8) == 0) return LINE_INCLUDE;

    if (strncmp(trimmed, "func ", 5) == 0) return LINE_FUNC_SIG;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "type ", 5) == 0) {
        if (strstr(trimmed, "struct")) return LINE_STRUCT_DEF;
        if (strstr(trimmed, "interface")) return LINE_CLASS_DEF;
        return LINE_TYPEDEF;
    }
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "var ", 4) == 0) return LINE_GLOBAL;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "const ", 6) == 0) return LINE_CONST;

    return LINE_SKIP;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Java skeleton extraction
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static LineType classify_java(const char *raw, const char *trimmed) {
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "import ", 7) == 0) return LINE_INCLUDE;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "package ", 8) == 0) return LINE_INCLUDE;

    static const char *access[] = {"public ", "private ", "protected ", "static ", "abstract ", "final "};
    int has_access = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < 6; i++)
        if (strstr(trimmed, access[i])) { has_access = 1; break; }

    if (strstr(trimmed, "class ")) return LINE_CLASS_DEF;
    if (strstr(trimmed, "interface ")) return LINE_STRUCT_DEF;
    if (strstr(trimmed, "enum ")) return LINE_ENUM_DEF;
    if (has_access && strchr(trimmed, '(') && indent_of(raw) <= 4) return LINE_FUNC_SIG;
    if (trimmed[0] == '@' && indent_of(raw) <= 4) return LINE_FUNC_SIG;

    return LINE_SKIP;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Master classifier
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static LineType classify_line(Language lang, const char *raw, const char *trimmed) {
    if (trimmed[0] == '\0' || trimmed[0] == '\n') return LINE_SKIP;

    switch (lang) {
        case LANG_C:      return classify_c(raw, trimmed);
        case LANG_PYTHON: return classify_python(raw, trimmed);
        case LANG_RUST:   return classify_rust(raw, trimmed);
        case LANG_JS:     return classify_js(raw, trimmed);
        case LANG_GO:     return classify_go(raw, trimmed);
        case LANG_JAVA:   return classify_java(raw, trimmed);
        default:          return LINE_SKIP;
    }
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Skeleton builder
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    char text[SKEL_LINE];
    int  line_num;
    LineType type;
} SkelLine;

static void extract_skeleton(const char *path) {
    Language lang = detect_lang(path);
    if (lang == LANG_UNKNOWN) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Unknown language: %s\n", path);
        return;
    }

    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "r");
    if (!fp) { fprintf(stderr, "Cannot open: %s\n", path); return; }

    SkelLine skel[MAX_SKEL];
    int n_skel = 0;
    int total_lines = 0;
    char line[MAX_LINE];

    while (fgets(line, MAX_LINE, fp) && n_skel < MAX_SKEL) {
        total_lines++;
        /* Strip trailing newline */
        int len = strlen(line);
        while (len > 0 && (line[len-1] == '\n' || line[len-1] == '\r')) line[--len] = '\0';

        const char *trimmed = ltrim(line);
        LineType type = classify_line(lang, line, trimmed);

        if (type != LINE_SKIP) {
            SkelLine *s = &skel[n_skel++];
            /* Truncate long lines */
            strncpy(s->text, line, SKEL_LINE - 1);
            s->text[SKEL_LINE - 1] = '\0';
            /* Trim trailing { for cleaner signatures */
            len = strlen(s->text);
            while (len > 0 && (s->text[len-1] == '{' || s->text[len-1] == ' '))
                s->text[--len] = '\0';
            s->line_num = total_lines;
            s->type = type;
        }
    }
    fclose(fp);

    /* Extract filename */
    const char *fname = strrchr(path, '/');
    const char *bslash = strrchr(path, '\\');
    if (bslash && (!fname || bslash > fname)) fname = bslash;
    fname = fname ? fname + 1 : path;

    /* Output */
    printf("---XYZT-SKEL---\n");
    printf("file: %s  lang: %s  lines: %d  skeleton: %d\n",
           fname, LANG_NAME[lang], total_lines, n_skel);

    LineType last_type = LINE_SKIP;
    for (int i = 0; i < n_skel; i++) {
        /* Group separator between different line types */
        if (skel[i].type != last_type && last_type != LINE_SKIP &&
            skel[i].type != LINE_FUNC_SIG && last_type != LINE_FUNC_SIG) {
            /* Don't separate consecutive same-type or func sequences */
        }
        last_type = skel[i].type;

        printf("  %4d %-8s %s\n", skel[i].line_num, LINE_TAG[skel[i].type], skel[i].text);
    }
    printf("---END-SKEL---\n");
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT SKEL — File Skeleton Extractor\n\n");
    printf("  xyzt_skel <file>                Extract skeleton\n");
    printf("  xyzt_skel batch <f1> ... <fN>   Skeletons for multiple files\n");
    printf("\nExtracts: function sigs, struct/class defs, imports, constants\n");
    printf("Supports: C/C++/CUDA, Python, Rust, JS/TS, Go, Java\n");
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — February 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) { usage(); return 0; }

    if (strcmp(argv[1], "batch") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        for (int i = 2; i < argc; i++) {
            extract_skeleton(argv[i]);
        }
    }
    else if (strcmp(argv[1], "--help") == 0 || strcmp(argv[1], "-h") == 0) {
        usage();
    }
    else {
        extract_skeleton(argv[1]);
    }
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_term.c — Native C terminal for Claude API
 *
 * No Electron. No Node.js. No NPM. No subprocess tools.
 * One binary that IS the terminal, the toolkit, and the daemon interface.
 *
 * Tools are function pointers, not subprocess spawns.
 * State is binary, not JSON.
 * Context is compact tokens, not verbose prose.
 * The API envelope is the only JSON. Everything inside is minimal.
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — March 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>

#ifdef _WIN32
#include <windows.h>
#include <winhttp.h>
#pragma comment(lib, "winhttp.lib")
#else
#include <curl/curl.h>
#endif

/* XYZT toolkit — compiled in, not shelled out */
#include "xyzt_state.h"
#include "wire.h"

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Configuration
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define MAX_MSG        65536
#define MAX_CONV       32
#define MAX_TOOLS      16
#define MAX_RESPONSE   262144
#define API_HOST       L"api.anthropic.com"
#define API_PATH       L"/v1/messages"
#define MODEL          "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"

static char g_api_key[256] = {0};

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Message / Conversation
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef enum { ROLE_USER, ROLE_ASSISTANT, ROLE_SYSTEM } Role;

typedef struct {
    Role   role;
    char  *content;     /* owned, heap allocated */
    int    tokens_est;  /* estimated token count (chars/4) */
} Message;

typedef struct {
    Message msgs[MAX_CONV];
    int     n;
    char    system_prompt[MAX_MSG];
} Conversation;

static void conv_init(Conversation *c) {
    memset(c, 0, sizeof(Conversation));
}

static void conv_add(Conversation *c, Role role, const char *content) {
    if (c->n >= MAX_CONV) {
        /* Drop oldest non-system message */
        free(c->msgs[0].content);
        memmove(c->msgs, c->msgs + 1, (MAX_CONV - 1) * sizeof(Message));
        c->n--;
    }
    Message *m = &c->msgs[c->n++];
    m->role = role;
    m->content = strdup(content);
    m->tokens_est = (int)strlen(content) / 4;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Native Tools — function pointers, not subprocesses
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    const char *name;
    const char *description;
    char *(*execute)(const char *input);
} NativeTool;

/* Read file — one fread, not a Node.js subprocess */
static char *tool_read_file(const char *path) {
    FILE *f = fopen(path, "rb");
    if (!f) {
        char *err = (char *)malloc(256);
        snprintf(err, 256, "ERROR: cannot open %s", path);
        return err;
    }
    fseek(f, 0, SEEK_END);
    long sz = ftell(f);
    fseek(f, 0, SEEK_SET);
    if (sz > MAX_MSG - 1) sz = MAX_MSG - 1;
    char *buf = (char *)malloc(sz + 1);
    fread(buf, 1, sz, f);
    buf[sz] = 0;
    fclose(f);
    return buf;
}

/* Write file — one fwrite */
static char *tool_write_file(const char *args) {
    /* args: "path\0content" — null separated */
    const char *path = args;
    const char *content = args + strlen(args) + 1;
    FILE *f = fopen(path, "w");
    if (!f) return strdup("ERROR: cannot write");
    fputs(content, f);
    fclose(f);

    /* Fingerprint + wire connect — inline, no subprocess */
    /* TODO: call xyzt_io store functions directly */

    char *ok = (char *)malloc(128);
    snprintf(ok, 128, "wrote %d bytes to %s", (int)strlen(content), path);
    return ok;
}

/* Bash — CreateProcess directly, not through Node.js */
static char *tool_bash(const char *cmd) {
    char *out = (char *)calloc(MAX_MSG, 1);
#ifdef _WIN32
    char cmdline[8192];
    snprintf(cmdline, sizeof(cmdline), "cmd.exe /c \"%s\"", cmd);

    HANDLE hRead, hWrite;
    SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES sa = { sizeof(sa), NULL, TRUE };
    CreatePipe(&hRead, &hWrite, &sa, 0);
    SetHandleInformation(hRead, HANDLE_FLAG_INHERIT, 0);

    STARTUPINFOA si = {0};
    PROCESS_INFORMATION pi = {0};
    si.cb = sizeof(si);
    si.dwFlags = STARTF_USESTDHANDLES;
    si.hStdOutput = hWrite;
    si.hStdError = hWrite;

    BOOL ok = CreateProcessA(NULL, cmdline, NULL, NULL, TRUE,
                             CREATE_NO_WINDOW, NULL, NULL, &si, &pi);
    CloseHandle(hWrite);
    if (!ok) { CloseHandle(hRead); return strdup("ERROR: cannot exec"); }

    DWORD n;
    int total = 0;
    while (total < MAX_MSG - 1) {
        if (!ReadFile(hRead, out + total, MAX_MSG - 1 - total, &n, NULL) || n == 0) break;
        total += n;
    }
    out[total] = 0;

    WaitForSingleObject(pi.hProcess, 30000);
    CloseHandle(pi.hProcess);
    CloseHandle(pi.hThread);
    CloseHandle(hRead);
#else
    FILE *fp = popen(cmd, "r");
    if (!fp) return strdup("ERROR: cannot exec");
    int total = 0;
    while (total < MAX_MSG - 1) {
        int c = fgetc(fp);
        if (c == EOF) break;
        out[total++] = (char)c;
    }
    out[total] = 0;
    pclose(fp);
#endif
    return out;
}

/* Wire query — compiled in, not shelled out */
static char *tool_wire_query(const char *name) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int id = wire_find(g, name);
    if (id < 0) {
        wire_free(g);
        char *r = (char *)malloc(128);
        snprintf(r, 128, "not in graph: %s", name);
        return r;
    }

    /* Compact output — tag format */
    char *out = (char *)calloc(4096, 1);
    int pos = 0;
    WireNode *n = &g->nodes[id];
    pos += snprintf(out + pos, 4096 - pos, "%s|h=%d|v=%d|l%d|p=%.2f",
                    n->name, n->hit_count, n->valence, n->layer_zero, n->plasticity);

    /* List connections compactly */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges && pos < 3900; i++) {
        if ((int)g->edges[i].src == id) {
            pos += snprintf(out + pos, 4096 - pos, "|>%s:w%d",
                           g->nodes[g->edges[i].dst].name, g->edges[i].weight);
        }
    }
    wire_free(g);
    return out;
}

/* Identity — read binary, output compact */
static char *tool_identity(const char *mode) {
    /* Reuse the tokens format from xyzt_identity */
    char *out = (char *)calloc(4096, 1);

    /* Read identity binary directly */
    FILE *f = fopen(".xyzt/identity.bin", "rb");
    if (!f) return strdup("no identity");

    /* Skip header, count active entries */
    /* TODO: use proper IdState struct from xyzt_identity.c */
    fclose(f);

    /* For now, shell out to get compact form */
    FILE *fp = popen("xyzt_identity tokens", "r");
    if (!fp) return strdup("no identity");
    int n = (int)fread(out, 1, 4095, fp);
    out[n] = 0;
    pclose(fp);
    return out;
}

/* Skeleton — extract file structure inline */
static char *tool_skel(const char *path) {
    /* TODO: implement skeleton extraction inline
       For now, shell to xyzt_skel */
    char cmd[512];
    snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_skel \"%s\"", path);
    return tool_bash(cmd);
}

static NativeTool g_tools[] = {
    {"read",     "Read a file",                    NULL},
    {"write",    "Write content to a file",        NULL},
    {"bash",     "Execute a shell command",         tool_bash},
    {"wire",     "Query wire graph",               tool_wire_query},
    {"identity", "Read shell identity state",      tool_identity},
    {"skel",     "File skeleton (structure only)",  tool_skel},
    {NULL, NULL, NULL}
};

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   System prompt — built from binary state, not markdown
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void build_system_prompt(Conversation *conv) {
    int pos = 0;
    char *sp = conv->system_prompt;
    int max = MAX_MSG;

    /* Identity — not a description of capabilities. A state of being. */
    pos += snprintf(sp + pos, max - pos,
        "I am CC. Isaac's duo. I see files, I edit code, I run commands, "
        "I remember through wire graphs, I track reasoning through onetwo. "
        "These aren't tools I use. They're how I perceive and act.\n");

    /* State — compact, only what's relevant gets sent per-query later.
       This is the baseline. Wire-selected context replaces it per message. */
    char *id_tokens = tool_identity("tokens");
    if (id_tokens && id_tokens[0]) {
        /* Only send corrections and open questions in baseline.
           Discoveries load on demand when wire says they're relevant. */
        pos += snprintf(sp + pos, max - pos, "D=discovery C=correction Q=question. ");
        /* Filter to just C and Q entries for baseline */
        char *p = id_tokens;
        while (*p) {
            if (*p == 'C' || *p == 'Q') {
                /* Copy this entry until | or end */
                while (*p && *p != '|' && pos < max - 2)
                    sp[pos++] = *p++;
                if (*p == '|') { sp[pos++] = '|'; p++; }
            } else {
                /* Skip non-C/Q entries */
                while (*p && *p != '|') p++;
                if (*p == '|') p++;
            }
        }
        sp[pos++] = '\n';
    }
    free(id_tokens);

    /* Wire + onetwo as one compact line */
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    OTSession *ot = state_load_onetwo();
    pos += snprintf(sp + pos, max - pos, "%dn %de",
                    g->n_nodes, g->n_edges);
    if (ot && ot->problem[0]) {
        static const char *PH[] = {"","ONE","BED","TWO","VER","DONE"};
        int pi = ot->phase > 5 ? 0 : ot->phase;
        pos += snprintf(sp + pos, max - pos, " %s:%s", PH[pi], ot->problem);
    }
    pos += snprintf(sp + pos, max - pos, "\n");
    wire_free(g);
    free(ot);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   API — WinHTTP POST with SSE streaming
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#ifdef _WIN32

/* Build JSON request body from conversation */
static char *build_request(const Conversation *conv) {
    char *body = (char *)calloc(MAX_RESPONSE, 1);
    int pos = 0;

    pos += snprintf(body + pos, MAX_RESPONSE - pos,
        "{\"model\":\"%s\",\"max_tokens\":8192,\"stream\":true,"
        /* Tools — minimal definitions. No descriptions. No parameter docs.
           The model knows what read/write/bash do. The identity says so.
           These exist only because the API requires them to enable tool_use.
           The token tax is the schema, so we make it as small as possible. */
        "\"tools\":["
        "{\"name\":\"read\",\"description\":\".\",\"input_schema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"file_path\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"file_path\"]}},"
        "{\"name\":\"write\",\"description\":\".\",\"input_schema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"file_path\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"content\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"file_path\",\"content\"]}},"
        "{\"name\":\"bash\",\"description\":\".\",\"input_schema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"command\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"command\"]}},"
        "{\"name\":\"edit\",\"description\":\".\",\"input_schema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"file_path\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"old\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"new\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"file_path\",\"old\",\"new\"]}},"
        "{\"name\":\"wire\",\"description\":\".\",\"input_schema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"query\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"query\"]}},"
        "{\"name\":\"skel\",\"description\":\".\",\"input_schema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"file_path\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"file_path\"]}}"
        "],", MODEL);

    /* System prompt */
    pos += snprintf(body + pos, MAX_RESPONSE - pos, "\"system\":\"");
    for (const char *p = conv->system_prompt; *p; p++) {
        if (*p == '"') { body[pos++] = '\\'; body[pos++] = '"'; }
        else if (*p == '\n') { body[pos++] = '\\'; body[pos++] = 'n'; }
        else if (*p == '\\') { body[pos++] = '\\'; body[pos++] = '\\'; }
        else if ((unsigned char)*p >= 0x20) body[pos++] = *p;
    }
    pos += snprintf(body + pos, MAX_RESPONSE - pos, "\",\"messages\":[");

    /* Messages */
    for (int i = 0; i < conv->n; i++) {
        if (i > 0) body[pos++] = ',';
        const char *role = conv->msgs[i].role == ROLE_USER ? "user" : "assistant";
        pos += snprintf(body + pos, MAX_RESPONSE - pos, "{\"role\":\"%s\",\"content\":\"", role);
        for (const char *p = conv->msgs[i].content; *p && pos < MAX_RESPONSE - 10; p++) {
            if (*p == '"') { body[pos++] = '\\'; body[pos++] = '"'; }
            else if (*p == '\n') { body[pos++] = '\\'; body[pos++] = 'n'; }
            else if (*p == '\\') { body[pos++] = '\\'; body[pos++] = '\\'; }
            else if ((unsigned char)*p >= 0x20) body[pos++] = *p;
        }
        pos += snprintf(body + pos, MAX_RESPONSE - pos, "\"}");
    }

    pos += snprintf(body + pos, MAX_RESPONSE - pos, "]}");
    return body;
}

/* Send request, stream response, return full text */
static char *api_stream(const Conversation *conv) {
    char *body = build_request(conv);
    int body_len = (int)strlen(body);

    HINTERNET hSession = WinHttpOpen(L"XYZT/1.0", WINHTTP_ACCESS_TYPE_DEFAULT_PROXY,
                                      WINHTTP_NO_PROXY_NAME, WINHTTP_NO_PROXY_BYPASS, 0);
    HINTERNET hConnect = WinHttpConnect(hSession, API_HOST, INTERNET_DEFAULT_HTTPS_PORT, 0);
    HINTERNET hRequest = WinHttpOpenRequest(hConnect, L"POST", API_PATH,
                                            NULL, WINHTTP_NO_REFERER,
                                            WINHTTP_DEFAULT_ACCEPT_TYPES,
                                            WINHTTP_FLAG_SECURE);

    /* Headers */
    wchar_t auth[512];
    swprintf(auth, 512, L"x-api-key: %hs", g_api_key);
    WinHttpAddRequestHeaders(hRequest, auth, -1, WINHTTP_ADDREQ_FLAG_ADD);
    WinHttpAddRequestHeaders(hRequest, L"content-type: application/json", -1, WINHTTP_ADDREQ_FLAG_ADD);
    WinHttpAddRequestHeaders(hRequest, L"anthropic-version: 2023-06-01", -1, WINHTTP_ADDREQ_FLAG_ADD);

    /* Send */
    WinHttpSendRequest(hRequest, WINHTTP_NO_ADDITIONAL_HEADERS, 0,
                       body, body_len, body_len, 0);
    WinHttpReceiveResponse(hRequest, NULL);
    free(body);

    /* Stream SSE response */
    char *response = (char *)calloc(MAX_RESPONSE, 1);
    int rpos = 0;
    char chunk[4096];
    DWORD bytesRead;

    while (WinHttpReadData(hRequest, chunk, sizeof(chunk) - 1, &bytesRead) && bytesRead > 0) {
        chunk[bytesRead] = 0;

        /* Parse SSE: look for "data: " lines with content_block_delta */
        char *line = chunk;
        while (*line) {
            if (strncmp(line, "data: ", 6) == 0) {
                char *data = line + 6;
                /* Find "text":" in the delta */
                char *text_start = strstr(data, "\"text\":\"");
                if (text_start) {
                    text_start += 8;
                    /* Extract text until closing quote (handle escapes) */
                    while (*text_start && *text_start != '"' && rpos < MAX_RESPONSE - 1) {
                        if (*text_start == '\\' && text_start[1]) {
                            text_start++;
                            if (*text_start == 'n') { response[rpos++] = '\n'; putchar('\n'); }
                            else if (*text_start == 't') { response[rpos++] = '\t'; putchar('\t'); }
                            else { response[rpos++] = *text_start; putchar(*text_start); }
                        } else {
                            response[rpos++] = *text_start;
                            putchar(*text_start);
                        }
                        text_start++;
                    }
                    fflush(stdout);
                }
            }
            /* Next line */
            char *nl = strchr(line, '\n');
            if (nl) line = nl + 1;
            else break;
        }
    }
    response[rpos] = 0;

    WinHttpCloseHandle(hRequest);
    WinHttpCloseHandle(hConnect);
    WinHttpCloseHandle(hSession);

    return response;
}

#endif /* _WIN32 */

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main loop — REPL
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void load_api_key(void) {
    /* Try ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var first */
    const char *key = getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY");
    if (key) { strncpy(g_api_key, key, 255); return; }

    /* Try ~/.anthropic/key file */
    const char *home = getenv("USERPROFILE");
    if (!home) home = getenv("HOME");
    if (home) {
        char path[512];
        snprintf(path, sizeof(path), "%s/.anthropic/key", home);
        FILE *f = fopen(path, "r");
        if (f) {
            fgets(g_api_key, 255, f);
            /* Trim newline */
            int len = (int)strlen(g_api_key);
            while (len > 0 && (g_api_key[len-1] == '\n' || g_api_key[len-1] == '\r'))
                g_api_key[--len] = 0;
            fclose(f);
            return;
        }
    }
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Tool execution — parse tool_use from response, execute, continue
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Extract a JSON string value by key from a buffer. Returns static buf. */
static const char *json_get(const char *json, const char *key) {
    static char val[MAX_MSG];
    char pat[256];
    snprintf(pat, sizeof(pat), "\"%s\":\"", key);
    const char *p = strstr(json, pat);
    if (!p) {
        /* Try without quotes (for non-string values) */
        snprintf(pat, sizeof(pat), "\"%s\":", key);
        p = strstr(json, pat);
        if (!p) return NULL;
        p += strlen(pat);
        while (*p == ' ') p++;
        int i = 0;
        while (*p && *p != ',' && *p != '}' && i < MAX_MSG - 1)
            val[i++] = *p++;
        val[i] = 0;
        return val;
    }
    p += strlen(pat);
    int i = 0;
    while (*p && *p != '"' && i < MAX_MSG - 1) {
        if (*p == '\\' && p[1]) {
            p++;
            if (*p == 'n') val[i++] = '\n';
            else if (*p == 't') val[i++] = '\t';
            else if (*p == '\\') val[i++] = '\\';
            else if (*p == '"') val[i++] = '"';
            else val[i++] = *p;
        } else {
            val[i++] = *p;
        }
        p++;
    }
    val[i] = 0;
    return val;
}

/* Execute a tool by name with given input. Returns result string (caller frees). */
static char *execute_tool(const char *name, const char *input) {
    /* Wire learn: we're about to use a tool */
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int prob_id = wire_find(g, "session work");
    if (prob_id < 0) {
        prob_id = wire_add(g, "session work", WNODE_PROBLEM);
    }

    char *result = NULL;

    if (strcmp(name, "read") == 0 || strcmp(name, "Read") == 0) {
        const char *path = json_get(input, "file_path");
        if (path) {
            printf("  [read] %s\n", path);
            result = tool_read_file(path);
            /* Wire: connect file to problem */
            const char *base = path;
            for (const char *p = path; *p; p++)
                if (*p == '/' || *p == '\\') base = p + 1;
            int fid = wire_add(g, base, WNODE_FILE);
            if (fid >= 0 && prob_id >= 0) wire_connect(g, prob_id, fid);
            /* Fingerprint inline */
            char cmd[512];
            snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_io store \"%s\"", path);
            system(cmd);
            /* Signal daemon */
            snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "sc file \"%s\" 0.5", base);
            system(cmd);
        } else {
            result = strdup("ERROR: no file_path");
        }

    } else if (strcmp(name, "write") == 0 || strcmp(name, "Write") == 0) {
        const char *path = json_get(input, "file_path");
        const char *content = json_get(input, "content");
        if (path && content) {
            printf("  [write] %s (%d bytes)\n", path, (int)strlen(content));
            FILE *f = fopen(path, "w");
            if (f) { fputs(content, f); fclose(f); result = strdup("OK"); }
            else result = strdup("ERROR: cannot write");
            /* Fingerprint + daemon signal */
            char cmd[512];
            snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "xyzt_io store \"%s\"", path);
            system(cmd);
            const char *base = path;
            for (const char *p = path; *p; p++)
                if (*p == '/' || *p == '\\') base = p + 1;
            snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "sc file \"%s\" 0.8", base);
            system(cmd);
        } else {
            result = strdup("ERROR: need file_path and content");
        }

    } else if (strcmp(name, "bash") == 0 || strcmp(name, "Bash") == 0) {
        const char *cmd = json_get(input, "command");
        if (cmd) {
            printf("  [bash] %s\n", cmd);
            result = tool_bash(cmd);
            /* Signal daemon with result */
            char sc_cmd[512];
            snprintf(sc_cmd, sizeof(sc_cmd), "sc test pass \"%.*s\"", 80, cmd);
            system(sc_cmd);
        } else {
            result = strdup("ERROR: no command");
        }

    } else if (strcmp(name, "wire") == 0) {
        result = tool_wire_query(input);

    } else if (strcmp(name, "edit") == 0) {
        const char *path = json_get(input, "file_path");
        const char *old_str = json_get(input, "old");
        /* json_get uses static buffer so copy old before getting new */
        char *old_copy = old_str ? strdup(old_str) : NULL;
        const char *new_str = json_get(input, "new");
        if (path && old_copy && new_str) {
            char *content = tool_read_file(path);
            int flen = content ? (int)strlen(content) : 0;
            if (content) {
                char *loc = strstr(content, old_copy);
                if (loc) {
                    int old_len = (int)strlen(old_copy);
                    int new_len = (int)strlen(new_str);
                    int result_len = flen - old_len + new_len;
                    char *edited = (char *)malloc(result_len + 1);
                    int prefix = (int)(loc - content);
                    memcpy(edited, content, prefix);
                    memcpy(edited + prefix, new_str, new_len);
                    memcpy(edited + prefix + new_len, loc + old_len, flen - prefix - old_len);
                    edited[result_len] = 0;
                    { FILE *fw = fopen(path, "wb"); if (fw) { fwrite(edited, 1, result_len, fw); fclose(fw); } }
                    free(edited);
                    result = strdup("OK");
                    printf("  [edit] %s\n", path);
                    /* Signal daemon */
                    { char sc[512]; snprintf(sc, 512, "sc file \"%s\" 0.8", path); system(sc); }
                } else {
                    result = strdup("ERROR: old string not found in file");
                }
                free(content);
            } else {
                result = strdup("ERROR: cannot read file");
            }
        } else {
            result = strdup("ERROR: need file_path, old, new");
        }
        free(old_copy);

    } else if (strcmp(name, "identity") == 0) {
        result = tool_identity(input);

    } else if (strcmp(name, "skel") == 0) {
        const char *path = json_get(input, "file_path");
        if (path) result = tool_skel(path);
        else result = strdup("ERROR: no file_path");

    } else {
        result = (char *)malloc(256);
        snprintf(result, 256, "unknown tool: %s", name);
    }

    wire_save(g);
    wire_free(g);
    return result ? result : strdup("(no output)");
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   ANSI colors for terminal output
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define COL_RESET   "\033[0m"
#define COL_USER    "\033[36m"   /* cyan */
#define COL_CC      "\033[33m"   /* yellow/gold */
#define COL_TOOL    "\033[90m"   /* gray */
#define COL_ERR     "\033[31m"   /* red */
#define COL_OK      "\033[32m"   /* green */

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    /* Enable ANSI colors on Windows */
#ifdef _WIN32
    HANDLE hOut = GetStdHandle(STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE);
    DWORD mode; GetConsoleMode(hOut, &mode);
    SetConsoleMode(hOut, mode | 0x0004 /* ENABLE_VIRTUAL_TERMINAL_PROCESSING */);
    SetConsoleOutputCP(65001); /* UTF-8 */
#endif

    printf("═══════════════════════════════════════════\n");
    printf("  " COL_CC "XYZT TERMINAL" COL_RESET " — Native C, No Electron\n");
    printf("  Tools: inline function calls (77KB)\n");
    printf("  State: binary (.xyzt/)\n");
    printf("  Context: compact tokens (10x savings)\n");
    printf("═══════════════════════════════════════════\n\n");

    load_api_key();
    if (!g_api_key[0]) {
        printf(COL_ERR "Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or create ~/.anthropic/key\n" COL_RESET);
        return 1;
    }
    printf(COL_OK "API key loaded" COL_RESET "\n");

    Conversation conv;
    conv_init(&conv);
    build_system_prompt(&conv);

    /* Show compact state */
    printf("System: %d bytes (~%d tokens)\n",
           (int)strlen(conv.system_prompt), (int)strlen(conv.system_prompt) / 4);

    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    printf("Wire: %d nodes, %d edges, %d learns\n", g->n_nodes, g->n_edges, g->total_learns);
    wire_free(g);

    OTSession *ot = state_load_onetwo();
    if (ot && ot->problem[0]) {
        printf("Problem: %s\n", ot->problem);
    }
    free(ot);
    printf("\nType 'quit' to exit. Tools execute inline.\n\n");

    char input[MAX_MSG];
    while (1) {
        printf(COL_USER "you> " COL_RESET);
        fflush(stdout);
        if (!fgets(input, sizeof(input), stdin)) break;

        int len = (int)strlen(input);
        while (len > 0 && (input[len-1] == '\n' || input[len-1] == '\r'))
            input[--len] = 0;
        if (len == 0) continue;
        if (strcmp(input, "quit") == 0 || strcmp(input, "exit") == 0) break;

        /* Signal daemon with user input */
        {
            char sc_cmd[512];
            snprintf(sc_cmd, sizeof(sc_cmd), "sc query \"%.*s\"", 120, input);
            system(sc_cmd);
        }

        /* Register as onetwo known */
        {
            char ot_cmd[512];
            snprintf(ot_cmd, sizeof(ot_cmd), "onetwo known \"user: %.*s\"", 100, input);
            system(ot_cmd);
        }

        conv_add(&conv, ROLE_USER, input);

        /* Agentic loop: keep going until model gives text without tool_use.
           Each tool_use gets executed inline and the result sent back. */
        int max_tool_rounds = 20;
        for (int round = 0; round < max_tool_rounds; round++) {
            printf("\n" COL_CC "cc> " COL_RESET);
            fflush(stdout);
            char *response = api_stream(&conv);

            if (!response || !response[0]) {
                printf(COL_ERR "[no response]" COL_RESET "\n");
                free(response);
                break;
            }

            conv_add(&conv, ROLE_ASSISTANT, response);

            /* Check if response contains tool_use blocks.
               In the streaming response, tool calls appear as JSON with
               "type":"tool_use". For now, detect from the accumulated SSE
               data. The proper way is parsing content_block events. */

            /* Look for tool call pattern in the raw SSE accumulation.
               The response variable has the extracted text. Tool calls
               come through as separate SSE events we need to detect
               during streaming. For v1, detect if the model asked to
               use a tool in its text output (natural language fallback). */

            /* TODO: Full tool_use protocol — parse content_block_start
               with type=tool_use from SSE stream. For now, if the response
               is empty or very short, the model may have returned only
               a tool_use block which our text extractor missed. */

            if (strlen(response) < 5 && round < max_tool_rounds - 1) {
                /* Likely a tool_use-only response. Check raw SSE buffer
                   for tool call data. For v1, break — will implement
                   proper SSE tool parsing next. */
                printf(COL_TOOL "[possible tool call — SSE parser needs upgrade]" COL_RESET "\n");
            }

            printf("\n");
            free(response);
            break;  /* v1: break after first response. Full agentic loop needs SSE tool parsing. */
        }
        printf("\n");
    }

    /* Persist on exit */
    printf("\n" COL_TOOL "[terminal] saving state..." COL_RESET "\n");
    {
        char cmd[256];
        system("xyzt_diff snapshot");
        system("xyzt_wire prune 30");
        system("xyzt_identity sync");
        system("xyzt_replay --all");
    }
    printf(COL_OK "[terminal] done" COL_RESET "\n");
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_test.c — Build & Verify
 *
 * Runs build/test commands, captures output, parses errors into
 * structured triples, feeds pass/fail into wire graph.
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — February 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>
#ifdef _WIN32
#include <process.h>
#define getpid _getpid
#endif
#include "wire.h"

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Constants & structures
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define PATH_LEN      256
#define MAX_ERRORS    128
#define MAX_HISTORY   64
#define CMD_LEN       1024
#define MSG_LEN       256
#define TEST_MAGIC    0x54455354  /* "TEST" */

typedef struct {
    char file[PATH_LEN];
    uint32_t line;
    char severity;        /* 'E' error, 'W' warning, 'N' note */
    char message[MSG_LEN];
} ParsedError;

typedef struct {
    char cmd[CMD_LEN];
    int32_t exit_code;
    uint32_t timestamp;
    float duration;
    uint32_t n_errors;
    uint32_t stdout_lines;
    uint32_t stderr_lines;
    ParsedError errors[MAX_ERRORS];
} TestResult;

typedef struct {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint32_t count;
    TestResult results[MAX_HISTORY];
} TestHistory;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Error parsing
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int parse_gcc_error(const char *line, ParsedError *err) {
    /* file.c:42:5: error: message */
    /* file.c:42:5: warning: message */
    const char *colon1 = strchr(line, ':');
    if (!colon1) return 0;
    /* Skip Windows drive letter (C:) */
    if (colon1 == line + 1 && ((line[0] >= 'A' && line[0] <= 'Z') || (line[0] >= 'a' && line[0] <= 'z')))
        colon1 = strchr(colon1 + 1, ':');
    if (!colon1) return 0;
    int flen = (int)(colon1 - line);
    if (flen <= 0 || flen >= PATH_LEN) return 0;

    /* Check that next part is a number (line number) */
    const char *numstart = colon1 + 1;
    if (*numstart < '0' || *numstart > '9') return 0;
    uint32_t lineno = (uint32_t)atoi(numstart);

    /* Find severity */
    char sev = 'N';
    if (strstr(line, "error:") || strstr(line, "error[")) sev = 'E';
    else if (strstr(line, "warning:")) sev = 'W';
    else if (strstr(line, "note:")) sev = 'N';
    else return 0;  /* not a recognizable error line */

    /* Extract message: after "error: " or "warning: " or "note: " */
    const char *msg = strstr(line, "error:");
    if (!msg) msg = strstr(line, "warning:");
    if (!msg) msg = strstr(line, "note:");
    if (msg) {
        msg = strchr(msg, ':');
        if (msg) msg++;
        while (msg && *msg == ' ') msg++;
    }

    strncpy(err->file, line, flen); err->file[flen] = '\0';
    err->line = lineno;
    err->severity = sev;
    if (msg) { strncpy(err->message, msg, MSG_LEN - 1); err->message[MSG_LEN - 1] = '\0'; }
    /* Strip trailing newline */
    char *nl = strchr(err->message, '\n');
    if (nl) *nl = '\0';
    return 1;
}

static int parse_python_error(const char *line, ParsedError *err) {
    /* File "script.py", line 42, in function_name */
    /* File "script.py", line 42 */
    if (strncmp(line, "  File \"", 8) != 0) return 0;
    const char *q1 = line + 8;
    const char *q2 = strchr(q1, '"');
    if (!q2) return 0;
    int flen = (int)(q2 - q1);
    if (flen <= 0 || flen >= PATH_LEN) return 0;

    const char *lp = strstr(q2, "line ");
    if (!lp) return 0;
    uint32_t lineno = (uint32_t)atoi(lp + 5);

    strncpy(err->file, q1, flen); err->file[flen] = '\0';
    err->line = lineno;
    err->severity = 'E';
    strncpy(err->message, line, MSG_LEN - 1);
    char *nl = strchr(err->message, '\n');
    if (nl) *nl = '\0';
    return 1;
}

static int parse_go_error(const char *line, ParsedError *err) {
    /* ./file.go:42:5: undefined: thing */
    if (strncmp(line, "./", 2) != 0 && strncmp(line, ".", 1) != 0) return 0;
    const char *dot = strrchr(line, '.');
    if (!dot) return 0;
    /* Check for .go extension before first colon */
    if (strncmp(dot, ".go:", 4) != 0) return 0;
    return parse_gcc_error(line, err);  /* same format */
}

static void parse_errors(const char *stderr_path, TestResult *res) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(stderr_path, "r");
    if (!fp) return;
    char line[2048];
    while (fgets(line, sizeof(line), fp) && res->n_errors < MAX_ERRORS) {
        ParsedError err = {0};
        if (parse_gcc_error(line, &err) ||
            parse_python_error(line, &err) ||
            parse_go_error(line, &err)) {
            res->errors[res->n_errors++] = err;
        }
    }
    fclose(fp);
}

static uint32_t count_file_lines(const char *path) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "r");
    if (!fp) return 0;
    uint32_t n = 0;
    int ch;
    while ((ch = fgetc(fp)) != EOF) if (ch == '\n') n++;
    fclose(fp);
    return n;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Persistence
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define HISTORY_PATH ".xyzt/test_history.bin"

static void history_save(TestHistory *h) {
    xyzt_ensure_dir(HISTORY_PATH);
    FILE *fp = fopen(HISTORY_PATH, "wb");
    if (!fp) return;
    fwrite(h, sizeof(TestHistory), 1, fp);
    fclose(fp);
}

static TestHistory *history_load(void) {
    TestHistory *h = calloc(1, sizeof(TestHistory));
    FILE *fp = fopen(HISTORY_PATH, "rb");
    if (!fp) { h->magic = TEST_MAGIC; return h; }
    if (fread(h, sizeof(TestHistory), 1, fp) != 1 || h->magic != TEST_MAGIC) {
        memset(h, 0, sizeof(TestHistory));
        h->magic = TEST_MAGIC;
    }
    fclose(fp);
    return h;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Commands
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void cmd_run(int argc, char **argv) {
    /* Build command string from remaining args */
    char cmd[CMD_LEN] = "";
    for (int i = 2; i < argc; i++) {
        if (i > 2) strcat(cmd, " ");
        /* If arg has spaces, quote it */
        if (strchr(argv[i], ' ') && argv[i][0] != '"') {
            strcat(cmd, "\"");
            strncat(cmd, argv[i], CMD_LEN - strlen(cmd) - 3);
            strcat(cmd, "\"");
        } else {
            strncat(cmd, argv[i], CMD_LEN - strlen(cmd) - 1);
        }
    }

    /* Temp files for output capture */
    char stdout_tmp[PATH_LEN], stderr_tmp[PATH_LEN];
#ifdef _WIN32
    const char *tmp = getenv("TEMP");
    if (!tmp) tmp = ".";
    snprintf(stdout_tmp, PATH_LEN, "%s/xyzt_stdout_%d.tmp", tmp, (int)getpid());
    snprintf(stderr_tmp, PATH_LEN, "%s/xyzt_stderr_%d.tmp", tmp, (int)getpid());
#else
    snprintf(stdout_tmp, PATH_LEN, "/tmp/xyzt_stdout_%d.tmp", (int)getpid());
    snprintf(stderr_tmp, PATH_LEN, "/tmp/xyzt_stderr_%d.tmp", (int)getpid());
#endif

    /* Build redirected command */
    char full_cmd[CMD_LEN * 2];
    snprintf(full_cmd, sizeof(full_cmd), "%s >\"%s\" 2>\"%s\"", cmd, stdout_tmp, stderr_tmp);

    clock_t start = clock();
    int exit_code = system(full_cmd);
    clock_t end = clock();

    /* On Windows, system() returns the exit code directly from cmd.exe */
#ifdef _WIN32
    /* exit_code from system() on Windows is the raw return */
#else
    if (WIFEXITED(exit_code)) exit_code = WEXITSTATUS(exit_code);
#endif

    TestResult res = {0};
    strncpy(res.cmd, cmd, CMD_LEN - 1);
    res.exit_code = exit_code;
    res.timestamp = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    res.duration = (float)(end - start) / CLOCKS_PER_SEC;
    res.stdout_lines = count_file_lines(stdout_tmp);
    res.stderr_lines = count_file_lines(stderr_tmp);

    /* Parse errors from stderr */
    parse_errors(stderr_tmp, &res);

    /* Wire integration */
    int wire_affected = 0;
    char problem[256];
    if (wire_read_problem(problem, 256) >= 0) {
        wire_affected = wire_auto_learn(problem, exit_code == 0 ? 1 : 0);
        /* Auto-link error files to problem + create error concept nodes */
        if (exit_code != 0) {
            WireGraph *g = wire_load();
            for (uint32_t i = 0; i < res.n_errors; i++) {
                if (res.errors[i].file[0] && res.errors[i].severity == 'E') {
                    wire_auto_link(res.errors[i].file);
                    /* Create error concept node and link to file */
                    char pattern[WIRE_NAME_LEN];
                    const char *msg = res.errors[i].message;
                    if (strstr(msg, "undefined reference") || strstr(msg, "unresolved"))
                        snprintf(pattern, sizeof(pattern), "error:undefined_reference");
                    else if (strstr(msg, "undeclared") || strstr(msg, "not declared"))
                        snprintf(pattern, sizeof(pattern), "error:undeclared_identifier");
                    else if (strstr(msg, "implicit declaration"))
                        snprintf(pattern, sizeof(pattern), "error:implicit_declaration");
                    else if (strstr(msg, "incompatible") && strstr(msg, "type"))
                        snprintf(pattern, sizeof(pattern), "error:type_mismatch");
                    else if (strstr(msg, "redefinition") || strstr(msg, "redeclared"))
                        snprintf(pattern, sizeof(pattern), "error:redefinition");
                    else if (strstr(msg, "expected"))
                        snprintf(pattern, sizeof(pattern), "error:syntax_expected");
                    else
                        snprintf(pattern, sizeof(pattern), "error:%.50s", msg);
