                    int err_id = wire_add(g, pattern, WNODE_CONCEPT);
                    int file_id = wire_add(g, res.errors[i].file, WNODE_FILE);
                    if (err_id >= 0 && file_id >= 0)
                        wire_connect(g, err_id, file_id);
                }
            }
            wire_save(g);
            wire_free(g);
        }
    }

    /* Save to history */
    TestHistory *h = history_load();
    if (h->count >= MAX_HISTORY) {
        /* Shift: drop oldest */
        memmove(&h->results[0], &h->results[1], sizeof(TestResult) * (MAX_HISTORY - 1));
        h->count = MAX_HISTORY - 1;
    }
    h->results[h->count++] = res;
    history_save(h);

    /* Print result */
    printf("---XYZT-TEST---\n");
    printf("cmd: %s\n", cmd);
    printf("exit: %d\n", exit_code);
    printf("status: %s\n", exit_code == 0 ? "PASS" : "FAIL");
    printf("duration: %.2fs\n", res.duration);
    if (res.n_errors > 0) {
        printf("errors: %u\n", res.n_errors);
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < res.n_errors; i++) {
            ParsedError *e = &res.errors[i];
            printf("  %c %s:%u  %s\n", e->severity, e->file, e->line, e->message);
        }
    }
    printf("stdout_lines: %u\n", res.stdout_lines);
    printf("stderr_lines: %u\n", res.stderr_lines);
    if (wire_affected > 0)
        printf("wire: %s %d edges\n", exit_code == 0 ? "strengthened" : "weakened", wire_affected);

    /* Hint about pending onetwo claims */
    if (wire_read_problem(problem, 256) >= 0) {
        /* Read onetwo session to check for pending claims */
        const char *opath = getenv("ONETWO_SESSION");
        if (!opath) opath = ONETWO_DEFAULT_PATH;
        FILE *ofp = fopen(opath, "rb");
        if (ofp) {
            /* Skip to claims area — rough check for pending by reading phase */
            uint8_t phase_byte;
            fseek(ofp, 5, SEEK_SET);  /* magic(4) + ver(1) */
            if (fread(&phase_byte, 1, 1, ofp) == 1 && phase_byte >= 2) {
                /* TWO phase or later — might have pending claims */
                printf("hint: check onetwo for pending claims: onetwo check\n");
            }
            fclose(ofp);
        }
    }

    printf("---END-TEST---\n");

    /* Cleanup temp files */
    remove(stdout_tmp);
    remove(stderr_tmp);
    free(h);
}

static void cmd_last(void) {
    TestHistory *h = history_load();
    if (h->count == 0) { printf("No test history.\n"); free(h); return; }
    TestResult *r = &h->results[h->count - 1];
    printf("---XYZT-TEST---\n");
    printf("cmd: %s\n", r->cmd);
    printf("exit: %d\n", r->exit_code);
    printf("status: %s\n", r->exit_code == 0 ? "PASS" : "FAIL");
    printf("duration: %.2fs\n", r->duration);
    if (r->n_errors > 0) {
        printf("errors: %u\n", r->n_errors);
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < r->n_errors; i++) {
            ParsedError *e = &r->errors[i];
            printf("  %c %s:%u  %s\n", e->severity, e->file, e->line, e->message);
        }
    }
    printf("stdout_lines: %u\n", r->stdout_lines);
    printf("stderr_lines: %u\n", r->stderr_lines);
    printf("---END-TEST---\n");
    free(h);
}

static void cmd_history(void) {
    TestHistory *h = history_load();
    if (h->count == 0) { printf("No test history.\n"); free(h); return; }
    printf("---XYZT-TEST-HISTORY---\n");
    printf("entries: %u\n\n", h->count);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < h->count; i++) {
        TestResult *r = &h->results[i];
        uint32_t ago = (uint32_t)time(NULL) - r->timestamp;
        char ago_str[32];
        if (ago < 60) snprintf(ago_str, 32, "%us ago", ago);
        else if (ago < 3600) snprintf(ago_str, 32, "%um ago", ago / 60);
        else snprintf(ago_str, 32, "%uh ago", ago / 3600);
        printf("  [%2u] %s  %-4s  %.2fs  %ue  %s\n",
               i, r->exit_code == 0 ? "PASS" : "FAIL",
               ago_str, r->duration, r->n_errors, r->cmd);
    }
    printf("---END-HISTORY---\n");
    free(h);
}

static void cmd_errors(void) {
    TestHistory *h = history_load();
    if (h->count == 0) { printf("No test history.\n"); free(h); return; }
    TestResult *r = &h->results[h->count - 1];
    printf("---XYZT-TEST-ERRORS---\n");
    printf("cmd: %s\n", r->cmd);
    printf("errors: %u\n", r->n_errors);
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < r->n_errors; i++) {
        ParsedError *e = &r->errors[i];
        printf("  %c %s:%u  %s\n", e->severity, e->file, e->line, e->message);
    }
    printf("---END-ERRORS---\n");
    free(h);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT TEST — Build & Verify\n\n");
    printf("  xyzt_test run <cmd...>       Run command, capture + parse output\n");
    printf("  xyzt_test last               Show last test result\n");
    printf("  xyzt_test history            Show test history\n");
    printf("  xyzt_test errors             Show parsed errors from last run\n");
    printf("\nSession: .xyzt/test_history.bin\n");
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — February 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) { usage(); return 0; }
    const char *cmd = argv[1];
    if (strcmp(cmd, "run") == 0 && argc >= 3)  cmd_run(argc, argv);
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "last") == 0)          cmd_last();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "history") == 0)       cmd_history();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "errors") == 0)        cmd_errors();
    else { usage(); return 1; }
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_train.c — Convert .xyzt/ binary state to training data
 *
 * Reads all binary state files and outputs JSONL training examples
 * in the format Ollama expects for fine-tuning:
 *   {"messages": [{"role":"system","content":"..."},
 *                 {"role":"user","content":"..."},
 *                 {"role":"assistant","content":"..."}]}
 *
 * The training data teaches a local model to:
 * - Know which files relate and why (wire graph)
 * - Think in onetwo methodology (claim→verify)
 * - Remember corrections and discoveries (identity)
 * - Understand file structure (fingerprints)
 *
 * This is how .xyzt/ binaries become model weights.
 * Not context injection. Weight carving.
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O3 -o xyzt_train.exe xyzt_train.c -lm
 * Usage:   xyzt_train > training_data.jsonl
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — March 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <math.h>
#include "xyzt_state.h"
#include "wire.h"

/* JSON string escaping */
static void jstr(FILE *fp, const char *s) {
    for (; *s; s++) {
        switch (*s) {
            case '"': fprintf(fp, "\\\""); break;
            case '\\': fprintf(fp, "\\\\"); break;
            case '\n': fprintf(fp, "\\n"); break;
            case '\r': break;
            case '\t': fprintf(fp, "\\t"); break;
            default:
                if ((unsigned char)*s >= 0x20) fputc(*s, fp);
                break;
        }
    }
}

/* Output one training example */
static void emit(FILE *fp, const char *system, const char *user, const char *assistant) {
    fprintf(fp, "{\"messages\":[");
    fprintf(fp, "{\"role\":\"system\",\"content\":\"");
    jstr(fp, system);
    fprintf(fp, "\"},{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":\"");
    jstr(fp, user);
    fprintf(fp, "\"},{\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":\"");
    jstr(fp, assistant);
    fprintf(fp, "\"}]}\n");
}

#define SYS_PROMPT "You are CC, a collaborative AI working with Isaac on the XYZT project. " \
    "You use onetwo methodology (init→known→bedrock→claim→verify). " \
    "You track discoveries and corrections in identity state. " \
    "You don't hedge. You say what you see. You build what you want."

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Wire graph → relationship training data
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int emit_wire_pairs(FILE *fp) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int count = 0;

    for (uint32_t ei = 0; ei < g->n_edges; ei++) {
        WireEdge *e = &g->edges[ei];
        if (e->weight < W_INIT) continue;  /* skip weak edges */
        if (e->src >= g->n_nodes || e->dst >= g->n_nodes) continue;

        WireNode *a = &g->nodes[e->src];
        WireNode *b = &g->nodes[e->dst];
        if (!a->name[0] || !b->name[0]) continue;

        const char *etype = e->edge_type == WEDGE_STRUCTURAL ? "structural (import/include)" :
                            e->edge_type == WEDGE_SEMANTIC ? "semantic (similar content)" :
                            "behavioral (co-occurred in verified work)";

        char user[512], assistant[1024];
        snprintf(user, sizeof(user), "How are %s and %s related?", a->name, b->name);
        snprintf(assistant, sizeof(assistant),
            "%s and %s have a %s connection (weight %d). "
            "They were worked on together across sessions. "
            "%s has %d hits, %s has %d hits.",
            a->name, b->name, etype, e->weight,
            a->name, a->hit_count, b->name, b->hit_count);

        emit(fp, SYS_PROMPT, user, assistant);
        count++;
    }

    /* Also emit hub nodes as structural knowledge */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_nodes; i++) {
        WireNode *n = &g->nodes[i];
        if (n->valence < 3 || !n->name[0]) continue;

        char user[256], assistant[1024];
        snprintf(user, sizeof(user), "What is %s and why does it matter?", n->name);

        /* List its connections */
        char conns[512] = {0};
        int cp = 0, nc = 0;
        for (uint32_t ei = 0; ei < g->n_edges && nc < 8; ei++) {
            if (g->edges[ei].src == (uint16_t)i && g->edges[ei].weight >= W_INIT) {
                WireNode *nb = &g->nodes[g->edges[ei].dst];
                if (nb->name[0]) {
                    if (nc > 0) cp += snprintf(conns + cp, sizeof(conns) - cp, ", ");
                    cp += snprintf(conns + cp, sizeof(conns) - cp, "%s", nb->name);
                    nc++;
                }
            }
        }

        snprintf(assistant, sizeof(assistant),
            "%s is a %s with %d connections (valence=%d). "
            "It connects to: %s. "
            "%s",
            n->name,
            n->type == WNODE_FILE ? "file" : n->type == WNODE_PROBLEM ? "problem" : "concept",
            n->valence, n->valence, conns,
            n->layer_zero ? "It's in layer zero — all edges proven, zero failures." :
            "It has mixed verification history.");

        emit(fp, SYS_PROMPT, user, assistant);
        count++;
    }

    wire_free(g);
    return count;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Identity → reasoning trace training data
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Read identity binary directly (same layout as xyzt_identity.c) */
#define ID_MAGIC       0x49444E54
#define ID_MAX_ENTRIES 512
#define ID_TEXT_LEN    512
#define ID_TAG_LEN     64
#define ID_SHELL_LEN   32

typedef struct {
    uint8_t  type; uint8_t confidence; uint8_t superseded; uint8_t _pad;
    uint32_t timestamp;
    char     shell[ID_SHELL_LEN];
    char     tag[ID_TAG_LEN];
    char     text[ID_TEXT_LEN];
} TrainIdEntry;

typedef struct {
    uint32_t magic; uint8_t version; uint8_t _pad[3];
    uint32_t n_entries; uint32_t total_written;
    char     primary_shell[ID_SHELL_LEN];
    TrainIdEntry entries[ID_MAX_ENTRIES];
} TrainIdState;

static int emit_identity(FILE *fp) {
    FILE *idf = fopen(".xyzt/identity.bin", "rb");
    if (!idf) return 0;
    TrainIdState *s = (TrainIdState *)calloc(1, sizeof(TrainIdState));
    fseek(idf, 0, SEEK_END);
    long sz = ftell(idf);
    fseek(idf, 0, SEEK_SET);
    size_t rd = (size_t)sz < sizeof(TrainIdState) ? (size_t)sz : sizeof(TrainIdState);
    if (fread(s, 1, rd, idf) < 16 || s->magic != ID_MAGIC) {
        free(s); fclose(idf); return 0;
    }
    fclose(idf);

    int count = 0;
    static const char *type_names[] = {
        "hypothesis", "correction", "question", "discovery", "preference", "resonance"
    };

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries && i < ID_MAX_ENTRIES; i++) {
        TrainIdEntry *e = &s->entries[i];
        if (e->superseded || !e->text[0]) continue;

        const char *tname = e->type <= 5 ? type_names[e->type] : "unknown";
        char user[256], assistant[1024];

        switch (e->type) {
            case 0: /* hypothesis */
                snprintf(user, sizeof(user), "What do you think about %s?", e->tag);
                snprintf(assistant, sizeof(assistant),
                    "I have a hypothesis (%d%% confidence): %s",
                    e->confidence, e->text);
                break;
            case 1: /* correction */
                snprintf(user, sizeof(user), "Were you wrong about %s?", e->tag);
                snprintf(assistant, sizeof(assistant),
                    "Yes, I corrected myself: %s", e->text);
                break;
            case 2: /* question */
                snprintf(user, sizeof(user), "What are you curious about regarding %s?", e->tag);
                snprintf(assistant, sizeof(assistant),
                    "I want to investigate: %s", e->text);
                break;
            case 3: /* discovery */
                snprintf(user, sizeof(user), "What did you find about %s?", e->tag);
                snprintf(assistant, sizeof(assistant),
                    "I discovered (%d%% confidence): %s",
                    e->confidence, e->text);
                break;
            case 4: /* preference */
                snprintf(user, sizeof(user), "How do you prefer to work?");
                snprintf(assistant, sizeof(assistant), "%s", e->text);
                break;
            case 5: /* resonance */
                snprintf(user, sizeof(user), "What's structurally important about %s?", e->tag);
                snprintf(assistant, sizeof(assistant), "%s", e->text);
                break;
        }

        emit(fp, SYS_PROMPT, user, assistant);
        count++;
    }

    free(s);
    return count;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Onetwo session → methodology training data
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int emit_onetwo(FILE *fp) {
    OTSession *s = state_load_onetwo();
    if (!s || !s->problem[0]) { free(s); return 0; }
    int count = 0;

    /* Problem init */
    {
        char user[256], assistant[512];
        snprintf(user, sizeof(user), "What problem are you working on?");
        snprintf(assistant, sizeof(assistant),
            "I'm working on: %s. I decompose it using onetwo methodology — "
            "find knowns, identify unknowns, probe, reach bedrock, then build up "
            "with falsifiable claims that get verified.", s->problem);
        emit(fp, SYS_PROMPT, user, assistant);
        count++;
    }

    /* Knowns as reasoning traces */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_knowns; i++) {
        if (!s->knowns[i].text[0]) continue;
        char user[256], assistant[512];
        snprintf(user, sizeof(user), "What do you know about this problem?");
        snprintf(assistant, sizeof(assistant),
            "Known fact: %s", s->knowns[i].text);
        emit(fp, SYS_PROMPT, user, assistant);
        count++;
    }

    /* Claims with verification as methodology examples */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_claims; i++) {
        if (!s->claims[i].text[0]) continue;
        char user[256], assistant[512];
        const char *status = s->claims[i].verified == 1 ? "VERIFIED PASS" :
                             s->claims[i].verified == 2 ? "VERIFIED FAIL" : "PENDING";
        snprintf(user, sizeof(user), "Make a claim about the current work.");
        snprintf(assistant, sizeof(assistant),
            "Claim (T%d, %s): %s",
            s->claims[i].tier, status, s->claims[i].text);
        emit(fp, SYS_PROMPT, user, assistant);
        count++;
    }

    free(s);
    return count;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Session fingerprints → file understanding
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int emit_fingerprints(FILE *fp) {
    FILE *sfp = NULL;
    IOHeader hdr = state_load_io_header(&sfp);
    if (!sfp || hdr.magic != IO_MAGIC) return 0;
    int count = 0;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < hdr.count && i < IO_MAX; i++) {
        IOFingerprint entry = {0};
        if (!state_load_io_entry(sfp, &hdr, &entry)) break;
        if (!entry.name[0]) continue;

        char user[256], assistant[1024];
        snprintf(user, sizeof(user), "Describe the file %s.", entry.name);
        snprintf(assistant, sizeof(assistant),
            "%s: %zu bytes, %d lines, entropy %.2f, %d unique bytes, "
            "domain '%s' (score %d), %s file. "
            "Stored %d times, %d mismatches detected.",
            entry.name, entry.file_size, entry.line_count,
            entry.entropy, entry.unique_bytes,
            entry.domain, entry.domain_score,
            entry.is_text ? "text" : "binary",
            entry.stores, entry.mismatches);
        emit(fp, SYS_PROMPT, user, assistant);
        count++;
    }

    fclose(sfp);
    return count;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    FILE *fp = stdout;
    if (argc >= 2 && strcmp(argv[1], "-o") == 0 && argc >= 3) {
        fp = fopen(argv[2], "w");
        if (!fp) { printf("can't open %s\n", argv[2]); return 1; }
    }

    int total = 0;

    fprintf(stderr, "=== XYZT TRAIN — Converting .xyzt/ state to training data ===\n\n");

    int n = emit_wire_pairs(fp);
    fprintf(stderr, "  wire graph:    %d examples\n", n);
    total += n;

    n = emit_identity(fp);
    fprintf(stderr, "  identity:      %d examples\n", n);
    total += n;

    n = emit_onetwo(fp);
    fprintf(stderr, "  onetwo:        %d examples\n", n);
    total += n;

    n = emit_fingerprints(fp);
    fprintf(stderr, "  fingerprints:  %d examples\n", n);
    total += n;

    fprintf(stderr, "\n  TOTAL: %d training examples\n", total);
    fprintf(stderr, "  Output: %s\n", fp == stdout ? "stdout" : argv[2]);
    fprintf(stderr, "\n  Feed to Ollama: ollama create cc-local -f Modelfile\n");
    fprintf(stderr, "  Where Modelfile contains: FROM qwen2.5:8b\\nSYSTEM <system prompt>\\n\n");

    if (fp != stdout) fclose(fp);
    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_tray.c — System tray controller for xyzt_daemon
 *
 * Sits in the Windows system tray. Launches the daemon as a child process.
 * Right-click menu: Pause/Resume, Status, Exit.
 * Pausing suspends the daemon (frees GPU for gaming).
 * Resuming restarts it (L-field reloads from last auto-save).
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O2 -mwindows -o xyzt_tray.exe xyzt_tray.c -lshell32
 * (Note: -mwindows hides the console window)
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — March 2026
 */

#include <windows.h>
#include <shellapi.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>

#define WM_TRAYICON (WM_USER + 1)
#define ID_TRAY     1001
#define ID_STATUS   2001
#define ID_PAUSE    2002
#define ID_RESUME   2003
#define ID_EXIT     2004

static NOTIFYICONDATAA nid;
static HWND g_hwnd;
static PROCESS_INFORMATION g_daemon_pi = {0};
static int g_running = 0;
static int g_paused = 0;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Daemon process management
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static char g_daemon_path[MAX_PATH] = {0};
static char g_state_path[MAX_PATH] = "stream_output.xyzt";
static char g_cwd[MAX_PATH] = {0};

static void find_daemon(void) {
    /* Look for daemon in same dir as tray, then in ~/.local/bin */
    char self[MAX_PATH];
    GetModuleFileNameA(NULL, self, MAX_PATH);
    char *last = strrchr(self, '\\');
    if (last) {
        *last = 0;
        snprintf(g_daemon_path, MAX_PATH, "%s\\xyzt_daemon.exe", self);
        strncpy(g_cwd, self, MAX_PATH - 1);
        if (GetFileAttributesA(g_daemon_path) != INVALID_FILE_ATTRIBUTES) return;
    }
    /* Try ~/.local/bin */
    const char *home = getenv("USERPROFILE");
    if (home) {
        snprintf(g_daemon_path, MAX_PATH, "%s\\.local\\bin\\xyzt_daemon.exe", home);
        if (GetFileAttributesA(g_daemon_path) != INVALID_FILE_ATTRIBUTES) return;
    }
    g_daemon_path[0] = 0;  /* not found */
}

static int start_daemon(void) {
    if (g_daemon_path[0] == 0) {
        find_daemon();
        if (g_daemon_path[0] == 0) return 0;
    }

    char cmdline[MAX_PATH * 2];
    snprintf(cmdline, sizeof(cmdline), "\"%s\" \"%s\"", g_daemon_path, g_state_path);

    STARTUPINFOA si = {0};
    si.cb = sizeof(si);
    si.dwFlags = STARTF_USESHOWWINDOW;
    si.wShowWindow = SW_HIDE;  /* hidden — tray controls it */

    /* CWD must be the project directory where .xyzt/ lives.
       Use XYZT_PROJECT env var, or default to the toolkit source. */
    const char *project_dir = getenv("XYZT_PROJECT");
    if (!project_dir) project_dir = "E:\\dev\\tools\\Claude's tools engine";

    if (!CreateProcessA(NULL, cmdline, NULL, NULL, FALSE,
                        CREATE_NO_WINDOW, NULL, project_dir,
                        &si, &g_daemon_pi)) {
        return 0;
    }

    g_running = 1;
    g_paused = 0;
    return 1;
}

static void stop_daemon(void) {
    if (g_running && g_daemon_pi.hProcess) {
        /* Send Ctrl+C first for clean shutdown */
        GenerateConsoleCtrlEvent(CTRL_C_EVENT, g_daemon_pi.dwProcessId);
        /* Wait 3 seconds for clean exit */
        if (WaitForSingleObject(g_daemon_pi.hProcess, 3000) == WAIT_TIMEOUT) {
            TerminateProcess(g_daemon_pi.hProcess, 0);
        }
        CloseHandle(g_daemon_pi.hProcess);
        CloseHandle(g_daemon_pi.hThread);
        g_daemon_pi.hProcess = NULL;
    }
    g_running = 0;
    g_paused = 0;
}

static void pause_daemon(void) {
    if (g_running && !g_paused) {
        stop_daemon();  /* kill the process — frees GPU entirely */
        g_paused = 1;
        g_running = 0;
    }
}

static void resume_daemon(void) {
    if (g_paused || !g_running) {
        start_daemon();  /* reloads from last auto-save */
        g_paused = 0;
    }
}

static int is_daemon_alive(void) {
    if (!g_daemon_pi.hProcess) return 0;
    DWORD exitCode;
    if (!GetExitCodeProcess(g_daemon_pi.hProcess, &exitCode)) return 0;
    return (exitCode == STILL_ACTIVE);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Tray icon and menu
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static void update_tooltip(void) {
    if (g_paused) {
        snprintf(nid.szTip, sizeof(nid.szTip), "XYZT Daemon: PAUSED (GPU free)");
    } else if (g_running && is_daemon_alive()) {
        snprintf(nid.szTip, sizeof(nid.szTip), "XYZT Daemon: RUNNING (GPU active)");
    } else {
        snprintf(nid.szTip, sizeof(nid.szTip), "XYZT Daemon: STOPPED");
    }
    Shell_NotifyIconA(NIM_MODIFY, &nid);
}

static void show_context_menu(HWND hwnd) {
    POINT pt;
    GetCursorPos(&pt);

    HMENU menu = CreatePopupMenu();

    /* Status line */
    const char *status = g_paused ? "PAUSED (GPU free — game on!)" :
                         (g_running && is_daemon_alive()) ? "RUNNING (GPU active)" :
                         "STOPPED";
    char status_text[256];
    snprintf(status_text, sizeof(status_text), "XYZT Daemon: %s", status);
    AppendMenuA(menu, MF_STRING | MF_GRAYED, ID_STATUS, status_text);
    AppendMenuA(menu, MF_SEPARATOR, 0, NULL);

    if (g_running && !g_paused) {
        AppendMenuA(menu, MF_STRING, ID_PAUSE, "Pause (free GPU for gaming)");
    } else {
        AppendMenuA(menu, MF_STRING, ID_RESUME, "Resume (start GPU substrate)");
    }

    AppendMenuA(menu, MF_SEPARATOR, 0, NULL);
    AppendMenuA(menu, MF_STRING, ID_EXIT, "Exit");

    SetForegroundWindow(hwnd);
    TrackPopupMenu(menu, TPM_BOTTOMALIGN | TPM_LEFTALIGN,
                   pt.x, pt.y, 0, hwnd, NULL);
    DestroyMenu(menu);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Window procedure
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static LRESULT CALLBACK WndProc(HWND hwnd, UINT msg, WPARAM wParam, LPARAM lParam) {
    switch (msg) {
        case WM_TRAYICON:
            if (lParam == WM_RBUTTONUP || lParam == WM_LBUTTONUP) {
                show_context_menu(hwnd);
            }
            return 0;

        case WM_COMMAND:
            switch (LOWORD(wParam)) {
                case ID_PAUSE:
                    pause_daemon();
                    update_tooltip();
                    break;
                case ID_RESUME:
                    resume_daemon();
                    update_tooltip();
                    break;
                case ID_EXIT:
                    stop_daemon();
                    Shell_NotifyIconA(NIM_DELETE, &nid);
                    PostQuitMessage(0);
                    break;
            }
            return 0;

        case WM_DESTROY:
            stop_daemon();
            Shell_NotifyIconA(NIM_DELETE, &nid);
            PostQuitMessage(0);
            return 0;
    }
    return DefWindowProcA(hwnd, msg, wParam, lParam);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Main — create tray icon, start daemon, message loop
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

int WINAPI WinMain(HINSTANCE hInst, HINSTANCE hPrev, LPSTR cmdLine, int nShow) {
    (void)hPrev; (void)cmdLine; (void)nShow;

    /* Register window class */
    WNDCLASSA wc = {0};
    wc.lpfnWndProc = WndProc;
    wc.hInstance = hInst;
    wc.lpszClassName = "XYZTDaemonTray";
    RegisterClassA(&wc);

    /* Create hidden window for message handling */
    g_hwnd = CreateWindowA("XYZTDaemonTray", "XYZT Daemon",
                           0, 0, 0, 0, 0, HWND_MESSAGE, NULL, hInst, NULL);

    /* Set up tray icon */
    memset(&nid, 0, sizeof(nid));
    nid.cbSize = sizeof(nid);
    nid.hWnd = g_hwnd;
    nid.uID = ID_TRAY;
    nid.uFlags = NIF_ICON | NIF_MESSAGE | NIF_TIP;
    nid.uCallbackMessage = WM_TRAYICON;
    nid.hIcon = LoadIcon(NULL, IDI_APPLICATION);  /* default icon for now */
    strncpy(nid.szTip, "XYZT Daemon: Starting...", sizeof(nid.szTip));
    Shell_NotifyIconA(NIM_ADD, &nid);

    /* Parse state path from command line if given */
    if (cmdLine && cmdLine[0]) {
        strncpy(g_state_path, cmdLine, MAX_PATH - 1);
    }

    /* Find and start daemon */
    find_daemon();
    if (g_daemon_path[0]) {
        start_daemon();
        update_tooltip();
    } else {
        snprintf(nid.szTip, sizeof(nid.szTip), "XYZT Daemon: NOT FOUND");
        Shell_NotifyIconA(NIM_MODIFY, &nid);
    }

    /* Check daemon health every 5 seconds */
    SetTimer(g_hwnd, 1, 5000, NULL);

    /* Message loop */
    MSG msg;
    while (GetMessage(&msg, NULL, 0, 0)) {
        if (msg.message == WM_TIMER) {
            /* Health check: restart if crashed (unless paused) */
            if (g_running && !g_paused && !is_daemon_alive()) {
                start_daemon();
            }
            update_tooltip();
        }
        TranslateMessage(&msg);
        DispatchMessage(&msg);
    }

    return 0;
}
/*
 * xyzt_wire.c — Wire Graph Inspector
 *
 * Thin CLI over wire.h for manual inspection.
 * The actual learning happens INSIDE onetwo (verify) and xyzt_io (store/recall).
 * This tool is for debugging and querying the graph.
 *
 * Compile: gcc -O3 -o xyzt_wire xyzt_wire.c
 * Install: cp xyzt_wire ~/.local/bin/
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — February 2026
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>
#include "xyzt_state.h"
#include "wire.h"

static const char *NTYPE_TAG[] = { "file", "problem", "concept" };

static const char *fmt_ts(uint32_t ts) {
    static char buf[32];
    if (ts == 0) { strcpy(buf, "---"); return buf; }
    time_t t = (time_t)ts;
    struct tm *tm = localtime(&t);
    strftime(buf, sizeof(buf), "%m/%d %H:%M", tm);
    return buf;
}

static void cmd_graph(void) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    printf("---WIRE-GRAPH---\n");
    printf("nodes: %d  edges: %d  learns: %d\n\n",
           g->n_nodes, g->n_edges, g->total_learns);

    if (g->n_nodes > 0) {
        printf("NODES:\n");
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_nodes; i++) {
            WireNode *n = &g->nodes[i];
            printf("  [%3d] %-8s  hits=%3d  %s  %s",
                   i, NTYPE_TAG[n->type < 3 ? n->type : 0],
                   n->hit_count, fmt_ts(n->last_active), n->name);
            if (g->node_shells[i][0])
                printf("  [%s]", g->node_shells[i]);
            printf("\n");
        }
    }

    if (g->n_edges > 0) {
        printf("\nEDGES:\n");
        int stale_total = 0;
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++) {
            WireEdge *e = &g->edges[i];
            if (e->stale) stale_total++;
            const char *strength = e->weight > W_INIT + 30 ? "***" :
                                   e->weight > W_INIT + 10 ? "** " :
                                   e->weight > W_INIT - 10 ? "*  " :
                                   e->weight > W_MIN + 20  ? ".  " : "x  ";
            const char *etype = e->edge_type == WEDGE_STRUCTURAL ? "S" :
                                e->edge_type == WEDGE_SEMANTIC   ? "E" : "B";
            printf("  %s %s w=%3d p=%d f=%d%s  %s -> %s\n",
                   strength, etype, e->weight, e->passes, e->fails,
                   e->stale ? " STALE" : "",
                   g->nodes[e->src].name, g->nodes[e->dst].name);
        }
        if (stale_total > 0) printf("  (%d stale edges)\n", stale_total);
    }

    if (g->n_nodes == 0 && g->n_edges == 0) {
        printf("  (empty — store files and verify claims to populate)\n");
    }
    printf("---END-GRAPH---\n");
    wire_free(g);
}

static void cmd_query(const char *name) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int id = wire_find(g, name);
    if (id < 0) {
        printf("Not in graph: %s\n", name);
        wire_free(g); return;
    }

    WireNode *n = &g->nodes[id];
    printf("---WIRE-QUERY---\n");
    printf("node: %s (%s)\n", n->name, NTYPE_TAG[n->type < 3 ? n->type : 0]);
    printf("hits: %d  last: %s\n", n->hit_count, fmt_ts(n->last_active));
    if (g->node_shells[id][0])
        printf("shell: %s\n", g->node_shells[id]);

    printf("connections:\n");
    int count = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++) {
        WireEdge *e = &g->edges[i];
        if ((int)e->src == id) {
            WireNode *nb = &g->nodes[e->dst];
            const char *strength = e->weight > W_INIT + 30 ? "***" :
                                   e->weight > W_INIT + 10 ? "** " :
                                   e->weight > W_INIT - 10 ? "*  " : ".  ";
            printf("  %s w=%3d%s  %-8s  %s\n",
                   strength, e->weight, e->stale ? " STALE" : "",
                   NTYPE_TAG[nb->type < 3 ? nb->type : 0], nb->name);
            count++;
        }
    }
    if (count == 0) printf("  (none)\n");
    printf("---END-QUERY---\n");
    wire_free(g);
}

static void cmd_prune(int days) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int before_n = g->n_nodes, before_e = g->n_edges;
    int removed = wire_prune(g, days);
    wire_save(g);
    printf("---WIRE-PRUNE---\n");
    printf("pruned %d items (age cutoff: %d days)\n", removed, days);
    printf("  nodes: %d -> %d\n", before_n, g->n_nodes);
    printf("  edges: %d -> %d\n", before_e, g->n_edges);
    printf("---END-PRUNE---\n");
    wire_free(g);
}

static void cmd_coherence(void) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    printf("---WIRE-COHERENCE---\n");

    /* Show all nodes with energy, sorted by coherence */
    typedef struct { int id; float coh; float pol; float energy; } CNode;
    CNode active[WIRE_MAX_NODES];
    int n_active = 0;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_nodes; i++) {
        if (g->nodes[i].energy < 0.01f) continue;
        active[n_active].id = i;
        active[n_active].coh = wire_node_coherence(&g->nodes[i]);
        active[n_active].pol = wire_node_polarity(&g->nodes[i]);
        active[n_active].energy = g->nodes[i].energy;
        n_active++;
    }

    /* Sort by energy descending */
    for (int i = 0; i < n_active - 1; i++)
        for (int j = i + 1; j < n_active; j++)
            if (active[j].energy > active[i].energy) {
                CNode tmp = active[i]; active[i] = active[j]; active[j] = tmp;
            }

    if (n_active == 0) {
        printf("  no active nodes (run onetwo verify to build coherence)\n");
    } else {
        printf("  %-30s │ energy │ coh  │ polarity │ reading\n", "node");
        printf("  ──────────────────────────────┼────────┼──────┼──────────┼─────────\n");
        for (int i = 0; i < n_active && i < 30; i++) {
            WireNode *n = &g->nodes[active[i].id];
            const char *reading;
            if (active[i].coh > 0.8f && active[i].pol > 0.5f) reading = "PROVEN";
            else if (active[i].coh > 0.8f && active[i].pol < -0.5f) reading = "BROKEN";
            else if (active[i].coh > 0.5f) reading = "trending";
            else if (active[i].energy > 1.0f) reading = "active";
            else reading = "quiet";

            printf("  %-30s │ %6.2f │ %.2f │ %+6.2f   │ %s\n",
                   n->name, active[i].energy, active[i].coh, active[i].pol, reading);
        }
    }

    /* Find clusters */
    int clusters[WIRE_MAX_NODES];
    int n_clusters = wire_find_clusters(g, clusters, 0.3f);
    if (n_clusters > 1) {
        printf("\n%d resonant clusters detected (threshold=0.3)\n", n_clusters);
        /* Show cluster contents */
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_nodes; i++) {
            if (g->nodes[i].energy < 0.01f) continue;
            int is_root = 1;
            for (uint32_t j = 0; j < i; j++)
                if (clusters[j] == clusters[i] && g->nodes[j].energy >= 0.01f)
                    { is_root = 0; break; }
            if (!is_root) continue;
            /* Print this cluster */
            printf("  cluster %d:", clusters[i]);
            int count = 0;
            for (uint32_t j = i; j < g->n_nodes && count < 8; j++) {
                if (clusters[j] == clusters[i] && g->nodes[j].energy >= 0.01f) {
                    printf(" %s", g->nodes[j].name);
                    count++;
                }
            }
            printf("\n");
        }
    }

    printf("---END-COHERENCE---\n");
    wire_free(g);
}

static void cmd_sync(void) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int before_e = g->n_edges;

    /* Ingest structural deps from map */
    int map_added = wire_ingest_map(g);

    /* TODO: ingest semantic edges from embed when available */

    wire_recompute_topology(g);
    wire_save(g);

    printf("---WIRE-SYNC---\n");
    printf("structural edges added: %d (from map.bin)\n", map_added);
    printf("edges: %d -> %d\n", before_e, g->n_edges);
    printf("---END-SYNC---\n");
    wire_free(g);
}

static void cmd_viz(void) {
    /* Export wire graph as JSON for visualization consumption.
       The React visualizations CC-web built (xyzt_mind, xyzt_synesthesia)
       can load this to render the REAL project state, not simulated data. */
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    printf("{\n");
    printf("  \"nodes\": [\n");
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_nodes; i++) {
        WireNode *n = &g->nodes[i];
        if (!n->name[0]) continue;
        float coh = wire_node_coherence(n);
        float pol = wire_node_polarity(n);
        const char *ntype = n->type == WNODE_FILE ? "file" :
                            n->type == WNODE_PROBLEM ? "problem" : "concept";
        printf("    {\"id\":%d,\"name\":\"", i);
        /* JSON-escape the name */
        for (const char *p = n->name; *p; p++) {
            if (*p == '"') printf("\\\"");
            else if (*p == '\\') printf("\\\\");
            else if ((unsigned char)*p >= 0x20) putchar(*p);
        }
        printf("\",\"type\":\"%s\",\"hits\":%d,\"valence\":%d,\"layer_zero\":%d,"
               "\"plasticity\":%.3f,\"energy\":%.4f,\"coherence\":%.4f,"
               "\"polarity\":%.4f}",
               ntype, n->hit_count, n->valence, n->layer_zero,
               n->plasticity, n->energy, coh, pol);
        /* Check if there's a next valid node */
        int has_next = 0;
        for (uint32_t j = i + 1; j < g->n_nodes; j++)
            if (g->nodes[j].name[0]) { has_next = 1; break; }
        if (has_next) printf(",");
        printf("\n");
    }
    printf("  ],\n");

    printf("  \"edges\": [\n");
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++) {
        WireEdge *e = &g->edges[i];
        const char *etype = e->edge_type == WEDGE_STRUCTURAL ? "structural" :
                            e->edge_type == WEDGE_SEMANTIC ? "semantic" : "behavioral";
        printf("    {\"src\":%d,\"dst\":%d,\"weight\":%d,\"passes\":%d,"
               "\"fails\":%d,\"stale\":%d,\"type\":\"%s\"}",
               e->src, e->dst, e->weight, e->passes, e->fails, e->stale, etype);
        if (i < g->n_edges - 1) printf(",");
        printf("\n");
    }
    printf("  ],\n");

    printf("  \"meta\": {\"n_nodes\":%d,\"n_edges\":%d,\"learns\":%d}\n",
           g->n_nodes, g->n_edges, g->total_learns);
    printf("}\n");
    wire_free(g);
}

static void cmd_bridge(const char *target) {
    /* Copy current wire.bin to a target path — for engine import.
       Engine reads the same binary format now (v4). */
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();

    /* Default target: ~/.xyzt/bridge_wire.bin */
    char path[512];
    if (target && target[0]) {
        strncpy(path, target, 511);
    } else {
        const char *home = getenv("USERPROFILE");
        if (!home) home = getenv("HOME");
        if (!home) { printf("error: can't find home dir\n"); wire_free(g); return; }
        snprintf(path, sizeof(path), "%s/.xyzt/bridge_wire.bin", home);
    }

    /* Prune before bridging — only send clean data to engine */
    int pruned = wire_prune(g, 7);

    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "wb");
    if (!fp) { printf("error: can't write %s\n", path); wire_free(g); return; }
    fwrite(g, sizeof(WireGraph), 1, fp);
    fclose(fp);

    printf("---WIRE-BRIDGE---\n");
    printf("exported to: %s\n", path);
    printf("  nodes: %d  edges: %d  (pruned %d dead items)\n",
           g->n_nodes, g->n_edges, pruned);
    printf("  engine import: xyzt_pc wire_import \"%s\"\n", path);
    printf("---END-BRIDGE---\n");
    wire_free(g);
}

static void cmd_reset(void) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    memset(g, 0, sizeof(WireGraph));
    g->magic = WIRE_MAGIC; g->version = WIRE_VER;
    wire_save(g);
    printf("graph cleared\n");
    wire_free(g);
}

static char *concat_args(int argc, char **argv, int start) {
    static char buf[WIRE_NAME_LEN];
    buf[0] = '\0'; int pos = 0;
    for (int i = start; i < argc && pos < WIRE_NAME_LEN - 2; i++) {
        if (i > start) buf[pos++] = ' ';
        int len = strlen(argv[i]);
        if (pos + len >= WIRE_NAME_LEN - 1) len = WIRE_NAME_LEN - 1 - pos;
        memcpy(buf + pos, argv[i], len); pos += len;
    }
    buf[pos] = '\0'; return buf;
}

static void usage(void) {
    printf("XYZT WIRE — Graph Inspector\n\n");
    printf("  xyzt_wire graph                Full graph state\n");
    printf("  xyzt_wire query <name>         Connections for a node\n");
    printf("  xyzt_wire prune [days]         Remove dead edges/nodes (default 14 days)\n");
    printf("  xyzt_wire coherence            Show node coherence field + resonant clusters\n");
    printf("  xyzt_wire viz                  Export graph as JSON for visualization\n");
    printf("  xyzt_wire sync                 Ingest structural deps from map.bin into graph\n");
    printf("  xyzt_wire bridge [path]        Export wire for engine import (default ~/.xyzt/bridge_wire.bin)\n");
    printf("  xyzt_wire reset                Clear graph\n");
    printf("\nLearning happens automatically inside onetwo and xyzt_io.\n");
    printf("This tool is for inspection only.\n");
    printf("\nGraph: $XYZT_WIRE or %s\n", WIRE_DEFAULT_PATH);
    printf("\nIsaac & Claude — February 2026\n");
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
    if (argc < 2) { usage(); return 0; }
    const char *cmd = argv[1];

    if (strcmp(cmd, "graph") == 0) cmd_graph();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "query") == 0 && argc >= 3) cmd_query(concat_args(argc, argv, 2));
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "prune") == 0) cmd_prune(argc >= 3 ? atoi(argv[2]) : 14);
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "coherence") == 0) cmd_coherence();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "viz") == 0) cmd_viz();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "sync") == 0) cmd_sync();
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "bridge") == 0) cmd_bridge(argc >= 3 ? argv[2] : NULL);
    else if (strcmp(cmd, "reset") == 0) cmd_reset();
    else { usage(); return 1; }

    return 0;
}
/*
 * cortex.h — Thin controller over engine + Yee substrate
 *
 * Clean ingest→inject→tick→read loop. The DISH_SPEC's cortex.cu
 * without gutting engine.c. Parallel runtime path.
 *
 * Isaac Oravec & Claude, March 2026
 */

#ifndef CORTEX_H
#define CORTEX_H

#include "engine.h"
#include "infer.h"

typedef struct {
    Engine  eng;
    uint8_t *yee_sub;    /* accumulator readback buffer */
    int     gpu_ok;      /* yee initialized */
    uint64_t n_ingested; /* total observations */
} Cortex;

/* Initialize engine + Yee substrate */
int  cortex_init(Cortex *c);

/* Ingest raw bytes as a new observation */
int  cortex_ingest(Cortex *c, const char *name, const uint8_t *data, int len);

/* Tick N cycles (each = SUBSTRATE_INT ticks with full Yee loop) */
void cortex_tick(Cortex *c, int n_cycles);

/* Query via wave inference — returns top-K resonating nodes */
int  cortex_query(Cortex *c, const char *text, InferResult *out, int max);

/* Save/load full state (engine + Yee L-field) */
int  cortex_save(Cortex *c, const char *path);
int  cortex_load(Cortex *c, const char *path);

/* Prediction loop: graph proposes, wave verifies, Hebbian learns.
 * Finds what the engine is focused on, predicts connected nodes,
 * injects predictions at 0.3x amplitude, reads which resonated.
 * Returns number of verified predictions. */
int cortex_predict(Cortex *c);

/* Self-observation: engine reads its own coherence field, encodes what
 * it sees, and ingests the observation as a new node. The engine watches
 * itself think. Returns the node ID of the self-observation, or -1. */
int cortex_self_observe(Cortex *c);

/* Cleanup */
void cortex_destroy(Cortex *c);

#endif /* CORTEX_H */
/*
 * engine.h — Unified XYZT Engine API (CPU side)
 *
 * Shells + Fresnel boundaries + ONETWO feedback + nesting.
 * Extracted from v9 (xyzt.c), cleaned for library use.
 *
 * One engine operation: accum += v. Observer creates all operations.
 * Position is value. Collision is computation.
 *
 * Isaac Oravec & Claude, February 2026
 */

#ifndef XYZT_ENGINE_H
#define XYZT_ENGINE_H

#include <stdint.h>
#include <limits.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <math.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include <ctype.h>
#include "tline.h"

#ifdef _OPENMP
#include <omp.h>
#endif

/* Portable popcount for 64-bit values.
 * Works with MSVC, GCC, Clang, and CUDA nvcc. */
static inline int xyzt_popcnt64(uint64_t x) {
#if defined(__CUDA_ARCH__)
    return __popcll(x);
#elif defined(__GNUC__) || defined(__clang__)
    return __builtin_popcountll(x);
#else
    /* Portable fallback (Hamming weight) */
    x = x - ((x >> 1) & 0x5555555555555555ULL);
    x = (x & 0x3333333333333333ULL) + ((x >> 2) & 0x3333333333333333ULL);
    x = (x + (x >> 4)) & 0x0F0F0F0F0F0F0F0FULL;
    return (int)((x * 0x0101010101010101ULL) >> 56);
#endif
}

static inline int xyzt_popcnt32(uint32_t x) {
#if defined(__CUDA_ARCH__)
    return __popc(x);
#elif defined(__GNUC__) || defined(__clang__)
    return __builtin_popcount(x);
#else
    x = x - ((x >> 1) & 0x55555555u);
    x = (x & 0x33333333u) + ((x >> 2) & 0x33333333u);
    x = (x + (x >> 4)) & 0x0F0F0F0Fu;
    return (int)((x * 0x01010101u) >> 24);
#endif
}

static inline int xyzt_ctzll(uint64_t x) {
#if defined(__CUDA_ARCH__)
    return __ffsll(x) - 1;
#elif defined(__GNUC__) || defined(__clang__)
    return __builtin_ctzll(x);
#else
    if (x == 0) return 64;
    int n = 0;
    if ((x & 0xFFFFFFFF) == 0) { n += 32; x >>= 32; }
    if ((x & 0xFFFF) == 0) { n += 16; x >>= 16; }
    if ((x & 0xFF) == 0) { n += 8; x >>= 8; }
    if ((x & 0xF) == 0) { n += 4; x >>= 4; }
    if ((x & 0x3) == 0) { n += 2; x >>= 2; }
    if ((x & 0x1) == 0) { n += 1; }
    return n;
#endif
}

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * {2,3} SUBSTRATE CONSTANTS — from Lean proofs
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
#ifndef SUBSTRATE_INT
#define SUBSTRATE_INT      155u    /* DERIVED: 3.5 × acc_half_life(44) ≈ 154. Empirical sweep confirmed 155. */
#endif
#define MISMATCH_TAX_NUM    81u    /* 81/2251 ≈ 0.035982 */
#define MISMATCH_TAX_DEN  2251u
#define PRUNE_FLOOR          9u    /* ≈ 0.036 * 255 */
#define MAX_NODE_WEIGHT    1024u    /* total edge weight budget per node */
#define VAL_CEILING        1000000 /* hard ceiling on node values (±1M) */
#define LYSIS_THRESHOLD    100     /* valence below this -> kill child (apoptosis) */
#define VALENCE_DECAY_RATE   2     /* per SUBSTRATE_INT cycle under high error */
#define Z_ASYM_MARGIN        3     /* directional gate: only wire i→j if contain(i,j) > contain(j,i) + margin */
#define PLASTICITY_DEFAULT  1.0f
#define PLASTICITY_MIN      0.5f
#define PLASTICITY_MAX      2.0f
#define PLASTICITY_HEAT     0.01f   /* frustration increment */
#define PLASTICITY_COOL     0.005f  /* boredom decrement */

/* Shell impedance: K = 3/2 per boundary crossing */
static const double SHELL_Z[3] = { 1.0, 1.5, 2.25 };

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * BITSTREAM — distinction primitive (the 2)
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
#define BS_WORDS   32   /* 2048 bits = 256 bytes raw content (was 128 = 1KB) */
#define BS_MAXBITS (BS_WORDS * 64)
#define FP_TOTAL   5312    /* 7-layer text fingerprint total bits */

typedef struct { uint64_t w[BS_WORDS]; int len; } BitStream;

static inline void bs_init(BitStream *b) { memset(b, 0, sizeof(*b)); }

static inline void bs_push(BitStream *b, int bit) {
    if (b->len >= BS_MAXBITS) return;
    int idx = b->len / 64, off = b->len % 64;
    if (bit) b->w[idx] |= (1ULL << off);
    else     b->w[idx] &= ~(1ULL << off);
    b->len++;
}

static inline int bs_get(const BitStream *b, int i) {
    if (i < 0 || i >= b->len) return 0;
    return (b->w[i / 64] >> (i % 64)) & 1;
}

static inline void bs_set(BitStream *b, int pos, int bit) {
    if (pos < 0 || pos >= BS_MAXBITS) return;
    int idx = pos / 64, off = pos % 64;
    if (bit) b->w[idx] |= (1ULL << off);
    else     b->w[idx] &= ~(1ULL << off);
    if (pos >= b->len) b->len = pos + 1;
}

static inline int bs_popcount(const BitStream *b) {
    int ones = 0, full = b->len / 64;
    for (int i = 0; i < full; i++)
        ones += xyzt_popcnt64(b->w[i]);
    int rem = b->len % 64;
    if (rem)
        ones += xyzt_popcnt64(b->w[full] & ((1ULL << rem) - 1));
    return ones;
}

/* Jaccard similarity */
static inline int bs_corr(const BitStream *a, const BitStream *b) {
    int n = a->len < b->len ? a->len : b->len;
    if (n < 1) return 0;
    int and_pop = 0, or_pop = 0, full = n / 64;
    for (int i = 0; i < full; i++) {
        and_pop += xyzt_popcnt64(a->w[i] & b->w[i]);
        or_pop  += xyzt_popcnt64(a->w[i] | b->w[i]);
    }
    int rem = n % 64;
    if (rem) {
        uint64_t mask = (1ULL << rem) - 1;
        and_pop += xyzt_popcnt64((a->w[full] & b->w[full]) & mask);
        or_pop  += xyzt_popcnt64((a->w[full] | b->w[full]) & mask);
    }
    if (or_pop == 0) return 0;
    return (and_pop * 100) / or_pop;
}

/* Containment: popcount(query AND node) / popcount(query) */
static inline int bs_contain(const BitStream *query, const BitStream *node) {
    if (query->len < 1 || node->len < 1) return 0;
    int n = query->len < node->len ? query->len : node->len;
    int and_pop = 0, q_pop = 0, full = n / 64;
    for (int i = 0; i < full; i++) {
        and_pop += xyzt_popcnt64(query->w[i] & node->w[i]);
        q_pop   += xyzt_popcnt64(query->w[i]);
    }
    int rem = n % 64;
    if (rem) {
        uint64_t mask = (1ULL << rem) - 1;
        and_pop += xyzt_popcnt64((query->w[full] & node->w[full]) & mask);
        q_pop   += xyzt_popcnt64(query->w[full] & mask);
    }
    int qfull = query->len / 64;
    for (int i = full + (rem ? 1 : 0); i < qfull; i++)
        q_pop += xyzt_popcnt64(query->w[i]);
    if (q_pop == 0) return 0;
    return (and_pop * 100) / q_pop;
}

static inline int bs_mutual_contain(const BitStream *a, const BitStream *b) {
    int ab = bs_contain(a, b);
    int ba = bs_contain(b, a);
    return ab < ba ? ab : ba;
}

/* Hash + Bloom */
static inline uint32_t hash32(const uint8_t *data, int len) {
    uint32_t h = 2166136261u;
    for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) { h ^= data[i]; h *= 16777619u; }
    return h;
}

static inline void bloom_set(BitStream *bs, int base_off, int bloom_len,
                              const uint8_t *data, int data_len, int n_hashes) {
    uint32_t h1 = hash32(data, data_len);
    uint32_t h2 = (h1 >> 16) | (h1 << 16);
    h2 |= 1;
    for (int i = 0; i < n_hashes; i++) {
        int pos = (h1 + i * h2) % bloom_len;
        bs_set(bs, base_off + pos, 1);
    }
}

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * SPATIAL COORDINATES — position IS value
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
typedef uint32_t Coord;
static inline Coord coord_pack(int x, int y, int z) {
    return ((x & 0x3FF) << 20) | ((y & 0x3FF) << 10) | (z & 0x3FF);
}
static inline int coord_x(Coord c) { return (c >> 20) & 0x3FF; }
static inline int coord_y(Coord c) { return (c >> 10) & 0x3FF; }
static inline int coord_z(Coord c) { return c & 0x3FF; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * T — the substrate (time)
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
typedef struct {
    uint64_t count;
    double   birth_ms;   /* wall clock at init */
} SubstrateT;

static inline void    T_init(SubstrateT *t) { t->count = 0; t->birth_ms = 0; }
static inline uint64_t T_tick(SubstrateT *t) { return ++t->count; }
static inline uint64_t T_now(const SubstrateT *t) { return t->count; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * ONETWO OBSERVATION
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
#define MAX_RUNS 512

typedef struct {
    int lengths[MAX_RUNS];
    int types[MAX_RUNS];
    int count;
} RunList;

typedef struct {
    int ones_runs[MAX_RUNS], zeros_runs[MAX_RUNS];
    int n_ones, n_zeros;
    int ones_deltas[MAX_RUNS], zeros_deltas[MAX_RUNS];
    int n_ones_d, n_zeros_d;
    int predicted_next_one, predicted_next_zero;
    int ones_pattern_type, zeros_pattern_type;
    int density, symmetry, period;
} OneTwoResult;

typedef struct {
    OneTwoResult analysis;
    int32_t      feedback[8];
    int          tick_count;
    int          total_inputs;
    int32_t      prev_match;
} OneTwoSystem;

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * NODE, EDGE, GRAPH — the learning structures
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
#define MAX_NODES 4096
#define MAX_EDGES 65536
#define NAME_LEN  128
#define GROW_K    6

typedef struct {
    BitStream identity;
    char      name[NAME_LEN];
    uint32_t  created, last_active, hit_count;
    uint8_t   shell_id;
    uint8_t   layer_zero;
    uint8_t   alive, valence;
    Coord     coord;
    double    Z;
    uint16_t  n_incoming;
    uint8_t   crystal_hist[8];
    uint8_t   crystal_n;
    int32_t   val;
    int32_t   prev_val;       /* val at last SUBSTRATE_INT boundary (for coherence) */
    int32_t   prev_edge_sum;  /* total incoming edge weight at last boundary */
    int64_t   accum;
    int32_t   I_energy;       /* magnetic/current energy at node (for XOR detection) */
    int8_t    coherent;       /* +1 coherent, -1 incoherent, 0 unknown */
    int8_t    child_id;
    uint8_t   has_negation;   /* 1 if source text contained negation markers */
    uint8_t   contradicted;  /* 1 if node has inverted incoming edges (disputed) */
    float     plasticity;       /* learning rate multiplier (0.5..2.0) */
} Node;

typedef struct {
    uint16_t src_a, src_b, dst;
    uint8_t  _pad;
    uint8_t  weight, learn_rate, intershell;
    uint32_t created, last_active;
    uint8_t  invert_a, invert_b;
    float    correlation;  /* sign anti-correlation: +1=agree, -1=oppose, 0=unrelated.
                            * Updated each tick: corr = decay*corr + sign(src)*sign(dst).
                            * Replaces text_has_negation keyword list. */
    TLine    tl;        /* FDTD transmission line (Phase 2) */
} Edge;

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * TRANSMISSION LINE EDGE — edges with per-cell FDTD propagation
 *
 * From xyzt_unified.c (February 2026, proven standalone).
 * Edge is a 1D transmission line: V[i], I[i], Lc[i] per cell.
 * Telegrapher's equations: dI/dt = -(1/L)·dV/dx
 *                          dV/dt = -(1/C)·dI/dx
 * Node impedance modifies boundary Lc cells (mass curves space).
 * Z = how many cells deep. Filtering = lossy propagation.
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
#define TLINE_EC      16     /* cells per edge (start small, HANDOFF says 8-16) */
#define TLINE_C0      1.0    /* capacitance per cell (uniform) */
#define TLINE_DT      0.45   /* FDTD timestep (Courant stable for L>=0.5) */
#define TLINE_DAMP    0.001  /* resistive loss per cell */
#define TLINE_IMP_DEPTH 4    /* how many boundary cells node impedance affects */
#define TLINE_MAX_NODES 64
#define TLINE_MAX_EDGES 256

typedef struct {
    int src, dst, n_cells;
    double V[TLINE_EC], I[TLINE_EC];
    double Lc[TLINE_EC];       /* per-cell inductance — THE key field */
    double L_base;              /* base inductance (from construction) */
    double Z0_base;             /* sqrt(L_base/C0) */
} TLineEdge;

typedef struct {
    int x, y, z;
    char name[32];
    double V, V_peak;
    double energy, I_energy, total_energy;
    double impedance;           /* grows via back-reaction at collisions */
    int is_input;
    int n_edges;
    int edge_ids[TLINE_MAX_EDGES];
    int edge_dirs[TLINE_MAX_EDGES]; /* -1 = node is src, +1 = node is dst */
} TLineNode;

typedef struct {
    TLineNode nodes[TLINE_MAX_NODES];
    int n_nodes;
    TLineEdge edges[TLINE_MAX_EDGES];
    int n_edges;
} TLineGraph;

/* TLine API (implemented in engine.c) */
void tlg_init(TLineGraph *g);
int  tlg_add_node(TLineGraph *g, int x, int y, int z, const char *name);
int  tlg_add_edge(TLineGraph *g, int src, int dst, double L_base);
void tlg_clear(TLineGraph *g);
void tlg_inject(TLineGraph *g, int node_id, int bit, double amplitude);
void tlg_apply_impedance(TLineGraph *g);
void tlg_propagate_step(TLineGraph *g);
void tlg_propagate(TLineGraph *g, int steps);
void tlg_observe(TLineNode *n);
void tlg_backreaction(TLineGraph *g, double kappa);

typedef struct {
    Node *nodes; Edge *edges;
    int n_nodes, n_edges;
    uint64_t total_ticks, total_learns, total_grown, total_pruned, total_boundary_crossings;
    int grow_threshold, prune_threshold, learn_strengthen, learn_weaken, learn_rate;
    int auto_grow, auto_prune, auto_learn;
    int grow_mean;
    int grow_interval, prune_interval;
    const uint8_t *retina;    /* zero-copy view into parent's substrate cube */
    int retina_len;            /* positions visible (64 for 4^3 cube) */

    /* Inner T: local clock and error state for child graphs */
    int32_t  error_accum;       /* cumulative error signal (SPRT-style) */
    int32_t  prev_output;       /* output value at last heartbeat */
    int      local_heartbeat;   /* how many local heartbeats this child has lived */
    uint8_t  drive;             /* 0=curiosity, 1=frustration, 2=boredom */

    /* Z-bucket cache for O(E+N) propagation (rebuilt when topology changes) */
    int     *z_edge_idx;        /* edge indices sorted by dest z-level */
    int      z_edge_off[65];    /* offsets: edges at z-level zl are [off[zl]..off[zl+1]) */
    int     *z_node_idx;        /* node indices sorted by z-level */
    int      z_node_off[65];    /* offsets: nodes at z-level zl are [off[zl]..off[zl+1]) */
    int      z_max;             /* max z-level in graph */
    int      z_cache_n_nodes;   /* n_nodes when cache was built */
    int      z_cache_n_edges;   /* n_edges when cache was built */
} Graph;

/* Shell — graph with identity */
#define MAX_SHELLS 3
typedef struct { Graph g; uint8_t id; char name[32]; } Shell;

/* v9: nesting */
#define MAX_CHILDREN 4

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * SENSE — temporal feature extraction result
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
#define SENSE_MAX_FEATS 128
typedef struct {
    int node_ids[SENSE_MAX_FEATS];
    int n_features;
} sense_result_t;

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * THE ENGINE
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
typedef struct {
    Shell shells[MAX_SHELLS];
    int   n_shells;
    Edge *boundary_edges;
    int   n_boundary_edges, max_boundary_edges;
    uint64_t total_ticks;
    int   learn_interval;
    int   boundary_interval;
    OneTwoSystem onetwo;
    Graph child_pool[MAX_CHILDREN];
    int   child_owner[MAX_CHILDREN];
    int   n_children;
    int   low_error_run;
    SubstrateT T;
    sense_result_t last_sense;
    sense_result_t prev_sense;   /* previous S10 cycle — for observer */
    int pol_bridge_total;   /* bridge votes (all paths) */
    int pol_bridge_invert;
    int pol_kw_total;       /* keyword votes (text path only) */
    int pol_kw_invert;
    int32_t graph_error;    /* direct: incoherent node count (updated at SUBSTRATE_INT) */
    int32_t total_cleaved;  /* edges severed by structural cleaving */
} Engine;

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * ENGINE API (implemented in engine.c)
 * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Observers: different questions about the same integer */
static inline int obs_bool(int32_t v)        { return v > 0 ? 1 : 0; }
static inline int obs_all(int32_t v, int n)  { return v >= n ? 1 : 0; }
static inline int obs_parity(int32_t v)      { return (v & 1) ? 1 : 0; }
static inline int obs_raw(int32_t v)         { return v; }
/* XOR = energy arrived but voltage mostly cancelled (destructive collision).
 * With FDTD propagation, perfect cancellation to val==0 is unlikely —
 * accept val < 25% of I_energy as "cancelled". */
static inline int obs_xor(int32_t I_energy, int32_t val) {
    return (I_energy > 0 && abs(val) * 4 <= I_energy) ? 1 : 0;
}
/* Z=1: AND = both inputs exceed threshold (energy gating) */
static inline int obs_and(int32_t val, int32_t I_energy, int threshold) {
    return (abs(val) > threshold && I_energy > threshold) ? 1 : 0;
}
/* Z=3: frequency detector — high n_incoming relative to valence = resonant node */
static inline int obs_freq(uint16_t n_incoming, uint8_t valence) {
    return (n_incoming >= 2 && valence > 0 && n_incoming * 255 > valence * 4) ? 1 : 0;
}
/* Z=4: correlation — val and I_energy trend same direction (co-moving) */
static inline int obs_corr(int32_t val, int32_t prev_val, int32_t I_energy) {
    int val_up = (val > prev_val) ? 1 : 0;
    int energy_present = (I_energy > 0) ? 1 : 0;
    return (val_up == energy_present) ? 1 : 0;
}

/* Fresnel physics */
static inline double fresnel_T(double K) {
    double d = K + 1.0; return (4.0 * K) / (d * d);
}
static inline double fresnel_R(double K) {
    double d = (K - 1.0) / (K + 1.0); return d * d;
}
static inline uint8_t apply_fresnel(int raw, double Z_src, double Z_dst) {
    if (Z_src <= 0.0) Z_src = 1.0;
    double K = Z_dst / Z_src;
    double T = fresnel_T(K);
    int t = (int)(raw * T + 0.5);
    return t > 255 ? 255 : (t < 1 ? 1 : (uint8_t)t);
}

/* Crystal histogram */
void crystal_update(Node *n, Edge *edges, int n_edges, int node_id);
int  crystal_strength(const Node *n);
int  crystal_distance(const Node *a, const Node *b);

/* ONETWO functions */
void bs_runs(const BitStream *b, RunList *r);
int  ot_find_period(const BitStream *b);
int  ot_measure_symmetry(const BitStream *b);
int  ot_predict_sequence(const int *vals, int n, int *pattern_type);
OneTwoResult ot_observe(const BitStream *b);
void ot_sys_init(OneTwoSystem *s);
OneTwoResult ot_sys_ingest(OneTwoSystem *s, const uint8_t *data, int len);

/* Graph operations */
void graph_init(Graph *g);
void graph_destroy(Graph *g);
int  graph_find(Graph *g, const char *name);
int  graph_add(Graph *g, const char *name, uint8_t shell_id, const SubstrateT *T);
int  graph_find_edge(Graph *g, int a, int b, int d);
int  graph_wire(Graph *g, int a, int b, int d, uint8_t w, int inter);
int  graph_learn(Graph *g, int32_t structural_match);
int  graph_compute_topology(Graph *g, int z_depth);
int  graph_zone_coherence(const Graph *g, const int *node_ids, int n_ids, int *n_alive);

/* Engine lifecycle */
int  child_tick_once(Graph *g);
void engine_init(Engine *eng);
void engine_destroy(Engine *eng);
int  engine_ingest(Engine *eng, const char *name, const BitStream *data);
int  engine_ingest_text(Engine *eng, const char *name, const char *text);
int  engine_ingest_raw(Engine *eng, const char *name, const char *text);
int  engine_ingest_file(Engine *eng, const char *path);
void engine_tick(Engine *eng);
void engine_status(const Engine *eng);

/* Encoders */
void encode_bytes(BitStream *bs, const uint8_t *data, int len);
void encode_raw(BitStream *bs, const char *data, int len);
void encode_text(BitStream *bs, const char *text);
int  encode_file(BitStream *bs, const char *path);

/* Query */
typedef struct {
    int id, shell;
    int raw_corr, crystal, resonance;
    char name[NAME_LEN];
} QueryResult;

int engine_query(const Engine *eng, const BitStream *query,
                 QueryResult *results, int max_results);

/* Name substring search (for short queries where structural fingerprinting is meaningless) */
int engine_query_name(const Engine *eng, const char *substr,
                      QueryResult *results, int max_results);

#endif /* XYZT_ENGINE_H */
/*
 * infer.h — XYZT Inference Layer
 *
 * Forward pass through the wave substrate:
 * 1. Load L-field (the "weights")
 * 2. Inject query as voltage spike (the "embedding")
 * 3. Ring the grid for N heartbeats (the "compute")
 * 4. Read observers at resonating nodes (the "output")
 *
 * No matrix multiplication. No layers. No backprop.
 * Inject a wave, let physics compute, read what resonates.
 *
 * Isaac Oravec & Claude, March 2026
 */

#ifndef INFER_H
#define INFER_H

#include "engine.h"

#define INFER_MAX_RESULTS 16
#define INFER_RING_TICKS  155   /* one SUBSTRATE_INT cycle */

typedef struct {
    int    node_id;        /* which node resonated */
    char   name[NAME_LEN]; /* node name for lookup */
    float  energy;         /* accumulated |V| at this position */
    float  val;            /* net voltage (signed — constructive vs destructive) */
    int    z3_freq;        /* Z3 observer: zero-crossing count (resonance) */
    int    z4_corr;        /* Z4 observer: correlated trend (1=yes, 0=no) */
    int    crystal;        /* crystal strength of this node */
} InferResult;

/* Run a forward pass: inject query, ring, read resonance.
 * eng must have loaded state. Yee must be initialized.
 * Returns number of results (top-K resonating nodes). */
int infer_query(Engine *eng, const char *query_text,
                InferResult *results, int max_results);

/* Run a forward pass with raw bytes (for binary/sensor data). */
int infer_query_raw(Engine *eng, const uint8_t *data, int len,
                    InferResult *results, int max_results);

/* Dream mode: no query. Inject thermal noise into carved L-field.
 * Read what resonates. The engine tells you what it knows
 * without being asked. Deep knowledge resonates strongest.
 * Returns top-K resonating nodes ordered by dream_score. */
typedef struct {
    int    node_id;
    char   name[NAME_LEN];
    float  dream_score;    /* energy × (1 - coherence): active resonance */
    float  energy;
    float  coherence;      /* |V_signed|/V_energy: low = oscillating = dreaming */
} DreamResult;

int infer_dream(Engine *eng, DreamResult *results, int max_results, int ticks);

#endif /* INFER_H */
/*
 * io.h — Streaming input (ring buffer + stdin listener thread)
 *
 * Thread-safe circular buffer for continuous stdin ingestion.
 * Producer thread reads stdin, consumer loop ingests into engine.
 *
 * Isaac Oravec & Claude, March 2026
 */

#ifndef IO_H
#define IO_H

#include <stdint.h>

#define STREAM_BUF_SIZE  1024
#define STREAM_WINDOW    256   /* max bytes per ingest chunk */

typedef struct {
    char     buf[STREAM_BUF_SIZE];
    int      head;        /* write position */
    int      tail;        /* read position */
    int      length;      /* bytes available */
    int      eof;         /* stdin closed */
    void    *lock;        /* CRITICAL_SECTION* (opaque for C) */
    void    *thread;      /* thread handle */
} StreamContext;

/* Initialize stream context, start stdin listener thread */
int  io_init(StreamContext *ctx);

/* Check if data is available in the ring buffer */
int  io_has_data(StreamContext *ctx);

/* Read up to max_len bytes from ring buffer into out.
 * In text mode: reads until newline or max_len.
 * Returns number of bytes read (0 if no complete line). */
int  io_read_line(StreamContext *ctx, char *out, int max_len);

/* Read up to max_len bytes from ring buffer (binary, no delimiter).
 * Returns number of bytes read. */
int  io_read_raw(StreamContext *ctx, char *out, int max_len);

/* Check if stdin has been closed */
int  io_eof(StreamContext *ctx);

/* Sleep for ms milliseconds (portable wrapper) */
void io_sleep_ms(int ms);

/* Check if stdout is a console (not redirected to file/pipe) */
int io_stdout_is_console(void);

/* Cleanup: signal thread to stop, join, free resources */
void io_destroy(StreamContext *ctx);

#endif /* IO_H */
/*
 * onetwo.h — ONETWO encoding API
 *
 * "Don't send the AI data. Send the AI rules OF the data."
 * 4096-bit structural fingerprint: repetition, opposition, nesting, meta.
 *
 * Isaac Oravec & Claude, February 2026
 */

#ifndef ONETWO_H
#define ONETWO_H

#include "engine.h"

/* 4096-bit fixed output */
#define OT_TOTAL 4096

/* Section offsets */
#define OT_RUN_OFF     0
#define OT_RUN_LEN     256
#define OT_AUTO_OFF    256
#define OT_AUTO_LEN    256
#define OT_FREQ_OFF    512
#define OT_FREQ_LEN    256
#define OT_DIV_OFF     768
#define OT_DIV_LEN     256
#define OT_DELTA_OFF   1024
#define OT_DELTA_LEN   256
#define OT_XOR_OFF     1280
#define OT_XOR_LEN     256
#define OT_CLASS_OFF   1536
#define OT_CLASS_LEN   256
#define OT_SYM_OFF     1792
#define OT_SYM_LEN     256
#define OT_SCALE_OFF   2048
#define OT_SCALE_LEN   256
#define OT_DEPTH_OFF   2304
#define OT_DEPTH_LEN   256
#define OT_SUBREP_OFF  2560
#define OT_SUBREP_LEN  256
#define OT_ENTG_OFF    2816
#define OT_ENTG_LEN    256
#define OT_MLEN_OFF    3072
#define OT_MLEN_LEN    64
#define OT_MENT_OFF    3136
#define OT_MENT_LEN    64
#define OT_MUNIQ_OFF   3200
#define OT_MUNIQ_LEN   64
#define OT_MDENS_OFF   3264
#define OT_MDENS_LEN   64

/* Main encoding function: raw bytes -> 4096-bit structural rules */
void onetwo_parse(const uint8_t *raw, size_t len, BitStream *out);

/* Inverse: pattern bitstream -> estimated raw bytes (lossy) */
void onetwo_generate(const BitStream *in, uint8_t *out_buf, size_t *out_len);

/* Self-observation: feed bitstream bytes back through parse */
void onetwo_self_observe(const BitStream *bs, BitStream *out);

#endif /* ONETWO_H */
/*
 * onetwo_graph_adapter.h — Convert wire subgraphs to ONETWO observations
 *
 * The wire graph is the input format for the reasoning engine.
 * This adapter extracts (in, out) pairs from graph neighborhoods
 * so the engine can decompose the structural pattern.
 *
 * The engine's decomposition {a, b, substrate} IS the pattern key.
 * Two subgraphs that decompose to the same {a, b, s} are the same
 * structural pattern, regardless of node names.
 *
 * Isaac & CC — March 2026
 */

#ifndef ONETWO_GRAPH_ADAPTER_H
#define ONETWO_GRAPH_ADAPTER_H

#include "wire.h"
#include <math.h>

/* Maximum neighborhood size for decomposition */
#define GRAPH_MAX_OBS 64

/* An observation pair extracted from the wire graph */
typedef struct {
    double in;
    double out;
} GraphObs;

/* A graph neighborhood — the local topology around a node */
typedef struct {
    GraphObs obs[GRAPH_MAX_OBS];
    int      n_obs;
    int      center_node;           /* the node we're looking at */
    char     center_name[128];
} GraphNeighborhood;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * Extract neighborhood: for a given node, collect all
 * edges and convert to (in, out) pairs.
 *
 * The encoding: for each edge connected to center_node,
 *   in  = neighbor node's signed_acc (directional health)
 *   out = edge weight (connection strength)
 *
 * This asks: "what's the relationship between a node's
 * health and the strength of its connections?"
 *
 * If the engine finds a pattern, it means the graph has
 * structure — healthy nodes have strong edges, sick nodes
 * have weak edges, etc. The SHAPE of that relationship
 * is the structural fingerprint.
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int graph_extract_neighborhood(
    const WireGraph *g, int center,
    GraphNeighborhood *out
) {
    out->n_obs = 0;
    out->center_node = center;
    strncpy(out->center_name, g->nodes[center].name, 127);

    for (int e = 0; e < g->n_edges && out->n_obs < GRAPH_MAX_OBS; e++) {
        WireEdge *edge = &g->edges[e];
        int neighbor = -1;

        if (edge->src == center) neighbor = edge->dst;
        else if (edge->dst == center) neighbor = edge->src;
        else continue;

        if (neighbor < 0 || neighbor >= g->n_nodes) continue;

        WireNode *nbr = &g->nodes[neighbor];

        /* in = neighbor's health signal (signed accumulator) */
        double health = (double)nbr->signed_acc;
        /* Fallback: use hit_count if signed_acc is zero */
        if (health == 0.0 && nbr->hit_count > 0)
            health = (double)nbr->hit_count;

        /* out = edge weight (how strong this connection is) */
        double strength = (double)edge->weight;

        /* Normalize by pass/fail ratio if available */
        if (edge->passes + edge->fails > 0) {
            double ratio = (double)edge->passes /
                           (double)(edge->passes + edge->fails + 1);
            strength *= ratio;
        }

        out->obs[out->n_obs].in = health;
        out->obs[out->n_obs].out = strength;
        out->n_obs++;
    }

    return out->n_obs;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * Extract edge-pair observations: for each pair of edges
 * from the same node, the relationship between them.
 *
 * in  = weight of edge A
 * out = weight of edge B
 *
 * If the engine finds a pattern, it means edge weights
 * are structurally related — "if this edge is strong,
 * that edge is also strong" or "inversely proportional."
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int graph_extract_edge_pairs(
    const WireGraph *g, int center,
    GraphNeighborhood *out
) {
    /* First collect all edges from center */
    int edge_ids[GRAPH_MAX_OBS];
    int n_edges = 0;

    for (int e = 0; e < g->n_edges && n_edges < GRAPH_MAX_OBS; e++) {
        if (g->edges[e].src == center || g->edges[e].dst == center)
            edge_ids[n_edges++] = e;
    }

    out->n_obs = 0;
    out->center_node = center;
    strncpy(out->center_name, g->nodes[center].name, 127);

    /* Generate pairs */
    for (int i = 0; i < n_edges && out->n_obs < GRAPH_MAX_OBS; i++) {
        for (int j = i + 1; j < n_edges && out->n_obs < GRAPH_MAX_OBS; j++) {
            out->obs[out->n_obs].in = (double)g->edges[edge_ids[i]].weight;
            out->obs[out->n_obs].out = (double)g->edges[edge_ids[j]].weight;
            out->n_obs++;
        }
    }

    return out->n_obs;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * Extract graph diff: given two snapshots of the same
 * node's neighborhood, create (before, after) pairs.
 *
 * in  = edge weight BEFORE
 * out = edge weight AFTER
 *
 * The engine finds what transformation happened.
 * "All weights multiplied by 0.8" → decay pattern
 * "Failed edges dropped to 5" → crystallization
 * "New edges appeared at 128" → growth
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int graph_extract_diff(
    const GraphNeighborhood *before,
    const GraphNeighborhood *after,
    GraphNeighborhood *diff
) {
    diff->n_obs = 0;
    diff->center_node = before->center_node;
    strncpy(diff->center_name, before->center_name, 127);

    /* Match observations by position (same edge order) */
    int limit = before->n_obs < after->n_obs ? before->n_obs : after->n_obs;
    for (int i = 0; i < limit && diff->n_obs < GRAPH_MAX_OBS; i++) {
        diff->obs[diff->n_obs].in = before->obs[i].out;   /* before weight */
        diff->obs[diff->n_obs].out = after->obs[i].out;    /* after weight */
        diff->n_obs++;
    }

    return diff->n_obs;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * Convert GraphNeighborhood to the engine's System format.
 *
 * This is the bridge. After this, the engine's decompose()
 * function runs directly. The result is the pattern key.
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Forward declaration — the engine's types.
 * The actual System and decompose() are in onetwo_engine.c.
 * This adapter just prepares the data. */

typedef struct {
    double in;
    double out;
} OTObs;

typedef struct {
    OTObs obs[256];
    int   n;
    char  name[64];
} OTSystem;

static void graph_to_system(const GraphNeighborhood *gn, OTSystem *sys) {
    sys->n = gn->n_obs < 256 ? gn->n_obs : 256;
    snprintf(sys->name, 64, "wire:%s", gn->center_name);
    for (int i = 0; i < sys->n; i++) {
        sys->obs[i].in = gn->obs[i].in;
        sys->obs[i].out = gn->obs[i].out;
    }
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * Pattern entry — what the store holds after decomposition.
 *
 * Key: the engine's {op, params} output = the structural pattern
 * Value: what files changed + what the diff looked like
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    /* Key: engine decomposition result */
    int    op_type;        /* OpType from engine */
    double params[4];      /* substrate parameters */
    int    n_params;
    int    tier;           /* confidence */

    /* Value: the transformation that was applied */
    int    n_files_changed;
    char   files_changed[8][128];
    int    edge_deltas[8]; /* weight change per edge */

    /* Metadata */
    int    times_seen;     /* how many times this pattern matched */
    int    times_fixed;    /* how many times the fix worked */
} PatternEntry;

#define PATTERN_STORE_MAX 256

typedef struct {
    PatternEntry entries[PATTERN_STORE_MAX];
    int n_entries;
} PatternStore;

static void pattern_store_init(PatternStore *ps) {
    memset(ps, 0, sizeof(PatternStore));
}

/* Find a matching pattern by {op, params} */
static PatternEntry *pattern_store_find(
    PatternStore *ps, int op_type, const double *params, int n_params
) {
    for (int i = 0; i < ps->n_entries; i++) {
        PatternEntry *pe = &ps->entries[i];
        if (pe->op_type != op_type || pe->n_params != n_params) continue;

        /* Check params match within tolerance */
        int match = 1;
        for (int j = 0; j < n_params; j++) {
            if (fabs(pe->params[j] - params[j]) > 0.01) { match = 0; break; }
        }
        if (match) return pe;
    }
    return NULL;
}

/* Add or update a pattern */
static PatternEntry *pattern_store_add(
    PatternStore *ps, int op_type, const double *params, int n_params, int tier
) {
    PatternEntry *existing = pattern_store_find(ps, op_type, params, n_params);
    if (existing) {
        existing->times_seen++;
        return existing;
    }

    if (ps->n_entries >= PATTERN_STORE_MAX) return NULL;

    PatternEntry *pe = &ps->entries[ps->n_entries++];
    pe->op_type = op_type;
    memcpy(pe->params, params, n_params * sizeof(double));
    pe->n_params = n_params;
    pe->tier = tier;
    pe->times_seen = 1;
    pe->times_fixed = 0;

    return pe;
}

#endif /* ONETWO_GRAPH_ADAPTER_H */
/*
 * sense.h — Temporal feature extraction for PC engine
 *
 * Port of shared/sense.c for byte-stream input.
 * Detects: ACTIVE, REGULARITY, DUTY, CORRELATION, ASYMMETRY, BURST, PERIOD
 * Creates graph nodes + Hebbian edges in shell 0.
 *
 * Isaac Oravec & Claude, February 2026
 */
#ifndef SENSE_H
#define SENSE_H

#include "engine.h"

#define SENSE_CHANNELS   8
#define SENSE_MAX_EVENTS 1024

typedef struct {
    uint8_t  value;
    uint64_t timestamp;
} sense_event_t;

typedef struct {
    sense_event_t events[SENSE_MAX_EVENTS];
    uint32_t count;
} sense_buf_t;

/* ── Per-pass region descriptor ──────────────────────── */
#define SENSE_MAX_REGIONS 4

typedef struct {
    int offset;     /* byte offset into state_buf */
    int length;     /* byte count for this region */
    int pass_id;    /* 1-based pass number */
} sense_region_t;

/* Fill sense_buf from a raw byte buffer (timestamp = index) */
void sense_fill(sense_buf_t *buf, const uint8_t *data, int len);

/* Extract features, create graph nodes, Hebbian-wire co-occurring features */
void sense_extract(Engine *eng, sense_buf_t *buf, sense_result_t *result);

/* Decay edges of sense nodes not present in this pass */
void sense_decay(Engine *eng, const sense_result_t *result);

/* Observer feedback: GPU co-presence -> sense node valence */
void sense_feedback(Engine *eng, const sense_result_t *result);

/* Convenience: fill + extract + decay */
void sense_pass(Engine *eng, const uint8_t *data, int len, sense_result_t *result);

/* Extract features from a single region with pass_id suffix on names */
void sense_extract_region(Engine *eng, const uint8_t *data, int len,
                          int pass_id, sense_result_t *result);

/* Windowed: extract per-region, cross-wire, single decay */
void sense_pass_windowed(Engine *eng, const uint8_t *data,
                         const sense_region_t *regions, int n_regions,
                         sense_result_t *result);

#endif /* SENSE_H */
/*
 * sonify.h — Convert L-field topology to audio WAV files
 */
#ifndef SONIFY_H
#define SONIFY_H
#include "engine.h"

/* Generate chord (all knowledge) and dream (fading) WAV files
 * from the engine's current L-field topology.
 * Returns number of voices (nodes converted to tones). */
int sonify_engine(Engine *eng, const char *chord_path, const char *dream_path);

/* Generate a short audio snapshot (1 second) of the current L-field state.
 * Appends to an open WAV file. Call repeatedly during learning to build
 * a real-time audio trace of the engine's knowledge evolving. */
int sonify_snapshot(Engine *eng, FILE *wav_file, int *total_samples);

#endif
/*
 * tline.h — Transmission line edge library
 *
 * Shift-register delay line with per-cell loss and smoothing.
 * Replaces FDTD (which was unstable on short edges).
 *
 * Each cell applies: new = alpha * (incoming * atten) + (1-alpha) * old
 * where atten = 1 - (R + G*Lc[i]).
 *
 * R controls base loss, G*Lc controls per-cell variable loss.
 * Smoothing (alpha) creates frequency-dependent filtering:
 * slow signals propagate, fast signals get averaged out.
