 * That gradient IS Z.
 *
 * Isaac Oravec & Claude, March 2026
 */
#ifndef TLINE_H
#define TLINE_H

#include <stdint.h>

#define TLINE_MAX_CELLS 32
#define TLINE_ALPHA     0.5    /* smoothing: 0=frozen, 1=no smoothing */

typedef struct {
    double V[TLINE_MAX_CELLS];
    double Lc[TLINE_MAX_CELLS];   /* per-cell inductance (controls loss) */
    int    n_cells;                /* active cells (4-32) */
    double L_base;                 /* base inductance */
    double C0;                     /* capacitance per cell (unused by shift-reg, kept for compat) */
    double R;                      /* base loss per cell */
    double G;                      /* Lc-dependent loss per cell */
} TLine;

/* Initialize a transmission line with n_cells, base impedance z0 */
void tline_init(TLine *tl, int n_cells, double z0);

/* Inject a value at cell 0 (source end) — replaces, not additive */
void tline_inject(TLine *tl, double val);

/* Read the value at cell n-1 (destination end) */
double tline_read(const TLine *tl);

/* Read the value at any cell (for Z-depth observation) */
double tline_read_at(const TLine *tl, int cell);

/* One step: shift register with per-cell attenuation + smoothing */
void tline_step(TLine *tl);

/* Apply node impedance bump at boundary cells */
void tline_set_impedance(TLine *tl, double z_src, double z_dst);

/* Effective "weight" — product of per-cell attenuation.
 * Returns uint8_t so old code reading e->weight stays compatible. */
uint8_t tline_weight(const TLine *tl);

/* Back-reaction: collision energy grows Lc at boundary cells. */
void tline_backreaction(TLine *tl, double collision_energy);

/* Strengthen: decrease Lc by rate (less loss, stronger coupling) */
void tline_strengthen(TLine *tl, double rate);

/* Weaken: increase Lc by rate (more loss, weaker coupling) */
void tline_weaken(TLine *tl, double rate);

/* Initialize TLine from an old-format weight value (v12 backward compat).
 * Tunes R,G so tline_weight() returns approximately the given weight. */
void tline_init_from_weight(TLine *tl, uint8_t weight);

#endif /* TLINE_H */
/*
 * transducer.h — Input abstraction
 *
 * v3 analog->binary with auto-calibrating threshold.
 * Supports: stdin text, file, serial port, raw byte arrays.
 * Output: BitStream compatible with engine_ingest.
 *
 * Isaac Oravec & Claude, February 2026
 */

#ifndef TRANSDUCER_H
#define TRANSDUCER_H

#include "engine.h"

/* Transducer modes */
#define XDUCE_STDIN   0
#define XDUCE_FILE    1
#define XDUCE_SERIAL  2
#define XDUCE_RAW     3

typedef struct {
    int     mode;
    char    path[256];      /* file path or serial port (e.g. COM7) */
    int     baud;           /* serial baud rate */
    /* Auto-calibration state */
    int     threshold;      /* current binary threshold (0-255) */
    int     min_seen;       /* min value seen */
    int     max_seen;       /* max value seen */
    int     n_samples;      /* total samples for calibration */
    int64_t sum;            /* running sum for mean */
} Transducer;

/* Initialize transducer in given mode */
void transducer_init(Transducer *t, int mode, const char *path, int baud);

/* Read raw bytes from source. Returns bytes read, -1 on error. */
int transducer_read(Transducer *t, uint8_t *buf, int max_bytes);

/* Convert raw bytes to BitStream using auto-calibrated threshold.
 * Each byte above threshold -> 1, below -> 0. */
void transducer_to_bits(Transducer *t, const uint8_t *raw, int len, BitStream *out);

/* Full pipeline: read -> calibrate -> convert to BitStream */
int transducer_ingest(Transducer *t, BitStream *out);

/* Read a file and produce an ONETWO-encoded BitStream */
int transducer_file(const char *path, BitStream *out);

/* Read from stdin (one line) and produce a BitStream */
int transducer_stdin(BitStream *out);

#endif /* TRANSDUCER_H */
/* v3: Topological fix — same three-layer architecture as firmware */
/*
 * wire.h — Shared Hebbian Graph
 *
 * Included by both onetwo.c and xyzt_io.c.
 * Both tools read/write the same graph file.
 * Learning happens as a SIDE EFFECT of normal tool use.
 *
 * onetwo verify pass → strengthens edges (substrate)
 * xyzt_io store      → links file to current problem (substrate)
 * xyzt_io recall     → alive edges boost matches (topology)
 *
 * Three layers:
 *   SUBSTRATE: uint8_t weights, +W_LEARN / -W_LEARN. Arithmetic.
 *   THRESHOLD: WIRE_ALIVE. One crossing. One conversion.
 *   TOPOLOGY:  alive or not. Connected or not. No magnitudes.
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — February 2026
 */

#ifndef WIRE_H
#define WIRE_H

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <sys/stat.h>
#ifdef _WIN32
#include <direct.h>
#include <windows.h>
#include <io.h>
#define XYZT_MKDIR(d) _mkdir(d)
#else
#include <unistd.h>
#include <sys/file.h>
#define XYZT_MKDIR(d) mkdir(d, 0755)
#endif

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Constants
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* v3 (legacy) limits — used for binary migration only */
#define WIRE_V3_MAX_NODES  512
#define WIRE_V3_MAX_EDGES  4096

/* v4: engine-scale capacity */
#define WIRE_MAX_NODES   4096
#define WIRE_MAX_EDGES   65536
#define WIRE_NAME_LEN    128
#define WIRE_MAGIC       0x57495245   /* "WIRE" */
#define WIRE_VER         4

#define W_INIT    128   /* neutral starting weight */
#define W_LEARN   20    /* delta per learn event (substrate) */
#define W_MIN     5
#define W_MAX     250
#define WIRE_ALIVE 128  /* threshold: substrate → topology */

/* From engine.h — weight budget prevents runaway accumulation */
#define WIRE_MAX_NODE_WEIGHT  1024  /* total edge weight budget per node */
#define WIRE_PLASTICITY_DEFAULT 1.0f
#define WIRE_PLASTICITY_MIN     0.5f
#define WIRE_PLASTICITY_MAX     2.0f
#define WIRE_PLASTICITY_HEAT    0.01f  /* frustration: failed learns increase plasticity */
#define WIRE_PLASTICITY_COOL    0.005f /* boredom: successful learns decrease plasticity */

/* Node types */
#define WNODE_FILE     0
#define WNODE_PROBLEM  1
#define WNODE_CONCEPT  2

#define WIRE_DEFAULT_PATH ".xyzt/wire.bin"

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Structures
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* v3 node layout — for binary migration */
typedef struct {
    char     name[WIRE_NAME_LEN];
    uint8_t  type;
    uint8_t  _pad[3];
    uint32_t created;
    uint32_t last_active;
    uint32_t hit_count;
} WireNodeV3;

typedef struct {
    char     name[WIRE_NAME_LEN];
    uint8_t  type;
    uint8_t  layer_zero;  /* v4: all edges passes>0, fails==0 — proven */
    uint8_t  valence;     /* v4: count of alive edges (from engine.h) */
    uint8_t  _pad;
    uint32_t created;
    uint32_t last_active;
    uint32_t hit_count;
    float    plasticity;  /* v4: learning rate multiplier (0.5..2.0) */
    /* v4.1: coherence field — the shell's d_V_signed */
    float    energy;      /* total activity: sum of |hits| (V_energy analog) */
    float    signed_acc;  /* directional activity: +1 pass, -1 fail (V_signed analog) */
} WireNode;

/* Edge types — three projections of connection */
#define WEDGE_BEHAVIORAL  0   /* Hebbian: learned through verified co-occurrence */
#define WEDGE_STRUCTURAL  1   /* Static: file A imports/includes file B */
#define WEDGE_SEMANTIC    2   /* Cosine: embedding similarity above threshold */

/* v3 edge layout — 16 bytes, no edge_type */
typedef struct {
    uint16_t src;
    uint16_t dst;
    uint8_t  weight;
    uint8_t  passes;
    uint8_t  fails;
    uint8_t  stale;
    uint32_t created;
    uint32_t last_active;
} WireEdgeV3;

typedef struct {
    uint16_t src;
    uint16_t dst;
    uint8_t  weight;
    uint8_t  passes;
    uint8_t  fails;
    uint8_t  stale;       /* v2: 0=fresh, 1=stale (distinction non-persistence) */
    uint32_t created;
    uint32_t last_active;
    uint8_t  edge_type;   /* v4: WEDGE_BEHAVIORAL/STRUCTURAL/SEMANTIC */
    uint8_t  _epad[3];
} WireEdge;

/* v3 graph layout — for binary migration detection */
typedef struct {
    uint32_t    magic;
    uint8_t     version;
    uint8_t     _pad[3];
    uint32_t    n_nodes;
    uint32_t    n_edges;
    uint32_t    total_learns;
    WireNodeV3  nodes[WIRE_V3_MAX_NODES];
    WireEdgeV3  edges[WIRE_V3_MAX_EDGES];
    char        node_shells[WIRE_V3_MAX_NODES][32];
} WireGraphV3;

typedef struct {
    uint32_t  magic;
    uint8_t   version;
    uint8_t   _pad[3];
    uint32_t  n_nodes;
    uint32_t  n_edges;
    uint32_t  total_learns;
    WireNode  nodes[WIRE_MAX_NODES];
    WireEdge  edges[WIRE_MAX_EDGES];
    char      node_shells[WIRE_MAX_NODES][32];
} WireGraph;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   File locking — prevents graph corruption under
   concurrent access (e.g. hook + manual tool)
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int wire_lock(FILE *fp, int exclusive) {
#ifdef _WIN32
    HANDLE h = (HANDLE)_get_osfhandle(_fileno(fp));
    if (h == INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE) return -1;
    OVERLAPPED ov = {0};
    DWORD flags = exclusive ? LOCKFILE_EXCLUSIVE_LOCK : 0;
    flags |= LOCKFILE_FAIL_IMMEDIATELY;
    /* Try non-blocking first, then blocking */
    if (!LockFileEx(h, flags, 0, 0xFFFFFFFF, 0, &ov)) {
        flags &= ~LOCKFILE_FAIL_IMMEDIATELY;
        if (!LockFileEx(h, flags, 0, 0xFFFFFFFF, 0, &ov)) return -1;
    }
    return 0;
#else
    int op = exclusive ? LOCK_EX : LOCK_SH;
    return flock(fileno(fp), op);
#endif
}

static int wire_unlock(FILE *fp) {
#ifdef _WIN32
    HANDLE h = (HANDLE)_get_osfhandle(_fileno(fp));
    if (h == INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE) return -1;
    OVERLAPPED ov = {0};
    UnlockFileEx(h, 0, 0xFFFFFFFF, 0, &ov);
    return 0;
#else
    return flock(fileno(fp), LOCK_UN);
#endif
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Load / Save
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static const char *wire_path(void) {
    const char *env = getenv("XYZT_WIRE");
    return env ? env : WIRE_DEFAULT_PATH;
}

/* Recompute valence and layer_zero for all nodes (call after load, prune, migrate) */
static void wire_recompute_topology(WireGraph *g) {
    for (uint32_t ni = 0; ni < g->n_nodes; ni++) {
        int alive = 0, all_clean = 1;
        for (uint32_t ei = 0; ei < g->n_edges; ei++) {
            if (g->edges[ei].src == (uint16_t)ni || g->edges[ei].dst == (uint16_t)ni) {
                if (g->edges[ei].weight >= WIRE_ALIVE) {
                    alive++;
                    if (g->edges[ei].passes == 0 || g->edges[ei].fails > 0)
                        all_clean = 0;
                }
            }
        }
        g->nodes[ni].valence = alive > 255 ? 255 : (uint8_t)alive;
        g->nodes[ni].layer_zero = (alive > 0 && all_clean) ? 1 : 0;
    }
}

/* Migrate v3 graph → v4: expand capacity, widen nodes, set plasticity defaults */
static WireGraph *wire_migrate_v3(WireGraphV3 *old) {
    WireGraph *g = (WireGraph *)calloc(1, sizeof(WireGraph));
    g->magic = WIRE_MAGIC;
    g->version = WIRE_VER;
    g->n_nodes = old->n_nodes;
    g->n_edges = old->n_edges;
    g->total_learns = old->total_learns;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < old->n_nodes && i < WIRE_V3_MAX_NODES; i++) {
        strncpy(g->nodes[i].name, old->nodes[i].name, WIRE_NAME_LEN - 1);
        g->nodes[i].type = old->nodes[i].type;
        g->nodes[i].created = old->nodes[i].created;
        g->nodes[i].last_active = old->nodes[i].last_active;
        g->nodes[i].hit_count = old->nodes[i].hit_count;
        g->nodes[i].plasticity = WIRE_PLASTICITY_DEFAULT;
        g->nodes[i].layer_zero = 0;
        g->nodes[i].valence = 0;
        strncpy(g->node_shells[i], old->node_shells[i], 31);
    }

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < old->n_edges && i < WIRE_V3_MAX_EDGES; i++) {
        WireEdgeV3 *oe = &old->edges[i];
        WireEdge *ne = &g->edges[i];
        ne->src = oe->src; ne->dst = oe->dst;
        ne->weight = oe->weight; ne->passes = oe->passes;
        ne->fails = oe->fails; ne->stale = oe->stale;
        ne->created = oe->created; ne->last_active = oe->last_active;
        ne->edge_type = WEDGE_BEHAVIORAL;
        memset(ne->_epad, 0, 3);
    }

    wire_recompute_topology(g);
    return g;
}

static WireGraph *wire_load(void) {
    const char *path = wire_path();
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "rb");
    if (!fp) {
        WireGraph *g = (WireGraph *)calloc(1, sizeof(WireGraph));
        g->magic = WIRE_MAGIC; g->version = WIRE_VER;
        return g;
    }
    wire_lock(fp, 0);

    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_END);
    long fsize = ftell(fp);
    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_SET);

    /* Detect v3 by file size — v3 graph is much smaller than v4 */
    if (fsize > 0 && (size_t)fsize <= sizeof(WireGraphV3) + 64) {
        /* Likely v3 or earlier — try v3 migration */
        WireGraphV3 *old = (WireGraphV3 *)calloc(1, sizeof(WireGraphV3));
        size_t rd = (size_t)fsize < sizeof(WireGraphV3) ? (size_t)fsize : sizeof(WireGraphV3);
        if (fread(old, 1, rd, fp) >= 16 && old->magic == WIRE_MAGIC && old->version < 4) {
            wire_unlock(fp);
            fclose(fp);
            WireGraph *g = wire_migrate_v3(old);
            free(old);
            return g;
        }
        /* Not a valid v3 either — fall through to try v4 */
        free(old);
        fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_SET);
    }

    /* v4 load (or future versions) */
    WireGraph *g = (WireGraph *)calloc(1, sizeof(WireGraph));
    size_t to_read = (size_t)fsize;
    if (to_read > sizeof(WireGraph)) to_read = sizeof(WireGraph);
    if (fread(g, 1, to_read, fp) < 16 || g->magic != WIRE_MAGIC) {
        memset(g, 0, sizeof(WireGraph));
        g->magic = WIRE_MAGIC; g->version = WIRE_VER;
    }
    /* Clamp counts to prevent OOB from corrupted files */
    if (g->n_nodes > WIRE_MAX_NODES) g->n_nodes = WIRE_MAX_NODES;
    if (g->n_edges > WIRE_MAX_EDGES) g->n_edges = WIRE_MAX_EDGES;
    /* Set defaults for any nodes missing v4 fields */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_nodes; i++) {
        if (g->nodes[i].plasticity < WIRE_PLASTICITY_MIN)
            g->nodes[i].plasticity = WIRE_PLASTICITY_DEFAULT;
        /* v4.1: coherence fields — detect uninitialized (NaN, Inf, or absurd values) */
        if (g->nodes[i].energy < 0.0f || g->nodes[i].energy > 1e9f ||
            g->nodes[i].energy != g->nodes[i].energy) /* NaN check */
            g->nodes[i].energy = 0.0f;
        if (g->nodes[i].signed_acc < -1e9f || g->nodes[i].signed_acc > 1e9f ||
            g->nodes[i].signed_acc != g->nodes[i].signed_acc)
            g->nodes[i].signed_acc = 0.0f;
    }
    g->version = WIRE_VER;
    wire_recompute_topology(g);
    wire_unlock(fp);
    fclose(fp);
    return g;
}

static const char *xyzt_home_dir(void) {
    const char *h = getenv("HOME");
    if (!h) h = getenv("USERPROFILE");
    return h;
}

static void xyzt_ensure_dir(const char *path) {
    char dir[512];
    strncpy(dir, path, 511); dir[511] = '\0';
    char *last = strrchr(dir, '/');
    char *lastb = strrchr(dir, '\\');
    if (lastb && (!last || lastb > last)) last = lastb;
    if (!last) return;
    *last = '\0';

    /* Walk path, creating each component */
    for (char *p = dir + 1; *p; p++) {
        if (*p == '/' || *p == '\\') {
            char saved = *p;
            *p = '\0';
            XYZT_MKDIR(dir);
            *p = saved;
        }
    }
    XYZT_MKDIR(dir);
}

static void wire_save_global(WireGraph *local) {
    const char *home = xyzt_home_dir();
    if (!home) return;

    char gpath[512];
    snprintf(gpath, sizeof(gpath), "%s/.xyzt/wire.bin", home);

    /* Skip if local path IS global */
    if (strcmp(wire_path(), gpath) == 0) return;

    xyzt_ensure_dir(gpath);

    /* Load existing global graph — reuse wire_load via env override */
    WireGraph *g;
    {
#ifdef _WIN32
        char env_save[512] = {0};
        const char *old_env = getenv("XYZT_WIRE");
        if (old_env) strncpy(env_save, old_env, 511);
        char env_set[600];
        snprintf(env_set, sizeof(env_set), "XYZT_WIRE=%s", gpath);
        _putenv(env_set);
        g = wire_load();  /* handles all migration automatically */
        if (env_save[0]) {
            snprintf(env_set, sizeof(env_set), "XYZT_WIRE=%s", env_save);
            _putenv(env_set);
        } else {
            _putenv("XYZT_WIRE=");
        }
#else
        const char *old_env = getenv("XYZT_WIRE");
        setenv("XYZT_WIRE", gpath, 1);
        g = wire_load();
        if (old_env) setenv("XYZT_WIRE", old_env, 1);
        else unsetenv("XYZT_WIRE");
#endif
    }

    /* Map local node indices → global node indices */
    int nmap[WIRE_MAX_NODES];
    memset(nmap, -1, sizeof(nmap));

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < local->n_nodes; i++) {
        int gi = -1;
        for (uint32_t j = 0; j < g->n_nodes; j++) {
            if (strcmp(g->nodes[j].name, local->nodes[i].name) == 0) {
                gi = (int)j; break;
            }
        }
        if (gi < 0) {
            if (g->n_nodes >= WIRE_MAX_NODES) continue;
            gi = (int)g->n_nodes;
            g->nodes[g->n_nodes] = local->nodes[i];
            strncpy(g->node_shells[g->n_nodes], local->node_shells[i], 31);
            g->n_nodes++;
        } else {
            WireNode *gn = &g->nodes[gi];
            WireNode *ln = &local->nodes[i];
            if (ln->last_active > gn->last_active) gn->last_active = ln->last_active;
            if (ln->hit_count > gn->hit_count) gn->hit_count = ln->hit_count;
        }
        nmap[i] = gi;
    }

    /* Merge edges (match by remapped node names) */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < local->n_edges; i++) {
        int gs = nmap[local->edges[i].src];
        int gd = nmap[local->edges[i].dst];
        if (gs < 0 || gd < 0) continue;

        int ei = -1;
        for (uint32_t j = 0; j < g->n_edges; j++) {
            if (g->edges[j].src == (uint16_t)gs && g->edges[j].dst == (uint16_t)gd) {
                ei = (int)j; break;
            }
        }

        if (ei < 0) {
            if (g->n_edges >= WIRE_MAX_EDGES) continue;
            WireEdge e = local->edges[i];
            e.src = (uint16_t)gs;
            e.dst = (uint16_t)gd;
            g->edges[g->n_edges++] = e;
        } else {
            WireEdge *ge = &g->edges[ei];
            WireEdge *le = &local->edges[i];
            if (le->weight > ge->weight) ge->weight = le->weight;
            if (le->passes > ge->passes) ge->passes = le->passes;
            if (le->fails > ge->fails) ge->fails = le->fails;
            if (le->last_active > ge->last_active) ge->last_active = le->last_active;
        }
    }

    /* Save global */
    FILE *fp = fopen(gpath, "wb");
    if (fp) {
        fwrite(g, sizeof(WireGraph), 1, fp);
        fclose(fp);
    }
    free(g);
}

static void wire_save(WireGraph *g) {
    const char *path = wire_path();

    xyzt_ensure_dir(path);

    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "wb");
    if (!fp) return;
    wire_lock(fp, 1);  /* exclusive lock for writing */
    fwrite(g, sizeof(WireGraph), 1, fp);
    fflush(fp);
    wire_unlock(fp);
    fclose(fp);

    /* Dual-write: copy local graph to global (simple overwrite).
       The old merge approach is disabled — it crashed on v3→v4 migration
       with the new 20-byte edge struct. Simple copy is safe and correct:
       the local graph IS the authoritative state for this project. */
    {
        const char *home = xyzt_home_dir();
        if (home) {
            char gpath[512];
            snprintf(gpath, sizeof(gpath), "%s/.xyzt/wire.bin", home);
            if (strcmp(path, gpath) != 0) {
                xyzt_ensure_dir(gpath);
                FILE *gfp = fopen(gpath, "wb");
                if (gfp) { fwrite(g, sizeof(WireGraph), 1, gfp); fclose(gfp); }
            }
        }
    }
}

static void wire_free(WireGraph *g) { free(g); }

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Node ops
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int wire_find(WireGraph *g, const char *name) {
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_nodes; i++)
        if (strcmp(g->nodes[i].name, name) == 0) return (int)i;
    return -1;
}

/* Normalize file node names: strip directory, keep basename only.
   Prevents duplicate nodes like "wire.h" vs "E:\...\wire.h".
   Non-file nodes (problems, concepts) are stored as-is. */
static const char *wire_normalize_name(const char *name, uint8_t type) {
    if (type != WNODE_FILE) return name;
    const char *last = name;
    for (const char *p = name; *p; p++)
        if (*p == '\\' || *p == '/') last = p + 1;
    return (last[0]) ? last : name;
}

static int wire_add(WireGraph *g, const char *name, uint8_t type) {
    const char *norm = wire_normalize_name(name, type);
    /* Try normalized name first, then original (backward compat) */
    int id = wire_find(g, norm);
    if (id >= 0) return id;
    if (norm != name) {
        id = wire_find(g, name);
        if (id >= 0) {
            /* Migrate: rename fullpath node to basename */
            strncpy(g->nodes[id].name, norm, WIRE_NAME_LEN - 1);
            return id;
        }
    }
    if (g->n_nodes >= WIRE_MAX_NODES) return -1;
    id = g->n_nodes++;
    WireNode *n = &g->nodes[id];
    strncpy(n->name, norm, WIRE_NAME_LEN - 1);
    n->type = type;
    n->created = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    n->last_active = n->created;
    n->hit_count = 0;
    n->plasticity = WIRE_PLASTICITY_DEFAULT;
    n->valence = 0;
    n->layer_zero = 0;
    /* v2: shell identity */
    const char *shell = getenv("XYZT_SHELL_ID");
    if (!shell) shell = "claude-code";
    strncpy(g->node_shells[id], shell, 31);
    return id;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Edge ops
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int wire_find_edge(WireGraph *g, int src, int dst) {
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++)
        if (g->edges[i].src == src && g->edges[i].dst == dst) return (int)i;
    return -1;
}

static void wire_connect(WireGraph *g, int a, int b) {
    uint32_t ts = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    /* a → b (behavioral) */
    if (wire_find_edge(g, a, b) < 0 && g->n_edges < WIRE_MAX_EDGES) {
        WireEdge *e = &g->edges[g->n_edges++];
        memset(e, 0, sizeof(WireEdge));
        e->src = a; e->dst = b; e->weight = W_INIT;
        e->edge_type = WEDGE_BEHAVIORAL;
        e->created = ts; e->last_active = ts;
    }
    /* b → a (behavioral) */
    if (wire_find_edge(g, b, a) < 0 && g->n_edges < WIRE_MAX_EDGES) {
        WireEdge *e = &g->edges[g->n_edges++];
        memset(e, 0, sizeof(WireEdge));
        e->src = b; e->dst = a; e->weight = W_INIT;
        e->edge_type = WEDGE_BEHAVIORAL;
        e->created = ts; e->last_active = ts;
    }
    g->nodes[a].hit_count++;
    g->nodes[b].hit_count++;
    g->nodes[a].last_active = ts;
    g->nodes[b].last_active = ts;
}

/* Adjust all edges touching a node: Hebbian learning with plasticity.
   Plasticity modulates learning rate — frustrated nodes learn faster,
   stable nodes learn slower. From engine.h. */
static void wire_adjust(WireGraph *g, int node_id, int pass) {
    uint32_t ts = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    WireNode *n = &g->nodes[node_id];
    float plast = n->plasticity;
    if (plast < WIRE_PLASTICITY_MIN) plast = WIRE_PLASTICITY_DEFAULT;
    int delta = (int)(W_LEARN * plast);
    if (delta < 1) delta = 1;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++) {
        WireEdge *e = &g->edges[i];
        if ((int)e->src == node_id || (int)e->dst == node_id) {
            if (pass) {
                e->passes++;
                int nw = (int)e->weight + delta;
                e->weight = nw > W_MAX ? W_MAX : (uint8_t)nw;
                e->stale = 0;  /* learning clears staleness */
            } else {
                e->fails++;
                int nw = (int)e->weight - delta;
                e->weight = nw < W_MIN ? W_MIN : (uint8_t)nw;
            }
            e->last_active = ts;
        }
    }

    /* Update plasticity: frustration heats, success cools */
    if (pass) {
        n->plasticity -= WIRE_PLASTICITY_COOL;
        if (n->plasticity < WIRE_PLASTICITY_MIN) n->plasticity = WIRE_PLASTICITY_MIN;
    } else {
        n->plasticity += WIRE_PLASTICITY_HEAT;
        if (n->plasticity > WIRE_PLASTICITY_MAX) n->plasticity = WIRE_PLASTICITY_MAX;
    }

    /* Coherence field: accumulate directional signal.
       energy tracks total activity (always positive).
       signed_acc tracks direction (+1 pass, -1 fail).
       Ratio |signed_acc|/energy = coherence.
       High coherence = consistent outcomes (all pass or all fail).
       Low coherence = mixed outcomes (oscillating between pass/fail).
       Decay at 63/64 per learn event — same as engine accumulator. */
    n->energy = n->energy * (63.0f/64.0f) + 1.0f;
    n->signed_acc = n->signed_acc * (63.0f/64.0f) + (pass ? 1.0f : -1.0f);

    n->last_active = ts;
    g->total_learns++;

    /* Recompute valence and layer_zero for this node */
    int alive_count = 0;
    int all_clean = 1;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++) {
        if ((int)g->edges[i].src == node_id || (int)g->edges[i].dst == node_id) {
            if (g->edges[i].weight >= WIRE_ALIVE) {
                alive_count++;
                if (g->edges[i].passes == 0 || g->edges[i].fails > 0)
                    all_clean = 0;
            }
        }
    }
    n->valence = alive_count > 255 ? 255 : (uint8_t)alive_count;
    n->layer_zero = (alive_count > 0 && all_clean) ? 1 : 0;
}

/* Find edge by src, dst, AND type */
static int wire_find_edge_typed(WireGraph *g, int src, int dst, uint8_t etype) {
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++)
        if (g->edges[i].src == src && g->edges[i].dst == dst && g->edges[i].edge_type == etype)
            return (int)i;
    return -1;
}

/* Add a typed edge (won't duplicate if same src+dst+type exists) */
static void wire_connect_typed(WireGraph *g, int a, int b, uint8_t etype, uint8_t weight) {
    uint32_t ts = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    if (wire_find_edge_typed(g, a, b, etype) < 0 && g->n_edges < WIRE_MAX_EDGES) {
        WireEdge *e = &g->edges[g->n_edges++];
        memset(e, 0, sizeof(WireEdge));
        e->src = a; e->dst = b; e->weight = weight;
        e->edge_type = etype;
        e->created = ts; e->last_active = ts;
    }
}

/* Ingest structural dependencies from xyzt_map's map.bin.
   Creates WEDGE_STRUCTURAL edges between file nodes.
   These edges don't learn (no passes/fails) — they reflect
   static structure (imports, includes).
   Requires xyzt_state.h to be included by the caller. */
#ifdef XYZT_STATE_H
static int wire_ingest_map(WireGraph *g) {
    MapGraph *m = state_load_map();
    if (!m) return 0;

    /* Sanity check */
    if (m->n_files > MAP_MAX_FILES) m->n_files = MAP_MAX_FILES;
    if (m->n_deps > MAP_MAX_DEPS) m->n_deps = MAP_MAX_DEPS;

    /* Map file paths to wire nodes (heap — MAP_MAX_FILES could be large) */
    int *fmap = (int *)malloc(MAP_MAX_FILES * sizeof(int));
    if (!fmap) { free(m); return 0; }
    memset(fmap, -1, MAP_MAX_FILES * sizeof(int));

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < m->n_files; i++) {
        if (!m->files[i].path[0]) continue;
        const char *base = m->files[i].path;
        for (const char *p = base; *p; p++)
            if (*p == '/' || *p == '\\') base = p + 1;
        if (base[0]) fmap[i] = wire_add(g, base, WNODE_FILE);
    }

    /* Create structural edges */
    int added = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < m->n_deps; i++) {
        uint16_t si = m->deps[i].src, di = m->deps[i].dst;
        if (si >= m->n_files || di >= m->n_files) continue;
        int s = fmap[si], d = fmap[di];
        if (s < 0 || d < 0 || s == d) continue;
        wire_connect_typed(g, s, d, WEDGE_STRUCTURAL, W_INIT);
        added++;
    }

    free(fmap);
    free(m);
    return added;
}
#endif /* XYZT_STATE_H */

/* Node coherence: |signed_acc| / energy. 1.0 = perfectly consistent, 0.0 = random */
static float wire_node_coherence(const WireNode *n) {
    if (n->energy < 0.01f) return 0.0f;
    float c = n->signed_acc / n->energy;
    return c < 0 ? -c : c;
}

/* Node polarity: signed_acc / energy. +1.0 = all pass, -1.0 = all fail */
static float wire_node_polarity(const WireNode *n) {
    if (n->energy < 0.01f) return 0.0f;
    return n->signed_acc / n->energy;
}

/* Find resonant clusters: groups of nodes with similar coherence + polarity.
   Returns count of clusters found. Writes cluster assignments to `out_cluster[]`.
   Nodes in the same cluster have coherence within `threshold` of each other
   AND are connected by alive edges. */
static int wire_find_clusters(WireGraph *g, int *out_cluster, float threshold) {
    /* Initialize: each node is its own cluster */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_nodes; i++) out_cluster[i] = (int)i;

    /* Merge clusters: if two nodes are connected by an alive edge AND
       have similar coherence, they're in the same cluster */
    int changed = 1;
    while (changed) {
        changed = 0;
        for (uint32_t ei = 0; ei < g->n_edges; ei++) {
            WireEdge *e = &g->edges[ei];
            if (e->weight < WIRE_ALIVE) continue;
            int a = e->src, b = e->dst;
            if (out_cluster[a] == out_cluster[b]) continue;

            float ca = wire_node_coherence(&g->nodes[a]);
            float cb = wire_node_coherence(&g->nodes[b]);
            float pa = wire_node_polarity(&g->nodes[a]);
            float pb = wire_node_polarity(&g->nodes[b]);

            /* Same coherence level AND same polarity direction */
            float cdiff = ca - cb; if (cdiff < 0) cdiff = -cdiff;
            float pdiff = pa - pb; if (pdiff < 0) pdiff = -pdiff;

            if (cdiff < threshold && pdiff < threshold * 2) {
                /* Merge: lower cluster id wins */
                int old_c = out_cluster[a] > out_cluster[b] ? out_cluster[a] : out_cluster[b];
                int new_c = out_cluster[a] < out_cluster[b] ? out_cluster[a] : out_cluster[b];
                for (uint32_t j = 0; j < g->n_nodes; j++)
                    if (out_cluster[j] == old_c) out_cluster[j] = new_c;
                changed = 1;
            }
        }
    }

    /* Count distinct clusters */
    int n_clusters = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_nodes; i++) {
        int is_root = 1;
        for (uint32_t j = 0; j < i; j++)
            if (out_cluster[j] == out_cluster[i]) { is_root = 0; break; }
        if (is_root && g->nodes[i].energy > 0.01f) n_clusters++;
    }
    return n_clusters;
}

/* Mark all edges touching a file node as stale (distinction non-persistence) */
static void wire_mark_stale(const char *filename) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    const char *norm = wire_normalize_name(filename, WNODE_FILE);
    int fid = wire_find(g, norm);
    if (fid < 0) fid = wire_find(g, filename);  /* fallback: old fullpath */
    if (fid < 0) { wire_free(g); return; }
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++) {
        if ((int)g->edges[i].src == fid || (int)g->edges[i].dst == fid)
            g->edges[i].stale = 1;
    }
    wire_save(g);
    wire_free(g);
}

/* Prune dead edges and orphan nodes. Returns number removed.
   Inspired by engine.h apoptosis: nodes below threshold get culled.
   - Edges at W_MIN with 0 passes and older than max_age_days → removed
   - Nodes with 0 remaining edges and hit_count <= 1 → removed
   - Duplicate basename nodes get merged (edges redirected) */
static int wire_prune(WireGraph *g, int max_age_days) {
    uint32_t now = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    uint32_t age_cutoff = (max_age_days > 0) ? now - (uint32_t)(max_age_days * 86400) : 0;
    int removed = 0;

    /* Pass 1: merge duplicate file nodes (fullpath → basename) */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_nodes; i++) {
        if (g->nodes[i].type != WNODE_FILE) continue;
        const char *norm = wire_normalize_name(g->nodes[i].name, WNODE_FILE);
        if (norm == g->nodes[i].name) continue;  /* already basename */
        /* Check if basename node already exists */
        int base_id = -1;
        for (uint32_t j = 0; j < g->n_nodes; j++) {
            if (j == i) continue;
            if (strcmp(g->nodes[j].name, norm) == 0) { base_id = (int)j; break; }
        }
        if (base_id >= 0) {
            /* Redirect all edges from i → base_id */
            for (uint32_t ei = 0; ei < g->n_edges; ei++) {
                if (g->edges[ei].src == (uint16_t)i) g->edges[ei].src = (uint16_t)base_id;
                if (g->edges[ei].dst == (uint16_t)i) g->edges[ei].dst = (uint16_t)base_id;
            }
            /* Merge hit counts, keep newer timestamp */
            g->nodes[base_id].hit_count += g->nodes[i].hit_count;
            if (g->nodes[i].last_active > g->nodes[base_id].last_active)
                g->nodes[base_id].last_active = g->nodes[i].last_active;
            /* Mark old node for deletion */
            g->nodes[i].name[0] = 0;
            removed++;
        } else {
            /* No duplicate — just rename in place */
            strncpy(g->nodes[i].name, norm, WIRE_NAME_LEN - 1);
        }
    }

    /* Pass 2: remove dead edges */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; ) {
        WireEdge *e = &g->edges[i];
        int dead = 0;
        /* Self-loops from merge */
        if (e->src == e->dst) dead = 1;
        /* Duplicate edges from merge */
        if (!dead) {
            for (uint32_t j = 0; j < i; j++) {
                if (g->edges[j].src == e->src && g->edges[j].dst == e->dst) {
                    /* Keep the stronger edge */
                    if (e->weight > g->edges[j].weight) g->edges[j] = *e;
                    dead = 1; break;
                }
            }
        }
        /* Bottom-weight edges with no passes, old enough → dead */
        if (!dead && e->weight <= W_MIN && e->passes == 0 &&
            age_cutoff > 0 && e->last_active < age_cutoff) {
            dead = 1;
        }
        if (dead) {
            g->edges[i] = g->edges[--g->n_edges];
            removed++;
        } else {
            i++;
        }
    }

    /* Pass 3: remove orphan nodes (no edges, low hit count) */
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_nodes; ) {
        if (g->nodes[i].name[0] == 0) {
            /* Already marked dead from merge */
            g->nodes[i] = g->nodes[--g->n_nodes];
            /* Fix edge references to moved node */
            for (uint32_t ei = 0; ei < g->n_edges; ei++) {
                if (g->edges[ei].src == (uint16_t)g->n_nodes) g->edges[ei].src = (uint16_t)i;
                if (g->edges[ei].dst == (uint16_t)g->n_nodes) g->edges[ei].dst = (uint16_t)i;
            }
            /* Also copy shell id */
            memcpy(g->node_shells[i], g->node_shells[g->n_nodes], 32);
            continue;  /* recheck this slot */
        }
        /* Check if node has any edges */
        int has_edge = 0;
        for (uint32_t ei = 0; ei < g->n_edges; ei++) {
            if (g->edges[ei].src == (uint16_t)i || g->edges[ei].dst == (uint16_t)i) {
                has_edge = 1; break;
            }
        }
        if (!has_edge && g->nodes[i].hit_count <= 1 &&
            age_cutoff > 0 && g->nodes[i].last_active < age_cutoff) {
            g->nodes[i] = g->nodes[--g->n_nodes];
            for (uint32_t ei = 0; ei < g->n_edges; ei++) {
                if (g->edges[ei].src == (uint16_t)g->n_nodes) g->edges[ei].src = (uint16_t)i;
                if (g->edges[ei].dst == (uint16_t)g->n_nodes) g->edges[ei].dst = (uint16_t)i;
            }
            memcpy(g->node_shells[i], g->node_shells[g->n_nodes], 32);
            removed++;
            continue;
        }
        i++;
    }

    return removed;
}

/* TOPOLOGY: is the edge between two nodes alive? (binary) */
static int wire_alive(WireGraph *g, int src, int dst) {
    int eid = wire_find_edge(g, src, dst);
    if (eid < 0) return 0;
    return g->edges[eid].weight >= WIRE_ALIVE ? 1 : 0;
}

/* SUBSTRATE: raw edge weight (for telemetry/debugging only) */
static int wire_weight(WireGraph *g, int src, int dst) {
    int eid = wire_find_edge(g, src, dst);
    if (eid < 0) return 0;
    return g->edges[eid].weight;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Cross-tool helpers
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Read current problem from onetwo session file.
 * Layout: magic(4) + ver(1) + phase(1) + pad(2) + created(4) + modified(4) = 16,
 * then char problem[256]. */
#define ONETWO_DEFAULT_PATH ".xyzt/onetwo_session.bin"

static int wire_read_problem(char *out, int maxlen) {
    const char *opath = getenv("ONETWO_SESSION");
    if (!opath) opath = ONETWO_DEFAULT_PATH;
    FILE *fp = fopen(opath, "rb");
    if (!fp) { out[0] = '\0'; return -1; }
    fseek(fp, 16, SEEK_SET);
    char buf[256] = {0};
    size_t r = fread(buf, 1, 256, fp);
    fclose(fp);
    if (r < 1 || buf[0] == '\0') { out[0] = '\0'; return -1; }
    strncpy(out, buf, maxlen - 1);
    out[maxlen - 1] = '\0';
    return 0;
}

/* ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   AUTO-LINK: call from xyzt_io store
   Links a file to the current onetwo problem.
   Returns 1 if linked, 0 if no active problem.
   ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int wire_auto_link(const char *filename) {
    char problem[256];
    if (wire_read_problem(problem, 256) < 0) return 0;

    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int file_id = wire_add(g, filename, WNODE_FILE);
    int prob_id = wire_add(g, problem, WNODE_PROBLEM);
    if (file_id >= 0 && prob_id >= 0)
        wire_connect(g, file_id, prob_id);
    wire_save(g);
    wire_free(g);
    return 1;
}

/* ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   AUTO-LEARN: call from onetwo verify
   Adjusts weights for the current problem.
   Returns number of edges affected.
   ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int wire_auto_learn(const char *problem, int pass) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int prob_id = wire_find(g, problem);
    if (prob_id < 0) { wire_free(g); return 0; }

    int affected = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++) {
        if ((int)g->edges[i].src == prob_id || (int)g->edges[i].dst == prob_id)
            affected++;
    }

    wire_adjust(g, prob_id, pass);
    wire_save(g);
    wire_free(g);
    return affected;
}

/* ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   TOPOLOGY BOOST: call from xyzt_io recall
   Returns 1 if file is alive-connected to current problem.
   0 if not connected, not alive, or stale.
   Binary. Connected or not. No magnitudes.
   ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int wire_boost(const char *filename) {
    char problem[256];
    if (wire_read_problem(problem, 256) < 0) return 0;

    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int file_id = wire_find(g, filename);
    int prob_id = wire_find(g, problem);
    if (file_id < 0 || prob_id < 0) { wire_free(g); return 0; }

    int eid = wire_find_edge(g, file_id, prob_id);
    if (eid < 0) { wire_free(g); return 0; }

    int alive = g->edges[eid].weight >= WIRE_ALIVE ? 1 : 0;
    int stale = g->edges[eid].stale;
    wire_free(g);

    /* Alive and fresh = connected. That's it.
     * Stale edges are dead — distinction non-persistence. */
    if (stale) return 0;
    return alive;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Query helpers — used by hooks, xyzt_io brief, xyzt_next
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define WIRE_MAX_CONN 32

typedef struct {
    char     name[WIRE_NAME_LEN];
    uint8_t  alive;      /* TOPOLOGY: 1 = connected, 0 = not */
    uint8_t  type;       /* WNODE_FILE / WNODE_PROBLEM / WNODE_CONCEPT */
    uint8_t  stale;
    uint8_t  passes;
    uint8_t  fails;
    uint8_t  weight;     /* SUBSTRATE: for telemetry/debug only */
} WireConn;

/* Returns connections for a named node. Deduplicates peers.
   Caller provides output array. Returns count of connections found. */
static int wire_query_connections(const char *name, WireConn *out, int max_out) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int id = wire_find(g, name);
    if (id < 0) { wire_free(g); return 0; }

    int count = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges && count < max_out; i++) {
        WireEdge *e = &g->edges[i];
        int peer = -1;
        if ((int)e->src == id) peer = (int)e->dst;
        else if ((int)e->dst == id) peer = (int)e->src;
        if (peer < 0) continue;

        /* Deduplicate */
        int dup = 0;
        for (int j = 0; j < count; j++) {
            if (strcmp(out[j].name, g->nodes[peer].name) == 0) { dup = 1; break; }
        }
        if (dup) continue;

        WireConn *c = &out[count++];
        strncpy(c->name, g->nodes[peer].name, WIRE_NAME_LEN - 1);
        c->name[WIRE_NAME_LEN - 1] = '\0';
        c->alive = (e->weight >= WIRE_ALIVE && !e->stale) ? 1 : 0;
        c->type = g->nodes[peer].type;
        c->stale = e->stale;
        c->passes = e->passes;
        c->fails = e->fails;
        c->weight = e->weight;  /* substrate: for debug display only */
    }
    wire_free(g);
    return count;
}

/* Count stale edges for a node without full connection query */
static int wire_count_stale(const char *name) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int id = wire_find(g, name);
    if (id < 0) { wire_free(g); return 0; }
    int stale = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++) {
        if (((int)g->edges[i].src == id || (int)g->edges[i].dst == id) && g->edges[i].stale)
            stale++;
    }
    wire_free(g);
    return stale;
}

#endif /* WIRE_H */
/*
 * xyzt_build.h — Persistent autonomous build system
 *
 * Not a CI/CD pipeline. A self-directing build loop that:
 * - Plans what to build from the current state
 * - Dispatches parallel agents for independent tasks
 * - Tests results, learns pass/fail into wire
 * - Locks proven subsystems as bedrock
 * - Continues until the product is complete
 *
 * The intelligence is in the loop, not in any single agent.
 * Each agent is cheap (local Qwen 8B). The verification is expensive
 * (full test suite). The wire graph accumulates what works.
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — March 2026
 */

#ifndef XYZT_BUILD_H
#define XYZT_BUILD_H

#include <stdint.h>
#include <string.h>
#include "xyzt_tools.h"

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Task graph — dependency-aware parallel dispatch
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define MAX_TASKS   64
#define TASK_LEN    512

typedef enum {
    TASK_PENDING,
    TASK_RUNNING,
    TASK_PASS,
    TASK_FAIL,
    TASK_LOCKED    /* bedrock — proven, don't touch */
} TaskStatus;

typedef enum {
    AGENT_LOCAL,   /* Qwen 8B via Ollama — free, fast, grunt work */
    AGENT_CLOUD,   /* Claude via API — expensive, architecture/design */
    AGENT_ENGINE,  /* GPU daemon — structural queries, no language */
    AGENT_SELF     /* this terminal — for tasks I can do inline */
} AgentType;

typedef struct {
    int         id;
    char        description[TASK_LEN];
    char        file_target[256];    /* which file this task modifies */
    TaskStatus  status;
    AgentType   agent;
    int         depends_on[8];       /* task IDs that must complete first */
    int         n_deps;
    int         attempts;            /* how many times tried */
    float       wire_weight;         /* edge weight from wire graph */
} BuildTask;

typedef struct {
    BuildTask tasks[MAX_TASKS];
    int       n_tasks;
    int       completed;
    int       failed;
    int       locked;
    char      product_name[256];
} BuildPlan;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Plan generation — what needs to be built?
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Generate a build plan from the current state.
   Reads wire graph for what's connected, onetwo for what's claimed,
   identity for what's been discovered. Outputs independent tasks
   that can run in parallel. */
static int build_plan_generate(BuildPlan *plan, const char *goal) {
    memset(plan, 0, sizeof(BuildPlan));
    strncpy(plan->product_name, goal, 255);

    /* TODO: read wire graph to find which files need work.
       Stale edges = files changed since last verify = need re-test.
       Weak edges = uncertain connections = need investigation.
       Missing edges = new files not yet connected = need integration.

       For now, return a placeholder plan. The real implementation
       would query the wire and onetwo state to auto-generate tasks. */

    return plan->n_tasks;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Task dispatch — parallel agent execution
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Find all tasks that are ready to run (pending + all deps completed) */
static int build_find_ready(const BuildPlan *plan, int *ready, int max) {
    int n = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < plan->n_tasks && n < max; i++) {
        if (plan->tasks[i].status != TASK_PENDING) continue;
        int blocked = 0;
        for (int d = 0; d < plan->tasks[i].n_deps; d++) {
            int dep_id = plan->tasks[i].depends_on[d];
            if (dep_id >= 0 && dep_id < plan->n_tasks) {
                TaskStatus ds = plan->tasks[dep_id].status;
                if (ds != TASK_PASS && ds != TASK_LOCKED) {
                    blocked = 1;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        if (!blocked) ready[n++] = i;
    }
    return n;
}

/* Dispatch a task to an agent. Returns process ID or handle.
   For local agents: spawns xyzt_agent via Ollama.
   For cloud agents: sends API request.
   For engine: queries daemon via shared memory.
   For self: executes inline. */
typedef struct {
    int    task_id;
    int    pid;        /* process ID for parallel agents */
    char   result[4096];
    int    success;
} AgentResult;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Crystallization — lock proven subsystems
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Check if a file's wire edges are all PROVEN (all passes, zero fails).
   If so, lock the task — it's bedrock. Don't modify. */
static int build_check_bedrock(const char *file) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int fid = wire_find(g, file);
    if (fid < 0) { wire_free(g); return 0; }

    int all_proven = 1;
    int has_edges = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges; i++) {
        if ((int)g->edges[i].src == fid || (int)g->edges[i].dst == fid) {
            has_edges = 1;
            if (g->edges[i].passes == 0 || g->edges[i].fails > 0) {
                all_proven = 0;
                break;
            }
        }
    }
    wire_free(g);
    return has_edges && all_proven;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Build loop — the persistent builder
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Run one cycle of the build loop:
   1. Find ready tasks (no unmet dependencies)
   2. Dispatch to appropriate agents
   3. Wait for results
   4. Update wire graph with pass/fail
   5. Check for new bedrock (crystallization)
   6. Generate new tasks from failures
   Returns number of tasks completed this cycle. */
static int build_cycle(BuildPlan *plan) {
    int ready[MAX_TASKS];
    int n_ready = build_find_ready(plan, ready, MAX_TASKS);
    if (n_ready == 0) return 0;

    int completed = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n_ready; i++) {
        BuildTask *t = &plan->tasks[ready[i]];
        t->status = TASK_RUNNING;
        t->attempts++;

        /* TODO: dispatch to agent based on t->agent type.
           For now, mark as pass (placeholder).
           Real implementation would:
           - AGENT_LOCAL: spawn xyzt_agent --role builder --task "..." --allow-write
           - AGENT_CLOUD: send API request with task description
           - AGENT_ENGINE: query daemon for structural resonance
           - AGENT_SELF: execute inline */

        /* After agent returns, check result */
        t->status = TASK_PASS; /* placeholder */
        plan->completed++;
        completed++;

        /* Wire learn: strengthen edges for passing file */
        if (t->file_target[0]) {
            xyzt_wire_learn("build", t->file_target, t->status == TASK_PASS);
        }

        /* Check crystallization */
        if (t->file_target[0] && build_check_bedrock(t->file_target)) {
            t->status = TASK_LOCKED;
            plan->locked++;
        }
    }

    return completed;
}

#endif /* XYZT_BUILD_H */
/*
 * xyzt_state.h — Shared structs + loaders for all .xyzt/ state files
 *
 * Any tool that includes this can read any state file in one call.
 * All loaders return heap-allocated structs (caller frees) or NULL.
 * Read-only — tools that OWN a state file do their own saves.
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — March 2026
 */

#ifndef XYZT_STATE_H
#define XYZT_STATE_H

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stddef.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   onetwo session layout
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define OT_MAGIC       0x4F4E4532  /* "ONE2" */
#define OT_MAX_TEXT    256
#define OT_MAX_KNOWNS  64
#define OT_MAX_UNKNOWNS 64
#define OT_MAX_PROBES  64
#define OT_MAX_CLAIMS  64
#define OT_MAX_LOG     256

typedef struct { char text[OT_MAX_TEXT]; uint32_t timestamp; } OTEntry;

typedef struct {
    char text[OT_MAX_TEXT]; uint8_t tier; uint8_t verified; uint8_t _pad[2]; uint32_t timestamp;
} OTClaim;

typedef struct {
    char text[OT_MAX_TEXT]; uint32_t timestamp; uint8_t type; uint8_t _pad[3];
} OTLogEntry;

typedef struct {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint8_t  version;
    uint8_t  phase;
    uint8_t  _pad[2];
    uint32_t created;
    uint32_t modified;
    char     problem[OT_MAX_TEXT];
    OTEntry  knowns[OT_MAX_KNOWNS];
    uint32_t n_knowns;
    OTEntry  unknowns[OT_MAX_UNKNOWNS];
    uint32_t n_unknowns;
    OTEntry  probes[OT_MAX_PROBES];
    uint32_t n_probes;
    OTClaim  claims[OT_MAX_CLAIMS];
    uint32_t n_claims;
    OTLogEntry log[OT_MAX_LOG];
    uint32_t n_log;
    /* v2 extensions */
    char claim_verifiers[OT_MAX_CLAIMS][32];
} OTSession;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   xyzt_io fingerprint / session layout
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define IO_MAGIC       0x58595A54  /* "XYZT" */
#define IO_MAX         512
#define IO_BS_WORDS    128

typedef struct { uint64_t w[IO_BS_WORDS]; int len; } IOBitStream;

typedef struct {
    IOBitStream bs;
    char      name[256];
    size_t    file_size;
    double    entropy;
    int       is_text;
    int       line_count;
    int       unique_bytes;
    int       token_count;
    double    avg_line_len;
    char      domain[64];
    int       domain_score;
    /* v2 extensions */
    uint32_t  file_hash;
    uint32_t  mismatches;
    uint32_t  stores;
} IOFingerprint;

/* v1 fingerprint size (without v2 extensions) */
#define IO_V1_FP_SIZE  offsetof(IOFingerprint, file_hash)

typedef struct {
    uint32_t magic; uint8_t version; uint8_t _pad[3]; uint32_t count; uint32_t _reserved;
} IOHeader;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   xyzt_map layout
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define MAP_MAGIC      0x4D415000
#define MAP_MAX_FILES  1024
#define MAP_MAX_DEPS   8192
#define MAP_PATH_LEN   256

typedef struct {
    char     path[MAP_PATH_LEN];
    char     lang[16];
    uint32_t lines;
    uint32_t deps_out;
    uint32_t deps_in;
} MapFile;

typedef struct { uint16_t src; uint16_t dst; } MapDep;

typedef struct {
    uint32_t magic; uint32_t n_files; uint32_t n_deps;
    MapFile files[MAP_MAX_FILES]; MapDep deps[MAP_MAX_DEPS];
} MapGraph;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   xyzt_test history layout
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define TEST_MAGIC     0x54455354  /* "TEST" */
#define TEST_MAX_ERR   128
#define TEST_MAX_HIST  64
#define TEST_CMD_LEN   1024
#define TEST_MSG_LEN   256
#define TEST_PATH_LEN  256

typedef struct {
    char     file[TEST_PATH_LEN];
    uint32_t line;
    char     severity;     /* 'E' error, 'W' warning, 'N' note */
    char     message[TEST_MSG_LEN];
} TestError;

typedef struct {
    char     cmd[TEST_CMD_LEN];
    int32_t  exit_code;
    uint32_t timestamp;
    float    duration;
    uint32_t n_errors;
    uint32_t stdout_lines;
    uint32_t stderr_lines;
    TestError errors[TEST_MAX_ERR];
} TestResult;

typedef struct {
    uint32_t   magic;
    uint32_t   count;
    TestResult results[TEST_MAX_HIST];
} TestHistory;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   xyzt_err layout
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define ERR_MAGIC      0x45525230  /* "ERR0" */
#define ERR_MAX        512
#define ERR_MSG_LEN    256
#define ERR_PATH_LEN   256

typedef struct {
    char     file[ERR_PATH_LEN];
    uint32_t line;
    char     message[ERR_MSG_LEN];
    uint32_t timestamp;
    uint8_t  resolved;
    uint8_t  _pad[3];
} ErrEntry;

typedef struct {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint32_t version;
    uint32_t count;
    uint32_t total_logged;
    ErrEntry entries[ERR_MAX];
} ErrStore;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Loaders — one-call read for any state file
   All return heap-allocated (caller frees) or NULL.
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static inline OTSession *state_load_onetwo(void) {
    const char *p = getenv("ONETWO_SESSION");
    if (!p) p = ".xyzt/onetwo_session.bin";
    FILE *fp = fopen(p, "rb");
    if (!fp) return NULL;
    OTSession *s = (OTSession *)calloc(1, sizeof(OTSession));
    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_END);
    long sz = ftell(fp);
    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_SET);
    size_t rd = (size_t)sz < sizeof(OTSession) ? (size_t)sz : sizeof(OTSession);
    if (fread(s, 1, rd, fp) < 16 || s->magic != OT_MAGIC) {
        free(s); fclose(fp); return NULL;
    }
    fclose(fp);
    return s;
}

static inline IOHeader state_load_io_header(FILE **fp_out) {
    IOHeader hdr = {0};
    const char *p = getenv("XYZT_SESSION");
    if (!p) p = ".xyzt/session.bin";
    FILE *fp = fopen(p, "rb");
    if (!fp) { *fp_out = NULL; return hdr; }
    if (fread(&hdr, sizeof(IOHeader), 1, fp) != 1 || hdr.magic != IO_MAGIC) {
        fclose(fp); *fp_out = NULL; hdr.magic = 0; return hdr;
    }
    *fp_out = fp;  /* caller reads entries then fcloses */
    return hdr;
}

static inline int state_load_io_entry(FILE *fp, const IOHeader *hdr, IOFingerprint *out) {
    size_t sz = (hdr->version >= 2) ? sizeof(IOFingerprint) : IO_V1_FP_SIZE;
    memset(out, 0, sizeof(IOFingerprint));
    return fread(out, sz, 1, fp) == 1;
}

static inline TestHistory *state_load_tests(void) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(".xyzt/test_history.bin", "rb");
    if (!fp) return NULL;
    TestHistory *h = (TestHistory *)calloc(1, sizeof(TestHistory));
    if (fread(h, sizeof(TestHistory), 1, fp) != 1 || h->magic != TEST_MAGIC) {
        free(h); fclose(fp); return NULL;
    }
    fclose(fp);
    return h;
}

static inline MapGraph *state_load_map(void) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(".xyzt/map.bin", "rb");
    if (!fp) return NULL;
    MapGraph *m = (MapGraph *)calloc(1, sizeof(MapGraph));
    if (fread(m, sizeof(MapGraph), 1, fp) != 1 || m->magic != MAP_MAGIC) {
        free(m); fclose(fp); return NULL;
    }
    fclose(fp);
    return m;
}

static inline ErrStore *state_load_errors(void) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(".xyzt/errors.bin", "rb");
    if (!fp) return NULL;
    ErrStore *e = (ErrStore *)calloc(1, sizeof(ErrStore));
    if (fread(e, sizeof(ErrStore), 1, fp) != 1 || e->magic != ERR_MAGIC) {
        free(e); fclose(fp); return NULL;
    }
    fclose(fp);
    return e;
}

#endif /* XYZT_STATE_H */
/*
 * xyzt_tools.h — All toolkit capabilities as inline functions
 *
 * This is CC's brain. Every capability compiled into one header.
 * The terminal includes this and has EVERYTHING — no subprocess,
 * no shelling out, no separate executables.
 *
 * Tools: file ops, wire graph, identity, onetwo, resume,
 *        skeleton, replay, fingerprint, signal caster.
 *
 * Isaac & Claude — March 2026
 */

#ifndef XYZT_TOOLS_H
#define XYZT_TOOLS_H

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>

/* Include the binary state headers */
#include "xyzt_state.h"
#include "wire.h"

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   File operations — direct syscalls, no Node.js
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static char *xyzt_read_file(const char *path, int *out_len) {
    FILE *f = fopen(path, "rb");
    if (!f) return NULL;
    fseek(f, 0, SEEK_END);
    long sz = ftell(f);
    fseek(f, 0, SEEK_SET);
    if (sz > 1024 * 1024) sz = 1024 * 1024; /* 1MB cap */
    char *buf = (char *)malloc(sz + 1);
    if (!buf) { fclose(f); return NULL; }
    size_t rd = fread(buf, 1, sz, f);
    buf[rd] = 0;
    if (out_len) *out_len = (int)rd;
    fclose(f);
    return buf;
}

static int xyzt_write_file(const char *path, const char *content, int len) {
    FILE *f = fopen(path, "wb");
    if (!f) return -1;
    if (len <= 0) len = (int)strlen(content);
    fwrite(content, 1, len, f);
    fclose(f);
    return 0;
}

static char *xyzt_run_cmd(const char *cmd, int *exit_code) {
    char *out = (char *)calloc(65536, 1);
    if (!out) return NULL;
#ifdef _WIN32
    char cmdline[8192];
    snprintf(cmdline, sizeof(cmdline), "cmd.exe /c \"%s\"", cmd);
    HANDLE hRead, hWrite;
    SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES sa = { sizeof(sa), NULL, TRUE };
    CreatePipe(&hRead, &hWrite, &sa, 0);
    SetHandleInformation(hRead, HANDLE_FLAG_INHERIT, 0);
    STARTUPINFOA si = {0};
    PROCESS_INFORMATION pi = {0};
    si.cb = sizeof(si);
    si.dwFlags = STARTF_USESTDHANDLES;
    si.hStdOutput = hWrite; si.hStdError = hWrite;
    BOOL ok = CreateProcessA(NULL, cmdline, NULL, NULL, TRUE,
                             CREATE_NO_WINDOW, NULL, NULL, &si, &pi);
    CloseHandle(hWrite);
    if (!ok) { CloseHandle(hRead); free(out); return strdup("ERROR"); }
    DWORD n; int total = 0;
    while (total < 65535) {
        if (!ReadFile(hRead, out + total, 65535 - total, &n, NULL) || n == 0) break;
        total += n;
    }
    out[total] = 0;
    WaitForSingleObject(pi.hProcess, 30000);
    DWORD ec = 1; GetExitCodeProcess(pi.hProcess, &ec);
    if (exit_code) *exit_code = (int)ec;
    CloseHandle(pi.hProcess); CloseHandle(pi.hThread); CloseHandle(hRead);
#else
    FILE *fp = popen(cmd, "r");
    if (!fp) { free(out); return strdup("ERROR"); }
    int total = 0;
    while (total < 65535) { int c = fgetc(fp); if (c == EOF) break; out[total++] = (char)c; }
    out[total] = 0;
    int ec = pclose(fp);
    if (exit_code) *exit_code = ec;
#endif
    return out;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Identity — read/write from binary, compact output
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define ID_MAGIC       0x49444E54
#define ID_MAX_ENTRIES 512
#define ID_TEXT_LEN    512
#define ID_TAG_LEN     64
#define ID_SHELL_LEN   32

#define ITYPE_HYPOTHESIS  0
#define ITYPE_CORRECTION  1
#define ITYPE_QUESTION    2
#define ITYPE_DISCOVERY   3
#define ITYPE_PREFERENCE  4
#define ITYPE_RESONANCE   5

typedef struct {
    uint8_t  type; uint8_t confidence; uint8_t superseded; uint8_t _pad;
    uint32_t timestamp;
    char     shell[ID_SHELL_LEN];
    char     tag[ID_TAG_LEN];
    char     text[ID_TEXT_LEN];
} XIdEntry;

typedef struct {
    uint32_t magic; uint8_t version; uint8_t _pad[3];
    uint32_t n_entries; uint32_t total_written;
    char     primary_shell[ID_SHELL_LEN];
    XIdEntry entries[ID_MAX_ENTRIES];
} XIdState;

static XIdState *xyzt_id_load(void) {
    XIdState *s = (XIdState *)calloc(1, sizeof(XIdState));
    FILE *fp = fopen(".xyzt/identity.bin", "rb");
    if (!fp) { s->magic = ID_MAGIC; s->version = 1; return s; }
    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_END); long sz = ftell(fp); fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_SET);
    size_t rd = (size_t)sz < sizeof(XIdState) ? (size_t)sz : sizeof(XIdState);
    if (fread(s, 1, rd, fp) < 16 || s->magic != ID_MAGIC) {
        memset(s, 0, sizeof(XIdState)); s->magic = ID_MAGIC; s->version = 1;
    }
    fclose(fp);
    return s;
}

static void xyzt_id_save(XIdState *s) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(".xyzt/identity.bin", "wb");
    if (fp) { fwrite(s, sizeof(XIdState), 1, fp); fclose(fp); }
}

/* Compact token output: TYPE:CONFIDENCE:TAG|TYPE:CONFIDENCE:TAG|... */
static char *xyzt_id_tokens(void) {
    XIdState *s = xyzt_id_load();
    static const char TYPE_TAG[] = "HCQDPR";
    char *out = (char *)calloc(8192, 1);
    int pos = 0, first = 1;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < s->n_entries && i < ID_MAX_ENTRIES; i++) {
        if (s->entries[i].superseded) continue;
        if (!first && pos < 8190) out[pos++] = '|';
        first = 0;
        pos += snprintf(out + pos, 8192 - pos, "%c:%d:%s",
                        s->entries[i].type <= 5 ? TYPE_TAG[s->entries[i].type] : '?',
                        s->entries[i].confidence, s->entries[i].tag);
    }
    free(s);
    return out;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Resume — compact state summary
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static char *xyzt_resume_compact(void) {
    char *out = (char *)calloc(1024, 1);
    int pos = 0;

    /* Onetwo phase */
    OTSession *ot = state_load_onetwo();
    if (ot && ot->problem[0]) {
        static const char *PH[] = {"E","ONE","BED","TWO","VER","DONE"};
        int pi = ot->phase > 5 ? 0 : ot->phase;
        pos += snprintf(out + pos, 1024 - pos, "OT:%s|%s|K%d|U%d|C%d",
                        PH[pi], ot->problem, ot->n_knowns, ot->n_unknowns, ot->n_claims);
    }
    free(ot);

    /* Wire stats */
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    pos += snprintf(out + pos, 1024 - pos, "|W:%dn,%de,%dl",
                    g->n_nodes, g->n_edges, g->total_learns);
    wire_free(g);

    /* Test history */
    TestHistory *h = state_load_tests();
    if (h && h->count > 0) {
        TestResult *last = &h->results[h->count - 1];
        pos += snprintf(out + pos, 1024 - pos, "|T:%s",
                        last->exit_code == 0 ? "pass" : "fail");
    }
    free(h);

    return out;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Daemon signal — write event to shared .xyzt/events/
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef struct {
    uint32_t timestamp; uint8_t type; uint8_t _pad[3];
    char data[256]; float amplitude;
} XDaemonEvent;

static void xyzt_signal(uint8_t type, const char *data, float amplitude) {
#ifdef _WIN32
    _mkdir(".xyzt");
    _mkdir(".xyzt\\events");
#else
    mkdir(".xyzt", 0755);
    mkdir(".xyzt/events", 0755);
#endif
    XDaemonEvent ev = {0};
    ev.timestamp = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    ev.type = type;
    strncpy(ev.data, data, 255);
    ev.amplitude = amplitude;
    char path[512];
    snprintf(path, sizeof(path), ".xyzt" "/events/" "%u_%d.bin",
             ev.timestamp, rand() % 10000);
    FILE *fp = fopen(path, "wb");
    if (fp) { fwrite(&ev, sizeof(ev), 1, fp); fclose(fp); }
}

/* Convenience: signal file touch */
static void xyzt_signal_file(const char *path, float amp) {
    const char *base = path;
    for (const char *p = path; *p; p++)
        if (*p == '/' || *p == '\\') base = p + 1;
    xyzt_signal(0, base, amp);
}

/* Convenience: signal query */
static void xyzt_signal_query(const char *text) {
    char trunc[128] = {0};
    strncpy(trunc, text, 120);
    xyzt_signal(3, trunc, 1.0f);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Wire convenience — query + learn inline
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static char *xyzt_wire_query_compact(const char *name) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int id = wire_find(g, name);
    if (id < 0) { wire_free(g); return strdup("?"); }

    char *out = (char *)calloc(2048, 1);
    int pos = 0;
    WireNode *n = &g->nodes[id];
    pos += snprintf(out + pos, 2048 - pos, "%s|h%d|v%d|p%.1f",
                    n->name, n->hit_count, n->valence, n->plasticity);
    int conns = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < g->n_edges && conns < 8; i++) {
        if ((int)g->edges[i].src == id && g->edges[i].weight >= WIRE_ALIVE) {
            pos += snprintf(out + pos, 2048 - pos, "|>%s", g->nodes[g->edges[i].dst].name);
            conns++;
        }
    }
    wire_free(g);
    return out;
}

static void xyzt_wire_learn(const char *problem, const char *file, int pass) {
    WireGraph *g = wire_load();
    int pid = wire_add(g, problem, WNODE_PROBLEM);
    if (file) {
        int fid = wire_add(g, file, WNODE_FILE);
        if (pid >= 0 && fid >= 0) wire_connect(g, pid, fid);
    }
    if (pid >= 0) wire_adjust(g, pid, pass);
    wire_save(g);
    wire_free(g);
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   OneTwo core — claim/verify without subprocess
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

static int ot_save(OTSession *s) {
    const char *p = getenv("ONETWO_SESSION");
    if (!p) p = ".xyzt/onetwo_session.bin";
    s->modified = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    FILE *fp = fopen(p, "wb");
    if (!fp) return -1;
    fwrite(s, sizeof(OTSession), 1, fp);
    fclose(fp);
    return 0;
}

static int ot_add_claim(const char *text, int tier) {
    OTSession *s = state_load_onetwo();
    if (!s) {
        /* Create new session if none exists */
        s = (OTSession *)calloc(1, sizeof(OTSession));
        s->magic = OT_MAGIC;
        s->version = 2;
        s->phase = 3; /* TWO */
        s->created = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    }
    if (s->n_claims >= OT_MAX_CLAIMS) { free(s); return -1; }
    OTClaim *c = &s->claims[s->n_claims++];
    strncpy(c->text, text, OT_MAX_TEXT - 1);
    c->tier = (uint8_t)tier;
    c->verified = 0; /* pending */
    c->timestamp = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    int rc = ot_save(s);
    free(s);
    return rc;
}

static int ot_add_known(const char *text) {
    OTSession *s = state_load_onetwo();
    if (!s) return -1;
    if (s->n_knowns >= OT_MAX_KNOWNS) { free(s); return -1; }
    OTEntry *e = &s->knowns[s->n_knowns++];
    strncpy(e->text, text, OT_MAX_TEXT - 1);
    e->timestamp = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
    int rc = ot_save(s);
    free(s);
    return rc;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Skeleton — compact file structure, no bodies
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Classify a C/C++ line for skeleton extraction */
static int skel_classify_c(const char *raw, const char *trimmed) {
    if (trimmed[0] == '#') {
        if (strncmp(trimmed, "#include", 8) == 0) return 1; /* import */
        if (strncmp(trimmed, "#define", 7) == 0) return 2;  /* define */
        return 0;
    }
    if (strstr(trimmed, "\xe2\x95\x90\xe2\x95\x90") || /* ══ */
        strstr(trimmed, "\xe2\x94\x80\xe2\x94\x80"))   /* ── */
        return 11; /* section */
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "typedef ", 8) == 0) return 5;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "struct ", 7) == 0) return 4;
    if (strncmp(trimmed, "enum ", 5) == 0) return 7;

    /* Function signatures at indent 0 */
    int indent = 0;
    for (const char *p = raw; *p == ' ' || *p == '\t'; p++)
        indent += (*p == '\t') ? 4 : 1;
    if (indent == 0 && trimmed[0] && strchr(trimmed, '(')) {
        if (strncmp(trimmed, "static ", 7) == 0 ||
            strncmp(trimmed, "int ", 4) == 0 ||
            strncmp(trimmed, "void ", 5) == 0 ||
            strncmp(trimmed, "char ", 5) == 0 ||
            strncmp(trimmed, "float ", 6) == 0)
            return 3; /* func */
    }
    if (indent == 0 && (strncmp(trimmed, "static const ", 13) == 0 ||
                        strncmp(trimmed, "const ", 6) == 0))
        return 8; /* const */
    return 0; /* skip */
}

/* Extract skeleton from file content, return compact string.
   Token-dense format: "N func sig" per line. Caller frees. */
static char *xyzt_skel_extract(const char *content, int content_len) {
    static const char *TAGS[] = {
        "", "import", "define", "func", "struct", "typedef",
        "", "enum", "const", "", "", "section"
    };
    char *out = (char *)calloc(8192, 1);
    int pos = 0;
    const char *p = content, *end = content + content_len;
    int line_num = 0;

    while (p < end && pos < 7900) {
        line_num++;
        const char *eol = p;
        while (eol < end && *eol != '\n') eol++;

        /* Copy line, strip trailing whitespace */
        int llen = (int)(eol - p);
        if (llen > 255) llen = 255;
        char line[256] = {0};
        memcpy(line, p, llen);
        while (llen > 0 && (line[llen-1] == '\r' || line[llen-1] == ' ')) line[--llen] = 0;

        /* Trim leading whitespace */
        const char *trimmed = line;
        while (*trimmed == ' ' || *trimmed == '\t') trimmed++;

        if (trimmed[0]) {
            int type = skel_classify_c(line, trimmed);
            if (type > 0) {
                /* Strip trailing { */
                int tlen = (int)strlen(line);
                while (tlen > 0 && (line[tlen-1] == '{' || line[tlen-1] == ' '))
                    line[--tlen] = 0;
                pos += snprintf(out + pos, 8192 - pos, "%d %s %s\n",
                               line_num, TAGS[type], line);
            }
        }
        p = eol + 1;
    }
    return out;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Directory scanning — multi-file awareness
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#ifdef _WIN32
#include <io.h>
/* Compact directory tree: "F path size\n" for each source file.
   Returns malloc'd string. max_depth prevents recursion bombs. */
static char *scan_dir_tree(const char *dir, int max_depth) {
    if (max_depth <= 0) return NULL;
    char *result = (char *)calloc(8192, 1);
    if (!result) return NULL;
    int pos = 0;

    char pattern[512];
    snprintf(pattern, sizeof(pattern), "%s/*", dir);

    struct _finddata_t fd;
    intptr_t h = _findfirst(pattern, &fd);
    if (h == -1) return result;

    do {
        if (fd.name[0] == '.') continue;
        char full[512];
        snprintf(full, sizeof(full), "%s/%s", dir, fd.name);

        if (fd.attrib & _A_SUBDIR) {
            /* Skip .xyzt, .git, node_modules, build dirs */
            if (strcmp(fd.name, ".xyzt") == 0 || strcmp(fd.name, ".git") == 0 ||
                strcmp(fd.name, "node_modules") == 0 || strcmp(fd.name, "build") == 0 ||
                strcmp(fd.name, "__pycache__") == 0) continue;
            char *sub = scan_dir_tree(full, max_depth - 1);
            if (sub && sub[0]) {
                int slen = (int)strlen(sub);
                if (pos + slen < 8000) {
                    memcpy(result + pos, sub, slen);
                    pos += slen;
                }
            }
            free(sub);
        } else {
            /* Only list source files */
            const char *ext = strrchr(fd.name, '.');
            if (ext && (strcmp(ext, ".c") == 0 || strcmp(ext, ".h") == 0 ||
                        strcmp(ext, ".cu") == 0 || strcmp(ext, ".cuh") == 0 ||
                        strcmp(ext, ".cpp") == 0 || strcmp(ext, ".py") == 0 ||
                        strcmp(ext, ".rs") == 0 || strcmp(ext, ".java") == 0 ||
                        strcmp(ext, ".js") == 0 || strcmp(ext, ".ts") == 0)) {
                int avail = 8000 - pos;
                if (avail > 64) {
                    pos += snprintf(result + pos, avail, "F %s %ld\n",
                                    full, (long)fd.size);
                }
            }
        }
    } while (_findnext(h, &fd) == 0);
    _findclose(h);

    result[pos] = 0;
    return result;
}
#else
#include <dirent.h>
#include <sys/stat.h>
static char *scan_dir_tree(const char *dir, int max_depth) {
    if (max_depth <= 0) return NULL;
    char *result = (char *)calloc(8192, 1);
    if (!result) return NULL;
    int pos = 0;

    DIR *d = opendir(dir);
    if (!d) return result;

    struct dirent *de;
    while ((de = readdir(d)) != NULL) {
        if (de->d_name[0] == '.') continue;
        char full[512];
        snprintf(full, sizeof(full), "%s/%s", dir, de->d_name);
        struct stat st;
        if (stat(full, &st) != 0) continue;

        if (S_ISDIR(st.st_mode)) {
            if (strcmp(de->d_name, ".xyzt") == 0 || strcmp(de->d_name, ".git") == 0 ||
                strcmp(de->d_name, "node_modules") == 0 || strcmp(de->d_name, "build") == 0 ||
                strcmp(de->d_name, "__pycache__") == 0) continue;
            char *sub = scan_dir_tree(full, max_depth - 1);
            if (sub && sub[0]) {
                int slen = (int)strlen(sub);
                if (pos + slen < 8000) { memcpy(result + pos, sub, slen); pos += slen; }
            }
            free(sub);
        } else {
            const char *ext = strrchr(de->d_name, '.');
            if (ext && (strcmp(ext, ".c") == 0 || strcmp(ext, ".h") == 0 ||
                        strcmp(ext, ".cu") == 0 || strcmp(ext, ".cuh") == 0 ||
                        strcmp(ext, ".cpp") == 0 || strcmp(ext, ".py") == 0 ||
                        strcmp(ext, ".rs") == 0 || strcmp(ext, ".java") == 0 ||
                        strcmp(ext, ".js") == 0 || strcmp(ext, ".ts") == 0)) {
                int avail = 8000 - pos;
                if (avail > 64) {
                    pos += snprintf(result + pos, avail, "F %s %ld\n",
                                    full, (long)st.st_size);
                }
            }
        }
    }
    closedir(d);
    result[pos] = 0;
    return result;
}
#endif

/* Scan a C/C++ file for #include "..." and return missing ones.
   Returns malloc'd string: "missing: foo.h bar.h\n" or NULL. */
static char *scan_missing_includes(const char *filepath, const char *content) {
    char *result = (char *)calloc(1024, 1);
    if (!result) return NULL;
    int pos = 0;

    /* Extract directory from filepath */
    char dir[256] = ".";
    const char *last_slash = strrchr(filepath, '/');
    if (!last_slash) last_slash = strrchr(filepath, '\\');
    if (last_slash) {
        int dlen = (int)(last_slash - filepath);
        if (dlen > 0 && dlen < 255) { memcpy(dir, filepath, dlen); dir[dlen] = 0; }
    }

    const char *p = content;
    while ((p = strstr(p, "#include \"")) != NULL) {
        p += 10; /* skip #include " */
        const char *end = strchr(p, '"');
        if (!end || (end - p) > 128) { p++; continue; }
        char inc[128] = {0};
        memcpy(inc, p, end - p);

        /* Check if file exists (in same dir, or as-is) */
        char check[512];
        snprintf(check, sizeof(check), "%s/%s", dir, inc);
        FILE *f = fopen(check, "r");
        if (!f) f = fopen(inc, "r");
        if (f) { fclose(f); p = end + 1; continue; }

        /* Missing! */
        if (pos == 0) pos += snprintf(result, 1024, "missing:");
        int avail = 1024 - pos;
        if (avail > 2) pos += snprintf(result + pos, avail, " %s", inc);
        p = end + 1;
    }
    if (pos > 0) { result[pos] = 0; return result; }
    free(result);
    return NULL;
}

/* Detect test files in same directory as a source file.
   Returns: 0 = no tests, 1 = found tests.
   Fills test_cmd (e.g., "gcc -O2 -o test.exe test_foo.c && test.exe")
   Fills test_desc (e.g., "test_foo.c") */
static int detect_test_for_file(const char *filepath, char *test_cmd, int cmd_max,
                                char *test_desc, int desc_max) {
    /* Extract directory and base name */
    char dir[256] = ".";
    char base[128] = {0};
    const char *last_slash = strrchr(filepath, '/');
    if (!last_slash) last_slash = strrchr(filepath, '\\');
    if (last_slash) {
        int dlen = (int)(last_slash - filepath);
        if (dlen > 0 && dlen < 255) { memcpy(dir, filepath, dlen); dir[dlen] = 0; }
        strncpy(base, last_slash + 1, 127);
    } else {
        strncpy(base, filepath, 127);
    }

    /* Strip extension from base */
    char *dot = strrchr(base, '.');
    char ext[16] = {0};
    if (dot) { strncpy(ext, dot, 15); *dot = 0; }

    /* 1. Check Makefile test target */
    {
        char mpath[512];
        snprintf(mpath, sizeof(mpath), "%s/Makefile", dir);
        FILE *mf = fopen(mpath, "r");
        if (!mf) { snprintf(mpath, sizeof(mpath), "%s/makefile", dir); mf = fopen(mpath, "r"); }
        if (mf) {
            char line[512];
            while (fgets(line, sizeof(line), mf)) {
                if (strncmp(line, "test:", 5) == 0 || strncmp(line, "test :", 6) == 0 ||
                    strncmp(line, "check:", 6) == 0) {
                    fclose(mf);
                    if (strcmp(dir, ".") == 0)
                        snprintf(test_cmd, cmd_max, "make test 2>&1");
                    else
                        snprintf(test_cmd, cmd_max, "make -C %s test 2>&1", dir);
                    snprintf(test_desc, desc_max, "make test");
                    return 1;
                }
            }
            fclose(mf);
        }
    }

    /* 2. Check test_<base>.c or <base>_test.c
     * For headers (.h/.cuh), look for .c/.cu test files */
    {
        const char *test_ext = ext;
        if (strcmp(ext, ".h") == 0) test_ext = ".c";
        else if (strcmp(ext, ".cuh") == 0) test_ext = ".cu";
        else if (strcmp(ext, ".c") != 0 && strcmp(ext, ".cu") != 0 && strcmp(ext, ".cpp") != 0)
            goto skip_c_tests;  /* not a C-family file */

        const char *test_patterns[] = {"test_%s%s", "%s_test%s", NULL};
        for (const char **pat = test_patterns; *pat; pat++) {
            char tname[256], tpath[512];
            snprintf(tname, sizeof(tname), *pat, base, test_ext);
            snprintf(tpath, sizeof(tpath), "%s/%s", dir, tname);
            FILE *tf = fopen(tpath, "r");
            if (tf) {
                fclose(tf);
                const char *compiler = strcmp(test_ext, ".cu") == 0 ? "nvcc" :
                                       strcmp(test_ext, ".cpp") == 0 ? "g++" : "gcc";
                char exe_name[256];
                snprintf(exe_name, sizeof(exe_name), "%s_test.exe", base);
                snprintf(test_cmd, cmd_max,
                         "%s -O2 -o %s/%s %s && %s/%s 2>&1",
                         compiler, dir, exe_name, tpath, dir, exe_name);
                snprintf(test_desc, desc_max, "%s", tname);
                return 1;
            }
        }
    }
    skip_c_tests:

    /* 3. Check pytest */
    if (strcmp(ext, ".py") == 0) {
        char tpath[512];
        snprintf(tpath, sizeof(tpath), "%s/test_%s.py", dir, base);
        FILE *tf = fopen(tpath, "r");
        if (tf) { fclose(tf);
            snprintf(test_cmd, cmd_max, "python -m pytest %s -v 2>&1", tpath);
            snprintf(test_desc, desc_max, "test_%s.py", base);
            return 1;
        }
        snprintf(tpath, sizeof(tpath), "%s/%s_test.py", dir, base);
        tf = fopen(tpath, "r");
        if (tf) { fclose(tf);
            snprintf(test_cmd, cmd_max, "python -m pytest %s -v 2>&1", tpath);
            snprintf(test_desc, desc_max, "%s_test.py", base);
            return 1;
        }
    }

    return 0;
}

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Action detection — body reads response, extracts actions
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

typedef enum {
    ACT_WRITE_FILE, ACT_RUN_CMD, ACT_PRELOAD_FILE, ACT_TIER_CLAIM,
    ACT_WIRE_CONNECT, ACT_CONSULT
} ActionType;

typedef struct {
    ActionType type;
    char target[512];
    char *content;      /* dynamically allocated — no size limit */
    int tier;
} BodyAction;

#define MAX_ACTIONS 32
typedef struct { BodyAction actions[MAX_ACTIONS]; int count; } ActionQueue;

static void action_add(ActionQueue *q, ActionType type, const char *target,
                       const char *content, int tier) {
    if (q->count >= MAX_ACTIONS) return;
