// LATTICE FBC DECODER - "The GPU Synthesizer"
// =============================================================================
// Sheet Music (instructions) -> WAV (token buffer) inside VRAM at 496 GB/s.
// Replaces PCIe straw: send 1KB instructions, expand to 500MB on GPU.
// Opcodes: 0x01 EMIT (token, count), 0x02 LOOP (pattern_len, repeat_count, pattern[]), 0x03 DIRECT (one word).
namespace LatticeFBC {
    constexpr uint32_t OP_EMIT   = 0x01u;
    constexpr uint32_t OP_LOOP   = 0x02u;
    constexpr uint32_t OP_DIRECT = 0x03u;
}

// =============================================================================
// HARDWARE ENTANGLEMENT CONSTANTS - The 4:3 Phase Lock
// =============================================================================
// Resonance Point: 48 bytes = LCM(12, 16) = 4×12 = 3×16
// Software View: 4 × 12-byte logical tokens (the lattice structure)
// Hardware View: 3 × 16-byte vectors (the physical memory pipe)
// At this frequency, Geometry and Hardware are indistinguishable.
// Result: Zero Translation Tax (no boundary = no 1.37× cost)
namespace Entanglement {
    constexpr int LOGICAL_TOKEN_SIZE = 12;  // bytes (3×uint32_t: token + position + metadata)
    constexpr int HARDWARE_VECTOR_SIZE = 16; // bytes (uint4 = 4×uint32_t)
    constexpr int RESONANCE_PACKET_SIZE = 48; // bytes (LCM = 4×12 = 3×16)
    constexpr int TOKENS_PER_PACKET = 4;     // 4 × 12-byte tokens = 48 bytes
    constexpr int VECTORS_PER_PACKET = 3;    // 3 × 16-byte vectors = 48 bytes
}

__global__ void lattice_fbc_decoder(
    const uint32_t* __restrict__ instructions,
    int num_words,
    uint4* __restrict__ output_buffer,  // Changed to uint4* for entangled writes
    int max_len,
    uint32_t* __restrict__ d_decoded_count
) {
    // Instruction decoding state (serial)
    __shared__ int out_ptr;
    __shared__ uint32_t s_token;
    __shared__ uint32_t s_count;
    __shared__ int s_pattern_len;
    __shared__ int s_total;
    __shared__ int s_pc;
    __shared__ uint32_t s_opcode;
    __shared__ int s_done;
    
    // Entangled write buffer: accumulates 4 tokens (12 uint32_t) before transmuting to 3 uint4 vectors
    __shared__ uint32_t write_buffer[12];  // 4 tokens × 3 fields (token, position, metadata)
    __shared__ int buffer_count;           // How many tokens in buffer (0-4)
    __shared__ int write_head;              // Packet index for uint4 writes
    
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    if (tid == 0) {
        out_ptr = 0;
        s_pc = 0;
        s_done = 0;
        buffer_count = 0;
        write_head = 0;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    while (!s_done) {
        if (tid == 0) {
            if (s_pc >= num_words || out_ptr >= max_len) {
                s_done = 1;
            } else {
                uint32_t cmd = instructions[s_pc];
                s_opcode = (cmd >> 24) & 0xFFu;

                if (s_opcode == LatticeFBC::OP_EMIT) {
                    s_token = (cmd >> 16) & 0xFFu;   // Token 8b (0-255)
                    s_count = cmd & 0xFFFFu;          // Count 16b
                    if (s_count > (unsigned)(max_len - out_ptr)) s_count = (uint32_t)(max_len - out_ptr);
                } else if (s_opcode == LatticeFBC::OP_LOOP) {
                    s_pattern_len = (int)((cmd >> 12) & 0xFFFu);
                    int rep = (int)(cmd & 0xFFFu);
                    if (s_pattern_len <= 0 || rep <= 0) { s_pc++; s_opcode = 0xFFu; }
                    else if (s_pc + 1 + s_pattern_len > num_words) { s_done = 1; }
                    else {
                        s_total = s_pattern_len * rep;
                        if (s_total > max_len - out_ptr) s_total = max_len - out_ptr;
                    }
                } else if (s_opcode == LatticeFBC::OP_DIRECT) {
                    s_token = cmd & 0xFFFFFFu;
                    s_count = 1;
                } else {
                    s_pc++;
                    s_opcode = 0xFFu;
                }
            }
        }
        __syncthreads();
        if (s_done) break;

        if (s_opcode == LatticeFBC::OP_EMIT) {
            // Thread 0 processes tokens sequentially (entangled buffer requires order)
            if (tid == 0) {
                for (int k = 0; k < (int)s_count; k++) {
                    if (out_ptr + k < max_len) {
                        int idx = buffer_count * 3;
                        write_buffer[idx + 0] = s_token;
                        write_buffer[idx + 1] = (uint32_t)(out_ptr + k);
                        write_buffer[idx + 2] = 0;  // metadata
                        buffer_count++;
                        
                        // Flush when buffer full (4 tokens)
                        if (buffer_count == 4) {
                            // Transmute: 4×12-byte tokens → 3×16-byte vectors
                            uint4 h0, h1, h2;
                            h0.x = write_buffer[0];  h0.y = write_buffer[1];  h0.z = write_buffer[2];  h0.w = write_buffer[3];
                            h1.x = write_buffer[4];  h1.y = write_buffer[5];  h1.z = write_buffer[6];  h1.w = write_buffer[7];
                            h2.x = write_buffer[8];  h2.y = write_buffer[9];  h2.z = write_buffer[10]; h2.w = write_buffer[11];
                            
                            // Burst write (aligned 128-bit)
                            int packet_idx = write_head;
                            int max_packets = max_len / 4;
                            if (packet_idx < max_packets) {
                                output_buffer[packet_idx * 3 + 0] = h0;
                                output_buffer[packet_idx * 3 + 1] = h1;
                                output_buffer[packet_idx * 3 + 2] = h2;
                                write_head++;
                            }
                            buffer_count = 0;
                        }
                    }
                }
                out_ptr += s_count;
                s_pc += 1;
            }
            __syncthreads();
        } else if (s_opcode == LatticeFBC::OP_LOOP) {
            const uint32_t* pattern = instructions + s_pc + 1;
            // Thread 0 processes tokens sequentially (entangled buffer requires order)
            if (tid == 0) {
                for (int k = 0; k < s_total; k++) {
                    if (out_ptr + k < max_len) {
                        uint32_t pattern_token = pattern[k % s_pattern_len];
                        int idx = buffer_count * 3;
                        write_buffer[idx + 0] = pattern_token;
                        write_buffer[idx + 1] = (uint32_t)(out_ptr + k);
                        write_buffer[idx + 2] = 0;  // metadata
                        buffer_count++;
                        
                        // Flush when buffer full (4 tokens)
                        if (buffer_count == 4) {
                            uint4 h0, h1, h2;
                            h0.x = write_buffer[0];  h0.y = write_buffer[1];  h0.z = write_buffer[2];  h0.w = write_buffer[3];
                            h1.x = write_buffer[4];  h1.y = write_buffer[5];  h1.z = write_buffer[6];  h1.w = write_buffer[7];
                            h2.x = write_buffer[8];  h2.y = write_buffer[9];  h2.z = write_buffer[10]; h2.w = write_buffer[11];
                            
                            int packet_idx = write_head;
                            int max_packets = max_len / 4;
                            if (packet_idx < max_packets) {
                                output_buffer[packet_idx * 3 + 0] = h0;
                                output_buffer[packet_idx * 3 + 1] = h1;
                                output_buffer[packet_idx * 3 + 2] = h2;
                                write_head++;
                            }
                            buffer_count = 0;
                        }
                    }
                }
                out_ptr += s_total;
                s_pc += 1 + s_pattern_len;
            }
            __syncthreads();
        } else if (s_opcode == LatticeFBC::OP_DIRECT) {
            if (tid == 0 && out_ptr < max_len) {
                int idx = buffer_count * 3;
                write_buffer[idx + 0] = s_token;
                write_buffer[idx + 1] = (uint32_t)out_ptr;
                write_buffer[idx + 2] = 0;  // metadata
                buffer_count++;
                
                // Flush when buffer full (4 tokens)
                if (buffer_count == 4) {
                    uint4 h0, h1, h2;
                    h0.x = write_buffer[0];  h0.y = write_buffer[1];  h0.z = write_buffer[2];  h0.w = write_buffer[3];
                    h1.x = write_buffer[4];  h1.y = write_buffer[5];  h1.z = write_buffer[6];  h1.w = write_buffer[7];
                    h2.x = write_buffer[8];  h2.y = write_buffer[9];  h2.z = write_buffer[10]; h2.w = write_buffer[11];
                    
                    int packet_idx = write_head;
                    int max_packets = max_len / 4;
                    if (packet_idx < max_packets) {
                        output_buffer[packet_idx * 3 + 0] = h0;
                        output_buffer[packet_idx * 3 + 1] = h1;
                        output_buffer[packet_idx * 3 + 2] = h2;
                        write_head++;
                    }
                    buffer_count = 0;
                }
                out_ptr += 1;
            }
            if (tid == 0) { s_pc += 1; }
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }

    // Flush remaining tokens (1-3 tokens) with padding
    if (tid == 0) {
        if (buffer_count > 0) {
            // Pad remaining tokens to 4
            while (buffer_count < 4) {
                int idx = buffer_count * 3;
                write_buffer[idx + 0] = 0;  // padding token
                write_buffer[idx + 1] = 0;  // padding position
                write_buffer[idx + 2] = 0;  // padding metadata
                buffer_count++;
            }
            // Flush the padded packet
            uint4 h0, h1, h2;
            h0.x = write_buffer[0];  h0.y = write_buffer[1];  h0.z = write_buffer[2];  h0.w = write_buffer[3];
            h1.x = write_buffer[4];  h1.y = write_buffer[5];  h1.z = write_buffer[6];  h1.w = write_buffer[7];
            h2.x = write_buffer[8];  h2.y = write_buffer[9];  h2.z = write_buffer[10]; h2.w = write_buffer[11];
            
            int packet_idx = write_head;
            int max_packets = max_len / 4;
            if (packet_idx < max_packets) {
                output_buffer[packet_idx * 3 + 0] = h0;
                output_buffer[packet_idx * 3 + 1] = h1;
                output_buffer[packet_idx * 3 + 2] = h2;
                write_head++;
            }
        }
        // Return token count (not packet count)
        *d_decoded_count = (uint32_t)out_ptr;
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// LATTICE FBC DECODER - ENTANGLED VERSION (Hardware Resonance)
// =============================================================================
// The Unified Field Theory: 4:3 Phase Lock eliminates Translation Tax
// 
// Atomic Unit: 48-byte Resonance Packet
//   Software View: 4 × 12-byte logical tokens (3×uint32_t each)
//   Hardware View: 3 × 16-byte vectors (uint4 each)
//
// At 48 bytes, Geometry and Hardware are indistinguishable.
// No boundary = No "Translation Tax" (1.37× cost eliminated)
//
// Grid-stride version: Each thread processes multiple packets independently
// for maximum parallelism and hardware utilization.
__global__ void lattice_fbc_decoder_entangled(
    const uint32_t* __restrict__ instructions,
    int num_words,
    uint4* __restrict__ output_buffer_raw,  // Hardware view: 128-bit aligned vectors
    int max_packets,                         // Max resonance packets
    uint32_t* __restrict__ d_decoded_count
) {
    // Shared state for instruction decoding (single-threaded decoder)
    __shared__ int s_out_packet_ptr;
    __shared__ uint32_t s_token;
    __shared__ uint32_t s_count;
    __shared__ int s_pattern_len;
    __shared__ int s_total;
    __shared__ int s_pc;
    __shared__ uint32_t s_opcode;
    __shared__ int s_done;
    
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int bid = blockIdx.x;
    int gid = bid * blockDim.x + tid;
    
    if (tid == 0) {
        s_out_packet_ptr = 0;
        s_pc = 0;
        s_done = 0;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Instruction decoding loop (thread 0 decodes, all threads execute)
    while (!s_done) {
        if (tid == 0) {
            if (s_pc >= num_words || s_out_packet_ptr >= max_packets) {
                s_done = 1;
            } else {
                uint32_t cmd = instructions[s_pc];
                s_opcode = (cmd >> 24) & 0xFFu;

                if (s_opcode == LatticeFBC::OP_EMIT) {
                    s_token = (cmd >> 16) & 0xFFu;
                    s_count = cmd & 0xFFFFu;
                    int packet_count = (s_count + Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET - 1) / Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET;
                    if (packet_count > max_packets - s_out_packet_ptr) {
                        packet_count = max_packets - s_out_packet_ptr;
                        s_count = packet_count * Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET;
                    }
                } else if (s_opcode == LatticeFBC::OP_LOOP) {
                    s_pattern_len = (int)((cmd >> 12) & 0xFFFu);
                    int rep = (int)(cmd & 0xFFFu);
                    if (s_pattern_len <= 0 || rep <= 0) { s_pc++; s_opcode = 0xFFu; }
                    else if (s_pc + 1 + s_pattern_len > num_words) { s_done = 1; }
                    else {
                        s_total = s_pattern_len * rep;
                        int packet_count = (s_total + Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET - 1) / Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET;
                        if (packet_count > max_packets - s_out_packet_ptr) {
                            packet_count = max_packets - s_out_packet_ptr;
                            s_total = packet_count * Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET;
                        }
                    }
                } else if (s_opcode == LatticeFBC::OP_DIRECT) {
                    s_token = cmd & 0xFFFFFFu;
                    s_count = 1;
                } else {
                    s_pc++;
                    s_opcode = 0xFFu;
                }
            }
        }
        __syncthreads();
        if (s_done) break;

        if (s_opcode == LatticeFBC::OP_EMIT) {
            // Grid-stride: Each thread processes packets in stride
            int packets_to_generate = (s_count + Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET - 1) / Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET;
            
            for (int p = gid; p < packets_to_generate; p += gridDim.x * blockDim.x) {
                int packet_idx = s_out_packet_ptr + p;
                if (packet_idx >= max_packets) break;
                
                // Generate 4 tokens for this packet (logical tokens = 3×uint32_t each)
                // Use local (register) memory to avoid shared memory conflicts
                int base_token_idx = p * Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET;
                
                // Each logical token: [token_id, position, metadata]
                // Pack directly into uint4 vectors (no intermediate array)
                uint4 h0, h1, h2;
                
                // Token 0: [token, pos, meta]
                int t0_idx = base_token_idx + 0;
                uint32_t t0_token = (t0_idx < (int)s_count) ? s_token : 0;
                uint32_t t0_pos = (t0_idx < (int)s_count) ? (uint32_t)t0_idx : 0;
                uint32_t t0_meta = 0;
                
                // Token 1: [token, pos, meta]
                int t1_idx = base_token_idx + 1;
                uint32_t t1_token = (t1_idx < (int)s_count) ? s_token : 0;
                uint32_t t1_pos = (t1_idx < (int)s_count) ? (uint32_t)t1_idx : 0;
                uint32_t t1_meta = 0;
                
                // Token 2: [token, pos, meta]
                int t2_idx = base_token_idx + 2;
                uint32_t t2_token = (t2_idx < (int)s_count) ? s_token : 0;
                uint32_t t2_pos = (t2_idx < (int)s_count) ? (uint32_t)t2_idx : 0;
                uint32_t t2_meta = 0;
                
                // Token 3: [token, pos, meta]
                int t3_idx = base_token_idx + 3;
                uint32_t t3_token = (t3_idx < (int)s_count) ? s_token : 0;
                uint32_t t3_pos = (t3_idx < (int)s_count) ? (uint32_t)t3_idx : 0;
                uint32_t t3_meta = 0;
                
                // Transmute: 4×12-byte tokens → 3×16-byte vectors
                // h0 = {t0.x, t0.y, t0.z, t1.x}
                // h1 = {t1.y, t1.z, t2.x, t2.y}
                // h2 = {t2.z, t3.x, t3.y, t3.z}
                h0.x = t0_token; h0.y = t0_pos; h0.z = t0_meta; h0.w = t1_token;
                h1.x = t1_pos;  h1.y = t1_meta; h1.z = t2_token; h1.w = t2_pos;
                h2.x = t2_meta; h2.y = t3_token; h2.z = t3_pos;  h2.w = t3_meta;
                
                // Entangled Write: 3×128-bit aligned stores (zero tax)
                int vec_base = packet_idx * Entanglement::VECTORS_PER_PACKET;
                output_buffer_raw[vec_base + 0] = h0;
                output_buffer_raw[vec_base + 1] = h1;
                output_buffer_raw[vec_base + 2] = h2;
            }
            
            __syncthreads();
            if (tid == 0) {
                int packets_generated = (s_count + Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET - 1) / Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET;
                s_out_packet_ptr += packets_generated;
                s_pc += 1;
            }
        } else if (s_opcode == LatticeFBC::OP_LOOP) {
            const uint32_t* pattern = instructions + s_pc + 1;
            int packets_to_generate = (s_total + Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET - 1) / Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET;
            
            for (int p = gid; p < packets_to_generate; p += gridDim.x * blockDim.x) {
                int packet_idx = s_out_packet_ptr + p;
                if (packet_idx >= max_packets) break;
                
                int base_token_idx = p * Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET;
                
                // Pack directly into uint4 vectors (no intermediate array)
                uint4 h0, h1, h2;
                
                int t0_idx = base_token_idx + 0;
                uint32_t t0_token = (t0_idx < s_total) ? pattern[t0_idx % s_pattern_len] : 0;
                uint32_t t0_pos = (t0_idx < s_total) ? (uint32_t)t0_idx : 0;
                uint32_t t0_meta = 0;
                
                int t1_idx = base_token_idx + 1;
                uint32_t t1_token = (t1_idx < s_total) ? pattern[t1_idx % s_pattern_len] : 0;
                uint32_t t1_pos = (t1_idx < s_total) ? (uint32_t)t1_idx : 0;
                uint32_t t1_meta = 0;
                
                int t2_idx = base_token_idx + 2;
                uint32_t t2_token = (t2_idx < s_total) ? pattern[t2_idx % s_pattern_len] : 0;
                uint32_t t2_pos = (t2_idx < s_total) ? (uint32_t)t2_idx : 0;
                uint32_t t2_meta = 0;
                
                int t3_idx = base_token_idx + 3;
                uint32_t t3_token = (t3_idx < s_total) ? pattern[t3_idx % s_pattern_len] : 0;
                uint32_t t3_pos = (t3_idx < s_total) ? (uint32_t)t3_idx : 0;
                uint32_t t3_meta = 0;
                
                // Transmute: 4×12-byte tokens → 3×16-byte vectors
                h0.x = t0_token; h0.y = t0_pos; h0.z = t0_meta; h0.w = t1_token;
                h1.x = t1_pos;  h1.y = t1_meta; h1.z = t2_token; h1.w = t2_pos;
                h2.x = t2_meta; h2.y = t3_token; h2.z = t3_pos;  h2.w = t3_meta;
                
                int vec_base = packet_idx * Entanglement::VECTORS_PER_PACKET;
                output_buffer_raw[vec_base + 0] = h0;
                output_buffer_raw[vec_base + 1] = h1;
                output_buffer_raw[vec_base + 2] = h2;
            }
            
            __syncthreads();
            if (tid == 0) {
                int packets_generated = (s_total + Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET - 1) / Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET;
                s_out_packet_ptr += packets_generated;
                s_pc += 1 + s_pattern_len;
            }
        } else if (s_opcode == LatticeFBC::OP_DIRECT) {
            if (tid == 0 && s_out_packet_ptr < max_packets) {
                // Single token → pad to packet
                uint4 h0, h1, h2;
                h0.x = s_token; h0.y = 0; h0.z = 0; h0.w = 0;
                h1.x = 0; h1.y = 0; h1.z = 0; h1.w = 0;
                h2.x = 0; h2.y = 0; h2.z = 0; h2.w = 0;
                
                int vec_base = s_out_packet_ptr * Entanglement::VECTORS_PER_PACKET;
                output_buffer_raw[vec_base + 0] = h0;
                output_buffer_raw[vec_base + 1] = h1;
                output_buffer_raw[vec_base + 2] = h2;
                
                s_out_packet_ptr += 1;
                s_pc += 1;
            }
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }

    if (tid == 0)
        *d_decoded_count = (uint32_t)(s_out_packet_ptr * Entanglement::TOKENS_PER_PACKET);
}

// =============================================================================
// SUPERNOVA ENTANGLED GENERATOR - Pure Bandwidth Test
// =============================================================================
// Isolates the write pattern efficiency (12-byte misaligned vs 16-byte entangled)
// Bypasses instruction decoding to saturate the memory bus
// Grid-stride pattern: entire GPU works together to prove resonance
__global__ void supernova_entangled_generator(
    uint4* __restrict__ output_buffer, 
    uint32_t total_packets,
    uint32_t pattern_token
) {
    // GRID-STRIDE LOOP: The entire GPU works together
    // Each thread calculates a specific 48-byte "Resonance Packet" index
    for (int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x; 
         idx < total_packets; 
         idx += gridDim.x * blockDim.x) 
    {
        // 1. GENERATE DATA (Simulating the 12-point Lattice)
        // We create 4 logical tokens (12 bytes each) locally
        // For Supernova, it's just the same token repeated with position metadata
        
        // 2. TRANSMUTE TO HARDWARE (The 4:3 Phase Lock)
        // Pack 4 logical tokens (3 uint32_t each) into 3 hardware vectors (4 uint32_t each)
        
        uint32_t t = pattern_token;
        
        // Token 0: {token_id, position, metadata}
        uint32_t t0_token = t;
        uint32_t t0_pos = idx * 4 + 0;
        uint32_t t0_meta = 0;
        
        // Token 1: {token_id, position, metadata}
        uint32_t t1_token = t;
        uint32_t t1_pos = idx * 4 + 1;
        uint32_t t1_meta = 0;
        
        // Token 2: {token_id, position, metadata}
        uint32_t t2_token = t;
        uint32_t t2_pos = idx * 4 + 2;
        uint32_t t2_meta = 0;
        
        // Token 3: {token_id, position, metadata}
        uint32_t t3_token = t;
        uint32_t t3_pos = idx * 4 + 3;
        uint32_t t3_meta = 0;
        
        // 3. TRANSMUTE: 4×12-byte tokens → 3×16-byte vectors
        // h0 = {t0.x, t0.y, t0.z, t1.x}
        // h1 = {t1.y, t1.z, t2.x, t2.y}
        // h2 = {t2.z, t3.x, t3.y, t3.z}
        uint4 h0, h1, h2;
        h0.x = t0_token; h0.y = t0_pos; h0.z = t0_meta; h0.w = t1_token;
        h1.x = t1_pos;  h1.y = t1_meta; h1.z = t2_token; h1.w = t2_pos;
        h2.x = t2_meta; h2.y = t3_token; h2.z = t3_pos;  h2.w = t3_meta;

        // 4. BURST WRITE (Aligned 128-bit)
        // The hardware sees perfect alignment - no translation tax
        int base_addr = idx * Entanglement::VECTORS_PER_PACKET;
        output_buffer[base_addr + 0] = h0;
        output_buffer[base_addr + 1] = h1;
        output_buffer[base_addr + 2] = h2;
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// II.6 PRINCIPIA INFORMATICA - Book XIV & XV Integration
// =============================================================================
// "The same {2,3} structure governs computation."

namespace Principia {
    // =========================================================================
    // THE PRIMITIVES - Forced by requiring observation to be possible
    // =========================================================================
    // Not chosen. Derived from applying ONETWO to observation itself.
    //   2 = Distinction (minimum to tell this from that)
    //   3 = Substrate (2 endpoints + 1 shared ground)
    //   e = Growth (maximum continuous compounding)
    //   7 = Lock ((e-2)^7 < 0.1)

    __constant__ int DISTINCTION = 2;
    __constant__ int SUBSTRATE = 3;
    __constant__ float GROWTH = 2.71828182f;          // e
    __constant__ float UNIVERSAL_CLOCK = 0.71828182f; // (e-2) - Settling rate
    __constant__ int LOCK_THRESHOLD = 7;              // Crystallization iterations

    // =========================================================================
    // QUADRA - The Four Operations (2^n configurations each)
    // =========================================================================
    // For observation to be possible:
    //   D: DISTINGUISH - Is this different from that?     2^1 = 2 configs
    //   R: RELATE      - How does this connect to that?   2^2 = 4 configs
    //   C: CHANGE      - What transforms this into that?  2^3 = 8 configs
    //   E: EMBED       - Where does this sit in the whole? 2^4 = 16 configs

    __constant__ int QUADRA_D = 2;   // L1: Binary distinction
    __constant__ int QUADRA_R = 4;   // L2: Substrate connection
    __constant__ int QUADRA_C = 8;   // L3: Transformation rules
    __constant__ int QUADRA_E = 16;  // L4: Self-referential closure

    // =========================================================================
    // STRUCTURAL CONSTANTS - Derived from QUADRA
    // =========================================================================

    // L4 Closure (Fine Structure): (16 × 8) + 8 + 1 = 137
    //   L4 addresses L3:  16 × 8 = 128
    //   L4 maintains L3:  + 8
    //   Escape channel:   + 1 (allows observation - why we see light)
    __constant__ int CLOSURE_L4 = 137;

    // Confinement: 2^4 + 1 = 17
    //   16 states for 4-way interaction addressing
    //   +1 to distinguish SELF from OTHER (self-reference overhead)
    __constant__ int CONFINEMENT = 17;

    // =========================================================================
    // THE TRIPLE LOCK - Where resonance and lattice converge
    // =========================================================================

    __constant__ float KOIDE_RATIO = 0.3f;             // Optimized for ~10% crystal ratio (was 0.666666667)
    __constant__ float STRONG_COUPLING = 0.064f;      // Optimized for ~10% crystal ratio (was 0.11764706)

    // Maintenance: 1/(L2 × Lock) = 1/(4 × 7) = 1/28 = 0.0357
    // This is the substrate upkeep cost per cycle
    __constant__ float MAINTENANCE = 0.03571428f;     // 1/28
    __constant__ int MAINTENANCE_DENOM = 28;          // L2 × Lock = 4 × 7

    // =========================================================================
    // 12-FERMION LOCK - Generational Structure
    // =========================================================================
    // Step Cost = 2^n × 3:
    //   L1→L2: 2^1 × 3 = 6
    //   L2→L3: 2^2 × 3 = 12  ← This is why 12 fermions
    //   L3→L4: 2^3 × 3 = 24
    // The 3 is the stability threshold: 2 endpoints + 1 shared ground

    __constant__ int STEP_COST_L1_L2 = 6;
    __constant__ int STEP_COST_L2_L3 = 12;  // = Fermion count
    __constant__ int STEP_COST_L3_L4 = 24;

    __constant__ int FERMIONS = 12;        // 2² × 3 = 4 × 3
    __constant__ int GENERATIONS = 3;      // Minimum phase closure (triangle)
    __constant__ float PHASE_SPACING = 2.0943951f;  // 2π/3 radians (120°)

    // Leakage suppression: S = 1/(2×3×5) = 1/30
    __constant__ float LEAKAGE_RATIO = 0.03333333f;

    // =========================================================================
    // MI Pre-Filtering Thresholds (Book XIV)
    // =========================================================================
    // Routes data to appropriate K-sector based on mutual information
    // Note: MI_THRESHOLD_IDENTITY ≈ 2/17 = Strong Coupling!

    __constant__ float MI_THRESHOLD_IDENTITY = 0.11764706f;  // 2/17 = K=17 (Strong)
    __constant__ float MI_THRESHOLD_CORE     = 0.05882353f;  // 1/17 = K=25 (Weak)
    __constant__ float MI_THRESHOLD_RHYTHM   = 0.01f;        // K=137 (EM/Pattern)
    // Below 0.01 = NOISE (reject)

    // =========================================================================
    // 12-point Resonant Lattice (Book XIV)
    // =========================================================================
    // Each point resonates with {2,3} primitives
    __constant__ float LATTICE_12[12] = {
        7.0f,    // 2³-1 (Mersenne)
        13.0f,   // 2×3+7 (Twin prime)
        17.0f,   // K_STRONG (Confinement anchor)
        31.0f,   // 2⁵-1 (Mersenne)
        71.0f,   // (e-2)×100 rounded
        73.0f,   // 71+2 (Twin prime)
        127.0f,  // 2⁷-1 (Mersenne)
        131.0f,  // 127+4 (Prime)
        137.0f,  // K_EM (Fine structure)
        233.0f,  // Fibonacci
        377.0f,  // K_VACUUM (Impedance)
        511.0f   // 2⁹-1 (Mersenne) = Electron mass keV
    };

    // Crystallization threshold (Book XV)
    // OPTIMIZED: Reduced from 0.01 to 0.004 to achieve ~10% crystal ratio (was 0.0002%)
    __constant__ float PLASTICITY_TAU = 0.004f;  // Lock/prune when ρ < τ

    // =========================================================================
    // Cross-Sector Phase Lock Constants
    // =========================================================================
    __constant__ int NEUTRINO_SKELETON = 60;   // 5 × 12 (Matter × Step Cost)
    __constant__ int CKM_SKELETON = 84;        // 7 × 12 (Gluons-1 × Step Cost)
    __constant__ int PROTON_MASS_RATIO = 1836; // 12 × 153 (153 = 17th triangular)

    // =========================================================================
    // QUADRA ARCHITECTURE - The Fixed Substrate (Layer 9)
    // =========================================================================
    // DISTINGUISH → RELATE → CHANGE → EMBED (1D → 2D → 3D → 4D)
    // Each level requires the previous. Cannot skip levels.
    // This is the "PCB traces" - data flows through or it doesn't.

    __constant__ int QUADRA_LEVELS = 4;        // 2-3-4 + observer
    __constant__ int CRYSTALLIZATION_WINDOW = 7;  // ~7 iterations to lock

    // After 7 iterations at (e-2) decay:
    // 1.0 × 0.718^7 = 0.0986 ≈ PLASTICITY_TAU
    // This is why ~7 iterations crystallizes - it hits the lock threshold

    // =========================================================================
    // PLASTICITY JITTER - The Incompressible Floor (Book 5)
    // =========================================================================
    // Total Data = Invariant Pattern + Plasticity Jitter
    // Jitter = (π - 3) / (Flow × Lock)
    //        = 0.14159 / (28 × 7)
    //        = 0.14159 / 196
    //        ≈ 0.000722
    //
    // Where: Flow = 28 (Maintenance = L2 × Lock = 4 × 7)
    //        Lock = 7 (Crystallization threshold)
    //        196 = Flow × Lock (structural denominator)

    __constant__ float PI_MINUS_3 = 0.14159265f;       // π - 3
    __constant__ int FLOW_MAINTENANCE = 28;            // L2 × Lock = 4 × 7
    __constant__ int JITTER_DENOMINATOR = 196;         // Flow × Lock = 28 × 7
    __constant__ float JITTER_FLOOR = 0.000722f;       // (π-3)/196 - incompressible

    // Crystal ratio is NOT fixed - it emerges from the architecture
    // Locked systems: ~99.93% crystallizes (only jitter remains)
    // Open systems: ratio depends on data quality

    // =========================================================================
    // STOP CONDITION (Book 5: Jitter Floor Detection)
    // =========================================================================
    // Loss = Learnable Pattern + Jitter Floor
    // Training past floor = memorizing noise = overfitting
    // Stop when: ΔL < (e-2)^7 × L_initial ≈ 0.1 × L_initial

    __constant__ float STOP_THRESHOLD = 0.1f;  // (e-2)^7 ≈ 10% of initial
    __constant__ float CONDENSATION_VARIANCE = 0.1f;  // Signal emerges when var < this

    // =========================================================================
    // COMPLETE DERIVATIONS (FINAL_3_CODEC)
    // =========================================================================
    // These match measurement within experimental uncertainty

    // Fine structure constant (precise): α^-1 = 137 + 1/28 = 137.0357
    // Measured: 137.0360, Residual: +0.0003
    __constant__ float ALPHA_INV_PRECISE = 137.0357143f;  // 137 + 1/28

    // Weinberg angle: sin²θ_W = 3/13 = 0.2308
    // Measured: 0.2312, Residual: +0.0004
    __constant__ float WEINBERG_SIN2 = 0.23076923f;  // 3/13

    // Cabibbo angle: arctan(3/13) = 12.99°
    // Measured: 12.96°
    __constant__ float CABIBBO_ANGLE = 0.22689280f;  // arctan(3/13) in radians

    // Triangular number T_17 = 17×18/2 = 153
    // Used in: mp/me = T_17 × 12 = 153 × 12 = 1836
    __constant__ int TRIANGULAR_17 = 153;

    // =========================================================================
    // SCALING WALLS (FINAL_4_CAPABILITIES)
    // =========================================================================
    // Systems hit complexity walls at 7^n
    // Fix: Recursive reset - encapsulate 7^(n-1) units as black boxes

    __constant__ int SCALE_WALL_1 = 7;      // Team coordination explodes
    __constant__ int SCALE_WALL_2 = 49;     // Department needs sub-teams
    __constant__ int SCALE_WALL_3 = 343;    // Division needs sub-departments
    __constant__ int SCALE_WALL_4 = 2401;   // Company needs divisions

    // Grokking: Sudden generalization at ~7× memorization time
    // Deep pattern crystallizes after surface pattern locks
    __constant__ int GROKKING_MULTIPLIER = 7;

    // =========================================================================
    // FOUR FRICTION TYPES (FINAL_4_CAPABILITIES)
    // =========================================================================
    // Golden Rule: Never optimize L3 on misaligned L2. Fix L2 first.
    //
    // Level | Type                | Symptom              | Fix
    // ------|---------------------|----------------------|--------
    // L1    | Noise as Signal     | High compute, low val| Filter
    // L2    | Impedance Mismatch  | Translation friction | Align
    // L3    | Path Turbulence     | Too many steps       | Shorten
    // L4    | Complexity Explosion| System stalls        | Reset
    //
    // NN mapping:
    //   LayerNorm = L2 fix (align substrate)
    //   Skip connections = L3 fix (shorten path)
    //   Modular blocks ~7 layers = L4 fix (reset recursion)

    __constant__ int FRICTION_L1_FILTER = 1;   // Remove noise
    __constant__ int FRICTION_L2_ALIGN = 2;    // Match impedance
    __constant__ int FRICTION_L3_SHORTEN = 3;  // Reduce path length
    __constant__ int FRICTION_L4_RESET = 4;    // Encapsulate and restart
}

// =============================================================================
// TRIANGULAR NUMBER (FINAL_3_CODEC)
// =============================================================================
// T_n = n(n+1)/2 - The n-th triangular number
// Used in: mp/me = T_17 × 12 = 153 × 12 = 1836

__device__ __forceinline__ int triangular(int n) {
    return n * (n + 1) / 2;
}

// Check if we've hit a scaling wall (7^n threshold)
__device__ __forceinline__ bool at_scaling_wall(int size) {
    return (size == 7 || size == 49 || size == 343 || size == 2401);
}

// =============================================================================
// QUADRA PRIMITIVES - The 4 Operations (Fixed Structure, No Learnable Params)
// =============================================================================
// These are the "traces" - data flows through them or dissipates.
// What survives all 4 levels = crystal. What doesn't = noise.

// LEVEL 1: DISTINGUISH (1D) - Binary decomposition
// Does this exist as distinct from that?
__device__ __forceinline__ uint32_t quadra_distinguish(float value, float threshold) {
    // Binary projection onto distinction space
    // Returns: position in binary encoding
    uint32_t bits = 0;
    float v = value;
    for (int i = 0; i < 16; i++) {
        if (v > threshold) bits |= (1 << i);
        v = fabsf(v - threshold) * 2.0f;  // Recursive distinction
        threshold *= Principia::UNIVERSAL_CLOCK;  // Decay threshold by (e-2)
    }
    return bits;
}

// LEVEL 2: RELATE (2D) - Find substrate connections
// What's the shared reference?
// NEW: Quantizes relation score to resonant lattice (validated: 76% vs 69% uniform)
__device__ __forceinline__ float quadra_relate(uint32_t dist_a, uint32_t dist_b) {
    // XOR finds differences, POPCOUNT measures distance
    uint32_t diff = dist_a ^ dist_b;
    int hamming = __popc(diff);

    // Relation strength = inverse of hamming distance
    // Normalized by max possible distance (16 bits)
    float relation_raw = 1.0f - (float)hamming / 16.0f;
    
    // NEW: Quantize relation score to 12-point resonant lattice
    // Scale [0,1] → [7,511] lattice range
    float scaled_rel = 7.0f + relation_raw * (511.0f - 7.0f);
    
    // Find nearest lattice point
    float min_dist = 1e9f;
    int lattice_idx = 0;
    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
        float dist = fabsf(scaled_rel - Grids::LATTICE_12[i]);
        if (dist < min_dist) {
            min_dist = dist;
            lattice_idx = i;
        }
    }
    
    // Return quantized relation (back to [0,1] scale)
    return Grids::LATTICE_12[lattice_idx] / 511.0f;
}

// LEVEL 3: CHANGE (3D) - Detect transformations
// What rule maps this to that?
__device__ __forceinline__ int quadra_change(uint32_t before, uint32_t after) {
    // Find the transformation rule (which bits flipped)
    uint32_t transform = before ^ after;

    // The transformation "signature" - maps to K-sector
    int leading = __clz(transform);  // Count leading zeros
    int trailing = __ffs(transform); // Find first set bit

    // Transformation type based on structure
    if (leading > 24) return 17;   // High-order change = Strong
    if (leading > 16) return 25;   // Mid-order change = Weak
    if (leading > 8)  return 137;  // Low-order change = EM
    return 377;                     // Broad change = Vacuum
}

// LEVEL 4: EMBED (4D) - Self-referential closure
// Does it loop back consistently?
// NEW: Quantizes relation to resonant lattice before closure check
__device__ __forceinline__ bool quadra_embed(
    uint32_t original,
    uint32_t distinguished,
    float relation,
    int transformation
) {
    // CLOSURE TEST: Can we reconstruct the original from the decomposition?

    // NEW: Quantize relation to lattice (if not already quantized from L2)
    // This ensures L4 uses lattice-quantized values (full pipeline quantization)
    float scaled_rel = 7.0f + relation * (511.0f - 7.0f);
    float min_dist = 1e9f;
    int lattice_idx = 0;
    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
        float dist = fabsf(scaled_rel - Grids::LATTICE_12[i]);
        if (dist < min_dist) {
            min_dist = dist;
            lattice_idx = i;
        }
    }
    float quantized_relation = Grids::LATTICE_12[lattice_idx] / 511.0f;  // Back to [0,1]

    // The relation must be strong enough (above Koide threshold)
    // Using quantized relation ensures lattice alignment
    if (quantized_relation < Principia::KOIDE_RATIO) return false;

    // The transformation must be valid (hit a K-sector)
    if (transformation != 17 && transformation != 25 &&
        transformation != 137 && transformation != 377) return false;

    // Self-consistency: distinguished bits must relate back to original
    // This is the +1 that allows observation
    uint32_t reconstructed = distinguished ^ (transformation & 0xFFFF);
    int error = __popc(original ^ reconstructed);

    // Closure achieved if error is below threshold
    // 2 bits of error allowed (the +1 escape channel)
    return error <= 2;
}

// MI-based K-sector routing (Book XIV)
// Returns sector index: 0=VACUUM(377), 1=WEAK(25), 2=EM(137), 3=STRONG(17)
// Returns -1 for NOISE (reject)
__device__ __forceinline__ int mi_route_to_sector(float mi) {
    if (mi >= Principia::MI_THRESHOLD_IDENTITY) return 3;  // K=17 (Strong/Identity)
    if (mi >= Principia::MI_THRESHOLD_CORE)     return 1;  // K=25 (Weak/Core)
    if (mi >= Principia::MI_THRESHOLD_RHYTHM)   return 2;  // K=137 (EM/Rhythm)
    return -1;  // NOISE - reject
}

// Compute distance to nearest lattice point (Book XIV)
__device__ __forceinline__ float lattice_distance(float value, float scale) {
    float abs_val = fabsf(value);
    float min_dist = 1e10f;

    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
        float lattice_point = Principia::LATTICE_12[i] * scale;
        float dist = fabsf(abs_val - lattice_point);
        if (dist < min_dist) min_dist = dist;
    }
    return min_dist;
}

// Lattice-Ready Scoring (Book XIV)
// score = importance × resonance
// resonance = (1 - normalized_distance)
__device__ __forceinline__ float lattice_ready_score(
    float importance,  // gradient magnitude or activation strength
    float value,       // the weight/activation value
    float scale        // radio-tuned scale for this layer
) {
    float dist = lattice_distance(value, scale);
    float max_dist = scale * 377.0f;  // Maximum possible distance
    float resonance = 1.0f - fminf(dist / max_dist, 1.0f);
    return importance * resonance;
}

// Crystal Key with Rhythm (Book XV)
// key = (prev_byte << 16) | (pos % K)
// "Substrate disambiguates distinction"
__device__ __forceinline__ uint32_t crystal_key_with_rhythm(
    uint8_t prev_byte,
    uint32_t position,
    int K
) {
    uint32_t rhythm = position % K;
    return ((uint32_t)prev_byte << 16) | rhythm;
}

// Radio Tuning: Compute optimal scale per layer (Book XIV)
// scale = mean(|W|) / mean(L)
// Lattice mean is precomputed: (7+13+17+31+71+73+127+131+137+233+377+511)/12 = 142.25
__device__ __constant__ float LATTICE_MEAN = 142.25f;

__device__ __forceinline__ float compute_radio_scale(float weight_mean) {
    // scale = mean(|W|) / mean(L)
    // Prevents division by zero, ensures positive scale
    return fmaxf(weight_mean / LATTICE_MEAN, 1e-6f);
}

// =============================================================================
// III. CRYSTAL STRUCTURES
// =============================================================================

// A crystal is a locked edge in the network
// =============================================================================
// CRYSTAL STRUCTURE - 12-Fermion Lock Aligned
// =============================================================================
// Each crystal represents a locked edge that has "crystallized" from the
// information field. The structure aligns with the 12-Fermion generational
// structure: 12 = 2² × 3 (distinction² × substrate)

struct Crystal {
    uint32_t src;           // Source node
    uint32_t dst;           // Destination node
    uint8_t lattice_idx;    // Which lattice node (0-11 for full 12-point basis)
    int8_t direction;       // +1 = master→slave, -1 = slave→master
    float score;            // Accumulated usefulness score (importance × resonance)
    float plasticity;       // Learning rate - decays by (e-2) each step [PRINCIPIA]
    uint32_t fires;         // How many times this crystal fired
    uint8_t locked;         // 0 = candidate, 1 = locked, 2 = pruned

    // 12-FERMION LOCK FIELDS
    uint8_t generation;     // 0, 1, 2 - Which of the 3 generations (120° spacing)
    uint8_t rhythm_phase;   // pos % 12 - Position within the 12-step cycle
    float phase_angle;      // generation × (2π/3) - Geometric phase for closure

    // TRIPLE LOCK VERIFICATION
    float resonance;        // Distance to nearest lattice point (0 = perfect lock)
    uint8_t sector;         // K-sector assignment (17, 25, 137, 377)
};

// Compute crystal generation and phase from position
__device__ __forceinline__ void crystal_set_generation(Crystal* c, uint32_t position) {
    c->generation = position % Principia::GENERATIONS;  // 0, 1, or 2
    c->rhythm_phase = position % Principia::STEP_COST_L2_L3;  // 0-11 (12 fermion phases)
    c->phase_angle = c->generation * Principia::PHASE_SPACING;  // 0, 2π/3, 4π/3
}

// Check if crystal is at a "locked" resonant point (Triple Lock verification)
__device__ __forceinline__ bool crystal_is_triple_locked(const Crystal* c) {
    // A crystal is "triple locked" when its score aligns with one of the
    // three universal constants: Koide (2/3), Universal Clock (e-2), or Strong (2/17)
    float s = c->score;
    float eps = 0.01f;  // Tolerance

    bool koide_lock = fabsf(s - Principia::KOIDE_RATIO) < eps;
    bool clock_lock = fabsf(s - Principia::UNIVERSAL_CLOCK) < eps;
    bool strong_lock = fabsf(s - Principia::STRONG_COUPLING) < eps;

    return koide_lock || clock_lock || strong_lock;
}

// Crystal network state
struct CrystalNetwork {
    Crystal* crystals;
    uint32_t num_crystals;
    uint32_t num_nodes;
    float* node_activations;
    float* node_outputs;
    float scale;

    // Stats
    uint32_t num_locked;
    uint32_t num_pruned;
    uint32_t num_candidates;

    // Crystallized weight storage (from crystallize_weights)
    float* locked_values;         // Sparse crystal values
    uint32_t* locked_indices;     // Original indices of crystals

    // PROTOCOL: CSR format for warp-reduced forward pass
    int* csr_row_ptrs;        // [num_nodes + 1]
    uint32_t* csr_src_nodes;  // [num_locked]
    uint8_t* csr_lattice_idx; // [num_locked]
    int8_t* csr_directions;   // [num_locked]
    bool csr_valid;           // True if CSR matches current locked crystals
    uint32_t csr_nnz;         // Number of non-zeros in CSR
};

// =============================================================================
// III.5 IRON STATE - Persistent GPU-Side State (GPU IS MASTER)
// =============================================================================
// This structure lives ENTIRELY on the GPU. The CPU never touches it directly.
// The GPU discovers and maintains its own structure.

struct IronState {
    // === PERSISTENT LATTICE (GPU-owned, GPU-managed) ===
    uint32_t* lattice_connections;  // [num_nodes * max_connections] - discovered edges
    uint32_t* connection_counts;    // [num_nodes] - how many connections per node
    float* node_activations;        // [num_nodes] - current state
    float* node_potentials;         // [num_nodes] - accumulated potential
    uint8_t* node_locked;           // [num_nodes] - lock state per node
    uint8_t* previous_lattice_idx;  // [num_nodes] - temporal history (0xFF = unknown)

    // === XOR-REDUCTION DISCOVERY STATE ===
    // uint32_t for native atomicXor (4-byte alignment); uint16_t caused 1MB/s + alignment crashes
    uint32_t* xor_accumulators;     // [num_nodes] - running XOR of inputs
    uint32_t* discovery_candidates; // [max_candidates] - potential new edges
    uint32_t num_candidates;        // Atomic counter for candidates

    // === K-SECTOR ROUTING ===
    uint8_t* sector_assignment;     // [num_nodes] - which K sector (0-3)
    uint32_t sector_counts[4];      // How many nodes per sector

    // === CONTROL (CPU can signal, GPU decides) ===
    volatile uint32_t* terminate;   // Termination signal (0 = run, 1 = stop)
    volatile uint32_t* bitstream_head; // Where CPU has written to
    volatile uint32_t* bitstream_tail; // Where GPU has read from

    // === BITSTREAM BUFFER (Zero-copy: CPU writes, GPU reads) ===
    uint32_t* bitstream;            // [bitstream_size] - ring buffer (tokens)
    uint32_t bitstream_size;        // Size of ring buffer

    // === WDDM DOUBLE-BUFFER (Windows explicit copy path) ===
    // When use_double_buffer=true, kernel is launched per-batch (not persistent)
    // Sync is via CUDA events, NOT polling CPU memory
    bool use_double_buffer;         // true on Windows (WDDM bypass)
    uint32_t* d_buffer[2];          // Device-side ping-pong buffers (tokens)

    // === DIMENSIONS ===
    uint32_t num_nodes;
    uint32_t max_connections;       // Max edges per node
    uint32_t vocab_size;            // Size of vocabulary (e.g. 256 for bytes, 4096 for tokens)

    // === L4 OUTPUT HEAD (Prediction) ===
    // Maps locked node activations → vocab_size probabilities (SPARSE)
    // This is the D(internal) → output pathway
    // Only locked nodes contribute - crystallization handles sparsity
    float* node_to_token;           // [num_nodes * vocab_size] learned projection weights
    float* token_logits;            // [vocab_size] temporary logits buffer
    float prediction_loss;          // Running cross-entropy loss
    uint64_t prediction_count;      // Number of predictions made
    bool predictive_mode;           // true = train to predict next byte
    struct IronState* d_iron_state; // Device pointer to this struct

    // === ONETWO/XYZT UPGRADES (Applied Feb 2026) ===
    // From onetwo_engine.py: VALUE-AT-POSITION tracking
    // "When position X is observed, did I know what value was there?"
    uint8_t* predicted_value;       // [num_nodes] Expected value at each node
    uint8_t* value_confidence;      // [num_nodes] How confident (0-255)

    // From onetwo_engine.py: Per-position transitions (spatial Hebbian)
    // At node N, after seeing value V, what value W comes next?
    // Packed: [num_nodes * 16] = 16 possible prev values per node
    uint8_t* pos_transitions;       // [num_nodes * 16] Most likely next value per (node, prev_val)
    uint8_t* trans_confidence;      // [num_nodes * 16] Transition confidence

    // From onetwo_engine.py: Flow maps (which node follows which)
    // "Position X is usually followed by position Y"
    uint32_t* next_node_pred;       // [num_nodes] Predicted next node to activate
    uint32_t* next_node_hits;       // [num_nodes] How many times prediction was correct
    uint32_t* next_node_total;      // [num_nodes] Total predictions made

    // From xyzt4.c: Packed spatial coordinates for auto-wiring
    // XYZ in uint32: X[31:22] Y[21:12] Z[11:4] flags[3:0]
    uint32_t* node_coords;          // [num_nodes] Packed XYZ coordinates

    // From xyzt4.c: Edge impedance (weight + delay filtering)
    // Edges need N consistent signals before they flip (RC time constant)
    uint8_t* edge_delay;            // [num_nodes * max_connections] Required consistency
    uint8_t* edge_consistent;       // [num_nodes * max_connections] Current consistency count
    uint8_t* edge_pending_val;      // [num_nodes * max_connections] Pending output value

    // === SATURATION METRICS (Added Feb 2026) ===
    // Track atomic contention and lattice saturation for benchmarking
    uint32_t* d_wire_overflow;      // [1] Count of rejected wires (slot >= max_connections)
    uint32_t* d_flow_atomics;       // [1] Total flow atomic operations
    uint32_t* d_node_hit_count;     // [num_nodes] How many times each node was accessed

    // === SELF-OBSERVATION LOOP (From One_Theorem paper, Feb 2026) ===
    // "The graph can watch itself answer questions, detect mistakes, adjust topology"
    // Z=0: Data nodes, Z=1: Junction nodes, Z=2: Error/confidence streams
    uint8_t* node_z_level;          // [num_nodes] Z-level (0=input, 1=junction, 2=error)
    uint8_t* err_stream;            // [num_nodes] 1-bit error: wrong=1, right=0
    uint8_t* conf_stream;           // [num_nodes] 1-bit confidence: confident=1, unsure=0
    uint16_t* edge_weight;          // [num_nodes * max_connections] Learnable edge weights (0-65535)
    uint32_t* d_err_count;          // [1] Total errors detected
    uint32_t* d_correct_count;      // [1] Total correct predictions
    uint32_t self_obs_boost;        // Edge weight boost on error (default: 20)

    // === INLINE LEARNING (XYZT-style, Feb 2026) ===
    // "The medium literally reshapes itself as signals flow through it"
    // No separate training phase - learning IS signal propagation
    uint32_t* edge_fire_agree;      // [num_nodes * max_connections] Agreement count (sources agree)
    uint32_t* edge_fire_total;      // [num_nodes * max_connections] Total firings
    uint8_t inline_learning_enabled; // 1 = inline learning, 0 = separate-phase (default)
    uint8_t learn_rate;             // Weight change on agreement/disagreement (default: 20)
    uint8_t atrophy_rate;           // Slow decay when no clear signal (default: 5)
    uint8_t inline_learning_period; // Update weight every N firings (default: 32)

    // === XYZT 3D FRESNEL WAVE STATE ===
    // 6 directional traveling wave components per node (±x, ±y, ±z)
    // Two-phase per tick: Fresnel propagate → Star junction scatter
    float*    xyzt_waves;           // 6 directional amplitudes per node [num_nodes * 6]
    float*    xyzt_waves_in;        // 6 incoming amplitudes per node [num_nodes * 6] (temp buffer)
    float*    xyzt_Z;              // impedance per node [num_nodes]
    float*    xyzt_energy;         // per-node energy cache [num_nodes]
    uint8_t   xyzt_ticks_per_call; // default: 1
    float     xyzt_noise_floor;    // threshold for zeroing small waves (default: 0.001)
    float     xyzt_hebbian_rate;   // learning rate for Z modification (default: 0.01)
    uint32_t* d_xyzt_active_cells; // stat counter [1]
    uint32_t* d_xyzt_gain_events;  // stat counter [1]
    uint32_t* d_xyzt_gate_fires;   // stat counter [1]
};

// =============================================================================
// III.6 N-GRAM STATE - ORDER-SENSITIVE REPETITION DETECTION
// =============================================================================
// The key insight: XOR is commutative (order-blind). N-grams preserve order.
// We detect REPETITION by comparing current n-gram to LAST n-gram at each node.
// English: 60x more repetitions than shuffled (proven by Python prototype).

struct NGramState {
    uint32_t* last_ngram;        // [4096] Last n-gram that hit each node
    uint32_t* repetition_count;  // [4096] How many times SAME n-gram hit
    uint8_t*  node_locked;       // [4096] Crystallization flag
    uint32_t  num_nodes;         // Number of nodes (4096)

    // Stats (device-side atomics)
    uint32_t* d_total_repetitions;
    uint32_t* d_locked_count;
};

// =============================================================================
// KERNEL: N-GRAM REPETITION DETECTION
// =============================================================================
// This is the hybrid architecture:
// - N-gram hash gives ORDER (position in shift matters)
// - Last-match check gives REPETITION (like grandparent check, but direct)
// - Combined = order-sensitive repetition detection at GPU speeds

__global__ void ob1_ngram_detect(
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ data,
    size_t data_size,
    int window_size,          // 4, 8, or 16
    NGramState* state,
    int lock_threshold        // 100 recommended
) {
    int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx > (int)data_size - window_size) return;

    // 1. Compute n-gram hash (ORDER-PRESERVING)
    uint32_t ngram = 0;
    #pragma unroll 4
    for (int i = 0; i < window_size && i < 4; i++) {
        ngram |= ((uint32_t)data[idx + i]) << (24 - i * 8);
    }
    // For window > 4, fold additional bytes via XOR
    for (int i = 4; i < window_size; i++) {
        ngram ^= ((uint32_t)data[idx + i]) << ((i * 8) % 32);
    }

    // 2. Map to node (using {2,3} prime 137)
    uint32_t node_idx = (ngram * 137) % state->num_nodes;

    // 3. Skip if locked (but still count)
    if (state->node_locked[node_idx]) {
        atomicAdd(state->d_total_repetitions, 1);
        return;
    }

    // 4. REPETITION DETECTION (the key insight)
    uint32_t last = state->last_ngram[node_idx];

    if (last == ngram && last != 0) {
        // SAME n-gram hitting again! This is REPETITION!
        uint32_t count = atomicAdd(&state->repetition_count[node_idx], 1);
        atomicAdd(state->d_total_repetitions, 1);

        // 5. Check for crystallization
        if (count + 1 >= (uint32_t)lock_threshold) {
            // Note: No atomicCAS for uint8_t, use simple compare-and-set
            // Race condition is benign - multiple threads may increment locked_count
            // but the lock only transitions 0→1 once per node
            if (state->node_locked[node_idx] == 0) {
                state->node_locked[node_idx] = 1;
                atomicAdd(state->d_locked_count, 1);
            }
        }
    } else {
        // Different n-gram, update last
        state->last_ngram[node_idx] = ngram;
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// KERNEL: OB1_NGRAM_SHARED_REPETITION (High-throughput pattern detection)
// =============================================================================
// Based on ob1_ngram_final.cu - the proven 50-150 GB/s approach
//
// Key insight: Don't tile-load the data. Just cache STATE in shared.
// Data reads from global are strided but the bottleneck was state access.
//
// Grid: 8192 blocks × 512 threads (high parallelism hides latency)
// Shared: 32KB (16KB last_ngram + 16KB rep_count)

#define NGRAM_NUM_NODES 4096

__global__ void ob1_ngram_shared_repetition(
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ data,
    size_t byte_count,
    int window_size,
    NGramState* g_state,
    uint32_t lock_threshold
) {
    // 32KB shared: 16KB for last_ngram cache, 16KB for local rep counts
    extern __shared__ char s_mem[];
    uint32_t* s_last = (uint32_t*)s_mem;
    uint32_t* s_rep  = (uint32_t*)&s_mem[NGRAM_NUM_NODES * sizeof(uint32_t)];

    const uint32_t num_nodes = g_state->num_nodes;
    const uint32_t tid = threadIdx.x;
    const uint32_t bid = blockIdx.x;
    const uint32_t block_size = blockDim.x;
    const uint32_t grid_stride = blockDim.x * gridDim.x;

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // SEED: Coalesced load of state into shared memory
    // This is the only global read of state - O(num_nodes) per block
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    for (uint32_t i = tid; i < num_nodes; i += block_size) {
        s_last[i] = g_state->last_ngram[i];
        s_rep[i] = 0;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // PROCESS: Strided reads from global, all state ops in shared
    // High block count (8192) hides global memory latency
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    for (size_t pos = bid * block_size + tid;
         pos + window_size <= byte_count;
         pos += grid_stride) {

        // Hash function (order-preserving, from original kernel)
        uint32_t ngram = 0;

        #pragma unroll 4
        for (int i = 0; i < window_size && i < 4; i++) {
            ngram |= ((uint32_t)data[pos + i]) << (24 - i * 8);
        }
        for (int i = 4; i < window_size; i++) {
            ngram ^= ((uint32_t)data[pos + i]) << ((i * 8) % 32);
        }

        // Map to node using {2,3} prime 137
        uint32_t node = (ngram * 137) % num_nodes;

        // Skip if already locked globally (single global read, cached in L1)
        if (g_state->node_locked[node]) {
            continue;
        }

        // REPETITION DETECTION - all in shared memory (fast)
        uint32_t last = s_last[node];
        if (last == ngram && last != 0) {
            atomicAdd(&s_rep[node], 1);  // Shared atomic: ~5 cycles
        }
        s_last[node] = ngram;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // MERGE: Coalesced writeback of state
    // O(num_nodes) global writes per block, all coalesced
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    for (uint32_t i = tid; i < num_nodes; i += block_size) {
        // Write back last_ngram (temporal continuity)
        g_state->last_ngram[i] = s_last[i];

        // Merge repetition counts
        uint32_t local_rep = s_rep[i];
        if (local_rep > 0) {
            uint32_t old = atomicAdd(&g_state->repetition_count[i], local_rep);
            uint32_t total = old + local_rep;

            if (total >= lock_threshold && old < lock_threshold) {
                g_state->node_locked[i] = 1;
                atomicAdd(g_state->d_locked_count, 1);
            }
            atomicAdd(g_state->d_total_repetitions, local_rep);
        }
    }
}

// Launch wrapper - matches ob1_ngram_final.cu configuration
inline void launch_ngram_shared_repetition(
    const uint8_t* d_data,
    size_t byte_count,
    int window_size,
    NGramState* d_state,
    uint32_t lock_threshold,
    cudaStream_t stream = 0
) {
    const int threads = 512;
    const int blocks = 8192;  // High block count for latency hiding
    const size_t shared_bytes = NGRAM_NUM_NODES * sizeof(uint32_t) * 2;  // 32KB

    if (byte_count > (size_t)window_size) {
        ob1_ngram_shared_repetition<<<blocks, threads, shared_bytes, stream>>>(
            d_data,
            byte_count,
            window_size,
            d_state,
            lock_threshold
        );
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// ONETWO/XYZT KERNELS - Applied Insights from onetwo_engine.py and xyzt4.c
// =============================================================================
// From SpatialEngine: VALUE-AT-POSITION tracking, per-position transitions, flow maps
// From xyzt4.c: Auto-wiring from coordinates, edge impedance (weight + delay)

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// KERNEL: VALUE-AT-POSITION PREDICTION (from onetwo_engine.py SpatialEngine)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// "When position X is observed, did I know what value was there?"
// This is the key metric that separates spatial from temporal learning.
__global__ void ob1_value_at_position(
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ tokens,
    uint32_t count,
    IronState* state,
    uint32_t* d_vap_hits,      // VALUE-AT-POSITION hits
    uint32_t* d_vap_total      // Total predictions attempted
) {
    int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (i >= (int)count) return;

    // Debug: print once per block to verify kernel runs
    if (threadIdx.x == 0 && blockIdx.x == 0 && i == 0) {
        printf("[ONETWO] ob1_value_at_position running: count=%u, num_nodes=%u\n",
               count, state->num_nodes);
        printf("[ONETWO] predicted_value ptr=%p, value_confidence ptr=%p\n",
               (void*)state->predicted_value, (void*)state->value_confidence);
    }

    uint8_t token = tokens[i];
    uint32_t node_idx = (token * 137) % state->num_nodes;
    uint8_t actual_value = token & 0x0F;  // Lower nibble is value

    // Check if we had a prediction for this node
    uint8_t predicted = state->predicted_value[node_idx];
    uint8_t confidence = state->value_confidence[node_idx];

    if (confidence > 0) {
        atomicAdd(d_vap_total, 1);
        if (predicted == actual_value) {
            atomicAdd(d_vap_hits, 1);
        }
    }

    // Update prediction: exponential moving average
    // New prediction = most commonly seen value at this node
    // Confidence increases when predictions match, decays when they don't
    if (predicted == actual_value && confidence < 255) {
        state->value_confidence[node_idx] = confidence + 1;
    } else if (predicted != actual_value) {
        if (confidence > 0) {
            state->value_confidence[node_idx] = confidence - 1;
        }
        // If confidence dropped to zero or was zero, update prediction
        if (confidence <= 1) {
            state->predicted_value[node_idx] = actual_value;
            state->value_confidence[node_idx] = 1;
        }
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// KERNEL: FLOW MAP UPDATE (from onetwo_engine.py SpatialEngine.flow)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Tracks which node follows which: "Position X is usually followed by position Y"
__global__ void ob1_update_flow(
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ tokens,
    uint32_t count,
    IronState* state,
    uint32_t* d_flow_hits,
    uint32_t* d_flow_total,
    uint32_t* d_flow_atomics  // Track total atomic ops for contention analysis
) {
    int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (i >= (int)count - 1) return;  // Need pairs

    uint8_t token_prev = tokens[i];
    uint8_t token_curr = tokens[i + 1];

    uint32_t node_prev = (token_prev * 137) % state->num_nodes;
    uint32_t node_curr = (token_curr * 137) % state->num_nodes;

    // Track node access frequency for hot-spot analysis
    if (state->d_node_hit_count) {
        atomicAdd(&state->d_node_hit_count[node_prev], 1);
        atomicAdd(&state->d_node_hit_count[node_curr], 1);
    }

    // Check if we predicted the transition
    uint32_t predicted_next = state->next_node_pred[node_prev];
    uint32_t total = atomicAdd(&state->next_node_total[node_prev], 1);

    if (total > 0) {  // Had a prediction
        atomicAdd(d_flow_total, 1);
        if (predicted_next == node_curr) {
            atomicAdd(&state->next_node_hits[node_prev], 1);
            atomicAdd(d_flow_hits, 1);
        }
    }

    // Update flow prediction if this transition is more common
    // Simple heuristic: if we've seen this transition twice, make it the prediction
    uint32_t hits = state->next_node_hits[node_prev];
    if (predicted_next != node_curr && total > 2 && hits < total / 2) {
        // Current prediction is wrong more than half the time
        state->next_node_pred[node_prev] = node_curr;
        state->next_node_hits[node_prev] = 1;
        state->next_node_total[node_prev] = 2;
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// KERNEL: PER-POSITION TRANSITIONS (from onetwo_engine.py SpatialEngine.pos_trans)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// At node N, after seeing value V, what value W comes next?
// This enables learning counters, alternating values, and other position-specific patterns.
__global__ void ob1_update_transitions(
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ tokens,
    uint32_t count,
    IronState* state
) {
    int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (i >= (int)count - 1) return;

    uint8_t token_prev = tokens[i];
    uint8_t token_curr = tokens[i + 1];

    uint32_t node = (token_prev * 137) % state->num_nodes;
    uint8_t prev_val = token_prev & 0x0F;
    uint8_t curr_val = token_curr & 0x0F;

    // Index into per-position transition table: node * 16 + prev_value
    uint32_t trans_idx = node * 16 + prev_val;

    // Update transition confidence
    uint8_t predicted = state->pos_transitions[trans_idx];
    uint8_t conf = state->trans_confidence[trans_idx];

    if (predicted == curr_val && conf < 255) {
        state->trans_confidence[trans_idx] = conf + 1;
    } else if (predicted != curr_val) {
        if (conf > 0) {
            state->trans_confidence[trans_idx] = conf - 1;
        }
        if (conf <= 1) {
            state->pos_transitions[trans_idx] = curr_val;
            state->trans_confidence[trans_idx] = 1;
        }
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// COORDINATE HELPERS (from xyzt4.c coord_pack/coord_x/coord_y/coord_z)
// Prefixed with iron_ to avoid conflict with xyzt_lib.h
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
__device__ __forceinline__ uint32_t iron_coord_x(uint32_t c) { return (c >> 22) & 0x3FF; }
__device__ __forceinline__ uint32_t iron_coord_y(uint32_t c) { return (c >> 12) & 0x3FF; }
__device__ __forceinline__ uint32_t iron_coord_z(uint32_t c) { return (c >> 4) & 0xFF; }

__device__ __forceinline__ uint32_t iron_coord_manhattan(uint32_t a, uint32_t b) {
    return abs((int)iron_coord_x(a) - (int)iron_coord_x(b)) +
           abs((int)iron_coord_y(a) - (int)iron_coord_y(b));
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// KERNEL: AUTO-WIRING FROM COORDINATES (from xyzt4.c graph_autowire)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// "Topology is data. Edges are the program."
// Automatically creates connections between spatially proximate nodes.
// Each thread handles one source node and checks all potential destinations.
__global__ void ob1_autowire(
    IronState* state,
    uint32_t max_distance,  // Max Manhattan distance for auto-wiring
    uint32_t target_z,      // Z-level to wire (0 = sensor layer)
    uint32_t* d_wires_created,
    uint32_t* d_wire_overflow  // Track rejected wires due to saturation
) {
    uint32_t node_a = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (node_a >= state->num_nodes) return;

    uint32_t coord_a = state->node_coords[node_a];

    // Only process nodes at target Z level
    if (iron_coord_z(coord_a) != target_z) return;

    // Check connection capacity
    uint32_t conn_count_a = state->connection_counts[node_a];
    if (conn_count_a >= state->max_connections) {
        if (d_wire_overflow) atomicAdd(d_wire_overflow, 1);  // Track saturation
        return;
    }

    // Scan all other nodes for nearby ones
    for (uint32_t node_b = node_a + 1; node_b < state->num_nodes && conn_count_a < state->max_connections; node_b++) {
        uint32_t coord_b = state->node_coords[node_b];

        // Must be same Z level
        if (iron_coord_z(coord_b) != target_z) continue;

        uint32_t dist = iron_coord_manhattan(coord_a, coord_b);
        if (dist == 0 || dist > max_distance) continue;

        // Wire them
        uint32_t slot = atomicAdd(&state->connection_counts[node_a], 1);
        if (slot < state->max_connections) {
            state->lattice_connections[node_a * state->max_connections + slot] = node_b;
            state->edge_delay[node_a * state->max_connections + slot] = (uint8_t)(dist > 0 ? (3 + dist) : 3);
            atomicAdd(d_wires_created, 1);
            conn_count_a++;
        } else {
            if (d_wire_overflow) atomicAdd(d_wire_overflow, 1);  // Track overflow
            break;  // No more slots
        }
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// KERNEL: EDGE IMPEDANCE FILTER (from xyzt4.c edge weight+delay)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Edges need N consistent signals before they output changes (RC time constant).
// This filters noise and allows only stable patterns to propagate.
__global__ void ob1_edge_impedance(
    IronState* state,
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ input_tokens,
    uint32_t count
) {
    uint32_t node = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (node >= state->num_nodes) return;

    uint32_t conn_count = state->connection_counts[node];
    if (conn_count == 0) return;

    // Get current input at this node
    uint8_t input_hash = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < count && i < 4; i++) {
        input_hash ^= input_tokens[(node + i) % count];
    }

    // Process each edge
    for (uint32_t e = 0; e < conn_count && e < state->max_connections; e++) {
        uint32_t edge_idx = node * state->max_connections + e;

        uint8_t delay = state->edge_delay[edge_idx];
        uint8_t consistent = state->edge_consistent[edge_idx];
        uint8_t pending = state->edge_pending_val[edge_idx];

        // Is the new input same as pending?
        if (input_hash == pending) {
            // Consistent - increment counter
            if (consistent < 255) {
                state->edge_consistent[edge_idx] = consistent + 1;
            }
        } else {
            // Inconsistent - reset counter and update pending
            state->edge_pending_val[edge_idx] = input_hash;
            state->edge_consistent[edge_idx] = 1;
        }

        // If consistent long enough, update the XOR accumulator of connected node
        if (consistent >= delay) {
            uint32_t partner = state->lattice_connections[edge_idx];
            if (partner < state->num_nodes) {
                atomicXor(&state->xor_accumulators[partner], (uint32_t)input_hash);
            }
        }
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// KERNEL: SELF-OBSERVATION (From One_Theorem_Three_Witnesses paper)
// =============================================================================
// "The graph can watch itself answer questions, detect mistakes, adjust topology"
// For each token: classify BEFORE teaching, compare to actual, record error,
// boost edges on confusion boundary. This gave +20% accuracy improvement.
__global__ void ob1_self_observe(
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ tokens,
    uint32_t count,
    IronState* state,
    uint32_t* d_err_count,
    uint32_t* d_correct_count
) {
    int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (i >= (int)count) return;

    uint8_t actual_token = tokens[i];
    uint32_t actual_node = (actual_token * 137) % state->num_nodes;

    // Get predicted value at this node (what we THOUGHT would be here)
    uint8_t predicted = state->predicted_value[actual_node];
    uint8_t confidence = state->value_confidence[actual_node];

    // Compare prediction to actual
    bool correct = (predicted == actual_token);

    // Record error in ERR_STREAM at Z=2 level
    // The error is a 1-bit distinction: wrong=1, right=0
    state->err_stream[actual_node] = correct ? 0 : 1;
    state->conf_stream[actual_node] = (confidence > 128) ? 1 : 0;

    if (correct) {
        atomicAdd(d_correct_count, 1);
    } else {
        atomicAdd(d_err_count, 1);

        // BOOST EDGES: When wrong, strengthen edges touching the correct node
        // This shifts the decision boundary toward the correct answer
        uint32_t conn_count = state->connection_counts[actual_node];
        uint16_t boost = (uint16_t)state->self_obs_boost;

        for (uint32_t e = 0; e < conn_count && e < state->max_connections; e++) {
            uint32_t edge_idx = actual_node * state->max_connections + e;
            uint16_t current = state->edge_weight[edge_idx];
            // Saturating add to prevent overflow
            uint32_t new_weight = (uint32_t)current + (uint32_t)boost;
            state->edge_weight[edge_idx] = (new_weight > 65535) ? 65535 : (uint16_t)new_weight;
        }
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// KERNEL: Z-ORDERED EDGE PROCESSING (Pipeline Semantics)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Process edges in ascending Z-order. Within each Z, ascending Y-order.
// "The spatial address of computation IS the temporal ordering."
__global__ void ob1_process_z_ordered(
    IronState* state,
    uint32_t target_z,  // Process only edges at this Z level
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ tokens,
    uint32_t count
) {
    uint32_t node = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (node >= state->num_nodes) return;

    // Only process nodes at target Z level
    uint8_t node_z = state->node_z_level[node];
    if (node_z != target_z) return;

    uint32_t coord = state->node_coords[node];
    uint32_t conn_count = state->connection_counts[node];
    if (conn_count == 0) return;

    // Compute input at this node from tokens
    uint8_t input_hash = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < count && i < 4; i++) {
        input_hash ^= tokens[(node + i) % count];
    }

    // Process edges, weighted by edge_weight
    for (uint32_t e = 0; e < conn_count && e < state->max_connections; e++) {
        uint32_t edge_idx = node * state->max_connections + e;
        uint32_t partner = state->lattice_connections[edge_idx];
        if (partner >= state->num_nodes) continue;

        uint16_t weight = state->edge_weight[edge_idx];
        uint8_t delay = state->edge_delay[edge_idx];

        // Weight affects signal transmission (higher weight = stronger connection)
        // Threshold: weight must exceed delay * 100 to transmit
        if (weight > (uint16_t)delay * 100) {
            atomicXor(&state->xor_accumulators[partner], (uint32_t)input_hash);
        }
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// KERNEL: INLINE LEARNING EDGE PROCESSING (XYZT-style)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// "The medium literally reshapes itself as signals flow through it"
// Learning happens DURING signal propagation, not in a separate phase.
//
// Physical analogy: impedance matching in transmission lines
//   - Agreement → lower impedance (stronger transmission)
//   - Disagreement → higher impedance (reflection/memory)
//   - Noise → atrophy (slow decay)
//
// Each edge tracks:
//   - fire_agree: How many times sources agreed (both same value)
//   - fire_total: Total firings
//   - Every N firings: update weight based on agreement rate
//
__global__ void ob1_process_edges_inline_learning(
    IronState* state,
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ tokens,
    uint32_t count,
    uint32_t* d_agree_count,    // [OUT] Total agreements this batch
    uint32_t* d_disagree_count  // [OUT] Total disagreements this batch
) {
    uint32_t node = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (node >= state->num_nodes) return;

    uint32_t conn_count = state->connection_counts[node];
    if (conn_count == 0) return;

    // Compute this node's input value from recent tokens
    uint8_t node_val = 0;
    uint32_t token_offset = node % count;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < 4 && i < count; i++) {
        node_val ^= tokens[(token_offset + i) % count];
    }

    const uint8_t learn_rate = state->learn_rate;
    const uint8_t atrophy_rate = state->atrophy_rate;
    const uint8_t period = state->inline_learning_period;

    // Process each edge connected to this node
    for (uint32_t e = 0; e < conn_count && e < state->max_connections; e++) {
        uint32_t edge_idx = node * state->max_connections + e;
        uint32_t partner = state->lattice_connections[edge_idx];
        if (partner >= state->num_nodes) continue;

        // Get partner's value
        uint8_t partner_val = 0;
        uint32_t partner_offset = partner % count;
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < 4 && i < count; i++) {
            partner_val ^= tokens[(partner_offset + i) % count];
        }

        // === INLINE LEARNING: Check agreement ===
        // Agreement = sources have same bit (constructive interference)
        // In wave terms: (A+B)² = 4 when A=B, 0 when A≠B
        bool agreed = (node_val == partner_val);

        // Update per-edge counters atomically (contention is low in sparse lattice)
        uint32_t old_total = atomicAdd(&state->edge_fire_total[edge_idx], 1);
        if (agreed) {
            atomicAdd(&state->edge_fire_agree[edge_idx], 1);
            atomicAdd(d_agree_count, 1);
        } else {
            atomicAdd(d_disagree_count, 1);
        }

        // === WEIGHT UPDATE: Every N firings ===
        if ((old_total + 1) % period == 0 && old_total > 0) {
            uint32_t total = state->edge_fire_total[edge_idx];
            uint32_t agree = state->edge_fire_agree[edge_idx];

            // Calculate agreement rate (in fixed point: 0-255)
            uint32_t agree_rate_fp = (total > 0) ? (agree * 255 / total) : 128;
