            uint16_t old_weight = state->edge_weight[edge_idx];
            int32_t new_weight = old_weight;

            if (agree_rate_fp > 191) {          // >75%: Constructive interference
                // Strengthen: lower impedance, better transmission
                new_weight += learn_rate;
            } else if (agree_rate_fp < 64) {     // <25%: Destructive interference
                // Weaken: higher impedance, creates reflection/memory
                new_weight -= learn_rate;
            } else {                             // 25-75%: No clear signal
                // Atrophy: slow decay (noise doesn't deserve bandwidth)
                new_weight -= atrophy_rate;
            }

            // Clamp to valid range [256, 65535]
            // Minimum 256 ensures edge doesn't die completely
            if (new_weight < 256) new_weight = 256;
            if (new_weight > 65535) new_weight = 65535;

            state->edge_weight[edge_idx] = (uint16_t)new_weight;

            // Reset counters for next period
            state->edge_fire_agree[edge_idx] = 0;
            state->edge_fire_total[edge_idx] = 0;
        }

        // === SIGNAL TRANSMISSION (same as before) ===
        uint16_t weight = state->edge_weight[edge_idx];
        uint8_t delay = state->edge_delay[edge_idx];

        if (weight > (uint16_t)delay * 100) {
            atomicXor(&state->xor_accumulators[partner], (uint32_t)node_val);
        }
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// KERNEL: INITIALIZE INLINE LEARNING STATE
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
__global__ void ob1_init_inline_learning(IronState* state) {
    uint32_t node = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (node >= state->num_nodes) return;

    uint32_t conn_count = state->connection_counts[node];
    for (uint32_t e = 0; e < conn_count && e < state->max_connections; e++) {
        uint32_t edge_idx = node * state->max_connections + e;
        state->edge_fire_agree[edge_idx] = 0;
        state->edge_fire_total[edge_idx] = 0;
        // Don't reset edge_weight - keep existing weights
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// KERNEL: INITIALIZE SELF-OBSERVATION STATE
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
__global__ void ob1_init_self_obs(IronState* state) {
    uint32_t node = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (node >= state->num_nodes) return;

    // Initialize Z-levels based on coordinate
    uint32_t coord = state->node_coords[node];
    uint32_t z = iron_coord_z(coord);
    state->node_z_level[node] = (uint8_t)(z % 3);  // 0, 1, or 2

    // Initialize error and confidence streams
    state->err_stream[node] = 0;
    state->conf_stream[node] = 0;

    // Initialize edge weights to midpoint (32768)
    uint32_t conn_count = state->connection_counts[node];
    for (uint32_t e = 0; e < conn_count && e < state->max_connections; e++) {
        uint32_t edge_idx = node * state->max_connections + e;
        state->edge_weight[edge_idx] = 32768;  // Start at midpoint
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// KERNEL: SEED LATTICE (One-time Geometric Initialization)
// =============================================================================
// Breaks symmetry by giving every node unique E8-derived XOR and activation.
// Called ONCE at startup, before any batches are processed.
__global__ void ob1_seed_lattice(IronState* state) {
    uint32_t idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= state->num_nodes) return;

    // 1. Get Geometric Identity
    uint32_t geom = get_e8_geometry(idx);

    // 2. Seed XOR (The Crystal Structure)
    state->xor_accumulators[idx] = geom;

    // 3. Seed Activations (The Crystal Sensitivity)
    // Map geometry to [0.1, 1.0] using 137 (closure constant)
    float sensitivity = 0.1f + 0.9f * ((float)(geom % 137) / 137.0f);
    state->node_activations[idx] = sensitivity;

    // 4. Reset potentials
    state->node_potentials[idx] = 0.0f;
}

// =============================================================================
// III.6 XOR-REDUCTION DISCOVERY (GPU finds structure without CPU)
// =============================================================================
// The GPU discovers connections by XOR-reducing input patterns.
// When patterns repeat (XOR stabilizes), a connection is validated.

__device__ __forceinline__ uint32_t xor_reduce_warp(uint32_t val) {
    // Reduce 32 values across warp via XOR
    #pragma unroll
    for (int offset = 16; offset > 0; offset >>= 1) {
        val ^= __shfl_xor_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, val, offset);
    }
    return val;
}

__device__ __forceinline__ bool validate_connection_k137(uint32_t src, uint32_t dst, uint32_t xor_pattern) {
    // A connection is valid if the XOR pattern maps to a K=137 residue
    uint32_t combined = (src * 137 + dst * 377) ^ xor_pattern;
    uint32_t residue = combined % 137;
    // Valid if residue falls in the "lock zone"
    return (residue < 17) || (residue > 120);  // ~25% acceptance rate
}

// =============================================================================
// III.6b FBC OPCODE-AWARE K-SECTOR ROUTING
// =============================================================================
// Maps FBC bytecode opcodes to their resonant K-sector.
// This makes the kernel aware of semantic intent from FBC.
//
// FBC Opcode Ranges:
//   0x40-0x4F: Control Flow (JMP, BRANCH) -> K=25 (Weak/Interaction)
//   0x50-0x5F: Functions (CALL, RET)      -> K=137 (EM/Structure)
//   0x70-0x7F: Types (TYPEOF, CAST)       -> K=17 (Strong/Identity)
//   0xA0-0xAF: Semantic (INVARIANT)       -> K=17 (Strong/Identity)
//   0xB0-0xBF: Pattern (LOOP_N)           -> K=137 (EM/Rhythm)
//   Others: Stack, Arith, Vars, Objects   -> K=377 (Vacuum/Data)

__device__ __forceinline__ uint32_t get_k_sector(uint8_t opcode) {
    uint8_t class_nibble = opcode >> 4;

    switch (class_nibble) {
        case 0x4:  // Control Flow (JMP, BRANCH, LOOP, BREAK)
            return 25;   // Weak - Interaction/Choice
        case 0x5:  // Functions (CALL, RET, CLOSURE, YIELD)
            return 137;  // EM - Structure/Rhythm
        case 0xB:  // Pattern (LOOP_N, PATTERN_SUM, PATTERN_REP)
            return 137;  // EM - Rhythm/Repetition
        case 0x7:  // Types (TYPEOF, INSTANCEOF, CAST)
            return 17;   // Strong - Identity/Truth
        case 0xA:  // Semantic (INVARIANT, INTENT, PRECOND)
            return 17;   // Strong - Identity/Truth
        default:   // Stack, Arithmetic, Variables, Objects, System
            return 377;  // Vacuum - Raw data impedance
    }
}

// Get lock zone threshold for a given K-sector
__device__ __forceinline__ uint32_t get_lock_threshold(uint32_t K) {
    // Smaller K = tighter lock zone (more selective)
    // K=17:  threshold 2 (~12% acceptance)
    // K=25:  threshold 3 (~12% acceptance)
    // K=137: threshold 8 (~6% acceptance)
    // K=377: threshold 8 (~2% acceptance)
    if (K == 17) return 2;
    if (K == 25) return 3;
    if (K == 137) return 8;
    return 8;  // K=377 default
}

// =============================================================================
// III.6b LATTICE SNAP (Maps raw bytes to 12-point physics lattice)
// =============================================================================
// The 12-point lattice: {7, 13, 17, 31, 71, 73, 127, 131, 137, 233, 377, 511}
// Each input byte "snaps" to the nearest resonant point.
// This enforces the Step Cost (L2→L3 = 12) at hardware level.

__device__ __forceinline__ int lattice_snap(uint8_t raw_byte) {
    // Scale byte (0-255) to lattice range (7-511)
    float scaled = 7.0f + (float)raw_byte * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 255.0f;

    // Find nearest lattice point (binary search would be faster, but 12 is small)
    float min_dist = 1e9f;
    int best_idx = 0;

    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
        float dist = fabsf(scaled - Grids::LATTICE_12[i]);
        if (dist < min_dist) {
            min_dist = dist;
            best_idx = i;
        }
    }

    return best_idx;  // Returns lattice index (0-11)
}

// Get K-sector from lattice index (maps to routing)
__device__ __forceinline__ int lattice_to_k_sector(int lattice_idx) {
    // Lattice points grouped by K-sector:
    // K=17 (Strong):  {7, 13, 17}     → idx 0-2
    // K=25 (Weak):    {31, 71, 73}    → idx 3-5
    // K=137 (EM):     {127, 131, 137} → idx 6-8
    // K=377 (Vacuum): {233, 377, 511} → idx 9-11
    if (lattice_idx < 3) return 3;       // K=17 (Strong)
    else if (lattice_idx < 6) return 1;  // K=25 (Weak)
    else if (lattice_idx < 9) return 2;  // K=137 (EM)
    else return 0;                        // K=377 (Vacuum)
}

// =============================================================================
// III.6c BATCH PROCESSING KERNEL (Windows WDDM path)
// =============================================================================
// This kernel processes a buffer of data in ONE launch.
// No PCIe polling. Data is already in device memory via DMA.
// Each thread processes one byte, applies lattice snap, updates state.

// FIX 2: Node-centric parallelization - eliminates atomic contention
// One thread per node, processes ALL tokens for that node
// NO ATOMICS - each thread owns its node completely
__global__ void ob1_process_batch_node_centric(
    uint32_t* d_buffer,     // Device buffer (tokens)
    uint32_t buffer_size,   // How many tokens in this batch
    IronState* state        // Persistent lattice state
) {
    // FIX 3: Precompute lattice in shared memory
    __shared__ float s_lattice[12];
    if (threadIdx.x < 12) {
        s_lattice[threadIdx.x] = Grids::LATTICE_12[threadIdx.x];
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Node-centric: one thread per node
    int node_idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (node_idx >= state->num_nodes) return;
    
    // This thread OWNS this node - no contention possible
    uint32_t local_xor = state->xor_accumulators[node_idx];  // Start with existing value
    float local_potential = 0.0f;
    float activation = state->node_activations[node_idx];
    if (activation == 0.0f) activation = 0.1f;
    
    // Process ALL tokens for this node (loop is fine - no contention)
    // OPTIMIZATION: Coalesced memory access - threads in warp read consecutive tokens
    // Each thread processes tokens sequentially, but memory access is coalesced across warps
    
    // Precompute constants to reduce work in loop
    float vocab_half = (float)state->vocab_size / 2.0f;
    float vocab_inv = 1.0f / vocab_half;
    
    // Process tokens with optimized loop
    for (uint32_t token_idx = 0; token_idx < buffer_size; token_idx++) {
        // Coalesced read: threads in same warp read consecutive memory locations
        // This is optimal: thread 0 reads token[0], thread 1 reads token[1], etc.
        uint32_t raw_token = d_buffer[token_idx];
        
        // Positional encoding (optimized)
        uint32_t position_hash = ((token_idx * 137) + (token_idx >> 4) * 17);  // >>4 instead of /16
        uint32_t positioned_token = raw_token ^ position_hash;
        
        // Update XOR (no atomic needed - we own this node)
        local_xor ^= positioned_token;
        
        // Update potential (optimized math)
        uint32_t token_mod = positioned_token % state->vocab_size;
        float potential_raw = activation * ((float)token_mod - vocab_half) * vocab_inv;
        local_potential += potential_raw;
    }
    
    // Quantize XOR to lattice
    float xor_float = (float)(local_xor % 512);
    float scaled_xor = 7.0f + xor_float * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 512.0f;
    
    float min_dist = 1e9f;
    int lattice_idx_xor = 0;
    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
        float dist = fabsf(scaled_xor - s_lattice[i]);
        if (dist < min_dist) {
            min_dist = dist;
            lattice_idx_xor = i;
        }
    }
    float quantized_xor = s_lattice[lattice_idx_xor];
    
    // Quantize potential to lattice
    float scaled_pot = fabsf(local_potential) * 511.0f;
    scaled_pot = 7.0f + scaled_pot * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 511.0f;
    min_dist = 1e9f;
    int lattice_idx_pot = 0;
    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
        float dist = fabsf(scaled_pot - s_lattice[i]);
        if (dist < min_dist) {
            min_dist = dist;
            lattice_idx_pot = i;
        }
    }
    float quantized_potential = s_lattice[lattice_idx_pot] / 511.0f;
    if (local_potential < 0.0f) quantized_potential = -quantized_potential;
    
    // Single write - NO ATOMICS (we own this node)
    state->xor_accumulators[node_idx] = (uint32_t)quantized_xor;
    state->node_potentials[node_idx] += quantized_potential;
}

// Hybrid kernel: One block per node, threads split tokens
// 4096 blocks = 4096 nodes (full parallelism)
// 256 threads per block = tokens split 256 ways
// 125K tokens / 256 threads = ~488 iterations per thread (not 125K!)
__global__ void ob1_process_batch_hybrid(
    uint32_t* d_buffer,     // Device buffer (tokens)
    uint32_t buffer_size,   // How many tokens in this batch
    IronState* state        // Persistent lattice state
) {
    // One block per node
    int node_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (node_idx >= state->num_nodes) return;
    
    // Shared memory (uint32_t for consistency with xor_accumulators)
    __shared__ uint32_t s_xor[256];
    __shared__ float s_potential[256];
    __shared__ float s_lattice[12];
    
    // Load lattice into shared memory
    if (threadIdx.x < 12) {
        s_lattice[threadIdx.x] = Grids::LATTICE_12[threadIdx.x];
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Start with existing XOR value
    uint32_t local_xor = state->xor_accumulators[node_idx];
    float local_potential = 0.0f;
    float activation = state->node_activations[node_idx];
    if (activation == 0.0f) activation = 0.1f;
    
    // Precompute constants
    float vocab_half = (float)state->vocab_size / 2.0f;
    float vocab_inv = 1.0f / vocab_half;
    
    // Each thread processes tokens in strided fashion
    // Thread 0: tokens[0], tokens[256], tokens[512], ...
    // Thread 1: tokens[1], tokens[257], tokens[513], ...
    // This gives coalesced memory access AND parallelizes token processing
    for (uint32_t token_idx = threadIdx.x; token_idx < buffer_size; token_idx += blockDim.x) {
        uint32_t raw_token = d_buffer[token_idx];
        
        // Positional encoding (optimized)
        uint32_t position_hash = ((token_idx * 137) + (token_idx >> 4) * 17);
        uint32_t positioned_token = raw_token ^ position_hash;
        
        // Accumulate XOR
        local_xor ^= positioned_token;
        
        // Accumulate potential (optimized)
        uint32_t token_mod = positioned_token % state->vocab_size;
        float potential_raw = activation * ((float)token_mod - vocab_half) * vocab_inv;
        local_potential += potential_raw;
    }
    
    // Block-level reduction: combine contributions from all threads
    s_xor[threadIdx.x] = local_xor;
    s_potential[threadIdx.x] = local_potential;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Optimized tree reduction (256 → 128 → 64 → 32 → 16 → 8 → 4 → 2 → 1)
    // Use warp shuffle for first reduction (faster than shared memory)
    int lane_id = threadIdx.x & 31;
    int warp_id = threadIdx.x >> 5;
    
    // Warp-level reduction (32 threads → 1)
    #pragma unroll
    for (int offset = 16; offset > 0; offset >>= 1) {
        local_xor ^= __shfl_xor_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, local_xor, offset);
        local_potential += __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, local_potential, offset);
    }
    
    // Store warp results in shared memory
    if (lane_id == 0) {
        s_xor[warp_id] = local_xor;
        s_potential[warp_id] = local_potential;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Final reduction across warps (if more than 1 warp)
    if (warp_id == 0 && blockDim.x > 32) {
        uint32_t warp_xor = (lane_id < (blockDim.x + 31) / 32) ? s_xor[lane_id] : 0;
        float warp_pot = (lane_id < (blockDim.x + 31) / 32) ? s_potential[lane_id] : 0.0f;
        
        #pragma unroll
        for (int offset = 16; offset > 0; offset >>= 1) {
            warp_xor ^= __shfl_xor_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, warp_xor, offset);
            warp_pot += __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, warp_pot, offset);
        }
        
        if (lane_id == 0) {
            s_xor[0] = warp_xor;
            s_potential[0] = warp_pot;
        }
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Thread 0 writes final result (no atomics - block owns this node)
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        uint32_t final_xor = s_xor[0];
        float final_potential = s_potential[0];
        
        // Quantize XOR to lattice
        float xor_float = (float)(final_xor % 512);
        float scaled_xor = 7.0f + xor_float * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 512.0f;
        
        float min_dist = 1e9f;
        int lattice_idx_xor = 0;
        #pragma unroll
        for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
            float dist = fabsf(scaled_xor - s_lattice[i]);
            if (dist < min_dist) {
                min_dist = dist;
                lattice_idx_xor = i;
            }
        }
        float quantized_xor = s_lattice[lattice_idx_xor];
        
        // Quantize potential to lattice
        float scaled_pot = fabsf(final_potential) * 511.0f;
        scaled_pot = 7.0f + scaled_pot * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 511.0f;
        min_dist = 1e9f;
        int lattice_idx_pot = 0;
        #pragma unroll
        for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
            float dist = fabsf(scaled_pot - s_lattice[i]);
            if (dist < min_dist) {
                min_dist = dist;
                lattice_idx_pot = i;
            }
        }
        float quantized_potential = s_lattice[lattice_idx_pot] / 511.0f;
        if (final_potential < 0.0f) quantized_potential = -quantized_potential;
        
        // Write result (no atomics - block owns this node)
        state->xor_accumulators[node_idx] = (uint32_t)quantized_xor;
        state->node_potentials[node_idx] += quantized_potential;
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// REDUCE-THEN-APPLY (Correct algorithm: O(tokens) + O(nodes), not O(nodes*tokens))
// =============================================================================
// XOR is associative: (t0 ^ t1 ^ ... ^ tN) is the same for all nodes; compute once, apply per node.
// Potential: sum_t((token_val - half)*inv) is the same for all nodes; compute once, scale by activation per node.
// Kernel 1: Collapse entire token buffer to 2 scalars (batch_xor, batch_pot).
// Kernel 2: Apply those 2 scalars to all nodes (with lattice quantization to match existing semantics).
//
// FEEDBACK ROTATION: d_feedback modulates position_hash to prevent XOR cancellation.
// Without feedback: batch_xor is constant every replay (same tokens -> same result)
// With feedback: rotation by feedback bits creates unique hashes each batch.

__device__ __forceinline__ uint32_t rotl32(uint32_t x, uint32_t r) {
    return (x << r) | (x >> (32 - r));
}

__global__ void ob1_reduce_tokens(
    uint32_t* d_buffer,
    uint32_t total_tokens,
    uint32_t* d_batch_xor,
    float* d_batch_pot,
    uint32_t vocab_size,
    uint32_t* d_feedback  // nullable for backwards compat
) {
    __shared__ uint32_t s_xor[256];
    __shared__ float s_pot[256];
    __shared__ uint32_t s_feedback;  // Broadcast feedback to all threads

    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    int stride = blockDim.x * gridDim.x;

    // Load feedback once per block (thread 0 reads, others wait)
    if (tid == 0) {
        s_feedback = (d_feedback != nullptr) ? *d_feedback : 0;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    uint32_t feedback = s_feedback;
    uint32_t rot_amount = (feedback % 31) + 1;  // 1-31 bit rotation (never 0 or 32)

    uint32_t local_xor = 0;
    float local_pot = 0.0f;
    float vocab_half = (float)vocab_size * 0.5f;
    float vocab_inv = 1.0f / vocab_half;

    for (int i = idx; i < (int)total_tokens; i += stride) {
        uint32_t raw_token = d_buffer[i];
        uint32_t position_hash = ((i * 137) + (i >> 4) * 17);

        // ROTATION: feedback-modulated hash prevents XOR cancellation
        uint32_t modulated_hash = rotl32(position_hash ^ feedback, rot_amount);
        uint32_t positioned_token = raw_token ^ modulated_hash;

        local_xor ^= positioned_token;
        local_pot += ((float)(positioned_token % vocab_size) - vocab_half) * vocab_inv;
    }

    s_xor[tid] = local_xor;
    s_pot[tid] = local_pot;
    __syncthreads();

    for (int s = blockDim.x / 2; s > 0; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) {
            s_xor[tid] ^= s_xor[tid + s];
            s_pot[tid] += s_pot[tid + s];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }

    if (tid == 0) {
        uint32_t block_xor = s_xor[0];
        atomicXor(d_batch_xor, block_xor);
        atomicAdd(d_batch_pot, s_pot[0]);

        // Update feedback with this block's contribution (rotation accumulates)
        if (d_feedback != nullptr) {
            atomicXor(d_feedback, rotl32(block_xor, 7));  // Rotate before XOR to prevent cancellation
        }
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// KERNEL: OB1_MULTI_PROJECTION (Universal Structural Fingerprint)
// =============================================================================
// IRON MULTI-SCALE FINGERPRINT
// =============================================================================
// Layer 0: 6 floats at same scale = entropy gradient (does structure exist?)
// Layer 1: 18 floats at 3 scales = hierarchical structure (what KIND?)
//
// Window sizes:
//   2 bytes  = character-level (digrams: "th", "he", ":\n", "  ")
//   8 bytes  = word-level (tokens: "def ", "the ", "    ")
//   64 bytes = line-level (blocks: indent patterns, import sections)
//
// 3 primes × 3 windows = 9 projections = 18 floats
// Each still runs at near-bandwidth speed.
// =============================================================================

// Projection primes - different hash collision patterns
#define IRON_PRIME_0 137
#define IRON_PRIME_1 251
#define IRON_PRIME_2 397

// Window sizes for multi-scale
#define IRON_WINDOW_CHAR  2    // Character-level (digrams)
#define IRON_WINDOW_WORD  8    // Word-level (tokens)
#define IRON_WINDOW_LINE  64   // Line-level (blocks)

// Original 6-float fingerprint (Layer 0 - single scale)
struct IronFingerprint {
    float pot[3];      // Potentials from 3 projections
    uint32_t xor_[3];  // XOR values (for debugging/extended analysis)
};

// Multi-scale 18-float fingerprint (Layer 1 - hierarchical)
// [0-2] = window 2 (char), [3-5] = window 8 (word), [6-8] = window 64 (line)
struct IronMultiScale {
    float pot[9];      // 3 primes × 3 windows
    uint32_t xor_[9];  // XOR values
};

__global__ void ob1_multi_projection(
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ data,
    size_t byte_count,
    IronFingerprint* d_fingerprint
) {
    // Shared memory for 3 parallel reductions
    __shared__ float s_pot0[256];
    __shared__ float s_pot1[256];
    __shared__ float s_pot2[256];
    __shared__ uint32_t s_xor0[256];
    __shared__ uint32_t s_xor1[256];
    __shared__ uint32_t s_xor2[256];

    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    const int stride = blockDim.x * gridDim.x;

    const float vocab_half = 128.0f;
    const float vocab_inv = 1.0f / vocab_half;

    // Local accumulators for 3 projections
    float local_pot0 = 0.0f, local_pot1 = 0.0f, local_pot2 = 0.0f;
    uint32_t local_xor0 = 0, local_xor1 = 0, local_xor2 = 0;

    // Single pass over data - 3 projections simultaneously
    for (size_t i = idx; i < byte_count; i += stride) {
        uint32_t raw = data[i];

        // Position hashes with different primes
        uint32_t hash0 = (uint32_t)((i * IRON_PRIME_0) + (i >> 4) * 17);
        uint32_t hash1 = (uint32_t)((i * IRON_PRIME_1) + (i >> 4) * 17);
        uint32_t hash2 = (uint32_t)((i * IRON_PRIME_2) + (i >> 4) * 17);

        // Position-content coupling
        uint32_t pos0 = raw ^ hash0;
        uint32_t pos1 = raw ^ hash1;
        uint32_t pos2 = raw ^ hash2;

        // XOR accumulate
        local_xor0 ^= pos0;
        local_xor1 ^= pos1;
        local_xor2 ^= pos2;

        // Potential: (positioned % 256 - 128) / 128
        // This is where discrimination happens
        local_pot0 += ((float)(pos0 & 0xFF) - vocab_half) * vocab_inv;
        local_pot1 += ((float)(pos1 & 0xFF) - vocab_half) * vocab_inv;
        local_pot2 += ((float)(pos2 & 0xFF) - vocab_half) * vocab_inv;
    }

    // Store to shared
    s_pot0[tid] = local_pot0; s_pot1[tid] = local_pot1; s_pot2[tid] = local_pot2;
    s_xor0[tid] = local_xor0; s_xor1[tid] = local_xor1; s_xor2[tid] = local_xor2;
    __syncthreads();

    // Parallel reduction
    for (int s = blockDim.x / 2; s > 0; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) {
            s_pot0[tid] += s_pot0[tid + s];
            s_pot1[tid] += s_pot1[tid + s];
            s_pot2[tid] += s_pot2[tid + s];
            s_xor0[tid] ^= s_xor0[tid + s];
            s_xor1[tid] ^= s_xor1[tid + s];
            s_xor2[tid] ^= s_xor2[tid + s];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }

    // Block 0 thread 0 writes final result (only 256 atomics total)
    if (tid == 0) {
        atomicAdd(&d_fingerprint->pot[0], s_pot0[0]);
        atomicAdd(&d_fingerprint->pot[1], s_pot1[0]);
        atomicAdd(&d_fingerprint->pot[2], s_pot2[0]);
        atomicXor(&d_fingerprint->xor_[0], s_xor0[0]);
        atomicXor(&d_fingerprint->xor_[1], s_xor1[0]);
        atomicXor(&d_fingerprint->xor_[2], s_xor2[0]);
    }
}

// Launch wrapper for multi-projection (Layer 0)
inline void launch_iron_fingerprint(
    const uint8_t* d_data,
    size_t byte_count,
    IronFingerprint* d_fingerprint,
    cudaStream_t stream = 0
) {
    const int threads = 256;
    const int blocks = 256;
    cudaMemsetAsync(d_fingerprint, 0, sizeof(IronFingerprint), stream);
    ob1_multi_projection<<<blocks, threads, 0, stream>>>(
        d_data,
        byte_count,
        d_fingerprint
    );
}

// =============================================================================
// MULTI-SCALE PROJECTION (Layer 1)
// Hash window_size consecutive bytes together before position coupling
// =============================================================================
__global__ void ob1_multiscale_projection(
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ data,
    size_t byte_count,
    int window_size,       // 2, 8, or 64
    uint32_t prime,        // 137, 251, or 397
    float* d_pot,          // Single output potential
    uint32_t* d_xor        // Single output XOR
) {
    __shared__ float s_pot[256];
    __shared__ uint32_t s_xor[256];

    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    const int stride = blockDim.x * gridDim.x;

    const float vocab_half = 128.0f;
    const float vocab_inv = 1.0f / vocab_half;

    float local_pot = 0.0f;
    uint32_t local_xor = 0;

    // Number of windows we can form
    size_t num_windows = (byte_count >= (size_t)window_size) ? (byte_count - window_size + 1) : 0;

    // Process windows
    for (size_t w = idx; w < num_windows; w += stride) {
        // Hash the window: combine window_size bytes into one value
        uint32_t window_hash = 0;
        for (int b = 0; b < window_size; b++) {
            // Rolling hash: shift and XOR each byte
            window_hash = (window_hash * 31) ^ data[w + b];
        }

        // Position hash for this window
        uint32_t pos_hash = (uint32_t)((w * prime) + (w >> 4) * 17);

        // Position-content coupling at WINDOW level
        uint32_t coupled = window_hash ^ pos_hash;

        // Accumulate
        local_xor ^= coupled;
        local_pot += ((float)(coupled & 0xFF) - vocab_half) * vocab_inv;
    }

    // Store to shared
    s_pot[tid] = local_pot;
    s_xor[tid] = local_xor;
    __syncthreads();

    // Parallel reduction
    for (int s = blockDim.x / 2; s > 0; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) {
            s_pot[tid] += s_pot[tid + s];
            s_xor[tid] ^= s_xor[tid + s];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }

    // Atomic write from thread 0
    if (tid == 0) {
        atomicAdd(d_pot, s_pot[0]);
        atomicXor(d_xor, s_xor[0]);
    }
}

// Launch wrapper for multi-scale fingerprint (Layer 1)
// Runs 9 projections: 3 windows × 3 primes
inline void launch_iron_multiscale(
    const uint8_t* d_data,
    size_t byte_count,
    IronMultiScale* d_multiscale,
    cudaStream_t stream = 0
) {
    const int threads = 256;
    const int blocks = 256;

    // Zero the output
    cudaMemsetAsync(d_multiscale, 0, sizeof(IronMultiScale), stream);

    // Window sizes and primes
    const int windows[3] = {IRON_WINDOW_CHAR, IRON_WINDOW_WORD, IRON_WINDOW_LINE};
    const uint32_t primes[3] = {IRON_PRIME_0, IRON_PRIME_1, IRON_PRIME_2};

    // 9 kernel launches: 3 windows × 3 primes
    // Output layout: [w0p0, w0p1, w0p2, w1p0, w1p1, w1p2, w2p0, w2p1, w2p2]
    for (int w = 0; w < 3; w++) {
        for (int p = 0; p < 3; p++) {
            int idx = w * 3 + p;
            ob1_multiscale_projection<<<blocks, threads, 0, stream>>>(
                d_data,
                byte_count,
                windows[w],
                primes[p],
                &d_multiscale->pot[idx],
                &d_multiscale->xor_[idx]
            );
        }
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// TEMPORAL FINGERPRINT (Layer 2)
// Sliding window through data, outputting per-chunk fingerprints
// The TRAJECTORY through fingerprint-space is the representation
// =============================================================================

// Single chunk fingerprint - one block per chunk, computes all 9 projections
__global__ void ob1_temporal_chunk(
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ data,
    size_t chunk_start,        // Start position in data
    size_t chunk_size,         // Size of this chunk
    size_t data_size,          // Total data size (for bounds)
    float* d_pot,              // Output: 9 potentials for this chunk
    uint32_t* d_xor            // Output: 9 XOR values for this chunk
) {
    // Thread config: 256 threads, each handles part of the chunk
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    const int num_threads = blockDim.x;

    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float s_pot[9][256];
    __shared__ uint32_t s_xor[9][256];

    // Initialize local accumulators
    float local_pot[9] = {0};
    uint32_t local_xor[9] = {0};

    const float vocab_half = 128.0f;
    const float vocab_inv = 1.0f / vocab_half;

    // Window sizes and primes
    const int windows[3] = {2, 8, 64};
    const uint32_t primes[3] = {137, 251, 397};

    // Process chunk - each thread handles a stride through the chunk
    size_t chunk_end = min(chunk_start + chunk_size, data_size);

    for (size_t pos = chunk_start + tid; pos < chunk_end; pos += num_threads) {
        uint8_t byte_val = data[pos];
        size_t local_pos = pos - chunk_start;  // Position within chunk

        // For each window/prime combination
        for (int w = 0; w < 3; w++) {
            int win_size = windows[w];

            // Can we form a complete window starting here?
            if (pos + win_size <= chunk_end) {
                // Hash the window
                uint32_t window_hash = 0;
                for (int b = 0; b < win_size; b++) {
                    window_hash = (window_hash * 31) ^ data[pos + b];
                }

                for (int p = 0; p < 3; p++) {
                    int idx = w * 3 + p;
                    uint32_t pos_hash = (uint32_t)((local_pos * primes[p]) + (local_pos >> 4) * 17);
                    uint32_t coupled = window_hash ^ pos_hash;

                    local_xor[idx] ^= coupled;
                    local_pot[idx] += ((float)(coupled & 0xFF) - vocab_half) * vocab_inv;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // Store to shared memory
    for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) {
        s_pot[i][tid] = local_pot[i];
        s_xor[i][tid] = local_xor[i];
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Parallel reduction
    for (int s = num_threads / 2; s > 0; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) {
            for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) {
                s_pot[i][tid] += s_pot[i][tid + s];
                s_xor[i][tid] ^= s_xor[i][tid + s];
            }
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }

    // Thread 0 writes output
    if (tid == 0) {
        for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) {
            d_pot[i] = s_pot[i][0];
            d_xor[i] = s_xor[i][0];
        }
    }
}

// Temporal trajectory - outputs array of fingerprints
struct TemporalTrajectory {
    float* pot;           // [num_chunks × 9] potentials
    uint32_t* xor_;       // [num_chunks × 9] XOR values
    size_t num_chunks;
    size_t chunk_size;
    size_t stride;
};

// Launch temporal fingerprinting
inline size_t launch_iron_temporal(
    const uint8_t* d_data,
    size_t byte_count,
    size_t chunk_size,     // e.g., 256 bytes
    size_t stride,         // e.g., 64 bytes
    float* d_pot_array,    // Pre-allocated: num_chunks × 9 floats
    uint32_t* d_xor_array, // Pre-allocated: num_chunks × 9 uint32s
    cudaStream_t stream = 0
) {
    if (byte_count < chunk_size) return 0;

    size_t num_chunks = (byte_count - chunk_size) / stride + 1;

    const int threads = 256;

    // Launch one block per chunk
    for (size_t c = 0; c < num_chunks; c++) {
        size_t chunk_start = c * stride;
        ob1_temporal_chunk<<<1, threads, 0, stream>>>(
            d_data,
            chunk_start,
            chunk_size,
            byte_count,
            d_pot_array + c * 9,
            d_xor_array + c * 9
        );
    }

    return num_chunks;
}

__global__ void ob1_update_lattice(
    IronState* state,
    uint32_t* d_batch_xor,
    float* d_batch_pot
) {
    int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= (int)state->num_nodes) return;

    __shared__ float s_lattice[12];
    if (threadIdx.x < 12) {
        s_lattice[threadIdx.x] = Grids::LATTICE_12[threadIdx.x];
    }
    __syncthreads();

    uint32_t batch_xor = *d_batch_xor;
    float batch_pot = *d_batch_pot;

    uint32_t old_xor = state->xor_accumulators[idx];

    // NODE DIFFERENTIATION: Each node sees batch_xor from a different angle
    // idx & 31 gives 32 unique rotation amounts (0-31 bits)
    // This creates 32 "pixels" instead of 1 replicated value
    uint32_t rot_amount = idx & 31;
    uint32_t rotated_batch = (batch_xor << rot_amount) | (batch_xor >> (32 - rot_amount));
    uint32_t new_raw_xor = old_xor ^ rotated_batch;

    float xor_float = (float)(new_raw_xor % 512);
    float scaled_xor = 7.0f + xor_float * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 512.0f;
    float min_dist = 1e9f;
    int lattice_idx_xor = 0;
    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
        float dist = fabsf(scaled_xor - s_lattice[i]);
        if (dist < min_dist) {
            min_dist = dist;
            lattice_idx_xor = i;
        }
    }
    float quantized_xor = s_lattice[lattice_idx_xor];
    state->xor_accumulators[idx] = new_raw_xor;  // Store RAW XOR, not quantized (fixes 511 attractor bug)

    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    // POTENTIAL UPDATE (RAW STORAGE - NO QUANTIZATION)
    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

    // 1. Get node's XOR state for phase modulation
    uint32_t xor_state = state->xor_accumulators[idx];

    // 2. Get E8-seeded activation (sensitivity)
    float activation = state->node_activations[idx];
    if (activation == 0.0f) activation = 0.1f;

    // 3. Calculate phase from XOR history (0.0 to 1.0)
    float xor_phase = (float)(xor_state % 137) / 137.0f;

    // 4. Calculate RAW contribution (no quantization!)
    float raw_contribution = batch_pot * activation * (0.5f + 0.5f * xor_phase);

    // 5. Accumulate RAW value directly
    state->node_potentials[idx] += raw_contribution;
}

// =============================================================================
// MODE 4: TILED NODE-CENTRIC (The "Car Pool" Kernel)
// =============================================================================
// Concept: Grid = one block per 256 nodes. Loop over buffer in TILES of 256 tokens.
// Load tile into shared memory once; all threads read from shared (broadcast).
// Result: 256x reduction in global memory bandwidth vs hybrid.
__global__ void ob1_process_batch_tiled(
    uint32_t* d_buffer,     // Device buffer (tokens)
    uint32_t buffer_size,   // How many tokens in this batch
    IronState* state        // Persistent lattice state
) {
    const int TILE_SIZE = 256;
    __shared__ uint32_t s_tokens[TILE_SIZE];
    __shared__ float s_lattice[12];

    if (threadIdx.x < 12) {
        s_lattice[threadIdx.x] = Grids::LATTICE_12[threadIdx.x];
    }

    int node_idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    bool valid_node = (node_idx < state->num_nodes);

    uint32_t local_xor = 0;
    float local_pot = 0.0f;
    float activation = 0.0f;

    if (valid_node) {
        local_xor = state->xor_accumulators[node_idx];
        activation = state->node_activations[node_idx];
        if (activation == 0.0f) activation = 0.1f;
    }

    float vocab_half = (float)state->vocab_size / 2.0f;
    float vocab_inv = 1.0f / vocab_half;

    for (uint32_t tile_start = 0; tile_start < buffer_size; tile_start += TILE_SIZE) {
        if (tile_start + threadIdx.x < buffer_size) {
            s_tokens[threadIdx.x] = d_buffer[tile_start + threadIdx.x];
        } else {
            s_tokens[threadIdx.x] = 0;
        }
        __syncthreads();

        uint32_t limit = (buffer_size - tile_start < (uint32_t)TILE_SIZE) ? (buffer_size - tile_start) : (uint32_t)TILE_SIZE;

        if (valid_node) {
            #pragma unroll 4
            for (uint32_t i = 0; i < limit; i++) {
                uint32_t raw_token = s_tokens[i];
                uint32_t abs_pos = tile_start + i;
                uint32_t position_hash = ((abs_pos * 137) + (abs_pos >> 4) * 17);
                uint32_t positioned_token = raw_token ^ position_hash;

                local_xor ^= positioned_token;

                uint32_t token_mod = positioned_token % state->vocab_size;
                float potential_raw = activation * ((float)token_mod - vocab_half) * vocab_inv;
                local_pot += potential_raw;
            }
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }

    if (valid_node) {
        float xor_float = (float)(local_xor % 512);
        float scaled_xor = 7.0f + xor_float * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 512.0f;

        float min_dist = 1e9f;
        int lattice_idx_xor = 0;
        #pragma unroll
        for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
            float dist = fabsf(scaled_xor - s_lattice[i]);
            if (dist < min_dist) {
                min_dist = dist;
                lattice_idx_xor = i;
            }
        }
        float quantized_xor = s_lattice[lattice_idx_xor];

        float scaled_pot = fabsf(local_pot) * 511.0f;
        scaled_pot = 7.0f + scaled_pot * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 511.0f;
        min_dist = 1e9f;
        int lattice_idx_pot = 0;
        #pragma unroll
        for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
            float dist = fabsf(scaled_pot - s_lattice[i]);
            if (dist < min_dist) {
                min_dist = dist;
                lattice_idx_pot = i;
            }
        }
        float quantized_potential = s_lattice[lattice_idx_pot] / 511.0f;
        if (local_pot < 0.0f) quantized_potential = -quantized_potential;

        state->xor_accumulators[node_idx] = (uint32_t)quantized_xor;
        state->node_potentials[node_idx] += quantized_potential;
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// MODE 5: MINI-TILED (High Occupancy - "Minivan Fleet")
// =============================================================================
// Tile = 32 (one warp). Blocks = 128 (4096/32). Bandwidth reduction 32x, fills GPU.
__global__ void ob1_process_batch_tiled_mini(
    uint32_t* d_buffer,
    uint32_t buffer_size,
    IronState* state
) {
    const int TILE_SIZE = 32;
    __shared__ uint32_t s_tokens[TILE_SIZE];
    __shared__ float s_lattice[12];

    if (threadIdx.x < 12) s_lattice[threadIdx.x] = Grids::LATTICE_12[threadIdx.x];

    int node_idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    bool valid_node = (node_idx < state->num_nodes);

    uint32_t local_xor = 0;
    float local_pot = 0.0f;
    float activation = 0.0f;

    if (valid_node) {
        local_xor = state->xor_accumulators[node_idx];
        activation = state->node_activations[node_idx];
        if (activation == 0.0f) activation = 0.1f;
    }

    float vocab_half = (float)state->vocab_size / 2.0f;
    float vocab_inv = 1.0f / vocab_half;

    for (uint32_t tile_start = 0; tile_start < buffer_size; tile_start += TILE_SIZE) {
        if (tile_start + threadIdx.x < buffer_size) {
            s_tokens[threadIdx.x] = d_buffer[tile_start + threadIdx.x];
        } else {
            s_tokens[threadIdx.x] = 0;
        }
        __syncthreads();

        uint32_t limit = (buffer_size - tile_start < (uint32_t)TILE_SIZE) ? (buffer_size - tile_start) : (uint32_t)TILE_SIZE;

        if (valid_node) {
            #pragma unroll
            for (uint32_t i = 0; i < limit; i++) {
                uint32_t raw_token = s_tokens[i];
                uint32_t abs_pos = tile_start + i;
                uint32_t position_hash = ((abs_pos * 137) + (abs_pos >> 4) * 17);
                uint32_t positioned_token = raw_token ^ position_hash;

                local_xor ^= positioned_token;
                uint32_t token_mod = positioned_token % state->vocab_size;
                local_pot += activation * ((float)token_mod - vocab_half) * vocab_inv;
            }
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }

    if (valid_node) {
        float xor_float = (float)(local_xor % 512);
        float scaled_xor = 7.0f + xor_float * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 512.0f;

        float min_dist = 1e9f;
        int lattice_idx_xor = 0;
        #pragma unroll
        for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
            float dist = fabsf(scaled_xor - s_lattice[i]);
            if (dist < min_dist) { min_dist = dist; lattice_idx_xor = i; }
        }
        float quantized_xor = s_lattice[lattice_idx_xor];

        float scaled_pot = fabsf(local_pot) * 511.0f;
        scaled_pot = 7.0f + scaled_pot * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 511.0f;
        min_dist = 1e9f;
        int lattice_idx_pot = 0;
        #pragma unroll
        for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
            float dist = fabsf(scaled_pot - s_lattice[i]);
            if (dist < min_dist) { min_dist = dist; lattice_idx_pot = i; }
        }
        float quantized_potential = s_lattice[lattice_idx_pot] / 511.0f;
        if (local_pot < 0.0f) quantized_potential = -quantized_potential;

        state->xor_accumulators[node_idx] = (uint32_t)quantized_xor;
        state->node_potentials[node_idx] += quantized_potential;
    }
}

// FIX 2: 2D parallelized version - eliminates nested loops (kept for comparison)
__global__ void ob1_process_batch_2d(
    uint32_t* d_buffer,     // Device buffer (tokens)
    uint32_t buffer_size,   // How many tokens in this batch
    IronState* state        // Persistent lattice state
) {
    // FIX 3: Precompute lattice in shared memory
    __shared__ float s_lattice[12];
    if (threadIdx.x < 12 && threadIdx.y == 0) {
        s_lattice[threadIdx.x] = Grids::LATTICE_12[threadIdx.x];
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    // FIX 2: 2D grid - one thread per (token, node) pair
    // blockIdx.x = token block, blockIdx.y = node block
    // threadIdx.x = token within block, threadIdx.y = node within block
    int token_idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    int node_idx = blockIdx.y * blockDim.y + threadIdx.y;
    
    // Bounds check
    if (token_idx >= buffer_size || node_idx >= state->num_nodes) return;
    
    // Process ONE token-node pair (no nested loops!)
    uint32_t raw_token = d_buffer[token_idx];
    
    // Positional encoding
    uint32_t position_hash = ((token_idx * 137) + (token_idx / 16) * 17);
    uint32_t positioned_token = raw_token ^ position_hash;
    
    // Lattice snap (L1)
    float scaled = 7.0f + (float)(positioned_token % state->vocab_size) * (511.0f - 7.0f) / (float)state->vocab_size;
    
    float min_dist = 1e9f;
    int lattice_idx = 0;
    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
        float dist = fabsf(scaled - s_lattice[i]);
        if (dist < min_dist) {
            min_dist = dist;
            lattice_idx = i;
        }
    }
    
    // Update XOR accumulator for THIS node (native 32-bit atomicXor - no cast, no misalignment)
    atomicXor(&state->xor_accumulators[node_idx], positioned_token);
    
    // Read back accumulated XOR (native 32-bit)
    uint32_t current_xor = state->xor_accumulators[node_idx];
    
    // Quantize XOR to lattice
    float xor_float = (float)(current_xor % 512);
    float scaled_xor = 7.0f + xor_float * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 512.0f;
    
    min_dist = 1e9f;
    int lattice_idx_xor = 0;
    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
        float dist = fabsf(scaled_xor - s_lattice[i]);
        if (dist < min_dist) {
            min_dist = dist;
            lattice_idx_xor = i;
        }
    }
    
    // Don't store quantized back - let XOR accumulate naturally
    // Quantization happens on read, not write
    
    // Update potential (atomic add)
    float activation = state->node_activations[node_idx];
    if (activation == 0.0f) activation = 0.1f;
    float potential_raw = activation * (float)((positioned_token % state->vocab_size) - (state->vocab_size / 2)) / (float)(state->vocab_size / 2);
    
    // Quantize potential to lattice
    float scaled_pot = fabsf(potential_raw) * 511.0f;
    scaled_pot = 7.0f + scaled_pot * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 511.0f;
    min_dist = 1e9f;
    int lattice_idx_pot = 0;
    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
        float dist = fabsf(scaled_pot - s_lattice[i]);
        if (dist < min_dist) {
            min_dist = dist;
            lattice_idx_pot = i;
        }
    }
    float quantized_potential = s_lattice[lattice_idx_pot] / 511.0f;
    if (potential_raw < 0.0f) quantized_potential = -quantized_potential;
    
    atomicAdd(&state->node_potentials[node_idx], quantized_potential);
}

// Original 1D version (kept for comparison/fallback)
__global__ void ob1_process_batch(
    uint32_t* d_buffer,     // Device buffer (tokens)
    uint32_t buffer_size,   // How many tokens in this batch
    IronState* state        // Persistent lattice state
) {
    // FIX 3: Precompute lattice in shared memory (faster than global memory)
    __shared__ float s_lattice[12];
    if (threadIdx.x < 12) {
        s_lattice[threadIdx.x] = Grids::LATTICE_12[threadIdx.x];
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    const uint32_t tid = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    const uint32_t stride = gridDim.x * blockDim.x;

    // Each block owns a subset of nodes (same as persistent kernel)
    const int nodes_per_block = (state->num_nodes + gridDim.x - 1) / gridDim.x;
    const int node_start = blockIdx.x * nodes_per_block;
    const int node_end = min(node_start + nodes_per_block, (int)state->num_nodes);

    // Process all tokens in buffer (strided access for coalescing)
    for (uint32_t token_idx = tid; token_idx < buffer_size; token_idx += stride) {
        uint32_t raw_token = d_buffer[token_idx];

        // =================================================================
        // POSITIONAL ENCODING: D(token) ⊗ D(position) → lattice
        // =================================================================
        // Uses 137 (fine structure) and 17 (confinement) as position modulators.
        uint32_t position_hash = ((token_idx * 137) + (token_idx / 16) * 17);
        uint32_t positioned_token = raw_token ^ position_hash;

        // =================================================================
        // STEP 1: LATTICE SNAP (L1 - Distinguish)
        // =================================================================
        // Scale token to lattice range (7-511)
        float scaled = 7.0f + (float)(positioned_token % state->vocab_size) * (511.0f - 7.0f) / (float)state->vocab_size;
        
        // FIX 3: Use shared memory lattice (faster)
        float min_dist = 1e9f;
        int lattice_idx = 0;
        #pragma unroll
        for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
            float dist = fabsf(scaled - s_lattice[i]);  // Shared memory
            if (dist < min_dist) {
                min_dist = dist;
                lattice_idx = i;
            }
        }
        
        int k_sector = lattice_to_k_sector(lattice_idx);
        float lattice_value = s_lattice[lattice_idx];  // Use shared memory

        // =================================================================
        // STEP 2: XOR-REDUCTION (L2 - Relate) + LATTICE QUANTIZATION
        // =================================================================
        // Update XOR accumulators for nodes this thread is responsible for
        // NEW: Quantize XOR values to resonant lattice (validated: 76% vs 69% uniform)
        for (int node = node_start + (tid % blockDim.x); node < node_end; node += blockDim.x) {
            uint32_t old_xor = state->xor_accumulators[node];
            uint32_t new_xor = old_xor ^ positioned_token;
            
            // NEW: Quantize XOR accumulator to 12-point resonant lattice
            // Scale XOR value (0-2^32) to lattice range (7-511)
            float xor_float = (float)(new_xor % 512);  // Modulo to keep in range
            float scaled_xor = 7.0f + xor_float * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 512.0f;
            
            // Find nearest lattice point (using shared memory)
            float min_dist = 1e9f;
            int lattice_idx_xor = 0;
            #pragma unroll
            for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
                float dist = fabsf(scaled_xor - s_lattice[i]);  // Shared memory
                if (dist < min_dist) {
                    min_dist = dist;
                    lattice_idx_xor = i;
                }
            }
            
            // Store quantized XOR value
            float quantized_xor = s_lattice[lattice_idx_xor];  // Shared memory
            state->xor_accumulators[node] = (uint32_t)quantized_xor;

            // Accumulate potential (also quantized to lattice)
            float activation = state->node_activations[node];
            if (activation == 0.0f) activation = 0.1f;
            float potential_raw = activation * (float)((positioned_token % state->vocab_size) - (state->vocab_size / 2)) / (float)(state->vocab_size / 2);
            
            // NEW: Quantize potential to lattice (using shared memory)
            float scaled_pot = fabsf(potential_raw) * 511.0f;  // Scale to [0,511]
            scaled_pot = 7.0f + scaled_pot * (511.0f - 7.0f) / 511.0f;
            min_dist = 1e9f;
            int lattice_idx_pot = 0;
            #pragma unroll
            for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
                float dist = fabsf(scaled_pot - s_lattice[i]);  // Shared memory
                if (dist < min_dist) {
                    min_dist = dist;
                    lattice_idx_pot = i;
                }
            }
            float quantized_potential = s_lattice[lattice_idx_pot] / 511.0f;  // Shared memory, back to original scale
            if (potential_raw < 0.0f) quantized_potential = -quantized_potential;
            state->node_potentials[node] += quantized_potential;
        }
    }

    __syncthreads();

    // =================================================================
    // STEP 3: CRYSTALLIZATION CHECK (L3 - Change, L4 - Embed)
    // =================================================================
    // After processing all bytes, check for structure formation
    for (int node = node_start + (tid % blockDim.x); node < node_end; node += blockDim.x) {
        uint32_t xor_val = state->xor_accumulators[node];
        float potential = state->node_potentials[node];

        // K-sector routing based on XOR pattern
        uint32_t K = Grids::K_VALUES[xor_val % 4];
        uint32_t threshold = K / 17;  // Lock threshold

        bool stabilized = (xor_val % K) < threshold;

        if (stabilized && xor_val > 0 && fabsf(potential) > 0.001f) {
            uint32_t conn_count = state->connection_counts[node];

            if (conn_count < state->max_connections) {
                uint32_t partner = (node ^ (xor_val & 0x3FF)) % state->num_nodes;

                if (partner != node && validate_connection_k137(node, partner, xor_val)) {
                    uint32_t slot = atomicAdd(&state->connection_counts[node], 1);
                    if (slot < state->max_connections) {
                        state->lattice_connections[node * state->max_connections + slot] = partner;
                    }

                    // Lock node if enough connections
                    if (slot + 1 >= 4) {
                        state->node_locked[node] = 1;
                        uint8_t sector_idx = (K == 377) ? 0 : (K == 25) ? 1 : (K == 137) ? 2 : 3;
                        state->sector_assignment[node] = sector_idx;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // Decay potential (clock tick)
        state->node_potentials[node] *= 0.99f;

        // Update activation for locked nodes
        // CRITICAL: Include potential to break the circular 0→0 dependency
        // Without this, all activations stay 0 and L4 can't learn
        if (state->node_locked[node]) {
            uint32_t conn_count = state->connection_counts[node];
            float sum = 0.0f;

            for (uint32_t c = 0; c < conn_count && c < state->max_connections; c++) {
                uint32_t partner = state->lattice_connections[node * state->max_connections + c];
                sum += state->node_activations[partner];
            }

            // Add own potential to break circular dependency
            // Also add a base activation from the XOR pattern to ensure non-zero activations
            float potential_contrib = state->node_potentials[node];
            float xor_contrib = (float)(state->xor_accumulators[node] & 0xFF) / 255.0f - 0.5f;

            if (conn_count > 0) {
                state->node_activations[node] = tanhf(sum / conn_count + potential_contrib + xor_contrib);
            } else {
                // No connections yet - use potential and xor directly
                state->node_activations[node] = tanhf(potential_contrib + xor_contrib);
            }
        }
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// III.6.5 PREDICTIVE TRAINING KERNEL - Next-Byte Prediction (L4 Output Head)
// =============================================================================
// This is the FIX: Train D(internal) → output, not just D(input) → internal
//
// For bytes[0:t], predict byte[t+1]:
//   1. Process bytes[0:t] through recognition (L1-L3)
//   2. Aggregate K-sector activations
//   3. Project to 256 logits via learned weights
//   4. Softmax → probability distribution
//   5. Cross-entropy loss against actual byte[t+1]
//   6. Update sector_to_byte weights
//
// Generation emerges: forward pass IS prediction, sample and repeat.

__global__ void ob1_predictive_batch(
    uint32_t* d_buffer,     // Input tokens
    uint32_t buffer_size,   // How many tokens
    IronState* state,       // Persistent state
    float learning_rate     // For output head updates
) {
    // Shared memory for max/sum (logits are in global memory state->token_logits)
    __shared__ float s_max_logit;
    __shared__ float s_sum_exp;
    __shared__ uint32_t s_num_locked;

    const uint32_t tid = threadIdx.x;
    const uint32_t num_nodes = state->num_nodes;
    const uint32_t vocab_size = state->vocab_size;

    if (tid == 0) {
        s_num_locked = 0;
        s_sum_exp = 0.0f;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Training mode: last token is target, rest is context
    bool inference_mode = (learning_rate == 0.0f);
    uint32_t target_token = 0;

    if (inference_mode) {
        if (buffer_size < 1) return;
    } else {
        if (buffer_size < 2) return;
        target_token = d_buffer[buffer_size - 1] % vocab_size;
    }

    // === PHASE 1: Count locked nodes ===
    for (uint32_t node = tid; node < num_nodes; node += blockDim.x) {
        if (state->node_locked[node]) {
            atomicAdd(&s_num_locked, 1);
        }
    }
    __syncthreads();

    if (s_num_locked == 0) return;

    // === PHASE 2: Project LOCKED node activations → vocab_size logits ===
    for (uint32_t b = tid; b < vocab_size; b += blockDim.x) {
        float logit = 0.0f;
        for (uint32_t node = 0; node < num_nodes; node++) {
            if (state->node_locked[node]) {
                float activation = fabsf(state->node_activations[node]);
                logit += activation * state->node_to_token[node * vocab_size + b];
            }
        }
        state->token_logits[b] = logit;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // === PHASE 3: Softmax (numerically stable) ===
    if (tid == 0) {
        s_max_logit = state->token_logits[0];
        for (uint32_t i = 1; i < vocab_size; i++) {
            if (state->token_logits[i] > s_max_logit) s_max_logit = state->token_logits[i];
        }
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Compute sum of exps
    float local_sum = 0.0f;
    for (uint32_t b = tid; b < vocab_size; b += blockDim.x) {
        local_sum += expf(state->token_logits[b] - s_max_logit);
    }
    
    // Warp reduction for local_sum
    for (int offset = 16; offset > 0; offset >>= 1) {
        local_sum += __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, local_sum, offset);
    }
    
    if ((tid % 32) == 0) {
        atomicAdd(&s_sum_exp, local_sum);
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // === PHASE 4: Cross-entropy loss (training only) ===
    if (!inference_mode) {
        float prob_target = expf(state->token_logits[target_token] - s_max_logit) / (s_sum_exp + 1e-10f);
        float loss = -logf(prob_target + 1e-10f);

        if (tid == 0) {
            atomicAdd(&state->prediction_loss, loss);
            atomicAdd((unsigned long long*)&state->prediction_count, 1ULL);
        }

        // Backprop to weights
        for (uint32_t node = tid; node < num_nodes; node += blockDim.x) {
            if (state->node_locked[node]) {
                float activation = fabsf(state->node_activations[node]);
                // Gradient for target: activation * (prob - 1)
                // Gradient for others: activation * prob
                for (uint32_t b = 0; b < vocab_size; b++) {
                    float prob = expf(state->token_logits[b] - s_max_logit) / (s_sum_exp + 1e-10f);
                    float grad = activation * (prob - (b == target_token ? 1.0f : 0.0f));
                    atomicAdd(&state->node_to_token[node * vocab_size + b], -learning_rate * grad);
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// III.6.6 GENERATION KERNEL - Sample next token from distribution
// =============================================================================
// After ob1_predictive_batch computes byte_logits, sample the next byte.
// Temperature scaling + sampling from the distribution.

__global__ void ob1_sample_next_token(
    IronState* state,
    float temperature,
    uint32_t random_seed,
    uint32_t* output_token    // Single token output
) {
    if (threadIdx.x != 0 || blockIdx.x != 0) return;

    uint32_t rng = random_seed;
    rng = rng * 1664525u + 1013904223u;
    float u = (float)(rng & 0xFFFFFF) / (float)0x1000000;

    // Temperature scaling
    float max_logit = -1e30f;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < state->vocab_size; i++) {
        if (state->token_logits[i] > max_logit) max_logit = state->token_logits[i];
    }

    float sum_exp = 0.0f;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < state->vocab_size; i++) {
        sum_exp += expf((state->token_logits[i] - max_logit) / temperature);
    }

    float cumsum = 0.0f;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < state->vocab_size; i++) {
        cumsum += expf((state->token_logits[i] - max_logit) / temperature) / sum_exp;
        if (u < cumsum) {
            *output_token = i;
            return;
        }
    }
    *output_token = state->vocab_size - 1;
}

// =============================================================================
// III.7 PERSISTENT DISCOVERY KERNEL (NEVER EXITS) - Linux Zero-Copy Path
// =============================================================================
// This kernel runs FOREVER. It is a hardware state machine.
// The GPU is the master. The CPU only feeds the bitstream.
// NOTE: Only used on Linux where zero-copy is efficient.

__global__ void ob1_persistent_router(IronState* state) {
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;

    // Each block owns a subset of nodes
    const int nodes_per_block = (state->num_nodes + gridDim.x - 1) / gridDim.x;
    const int node_start = blockIdx.x * nodes_per_block;
    const int node_end = min(node_start + nodes_per_block, (int)state->num_nodes);

    // === THE INFINITE LOOP (GPU IS MASTER) ===
    while (*state->terminate == 0) {
        __shared__ uint32_t block_input;
        __shared__ int block_has_data;

        // =====================================================================
        // PHASE 1: SAMPLE BITSTREAM (Read the tokens)
        // =====================================================================
        // FIX: Only block 0, thread 0 manages bitstream to prevent race
        // All blocks read the same input token via shared memory broadcast

        if (blockIdx.x == 0 && tid == 0) {
            __threadfence_system();
            uint32_t head = *state->bitstream_head;
            uint32_t tail = *state->bitstream_tail;

            if (tail < head) {
                uint32_t read_pos = tail % state->bitstream_size;
                block_input = state->bitstream[read_pos];
                block_has_data = 1;
                *state->bitstream_tail = tail + 1;
                __threadfence_system();
            } else {
                block_has_data = 0;
            }
        }

        // Step 2: All blocks sync and check for data
        // Non-zero blocks read the current byte from bitstream directly
        // (they're processing the same input in parallel)
        __syncthreads();

        if (blockIdx.x != 0) {
            uint32_t current_tail = *state->bitstream_tail;
            if (current_tail > 0) {
                uint32_t read_pos = (current_tail - 1) % state->bitstream_size;
                block_input = state->bitstream[read_pos];
                block_has_data = 1;
            } else {
                block_has_data = 0;
            }
        }
        __syncthreads();

        if (!block_has_data) {
            __nanosleep(1000000);
            continue;
        }

        uint32_t input_token = block_input;

        // Each thread processes its assigned nodes
        for (int node = node_start + tid; node < node_end; node += blockDim.x) {
            uint32_t old_xor = state->xor_accumulators[node];
            uint32_t new_xor = old_xor ^ input_token;
            state->xor_accumulators[node] = new_xor;

            float activation = state->node_activations[node];
            if (activation == 0.0f) {
                activation = 0.1f;
                state->node_activations[node] = activation;
            }
            float potential = activation * (float)((input_token % state->vocab_size) - (state->vocab_size / 2)) / (float)(state->vocab_size / 2);
            state->node_potentials[node] += potential;
        }
        __syncthreads();

        // Lattice node validation
        for (int node = node_start + tid; node < node_end; node += blockDim.x) {
            uint32_t xor_val = state->xor_accumulators[node];
            float potential = state->node_potentials[node];

            uint32_t K = Grids::K_VALUES[xor_val % 4];
            uint32_t threshold = K / 17;

            if ((xor_val % K) < threshold && xor_val > 0 && fabsf(potential) > 0.001f) {
                uint32_t conn_count = state->connection_counts[node];
                if (conn_count < state->max_connections) {
                    uint32_t partner = (node ^ (xor_val & 0x3FF)) % state->num_nodes;
                    if (partner != node && validate_connection_k137(node, partner, xor_val)) {
                        uint32_t slot = atomicAdd(&state->connection_counts[node], 1);
                        if (slot < state->max_connections) {
                            state->lattice_connections[node * state->max_connections + slot] = partner;
                        }
                        if (slot + 1 >= 4) {
                            state->node_locked[node] = 1;
                            uint8_t sector_idx = (K == 377) ? 0 : (K == 25) ? 1 : (K == 137) ? 2 : 3;
                            state->sector_assignment[node] = sector_idx;
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
            state->node_potentials[node] *= 0.9999f;
        }

        // Yield after processing - balance throughput vs power
        // 10us = reasonable for sustained processing, not burning GPU
        __nanosleep(10000);
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// III.8 IRON ENGINE INITIALIZATION
// =============================================================================

IronState* init_iron_state(uint32_t num_nodes, uint32_t max_connections, uint32_t bitstream_size, uint32_t vocab_size) {
    IronState* h_state = (IronState*)malloc(sizeof(IronState));
    h_state->num_nodes = num_nodes;
    h_state->max_connections = max_connections;
    h_state->bitstream_size = bitstream_size;

    // Allocate GPU-side persistent state (GPU-owned)
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->lattice_connections, num_nodes * max_connections * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->connection_counts, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->node_activations, num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->node_potentials, num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->node_locked, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->previous_lattice_idx, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->xor_accumulators, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->discovery_candidates, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->sector_assignment, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));

    // L4 OUTPUT HEAD: Locked nodes → vocab_size predictions (SPARSE)
    h_state->vocab_size = vocab_size;
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->node_to_token, num_nodes * vocab_size * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->token_logits, vocab_size * sizeof(float));
    h_state->prediction_loss = 0.0f;
    h_state->prediction_count = 0;
    h_state->predictive_mode = true;

    // === ONETWO/XYZT UPGRADES ALLOCATION ===
    // VALUE-AT-POSITION (from onetwo_engine.py SpatialEngine.value_map)
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->predicted_value, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->value_confidence, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));

    // Per-position transitions (from onetwo_engine.py SpatialEngine.pos_trans)
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->pos_transitions, num_nodes * 16 * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->trans_confidence, num_nodes * 16 * sizeof(uint8_t));

    // Flow maps (from onetwo_engine.py SpatialEngine.flow)
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->next_node_pred, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->next_node_hits, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->next_node_total, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Packed coordinates (from xyzt4.c coord_pack)
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->node_coords, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Edge impedance (from xyzt4.c edge weight+delay)
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->edge_delay, num_nodes * max_connections * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->edge_consistent, num_nodes * max_connections * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_state->edge_pending_val, num_nodes * max_connections * sizeof(uint8_t));

    // Initialize to zero
    cudaMemset(h_state->lattice_connections, 0, num_nodes * max_connections * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_state->connection_counts, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_state->node_activations, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemset(h_state->node_potentials, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemset(h_state->node_locked, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_state->previous_lattice_idx, 0xFF, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t)); // 0xFF = Unknown
    cudaMemset(h_state->xor_accumulators, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_state->sector_assignment, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));

    // === ONETWO/XYZT UPGRADES INITIALIZATION ===
    cudaMemset(h_state->predicted_value, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_state->value_confidence, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_state->pos_transitions, 0, num_nodes * 16 * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_state->trans_confidence, 0, num_nodes * 16 * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_state->next_node_pred, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_state->next_node_hits, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_state->next_node_total, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_state->edge_delay, 3, num_nodes * max_connections * sizeof(uint8_t)); // Default delay=3
    cudaMemset(h_state->edge_consistent, 0, num_nodes * max_connections * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_state->edge_pending_val, 0, num_nodes * max_connections * sizeof(uint8_t));

    // Initialize node coordinates in a 16x16x16 grid (XYZT-compatible)
    uint32_t* h_coords = (uint32_t*)malloc(num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; i++) {
        uint32_t x = i & 0xF;
        uint32_t y = (i >> 4) & 0xF;
        uint32_t z = (i >> 8) & 0xF;
        // Pack: X[31:22] Y[21:12] Z[11:4] flags[3:0]
        h_coords[i] = ((x & 0x3FF) << 22) | ((y & 0x3FF) << 12) | ((z & 0xFF) << 4);
    }
    cudaMemcpy(h_state->node_coords, h_coords, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);
    free(h_coords);

    // Initialize L4 output head with small random weights
    size_t l4_size = (size_t)num_nodes * vocab_size * sizeof(float);
    float* h_node_to_token = (float*)malloc(l4_size);
    float scale = 0.01f / sqrtf((float)num_nodes);
    for (size_t i = 0; i < (size_t)num_nodes * vocab_size; i++) {
        h_node_to_token[i] = ((float)rand() / RAND_MAX - 0.5f) * scale;
    }
    cudaMemcpy(h_state->node_to_token, h_node_to_token, l4_size, cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);
    free(h_node_to_token);
    cudaMemset(h_state->token_logits, 0, vocab_size * sizeof(float));

    // ZERO-COPY: Control signals (CPU writes, GPU reads directly)
    cudaHostAlloc((void**)&h_state->terminate, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaHostAllocMapped);
    cudaHostAlloc((void**)&h_state->bitstream_head, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaHostAllocMapped);
    cudaHostAlloc((void**)&h_state->bitstream_tail, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaHostAllocMapped);
    *h_state->terminate = 0;
    *h_state->bitstream_head = 0;
    *h_state->bitstream_tail = 0;

    // ZERO-COPY: Bitstream buffer (CPU writes, GPU reads without cudaMemcpy)
    cudaHostAlloc((void**)&h_state->bitstream, bitstream_size * sizeof(uint32_t), cudaHostAllocMapped);
    memset((void*)h_state->bitstream, 0, bitstream_size * sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Copy state struct to device
    IronState* d_state;
    cudaMalloc(&d_state, sizeof(IronState));
    cudaMemcpy(d_state, h_state, sizeof(IronState), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    h_state->d_iron_state = d_state;
    return h_state;
}

// Feed data to the Iron (CPU just writes to zero-copy buffer)
void iron_feed(IronState* h_state, const uint32_t* data, uint32_t size) {
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < size; i++) {
        uint32_t pos = (*h_state->bitstream_head) % h_state->bitstream_size;
        h_state->bitstream[pos] = data[i];
        (*h_state->bitstream_head)++;
    }
}

// Launch the persistent router (GPU takes over)
void iron_start(IronState* d_state) {
    // Launch with enough blocks to cover all nodes
    // This kernel NEVER RETURNS until terminated
    ob1_persistent_router<<<128, 256>>>(d_state);
}

// Signal termination (GPU will exit on next iteration)
void iron_stop(IronState* h_state) {
    *h_state->terminate = 1;
    cudaDeviceSynchronize();  // Wait for kernel to exit
}

// =============================================================================
// III.8.5 ONETWO PROCESSING - Combined XYZT Upgrades
// =============================================================================
// Runs all ONETWO/XYZT kernels on a batch of tokens:
// 1. VALUE-AT-POSITION prediction (SpatialEngine insight)
// 2. Flow map update (sequence prediction)
// 3. Per-position transitions (spatial Hebbian learning)
// 4. Edge impedance filtering (RC time constant)
//
// Returns: ONETWO stats (vap_rate, flow_rate)

struct OneTwo_Stats {
    uint32_t vap_hits;
    uint32_t vap_total;
    uint32_t flow_hits;
    uint32_t flow_total;
    uint32_t err_count;       // Self-observation errors
    uint32_t correct_count;   // Self-observation correct
    uint32_t agree_count;     // Inline learning: source agreements
    uint32_t disagree_count;  // Inline learning: source disagreements
    float vap_rate;           // VALUE-AT-POSITION accuracy %
    float flow_rate;          // Flow prediction accuracy %
    float self_obs_rate;      // Self-observation accuracy %
    float agree_rate;         // Inline learning: agreement rate %
    uint8_t inline_mode;      // 1 if inline learning was used
};

// =============================================================================
// PRE-ALLOCATED BUFFER POOL (eliminates cudaMalloc/cudaFree per call)
// =============================================================================
// 9 allocations per onetwo_process call was killing throughput (0.09 MB/s).
// Pre-allocate once at startup, reuse forever. cudaMemset is ~1000x cheaper
// than cudaMalloc.

#define ONETWO_MAX_TOKENS (4 * 1024 * 1024)  // 4 MB max per call

struct OneTwoBuffers {
    // Token buffer (reused each call, up to 4MB)
    uint8_t* d_tokens;

    // Counter buffers (8 x uint32_t, pre-allocated)
    uint32_t* d_vap_hits;
    uint32_t* d_vap_total;
    uint32_t* d_flow_hits;
    uint32_t* d_flow_total;
    uint32_t* d_err_count;
    uint32_t* d_correct_count;
    uint32_t* d_agree_count;
    uint32_t* d_disagree_count;

    // CUDA stream for async ops
    cudaStream_t stream;

    // Pinned host staging buffer for fast H2D copies
    uint8_t* h_pinned;

    bool initialized;
};

// Allocate buffer pool once at startup
inline void onetwo_buffers_init(OneTwoBuffers* buf) {
    cudaMalloc(&buf->d_tokens, ONETWO_MAX_TOKENS);
    cudaMalloc(&buf->d_vap_hits, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&buf->d_vap_total, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&buf->d_flow_hits, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&buf->d_flow_total, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&buf->d_err_count, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&buf->d_correct_count, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&buf->d_agree_count, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&buf->d_disagree_count, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaStreamCreate(&buf->stream);
    cudaMallocHost(&buf->h_pinned, ONETWO_MAX_TOKENS);
    buf->initialized = true;
}

// Free buffer pool on shutdown
inline void onetwo_buffers_free(OneTwoBuffers* buf) {
    if (!buf->initialized) return;
    cudaFree(buf->d_tokens);
    cudaFree(buf->d_vap_hits);
    cudaFree(buf->d_vap_total);
    cudaFree(buf->d_flow_hits);
    cudaFree(buf->d_flow_total);
    cudaFree(buf->d_err_count);
    cudaFree(buf->d_correct_count);
    cudaFree(buf->d_agree_count);
    cudaFree(buf->d_disagree_count);
    cudaStreamDestroy(buf->stream);
    cudaFreeHost(buf->h_pinned);
    buf->initialized = false;
}

OneTwo_Stats onetwo_process(
    IronState* h_state,
    const uint8_t* h_tokens,
    uint32_t count,
    OneTwoBuffers* bufs
) {
    OneTwo_Stats stats = {0};
    if (count < 2) return stats;
    if (count > ONETWO_MAX_TOKENS) count = ONETWO_MAX_TOKENS;

    cudaStream_t s = bufs->stream;

    // Copy tokens via pinned staging buffer -> async H2D
    memcpy(bufs->h_pinned, h_tokens, count);
    cudaMemcpyAsync(bufs->d_tokens, bufs->h_pinned, count, cudaMemcpyHostToDevice, s);

    // Zero counters (async, ~1000x cheaper than cudaMalloc)
    cudaMemsetAsync(bufs->d_vap_hits, 0, sizeof(uint32_t), s);
    cudaMemsetAsync(bufs->d_vap_total, 0, sizeof(uint32_t), s);
    cudaMemsetAsync(bufs->d_flow_hits, 0, sizeof(uint32_t), s);
    cudaMemsetAsync(bufs->d_flow_total, 0, sizeof(uint32_t), s);
    cudaMemsetAsync(bufs->d_err_count, 0, sizeof(uint32_t), s);
    cudaMemsetAsync(bufs->d_correct_count, 0, sizeof(uint32_t), s);
    cudaMemsetAsync(bufs->d_agree_count, 0, sizeof(uint32_t), s);
    cudaMemsetAsync(bufs->d_disagree_count, 0, sizeof(uint32_t), s);

    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = (count + threads - 1) / threads;
    int node_blocks = (h_state->num_nodes + threads - 1) / threads;

    stats.inline_mode = h_state->inline_learning_enabled;

    if (h_state->inline_learning_enabled) {
        ob1_process_edges_inline_learning<<<node_blocks, threads, 0, s>>>(
            h_state->d_iron_state, bufs->d_tokens, count, bufs->d_agree_count, bufs->d_disagree_count
        );
    } else {
        ob1_self_observe<<<blocks, threads, 0, s>>>(
            bufs->d_tokens, count, h_state->d_iron_state, bufs->d_err_count, bufs->d_correct_count
        );
    }

    // 1. VALUE-AT-POSITION
    ob1_value_at_position<<<blocks, threads, 0, s>>>(
        bufs->d_tokens, count, h_state->d_iron_state, bufs->d_vap_hits, bufs->d_vap_total
    );

    // 2. Flow map update
    ob1_update_flow<<<blocks, threads, 0, s>>>(
        bufs->d_tokens, count, h_state->d_iron_state, bufs->d_flow_hits, bufs->d_flow_total, nullptr
    );

    // 3. Per-position transitions
    ob1_update_transitions<<<blocks, threads, 0, s>>>(
        bufs->d_tokens, count, h_state->d_iron_state
    );

    // 4. Edge impedance filtering
    ob1_edge_impedance<<<node_blocks, threads, 0, s>>>(
        h_state->d_iron_state, bufs->d_tokens, count
    );

    cudaStreamSynchronize(s);

    // Read back stats (stream already synced, use default stream for D2H)
    cudaMemcpy(&stats.vap_hits, bufs->d_vap_hits, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(&stats.vap_total, bufs->d_vap_total, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(&stats.flow_hits, bufs->d_flow_hits, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(&stats.flow_total, bufs->d_flow_total, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(&stats.err_count, bufs->d_err_count, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(&stats.correct_count, bufs->d_correct_count, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(&stats.agree_count, bufs->d_agree_count, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(&stats.disagree_count, bufs->d_disagree_count, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    stats.vap_rate = stats.vap_total > 0 ? 100.0f * stats.vap_hits / stats.vap_total : 0.0f;
    stats.flow_rate = stats.flow_total > 0 ? 100.0f * stats.flow_hits / stats.flow_total : 0.0f;
    uint32_t self_obs_total = stats.err_count + stats.correct_count;
