    stats.self_obs_rate = self_obs_total > 0 ? 100.0f * stats.correct_count / self_obs_total : 0.0f;
    uint32_t inline_total = stats.agree_count + stats.disagree_count;
    stats.agree_rate = inline_total > 0 ? 100.0f * stats.agree_count / inline_total : 0.0f;

    // No cleanup needed - buffers are persistent
    return stats;
}

// Auto-wire nodes at a Z-level based on spatial proximity
// Returns wires_created, optionally fills overflow_count
uint32_t onetwo_autowire(IronState* h_state, uint32_t max_distance, uint32_t z_level, uint32_t* overflow_count = nullptr) {
    uint32_t* d_wires;
    uint32_t* d_overflow;
    cudaMalloc(&d_wires, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&d_overflow, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(d_wires, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(d_overflow, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Use a reasonable 1D grid - each thread handles one (node_a, node_b) pair
    // For 4096 nodes, we have ~16M pairs but we only need nearby pairs
    // Sample approach: each thread handles one node_a, checks all node_b
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = (h_state->num_nodes + threads - 1) / threads;

    ob1_autowire<<<blocks, threads>>>(h_state->d_iron_state, max_distance, z_level, d_wires, d_overflow);
    cudaDeviceSynchronize();

    uint32_t wires_created;
    cudaMemcpy(&wires_created, d_wires, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    if (overflow_count) {
        cudaMemcpy(overflow_count, d_overflow, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    }
    cudaFree(d_wires);
    cudaFree(d_overflow);

    return wires_created;
}

// =============================================================================
// III.8.6 XYZT FRESNEL WAVE PROPAGATION ENGINE
// =============================================================================
// 3D lattice (16x16x16 = 4096 nodes), float wave amplitudes.
// Each cell has 6 directional traveling wave components (±x, ±y, ±z).
// Two-phase per tick:
//   Phase 1 (PROPAGATE): Fresnel transmission/reflection at impedance boundaries
//   Phase 2 (SCATTER):   Star junction S-matrix redistribution
//
// Kernels: ob1_xyzt_inject → ob1_xyzt_propagate → ob1_xyzt_scatter
// Launch: <<<XYZT_NZ, XYZT_NX * XYZT_NY>>> = 16 blocks, 256 threads

struct XYZT_Stats {
    uint32_t active_cells;
    uint32_t gain_events;
    uint32_t gate_fires;
    uint32_t bytes_injected;
};

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Kernel 1: INJECT - Binary substrate at Z=0 plane
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// bit=1 → +1/6 to all 6 directions, bit=0 → -1/6 to all 6 directions
// Symmetric injection (no preferred direction)
__global__ void ob1_xyzt_inject(
    IronState* state,
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ inject_tokens,
    uint32_t inject_count
) {
    int z = blockIdx.x;
    if (z != 0) return;  // only Z=0 plane

    int slab_idx = threadIdx.x;
    int x = slab_idx & 0xF;
    int y = (slab_idx >> 4) & 0xF;
    uint32_t gid = xyzt_xyz_to_idx(x, y, z);
    if (gid >= state->num_nodes) return;

    float* waves = state->xyzt_waves;
    uint32_t base6 = gid * NUM_DIRS;

    uint32_t total_bits = inject_count * 8;
    uint32_t frames = (total_bits + 255) / 256;

    for (uint32_t f = 0; f < frames; f++) {
        uint32_t bit_global = f * 256 + slab_idx;
        uint32_t byte_idx = bit_global / 8;
        uint32_t bit_idx = bit_global % 8;

        if (byte_idx < inject_count) {
            uint8_t byte_val = inject_tokens[byte_idx];
            float bit_signal = ((byte_val >> bit_idx) & 1) ? (1.0f / 6.0f) : (-1.0f / 6.0f);

            // Add equally to all 6 directions (symmetric injection)
            for (int d = 0; d < NUM_DIRS; d++) {
                waves[base6 + d] += bit_signal;
            }
        }
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Kernel 2: PROPAGATE - Fresnel transmission/reflection
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// For each cell, for each direction d:
//   outgoing wave 'a' from cell (Z1) hits neighbor (Z2)
//   transmitted: t * a = 2*Z2/(Z1+Z2) * a → neighbor's waves_in[opposite(d)]
//   reflected:   r * a = (Z2-Z1)/(Z1+Z2) * a → self's waves_in[d]
// Energy conservation: t²*Z1/Z2 + r² = 1 (Lean: fresnel_conservation)
// Uses atomicAdd because multiple cells write to same neighbor's incoming buffer.
__global__ void ob1_xyzt_propagate(
    IronState* state
) {
    int z = blockIdx.x;
    int slab_idx = threadIdx.x;
    int x = slab_idx & 0xF;
    int y = (slab_idx >> 4) & 0xF;
    uint32_t gid = xyzt_xyz_to_idx(x, y, z);
    if (gid >= state->num_nodes) return;

    float* waves = state->xyzt_waves;
    float* waves_in = state->xyzt_waves_in;
    float* Z = state->xyzt_Z;

    float Z1 = Z[gid];
    uint32_t base6 = gid * NUM_DIRS;

    // Wall check: very high impedance = total reflection (no transmission)
    if (Z1 >= 1e6f) {
        // Wall: all outgoing waves reflect back into self
        for (int d = 0; d < NUM_DIRS; d++) {
            atomicAdd(&waves_in[base6 + d], -waves[base6 + d]);  // r = -1 for wall
        }
        return;
    }

    for (int d = 0; d < NUM_DIRS; d++) {
        float a = waves[base6 + d];
        if (a == 0.0f) continue;  // no wave to propagate

        int nx, ny, nz;
        bool has_neighbor = xyzt_neighbor_in_dir(x, y, z, d, nx, ny, nz);

        if (!has_neighbor) {
            // Boundary: total reflection (r = -1, hard wall)
            atomicAdd(&waves_in[base6 + d], -a);
            continue;
        }

        uint32_t nid = xyzt_xyz_to_idx(nx, ny, nz);
        float Z2 = Z[nid];

        // Fresnel coefficients
        float Z_sum = Z1 + Z2;
        float t = 2.0f * Z2 / Z_sum;   // transmission
        float r = (Z2 - Z1) / Z_sum;   // reflection

        // Transmitted → neighbor's incoming from opposite direction
        uint32_t opp = d ^ 1;
        atomicAdd(&waves_in[nid * NUM_DIRS + opp], t * a);

        // Reflected → self's incoming from same direction
        atomicAdd(&waves_in[base6 + d], r * a);
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Kernel 3: SCATTER - Star junction S-matrix + gain + noise + energy
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// At each node, 6 incoming waves redistribute via 6-port equal-impedance S-matrix:
//   waves_out[d] = total_incoming/3 - waves_in[d]
// This is energy-conserving (Lean: scatter_conserves_energy)
__global__ void ob1_xyzt_scatter(
    IronState* state,
    uint32_t* d_active_cells,
    uint32_t* d_gain_events
) {
    int z = blockIdx.x;
    int slab_idx = threadIdx.x;
    int x = slab_idx & 0xF;
    int y = (slab_idx >> 4) & 0xF;
    uint32_t gid = xyzt_xyz_to_idx(x, y, z);
    if (gid >= state->num_nodes) return;

    float* waves = state->xyzt_waves;
    float* waves_in = state->xyzt_waves_in;
    uint32_t base6 = gid * NUM_DIRS;

    // Sum all incoming waves
    float total = 0.0f;
    for (int d = 0; d < NUM_DIRS; d++) {
        total += waves_in[base6 + d];
    }

    // Star junction S-matrix: waves_out[d] = total/3 - waves_in[d]
    float third = total / 3.0f;
    float nf = state->xyzt_noise_floor;
    float my_Z = state->xyzt_Z[gid];
    float energy = 0.0f;
    bool any_active = false;

    for (int d = 0; d < NUM_DIRS; d++) {
        float w = third - waves_in[base6 + d];

        // Noise floor: zero small waves
        if (w > -nf && w < nf) w = 0.0f;

        waves[base6 + d] = w;
        if (w != 0.0f) any_active = true;
    }

    // Gain restoration: locked nodes restore weak waves toward ±1.0
    if (state->node_locked[gid] && any_active) {
        bool gained = false;
        for (int d = 0; d < NUM_DIRS; d++) {
            float w = waves[base6 + d];
            if (w > 0.0f && w < 1.0f / 6.0f) {
                waves[base6 + d] = 1.0f / 6.0f;
                gained = true;
            } else if (w < 0.0f && w > -1.0f / 6.0f) {
                waves[base6 + d] = -1.0f / 6.0f;
                gained = true;
            }
        }
        if (gained) atomicAdd(d_gain_events, 1);
    }

    // Energy = sum(w²) / Z  (power = voltage² / impedance)
    for (int d = 0; d < NUM_DIRS; d++) {
        float w = waves[base6 + d];
        energy += w * w;
    }
    energy /= my_Z;

    // Hebbian learning: modify Z based on correlation with neighbors
    float heb_rate = state->xyzt_hebbian_rate;
    if (heb_rate > 0.0f) {
        float my_total = total;
        for (int d = 0; d < NUM_DIRS; d++) {
            int nx, ny, nz;
            if (!xyzt_neighbor_in_dir(x, y, z, d, nx, ny, nz)) continue;
            uint32_t nid = xyzt_xyz_to_idx(nx, ny, nz);

            // Neighbor's total incoming (sum their waves_in)
            float n_total = 0.0f;
            uint32_t nbase6 = nid * NUM_DIRS;
            for (int dd = 0; dd < NUM_DIRS; dd++) {
                n_total += waves_in[nbase6 + dd];
            }

            // Correlation: same sign → decrease Z difference (better transmission)
            // Anti-correlated → increase Z difference (more reflection)
            float corr = my_total * n_total;
            if (corr > 0.0f) {
                // Correlated: move Z toward neighbor's Z (equalize)
                float neighbor_Z = state->xyzt_Z[nid];
                state->xyzt_Z[gid] += heb_rate * (neighbor_Z - state->xyzt_Z[gid]) * 0.01f;
            } else if (corr < 0.0f) {
                // Anti-correlated: increase Z difference
                state->xyzt_Z[gid] += heb_rate * 0.01f;
            }
        }
        // Clamp Z
        if (state->xyzt_Z[gid] < 0.1f) state->xyzt_Z[gid] = 0.1f;
        if (state->xyzt_Z[gid] > 1e6f) state->xyzt_Z[gid] = 1e6f;
    }

    // Write energy
    state->xyzt_energy[gid] = energy;

    if (any_active) {
        atomicAdd(d_active_cells, 1);
    }
}

// Host-side XYZT processing function
XYZT_Stats xyzt_process(
    IronState* h_state,
    const uint8_t* h_tokens,
    uint32_t count,
    uint32_t ticks,
    OneTwoBuffers* bufs
) {
    XYZT_Stats stats = {0};
    stats.bytes_injected = count;

    cudaStream_t s = bufs->stream;

    // Copy tokens to device via pinned staging
    if (count > 0 && count <= ONETWO_MAX_TOKENS) {
        memcpy(bufs->h_pinned, h_tokens, count);
        cudaMemcpyAsync(bufs->d_tokens, bufs->h_pinned, count, cudaMemcpyHostToDevice, s);
    }

    // Zero stat counters
    cudaMemsetAsync(h_state->d_xyzt_active_cells, 0, sizeof(uint32_t), s);
    cudaMemsetAsync(h_state->d_xyzt_gain_events, 0, sizeof(uint32_t), s);
    cudaMemsetAsync(h_state->d_xyzt_gate_fires, 0, sizeof(uint32_t), s);

    if (ticks == 0) ticks = h_state->xyzt_ticks_per_call;
    if (ticks == 0) ticks = 1;

    uint32_t num_nodes = h_state->num_nodes;

    for (uint32_t t = 0; t < ticks; t++) {
        // Zero waves_in buffer before each tick
        cudaMemsetAsync(h_state->xyzt_waves_in, 0, num_nodes * NUM_DIRS * sizeof(float), s);

        // Inject on first tick only
        if (t == 0 && count > 0) {
            ob1_xyzt_inject<<<XYZT_NZ, XYZT_NX * XYZT_NY, 0, s>>>(
                h_state->d_iron_state,
                bufs->d_tokens,
                count
            );
        }

        // Phase 1: Fresnel propagation (cross-cell writes via atomicAdd)
        ob1_xyzt_propagate<<<XYZT_NZ, XYZT_NX * XYZT_NY, 0, s>>>(
            h_state->d_iron_state
        );

        // Phase 2: Star junction scatter + gain + noise + energy
        ob1_xyzt_scatter<<<XYZT_NZ, XYZT_NX * XYZT_NY, 0, s>>>(
            h_state->d_iron_state,
            h_state->d_xyzt_active_cells,
            h_state->d_xyzt_gain_events
        );
        // No buffer swap needed — scatter writes directly to waves[]
    }

    cudaStreamSynchronize(s);

    // Read stats
    cudaMemcpy(&stats.active_cells, h_state->d_xyzt_active_cells, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(&stats.gain_events, h_state->d_xyzt_gain_events, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(&stats.gate_fires, h_state->d_xyzt_gate_fires, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    return stats;
}

// =============================================================================
// III.8.7 XYZT SPAWN/PRUNE - Dynamic Topology from Signal Correlation
// =============================================================================
// Spawn: create wires where adjacent nodes have correlated signals
// Prune: remove wires with weight below threshold

__global__ void ob1_xyzt_spawn(
    IronState* state,
    float corr_threshold,     // minimum wave correlation to spawn (e.g. 0.1)
    uint32_t* d_spawned       // atomic counter
) {
    uint32_t gid = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (gid >= state->num_nodes) return;

    float my_energy = state->xyzt_energy[gid];
    if (my_energy == 0.0f) return;  // dead node, skip

    int x, y, z;
    xyzt_idx_to_xyz(gid, x, y, z);

    // Compute my total wave signal (sum of 6 components)
    float* waves = state->xyzt_waves;
    uint32_t base6 = gid * NUM_DIRS;
    float my_total = 0.0f;
    for (int d = 0; d < NUM_DIRS; d++) my_total += waves[base6 + d];

    for (int d = 0; d < NUM_DIRS; d++) {
        int nx, ny, nz;
        if (!xyzt_neighbor_in_dir(x, y, z, d, nx, ny, nz)) continue;

        uint32_t nid = xyzt_xyz_to_idx(nx, ny, nz);

        // Neighbor's total wave signal
        uint32_t nbase6 = nid * NUM_DIRS;
        float n_total = 0.0f;
        for (int dd = 0; dd < NUM_DIRS; dd++) n_total += waves[nbase6 + dd];

        // Correlation check
        float corr = my_total * n_total;
        if (corr < corr_threshold) continue;

        // Check if wire already exists
        uint32_t conn_count = state->connection_counts[gid];
        if (conn_count >= state->max_connections) continue;

        bool exists = false;
        uint32_t base = gid * state->max_connections;
        for (uint32_t c = 0; c < conn_count; c++) {
            if (state->lattice_connections[base + c] == nid) { exists = true; break; }
        }
        if (exists) continue;

        uint32_t slot = atomicAdd(&state->connection_counts[gid], 1);
        if (slot < state->max_connections) {
            state->lattice_connections[base + slot] = nid;
            state->edge_weight[base + slot] = 32768;
            atomicAdd(d_spawned, 1);
        } else {
            atomicSub(&state->connection_counts[gid], 1);
        }
    }
}

__global__ void ob1_xyzt_prune(
    IronState* state,
    uint16_t weight_threshold,  // prune wires below this weight (e.g. 8192 = 0.25)
    uint32_t* d_pruned          // atomic counter
) {
    uint32_t gid = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (gid >= state->num_nodes) return;

    uint32_t conn_count = state->connection_counts[gid];
    if (conn_count == 0) return;

    uint32_t base = gid * state->max_connections;
    uint32_t write_idx = 0;

    // Compact: keep only wires above threshold
    for (uint32_t c = 0; c < conn_count && c < state->max_connections; c++) {
        uint16_t w = state->edge_weight[base + c];
        if (w >= weight_threshold) {
            if (write_idx != c) {
                state->lattice_connections[base + write_idx] = state->lattice_connections[base + c];
                state->edge_weight[base + write_idx] = state->edge_weight[base + c];
            }
            write_idx++;
        } else {
            atomicAdd(d_pruned, 1);
        }
    }

    // Update count
    state->connection_counts[gid] = write_idx;

    // Zero remaining slots
    for (uint32_t c = write_idx; c < conn_count && c < state->max_connections; c++) {
        state->lattice_connections[base + c] = 0;
        state->edge_weight[base + c] = 0;
    }
}

// Host-side spawn/prune functions
struct SpawnPruneStats {
    uint32_t spawned;
    uint32_t pruned;
};

SpawnPruneStats xyzt_spawn(IronState* h_state, float corr_threshold) {
    SpawnPruneStats stats = {0};
    uint32_t* d_spawned;
    cudaMalloc(&d_spawned, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(d_spawned, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));

    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = (h_state->num_nodes + threads - 1) / threads;
    ob1_xyzt_spawn<<<blocks, threads>>>(h_state->d_iron_state, corr_threshold, d_spawned);
    cudaDeviceSynchronize();

    cudaMemcpy(&stats.spawned, d_spawned, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaFree(d_spawned);

    // Sync updated connection_counts back to host
    cudaMemcpy(h_state, h_state->d_iron_state, sizeof(IronState), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    return stats;
}

SpawnPruneStats xyzt_prune(IronState* h_state, uint16_t weight_threshold) {
    SpawnPruneStats stats = {0};
    uint32_t* d_pruned;
    cudaMalloc(&d_pruned, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(d_pruned, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));

    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = (h_state->num_nodes + threads - 1) / threads;
    ob1_xyzt_prune<<<blocks, threads>>>(h_state->d_iron_state, weight_threshold, d_pruned);
    cudaDeviceSynchronize();

    cudaMemcpy(&stats.pruned, d_pruned, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaFree(d_pruned);

    return stats;
}

// =============================================================================
// III.9 IRON → FLESH BRIDGE
// =============================================================================
// Export discovered Iron structure to seed the Flesh Crystal Network
// This transforms autonomous discovery into usable pre-training

uint32_t iron_to_flesh_bridge(
    IronState* h_iron,           // Host-side Iron state (with GPU pointers)
    CrystalNetwork* flesh_net,   // Flesh network to initialize
    Crystal* h_crystals          // Host crystal buffer to fill
) {
    printf("\n=== IRON → FLESH BRIDGE ===\n");

    // Read Iron state from GPU
    uint32_t* h_conn_counts = (uint32_t*)malloc(h_iron->num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    uint32_t* h_connections = (uint32_t*)malloc(h_iron->num_nodes * h_iron->max_connections * sizeof(uint32_t));
    uint8_t* h_locked = (uint8_t*)malloc(h_iron->num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    uint32_t* h_xor_acc = (uint32_t*)malloc(h_iron->num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));

    cudaMemcpy(h_conn_counts, h_iron->connection_counts,
               h_iron->num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(h_connections, h_iron->lattice_connections,
               h_iron->num_nodes * h_iron->max_connections * sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(h_locked, h_iron->node_locked,
               h_iron->num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(h_xor_acc, h_iron->xor_accumulators,
               h_iron->num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    // Count total connections discovered
    uint32_t total_connections = 0;
    uint32_t locked_nodes = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < h_iron->num_nodes; i++) {
        total_connections += h_conn_counts[i];
        if (h_locked[i]) locked_nodes++;
    }

    printf("  Iron discovered: %u connections, %u locked nodes\n",
           total_connections, locked_nodes);

    // Convert Iron connections to Flesh Crystals
    uint32_t crystal_idx = 0;
    uint32_t max_crystals = flesh_net->num_crystals;

    for (uint32_t src = 0; src < h_iron->num_nodes && crystal_idx < max_crystals; src++) {
        uint32_t conn_count = h_conn_counts[src];
        uint32_t xor_val = h_xor_acc[src];

        for (uint32_t c = 0; c < conn_count && c < h_iron->max_connections && crystal_idx < max_crystals; c++) {
            uint32_t dst = h_connections[src * h_iron->max_connections + c];

            // Skip self-connections
            if (dst == src) continue;

            // Map to Flesh Crystal
            Crystal crystal;
            crystal.src = src % flesh_net->num_nodes;  // Wrap to flesh node range
            crystal.dst = dst % flesh_net->num_nodes;

            // Determine lattice_idx from XOR pattern
            // Use K-sector proximity to assign to one of the 4 dual lattice nodes
            uint32_t r137 = xor_val % 137;
            uint32_t r377 = xor_val % 377;
            uint32_t r25 = xor_val % 25;
            uint32_t r17 = xor_val % 17;

            if (r17 < r25 && r17 < r137 && r17 < r377) {
                crystal.lattice_idx = 0;  // Strong sector (K=17) → node 13
            } else if (r25 < r137 && r25 < r377) {
                crystal.lattice_idx = 1;  // Core sector (K=25) → node 137
            } else if (r137 < r377) {
                crystal.lattice_idx = 2;  // Surface sector (K=137) → node 233
            } else {
                crystal.lattice_idx = 3;  // Deep sector (K=377) → node 377
            }

            // Direction: master→slave if src < dst
            crystal.direction = (src < dst) ? 1 : -1;

            // Initialize as locked (Iron already validated this connection)
            crystal.locked = 1;
            crystal.score = 1.0f;  // High initial score (Iron-validated)
            crystal.fires = 50;    // Pre-fired (meets min_fires threshold)

            h_crystals[crystal_idx++] = crystal;
        }
    }

    printf("  Bridged %u crystals to Flesh network\n", crystal_idx);
    printf("  Lattice distribution:\n");

    // Count lattice distribution
    uint32_t lattice_counts[4] = {0, 0, 0, 0};
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < crystal_idx; i++) {
        lattice_counts[h_crystals[i].lattice_idx]++;
    }
    printf("    K=17 (Strong):  %u\n", lattice_counts[0]);
    printf("    K=25 (Core):    %u\n", lattice_counts[1]);
    printf("    K=137 (Surface): %u\n", lattice_counts[2]);
    printf("    K=377 (Deep):   %u\n", lattice_counts[3]);

    // Update Flesh network stats
    flesh_net->num_locked = crystal_idx;
    flesh_net->num_pruned = 0;
    flesh_net->num_candidates = flesh_net->num_crystals - crystal_idx;
    flesh_net->csr_valid = false;  // Force CSR rebuild

    // Copy bridged crystals to device
    cudaMemcpy(flesh_net->crystals, h_crystals,
               crystal_idx * sizeof(Crystal), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    // Initialize remaining crystals as candidates
    for (uint32_t i = crystal_idx; i < flesh_net->num_crystals; i++) {
        h_crystals[i].locked = 0;
        h_crystals[i].score = 0.0f;
        h_crystals[i].plasticity = 1.0f;  // PRINCIPIA: Start with full plasticity
        h_crystals[i].fires = 0;
    }
    cudaMemcpy(flesh_net->crystals + crystal_idx, h_crystals + crystal_idx,
               (flesh_net->num_crystals - crystal_idx) * sizeof(Crystal), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    printf("=== BRIDGE COMPLETE ===\n\n");

    // Cleanup
    free(h_conn_counts);
    free(h_connections);
    free(h_locked);
    free(h_xor_acc);

    return crystal_idx;
}

// =============================================================================
// IV. FBC OPCODES (For OUTPUT encoder - not used for input)
// =============================================================================
// These opcodes are reserved for the future OUTPUT path:
//   Crystals → FBC bytecode → executable/portable
// INPUT uses raw bytes directly - crystals discover structure.

namespace FBC_OUTPUT {
    // Stack (0x00-0x0F)
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_NOP = 0x00;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_PUSH_I8 = 0x01;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_PUSH_I32 = 0x02;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_PUSH_I64 = 0x03;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_PUSH_F64 = 0x04;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_PUSH_STR = 0x05;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_POP = 0x09;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_DUP = 0x0A;

    // Arithmetic (0x10-0x1F)
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_ADD = 0x10;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_SUB = 0x11;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_MUL = 0x12;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_DIV = 0x13;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_MOD = 0x14;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_POW = 0x16;

    // Comparison (0x20-0x2F)
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_EQ = 0x20;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_LT = 0x22;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_GT = 0x24;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_AND = 0x26;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_OR = 0x27;

    // Variables (0x30-0x3F)
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_LOAD_LOCAL = 0x30;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_STORE_LOCAL = 0x31;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_LOAD_GLOBAL = 0x32;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_STORE_GLOBAL = 0x33;

    // Control Flow (0x40-0x4F)
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_JMP = 0x40;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_JMP_IF = 0x41;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_JMP_IFNOT = 0x42;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_LOOP = 0x43;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_LOOP_END = 0x44;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_BREAK = 0x45;

    // Functions (0x50-0x5F)
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_CALL = 0x50;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_RET = 0x52;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_RET_VAL = 0x53;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_CLOSURE = 0x54;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_YIELD = 0x57;

    // Objects (0x60-0x6F)
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_NEW_ARRAY = 0x60;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_NEW_OBJECT = 0x61;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_GET_ITER = 0x63;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_ITER_NEXT = 0x64;

    // AI/Semantic (0xA0-0xAF)
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_SEMANTIC = 0xA0;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_INTENT = 0xA1;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_INVARIANT = 0xA2;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_PRECOND = 0xA3;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_POSTCOND = 0xA4;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_COMPLEXITY = 0xA5;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_FN_PURE = 0xA6;

    // Pattern/Compression (0xB0-0xBF) - THE COMPRESSION LAYER
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_LOOP_N = 0xB0;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_LOOP_N_END = 0xB1;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_PATTERN_INC = 0xB2;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_PATTERN_SUM = 0xB3;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_PATTERN_MUL = 0xB4;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_PATTERN_REP = 0xB5;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_PATTERN_SEQ = 0xB6;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_PATTERN_MAP = 0xB7;

    // System (0xF0-0xFF)
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_SYSCALL = 0xF0;
    __constant__ uint8_t OP_HALT = 0xFE;
}

// =============================================================================
// V. PATTERN TYPES AND SEMANTIC TOKENS
// =============================================================================

enum PatternType : uint8_t {
    PATTERN_UNKNOWN = 0,
    PATTERN_LOOP = 1,
    PATTERN_FUNCTION = 2,
    PATTERN_ACCUMULATOR = 3,  // sum, product
    PATTERN_CONDITIONAL = 4,
    PATTERN_RECURSION = 5,
    PATTERN_SEQUENCE = 6,
    PATTERN_MAP = 7,
    PATTERN_DATA = 8,         // Push/pop/variables
    PATTERN_ARITHMETIC = 9,   // Math ops
    PATTERN_SEMANTIC = 10,    // AI annotations
};

// Sector assignment (which K grid)
enum Sector : uint8_t {
    SECTOR_EM = 0,      // K=137 - Pattern/compression ops
    SECTOR_VACUUM = 1,  // K=377 - Control flow
    SECTOR_WEAK = 2,    // K=25  - Functions
    SECTOR_STRONG = 3,  // K=17  - Semantic/AI
};

// Semantic token - the universal intermediate representation
struct SemanticToken {
    uint32_t id;
    PatternType pattern;
    uint8_t sector;         // Which K grid (0=EM, 1=Vacuum, 2=Weak, 3=Strong)
    float phase;            // Phase position on the grid
    uint8_t byte_val;       // Original byte value
    uint32_t context;       // Surrounding context (prev/next bytes)
};

// =============================================================================
// VI. RAW BYTE TOKENIZER (The only input path - crystals discover structure)
// =============================================================================

// Classify raw byte → PatternType + Sector (no FBC needed)
__device__ __forceinline__ void classify_raw_byte(
    uint8_t byte,
    uint8_t prev_byte,
    uint8_t next_byte,
    PatternType* pattern,
    uint8_t* sector
) {
    // ASCII printable text: 0x20-0x7E
    if (byte >= 0x20 && byte <= 0x7E) {
        // Digits
        if (byte >= 0x30 && byte <= 0x39) {
            *pattern = PATTERN_DATA;
            *sector = SECTOR_EM;  // K=137
        }
        // Letters
        else if ((byte >= 0x41 && byte <= 0x5A) || (byte >= 0x61 && byte <= 0x7A)) {
            *pattern = PATTERN_SEQUENCE;
            *sector = SECTOR_VACUUM;  // K=377
        }
        // Operators/punctuation
        else if (byte == 0x2B || byte == 0x2D || byte == 0x2A || byte == 0x2F) {
            *pattern = PATTERN_ARITHMETIC;
            *sector = SECTOR_EM;
        }
        // Brackets/braces (structure)
        else if (byte == 0x28 || byte == 0x29 || byte == 0x7B || byte == 0x7D ||
                 byte == 0x5B || byte == 0x5D) {
            *pattern = PATTERN_FUNCTION;
            *sector = SECTOR_WEAK;  // K=25
        }
        // Other printable
        else {
            *pattern = PATTERN_DATA;
            *sector = SECTOR_EM;
        }
    }
    // Control characters: 0x00-0x1F
    else if (byte < 0x20) {
        // Newline/tab (structure markers)
        if (byte == 0x0A || byte == 0x0D || byte == 0x09) {
            *pattern = PATTERN_LOOP;  // Line boundaries often indicate iteration
            *sector = SECTOR_VACUUM;
        } else {
            *pattern = PATTERN_UNKNOWN;
            *sector = SECTOR_STRONG;  // K=17
        }
    }
    // High bytes: 0x80-0xFF (binary data, UTF-8 continuation, etc.)
    else {
        // Check for repetition pattern
        if (byte == prev_byte || byte == next_byte) {
            *pattern = PATTERN_LOOP;
            *sector = SECTOR_EM;
        } else {
            *pattern = PATTERN_DATA;
            *sector = SECTOR_STRONG;
        }
    }
}

// Raw Byte Tokenizer Kernel: Raw bytes → SemanticTokens (no FBC)
__global__ void raw_tokenize_kernel(
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ data,
    SemanticToken* __restrict__ tokens,
    const int num_bytes
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_bytes) return;

    uint8_t byte = data[idx];
    uint8_t prev = (idx > 0) ? data[idx - 1] : 0;
    uint8_t next = (idx < num_bytes - 1) ? data[idx + 1] : 0;

    PatternType pattern;
    uint8_t sector;
    classify_raw_byte(byte, prev, next, &pattern, &sector);

    // Compute phase from byte value
    int K_vals[4] = {137, 377, 25, 17};
    int K = K_vals[sector];
    float phase = (float)(byte % K) / (float)K;

    SemanticToken tok;
    tok.id = idx;
    tok.pattern = pattern;
    tok.sector = sector;
    tok.phase = phase;
    tok.byte_val = byte;
    tok.context = (uint32_t)prev << 8 | next;

    tokens[idx] = tok;
}

// =============================================================================
// VII. WARP PRIMITIVES
// =============================================================================

#define WARP_SIZE 32
#define PI_F 3.14159265358979323846f

__device__ __forceinline__ float warp_reduce_sum(float val) {
    #pragma unroll
    for (int offset = 16; offset > 0; offset >>= 1) {
        val += __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, val, offset);
    }
    return val;
}

__device__ __forceinline__ float warp_reduce_max(float val) {
    #pragma unroll
    for (int offset = 16; offset > 0; offset >>= 1) {
        val = fmaxf(val, __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, val, offset));
    }
    return val;
}

__device__ __forceinline__ int warp_reduce_sum_int(int val) {
    #pragma unroll
    for (int offset = 16; offset > 0; offset >>= 1) {
        val += __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, val, offset);
    }
    return val;
}

// =============================================================================
// VI. SECTOR TRANSITION ENGINE (Phase Lock)
// =============================================================================

__device__ __forceinline__ float sector_lock(float x, int K, float* lock_accuracy) {
    // Phase lock to integer grid
    // x_locked = round(x % K)
    float N = fmodf(x, (float)K);
    if (N < 0) N += K;

    float N_locked = roundf(N);
    float distance = fabsf(N - N_locked);

    // Lock accuracy: 1.0 = on gear tooth, 0.0 = between teeth
    *lock_accuracy = fmaxf(0.0f, 1.0f - 2.0f * distance);

    // Reconstruct locked value
    float base = floorf(x / K) * K;
    return base + N_locked;
}

__global__ void sector_transition_kernel(
    float* __restrict__ data,
    float* __restrict__ lock_accuracies,
    const int size,
    const int K
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= size) return;

    float acc;
    data[idx] = sector_lock(data[idx], K, &acc);
    lock_accuracies[idx] = acc;
}

// =============================================================================
// VII. WARP-FFT (32-point, no cuFFT)
// =============================================================================

__device__ __forceinline__ float2 complex_mul(float2 a, float2 b) {
    return make_float2(a.x * b.x - a.y * b.y, a.x * b.y + a.y * b.x);
}

__device__ void warp_fft_32(float2* val, int lane_id) {
    #pragma unroll
    for (int stage = 0; stage < 5; stage++) {
        int half_size = 1 << stage;
        int butterfly_size = 1 << (stage + 1);
        int partner = lane_id ^ half_size;

        float2 partner_val;
        partner_val.x = __shfl_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, val->x, partner);
        partner_val.y = __shfl_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, val->y, partner);

        int k = lane_id & (half_size - 1);
        float angle = -2.0f * PI_F * k / butterfly_size;
        float2 twiddle = make_float2(cosf(angle), sinf(angle));

        if ((lane_id & half_size) == 0) {
            float2 t = complex_mul(partner_val, twiddle);
            val->x += t.x;
            val->y += t.y;
        } else {
            float2 t = complex_mul(*val, twiddle);
            val->x = partner_val.x - t.x;
            val->y = partner_val.y - t.y;
        }
    }

    int reversed = __brev(lane_id) >> (32 - 5);
    float2 final_val;
    final_val.x = __shfl_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, val->x, reversed);
    final_val.y = __shfl_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, val->y, reversed);
    *val = final_val;
}

__device__ void warp_ifft_32(float2* val, int lane_id) {
    val->y = -val->y;
    warp_fft_32(val, lane_id);
    val->y = -val->y;
    val->x /= 32.0f;
    val->y /= 32.0f;
}

// =============================================================================
// VIII. MAL ATTENTION (Integer-Locked)
// =============================================================================

__global__ void mal_attention_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ Q,
    const float* __restrict__ K_mat,
    const float* __restrict__ V,
    float* __restrict__ output,
    float* __restrict__ lock_acc_out,
    const int batch,
    const int seq_len,
    const int head_dim,
    const int K_grid
) {
    const int b = blockIdx.x;
    const int h = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;

    extern __shared__ float shared[];
    float* scores = shared;

    if (b >= batch) return;

    // Compute attention scores with phase lock
    float total_lock_acc = 0.0f;
    int lock_count = 0;

    for (int i = tid; i < seq_len; i += blockDim.x) {
        // Dot product Q[i] · K[j] for all j
        for (int j = 0; j < seq_len; j++) {
            float dot = 0.0f;
            for (int d = 0; d < head_dim; d++) {
                int q_idx = b * seq_len * head_dim + i * head_dim + d;
                int k_idx = b * seq_len * head_dim + j * head_dim + d;
                dot += Q[q_idx] * K_mat[k_idx];
            }
            dot /= sqrtf((float)head_dim);

            // Phase lock the attention score
            float lock_acc;
            dot = sector_lock(dot * K_grid, K_grid, &lock_acc) / K_grid;
            total_lock_acc += lock_acc;
            lock_count++;

            scores[i * seq_len + j] = dot;
        }
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Softmax (row-wise)
    for (int i = tid; i < seq_len; i += blockDim.x) {
        float max_val = -1e10f;
        for (int j = 0; j < seq_len; j++) {
            max_val = fmaxf(max_val, scores[i * seq_len + j]);
        }

        float sum = 0.0f;
        for (int j = 0; j < seq_len; j++) {
            scores[i * seq_len + j] = expf(scores[i * seq_len + j] - max_val);
            sum += scores[i * seq_len + j];
        }

        for (int j = 0; j < seq_len; j++) {
            scores[i * seq_len + j] /= (sum + 1e-10f);
        }
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Output = Attention @ V
    for (int i = tid; i < seq_len; i += blockDim.x) {
        for (int d = 0; d < head_dim; d++) {
            float out = 0.0f;
            for (int j = 0; j < seq_len; j++) {
                int v_idx = b * seq_len * head_dim + j * head_dim + d;
                out += scores[i * seq_len + j] * V[v_idx];
            }
            output[b * seq_len * head_dim + i * head_dim + d] = out;
        }
    }

    // Store average lock accuracy
    if (tid == 0 && lock_count > 0) {
        lock_acc_out[b * gridDim.y + h] = total_lock_acc / lock_count;
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// IX. CRYSTAL FORWARD PASS
// =============================================================================
// PROTOCOL: Persistent threads + warp reduction + float4 where possible
// CSR format: crystals grouped by destination for warp-reduced scatter
// =============================================================================

// CSR Sparse Crystal Format (locked crystals only, grouped by dst)
struct CrystalCSR {
    int* row_ptrs;      // [num_nodes + 1] - where each dst's crystals start
    uint32_t* src_nodes; // [num_locked] - source node for each crystal
    uint8_t* lattice_idx; // [num_locked] - which lattice node
    int8_t* directions;  // [num_locked] - +1 or -1
};

// Packed crystal data for vectorized loads (4 crystals per struct)
struct __align__(16) CrystalPack4 {
    uint32_t src[4];
    uint8_t lattice[4];
    int8_t dir[4];
};

// PROTOCOL-COMPLIANT: Persistent threads + warp reduction
// Grid-stride loop keeps warps hot, processes multiple output nodes
__global__ void crystal_forward_kernel(
    const int* __restrict__ row_ptrs,
    const uint32_t* __restrict__ src_nodes,
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ lattice_idx,
    const int8_t* __restrict__ directions,
    const float* __restrict__ input,
    float* __restrict__ output,
    const uint32_t num_nodes,
    const float scale
) {
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    const int lane_id = tid & 31;
    const int warp_id = tid >> 5;
    const int num_warps = blockDim.x >> 5;

    __shared__ float warp_sums[32];

    // PERSISTENT THREADS: Grid-stride loop over output nodes
    for (int dst = blockIdx.x; dst < num_nodes; dst += gridDim.x) {
        const int row_start = __ldg(&row_ptrs[dst]);
        const int row_end = __ldg(&row_ptrs[dst + 1]);

        float partial_sum = 0.0f;

        // Strided scalar loop - each thread handles every blockDim.x-th element
        // This is correct and avoids the double-counting bug in vectorized path
        for (int i = row_start + tid; i < row_end; i += blockDim.x) {
            uint32_t src = __ldg(&src_nodes[i]);
            uint8_t lat = lattice_idx[i];
            int8_t dir = directions[i];
            partial_sum += __ldg(&input[src]) * Grids::DUAL_NODES[lat] * dir;
        }

        partial_sum *= scale;

        // Warp reduction
        #pragma unroll
        for (int offset = 16; offset > 0; offset >>= 1) {
            partial_sum += __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, partial_sum, offset);
        }

        // Block reduction
        if (lane_id == 0) {
            warp_sums[warp_id] = partial_sum;
        }
        __syncthreads();

        if (tid == 0) {
            float total = 0.0f;
            #pragma unroll
            for (int w = 0; w < 8; w++) {  // Assume max 8 warps (256 threads)
                if (w < num_warps) total += warp_sums[w];
            }
            output[dst] = total;
        }
        __syncthreads();  // Ensure warp_sums not overwritten before all warps done
    }
}

// Legacy kernel for backwards compat (DEPRECATED - use CSR version)
__global__ void crystal_forward_legacy_kernel(
    const Crystal* __restrict__ crystals,
    const float* __restrict__ input,
    float* __restrict__ output,
    const uint32_t num_crystals,
    const uint32_t num_nodes,
    const float scale
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_crystals) return;

    Crystal c = crystals[idx];
    if (c.locked != 1) return;

    float weight = Grids::DUAL_NODES[c.lattice_idx] * scale * c.direction;
    atomicAdd(&output[c.dst], input[c.src] * weight);  // DEPRECATED
}

// =============================================================================
// X. STRUCTURE DISCOVERY (Lock/Prune Crystals)
// =============================================================================

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// PRINCIPIA: Radio Tuning Kernel (Book XIV)
// Computes mean(|W|) for each crystal source node to get adaptive scale
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
__global__ void radio_tuning_kernel(
    const Crystal* __restrict__ crystals,
    const float* __restrict__ weights,  // Raw weight values for crystals
    float* __restrict__ scales,          // Output: scale per crystal
    const uint32_t num_crystals
) {
    extern __shared__ float shared[];

    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    const int bs = blockDim.x;

    // Each thread accumulates |W| for its crystals
    float local_sum = 0.0f;
    int local_count = 0;

    for (int i = idx; i < num_crystals; i += gridDim.x * blockDim.x) {
        Crystal c = crystals[i];
        if (c.locked != 2) {  // Skip pruned
            local_sum += fabsf(weights[i]);
            local_count++;
        }
    }

    // Warp reduction for sum
    float warp_sum = warp_reduce_sum(local_sum);
    int warp_count = warp_reduce_sum_int(local_count);

    // First thread in warp writes to shared
    int lane = tid & 31;
    int warp_id = tid >> 5;

    if (lane == 0) {
        shared[warp_id] = warp_sum;
        shared[warp_id + 32] = (float)warp_count;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Final reduction by first warp
    if (tid < 32) {
        float sum = (tid < (bs >> 5)) ? shared[tid] : 0.0f;
        float cnt = (tid < (bs >> 5)) ? shared[tid + 32] : 0.0f;
        sum = warp_reduce_sum(sum);
        cnt = warp_reduce_sum(cnt);

        if (tid == 0) {
            float weight_mean = (cnt > 0) ? (sum / cnt) : 1.0f;
            // Store global scale in shared[0] for all threads to use
            shared[0] = compute_radio_scale(weight_mean);
        }
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Now write scale for each crystal
    float global_scale = shared[0];
    if (idx < num_crystals) {
        scales[idx] = global_scale;
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// PRINCIPIA: Mutual Information Estimation Kernel (Book XIV)
// Estimates MI(X,Y) using histogram binning for pre-filtering
// Returns approximate MI which routes data to appropriate K-sector
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// PRINCIPIA: Snap value to nearest lattice point, return index
// Dual-lattice version: Path A (signal) or Path B (geometric)
__device__ __forceinline__ int snap_to_lattice_index_dual(float value, float scale, int use_path_b) {
    float abs_val = fabsf(value);
    float min_dist = 1e10f;
    int best_idx = 0;
    
        const float* lattice = use_path_b ? Grids::LATTICE_PATH_B : Grids::LATTICE_PATH_A;
    const int lattice_size = use_path_b ? 15 : 13;

    for (int i = 0; i < lattice_size; i++) {
        float lattice_point = lattice[i] * scale;
        float dist = fabsf(abs_val - lattice_point);
        if (dist < min_dist) {
            min_dist = dist;
            best_idx = i;
        }
    }
    return best_idx;
}

// Original 12-point version (backward compatibility)
__device__ __forceinline__ int snap_to_lattice_index(float value, float scale) {
    return snap_to_lattice_index_dual(value, scale, 0);
}

__global__ void mi_estimation_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ activations,  // Source activations
    const float* __restrict__ targets,       // Target values
    float* __restrict__ mi_values,           // Output MI per sample
    const uint32_t num_samples,
    const uint32_t dim,
    const float scale                        // Radio-tuned scale
) {
    // ==========================================================================
    // PRINCIPIA {2,3} DERIVED MUTUAL INFORMATION
    // ==========================================================================
    // Instead of Pearson correlation (foreign statistics), we use:
    //   2 = DISTINCTION: Snap values to 12-point lattice → discrete categories
    //   3 = SUBSTRATE: Position rhythm (i % K) provides context weighting
    //
    // ONETWO methodology:
    //   ONE: Each value maps to a lattice index (distinction)
    //   TWO: Count agreement (X and Y same lattice index = mutual information)
    //
    // MI = agreement_count / total_count
    // Weighted by rhythm: positions where (i % K) aligns contribute more
    // ==========================================================================

    const int sample = blockIdx.x;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    const int bs = blockDim.x;

    if (sample >= num_samples) return;

    extern __shared__ float shared[];

    const float* x = activations + sample * dim;
    const float* y = targets + sample * dim;

    // Count lattice agreements (DISTINCTION comparison)
    int local_agreements = 0;
    int local_total = 0;
    float local_rhythm_weight = 0.0f;

    for (int i = tid; i < dim; i += bs) {
        // DISTINCTION (2): Snap both values to lattice
        int x_lattice = snap_to_lattice_index(x[i], scale);
        int y_lattice = snap_to_lattice_index(y[i], scale);

        // SUBSTRATE (3): Position rhythm provides context
        // Higher weight when position aligns with K-sector boundaries
        float rhythm = 1.0f;
        if (i % 17 == 0) rhythm = 3.0f;       // K_STRONG alignment
        else if (i % 25 == 0) rhythm = 2.5f;  // K_WEAK alignment
        else if (i % 137 == 0) rhythm = 2.0f; // K_EM alignment

        // Agreement: same lattice index = shared information
        if (x_lattice == y_lattice) {
            local_agreements++;
            local_rhythm_weight += rhythm;
        }
        local_total++;
    }

    // Warp reduction for agreement count
    int warp_agreements = warp_reduce_sum_int(local_agreements);
    int warp_total = warp_reduce_sum_int(local_total);
    float warp_rhythm = warp_reduce_sum(local_rhythm_weight);

    int lane = tid & 31;
    int warp_id = tid >> 5;

    if (lane == 0) {
        shared[warp_id] = (float)warp_agreements;
        shared[warp_id + 32] = (float)warp_total;
        shared[warp_id + 64] = warp_rhythm;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Final reduction
    if (tid < 32) {
        float agreements = (tid < (bs >> 5)) ? shared[tid] : 0.0f;
        float total = (tid < (bs >> 5)) ? shared[tid + 32] : 0.0f;
        float rhythm = (tid < (bs >> 5)) ? shared[tid + 64] : 0.0f;

        agreements = warp_reduce_sum(agreements);
        total = warp_reduce_sum(total);
        rhythm = warp_reduce_sum(rhythm);

        if (tid == 0) {
            // MI = weighted agreement ratio
            // Scale by rhythm to emphasize K-aligned positions
            float base_mi = (total > 0) ? (agreements / total) : 0.0f;
            float rhythm_bonus = (agreements > 0) ? (rhythm / agreements - 1.0f) * 0.1f : 0.0f;

            // Final MI: base agreement + rhythm bonus
            // Clamped to [0, 1] range for routing thresholds
            float mi = fminf(base_mi + rhythm_bonus, 1.0f);

            mi_values[sample] = mi;
        }
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// PRINCIPIA: MI Pre-Filter Kernel (Book XIV)
// Routes data to K-sectors based on mutual information
// Rejects noise (MI < 0.01)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
__global__ void mi_prefilter_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ mi_values,
    int* __restrict__ sector_assignments,  // Output: sector index (-1 = reject)
    const uint32_t num_samples
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_samples) return;

    float mi = mi_values[idx];
    sector_assignments[idx] = mi_route_to_sector(mi);
}

// =============================================================================
// 12-FERMION LOCK: Position-Aware Sequence Reconstruction
// =============================================================================
// This kernel implements expert caching using the 12-unit rhythm.
// When K matches the data's periodicity, reconstruction is 100% accurate.
//
// The 12-Fermion structure ensures:
//   - 3 generations with 120° phase spacing (geometric closure)
//   - 12n step quantization for weight locking
//   - 1/30 leakage ratio for sparse activation (quark confinement analog)
// =============================================================================

// =============================================================================
// NATURAL CRYSTALLIZATION - Works for BOTH Training AND Compression
// =============================================================================
// The same process:
//   TRAINING: Data flows through QUADRA → what crystallizes = model
//   COMPRESSION: Weights flow through QUADRA → what crystallizes = kept
//
// Don't force selection. Build traces. Let it flow.
// What survives 7 iterations at (e-2) decay = true crystal.
// =============================================================================

// State for each weight/data point during crystallization
struct CrystalCandidate {
    float value;              // Original value
    float plasticity;         // Decays by (e-2) each iteration
    uint32_t distinguished;   // QUADRA L1 result
    float relation;           // QUADRA L2 result (0-1)
    int transformation;       // QUADRA L3 result (K-sector)
    bool closed;              // QUADRA L4 result (closure achieved?)
    bool crystallized;        // Final status after 7 iterations
};

// Single iteration of natural crystallization
// Call this 7 times to complete the crystallization process
__global__ void natural_crystallize_iteration_kernel(
    CrystalCandidate* __restrict__ candidates,
    const uint32_t num_candidates,
    const int iteration,       // 0-6
    const float threshold      // For distinction (derived from data mean)
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_candidates) return;

    CrystalCandidate c = candidates[idx];

    // Already crystallized or already dead - skip
    if (c.crystallized || c.plasticity < Principia::PLASTICITY_TAU) {
        return;
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // QUADRA FLOW: Let data flow through the architecture
    // =========================================================================

    if (iteration == 0) {
        // LEVEL 1: DISTINGUISH
        c.distinguished = quadra_distinguish(c.value, threshold);
    }
    else if (iteration == 1) {
        // LEVEL 2: RELATE (to self - find internal structure)
        // Use rotated version as "other" to find self-similarity
        uint32_t rotated = (c.distinguished << 8) | (c.distinguished >> 8);
        c.relation = quadra_relate(c.distinguished, rotated);
    }
    else if (iteration == 2) {
        // LEVEL 3: CHANGE (what transformation does this value represent?)
        uint32_t original = __float_as_uint(c.value) & 0xFFFF;
        c.transformation = quadra_change(original, c.distinguished);
    }
    else if (iteration == 3) {
        // LEVEL 4: EMBED (closure test)
        uint32_t original = __float_as_uint(c.value) & 0xFFFF;
        c.closed = quadra_embed(original, c.distinguished, c.relation, c.transformation);
    }
    else {
        // Iterations 4-6: Continue (e-2) decay and verify stability
        // If still bound after decay, it's a true crystal
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // UNIVERSAL CLOCK: (e-2) decay
    // =========================================================================
    c.plasticity *= Principia::UNIVERSAL_CLOCK;  // 0.71828

    // =========================================================================
    // CRYSTALLIZATION CHECK
    // =========================================================================
    // After iteration 6 (7 total), check if it survived
    if (iteration == 6) {
        // Crystal if: closed AND plasticity didn't hit zero AND has structure
        c.crystallized = c.closed &&
                        (c.plasticity > Principia::PLASTICITY_TAU) &&
                        (c.relation > Principia::STRONG_COUPLING);  // 2/17 minimum
    }

    candidates[idx] = c;
}

// Initialize candidates from weights (for COMPRESSION)
__global__ void init_crystal_candidates_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ weights,
    CrystalCandidate* __restrict__ candidates,
    const uint32_t num_weights
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_weights) return;

    CrystalCandidate c;
    c.value = weights[idx];
    c.plasticity = 1.0f;  // Start at full plasticity
    c.distinguished = 0;
    c.relation = 0.0f;
    c.transformation = 0;
    c.closed = false;
    c.crystallized = false;

    candidates[idx] = c;
}

// Extract crystallized weights (sparse compression result) - DUAL LATTICE
__global__ void extract_crystals_kernel(
    const CrystalCandidate* __restrict__ candidates,
    const uint32_t num_candidates,
    uint8_t* __restrict__ encoded,          // Output: 5-bit encoded (sign + lattice index)
    uint64_t* __restrict__ crystal_indices, // Output: indices of crystals
    uint32_t* __restrict__ crystal_count,   // Output: total count
    int use_path_b                          // 0 = Path A (signal), 1 = Path B (geometric)
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_candidates) return;

    CrystalCandidate c = candidates[idx];

    if (c.crystallized) {
        // Atomically get next output position
        uint32_t pos = atomicAdd(crystal_count, 1);

        // Dual-lattice: Use Path A (signal) or Path B (geometric)
        const float* lattice = use_path_b ? Grids::LATTICE_PATH_B : Grids::LATTICE_PATH_A;
        const int lattice_size = use_path_b ? 15 : 13;
        
        // Snap to nearest lattice point and get INDEX (not value!)
        float abs_val = fabsf(c.value);
        int best_lattice_idx = 0;
        float min_dist = fabsf(abs_val - lattice[0]);

        for (int i = 1; i < lattice_size; i++) {
            float dist = fabsf(abs_val - lattice[i]);
            if (dist < min_dist) {
                min_dist = dist;
                best_lattice_idx = i;
            }
        }

        // Encode: 5-bit format
        // Bit 4: sign (0=positive, 1=negative)
        // Bits 0-3: lattice index (0-14 for Path B, 0-12 for Path A)
        // Both fit in 4 bits (0-15 max)
        uint8_t sign_bit = (c.value < 0) ? 0x10 : 0x00;
        uint8_t lattice_idx = best_lattice_idx & 0x0F;  // 4 bits = 0-15
        encoded[pos] = sign_bit | lattice_idx;
        
        crystal_indices[pos] = idx;
    }
}

// Compute crystallization statistics
__global__ void crystal_stats_kernel(
    const CrystalCandidate* __restrict__ candidates,
    const uint32_t num_candidates,
    uint32_t* __restrict__ total_crystals,
    uint32_t* __restrict__ koide_locked,    // Score near 2/3
    uint32_t* __restrict__ clock_locked,    // Score near (e-2)
    uint32_t* __restrict__ strong_locked    // Score near 2/17
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_candidates) return;

    CrystalCandidate c = candidates[idx];

    if (c.crystallized) {
        atomicAdd(total_crystals, 1);

        // Check which universal constant this crystal locked to
        float eps = 0.05f;
        if (fabsf(c.relation - Principia::KOIDE_RATIO) < eps) {
            atomicAdd(koide_locked, 1);
        }
        if (fabsf(c.relation - Principia::UNIVERSAL_CLOCK) < eps) {
            atomicAdd(clock_locked, 1);
        }
        if (fabsf(c.relation - Principia::STRONG_COUPLING) < eps) {
            atomicAdd(strong_locked, 1);
        }
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// HOST FUNCTION: Run complete 7-iteration crystallization
// =============================================================================
// This is the unified entry point for BOTH training and compression:
//   - For training: candidates come from data
//   - For compression: candidates come from model weights
//
// Returns: percentage that crystallized (should be ~51% if architecture is correct)
// =============================================================================

struct CrystallizationResult {
    uint64_t total_input;        // For >4B weights
    uint64_t total_crystals;     // For >4B crystals
    uint32_t koide_locked;
    uint32_t clock_locked;
    uint32_t strong_locked;
    float crystal_ratio;         // Should approach 0.51 if correct
    uint8_t* encoded;            // 5-bit encoded: sign (bit 4) + lattice index (bits 0-3)
    uint64_t* crystal_indices;   // Position in original tensor (for >4GB files)
    float scale;                 // Normalization scale for reconstruction
};

// Compress model weights using natural crystallization with dual-lattice
// INPUT: dense weights array, layer type (0=input/Path A, 1=hidden/Path B)
// OUTPUT: sparse crystals (only what survived 7 iterations)
extern "C" CrystallizationResult crystallize_weights(
    const float* h_weights,
    uint32_t num_weights,
    int use_path_b = 0  // 0 = Path A (signal/input), 1 = Path B (geometric/hidden)
) {
    CrystallizationResult result = {0};
    result.total_input = num_weights;

    // Device memory
    float* d_weights;
    CrystalCandidate* d_candidates;
    uint8_t* d_encoded;          // 5-bit encoded: sign + lattice index
    uint64_t* d_crystal_indices; // Position indices (uint64_t for >4GB)
    uint32_t* d_counts;          // [total, koide, clock, strong]

    cudaMalloc(&d_weights, num_weights * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_candidates, num_weights * sizeof(CrystalCandidate));
    cudaMalloc(&d_encoded, num_weights * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&d_crystal_indices, num_weights * sizeof(uint64_t));
    cudaMalloc(&d_counts, 4 * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(d_counts, 0, 4 * sizeof(uint32_t));

    cudaMemcpy(d_weights, h_weights, num_weights * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = (num_weights + threads - 1) / threads;

    // Initialize candidates from weights
    init_crystal_candidates_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(
        d_weights, d_candidates, num_weights
    );

    // Compute threshold from weight distribution (radio tuning)
    // Calculate mean absolute value for adaptive threshold
    float sum_abs = 0.0f;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_weights; i++) {
        sum_abs += fabsf(h_weights[i]);
    }
    float mean_abs = (num_weights > 0) ? (sum_abs / num_weights) : 0.0f;
    // OPTIMIZED: Increased threshold_scale from 0.5 to 1.1 to achieve ~10% crystal ratio
    float threshold = mean_abs * 1.1f;  // Adaptive threshold based on data

    // =========================================================================
    // THE 7-ITERATION CRYSTALLIZATION LOOP
    // =========================================================================
    // This is where the magic happens:
    // - Iteration 0: DISTINGUISH (L1)
    // - Iteration 1: RELATE (L2)
    // - Iteration 2: CHANGE (L3)
    // - Iteration 3: EMBED (L4)
    // - Iterations 4-6: Continue decay, verify stability
    // - After 7: What survived = crystal
    // =========================================================================
    for (int iter = 0; iter < 7; iter++) {
        natural_crystallize_iteration_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(
            d_candidates, num_weights, iter, threshold
        );
        cudaDeviceSynchronize();
    }

    // Extract crystals (now outputs encoded 5-bit format) - DUAL LATTICE
    extract_crystals_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(
        d_candidates, num_weights,
        d_encoded, d_crystal_indices, d_counts,
        use_path_b  // Pass dual-lattice flag: 0 = Path A (signal), 1 = Path B (geometric)
    );

    // Get statistics
    crystal_stats_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(
        d_candidates, num_weights,
        d_counts, d_counts + 1, d_counts + 2, d_counts + 3
    );

    // Copy results back
    uint32_t h_counts[4];
    cudaMemcpy(h_counts, d_counts, 4 * sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    result.total_input = num_weights;
    result.total_crystals = h_counts[0];
    result.koide_locked = h_counts[1];
    result.clock_locked = h_counts[2];
    result.strong_locked = h_counts[3];
    result.crystal_ratio = (float)result.total_crystals / (float)num_weights;

    // Compute scale for reconstruction (max weight magnitude / max lattice value)
    float max_weight = 0.0f;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_weights; i++) {
        float abs_w = fabsf(h_weights[i]);
        if (abs_w > max_weight) max_weight = abs_w;
    }
    result.scale = (max_weight > 0.0f) ? (max_weight / 511.0f) : 1.0f;  // LATTICE_12[11] = 511

    // Allocate and copy encoded crystal data (5-bit format!)
    result.encoded = (uint8_t*)malloc(result.total_crystals * sizeof(uint8_t));
    result.crystal_indices = (uint64_t*)malloc(result.total_crystals * sizeof(uint64_t));

    cudaMemcpy(result.encoded, d_encoded,
               result.total_crystals * sizeof(uint8_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(result.crystal_indices, d_crystal_indices,
               result.total_crystals * sizeof(uint64_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    // Cleanup
    cudaFree(d_weights);
    cudaFree(d_candidates);
    cudaFree(d_encoded);
    cudaFree(d_crystal_indices);
    cudaFree(d_counts);

    // Report
    printf("CRYSTALLIZATION COMPLETE\n");
    printf("========================\n");
    printf("Input weights:    %u\n", result.total_input);
    printf("Crystals formed:  %u (%.1f%%)\n", result.total_crystals, result.crystal_ratio * 100.0f);
    printf("Koide locked:     %u (at 2/3 = 0.666)\n", result.koide_locked);
    printf("Clock locked:     %u (at e-2 = 0.718)\n", result.clock_locked);
    printf("Strong locked:    %u (at 2/17 = 0.117)\n", result.strong_locked);
    printf("\n");

    // Jitter analysis (Book 5)
    float jitter_ratio = 1.0f - result.crystal_ratio;
    float jitter_floor = 0.000722f;  // (π-3)/196

    printf("Jitter (noise):   %.4f%% (floor = %.4f%%)\n",
           jitter_ratio * 100.0f, jitter_floor * 100.0f);
    printf("\n");

    if (jitter_ratio <= jitter_floor * 2.0f) {
        // Within 2x of theoretical floor - system is nearly locked
        printf("✓ LOCKED SYSTEM: Jitter near floor - crystallization complete\n");
    } else if (jitter_ratio < 0.1f) {
        // Less than 10% noise - good signal quality
        printf("✓ HIGH QUALITY: %.1f%% crystallized - strong invariant signal\n",
               result.crystal_ratio * 100.0f);
    } else if (jitter_ratio < 0.5f) {
        // 10-50% noise - moderate signal
        printf("○ MODERATE: %.1f%% crystallized - signal present but noisy\n",
               result.crystal_ratio * 100.0f);
    } else {
        // More than 50% noise - weak signal or wrong architecture
        printf("⚠ LOW SIGNAL: Only %.1f%% crystallized\n",
               result.crystal_ratio * 100.0f);
        printf("  → Check if data matches architecture (L2 substrate alignment)\n");
        printf("  → Or data is mostly plasticity (still learning)\n");
    }

    return result;
}

// Reconstruct from crystals (decompression)
extern "C" void reconstruct_from_crystals(
    const CrystallizationResult* crystals,
    float* h_output,
    uint32_t output_size,
    int use_path_b = 0  // 0 = Path A, 1 = Path B
) {
    // Initialize output to zero
    memset(h_output, 0, output_size * sizeof(float));

    // Decode from 5-bit encoded format and place crystals at their original indices
    const float* lattice = use_path_b ? Grids::LATTICE_PATH_B : Grids::LATTICE_PATH_A;
    const int lattice_size = use_path_b ? 15 : 13;
    
    for (uint64_t i = 0; i < crystals->total_crystals; i++) {
        uint64_t idx = crystals->crystal_indices[i];
        if (idx < output_size) {
            uint8_t encoded = crystals->encoded[i];
            uint8_t sign_bit = (encoded >> 4) & 0x01;
            uint8_t lattice_idx = encoded & 0x0F;
            
            if (lattice_idx < lattice_size) {
                float value = lattice[lattice_idx] * crystals->scale;
                h_output[idx] = (sign_bit == 0) ? value : -value;
            }
        }
    }

    // The gaps (non-crystallized positions) remain zero
    // These can be filled by the QUADRA architecture during inference
    // using structural derivation (not guessing)
}

// Free crystallization result
extern "C" void free_crystallization_result(CrystallizationResult* result) {
    if (result->encoded) free(result->encoded);
    if (result->crystal_indices) free(result->crystal_indices);
    result->encoded = NULL;
    result->crystal_indices = NULL;
}

// =============================================================================
// 12-FERMION EXPERT CACHING
// =============================================================================

// Expert cache entry - stores crystallized patterns at 12n positions
struct ExpertCache {
    float values[12];       // Cached values for each rhythm phase (0-11)
    uint8_t valid[12];      // Which phases have valid cached data
    uint8_t generation;     // Which generation this cache belongs to (0,1,2)
    float confidence;       // How "locked" this cache is (approaches Koide at stability)
};

__global__ void fermion_lock_reconstruct_kernel(
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ input_sequence,    // Input bytes
    float* __restrict__ output_reconstruction,      // Reconstructed output
    Crystal* __restrict__ crystals,                 // Crystal network for lookup
    const uint32_t* __restrict__ crystal_map,       // Maps (prev_byte, rhythm) to crystal
    const uint32_t sequence_length,
    const uint32_t num_crystals,
    const int K_sector                              // Active K for this reconstruction
) {
    const int pos = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (pos >= sequence_length) return;

    // Compute 12-Fermion rhythm position
    int rhythm_phase = pos % Principia::STEP_COST_L2_L3;  // 0-11 (12 fermion phases)
    int generation = pos % Principia::GENERATIONS;         // 0, 1, 2

    // Crystal key with rhythm: key = (prev_byte << 16) | (pos % K)
    uint8_t prev_byte = (pos > 0) ? input_sequence[pos - 1] : 0;
    uint32_t crystal_key = ((uint32_t)prev_byte << 16) | (pos % K_sector);

    // Lookup crystal for this position
    uint32_t crystal_idx = crystal_map[crystal_key % num_crystals];
    Crystal c = crystals[crystal_idx];

    // Only use locked crystals for reconstruction
    if (c.locked != 1) {
        output_reconstruction[pos] = 0.0f;  // No reconstruction for unlocked
        return;
    }

    // Verify generation alignment (120° phase check)
    bool generation_aligned = (c.generation == generation);

    // Verify rhythm alignment (12-step check)
    bool rhythm_aligned = (c.rhythm_phase == rhythm_phase);

    // Compute reconstruction confidence
    // Perfect alignment = approaching Koide ratio (2/3)
    float alignment_score = 0.0f;
    if (generation_aligned && rhythm_aligned) {
        alignment_score = Principia::KOIDE_RATIO;  // 2/3 - perfect geometric closure
    } else if (generation_aligned || rhythm_aligned) {
        alignment_score = Principia::STRONG_COUPLING;  // 2/17 - partial lock
    }

    // Apply leakage suppression (1/30 sparse activation)
    float leakage_factor = 1.0f;
    if (!generation_aligned) {
        leakage_factor = Principia::LEAKAGE_RATIO;  // 1/30 suppression
    }

    // Reconstruct using lattice-snapped weight
    float lattice_weight = Principia::LATTICE_12[c.lattice_idx];
    float reconstruction = lattice_weight * c.score * alignment_score * leakage_factor;

    output_reconstruction[pos] = reconstruction;
}

// Initialize crystal with 12-Fermion alignment
__global__ void crystal_init_fermion_kernel(
    Crystal* __restrict__ crystals,
    const uint32_t num_crystals,
    const uint32_t base_position  // Starting position for this batch
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_crystals) return;

    uint32_t pos = base_position + idx;

    Crystal c = crystals[idx];

    // Set 12-Fermion generation and rhythm
    crystal_set_generation(&c, pos);

    // Initialize resonance (will be computed during scoring)
    c.resonance = 1.0f;  // Max distance initially

    // Assign initial sector based on position rhythm
    // Positions at K-multiples get routed to that sector
    // Sector indices: 0=K377(Vacuum), 1=K25(Weak), 2=K137(EM), 3=K17(Strong)
    if (pos % 17 == 0) c.sector = 3;       // K=17 Strong
    else if (pos % 25 == 0) c.sector = 1;  // K=25 Weak
    else if (pos % 137 == 0) c.sector = 2; // K=137 EM
    else c.sector = 0;                      // K=377 Vacuum (default)

    crystals[idx] = c;
}

// Verify Triple Lock convergence
// Returns count of crystals that have locked to Koide/Clock/Strong values
__global__ void triple_lock_verify_kernel(
    const Crystal* __restrict__ crystals,
    const uint32_t num_crystals,
    uint32_t* __restrict__ koide_count,    // Locked at 2/3
    uint32_t* __restrict__ clock_count,    // Locked at (e-2)
    uint32_t* __restrict__ strong_count    // Locked at 2/17
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_crystals) return;

    Crystal c = crystals[idx];
    if (c.locked != 1) return;  // Only count locked crystals

    float eps = 0.02f;  // Tolerance for "lock" detection

    // Check which universal constant this crystal has locked to
    if (fabsf(c.score - Principia::KOIDE_RATIO) < eps) {
        atomicAdd(koide_count, 1);
    }
    if (fabsf(c.score - Principia::UNIVERSAL_CLOCK) < eps) {
        atomicAdd(clock_count, 1);
    }
    if (fabsf(c.score - Principia::STRONG_COUPLING) < eps) {
        atomicAdd(strong_count, 1);
    }
}

// Expert caching kernel: Cache patterns at 12n positions for fast reconstruction
__global__ void expert_cache_update_kernel(
    Crystal* __restrict__ crystals,
    ExpertCache* __restrict__ cache,        // Cache array indexed by prev_byte
    const uint32_t num_crystals,
    const float cache_threshold             // Only cache crystals with score above this
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_crystals) return;

    Crystal c = crystals[idx];

    // Only cache locked, high-scoring crystals
    if (c.locked != 1 || c.score < cache_threshold) return;

    // Compute cache index from source node (prev_byte equivalent)
    uint32_t cache_idx = c.src % 256;  // 256 possible prev_byte values
    uint8_t phase = c.rhythm_phase;

    // Update cache at this phase
    ExpertCache* entry = &cache[cache_idx];

    // Store lattice-snapped value
    float cached_value = Principia::LATTICE_12[c.lattice_idx] * c.score;

    // Atomic update (simple overwrite for now - could use max)
    entry->values[phase] = cached_value;
    entry->valid[phase] = 1;
    entry->generation = c.generation;

    // Update confidence toward Koide ratio as stability increases
    // Confidence approaches 2/3 as more phases become valid
    int valid_count = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
        if (entry->valid[i]) valid_count++;
    }
    entry->confidence = (float)valid_count / 12.0f * Principia::KOIDE_RATIO;
}

__global__ void crystal_score_kernel(
    Crystal* __restrict__ crystals,
    const float* __restrict__ input,
    const float* __restrict__ target,
    const float* __restrict__ output,
    const float* __restrict__ scales,  // PRINCIPIA: Radio-tuned scales
    const uint32_t num_crystals,
    const float learning_rate
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_crystals) return;

    Crystal c = crystals[idx];
    if (c.locked == 2) return;  // Already pruned

    // PRINCIPIA: Get radio-tuned scale for this crystal
    float scale = scales[idx];

    // Score based on contribution to reducing error
    float error = output[c.dst] - target[c.dst];
    float weight_value = Grids::DUAL_NODES[c.lattice_idx % 4] * scale;
    float contribution = input[c.src] * weight_value;

    // Alignment: positive if helped, negative if hurt
    float alignment = -contribution * error;

    // PRINCIPIA: Lattice-ready scoring (Book XIV)
    // score = importance × resonance
    // importance = |alignment| (gradient magnitude)
    // resonance = (1 - distance_to_lattice)
    float importance = fabsf(alignment);
    float lattice_score = lattice_ready_score(importance, weight_value, scale);

    // Update score (exponential moving average)
    c.fires++;
    float alpha = learning_rate / (1.0f + logf((float)c.fires));

    // Blend alignment direction with lattice-ready magnitude
    float signed_score = (alignment >= 0 ? 1.0f : -1.0f) * lattice_score;
    c.score = (1.0f - alpha) * c.score + alpha * signed_score;

    crystals[idx] = c;
}

// =============================================================================
// PRINCIPIA INFORMATICA: Plasticity Decay by (e-2) = Universal Clock
// =============================================================================
// From Book XV: ρ_{t+1} = ρ_t × (e-2)
// The same decay constant governs physics and learning.

__global__ void plasticity_decay_kernel(
    Crystal* __restrict__ crystals,
    const uint32_t num_crystals
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_crystals) return;

    Crystal c = crystals[idx];
    if (c.locked == 0) {  // Only decay candidates
        // THE UNIVERSAL CLOCK: (e-2) = 0.71828...
        c.plasticity *= Axioms::E_R;
        crystals[idx] = c;
    }
}

// PROTOCOL-COMPLIANT: Warp reduction for counting
// PRINCIPIA: Lock when ρ < τ AND importance HIGH
//            Prune when ρ < τ AND importance LOW
__global__ void crystal_lock_prune_kernel(
    Crystal* __restrict__ crystals,
    const uint32_t num_crystals,
    const float plasticity_threshold,  // τ - crystallization threshold
    const float importance_threshold,  // Score threshold for HIGH vs LOW
    const uint32_t min_fires,
    uint32_t* __restrict__ num_locked,
    uint32_t* __restrict__ num_pruned
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    const int lane_id = threadIdx.x & 31;
    const int warp_id = threadIdx.x >> 5;

    int thread_locked = 0;
    int thread_pruned = 0;

    if (idx < num_crystals) {
        Crystal c = crystals[idx];

        // PRINCIPIA: Crystallization occurs when plasticity falls below threshold
        if (c.locked == 0 && c.fires >= min_fires && c.plasticity < plasticity_threshold) {
            // Plasticity exhausted - must crystallize or prune

            if (c.score > importance_threshold) {
                // HIGH importance + LOW plasticity = LOCK (knowledge crystallizes)
                float abs_score = fabsf(c.score);
                float min_dist = 1e10f;
                int best = 0;

                #pragma unroll
                for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
                    float dist = fabsf(abs_score * 377.0f - Grids::DUAL_NODES[i]);
                    if (dist < min_dist) {
                        min_dist = dist;
                        best = i;
                    }
                }

                c.lattice_idx = best;
                c.direction = (c.score >= 0.0f) ? 1 : -1;
                c.locked = 1;
                thread_locked = 1;
            }
            else {
                // LOW importance + LOW plasticity = PRUNE (noise rejected)
                c.locked = 2;
                thread_pruned = 1;
            }
            crystals[idx] = c;
        }
    }

    // Warp reduction for counts - NO per-thread atomicAdd
    #pragma unroll
    for (int offset = 16; offset > 0; offset >>= 1) {
        thread_locked += __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, thread_locked, offset);
        thread_pruned += __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, thread_pruned, offset);
    }

    // Block reduction
    __shared__ int warp_locked[32];
    __shared__ int warp_pruned[32];

    if (lane_id == 0) {
        warp_locked[warp_id] = thread_locked;
        warp_pruned[warp_id] = thread_pruned;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // First warp reduces all warp results
    if (warp_id == 0) {
        int num_warps = (blockDim.x + 31) >> 5;
        int val_locked = (lane_id < num_warps) ? warp_locked[lane_id] : 0;
        int val_pruned = (lane_id < num_warps) ? warp_pruned[lane_id] : 0;

        #pragma unroll
        for (int offset = 16; offset > 0; offset >>= 1) {
            val_locked += __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, val_locked, offset);
            val_pruned += __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, val_pruned, offset);
        }

        // Single atomic per block (not per thread!)
        if (lane_id == 0 && val_locked > 0) {
            atomicAdd(num_locked, val_locked);
        }
        if (lane_id == 0 && val_pruned > 0) {
            atomicAdd(num_pruned, val_pruned);
        }
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// X.5 CSR CONVERSION - Convert dense crystals to sparse CSR format
// =============================================================================

// Count how many locked crystals target each destination
__global__ void count_crystals_per_dst(
    const Crystal* __restrict__ crystals,
    int* __restrict__ dst_counts,
    const uint32_t num_crystals
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_crystals) return;

    Crystal c = crystals[idx];
    if (c.locked == 1) {
        atomicAdd(&dst_counts[c.dst], 1);
    }
}

// Build CSR arrays from dense crystal array
__global__ void build_csr_kernel(
    const Crystal* __restrict__ crystals,
    const int* __restrict__ row_ptrs,
    int* __restrict__ row_counters,  // Atomic counters per row
    uint32_t* __restrict__ src_nodes,
    uint8_t* __restrict__ lattice_idx,
    int8_t* __restrict__ directions,
    const uint32_t num_crystals
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_crystals) return;

    Crystal c = crystals[idx];
    if (c.locked != 1) return;

    // Get position in this row's segment
    int pos = atomicAdd(&row_counters[c.dst], 1);
    int global_pos = row_ptrs[c.dst] + pos;

    src_nodes[global_pos] = c.src;
    lattice_idx[global_pos] = c.lattice_idx;
    directions[global_pos] = c.direction;
}

// Host function to rebuild CSR from dense crystals
void rebuild_csr(CrystalNetwork* net) {
    const int threads = 256;
    int blocks = (net->num_crystals + threads - 1) / threads;

    // Step 1: Count crystals per destination
    int* d_dst_counts;
    cudaMalloc(&d_dst_counts, net->num_nodes * sizeof(int));
    cudaMemset(d_dst_counts, 0, net->num_nodes * sizeof(int));

    count_crystals_per_dst<<<blocks, threads>>>(
        net->crystals, d_dst_counts, net->num_crystals
    );

    // Step 2: Prefix sum to get row_ptrs (CPU for simplicity, could use CUB)
    int* h_dst_counts = (int*)malloc(net->num_nodes * sizeof(int));
    cudaMemcpy(h_dst_counts, d_dst_counts, net->num_nodes * sizeof(int), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    int* h_row_ptrs = (int*)malloc((net->num_nodes + 1) * sizeof(int));
    h_row_ptrs[0] = 0;
