    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < net->num_nodes; i++) {
        h_row_ptrs[i + 1] = h_row_ptrs[i] + h_dst_counts[i];
    }
    uint32_t nnz = h_row_ptrs[net->num_nodes];

    // Step 3: Allocate CSR arrays (free old if exists)
    if (net->csr_row_ptrs) cudaFree(net->csr_row_ptrs);
    if (net->csr_src_nodes) cudaFree(net->csr_src_nodes);
    if (net->csr_lattice_idx) cudaFree(net->csr_lattice_idx);
    if (net->csr_directions) cudaFree(net->csr_directions);

    cudaMalloc(&net->csr_row_ptrs, (net->num_nodes + 1) * sizeof(int));
    cudaMalloc(&net->csr_src_nodes, nnz * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&net->csr_lattice_idx, nnz * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&net->csr_directions, nnz * sizeof(int8_t));

    cudaMemcpy(net->csr_row_ptrs, h_row_ptrs, (net->num_nodes + 1) * sizeof(int), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    // Step 4: Build CSR arrays
    int* d_row_counters;
    cudaMalloc(&d_row_counters, net->num_nodes * sizeof(int));
    cudaMemset(d_row_counters, 0, net->num_nodes * sizeof(int));

    build_csr_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(
        net->crystals, net->csr_row_ptrs, d_row_counters,
        net->csr_src_nodes, net->csr_lattice_idx, net->csr_directions,
        net->num_crystals
    );

    net->csr_nnz = nnz;
    net->csr_valid = true;

    // Cleanup
    cudaFree(d_dst_counts);
    cudaFree(d_row_counters);
    free(h_dst_counts);
    free(h_row_ptrs);
}

// Initialize CSR fields to null
void init_csr(CrystalNetwork* net) {
    net->csr_row_ptrs = nullptr;
    net->csr_src_nodes = nullptr;
    net->csr_lattice_idx = nullptr;
    net->csr_directions = nullptr;
    net->csr_valid = false;
    net->csr_nnz = 0;
}

// =============================================================================
// XI. ULTRA-SIEVE (Vectorized Crystal Snap)
// =============================================================================

__global__ void ultra_sieve_kernel(
    const float4* __restrict__ input,
    float4* __restrict__ output,
    uint32_t* __restrict__ indices,
    const int num_float4,
    const float scale,
    const float threshold
) {
    const int tid = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    const int lane_id = threadIdx.x & 31;
    const int warp_id = threadIdx.x >> 5;

    __shared__ int warp_locks[32];
    int thread_locks = 0;

    if (tid < num_float4) {
        float4 in = __ldg(&input[tid]);
        float4 out;
        uint8_t idx[4];

        float weights[4] = {in.x, in.y, in.z, in.w};

        #pragma unroll
        for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
            float w = weights[i];
            float sign = (w >= 0.0f) ? 1.0f : -1.0f;
            float normalized = fabsf(w) / scale;

            float min_dist = 1e10f;
            int best = 255;

            #pragma unroll
            for (int j = 0; j < 4; j++) {
                float dist = fabsf(normalized - Grids::DUAL_NODES[j]);
                if (dist < min_dist) {
                    min_dist = dist;
                    best = j;
                }
            }

            if (min_dist < threshold * 377.0f) {
                ((float*)&out)[i] = Grids::DUAL_NODES[best] * sign * scale;
                idx[i] = best;
                thread_locks++;
            } else {
                ((float*)&out)[i] = 0.0f;
                idx[i] = 255;
            }
        }

        output[tid] = out;
        indices[tid] = idx[0] | (idx[1] << 8) | (idx[2] << 16) | (idx[3] << 24);
    }

    // Warp reduction for stats
    int warp_total = warp_reduce_sum_int(thread_locks);
    if (lane_id == 0) warp_locks[warp_id] = warp_total;
    __syncthreads();

    if (warp_id == 0) {
        int val = (lane_id < (blockDim.x >> 5)) ? warp_locks[lane_id] : 0;
        warp_reduce_sum_int(val);
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// XII. PATTERN EMBEDDING (FBC Semantics → Vectors)
// =============================================================================

__global__ void pattern_embed_kernel(
    const SemanticToken* __restrict__ tokens,
    float* __restrict__ embeddings,
    const int num_tokens,
    const int embed_dim
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_tokens) return;

    SemanticToken tok = tokens[idx];

    // Base embedding from token ID
    float* embed = &embeddings[idx * embed_dim];

    // Initialize with sinusoidal position encoding
    for (int d = 0; d < embed_dim; d++) {
        float freq = 1.0f / powf(10000.0f, (2.0f * (d / 2)) / embed_dim);
        if (d % 2 == 0) {
            embed[d] = sinf(tok.id * freq);
        } else {
            embed[d] = cosf(tok.id * freq);
        }
    }

    // Add pattern type encoding
    embed[tok.pattern % embed_dim] += 1.0f;

    // Add sector encoding (phase lock to grid)
    int K_vals[4] = {137, 377, 25, 17};
    int K = K_vals[tok.sector % 4];
    embed[(tok.sector + embed_dim/2) % embed_dim] += (float)K / 377.0f;

    // Add phase
    embed[(embed_dim - 1)] = tok.phase;
}

// =============================================================================
// XIII. AGI CORE - Full Forward Pass
// =============================================================================

__global__ void agi_forward_kernel(
    // Input
    const SemanticToken* __restrict__ tokens,
    const int num_tokens,

    // Crystal network
    Crystal* __restrict__ crystals,
    const uint32_t num_crystals,

    // Buffers
    float* __restrict__ embeddings,
    float* __restrict__ hidden,
    float* __restrict__ output,

    // Config
    const int embed_dim,
    const int hidden_dim,
    const float scale,
    const int K_grid
) {
    // This kernel orchestrates a single forward pass
    // In practice, you'd call multiple kernels in sequence

    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;

    // Step 1: Embed tokens (handled by pattern_embed_kernel)
    // Step 2: Crystal forward (handled by crystal_forward_kernel)
    // Step 3: MAL attention (handled by mal_attention_kernel)
    // Step 4: Output projection

    if (idx < num_tokens * embed_dim) {
        // Phase lock the output
        float lock_acc;
        output[idx] = sector_lock(hidden[idx], K_grid, &lock_acc);
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// XIV. CRYSTAL PERSISTENCE (Save/Load)
// =============================================================================

struct CrystalHeader {
    uint32_t magic;         // 0x43525354 = "CRST"
    uint32_t version;
    uint32_t num_crystals;
    uint32_t num_nodes;
    float scale;
    uint32_t num_locked;
    uint32_t num_pruned;
};

void save_crystals(const char* path, const CrystalNetwork* net) {
    FILE* f = fopen(path, "wb");
    if (!f) return;

    CrystalHeader header;
    header.magic = 0x43525354;
    header.version = 1;
    header.num_crystals = net->num_crystals;
    header.num_nodes = net->num_nodes;
    header.scale = net->scale;
    header.num_locked = net->num_locked;
    header.num_pruned = net->num_pruned;

    fwrite(&header, sizeof(header), 1, f);

    // Copy crystals from device
    Crystal* h_crystals = (Crystal*)malloc(net->num_crystals * sizeof(Crystal));
    cudaMemcpy(h_crystals, net->crystals, net->num_crystals * sizeof(Crystal), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    // Only save locked crystals (sparse)
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < net->num_crystals; i++) {
        if (h_crystals[i].locked == 1) {
            fwrite(&h_crystals[i], sizeof(Crystal), 1, f);
        }
    }

    free(h_crystals);
    fclose(f);
}

CrystalNetwork* load_crystals(const char* path) {
    FILE* f = fopen(path, "rb");
    if (!f) return nullptr;

    CrystalHeader header;
    fread(&header, sizeof(header), 1, f);

    if (header.magic != 0x43525354) {
        fclose(f);
        return nullptr;
    }

    CrystalNetwork* net = (CrystalNetwork*)malloc(sizeof(CrystalNetwork));
    net->num_crystals = header.num_locked;  // Only locked crystals saved
    net->num_nodes = header.num_nodes;
    net->scale = header.scale;
    net->num_locked = header.num_locked;
    net->num_pruned = 0;
    net->num_candidates = 0;

    Crystal* h_crystals = (Crystal*)malloc(net->num_crystals * sizeof(Crystal));
    fread(h_crystals, sizeof(Crystal), net->num_crystals, f);

    cudaMalloc(&net->crystals, net->num_crystals * sizeof(Crystal));
    cudaMemcpy(net->crystals, h_crystals, net->num_crystals * sizeof(Crystal), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    cudaMalloc(&net->node_activations, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&net->node_outputs, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float));

    // PROTOCOL: Initialize CSR for warp-reduced forward pass
    init_csr(net);

    free(h_crystals);
    fclose(f);

    return net;
}

// =============================================================================
// XIV. TRANSFORMER KERNELS (merged from ob1_bidirectional_transformer.cu)
// =============================================================================
// These complete the transformer architecture - softmax, reshape, activations

// Block-level reduction helpers
__device__ void block_reduce_sum_shared(float* shared, int tid, int block_size) {
    __syncthreads();
    for (int s = block_size / 2; s > 32; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) shared[tid] += shared[tid + s];
        __syncthreads();
    }
    if (tid < 32) {
        volatile float* vs = shared;
        if (block_size >= 64) vs[tid] += vs[tid + 32];
        vs[tid] += vs[tid + 16];
        vs[tid] += vs[tid + 8];
        vs[tid] += vs[tid + 4];
        vs[tid] += vs[tid + 2];
        vs[tid] += vs[tid + 1];
    }
}

__device__ void block_reduce_max_shared(float* shared, int tid, int block_size) {
    __syncthreads();
    for (int s = block_size / 2; s > 32; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) shared[tid] = fmaxf(shared[tid], shared[tid + s]);
        __syncthreads();
    }
    if (tid < 32) {
        volatile float* vs = shared;
        if (block_size >= 64) vs[tid] = fmaxf(vs[tid], vs[tid + 32]);
        vs[tid] = fmaxf(vs[tid], vs[tid + 16]);
        vs[tid] = fmaxf(vs[tid], vs[tid + 8]);
        vs[tid] = fmaxf(vs[tid], vs[tid + 4]);
        vs[tid] = fmaxf(vs[tid], vs[tid + 2]);
        vs[tid] = fmaxf(vs[tid], vs[tid + 1]);
    }
}

// Parallel RMS Norm
__global__ void rms_norm_kernel(
    float* __restrict__ out,
    const float* __restrict__ x,
    const float* __restrict__ gamma,
    int rows, int cols
) {
    extern __shared__ float shared[];
    const int row = blockIdx.x;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    const int bs = blockDim.x;

    if (row >= rows) return;

    const float* x_row = x + row * cols;
    float* out_row = out + row * cols;

    float sum_sq = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += bs) {
        float v = x_row[i];
        sum_sq += v * v;
    }
    shared[tid] = sum_sq;
    block_reduce_sum_shared(shared, tid, bs);

    __shared__ float rms_inv;
    if (tid == 0) rms_inv = rsqrtf(shared[0] / cols + 1e-6f);
    __syncthreads();

    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += bs) {
        out_row[i] = gamma[i] * x_row[i] * rms_inv;
    }
}

// Parallel Softmax with Mask
__global__ void softmax_masked_kernel(
    float* __restrict__ x,
    const float* __restrict__ mask,
    int rows, int cols
) {
    extern __shared__ float shared[];
    const int row = blockIdx.x;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    const int bs = blockDim.x;

    if (row >= rows) return;

    float* x_row = x + row * cols;
    const float* mask_row = mask + (row % cols) * cols;

    // Apply mask and find max
    float local_max = -FLT_MAX;
    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += bs) {
        x_row[i] += mask_row[i];
        local_max = fmaxf(local_max, x_row[i]);
    }
    shared[tid] = local_max;
    block_reduce_max_shared(shared, tid, bs);

    __shared__ float row_max;
    if (tid == 0) row_max = shared[0];
    __syncthreads();

    // Exp and sum
    float local_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += bs) {
        float e = expf(x_row[i] - row_max);
        x_row[i] = e;
        local_sum += e;
    }
    shared[tid] = local_sum;
    block_reduce_sum_shared(shared, tid, bs);

    __shared__ float sum_inv;
    if (tid == 0) sum_inv = 1.0f / (shared[0] + 1e-10f);
    __syncthreads();

    // Normalize
    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += bs) {
        x_row[i] *= sum_inv;
    }
}

// Bidirectional Attention Mask Generation
__global__ void generate_attention_mask_kernel(
    float* __restrict__ mask,
    int seq, int K_idx
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= seq * seq) return;

    const int i = idx / seq;
    const int j = idx % seq;

    // Causal: can only attend to past
    if (j > i) {
        mask[idx] = -FLT_MAX;
        return;
    }

    // Bidirectional pattern using {2,3} structure
    int K_vals[4] = {377, 25, 137, 17};
    int K = K_vals[K_idx];
    int address = fast_mod(i * 128, K_idx);
    int distance = i - j;
    int metric = fast_mod(distance * distance * 9, K_idx);

    bool local = (distance < 8);
    bool physics = ((address + metric) % K) < (K / 8);

    mask[idx] = (local || physics) ? 0.0f : -FLT_MAX;
}

// Multi-head reshape: [batch, seq, d_model] -> [batch, n_heads, seq, head_dim]
__global__ void reshape_for_heads_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ in,
    float* __restrict__ out,
    int batch, int seq, int n_heads, int head_dim
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    const int total = batch * n_heads * seq * head_dim;
    if (idx >= total) return;

    const int d = idx % head_dim;
    const int s = (idx / head_dim) % seq;
    const int h = (idx / (head_dim * seq)) % n_heads;
    const int b = idx / (head_dim * seq * n_heads);

    const int in_idx = b * seq * n_heads * head_dim + s * n_heads * head_dim + h * head_dim + d;
    out[idx] = in[in_idx];
}

// Multi-head reshape back: [batch, n_heads, seq, head_dim] -> [batch, seq, d_model]
__global__ void reshape_from_heads_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ in,
    float* __restrict__ out,
    int batch, int seq, int n_heads, int head_dim
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    const int total = batch * seq * n_heads * head_dim;
    if (idx >= total) return;

    const int d_model = n_heads * head_dim;
    const int d = idx % d_model;
    const int s = (idx / d_model) % seq;
    const int b = idx / (seq * d_model);

    const int h = d / head_dim;
    const int hd = d % head_dim;
    const int in_idx = b * n_heads * seq * head_dim + h * seq * head_dim + s * head_dim + hd;

    out[idx] = in[in_idx];
}

// GELU Activation
__global__ void gelu_kernel(float* x, int size) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= size) return;
    float v = x[idx];
    x[idx] = v * 0.5f * (1.0f + tanhf(0.7978845608f * (v + 0.044715f * v * v * v)));
}

// Residual Add
__global__ void residual_add_kernel(float* x, const float* r, int size) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= size) return;
    x[idx] += r[idx];
}

// Rotary Position Embeddings (RoPE)
__global__ void apply_rope_kernel(
    float* q, float* k,
    int batch, int n_heads, int seq, int head_dim
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    const int total = batch * n_heads * seq * (head_dim / 2);
    if (idx >= total) return;

    const int d = idx % (head_dim / 2);
    const int s = (idx / (head_dim / 2)) % seq;
    const int h = (idx / ((head_dim / 2) * seq)) % n_heads;
    const int b = idx / ((head_dim / 2) * seq * n_heads);

    float freq = 1.0f / powf(10000.0f, 2.0f * d / head_dim);
    float angle = s * freq;
    float cos_a = cosf(angle);
    float sin_a = sinf(angle);

    int base = b * n_heads * seq * head_dim + h * seq * head_dim + s * head_dim;
    int i1 = base + 2 * d;
    int i2 = base + 2 * d + 1;

    float q1 = q[i1], q2 = q[i2];
    q[i1] = q1 * cos_a - q2 * sin_a;
    q[i2] = q1 * sin_a + q2 * cos_a;

    float k1 = k[i1], k2 = k[i2];
    k[i1] = k1 * cos_a - k2 * sin_a;
    k[i2] = k1 * sin_a + k2 * cos_a;
}

// =============================================================================
// XV. AGI STEP - One Learning Iteration
// =============================================================================

void agi_step(
    CrystalNetwork* net,
    const float* input,
    const float* target,
    float* output,
    const int batch_size,
    const int seq_len,
    const int dim,
    const float learning_rate,
    const float lock_threshold,
    const float prune_threshold
) {
    const int threads = 256;
    const int total_elements = batch_size * seq_len * dim;
    const int n_heads = 4;  // Multi-head attention
    const int head_dim = dim / n_heads;

    // =========================================================================
    // UNIFIED FORWARD PASS - Full transformer stack with crystal learning
    // =========================================================================

    // Allocate working buffers
    float *d_input, *d_normed, *d_attn_out, *d_hidden, *d_crystal_out;
    float *d_Q, *d_K, *d_V, *d_attn_mask, *d_lock_acc;
    float *d_gamma;  // RMSNorm scale (initialized to 1.0)

    cudaMalloc(&d_input, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_normed, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_attn_out, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_hidden, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_crystal_out, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_Q, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_K, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_V, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_attn_mask, seq_len * seq_len * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_lock_acc, batch_size * n_heads * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_gamma, dim * sizeof(float));

    // Initialize gamma to 1.0 (no learnable params - crystal philosophy)
    float* h_gamma = (float*)malloc(dim * sizeof(float));
    for (int i = 0; i < dim; i++) h_gamma[i] = 1.0f;
    cudaMemcpy(d_gamma, h_gamma, dim * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);
    free(h_gamma);

    // Copy input to device
    cudaMemcpy(d_input, input, total_elements * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    int blocks = (total_elements + threads - 1) / threads;

    // =========================================================================
    // LAYER 1: Pre-attention RMSNorm
    // =========================================================================
    int norm_blocks = batch_size * seq_len;
    size_t shared_size = threads * sizeof(float);
    rms_norm_kernel<<<norm_blocks, threads, shared_size>>>(
        d_normed, d_input, d_gamma, batch_size * seq_len, dim
    );

    // =========================================================================
    // LAYER 2: Generate Q, K, V (identity projection - crystals do the learning)
    // =========================================================================
    cudaMemcpy(d_Q, d_normed, total_elements * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);
    cudaMemcpy(d_K, d_normed, total_elements * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);
    cudaMemcpy(d_V, d_normed, total_elements * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);

    // =========================================================================
    // LAYER 3: Reshape for multi-head attention
    // =========================================================================
    float *d_Q_heads, *d_K_heads, *d_V_heads;
    cudaMalloc(&d_Q_heads, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_K_heads, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_V_heads, total_elements * sizeof(float));

    int reshape_total = batch_size * n_heads * seq_len * head_dim;
    int reshape_blocks = (reshape_total + threads - 1) / threads;

    reshape_for_heads_kernel<<<reshape_blocks, threads>>>(
        d_Q, d_Q_heads, batch_size, seq_len, n_heads, head_dim
    );
    reshape_for_heads_kernel<<<reshape_blocks, threads>>>(
        d_K, d_K_heads, batch_size, seq_len, n_heads, head_dim
    );
    reshape_for_heads_kernel<<<reshape_blocks, threads>>>(
        d_V, d_V_heads, batch_size, seq_len, n_heads, head_dim
    );

    // =========================================================================
    // LAYER 4: Apply RoPE positional encoding
    // =========================================================================
    int rope_total = batch_size * n_heads * seq_len * (head_dim / 2);
    int rope_blocks = (rope_total + threads - 1) / threads;
    apply_rope_kernel<<<rope_blocks, threads>>>(
        d_Q_heads, d_K_heads, batch_size, n_heads, seq_len, head_dim
    );

    // =========================================================================
    // LAYER 5: Generate bidirectional attention mask with K-sector gating
    // =========================================================================
    int mask_total = seq_len * seq_len;
    int mask_blocks = (mask_total + threads - 1) / threads;
    generate_attention_mask_kernel<<<mask_blocks, threads>>>(
        d_attn_mask, seq_len, 2  // K_idx=2 means K=137 (EM sector)
    );

    // =========================================================================
    // LAYER 6: MAL Integer-Locked Attention
    // =========================================================================
    dim3 attn_grid(batch_size, n_heads);
    size_t attn_shared = seq_len * seq_len * sizeof(float);
    mal_attention_kernel<<<attn_grid, threads, attn_shared>>>(
        d_Q_heads, d_K_heads, d_V_heads, d_attn_out, d_lock_acc,
        batch_size, seq_len, head_dim, 137  // K_EM = 137 (electromagnetic sector)
    );

    // =========================================================================
    // LAYER 7: Reshape back from multi-head
    // =========================================================================
    reshape_from_heads_kernel<<<reshape_blocks, threads>>>(
        d_attn_out, d_hidden, batch_size, seq_len, n_heads, head_dim
    );

    // =========================================================================
    // LAYER 8: Residual connection (attention)
    // =========================================================================
    residual_add_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(d_hidden, d_input, total_elements);

    // =========================================================================
    // LAYER 9: Pre-FFN RMSNorm
    // =========================================================================
    rms_norm_kernel<<<norm_blocks, threads, shared_size>>>(
        d_normed, d_hidden, d_gamma, batch_size * seq_len, dim
    );

    // =========================================================================
    // LAYER 10: Crystal Forward Pass (THIS IS THE FFN - structure IS knowledge)
    // =========================================================================
    cudaMemset(net->node_outputs, 0, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float));

    // Map normalized hidden to node activations
    int copy_size = (total_elements < (int)net->num_nodes) ? total_elements : net->num_nodes;
    cudaMemcpy(net->node_activations, d_normed, copy_size * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);

    // Rebuild CSR if structure changed
    if (!net->csr_valid && net->num_locked > 0) {
        rebuild_csr(net);
    }

    // PROTOCOL: Crystal forward with warp reduction (no atomicAdd!)
    if (net->csr_valid && net->csr_nnz > 0) {
        crystal_forward_kernel<<<128, threads>>>(
            net->csr_row_ptrs, net->csr_src_nodes, net->csr_lattice_idx,
            net->csr_directions, net->node_activations, net->node_outputs,
            net->num_nodes, net->scale
        );
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // LAYER 11: GELU activation on crystal output
    // =========================================================================
    int gelu_blocks = (net->num_nodes + threads - 1) / threads;
    gelu_kernel<<<gelu_blocks, threads>>>(net->node_outputs, net->num_nodes);

    // =========================================================================
    // LAYER 12: Residual connection (FFN/crystals)
    // =========================================================================
    // Map crystal output back and add residual
    cudaMemcpy(d_crystal_out, net->node_outputs, copy_size * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);
    residual_add_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(d_crystal_out, d_hidden, copy_size);

    // Final output
    cudaMemcpy(net->node_outputs, d_crystal_out, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);

    // =========================================================================
    // LEARNING: Score crystals and lock/prune based on contribution
    // =========================================================================
    float* d_target;
    cudaMalloc(&d_target, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemcpy(d_target, target, total_elements * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    int crystal_blocks = (net->num_crystals + threads - 1) / threads;

    // PRINCIPIA: Compute radio-tuned scales (Book XIV)
    float* d_scales;
    float* d_weights;
    cudaMalloc(&d_scales, net->num_crystals * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_weights, net->num_crystals * sizeof(float));

    // Extract weight values from crystals (lattice_idx determines weight)
    // For now, use node activations as weight proxy
    cudaMemcpy(d_weights, net->node_activations,
               min(net->num_crystals, net->num_nodes) * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);

    int radio_shared = 64 * sizeof(float);  // For warp reductions
    radio_tuning_kernel<<<crystal_blocks, threads, radio_shared>>>(
        net->crystals, d_weights, d_scales, net->num_crystals
    );

    crystal_score_kernel<<<crystal_blocks, threads>>>(
        net->crystals, net->node_activations, d_target, net->node_outputs,
        d_scales, net->num_crystals, learning_rate
    );

    cudaFree(d_weights);
    cudaFree(d_scales);

    // PRINCIPIA: Decay plasticity by (e-2) = universal clock
    plasticity_decay_kernel<<<crystal_blocks, threads>>>(
        net->crystals, net->num_crystals
    );

    // Lock/Prune decisions (warp reduction for counting)
    // PRINCIPIA: Lock when ρ < τ AND importance HIGH; Prune when ρ < τ AND importance LOW
    uint32_t *d_locked, *d_pruned;
    cudaMalloc(&d_locked, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&d_pruned, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(d_locked, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(d_pruned, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));

    const float plasticity_tau = 0.10f;  // Crystallization threshold (raised for 7 iterations: 0.718^7 = 0.0983)
    crystal_lock_prune_kernel<<<crystal_blocks, threads>>>(
        net->crystals, net->num_crystals,
        plasticity_tau, lock_threshold, 50,  // plasticity_threshold, importance_threshold
        d_locked, d_pruned
    );

    uint32_t h_locked, h_pruned;
    cudaMemcpy(&h_locked, d_locked, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(&h_pruned, d_pruned, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    net->num_locked += h_locked;
    net->num_pruned += h_pruned;

    if (h_locked > 0 || h_pruned > 0) {
        net->csr_valid = false;
    }

    // Copy output back
    cudaMemcpy(output, net->node_outputs, batch_size * seq_len * dim * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    // Cleanup
    cudaFree(d_input);
    cudaFree(d_normed);
    cudaFree(d_attn_out);
    cudaFree(d_hidden);
    cudaFree(d_crystal_out);
    cudaFree(d_Q);
    cudaFree(d_K);
    cudaFree(d_V);
    cudaFree(d_Q_heads);
    cudaFree(d_K_heads);
    cudaFree(d_V_heads);
    cudaFree(d_attn_mask);
    cudaFree(d_lock_acc);
    cudaFree(d_gamma);
    cudaFree(d_target);
    cudaFree(d_locked);
    cudaFree(d_pruned);
}

// =============================================================================
// XVI. OUTPUT DECODER (Crystals → Bytes)
// =============================================================================
// Reverse of input: activations → tokens → bytes
// Completes the symmetric loop: bytes → crystals → bytes

// Reverse embedding: activation pattern → SemanticToken
__global__ void activation_to_tokens_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ activations,
    SemanticToken* __restrict__ tokens,
    const int num_tokens,
    const int embed_dim
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_tokens) return;

    const float* embed = &activations[idx * embed_dim];

    // Find dominant pattern type (highest activation in pattern slots)
    PatternType best_pattern = PATTERN_UNKNOWN;
    float best_pattern_score = -1e10f;
    for (int p = 0; p <= PATTERN_SEMANTIC; p++) {
        float score = embed[p % embed_dim];
        if (score > best_pattern_score) {
            best_pattern_score = score;
            best_pattern = (PatternType)p;
        }
    }

    // Find sector from mid-range activations
    int K_vals[4] = {137, 377, 25, 17};
    uint8_t best_sector = 0;
    float best_sector_score = -1e10f;
    for (int s = 0; s < 4; s++) {
        float score = embed[(s + embed_dim/2) % embed_dim];
        if (score > best_sector_score) {
            best_sector_score = score;
            best_sector = s;
        }
    }

    // Phase from last element
    float phase = embed[embed_dim - 1];
    if (phase < 0.0f) phase = 0.0f;
    if (phase > 1.0f) phase = fmodf(phase, 1.0f);

    // Reconstruct byte value from phase and sector
    int K = K_vals[best_sector];
    uint8_t byte_val = (uint8_t)((int)(phase * K) % 256);

    SemanticToken tok;
    tok.id = idx;
    tok.pattern = best_pattern;
    tok.sector = best_sector;
    tok.phase = phase;
    tok.byte_val = byte_val;
    tok.context = 0;

    tokens[idx] = tok;
}

// Reverse tokenization: SemanticToken → byte
__global__ void tokens_to_bytes_kernel(
    const SemanticToken* __restrict__ tokens,
    uint8_t* __restrict__ bytes,
    const int num_tokens
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_tokens) return;

    SemanticToken tok = tokens[idx];

    // Reconstruct byte from pattern + sector + phase
    int K_vals[4] = {137, 377, 25, 17};
    int K = K_vals[tok.sector % 4];

    // Base byte from phase
    uint8_t byte = (uint8_t)((int)(tok.phase * K) % 256);

    // Adjust based on pattern type
    switch (tok.pattern) {
        case PATTERN_DATA:
            // Keep as is - data bytes
            break;
        case PATTERN_SEQUENCE:
            // Letters: bias toward ASCII letter range
            if (byte < 0x41) byte = 0x41 + (byte % 26);
            else if (byte > 0x7A) byte = 0x61 + (byte % 26);
            break;
        case PATTERN_ARITHMETIC:
            // Operators: +, -, *, /
            byte = 0x2B + (byte % 4);  // +, -, ., /
            if (byte == 0x2E) byte = 0x2A;  // . → *
            break;
        case PATTERN_FUNCTION:
            // Brackets
            byte = (byte % 2 == 0) ? 0x28 : 0x29;  // ( or )
            break;
        case PATTERN_LOOP:
            // Newline/structure
            byte = (byte % 3 == 0) ? 0x0A : (byte % 3 == 1) ? 0x0D : 0x09;
            break;
        case PATTERN_CONDITIONAL:
            // Comparison ops
            byte = (byte % 3 == 0) ? 0x3C : (byte % 3 == 1) ? 0x3D : 0x3E; // <, =, >
            break;
        default:
            // Use stored byte_val directly
            byte = tok.byte_val;
            break;
    }

    bytes[idx] = byte;
}

// FBC Output: SemanticToken → FBC opcode (for portable export)
__global__ void tokens_to_fbc_kernel(
    const SemanticToken* __restrict__ tokens,
    uint8_t* __restrict__ fbc_opcodes,
    uint32_t* __restrict__ fbc_operands,
    const int num_tokens
) {
    const int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_tokens) return;

    SemanticToken tok = tokens[idx];

    // Map pattern type to FBC opcode
    uint8_t opcode;
    uint32_t operand = 0;

    switch (tok.pattern) {
        case PATTERN_DATA:
            opcode = 0x02;  // PUSH_I32
            operand = tok.byte_val;
            break;
        case PATTERN_ARITHMETIC:
            opcode = 0x10 + (tok.byte_val % 5);  // ADD, SUB, MUL, DIV, MOD
            break;
        case PATTERN_LOOP:
            opcode = 0xB0;  // LOOP_N
            operand = tok.context;
            break;
        case PATTERN_ACCUMULATOR:
            opcode = 0xB3;  // PATTERN_SUM
            break;
        case PATTERN_SEQUENCE:
            opcode = 0xB6;  // PATTERN_SEQ
            break;
        case PATTERN_MAP:
            opcode = 0xB7;  // PATTERN_MAP
            break;
        case PATTERN_FUNCTION:
            opcode = 0x50;  // CALL
            operand = tok.byte_val;
            break;
        case PATTERN_CONDITIONAL:
            opcode = 0x41;  // JMP_IF
            break;
        case PATTERN_RECURSION:
            opcode = 0x54;  // CLOSURE
            break;
        case PATTERN_SEMANTIC:
            opcode = 0xA0;  // SEMANTIC
            break;
        default:
            opcode = 0x00;  // NOP
            break;
    }

    fbc_opcodes[idx] = opcode;
    fbc_operands[idx] = operand;
}

// =============================================================================
// XVII. OUTPUT PIPELINE FUNCTIONS
// =============================================================================

struct RawOutput {
    uint8_t* data;
    int num_bytes;
};

struct FBCOutput {
    uint8_t* opcodes;
    uint32_t* operands;
    int num_ops;
};

// Full output pipeline: crystal activations → raw bytes
void crystals_to_bytes(
    CrystalNetwork* net,
    RawOutput* output,
    const int embed_dim
) {
    int threads = 256;
    int num_tokens = output->num_bytes;

    // Allocate device buffers
    SemanticToken* d_tokens;
    uint8_t* d_bytes;

    cudaMalloc(&d_tokens, num_tokens * sizeof(SemanticToken));
    cudaMalloc(&d_bytes, num_tokens * sizeof(uint8_t));

    // 1. Activations → Tokens (reverse embedding)
    int blocks = (num_tokens + threads - 1) / threads;
    activation_to_tokens_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(
        net->node_outputs, d_tokens, num_tokens, embed_dim
    );

    // 2. Tokens → Bytes (reverse tokenization)
    tokens_to_bytes_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(d_tokens, d_bytes, num_tokens);

    // 3. Copy back
    cudaMemcpy(output->data, d_bytes, num_tokens * sizeof(uint8_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    cudaFree(d_tokens);
    cudaFree(d_bytes);
}

// Full output pipeline: crystal activations → FBC bytecode
void crystals_to_fbc(
    CrystalNetwork* net,
    FBCOutput* output,
    const int embed_dim
) {
    int threads = 256;
    int num_tokens = output->num_ops;

    // Allocate device buffers
    SemanticToken* d_tokens;
    uint8_t* d_opcodes;
    uint32_t* d_operands;

    cudaMalloc(&d_tokens, num_tokens * sizeof(SemanticToken));
    cudaMalloc(&d_opcodes, num_tokens * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&d_operands, num_tokens * sizeof(uint32_t));

    // 1. Activations → Tokens
    int blocks = (num_tokens + threads - 1) / threads;
    activation_to_tokens_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(
        net->node_outputs, d_tokens, num_tokens, embed_dim
    );

    // 2. Tokens → FBC
    tokens_to_fbc_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(d_tokens, d_opcodes, d_operands, num_tokens);

    // 3. Copy back
    cudaMemcpy(output->opcodes, d_opcodes, num_tokens * sizeof(uint8_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(output->operands, d_operands, num_tokens * sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    cudaFree(d_tokens);
    cudaFree(d_opcodes);
    cudaFree(d_operands);
}

// =============================================================================
// XVIII. RAW BYTES → CRYSTAL PIPELINE (Input path)
// =============================================================================
// INPUT:  Raw bytes → tokenize → crystals (discover structure)
// OUTPUT: Crystals → bytes or FBC (export discovered structure)

struct RawInput {
    uint8_t* data;          // Raw byte stream
    int num_bytes;          // Length
};

// Raw bytes to crystals (inference only)
void raw_to_crystals(
    RawInput* raw,
    CrystalNetwork* net,
    float* output,
    const int embed_dim
) {
    int threads = 256;

    // 1. Allocate device buffers
    uint8_t* d_data;
    SemanticToken* d_tokens;
    float* d_embeddings;

    cudaMalloc(&d_data, raw->num_bytes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&d_tokens, raw->num_bytes * sizeof(SemanticToken));
    cudaMalloc(&d_embeddings, raw->num_bytes * embed_dim * sizeof(float));

    // 2. Copy raw data to device
    cudaMemcpy(d_data, raw->data, raw->num_bytes * sizeof(uint8_t), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    // 3. Tokenize raw bytes → SemanticTokens (NO FBC!)
    int blocks = (raw->num_bytes + threads - 1) / threads;
    raw_tokenize_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(d_data, d_tokens, raw->num_bytes);

    // 4. Embed tokens (same as FBC path)
    pattern_embed_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(d_tokens, d_embeddings, raw->num_bytes, embed_dim);

    // 5. Map to activations
    int total_activations = raw->num_bytes * embed_dim;
    cudaMemset(net->node_activations, 0, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    if (total_activations <= (int)net->num_nodes) {
        cudaMemcpy(net->node_activations, d_embeddings, total_activations * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);
    } else {
        cudaMemcpy(net->node_activations, d_embeddings, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);
    }

    // 6. PROTOCOL: Crystal forward pass using CSR (warp reduction, no atomicAdd!)
    cudaMemset(net->node_outputs, 0, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    if (!net->csr_valid && net->num_locked > 0) {
        rebuild_csr(net);
    }
    if (net->csr_valid && net->csr_nnz > 0) {
        crystal_forward_kernel<<<128, threads>>>(
            net->csr_row_ptrs, net->csr_src_nodes, net->csr_lattice_idx,
            net->csr_directions, net->node_activations, net->node_outputs,
            net->num_nodes, net->scale
        );
    }

    // 7. Copy output
    cudaMemcpy(output, net->node_outputs, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    // Cleanup
    cudaFree(d_data);
    cudaFree(d_tokens);
    cudaFree(d_embeddings);
}

// Raw bytes step with learning - UNIFIED PIPELINE (full transformer + crystals)
void raw_step(
    RawInput* raw,
    CrystalNetwork* net,
    const float* target,
    float* output,
    const int embed_dim,
    const float learning_rate,
    const float lock_threshold,
    const float prune_threshold
) {
    const int threads = 256;
    const int seq_len = raw->num_bytes;
    const int n_heads = 4;
    const int head_dim = embed_dim / n_heads;
    const int total_elements = seq_len * embed_dim;

    // =========================================================================
    // UNIFIED RAW PIPELINE - Full transformer stack with crystal learning
    // =========================================================================

    // Allocate tokenization buffers
    uint8_t* d_data;
    SemanticToken* d_tokens;
    float* d_embeddings;

    cudaMalloc(&d_data, seq_len * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&d_tokens, seq_len * sizeof(SemanticToken));
    cudaMalloc(&d_embeddings, total_elements * sizeof(float));

    // Copy raw data to device
    cudaMemcpy(d_data, raw->data, seq_len * sizeof(uint8_t), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    // =========================================================================
    // STAGE 0: Raw → Tokens → Embeddings
    // =========================================================================
    int tok_blocks = (seq_len + threads - 1) / threads;
    raw_tokenize_kernel<<<tok_blocks, threads>>>(d_data, d_tokens, seq_len);
    pattern_embed_kernel<<<tok_blocks, threads>>>(d_tokens, d_embeddings, seq_len, embed_dim);

    // =========================================================================
    // UNIFIED FORWARD PASS (same as agi_step)
    // =========================================================================
    float *d_normed, *d_attn_out, *d_hidden, *d_crystal_out;
    float *d_Q, *d_K, *d_V, *d_attn_mask, *d_lock_acc;
    float *d_gamma;

    cudaMalloc(&d_normed, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_attn_out, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_hidden, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_crystal_out, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_Q, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_K, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_V, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_attn_mask, seq_len * seq_len * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_lock_acc, n_heads * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_gamma, embed_dim * sizeof(float));

    // Initialize gamma to 1.0
    float* h_gamma = (float*)malloc(embed_dim * sizeof(float));
    for (int i = 0; i < embed_dim; i++) h_gamma[i] = 1.0f;
    cudaMemcpy(d_gamma, h_gamma, embed_dim * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);
    free(h_gamma);

    int blocks = (total_elements + threads - 1) / threads;
    size_t shared_size = threads * sizeof(float);

    // LAYER 1: Pre-attention RMSNorm
    rms_norm_kernel<<<seq_len, threads, shared_size>>>(
        d_normed, d_embeddings, d_gamma, seq_len, embed_dim
    );

    // LAYER 2: Q, K, V (identity projection)
    cudaMemcpy(d_Q, d_normed, total_elements * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);
    cudaMemcpy(d_K, d_normed, total_elements * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);
    cudaMemcpy(d_V, d_normed, total_elements * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);

    // LAYER 3: Reshape for multi-head
    float *d_Q_heads, *d_K_heads, *d_V_heads;
    cudaMalloc(&d_Q_heads, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_K_heads, total_elements * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_V_heads, total_elements * sizeof(float));

    int reshape_blocks = (total_elements + threads - 1) / threads;
    reshape_for_heads_kernel<<<reshape_blocks, threads>>>(d_Q, d_Q_heads, 1, seq_len, n_heads, head_dim);
    reshape_for_heads_kernel<<<reshape_blocks, threads>>>(d_K, d_K_heads, 1, seq_len, n_heads, head_dim);
    reshape_for_heads_kernel<<<reshape_blocks, threads>>>(d_V, d_V_heads, 1, seq_len, n_heads, head_dim);

    // LAYER 4: RoPE
    int rope_blocks = (n_heads * seq_len * (head_dim / 2) + threads - 1) / threads;
    apply_rope_kernel<<<rope_blocks, threads>>>(d_Q_heads, d_K_heads, 1, n_heads, seq_len, head_dim);

    // LAYER 5: Attention mask
    int mask_blocks = (seq_len * seq_len + threads - 1) / threads;
    generate_attention_mask_kernel<<<mask_blocks, threads>>>(d_attn_mask, seq_len, 2);

    // LAYER 6: MAL Attention
    dim3 attn_grid(1, n_heads);
    size_t attn_shared = seq_len * seq_len * sizeof(float);
    mal_attention_kernel<<<attn_grid, threads, attn_shared>>>(
        d_Q_heads, d_K_heads, d_V_heads, d_attn_out, d_lock_acc,
        1, seq_len, head_dim, 137
    );

    // LAYER 7: Reshape back
    reshape_from_heads_kernel<<<reshape_blocks, threads>>>(d_attn_out, d_hidden, 1, seq_len, n_heads, head_dim);

    // LAYER 8: Residual (attention)
    residual_add_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(d_hidden, d_embeddings, total_elements);

    // LAYER 9: Pre-FFN RMSNorm
    rms_norm_kernel<<<seq_len, threads, shared_size>>>(d_normed, d_hidden, d_gamma, seq_len, embed_dim);

    // LAYER 10: Crystal Forward
    cudaMemset(net->node_outputs, 0, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    int copy_size = (total_elements < (int)net->num_nodes) ? total_elements : net->num_nodes;
    cudaMemcpy(net->node_activations, d_normed, copy_size * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);

    if (!net->csr_valid && net->num_locked > 0) {
        rebuild_csr(net);
    }
    if (net->csr_valid && net->csr_nnz > 0) {
        crystal_forward_kernel<<<128, threads>>>(
            net->csr_row_ptrs, net->csr_src_nodes, net->csr_lattice_idx,
            net->csr_directions, net->node_activations, net->node_outputs,
            net->num_nodes, net->scale
        );
    }

    // LAYER 11: GELU
    int gelu_blocks = (net->num_nodes + threads - 1) / threads;
    gelu_kernel<<<gelu_blocks, threads>>>(net->node_outputs, net->num_nodes);

    // LAYER 12: Residual (FFN)
    cudaMemcpy(d_crystal_out, net->node_outputs, copy_size * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);
    residual_add_kernel<<<blocks, threads>>>(d_crystal_out, d_hidden, copy_size);
    cudaMemcpy(net->node_outputs, d_crystal_out, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);

    // =========================================================================
    // LEARNING: Score and lock/prune crystals
    // =========================================================================
    float* d_target;
    cudaMalloc(&d_target, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemcpy(d_target, target, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    int crystal_blocks = (net->num_crystals + threads - 1) / threads;

    // PRINCIPIA: Compute radio-tuned scales (Book XIV)
    float* d_scales;
    float* d_weights;
    cudaMalloc(&d_scales, net->num_crystals * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&d_weights, net->num_crystals * sizeof(float));

    cudaMemcpy(d_weights, net->node_activations,
               min(net->num_crystals, net->num_nodes) * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice);

    int radio_shared = 64 * sizeof(float);
    radio_tuning_kernel<<<crystal_blocks, threads, radio_shared>>>(
        net->crystals, d_weights, d_scales, net->num_crystals
    );

    crystal_score_kernel<<<crystal_blocks, threads>>>(
        net->crystals, net->node_activations, d_target, net->node_outputs,
        d_scales, net->num_crystals, learning_rate
    );

    cudaFree(d_weights);
    cudaFree(d_scales);

    // PRINCIPIA: Decay plasticity by (e-2) = universal clock
    plasticity_decay_kernel<<<crystal_blocks, threads>>>(
        net->crystals, net->num_crystals
    );

    // PRINCIPIA: Lock when ρ < τ AND importance HIGH; Prune when ρ < τ AND importance LOW
    uint32_t *d_locked, *d_pruned;
    cudaMalloc(&d_locked, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&d_pruned, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(d_locked, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(d_pruned, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));

    const float plasticity_tau = 0.10f;  // Crystallization threshold (raised for 7 iterations: 0.718^7 = 0.0983)
    crystal_lock_prune_kernel<<<crystal_blocks, threads>>>(
        net->crystals, net->num_crystals,
        plasticity_tau, lock_threshold, 50,  // plasticity_threshold, importance_threshold
        d_locked, d_pruned
    );

    uint32_t h_locked, h_pruned;
    cudaMemcpy(&h_locked, d_locked, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(&h_pruned, d_pruned, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    net->num_locked += h_locked;
    net->num_pruned += h_pruned;

    if (h_locked > 0 || h_pruned > 0) {
        net->csr_valid = false;
    }

    // Output
    cudaMemcpy(output, net->node_outputs, net->num_nodes * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    // Cleanup
    cudaFree(d_data);
    cudaFree(d_tokens);
    cudaFree(d_embeddings);
    cudaFree(d_normed);
    cudaFree(d_attn_out);
    cudaFree(d_hidden);
    cudaFree(d_crystal_out);
    cudaFree(d_Q);
    cudaFree(d_K);
    cudaFree(d_V);
    cudaFree(d_Q_heads);
    cudaFree(d_K_heads);
    cudaFree(d_V_heads);
    cudaFree(d_attn_mask);
    cudaFree(d_lock_acc);
    cudaFree(d_gamma);
    cudaFree(d_target);
    cudaFree(d_locked);
    cudaFree(d_pruned);
}

// =============================================================================
// XVII.5 OPTIMIZED PRIMITIVES - Merged from specialized kernels
// =============================================================================
// ONETWO: These are the useful optimizations extracted from other files
// DISTINGUISH: What's unique and worth keeping
// RELATE: How they connect to crystallization
// EMBED: Consolidated into single source of truth
//
// NOTE: complex_mul, complex_add, complex_sub, warp_fft_32, warp_ifft_32
//       are defined earlier in Section VI.1 (see ~line 1211)

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// FLOAT4 VECTORIZED LATTICE SNAP (4x throughput)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// PRINCIPIA: Vectorized DISTINGUISH operation

// Dual-lattice snap: Path A (signal) or Path B (geometric)
__device__ __forceinline__ void snap_to_lattice_float4_dual(
    float4 input,
    float4* output,
    uint8_t* indices,
    const float scale,
    const float threshold,
    int* lock_count,
    int use_path_b  // 0 = Path A (signal), 1 = Path B (geometric)
) {
    float weights[4] = {input.x, input.y, input.z, input.w};
    float results[4];
    int locks = 0;
    const float max_dist = threshold * 377.0f;
    
        const float* lattice = use_path_b ? Grids::LATTICE_PATH_B : Grids::LATTICE_PATH_A;
    const int lattice_size = use_path_b ? 15 : 13;

    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
        float w = weights[i];
        float sign = (w >= 0.0f) ? 1.0f : -1.0f;
        float normalized = fabsf(w) / scale;

        float min_dist = 1e10f;
        int best_idx = 255;

        // Search dual lattice (Path A or Path B)
        for (int j = 0; j < lattice_size; j++) {
            float dist = fabsf(normalized - lattice[j]);
            if (dist < min_dist) {
                min_dist = dist;
                best_idx = j;
            }
        }

        if (min_dist < max_dist) {
            results[i] = lattice[best_idx] * sign * scale;
            indices[i] = (uint8_t)best_idx;
            locks++;
        } else {
            results[i] = 0.0f;
            indices[i] = 255;  // Invalid
        }
    }

    *output = make_float4(results[0], results[1], results[2], results[3]);
    *lock_count = locks;
}

// Original 12-point version (backward compatibility)
__device__ __forceinline__ void snap_to_lattice_float4(
    float4 input,
    float4* output,
    uint8_t* indices,
    const float scale,
    const float threshold,
    int* lock_count
) {
    snap_to_lattice_float4_dual(input, output, indices, scale, threshold, lock_count, 0);
}

// Vectorized crystallization kernel (4 weights per thread)
__global__ void crystallize_vectorized_kernel(
    const float4* __restrict__ input,
    float4* __restrict__ output,
    uint32_t* __restrict__ indices_packed,
    int* __restrict__ block_locks,
    const int num_float4,
    const float scale,
    const float threshold
) {
    const int tid = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    const int lane_id = threadIdx.x & 31;
    const int warp_id = threadIdx.x >> 5;

    __shared__ int warp_locks[32];
    int thread_locks = 0;

    if (tid < num_float4) {
        float4 in = __ldg(&input[tid]);
        float4 out;
        uint8_t idx[4];

        snap_to_lattice_float4(in, &out, idx, scale, threshold, &thread_locks);

        output[tid] = out;
        indices_packed[tid] = idx[0] | (idx[1] << 8) | (idx[2] << 16) | (idx[3] << 24);
    }

    // Warp reduction for lock count
    int warp_total = thread_locks;
    #pragma unroll
    for (int offset = 16; offset > 0; offset >>= 1) {
        warp_total += __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, warp_total, offset);
    }
    if (lane_id == 0) warp_locks[warp_id] = warp_total;
    __syncthreads();

    // Block reduction
    if (warp_id == 0) {
        int val = (lane_id < (blockDim.x >> 5)) ? warp_locks[lane_id] : 0;
        #pragma unroll
        for (int offset = 16; offset > 0; offset >>= 1) {
            val += __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, val, offset);
        }
        if (lane_id == 0) block_locks[blockIdx.x] = val;
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// PARALLEL RMS NORM (Block-level, not single-threaded)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// PRINCIPIA: Normalization = finding shared substrate (L2)

__global__ void rms_norm_parallel_kernel(
    float* __restrict__ out,
    const float* __restrict__ x,
    const float* __restrict__ gamma,
    const int rows,
    const int cols
) {
    extern __shared__ float shared[];

    const int row = blockIdx.x;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    const int block_size = blockDim.x;

    if (row >= rows) return;

    const float* x_row = x + row * cols;
    float* out_row = out + row * cols;

    // Step 1: Compute sum of squares (parallel)
    float sum_sq = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += block_size) {
        float v = x_row[i];
        sum_sq += v * v;
    }

    // Reduce within block
    shared[tid] = sum_sq;
    __syncthreads();
    for (int s = block_size / 2; s > 32; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) shared[tid] += shared[tid + s];
        __syncthreads();
    }
    if (tid < 32) {
        volatile float* vs = shared;
        if (block_size >= 64) vs[tid] += vs[tid + 32];
        vs[tid] += vs[tid + 16];
        vs[tid] += vs[tid + 8];
        vs[tid] += vs[tid + 4];
        vs[tid] += vs[tid + 2];
        vs[tid] += vs[tid + 1];
    }

    // Compute RMS
    __shared__ float rms_inv;
    if (tid == 0) {
        float rms = sqrtf(shared[0] / cols + 1e-6f);
        rms_inv = 1.0f / rms;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Step 2: Normalize and scale (parallel)
    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += block_size) {
        out_row[i] = gamma[i] * x_row[i] * rms_inv;
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// PARALLEL LAYER NORM
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

__global__ void layer_norm_parallel_kernel(
    float* __restrict__ x,
    const float* __restrict__ gamma,
    const float* __restrict__ beta,
    const int rows,
    const int cols
) {
    extern __shared__ float shared[];
    float* shared_sum = shared;
    float* shared_sq = shared + blockDim.x;

    const int row = blockIdx.x;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    const int block_size = blockDim.x;

    if (row >= rows) return;

    float* x_row = x + row * cols;

    // Step 1: Compute mean (parallel)
    float local_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += block_size) {
        local_sum += x_row[i];
    }
    shared_sum[tid] = local_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    for (int s = block_size / 2; s > 0; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + s];
        __syncthreads();
    }

    __shared__ float mean;
    if (tid == 0) {
        mean = shared_sum[0] / cols;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Step 2: Compute variance (parallel)
    float local_sq = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += block_size) {
        float diff = x_row[i] - mean;
        local_sq += diff * diff;
    }
    shared_sq[tid] = local_sq;
    __syncthreads();
    for (int s = block_size / 2; s > 0; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) shared_sq[tid] += shared_sq[tid + s];
        __syncthreads();
    }

    __shared__ float std_inv;
    if (tid == 0) {
        std_inv = rsqrtf(shared_sq[0] / cols + 1e-5f);
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Step 3: Normalize (parallel)
    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += block_size) {
        x_row[i] = gamma[i] * (x_row[i] - mean) * std_inv + beta[i];
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// PARALLEL SOFTMAX
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

__global__ void softmax_parallel_kernel(
    float* __restrict__ x,
    const int rows,
    const int cols
) {
    extern __shared__ float shared[];

    const int row = blockIdx.x;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    const int block_size = blockDim.x;

    if (row >= rows) return;

    float* x_row = x + row * cols;

    // Step 1: Find max (parallel)
    float local_max = -FLT_MAX;
    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += block_size) {
        local_max = fmaxf(local_max, x_row[i]);
    }
    shared[tid] = local_max;
    __syncthreads();
    for (int s = block_size / 2; s > 0; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) shared[tid] = fmaxf(shared[tid], shared[tid + s]);
        __syncthreads();
    }

    __shared__ float row_max;
    if (tid == 0) row_max = shared[0];
    __syncthreads();

    // Step 2: Compute exp and sum (parallel)
    float local_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += block_size) {
        float exp_val = expf(x_row[i] - row_max);
        x_row[i] = exp_val;
        local_sum += exp_val;
    }
    shared[tid] = local_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    for (int s = block_size / 2; s > 0; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) shared[tid] += shared[tid + s];
        __syncthreads();
    }

    __shared__ float row_sum;
    if (tid == 0) row_sum = shared[0];
    __syncthreads();

    // Step 3: Normalize (parallel)
    for (int i = tid; i < cols; i += block_size) {
        x_row[i] /= row_sum;
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// RESONANT TOP-K ROUTER (K-137/K-377 pruning)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// PRINCIPIA: Expert routing using sector thresholds
// K-137 Rule: Prune expert if confidence < 13.7% (EM sector boundary)
// K-377 Rule: Hard cutoff at 3.77% (Vacuum impedance)

#define RESONANT_TOP_K 4
#define K_137_THRESHOLD 0.137f
#define K_377_THRESHOLD 0.0377f

__device__ void resonant_topk_route(
    const float* router_logits,
    int* expert_indices,
    float* expert_weights,
    int* num_active,
    int num_experts
) {
    // Single-thread selection (fast for ~128 experts)
    if (threadIdx.x != 0) return;

    float top_vals[RESONANT_TOP_K];
    int top_idx[RESONANT_TOP_K];

    for (int k = 0; k < RESONANT_TOP_K; k++) {
        top_vals[k] = -1e38f;
        top_idx[k] = 0;
    }

    // Find top-K
    for (int i = 0; i < num_experts; i++) {
        float val = router_logits[i];

        for (int k = 0; k < RESONANT_TOP_K; k++) {
            if (val > top_vals[k]) {
                for (int j = RESONANT_TOP_K - 1; j > k; j--) {
                    top_vals[j] = top_vals[j-1];
                    top_idx[j] = top_idx[j-1];
                }
                top_vals[k] = val;
                top_idx[k] = i;
                break;
            }
        }
    }

    // Softmax over top-k
    float max_val = top_vals[0];
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int k = 0; k < RESONANT_TOP_K; k++) {
        top_vals[k] = expf(top_vals[k] - max_val);
        sum += top_vals[k];
    }
    for (int k = 0; k < RESONANT_TOP_K; k++) {
        top_vals[k] /= sum;
    }

    // PRINCIPIA: Resonance pruning using K-sector thresholds
    int active = 0;
    for (int k = 0; k < RESONANT_TOP_K; k++) {
        float confidence = top_vals[k];

        // K-137: Prune if below 13.7% confidence (except top 2)
        if (confidence < K_137_THRESHOLD && k >= 2) continue;

        // K-377: Hard cutoff for 4th expert at 3.77%
        if (k == 3 && confidence < K_377_THRESHOLD) continue;

        expert_indices[active] = top_idx[k];
        expert_weights[active] = confidence;
        active++;
    }

    *num_active = active;

    // Renormalize weights after pruning
    if (active > 0 && active < RESONANT_TOP_K) {
        float new_sum = 0.0f;
        for (int k = 0; k < active; k++) {
            new_sum += expert_weights[k];
        }
        for (int k = 0; k < active; k++) {
            expert_weights[k] /= new_sum;
        }
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// FREQUENCY ANALYSIS KERNEL (Using warp FFT)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// PRINCIPIA: Find crystallization candidates via frequency decomposition

__global__ void frequency_crystallize_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ input,
    float* __restrict__ freq_magnitudes,
    uint8_t* __restrict__ freq_sectors,
    const int num_windows,
    const int window_size  // Must be 32 for warp FFT
) {
    const int window_id = blockIdx.x;
    const int lane_id = threadIdx.x & 31;

    if (window_id >= num_windows) return;

    // Load window into complex (real part only)
    float2 val;
    val.x = (lane_id < window_size) ? input[window_id * window_size + lane_id] : 0.0f;
    val.y = 0.0f;

    // Warp FFT
    warp_fft_32(&val, lane_id);

    // Compute magnitude
    float mag = sqrtf(val.x * val.x + val.y * val.y);

    // Store magnitude
    freq_magnitudes[window_id * window_size + lane_id] = mag;

    // Classify into K-sector based on frequency bin
    // Low freq (0-7) = Strong (K=17)
    // Mid freq (8-15) = Weak (K=25)
    // High freq (16-23) = EM (K=137)
    // Highest freq (24-31) = Vacuum (K=377)
    // Sector indices: 0=K377, 1=K25, 2=K137, 3=K17
    uint8_t sector;
    if (lane_id < 8) sector = 3;       // K=17 Strong
    else if (lane_id < 16) sector = 1; // K=25 Weak
    else if (lane_id < 24) sector = 2; // K=137 EM
    else sector = 0;                    // K=377 Vacuum

    freq_sectors[window_id * window_size + lane_id] = sector;
}

// =============================================================================
// IRON PYRAMID: KERNEL EXTENSIONS
// =============================================================================
// This section implements the "Electronic Cascade" and FBC Hyper-Sieve.
// These kernels are available when included as a library (OB1_AS_LIBRARY).

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 1. VOCABULARY PROBE (The Tongue)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Tests all 256 possible next tokens against the lattice state.
// Output: 256 float energy values (lower is better).
__global__ void ob1_probe_vocab(
    IronState* state,
    const uint32_t* d_feedback,
    float* d_output_energies
) {
    uint32_t candidate_token = blockIdx.x; // 0-255
    uint32_t feedback = (d_feedback != nullptr) ? d_feedback[0] : 0;

    // Perception Twist (Same as update logic)
    uint32_t shift = feedback & 0x1F;
    if (shift == 0) shift = 1;
    uint32_t modulated = (candidate_token << shift) | (candidate_token >> (32 - shift));
    modulated ^= feedback;

    // Calculate Friction across all nodes
    __shared__ float s_friction[256];
    float local_friction = 0.0f;

    for (uint32_t i = threadIdx.x; i < state->num_nodes; i += blockDim.x) {
        uint32_t node_xor = state->xor_accumulators[i];
        uint32_t interaction = node_xor ^ modulated;
        local_friction += (float)__popc(interaction);
    }

    // Reduce within block
    s_friction[threadIdx.x] = local_friction;
    __syncthreads();
    for (int s = blockDim.x / 2; s > 0; s >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < (uint32_t)s) s_friction[threadIdx.x] += s_friction[threadIdx.x + s];
        __syncthreads();
    }

    if (threadIdx.x == 0) d_output_energies[candidate_token] = s_friction[0];
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 2. E8 -> E6 PROJECTION (Sync Intuition)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Downloads a slice of the E8 Crystal into the E6 Filter state.
__global__ void ob1_project_e8_to_e6(
    IronState* state,
    uint32_t* d_e6_state
) {
    if (threadIdx.x >= 6) return;
    // Map E6 Prime Indices to sample the crystal
    const int indices[6] = {5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19};
    d_e6_state[threadIdx.x] = state->xor_accumulators[indices[threadIdx.x]];
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 3. E6 STANDARD FILTER (The Sieve)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Standard 32-bit token filter.
__global__ void ob1_e6_filter(
    const uint32_t* tokens,
    uint32_t num_tokens,
    const uint32_t* d_e6_state,
    uint8_t* d_keep_mask,
    float threshold
) {
    uint32_t idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_tokens) return;

    uint32_t token = tokens[idx];
    float friction = 0.0f;
    constexpr int primes[6] = {5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19};

    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 6; i++) {
        uint32_t s = d_e6_state[i];
        uint32_t rot = (s << primes[i]) | (s >> (32 - primes[i]));
        friction += (float)__popc(rot ^ token);
    }
    d_keep_mask[idx] = (friction < threshold) ? 1 : 0;
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 4. FBC HYPER-SIEVE (The 300 GB/s Gatekeeper)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Unpacks 6 tokens per word, expands geometry, filters, writes packed mask.
__global__ void ob1_e6_filter_fbc(
    const uint32_t* packed_tokens,
    uint32_t num_packed_words,
    const uint32_t* d_e6_state,
    uint8_t* d_keep_mask,
    float threshold
) {
    uint32_t idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_packed_words) return;

    uint32_t raw_word = packed_tokens[idx];
    uint8_t batch_mask = 0;

    constexpr int primes[6] = {5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19};
    // Holographic Expansion Factor (Spreads 5 bits to 20 bits)
    constexpr uint32_t EXPANSION_FACTOR = 0x08421;

    #pragma unroll
    for (int t = 0; t < 6; t++) {
        uint32_t token_5b = (raw_word >> (t * 5)) & 0x1F;
        uint32_t expanded = token_5b * EXPANSION_FACTOR;

        float friction = 0.0f;
        #pragma unroll
        for (int i = 0; i < 6; i++) {
            uint32_t s = d_e6_state[i];
            uint32_t rot = (s << primes[i]) | (s >> (32 - primes[i]));
            friction += (float)__popc(rot ^ expanded);
        }

        if (friction < threshold) batch_mask |= (1 << t);
    }
    d_keep_mask[idx] = batch_mask;
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 5. FBC SURVIVOR UNPACK (The Bridge)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Extracts valid tokens for the E8 Crystal.
__global__ void ob1_fbc_unpack_survivors(
    const uint32_t* packed_data,
    const uint8_t* packed_mask,
    uint32_t num_words,
    uint32_t* output_buffer,
    uint32_t* output_counter,
    uint32_t max_output
) {
    uint32_t idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= num_words) return;

    uint8_t mask = packed_mask[idx];
    if (mask == 0) return;

    uint32_t raw_word = packed_data[idx];
    constexpr uint32_t EXPANSION_FACTOR = 0x08421;

    #pragma unroll
    for (int t = 0; t < 6; t++) {
        if (mask & (1 << t)) {
            uint32_t token_5b = (raw_word >> (t * 5)) & 0x1F;
            uint32_t expanded = token_5b * EXPANSION_FACTOR;

            uint32_t out_idx = atomicAdd(output_counter, 1);
            if (out_idx < max_output) {
                output_buffer[out_idx] = expanded;
            }
        }
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 6. LATTICE REDUCE (Feedback Generation)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Reduces lattice state to a single feedback word for next batch.
__global__ void ob1_lattice_reduce(
    IronState* state,
    uint32_t* d_feedback
) {
    __shared__ uint32_t s_xor[256];

    uint32_t tid = threadIdx.x;
    uint32_t node = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;

    // Load node XOR (or 0 if out of bounds)
    s_xor[tid] = (node < state->num_nodes) ? state->xor_accumulators[node] : 0;
    __syncthreads();

    // Block reduction
    for (int s = blockDim.x / 2; s > 0; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < (uint32_t)s) {
            s_xor[tid] ^= s_xor[tid + s];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }

    // Atomic update to global feedback
    if (tid == 0) {
        atomicXor(d_feedback, s_xor[0]);
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// XVII.5 THE OBSERVER - Crystallization Check (Memory Formation)
// =============================================================================
// Called after ob1_update_lattice to check if stable patterns should lock.
// This is the "solid memory" that forms from "fluid physics".
//
// CLOSURE TEST (not distance): A token resonates with a node if:
//   snap_to_lattice(state ^ token) == snap_to_lattice(state)
// i.e., the transform is idempotent - it doesn't move the state to a new
// lattice point. This is structural closure, not arbitrary modulo arithmetic.

// Snap to lattice index (0-11)
__device__ __forceinline__ uint8_t snap_to_lattice_idx(uint32_t val) {
    float scaled = (float)(val & 0x1FF);  // 0-511 range
    uint8_t best_idx = 0;
    float min_dist = 512.0f;

    #pragma unroll
    for (int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
        float dist = fabsf(scaled - Grids::LATTICE_12[i]);
        if (dist < min_dist) {
            min_dist = dist;
            best_idx = (uint8_t)i;
        }
    }
    return best_idx;
}

__global__ void ob1_check_crystallization(
    uint32_t* tokens,
    uint32_t count,
    IronState* state
) {
    int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (i >= (int)count) return;

    uint32_t token = tokens[i];
    uint32_t idx = (token * 137) % state->num_nodes;
    uint32_t xor_val = state->xor_accumulators[idx];

    // 1. Unpack History (Nibble Packing)
    // [High 4 bits: Grandparent] [Low 4 bits: Previous]
    uint8_t history_packed = state->previous_lattice_idx[idx];
    uint8_t prev_lattice = history_packed & 0x0F;
    uint8_t grand_lattice = (history_packed >> 4) & 0x0F;

    uint8_t current_lattice = snap_to_lattice_idx(xor_val);

    // 2. RESONANCE (Spatial Accuracy)
    float scaled = (float)(xor_val & 0x1FF);
    float lattice_point = Grids::LATTICE_12[current_lattice];
    float dist = fabsf(scaled - lattice_point);

    // VOID RESONANCE: 0 is Identity (A XOR A = 0)
    // The ultimate attractor. D(empty) = 1.
    bool is_resonant = (dist < 5.0f) || (scaled < 1.0f);

    // 3. CLOSURE (Temporal Stability OR Oscillation)
    // Signal oscillates (A->B->A). Noise walks (A->B->C).
    // Check 1: Stable (Period 1)
    bool is_stable = (prev_lattice != 0xF && current_lattice == prev_lattice);
    // Check 2: Oscillating (Period 2) - "The Ping Pong Check"
    bool is_oscillating = (grand_lattice != 0xF && current_lattice == grand_lattice);

    bool is_closed = is_stable || is_oscillating;

    // 4. Update Logic
    if (is_closed && is_resonant) {
        uint32_t slot = atomicAdd(&state->connection_counts[idx], 1);
        if (slot + 1 >= 6) state->node_locked[idx] = 1;
    } else if (prev_lattice != 0xF && !is_closed) {
        // Decay
        uint32_t c = state->connection_counts[idx];
        if (c > 0) state->connection_counts[idx] = c >> 1;
    }

    // 5. Pack History: New Grandparent is Old Previous. New Previous is Current.
    uint8_t new_prev = current_lattice & 0x0F;
    uint8_t new_grand = prev_lattice & 0x0F;
    state->previous_lattice_idx[idx] = (new_grand << 4) | new_prev;
}

// =============================================================================
// XVIII. MAIN - Test AGI Core with FBC and RAW paths
// =============================================================================

#ifndef OB1_AS_LIBRARY
int main() {
    printf("================================================================\n");
    printf("0B-1 AGI CORE - Unified CUDA Implementation\n");
    printf("================================================================\n\n");

    printf("STACK:\n");
    printf("  [1] FBC Pattern Semantics\n");
    printf("  [2] MAL Integer-Locked Attention (K=137,377,25,17)\n");
    printf("  [3] Crystal Structure Discovery (Lock/Prune)\n");
    printf("  [4] Optimized CUDA Kernels (28B+ weights/sec)\n\n");

    // Test parameters
    const int NUM_NODES = 1024;
    const int NUM_CRYSTALS = NUM_NODES * 32;  // ~32 connections per node
    const int DIM = 64;
    const float SCALE = 0.001f;

    printf("Initializing crystal network...\n");
    printf("  Nodes: %d\n", NUM_NODES);
    printf("  Crystals: %d\n", NUM_CRYSTALS);

    // Allocate crystal network
    CrystalNetwork net;
    net.num_nodes = NUM_NODES;
    net.num_crystals = NUM_CRYSTALS;
    net.scale = SCALE;
    net.num_locked = 0;
    net.num_pruned = 0;
    net.num_candidates = NUM_CRYSTALS;

    cudaMalloc(&net.crystals, NUM_CRYSTALS * sizeof(Crystal));
    cudaMalloc(&net.node_activations, NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&net.node_outputs, NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));

    // PROTOCOL: Initialize CSR for warp-reduced forward pass
    init_csr(&net);

    // Initialize crystals as candidates
    Crystal* h_crystals = (Crystal*)malloc(NUM_CRYSTALS * sizeof(Crystal));
    unsigned int seed = 137;
    for (int i = 0; i < NUM_CRYSTALS; i++) {
        seed = seed * 1103515245 + 12345;
        h_crystals[i].src = seed % NUM_NODES;
        seed = seed * 1103515245 + 12345;
        h_crystals[i].dst = seed % NUM_NODES;
        h_crystals[i].lattice_idx = 0;
        h_crystals[i].direction = 1;
        h_crystals[i].score = 0.0f;
        h_crystals[i].plasticity = 1.0f;  // PRINCIPIA: Start with full plasticity
        h_crystals[i].fires = 0;
        h_crystals[i].locked = 0;  // Candidate
    }
    cudaMemcpy(net.crystals, h_crystals, NUM_CRYSTALS * sizeof(Crystal), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    // Training data - simple pattern: shift by 1
    float* h_input = (float*)malloc(NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));
    float* h_target = (float*)malloc(NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));
    float* h_output = (float*)malloc(NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));

    for (int i = 0; i < NUM_NODES; i++) {
        h_input[i] = (float)(i % 10) / 10.0f;
        h_target[i] = (float)((i + 1) % 10) / 10.0f;
    }

    printf("\nTraining (structure discovery)...\n");

    cudaEvent_t start, stop;
    cudaEventCreate(&start);
    cudaEventCreate(&stop);

    cudaEventRecord(start);

    const int STEPS = 1000;
    for (int step = 0; step < STEPS; step++) {
        agi_step(&net, h_input, h_target, h_output, 1, 1, NUM_NODES,
                 0.01f, 0.5f, -0.1f);

        if ((step + 1) % 200 == 0) {
            // Compute MSE
            float mse = 0.0f;
            for (int i = 0; i < NUM_NODES; i++) {
                float diff = h_output[i] - h_target[i];
                mse += diff * diff;
            }
            mse /= NUM_NODES;

            printf("  Step %d: MSE=%.6f, Locked=%u, Pruned=%u\n",
                   step + 1, mse, net.num_locked, net.num_pruned);
        }
    }

    cudaEventRecord(stop);
    cudaEventSynchronize(stop);

    float ms;
    cudaEventElapsedTime(&ms, start, stop);

    printf("\nResults:\n");
    printf("  Total time: %.2f ms\n", ms);
    printf("  Steps/sec: %.0f\n", STEPS / (ms / 1000.0f));
    printf("  Locked crystals: %u (%.1f%%)\n", net.num_locked, 100.0f * net.num_locked / NUM_CRYSTALS);
    printf("  Pruned crystals: %u (%.1f%%)\n", net.num_pruned, 100.0f * net.num_pruned / NUM_CRYSTALS);
    printf("  Active candidates: %u\n", NUM_CRYSTALS - net.num_locked - net.num_pruned);

    // Test saving crystals
    printf("\nSaving crystals to ob1_crystals.bin...\n");
    save_crystals("ob1_crystals.bin", &net);

    // Verify load
    printf("Loading crystals...\n");
    CrystalNetwork* loaded = load_crystals("ob1_crystals.bin");
    if (loaded) {
        printf("  Loaded %u locked crystals\n", loaded->num_locked);
        cudaFree(loaded->crystals);
        cudaFree(loaded->node_activations);
        cudaFree(loaded->node_outputs);
        free(loaded);
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // RAW BYTES TEST - The only input path
    // =========================================================================
    printf("\n================================================================\n");
    printf("RAW BYTES TEST - Direct Input (No FBC)\n");
    printf("================================================================\n\n");

    // Test with raw text: "Hello, World!"
    uint8_t raw_text[] = "Hello, World! This is raw text input - no FBC compilation needed.";
    int raw_len = sizeof(raw_text) - 1;  // Exclude null terminator

    printf("Test input: \"%s\"\n", raw_text);
    printf("  Raw bytes: %d\n\n", raw_len);

    // Create raw input
    RawInput raw_test;
    raw_test.data = raw_text;
    raw_test.num_bytes = raw_len;

    // Reset crystal network for raw test
    net.num_locked = 0;
    net.num_pruned = 0;
    cudaMemcpy(net.crystals, h_crystals, NUM_CRYSTALS * sizeof(Crystal), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    // Create target (simple pattern based on character frequency)
    float* raw_target = (float*)malloc(NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));
    float* raw_output = (float*)malloc(NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));
    for (int i = 0; i < NUM_NODES; i++) {
        raw_target[i] = (float)(raw_text[i % raw_len]) / 255.0f;
    }

    printf("Training on raw text...\n");

    cudaEventRecord(start);

    const int RAW_STEPS = 500;
    for (int step = 0; step < RAW_STEPS; step++) {
        raw_step(&raw_test, &net, raw_target, raw_output, DIM,
                 0.01f, 0.5f, -0.1f);

        if ((step + 1) % 100 == 0) {
            float mse = 0.0f;
            for (int i = 0; i < NUM_NODES; i++) {
                float diff = raw_output[i] - raw_target[i];
                mse += diff * diff;
            }
            mse /= NUM_NODES;
            printf("  Step %d: MSE=%.6f, Locked=%u\n", step + 1, mse, net.num_locked);
        }
    }

    cudaEventRecord(stop);
    cudaEventSynchronize(stop);
    cudaEventElapsedTime(&ms, start, stop);

    printf("\nRAW Pipeline Results:\n");
    printf("  Total time: %.2f ms\n", ms);
    printf("  Steps/sec: %.0f\n", RAW_STEPS / (ms / 1000.0f));
    printf("  Crystals locked for RAW: %u\n", net.num_locked);

    free(raw_target);
    free(raw_output);

    // =========================================================================
    // OUTPUT DECODER TEST - Round trip: input → crystals → output
    // =========================================================================
    printf("\n================================================================\n");
    printf("OUTPUT DECODER TEST - Round Trip\n");
    printf("================================================================\n\n");

    // First, do a forward pass with the raw input
    printf("Forward pass: raw bytes → crystals\n");
    raw_to_crystals(&raw_test, &net, h_output, DIM);

    // Now decode: crystals → bytes
    printf("Reverse pass: crystals → bytes\n");

    RawOutput decoded_output;
    decoded_output.num_bytes = raw_len;
    decoded_output.data = (uint8_t*)malloc(raw_len + 1);

    crystals_to_bytes(&net, &decoded_output, DIM);
    decoded_output.data[raw_len] = '\0';  // Null terminate for printing

    printf("\nOriginal: \"%s\"\n", raw_text);
    printf("Decoded:  \"%s\"\n", decoded_output.data);

    // Compare byte-by-byte
    int matches = 0;
    int pattern_matches = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < raw_len; i++) {
        if (decoded_output.data[i] == raw_text[i]) matches++;
        // Check if same "class" (letter, digit, punct, space)
        bool orig_letter = (raw_text[i] >= 'A' && raw_text[i] <= 'Z') ||
                          (raw_text[i] >= 'a' && raw_text[i] <= 'z');
        bool dec_letter = (decoded_output.data[i] >= 'A' && decoded_output.data[i] <= 'Z') ||
                         (decoded_output.data[i] >= 'a' && decoded_output.data[i] <= 'z');
        if (orig_letter == dec_letter) pattern_matches++;
    }

    printf("\nReconstruction accuracy:\n");
    printf("  Exact byte match: %d/%d (%.1f%%)\n", matches, raw_len, 100.0f * matches / raw_len);
    printf("  Pattern match: %d/%d (%.1f%%)\n", pattern_matches, raw_len, 100.0f * pattern_matches / raw_len);

    // FBC output test
    printf("\nFBC Export test:\n");
    FBCOutput fbc_out;
    fbc_out.num_ops = raw_len;
    fbc_out.opcodes = (uint8_t*)malloc(raw_len);
    fbc_out.operands = (uint32_t*)malloc(raw_len * sizeof(uint32_t));

    crystals_to_fbc(&net, &fbc_out, DIM);

    // Count opcode distribution
    int opcode_counts[256] = {0};
