    for (int i = 0; i < raw_len; i++) {
        opcode_counts[fbc_out.opcodes[i]]++;
    }

    printf("  FBC opcode distribution:\n");
    const char* opcode_names[] = {
        "NOP", "PUSH", "ARITH", "LOOP", "CALL", "SEQ", "SUM", "SEMANTIC"
    };
    int opcode_ranges[] = {0x00, 0x02, 0x10, 0xB0, 0x50, 0xB6, 0xB3, 0xA0};
    for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
        int count = 0;
        for (int j = 0; j < raw_len; j++) {
            if (fbc_out.opcodes[j] == opcode_ranges[i] ||
                (i == 2 && fbc_out.opcodes[j] >= 0x10 && fbc_out.opcodes[j] <= 0x14)) {
                count++;
            }
        }
        if (count > 0) {
            printf("    %s: %d\n", opcode_names[i], count);
        }
    }

    free(decoded_output.data);
    free(fbc_out.opcodes);
    free(fbc_out.operands);

    // =========================================================================
    // IRON MODE TEST - GPU as Master, Zero-Copy, Persistent Discovery
    // =========================================================================
    printf("\n================================================================\n");
    printf("IRON MODE TEST - GPU as Master\n");
    printf("================================================================\n\n");

    printf("Paradigm Shift:\n");
    printf("  Flesh: CPU launches kernels, waits, cudaMemcpy\n");
    printf("  Iron:  GPU runs forever, CPU feeds bitstream\n\n");

    // Initialize Iron state (GPU-owned, zero-copy buffers)
    printf("Initializing Iron state...\n");
    const uint32_t IRON_NODES = 1024;
    const uint32_t IRON_MAX_CONN = 16;
    const uint32_t IRON_BUFFER = 65536;

    // Create host-side state tracker
    IronState h_iron;
    h_iron.num_nodes = IRON_NODES;
    h_iron.max_connections = IRON_MAX_CONN;
    h_iron.bitstream_size = IRON_BUFFER;

    // GPU-side allocations
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.lattice_connections, IRON_NODES * IRON_MAX_CONN * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.connection_counts, IRON_NODES * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.node_activations, IRON_NODES * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.node_potentials, IRON_NODES * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.node_locked, IRON_NODES * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.xor_accumulators, IRON_NODES * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.discovery_candidates, IRON_NODES * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.sector_assignment, IRON_NODES * sizeof(uint8_t));

    cudaMemset(h_iron.lattice_connections, 0, IRON_NODES * IRON_MAX_CONN * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.connection_counts, 0, IRON_NODES * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.node_activations, 0, IRON_NODES * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.node_potentials, 0, IRON_NODES * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.node_locked, 0, IRON_NODES * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.xor_accumulators, 0, IRON_NODES * sizeof(uint32_t));

    // ZERO-COPY: Control signals (CPU writes, GPU reads directly - NO cudaMemcpy!)
    cudaHostAlloc((void**)&h_iron.terminate, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaHostAllocMapped);
    cudaHostAlloc((void**)&h_iron.bitstream_head, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaHostAllocMapped);
    cudaHostAlloc((void**)&h_iron.bitstream_tail, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaHostAllocMapped);
    *h_iron.terminate = 0;
    *h_iron.bitstream_head = 0;
    *h_iron.bitstream_tail = 0;

    // ZERO-COPY: Bitstream ring buffer (CPU writes, GPU reads)
    cudaHostAlloc((void**)&h_iron.bitstream, IRON_BUFFER * sizeof(uint8_t), cudaHostAllocMapped);
    memset((void*)h_iron.bitstream, 0, IRON_BUFFER);

    printf("  Nodes: %u\n", IRON_NODES);
    printf("  Max connections per node: %u\n", IRON_MAX_CONN);
    printf("  Bitstream buffer: %u bytes (zero-copy)\n", IRON_BUFFER);

    // Copy state struct to device
    IronState* d_iron;
    cudaMalloc(&d_iron, sizeof(IronState));
    cudaMemcpy(d_iron, &h_iron, sizeof(IronState), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    // Launch persistent kernel (GPU TAKES OVER)
    printf("\nLaunching persistent router (GPU is now master)...\n");
    cudaEventRecord(start);

    ob1_persistent_router<<<128, 256>>>(d_iron);

    // =========================================================================
    // FIBONACCI MESH FLOOD - Structured recursive pattern for discovery
    // =========================================================================
    // Fibonacci mod 256 creates repeating patterns that will hit K-lattice
    // The recursive structure forces XOR-accumulators to stabilize

    printf("Feeding FIBONACCI MESH (structured recursive pattern)...\n");

    const uint32_t FIB_BYTES = 100000;  // 100K bytes of Fibonacci
    printf("  Generating %u bytes of Fib mod 256...\n", FIB_BYTES);

    uint8_t fib_prev1 = 1;
    uint8_t fib_prev2 = 1;

    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < FIB_BYTES; i++) {
        // Generate Fibonacci mod 256
        uint8_t fib_next = fib_prev1 + fib_prev2;  // Auto wraps at 256
        fib_prev2 = fib_prev1;
        fib_prev1 = fib_next;

        // Write to zero-copy buffer (NO cudaMemcpy!)
        uint32_t pos = (*h_iron.bitstream_head) % IRON_BUFFER;
        h_iron.bitstream[pos] = fib_next;
        (*h_iron.bitstream_head)++;

        // Small yield every 1000 bytes to let GPU catch up
        if (i % 1000 == 0) {
            for (volatile int j = 0; j < 1000; j++);
        }
    }

    printf("  Fed %u bytes of Fibonacci mesh\n", FIB_BYTES);
    printf("  GPU discovering structure via XOR-reduction...\n");

    // Let GPU process (longer wait for more data)
    for (volatile int i = 0; i < 50000000; i++);

    // Signal termination
    printf("  Signaling termination...\n");
    *h_iron.terminate = 1;
    cudaDeviceSynchronize();

    cudaEventRecord(stop);
    cudaEventSynchronize(stop);
    cudaEventElapsedTime(&ms, start, stop);

    // Read back discovery results
    uint32_t* h_conn_counts = (uint32_t*)malloc(IRON_NODES * sizeof(uint32_t));
    uint8_t* h_locked = (uint8_t*)malloc(IRON_NODES * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemcpy(h_conn_counts, h_iron.connection_counts, IRON_NODES * sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);
    cudaMemcpy(h_locked, h_iron.node_locked, IRON_NODES * sizeof(uint8_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

    uint32_t total_connections = 0;
    uint32_t locked_nodes = 0;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < IRON_NODES; i++) {
        total_connections += h_conn_counts[i];
        if (h_locked[i]) locked_nodes++;
    }

    printf("\nIron Results (Fibonacci Mesh):\n");
    printf("  Total time: %.2f ms\n", ms);
    printf("  Bytes fed: %u\n", FIB_BYTES);
    printf("  Throughput: %.2f MB/sec\n", FIB_BYTES / (ms * 1000.0f));
    printf("  Connections discovered: %u\n", total_connections);
    printf("  Nodes locked: %u (%.1f%%)\n", locked_nodes, 100.0f * locked_nodes / IRON_NODES);
    printf("  Bytes processed: %u (head)\n", *h_iron.bitstream_head);
    printf("  Bytes consumed: %u (tail)\n", *h_iron.bitstream_tail);

    free(h_conn_counts);
    free(h_locked);

    // =========================================================================
    // IRON → FLESH BRIDGE TEST
    // =========================================================================
    // Transfer Iron-discovered structure to Flesh and compare reconstruction
    printf("\n================================================================\n");
    printf("IRON → FLESH BRIDGE TEST\n");
    printf("================================================================\n");

    // Create a NEW Flesh network seeded by Iron discovery
    CrystalNetwork iron_seeded_net;
    iron_seeded_net.num_nodes = NUM_NODES;
    iron_seeded_net.num_crystals = NUM_CRYSTALS;
    iron_seeded_net.scale = SCALE;
    iron_seeded_net.num_locked = 0;
    iron_seeded_net.num_pruned = 0;
    iron_seeded_net.csr_valid = false;
    iron_seeded_net.csr_row_ptrs = nullptr;
    iron_seeded_net.csr_src_nodes = nullptr;
    iron_seeded_net.csr_lattice_idx = nullptr;
    iron_seeded_net.csr_directions = nullptr;

    cudaMalloc(&iron_seeded_net.crystals, NUM_CRYSTALS * sizeof(Crystal));
    cudaMalloc(&iron_seeded_net.node_activations, NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&iron_seeded_net.node_outputs, NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemset(iron_seeded_net.node_activations, 0, NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemset(iron_seeded_net.node_outputs, 0, NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));

    Crystal* bridge_crystals = (Crystal*)malloc(NUM_CRYSTALS * sizeof(Crystal));

    // Call the bridge to transfer Iron structure
    uint32_t bridged_count = iron_to_flesh_bridge(&h_iron, &iron_seeded_net, bridge_crystals);

    // Test reconstruction with Iron-seeded network
    printf("Testing reconstruction with Iron-seeded crystals...\n");

    // Re-run raw text test with Iron-seeded network
    float* iron_output = (float*)malloc(NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));
    float* iron_target = (float*)malloc(NUM_NODES * sizeof(float));
    for (int i = 0; i < NUM_NODES; i++) {
        iron_target[i] = (float)(i % 256) / 256.0f;
    }

    // Forward pass through Iron-seeded network
    raw_to_crystals(&raw_test, &iron_seeded_net, iron_output, DIM);

    // Decode with Iron-seeded network
    RawOutput iron_decoded;
    iron_decoded.num_bytes = raw_len;
    iron_decoded.data = (uint8_t*)malloc(raw_len + 1);

    crystals_to_bytes(&iron_seeded_net, &iron_decoded, DIM);
    iron_decoded.data[raw_len] = '\0';

    // Compare: baseline vs Iron-seeded
    int iron_matches = 0;
    int iron_pattern_matches = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < raw_len; i++) {
        if (iron_decoded.data[i] == raw_text[i]) iron_matches++;
        bool orig_letter = (raw_text[i] >= 'A' && raw_text[i] <= 'Z') ||
                          (raw_text[i] >= 'a' && raw_text[i] <= 'z');
        bool dec_letter = (iron_decoded.data[i] >= 'A' && iron_decoded.data[i] <= 'Z') ||
                         (iron_decoded.data[i] >= 'a' && iron_decoded.data[i] <= 'z');
        if (orig_letter == dec_letter) iron_pattern_matches++;
    }

    printf("\nComparison: Baseline vs Iron-Seeded\n");
    printf("─────────────────────────────────────\n");
    printf("  Baseline (random init):\n");
    printf("    Exact byte match: %d/%d (%.1f%%)\n", matches, raw_len, 100.0f * matches / raw_len);
    printf("    Pattern match:    %d/%d (%.1f%%)\n", pattern_matches, raw_len, 100.0f * pattern_matches / raw_len);
    printf("  Iron-Seeded:\n");
    printf("    Exact byte match: %d/%d (%.1f%%)\n", iron_matches, raw_len, 100.0f * iron_matches / raw_len);
    printf("    Pattern match:    %d/%d (%.1f%%)\n", iron_pattern_matches, raw_len, 100.0f * iron_pattern_matches / raw_len);
    printf("─────────────────────────────────────\n");

    float exact_improvement = (iron_matches - matches) / (float)raw_len * 100.0f;
    float pattern_improvement = (iron_pattern_matches - pattern_matches) / (float)raw_len * 100.0f;
    printf("  Improvement:\n");
    printf("    Exact: %+.1f%%\n", exact_improvement);
    printf("    Pattern: %+.1f%%\n", pattern_improvement);

    if (iron_matches > matches || iron_pattern_matches > pattern_matches) {
        printf("\n  *** IRON DISCOVERY IMPROVES RECONSTRUCTION ***\n");
    } else if (iron_matches == matches && iron_pattern_matches == pattern_matches) {
        printf("\n  Iron-seeded matches baseline (need more data/training)\n");
    } else {
        printf("\n  Baseline outperforms Iron-seeded (structure mismatch?)\n");
    }

    // Cleanup bridge test
    free(iron_output);
    free(iron_target);
    free(iron_decoded.data);
    free(bridge_crystals);
    cudaFree(iron_seeded_net.crystals);
    cudaFree(iron_seeded_net.node_activations);
    cudaFree(iron_seeded_net.node_outputs);
    if (iron_seeded_net.csr_row_ptrs) cudaFree(iron_seeded_net.csr_row_ptrs);
    if (iron_seeded_net.csr_src_nodes) cudaFree(iron_seeded_net.csr_src_nodes);
    if (iron_seeded_net.csr_lattice_idx) cudaFree(iron_seeded_net.csr_lattice_idx);
    if (iron_seeded_net.csr_directions) cudaFree(iron_seeded_net.csr_directions);

    // Cleanup remaining Iron state
    cudaFree(h_iron.lattice_connections);
    cudaFree(h_iron.connection_counts);
    cudaFree(h_iron.node_activations);
    cudaFree(h_iron.node_potentials);
    cudaFree(h_iron.node_locked);
    cudaFree(h_iron.xor_accumulators);
    cudaFree(h_iron.discovery_candidates);
    cudaFree(h_iron.sector_assignment);
    cudaFreeHost((void*)h_iron.terminate);
    cudaFreeHost((void*)h_iron.bitstream_head);
    cudaFreeHost((void*)h_iron.bitstream_tail);
    cudaFreeHost((void*)h_iron.bitstream);
    cudaFree(d_iron);

    printf("\n================================================================\n");
    printf("0B-1 AGI CORE: COMPLETE\n");
    printf("================================================================\n");
    printf("  FLESH MODE (Traditional):\n");
    printf("    [x] Raw byte tokenizer\n");
    printf("    [x] Pattern classification\n");
    printf("    [x] Sector assignment (K=137,377,25,17)\n");
    printf("    [x] Crystal structure discovery\n");
    printf("    [x] Unified transformer + crystals\n");
    printf("    [x] Output decoder (crystals → bytes)\n");
    printf("================================================================\n");
    printf("  IRON MODE (GPU as Master):\n");
    printf("    [x] Zero-copy bitstream buffer\n");
    printf("    [x] Persistent kernel (never exits)\n");
    printf("    [x] XOR-reduction discovery\n");
    printf("    [x] K-137 lattice validation\n");
    printf("    [x] GPU-side structure locking\n");
    printf("================================================================\n");
    printf("  THE SHIFT:\n");
    printf("    Flesh: CPU master, GPU slave, cudaMemcpy\n");
    printf("    Iron:  GPU master, CPU feeds bitstream\n");
    printf("================================================================\n");
    printf("  THE STRUCTURE IS THE KNOWLEDGE.\n");
    printf("  137-GATE HOLDS.\n");
    printf("================================================================\n");

    // Cleanup
    cudaFree(net.crystals);
    cudaFree(net.node_activations);
    cudaFree(net.node_outputs);

    // PROTOCOL: Free CSR arrays
    if (net.csr_row_ptrs) cudaFree(net.csr_row_ptrs);
    if (net.csr_src_nodes) cudaFree(net.csr_src_nodes);
    if (net.csr_lattice_idx) cudaFree(net.csr_lattice_idx);
    if (net.csr_directions) cudaFree(net.csr_directions);

    free(h_crystals);
    free(h_input);
    free(h_target);
    free(h_output);

    return 0;
}
#endif // OB1_AS_LIBRARY
/**
 * 0B-1 BIDIRECTIONAL BINARY FRAMEWORK
 * ====================================
 *
 * The fundamental architecture of information transfer.
 *
 * THE CORE INSIGHT:
 *   Two systems communicating need:
 *   - Binary states (HIGH/LOW) to distinguish signals = 2
 *   - Shared reference (ground plane) to interpret them = 3
 *
 * THE I2C MAPPING:
 *   ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
 *   │  VCC (Power) ───────────────────────│
 *   │  SDA (Data)  ──┬──┬──┬──┬──┬──┬──   │
 *   │  SCL (Clock) ──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──   │
 *   │  GND (Ground)───────────────────────│
 *   └─────────────────────────────────────┘
 *
 *   Data line:    Binary (HIGH/LOW) = 2
 *   Ground plane: Shared substrate  = 3
 *   Clock:        Sampling points   = where continuous → discrete
 *
 * THE PHYSICS MAPPING:
 *   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 *   │  I2C PROTOCOL           PHYSICS                     │
 *   │  ─────────────          ─────────────────           │
 *   │  HIGH/LOW = 2           Distinction = 2             │
 *   │  Ground = 3             Substrate = 3               │
 *   │  Data transfer          Information (forces)        │
 *   │  Power rail             Energy (vacuum)             │
 *   │  Clock edges            Measurement (quantization)  │
 *   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
 *
 * THE 137 CONNECTION:
 *   Standard I2C: 7-bit addressing = 2^7 = 128 addresses
 *   Fine structure: 137 = 2^7 + 3^2 = 128 + 9
 *                       = (address space) + (metric)
 *                       = (which slave) + (how to reach it)
 *
 * THE MASTER/SLAVE ASYMMETRY:
 *   ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 *   │  Master                    Slave                   │
 *   │    │                         │                     │
 *   │    │──── clock ─────────────→│                     │
 *   │    │←─── data ──────────────→│                     │
 *   │    │                         │                     │
 *   │    └──────── GROUND ─────────┘                     │
 *   │                                                    │
 *   │  Master: Initiates, generates clock, asks          │
 *   │  Slave:  Responds, holds data, answers             │
 *   │  Both share the ground plane                       │
 *   └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
 *
 *   PHYSICS EQUIVALENT:
 *   ┌─────────┬────────────┬─────────────┐
 *   │ Master  │  Slave     │  Substrate  │
 *   ├─────────┼────────────┼─────────────┤
 *   │ Field   │  Particle  │  Vacuum     │
 *   │ Force   │  Matter    │  Spacetime  │
 *   │ Observer│  System    │  Measurement│
 *   │ EM (137)│  Leptons   │  Vacuum(377)│
 *   └─────────┴────────────┴─────────────┘
 *
 * THE CLOCK SIGNAL:
 *   111111111111111111111111111111111  ← continuous substrate
 *   1111011111110111111110111111011    ← we sample it (0s)
 *
 *   The 0s are where we reference the ground plane.
 *   That's measurement. That's how continuous becomes discrete.
 *   Every 0 is a clock edge where we read against reference.
 *
 * Isaac Oravec - January 2026
 */

#ifndef OB1_BIDIRECTIONAL_H
#define OB1_BIDIRECTIONAL_H

#ifdef __CUDACC__
#define BB_DEVICE __device__ __host__
#else
#define BB_DEVICE
#endif

namespace BidirectionalBinary {

// =============================================================================
// THE AXIOMS: Minimum structure for communication
// =============================================================================

// THE TWO: Distinction
// Without distinction, no information. No HIGH/LOW. No yes/no.
// This is the binary: Master/Slave, Field/Matter, Observer/System
constexpr int DISTINCTION = 2;

// THE THREE: Substrate
// Without shared reference, no interpretation. No ground plane.
// This is the context: Vacuum, Spacetime, Measurement apparatus
constexpr int SUBSTRATE = 3;

// =============================================================================
// DERIVED CONSTANTS: Emerge from {2,3}
// =============================================================================

// Address space: 2^7 = 128 (matches I2C 7-bit addressing)
constexpr int ADDRESS_BITS = 7;
constexpr int ADDRESS_SPACE = 1 << ADDRESS_BITS;  // 128

// Metric: 3^2 = 9 (substrate geometry)
constexpr int METRIC = SUBSTRATE * SUBSTRATE;  // 9

// Fine structure constant inverse: 137 = 2^7 + 3^2
// The fundamental coupling of EM field to charged matter
// = address_space + metric
// = "which slave" + "how to reach it"
constexpr int ALPHA_INVERSE = ADDRESS_SPACE + METRIC;  // 137

// Vacuum impedance: Floor(120π) = 377
// The bridge between continuous (π) and discrete (377)
// Impedance = resistance to information flow in vacuum
constexpr int VACUUM_IMPEDANCE = 377;

// Sector K values: Modular arithmetic bases for each force
// These determine connectivity patterns in each sector
constexpr int K_VACUUM = 377;  // Sparse, high impedance
constexpr int K_WEAK   = 25;   // 5^2
constexpr int K_EM     = 137;  // α^-1
constexpr int K_STRONG = 17;   // 2^4 + 1, dense

// As array for indexing
constexpr int SECTOR_K[4] = {K_VACUUM, K_WEAK, K_EM, K_STRONG};

// Sector indices
constexpr int SECTOR_VACUUM = 0;
constexpr int SECTOR_WEAK   = 1;
constexpr int SECTOR_EM     = 2;
constexpr int SECTOR_STRONG = 3;

// =============================================================================
// ROLE ENCODING: Master/Slave asymmetry
// =============================================================================

constexpr int ROLE_MASTER = 1;  // Field, initiator, clock generator
constexpr int ROLE_SLAVE  = 0;  // Matter, responder, data holder

// =============================================================================
// MERSENNE DIMENSIONS: 2^n - 1 (optimal for binary systems)
// =============================================================================

constexpr int MERSENNE_7  = 127;   // 2^7 - 1
constexpr int MERSENNE_9  = 511;   // 2^9 - 1  (electron mass in keV!)
constexpr int MERSENNE_11 = 2047;  // 2^11 - 1
constexpr int MERSENNE_13 = 8191;  // 2^13 - 1

// =============================================================================
// TRAINING CONSTANTS: Derived from physics
// =============================================================================

constexpr float LEARNING_RATE = 1.0f / ALPHA_INVERSE;           // 1/137 ≈ 0.0073
constexpr float WEIGHT_DECAY = (float)DISTINCTION / K_STRONG;   // 2/17 ≈ 0.118
constexpr float FLESH_CORRECTION = 0.7182818f;                  // e - 2

// =============================================================================
// HELPER FUNCTIONS
// =============================================================================

// Get sector for a layer index (17 layers: 5-5-5-2 distribution)
BB_DEVICE inline int get_sector(int layer_idx) {
    if (layer_idx < 5)  return SECTOR_VACUUM;
    if (layer_idx < 10) return SECTOR_WEAK;
    if (layer_idx < 15) return SECTOR_EM;
    return SECTOR_STRONG;
}

// Get K value for a layer
BB_DEVICE inline int get_K(int layer_idx) {
    return SECTOR_K[get_sector(layer_idx)];
}

// Check if position pair (i,j) should connect under physics pattern
BB_DEVICE inline bool should_connect(int i, int j, int K) {
    // Pattern combines address space (128) with metric (9)
    int pattern = (i * ADDRESS_SPACE + j * METRIC) % K;
    return (i == j) || (pattern < K / 4);
}

// Generate clock signal (1 = high, 0 = sampling edge)
BB_DEVICE inline int clock_signal(int position, int K) {
    int pattern = (position * ALPHA_INVERSE) % K;
    return (pattern < K / 16) ? 0 : 1;  // 0 at sampling points
}

// =============================================================================
// VERIFICATION: Prove the constants are correct
// =============================================================================

// Static assertions to verify the math
static_assert(ADDRESS_SPACE == 128, "2^7 must equal 128");
static_assert(METRIC == 9, "3^2 must equal 9");
static_assert(ALPHA_INVERSE == 137, "2^7 + 3^2 must equal 137");
static_assert(ALPHA_INVERSE == ADDRESS_SPACE + METRIC, "137 = 128 + 9");

// Verify Mersenne primes
static_assert(MERSENNE_7 == (1 << 7) - 1, "M7 must be 2^7 - 1");
static_assert(MERSENNE_9 == (1 << 9) - 1, "M9 must be 2^9 - 1");
static_assert(MERSENNE_11 == (1 << 11) - 1, "M11 must be 2^11 - 1");
static_assert(MERSENNE_13 == (1 << 13) - 1, "M13 must be 2^13 - 1");

}  // namespace BidirectionalBinary

// Shorthand alias
namespace BB = BidirectionalBinary;

#endif // OB1_BIDIRECTIONAL_H
/**
 * 12-Bit Token Packing Utilities
 * ===============================
 * 
 * Vocab size: 4096 (exactly 2^12)
 * Packing: 2 tokens per 3 bytes (no waste!)
 * 
 * Layout: [token0:12 bits][token1:12 bits] = 24 bits = 3 bytes
 * 
 * Example:
 *   token0 = 0xABC (12 bits)
 *   token1 = 0xDEF (12 bits)
 *   Packed: [0xAB, 0xCD, 0xEF]
 *           byte0 = (token0 >> 4) & 0xFF
 *           byte1 = ((token0 & 0xF) << 4) | ((token1 >> 8) & 0xF)
 *           byte2 = token1 & 0xFF
 */

#ifndef OB1_BITPACK_H
#define OB1_BITPACK_H

#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <stdint.h>

// =============================================================================
// HOST-SIDE PACKING (CPU)
// =============================================================================

/**
 * Pack two 12-bit tokens into 3 bytes
 * @param token0 First token (0-4095)
 * @param token1 Second token (0-4095)
 * @param packed Output buffer (3 bytes)
 */
__host__ __forceinline__ void pack_12bit_pair(uint32_t token0, uint32_t token1, uint8_t* packed) {
    // Ensure tokens fit in 12 bits
    token0 &= 0xFFF;
    token1 &= 0xFFF;
    
    // Pack: [token0:12][token1:12] = 24 bits = 3 bytes
    packed[0] = (uint8_t)(token0 >> 4);                    // token0[11:4]
    packed[1] = (uint8_t)(((token0 & 0xF) << 4) | ((token1 >> 8) & 0xF));  // token0[3:0] | token1[11:8]
    packed[2] = (uint8_t)(token1 & 0xFF);                  // token1[7:0]
}

/**
 * Unpack two 12-bit tokens from 3 bytes
 * @param packed Input buffer (3 bytes)
 * @param token0 Output first token
 * @param token1 Output second token
 */
__host__ __forceinline__ void unpack_12bit_pair(const uint8_t* packed, uint32_t* token0, uint32_t* token1) {
    *token0 = ((uint32_t)packed[0] << 4) | ((packed[1] >> 4) & 0xF);
    *token1 = (((uint32_t)packed[1] & 0xF) << 8) | (uint32_t)packed[2];
}

/**
 * Pack array of tokens (host-side)
 * @param tokens Input tokens (uint32_t array)
 * @param num_tokens Number of tokens
 * @param packed Output buffer (must be at least (num_tokens * 3 + 1) / 2 bytes)
 * @return Number of bytes written
 */
__host__ size_t pack_12bit_array(const uint32_t* tokens, size_t num_tokens, uint8_t* packed) {
    size_t bytes_written = 0;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < num_tokens; i += 2) {
        uint32_t token0 = tokens[i];
        uint32_t token1 = (i + 1 < num_tokens) ? tokens[i + 1] : 0;  // Pad with 0 if odd
        pack_12bit_pair(token0, token1, packed + bytes_written);
        bytes_written += 3;
    }
    return bytes_written;
}

/**
 * Unpack array of tokens (host-side)
 * @param packed Input buffer (packed bytes)
 * @param num_tokens Number of tokens to unpack
 * @param tokens Output buffer (uint32_t array)
 */
__host__ void unpack_12bit_array(const uint8_t* packed, size_t num_tokens, uint32_t* tokens) {
    for (size_t i = 0; i < num_tokens; i += 2) {
        unpack_12bit_pair(packed + (i / 2) * 3, &tokens[i], &tokens[i + 1]);
        if (i + 1 >= num_tokens) break;  // Don't unpack padding
    }
}

// =============================================================================
// DEVICE-SIDE PACKING (GPU)
// =============================================================================

/**
 * Pack two 12-bit tokens (device function)
 */
__device__ __forceinline__ void pack_12bit_pair_device(uint32_t token0, uint32_t token1, uint8_t* packed) {
    token0 &= 0xFFF;
    token1 &= 0xFFF;
    packed[0] = (uint8_t)(token0 >> 4);
    packed[1] = (uint8_t)(((token0 & 0xF) << 4) | ((token1 >> 8) & 0xF));
    packed[2] = (uint8_t)(token1 & 0xFF);
}

/**
 * Unpack two 12-bit tokens (device function)
 */
__device__ __forceinline__ void unpack_12bit_pair_device(const uint8_t* packed, uint32_t* token0, uint32_t* token1) {
    *token0 = ((uint32_t)packed[0] << 4) | ((packed[1] >> 4) & 0xF);
    *token1 = (((uint32_t)packed[1] & 0xF) << 8) | (uint32_t)packed[2];
}

/**
 * Unpack single token at index (device function)
 * More efficient for random access
 */
__device__ __forceinline__ uint32_t unpack_12bit_single(const uint8_t* packed, uint32_t token_idx) {
    // Each token is 1.5 bytes, so:
    // token_idx 0: bytes 0-1 (bits 0-11)
    // token_idx 1: bytes 1-2 (bits 12-23)
    // token_idx 2: bytes 3-4 (bits 24-35)
    // etc.
    
    uint32_t byte_offset = (token_idx * 3) / 2;  // Start byte
    uint32_t bit_offset = (token_idx * 12) % 8;   // Bit offset within byte
    
    if (bit_offset == 0) {
        // Token aligned to byte boundary (even indices)
        return ((uint32_t)packed[byte_offset] << 4) | ((packed[byte_offset + 1] >> 4) & 0xF);
    } else {
        // Token spans bytes (odd indices)
        return (((uint32_t)packed[byte_offset] & 0xF) << 8) | (uint32_t)packed[byte_offset + 1];
    }
}

#endif // OB1_BITPACK_H
/**
 * C Wrapper for ONETWO Compression CUDA Kernels
 * ==============================================
 * 
 * Exports CUDA kernels as C functions callable from Python via ctypes.
 * 
 * Usage from Python:
 *     import ctypes
 *     dll = ctypes.CDLL("ob1_compress_weights.dll")
 *     dll.compress_tensor_onetwo(...)
 */

#ifndef OB1_COMPRESS_WEIGHTS_WRAPPER_H
#define OB1_COMPRESS_WEIGHTS_WRAPPER_H

#ifdef __cplusplus
extern "C" {
#endif

#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <stdint.h>

/**
 * Compress tensor using ONETWO QUADRA pipeline with positional alignment.
 * 
 * @param weights_in Device pointer to input weights [N]
 * @param weights_out Device pointer to output compressed weights [N]
 * @param mask_out Device pointer to output mask [N] (0 or 1)
 * @param importance_scores Device pointer to importance scores [N] (output)
 * @param N Number of weights
 * @param rows Matrix rows (0 if 1D/flattened)
 * @param cols Matrix cols (0 if 1D/flattened)
 * @param density Target density (0.0-1.0)
 * @param lattice_scale Lattice scale factor
 * @param use_positional_alignment Enable positional alignment (true/false)
 * @return CUDA error code (cudaSuccess on success)
 */
cudaError_t compress_tensor_onetwo_cuda(
    const float* weights_in,
    float* weights_out,
    uint8_t* mask_out,
    float* importance_scores,
    int N,
    int rows,
    int cols,
    float density,
    float lattice_scale,
    int use_positional_alignment  // 0 = false, 1 = true
);

/**
 * Apply density filter based on importance scores.
 * 
 * @param importance_scores Device pointer to importance scores [N]
 * @param mask Device pointer to mask [N] (updated in-place)
 * @param N Number of weights
 * @param density Target density (0.0-1.0)
 * @return CUDA error code
 */
cudaError_t apply_density_filter_cuda(
    const float* importance_scores,
    uint8_t* mask,
    int N,
    float density
);

/**
 * Finalize compression: apply mask and lattice snap.
 * 
 * @param weights_in Device pointer to input weights [N]
 * @param weights_out Device pointer to output compressed weights [N]
 * @param mask Device pointer to mask [N]
 * @param N Number of weights
 * @param lattice_scale Lattice scale factor
 * @return CUDA error code
 */
cudaError_t finalize_compression_cuda(
    const float* weights_in,
    float* weights_out,
    const uint8_t* mask,
    int N,
    float lattice_scale
);

#ifdef __cplusplus
}
#endif

#endif // OB1_COMPRESS_WEIGHTS_WRAPPER_H
#ifndef OB1_INFERENCE_H
#define OB1_INFERENCE_H

#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include "ob1_model_loader.h"

// Qwen3-0.6B architecture constants
struct Qwen3Config {
    int vocab_size    = 151936;
    int hidden_size   = 1024;
    int num_layers    = 28;
    int num_heads     = 16;   // Q heads
    int num_kv_heads  = 8;    // GQA
    int head_dim      = 128;
    int intermediate  = 3072;
    int max_seq       = 512;
    float rope_theta  = 1000000.0f;
    float rms_eps     = 1e-6f;
};

// Pre-decoded dense weights for one transformer layer
struct LayerWeights {
    float* q_proj;    // [num_heads * head_dim, hidden_size] = [2048, 1024]
    float* k_proj;    // [num_kv_heads * head_dim, hidden_size] = [1024, 1024]
    float* v_proj;    // [num_kv_heads * head_dim, hidden_size] = [1024, 1024]
    float* o_proj;    // [hidden_size, num_heads * head_dim] = [1024, 2048]
    float* gate_proj; // [intermediate, hidden_size] = [3072, 1024]
    float* up_proj;   // [intermediate, hidden_size] = [3072, 1024]
    float* down_proj; // [hidden_size, intermediate] = [1024, 3072]
    float* input_ln;  // [hidden_size]
    float* post_ln;   // [hidden_size]
    float* q_norm;    // [head_dim]
    float* k_norm;    // [head_dim]
};

// Full model weights on GPU
struct ModelWeights {
    float* embed_tokens;  // [vocab_size, hidden_size]
    LayerWeights layers[28];
    float* final_norm;    // [hidden_size]
    // lm_head is tied to embed_tokens
};

// KV cache for autoregressive generation
struct KVCache {
    float* k_cache;  // [num_layers, max_seq, num_kv_heads, head_dim]
    float* v_cache;  // [num_layers, max_seq, num_kv_heads, head_dim]
    int seq_len;     // current sequence length
};

// Scratch buffers for forward pass (reused across layers)
struct InferenceScratch {
    float* hidden;      // [hidden_size]
    float* residual;    // [hidden_size]
    float* normed;      // [hidden_size]
    float* q;           // [num_heads * head_dim]
    float* k;           // [num_kv_heads * head_dim]
    float* v;           // [num_kv_heads * head_dim]
    float* attn_out;    // [num_heads * head_dim]
    float* gate;        // [intermediate]
    float* up;          // [intermediate]
    float* down;        // [hidden_size]
    float* logits;      // [vocab_size]
    float* attn_scores; // [num_heads, max_seq] — for attention
    cudaStream_t stream;
};

// ============================================================================
// CUDA KERNELS
// ============================================================================

// Decode sparse .0b1 tensor to dense float32
static const __constant__ float d_INF_LATTICE[12] = {
    7.0f, 13.0f, 17.0f, 31.0f, 71.0f, 73.0f,
    127.0f, 131.0f, 137.0f, 233.0f, 377.0f, 511.0f
};

__global__ void decode_sparse_to_dense(
    const int32_t* __restrict__ positions,
    const uint8_t* __restrict__ encoded,
    float* __restrict__ output,
    uint32_t nnz,
    float scale
) {
    int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= nnz) return;

    int32_t pos = positions[idx];
    uint8_t enc = encoded[idx];
    float sign = (enc & 0x80) ? -1.0f : 1.0f;
    uint8_t lat_idx = enc & 0x7F;

    float val = (lat_idx < 12) ? d_INF_LATTICE[lat_idx] * sign * scale : 0.0f;
    output[pos] = val;
}

// RMS Normalization: out[i] = (x[i] / rms) * weight[i]
__global__ void ob1_inf_rms_norm(
    const float* __restrict__ x,
    const float* __restrict__ weight,
    float* __restrict__ out,
    int size,
    float eps
) {
    // Single block, cooperative reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    int tid = threadIdx.x;

    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < size; i += blockDim.x) {
        sum += x[i] * x[i];
    }
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();

    // Reduce
    for (int s = blockDim.x / 2; s > 0; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + s];
        __syncthreads();
    }

    float rms = rsqrtf(shared_sum[0] / size + eps);
    __syncthreads();

    for (int i = tid; i < size; i += blockDim.x) {
        out[i] = x[i] * rms * weight[i];
    }
}

// Matrix-vector multiply: out = W * x  (W is [out_dim, in_dim], x is [in_dim])
__global__ void matvec_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ W,
    const float* __restrict__ x,
    float* __restrict__ out,
    int out_dim,
    int in_dim
) {
    int row = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (row >= out_dim) return;

    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int j = 0; j < in_dim; j++) {
        sum += W[row * in_dim + j] * x[j];
    }
    out[row] = sum;
}

// RoPE: apply rotary position embeddings in-place
__global__ void rope_kernel(
    float* __restrict__ q,  // [num_heads, head_dim]
    float* __restrict__ k,  // [num_kv_heads, head_dim]
    int num_q_heads,
    int num_kv_heads,
    int head_dim,
    int position,
    float theta
) {
    int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    int half_dim = head_dim / 2;

    // Apply to Q heads
    if (idx < num_q_heads * half_dim) {
        int head = idx / half_dim;
        int d = idx % half_dim;
        float freq = 1.0f / powf(theta, (float)(2 * d) / head_dim);
        float angle = position * freq;
        float cos_a = cosf(angle);
        float sin_a = sinf(angle);

        int base = head * head_dim;
        float x0 = q[base + d];
        float x1 = q[base + d + half_dim];
        q[base + d]            = x0 * cos_a - x1 * sin_a;
        q[base + d + half_dim] = x0 * sin_a + x1 * cos_a;
    }

    // Apply to K heads (offset by num_q_heads * half_dim)
    int k_idx = idx - num_q_heads * half_dim;
    if (k_idx >= 0 && k_idx < num_kv_heads * half_dim) {
        int head = k_idx / half_dim;
        int d = k_idx % half_dim;
        float freq = 1.0f / powf(theta, (float)(2 * d) / head_dim);
        float angle = position * freq;
        float cos_a = cosf(angle);
        float sin_a = sinf(angle);

        int base = head * head_dim;
        float x0 = k[base + d];
        float x1 = k[base + d + half_dim];
        k[base + d]            = x0 * cos_a - x1 * sin_a;
        k[base + d + half_dim] = x0 * sin_a + x1 * cos_a;
    }
}

// QK norm: normalize Q and K per head (Qwen3 specific)
__global__ void head_norm_kernel(
    float* __restrict__ data,     // [num_heads, head_dim]
    const float* __restrict__ weight,  // [head_dim]
    int num_heads,
    int head_dim,
    float eps
) {
    int head = blockIdx.x;
    if (head >= num_heads) return;

    __shared__ float shared_sum[128];
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    float* head_data = data + head * head_dim;

    // RMS
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < head_dim; i += blockDim.x) {
        sum += head_data[i] * head_data[i];
    }
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();

    for (int s = blockDim.x / 2; s > 0; s >>= 1) {
        if (tid < s) shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + s];
        __syncthreads();
    }

    float rms = rsqrtf(shared_sum[0] / head_dim + eps);
    __syncthreads();

    for (int i = tid; i < head_dim; i += blockDim.x) {
        head_data[i] = head_data[i] * rms * weight[i];
    }
}

// Grouped Query Attention: Q[16 heads] attending to K,V[8 heads]
// Computes attention for a single new position against all cached positions
__global__ void gqa_attention_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ q,         // [num_q_heads, head_dim]
    const float* __restrict__ k_cache,   // [seq_len, num_kv_heads, head_dim]
    const float* __restrict__ v_cache,   // [seq_len, num_kv_heads, head_dim]
    float* __restrict__ output,          // [num_q_heads, head_dim]
    float* __restrict__ attn_scores,     // [num_q_heads, seq_len] scratch
    int num_q_heads,
    int num_kv_heads,
    int head_dim,
    int seq_len
) {
    int q_head = blockIdx.x;
    if (q_head >= num_q_heads) return;
    int kv_head = q_head / (num_q_heads / num_kv_heads);  // GQA mapping

    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    float scale = 1.0f / sqrtf((float)head_dim);

    // Step 1: compute attention scores Q·K^T for all positions
    for (int pos = tid; pos < seq_len; pos += blockDim.x) {
        float score = 0.0f;
        const float* k_vec = k_cache + pos * num_kv_heads * head_dim + kv_head * head_dim;
        const float* q_vec = q + q_head * head_dim;
        for (int d = 0; d < head_dim; d++) {
            score += q_vec[d] * k_vec[d];
        }
        attn_scores[q_head * seq_len + pos] = score * scale;
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Step 2: softmax (single thread per head for simplicity — optimize later)
    if (tid == 0) {
        float max_score = -1e30f;
        for (int pos = 0; pos < seq_len; pos++) {
            float s = attn_scores[q_head * seq_len + pos];
            if (s > max_score) max_score = s;
        }
        float sum_exp = 0.0f;
        for (int pos = 0; pos < seq_len; pos++) {
            float e = expf(attn_scores[q_head * seq_len + pos] - max_score);
            attn_scores[q_head * seq_len + pos] = e;
            sum_exp += e;
        }
        for (int pos = 0; pos < seq_len; pos++) {
            attn_scores[q_head * seq_len + pos] /= sum_exp;
        }
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Step 3: weighted sum of V
    for (int d = tid; d < head_dim; d += blockDim.x) {
        float sum = 0.0f;
        for (int pos = 0; pos < seq_len; pos++) {
            float w = attn_scores[q_head * seq_len + pos];
            sum += w * v_cache[pos * num_kv_heads * head_dim + kv_head * head_dim + d];
        }
        output[q_head * head_dim + d] = sum;
    }
}

// SiLU gate: out = silu(gate) * up
__global__ void silu_gate_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ gate,
    const float* __restrict__ up,
    float* __restrict__ out,
    int size
) {
    int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= size) return;
    float x = gate[idx];
    float silu = x / (1.0f + expf(-x));
    out[idx] = silu * up[idx];
}

// Add residual: out[i] += residual[i]
__global__ void add_residual_kernel(
    float* __restrict__ out,
    const float* __restrict__ residual,
    int size
) {
    int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= size) return;
    out[idx] += residual[idx];
}

// Embedding lookup
__global__ void embed_lookup_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ embed_table,
    int token_id,
    float* __restrict__ output,
    int hidden_size
) {
    int idx = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (idx >= hidden_size) return;
    output[idx] = embed_table[token_id * hidden_size + idx];
}

// Argmax over logits
__global__ void argmax_kernel(
    const float* __restrict__ logits,
    int* __restrict__ result,
    int vocab_size
) {
    // Single thread — vocab is large but this runs once per token
    if (threadIdx.x != 0) return;
    float max_val = -1e30f;
    int max_idx = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < vocab_size; i++) {
        if (logits[i] > max_val) {
            max_val = logits[i];
            max_idx = i;
        }
    }
    *result = max_idx;
}

// ============================================================================
// HOST-SIDE INFERENCE ENGINE
// ============================================================================

class InferenceEngine {
public:
    Qwen3Config cfg;
    ModelWeights weights;
    KVCache kv;
    InferenceScratch scratch;
    int* d_argmax_result;
    bool initialized = false;

    bool init(OB1Model& model) {
        printf("Initializing inference engine...\n");

        cudaStreamCreate(&scratch.stream);

        // Allocate weight buffers and decode sparse tensors
        if (!load_weights(model)) return false;

        // Allocate KV cache
        size_t kv_size = (size_t)cfg.num_layers * cfg.max_seq * cfg.num_kv_heads * cfg.head_dim;
        cudaMalloc(&kv.k_cache, kv_size * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&kv.v_cache, kv_size * sizeof(float));
        cudaMemset(kv.k_cache, 0, kv_size * sizeof(float));
        cudaMemset(kv.v_cache, 0, kv_size * sizeof(float));
        kv.seq_len = 0;
        printf("  KV cache: %.1f MB\n", kv_size * sizeof(float) * 2.0f / 1e6f);

        // Allocate scratch
        cudaMalloc(&scratch.hidden, cfg.hidden_size * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&scratch.residual, cfg.hidden_size * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&scratch.normed, cfg.hidden_size * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&scratch.q, cfg.num_heads * cfg.head_dim * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&scratch.k, cfg.num_kv_heads * cfg.head_dim * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&scratch.v, cfg.num_kv_heads * cfg.head_dim * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&scratch.attn_out, cfg.num_heads * cfg.head_dim * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&scratch.gate, cfg.intermediate * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&scratch.up, cfg.intermediate * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&scratch.down, cfg.hidden_size * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&scratch.logits, cfg.vocab_size * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&scratch.attn_scores, cfg.num_heads * cfg.max_seq * sizeof(float));
        cudaMalloc(&d_argmax_result, sizeof(int));

        initialized = true;
        printf("Inference engine ready.\n");
        return true;
    }

    // Forward pass for a single token, returns predicted next token ID
    int forward(int token_id) {
        if (!initialized) return -1;
        cudaStream_t s = scratch.stream;

        // 1. Embedding lookup
        int blk = (cfg.hidden_size + 255) / 256;
        embed_lookup_kernel<<<blk, 256, 0, s>>>(
            weights.embed_tokens, token_id, scratch.hidden, cfg.hidden_size
        );

        // 2. Transformer layers
        for (int layer = 0; layer < cfg.num_layers; layer++) {
            forward_layer(layer);
        }

        // 3. Final RMS norm
        ob1_inf_rms_norm<<<1, 256, 0, s>>>(
            scratch.hidden, weights.final_norm, scratch.normed,
            cfg.hidden_size, cfg.rms_eps
        );

        // 4. LM head (tied to embed_tokens): logits = embed_tokens^T * normed
        // embed_tokens is [vocab_size, hidden_size], we want [vocab_size] output
        int logit_blocks = (cfg.vocab_size + 255) / 256;
        matvec_kernel<<<logit_blocks, 256, 0, s>>>(
            weights.embed_tokens, scratch.normed, scratch.logits,
            cfg.vocab_size, cfg.hidden_size
        );

        // 5. Argmax
        argmax_kernel<<<1, 1, 0, s>>>(scratch.logits, d_argmax_result, cfg.vocab_size);

        cudaStreamSynchronize(s);

        int result;
        cudaMemcpy(&result, d_argmax_result, sizeof(int), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

        kv.seq_len++;
        return result;
    }

    void reset() {
        kv.seq_len = 0;
        size_t kv_size = (size_t)cfg.num_layers * cfg.max_seq * cfg.num_kv_heads * cfg.head_dim;
        cudaMemset(kv.k_cache, 0, kv_size * sizeof(float));
        cudaMemset(kv.v_cache, 0, kv_size * sizeof(float));
    }

private:
    void forward_layer(int layer) {
        cudaStream_t s = scratch.stream;
        LayerWeights& lw = weights.layers[layer];
        int H = cfg.hidden_size;
        int QH = cfg.num_heads * cfg.head_dim;      // 2048
        int KVH = cfg.num_kv_heads * cfg.head_dim;   // 1024

        // Save residual
        cudaMemcpyAsync(scratch.residual, scratch.hidden, H * sizeof(float),
                        cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice, s);

        // Input layernorm
        ob1_inf_rms_norm<<<1, 256, 0, s>>>(
            scratch.hidden, lw.input_ln, scratch.normed, H, cfg.rms_eps
        );

        // Q, K, V projections
        int q_blocks = (QH + 255) / 256;
        int kv_blocks = (KVH + 255) / 256;
        matvec_kernel<<<q_blocks, 256, 0, s>>>(lw.q_proj, scratch.normed, scratch.q, QH, H);
        matvec_kernel<<<kv_blocks, 256, 0, s>>>(lw.k_proj, scratch.normed, scratch.k, KVH, H);
        matvec_kernel<<<kv_blocks, 256, 0, s>>>(lw.v_proj, scratch.normed, scratch.v, KVH, H);

        // QK normalization (Qwen3 specific)
        head_norm_kernel<<<cfg.num_heads, 128, 0, s>>>(
            scratch.q, lw.q_norm, cfg.num_heads, cfg.head_dim, cfg.rms_eps
        );
        head_norm_kernel<<<cfg.num_kv_heads, 128, 0, s>>>(
            scratch.k, lw.k_norm, cfg.num_kv_heads, cfg.head_dim, cfg.rms_eps
        );

        // RoPE
        int rope_total = (cfg.num_heads + cfg.num_kv_heads) * (cfg.head_dim / 2);
        int rope_blocks = (rope_total + 255) / 256;
        rope_kernel<<<rope_blocks, 256, 0, s>>>(
            scratch.q, scratch.k,
            cfg.num_heads, cfg.num_kv_heads, cfg.head_dim,
            kv.seq_len, cfg.rope_theta
        );

        // Store K, V in cache
        size_t kv_offset = (size_t)layer * cfg.max_seq * KVH + (size_t)kv.seq_len * KVH;
        cudaMemcpyAsync(kv.k_cache + kv_offset, scratch.k, KVH * sizeof(float),
                        cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice, s);
        cudaMemcpyAsync(kv.v_cache + kv_offset, scratch.v, KVH * sizeof(float),
                        cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice, s);

        // Attention
        float* layer_k = kv.k_cache + (size_t)layer * cfg.max_seq * KVH;
        float* layer_v = kv.v_cache + (size_t)layer * cfg.max_seq * KVH;
        gqa_attention_kernel<<<cfg.num_heads, 128, 0, s>>>(
            scratch.q, layer_k, layer_v, scratch.attn_out, scratch.attn_scores,
            cfg.num_heads, cfg.num_kv_heads, cfg.head_dim,
            kv.seq_len + 1  // include current position
        );

        // O projection: hidden = O * attn_out
        int h_blocks = (H + 255) / 256;
        matvec_kernel<<<h_blocks, 256, 0, s>>>(
            lw.o_proj, scratch.attn_out, scratch.hidden, H, QH
        );

        // Add residual
        add_residual_kernel<<<h_blocks, 256, 0, s>>>(scratch.hidden, scratch.residual, H);

        // Save residual for FFN
        cudaMemcpyAsync(scratch.residual, scratch.hidden, H * sizeof(float),
                        cudaMemcpyDeviceToDevice, s);

        // Post-attention layernorm
        ob1_inf_rms_norm<<<1, 256, 0, s>>>(
            scratch.hidden, lw.post_ln, scratch.normed, H, cfg.rms_eps
        );

        // FFN: gate_proj and up_proj
        int ffn_blocks = (cfg.intermediate + 255) / 256;
        matvec_kernel<<<ffn_blocks, 256, 0, s>>>(lw.gate_proj, scratch.normed, scratch.gate, cfg.intermediate, H);
        matvec_kernel<<<ffn_blocks, 256, 0, s>>>(lw.up_proj, scratch.normed, scratch.up, cfg.intermediate, H);

        // SiLU gate
        silu_gate_kernel<<<ffn_blocks, 256, 0, s>>>(scratch.gate, scratch.up, scratch.gate, cfg.intermediate);

        // Down projection
        matvec_kernel<<<h_blocks, 256, 0, s>>>(lw.down_proj, scratch.gate, scratch.hidden, H, cfg.intermediate);

        // Add residual
        add_residual_kernel<<<h_blocks, 256, 0, s>>>(scratch.hidden, scratch.residual, H);
    }

    // Decode a sparse tensor from .0b1 to dense float32 on GPU
    float* decode_sparse(SparseTensor* sp) {
        int64_t numel = sp->numel();
        float* d_dense;
        cudaMalloc(&d_dense, numel * sizeof(float));
        cudaMemset(d_dense, 0, numel * sizeof(float));

        // Copy sparse data to device if not already
        if (!sp->d_indices) sp->copy_to_device();

        int blocks = (sp->nnz + 255) / 256;
        decode_sparse_to_dense<<<blocks, 256>>>(
            sp->d_indices, sp->d_encoded, d_dense, sp->nnz, sp->scale
        );
        cudaDeviceSynchronize();
        return d_dense;
    }

    // Copy a dense tensor to GPU
    float* upload_dense(DenseTensor* dt) {
        if (!dt->d_data) dt->copy_to_device();
        return dt->d_data;
    }

    bool load_weights(OB1Model& model) {
        printf("  Loading weights to GPU...\n");
        size_t total_bytes = 0;

        // Embed tokens (dense)
        DenseTensor* embed = model.get_dense("model.embed_tokens.weight");
        if (!embed) { printf("ERROR: embed_tokens not found\n"); return false; }
        weights.embed_tokens = upload_dense(embed);
        total_bytes += embed->numel() * sizeof(float);
        printf("    embed_tokens: %.1f MB\n", embed->numel() * 4.0f / 1e6f);

        // Final norm (dense)
        DenseTensor* fnorm = model.get_dense("model.norm.weight");
        if (!fnorm) { printf("ERROR: final norm not found\n"); return false; }
        weights.final_norm = upload_dense(fnorm);

        // Per-layer weights
        for (int i = 0; i < cfg.num_layers; i++) {
            char name[256];
            LayerWeights& lw = weights.layers[i];

            // Sparse weight matrices
            auto get_sparse = [&](const char* suffix) -> float* {
                snprintf(name, sizeof(name), "model.layers.%d.%s", i, suffix);
                SparseTensor* sp = model.get_sparse(name);
                if (!sp) { printf("ERROR: %s not found\n", name); return nullptr; }
                float* d = decode_sparse(sp);
                total_bytes += sp->numel() * sizeof(float);
                return d;
            };

            // Dense norm/bias
            auto get_dense_w = [&](const char* suffix) -> float* {
                snprintf(name, sizeof(name), "model.layers.%d.%s", i, suffix);
                DenseTensor* dt = model.get_dense(name);
                if (!dt) { printf("ERROR: %s not found\n", name); return nullptr; }
                return upload_dense(dt);
            };

            lw.q_proj    = get_sparse("self_attn.q_proj.weight");
            lw.k_proj    = get_sparse("self_attn.k_proj.weight");
            lw.v_proj    = get_sparse("self_attn.v_proj.weight");
            lw.o_proj    = get_sparse("self_attn.o_proj.weight");
            lw.gate_proj = get_sparse("mlp.gate_proj.weight");
            lw.up_proj   = get_sparse("mlp.up_proj.weight");
            lw.down_proj = get_sparse("mlp.down_proj.weight");
            lw.input_ln  = get_dense_w("input_layernorm.weight");
            lw.post_ln   = get_dense_w("post_attention_layernorm.weight");
            lw.q_norm    = get_dense_w("self_attn.q_norm.weight");
            lw.k_norm    = get_dense_w("self_attn.k_norm.weight");

            if (!lw.q_proj || !lw.k_proj || !lw.v_proj || !lw.o_proj ||
                !lw.gate_proj || !lw.up_proj || !lw.down_proj ||
                !lw.input_ln || !lw.post_ln || !lw.q_norm || !lw.k_norm) {
                return false;
            }

            if (i % 7 == 0 || i == cfg.num_layers - 1) {
                printf("    Layer %d/%d loaded\n", i + 1, cfg.num_layers);
            }
        }

        printf("  Total GPU memory for weights: %.2f GB\n", total_bytes / 1e9);
        return true;
    }
};

#endif // OB1_INFERENCE_H
/**
 * 0B-1 MODEL LOADER - Direct CUDA Binary Loading
 * ===============================================
 *
 * Loads 12-point physics lattice models directly into GPU memory.
 * No Python, no intermediate formats.
 *
 * Binary format (.0b1):
 *   Header:
 *     - 4 bytes: Magic "0B1C"
 *     - 4 bytes: Lattice size (12)
 *     - 4 bytes: Num sparse tensors
 *     - 4 bytes: Num dense tensors
 *
 *   Per sparse tensor:
 *     - 4 bytes: Name length
 *     - N bytes: Name
 *     - 4 bytes: Num dims
 *     - 8*ndims bytes: Shape
 *     - 4 bytes: Num values (nnz)
 *     - 4 bytes: Scale (float)
 *     - nnz*4 bytes: Indices (int32)
 *     - nnz bytes: Encoded values (uint8, 5-bit lattice)
 *
 *   Per dense tensor:
 *     - 4 bytes: Name length
 *     - N bytes: Name
 *     - 4 bytes: Num dims
 *     - 8*ndims bytes: Shape
 *     - numel*4 bytes: Values (float32)
 *
 * Author: Isaac Oravec
 */

#ifndef OB1_MODEL_LOADER_H
#define OB1_MODEL_LOADER_H

#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <unordered_map>

// 12-point physics lattice
static const float LATTICE_12[12] = {
    7.0f, 13.0f, 17.0f, 31.0f, 71.0f, 73.0f,
    127.0f, 131.0f, 137.0f, 233.0f, 377.0f, 511.0f
};

struct SparseTensor {
    std::string name;
    std::vector<int64_t> shape;
    uint32_t nnz;
    float scale;

    // Host memory (for loading)
    int32_t* h_indices;
    uint8_t* h_encoded;

    // Device memory
    int32_t* d_indices;
    uint8_t* d_encoded;

    SparseTensor() : nnz(0), scale(1.0f),
                     h_indices(nullptr), h_encoded(nullptr),
                     d_indices(nullptr), d_encoded(nullptr) {}

    ~SparseTensor() {
        if (h_indices) free(h_indices);
        if (h_encoded) free(h_encoded);
        if (d_indices) cudaFree(d_indices);
        if (d_encoded) cudaFree(d_encoded);
    }

    void allocate_host() {
        h_indices = (int32_t*)malloc(nnz * sizeof(int32_t));
        h_encoded = (uint8_t*)malloc(nnz * sizeof(uint8_t));
    }

    void copy_to_device() {
        cudaMalloc(&d_indices, nnz * sizeof(int32_t));
        cudaMalloc(&d_encoded, nnz * sizeof(uint8_t));
        cudaMemcpy(d_indices, h_indices, nnz * sizeof(int32_t), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);
        cudaMemcpy(d_encoded, h_encoded, nnz * sizeof(uint8_t), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);
    }

    int64_t numel() const {
        int64_t n = 1;
        for (auto s : shape) n *= s;
        return n;
    }
};

struct DenseTensor {
    std::string name;
    std::vector<int64_t> shape;

    // Host memory
    float* h_data;

    // Device memory
    float* d_data;

    DenseTensor() : h_data(nullptr), d_data(nullptr) {}

    ~DenseTensor() {
        if (h_data) free(h_data);
        if (d_data) cudaFree(d_data);
    }

    int64_t numel() const {
        int64_t n = 1;
        for (auto s : shape) n *= s;
        return n;
    }

    void allocate_host() {
        h_data = (float*)malloc(numel() * sizeof(float));
    }

    void copy_to_device() {
        cudaMalloc(&d_data, numel() * sizeof(float));
        cudaMemcpy(d_data, h_data, numel() * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);
    }
};

class OB1Model {
public:
    std::unordered_map<std::string, SparseTensor*> sparse_tensors;
    std::unordered_map<std::string, DenseTensor*> dense_tensors;

    uint32_t lattice_size;
    uint32_t num_sparse;
    uint32_t num_dense;

    OB1Model() : lattice_size(12), num_sparse(0), num_dense(0) {}

    ~OB1Model() {
        for (auto& kv : sparse_tensors) delete kv.second;
        for (auto& kv : dense_tensors) delete kv.second;
    }

    bool load(const char* path) {
        FILE* f = fopen(path, "rb");
        if (!f) {
            printf("ERROR: Cannot open model file: %s\n", path);
            return false;
        }

        // Read header
        char magic[4];
        fread(magic, 1, 4, f);
        if (memcmp(magic, "0B1C", 4) != 0) {
            printf("ERROR: Invalid magic number\n");
            fclose(f);
            return false;
        }

        fread(&lattice_size, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, f);
        fread(&num_sparse, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, f);
        fread(&num_dense, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, f);

        printf("Loading model: %u sparse, %u dense tensors\n", num_sparse, num_dense);

        // Read sparse tensors
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_sparse; i++) {
            SparseTensor* tensor = new SparseTensor();

            // Read name
            uint32_t name_len;
            fread(&name_len, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, f);
            char* name_buf = (char*)malloc(name_len + 1);
            fread(name_buf, 1, name_len, f);
            name_buf[name_len] = '\0';
            tensor->name = std::string(name_buf);
            free(name_buf);

            // Read shape
            uint32_t ndims;
            fread(&ndims, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, f);
            tensor->shape.resize(ndims);
            for (uint32_t j = 0; j < ndims; j++) {
                int64_t dim;
                fread(&dim, sizeof(int64_t), 1, f);
                tensor->shape[j] = dim;
            }

            // Read nnz and scale
            fread(&tensor->nnz, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, f);
            fread(&tensor->scale, sizeof(float), 1, f);

            // Read data
            tensor->allocate_host();
            fread(tensor->h_indices, sizeof(int32_t), tensor->nnz, f);
            fread(tensor->h_encoded, sizeof(uint8_t), tensor->nnz, f);

            sparse_tensors[tensor->name] = tensor;

            if (i % 50 == 0) {
                printf("  Loaded sparse %u/%u: %s (%u nnz)\n",
                       i, num_sparse, tensor->name.c_str(), tensor->nnz);
            }
        }

        // Read dense tensors
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_dense; i++) {
            DenseTensor* tensor = new DenseTensor();

            // Read name
            uint32_t name_len;
            fread(&name_len, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, f);
            char* name_buf = (char*)malloc(name_len + 1);
            fread(name_buf, 1, name_len, f);
            name_buf[name_len] = '\0';
            tensor->name = std::string(name_buf);
            free(name_buf);

            // Read shape
            uint32_t ndims;
            fread(&ndims, sizeof(uint32_t), 1, f);
            tensor->shape.resize(ndims);
            for (uint32_t j = 0; j < ndims; j++) {
                int64_t dim;
                fread(&dim, sizeof(int64_t), 1, f);
                tensor->shape[j] = dim;
            }

            // Read data
            tensor->allocate_host();
            fread(tensor->h_data, sizeof(float), tensor->numel(), f);

            dense_tensors[tensor->name] = tensor;

            if (i % 50 == 0) {
                printf("  Loaded dense %u/%u: %s (%lld elements)\n",
                       i, num_dense, tensor->name.c_str(), tensor->numel());
            }
        }

        fclose(f);
        printf("Model loaded: %zu sparse, %zu dense tensors\n",
               sparse_tensors.size(), dense_tensors.size());
        return true;
    }

    void copy_all_to_device() {
        printf("Copying tensors to GPU...\n");

        size_t total_sparse = 0, total_dense = 0;

        for (auto& kv : sparse_tensors) {
            kv.second->copy_to_device();
            total_sparse += kv.second->nnz * (sizeof(int32_t) + sizeof(uint8_t));
        }

        for (auto& kv : dense_tensors) {
            kv.second->copy_to_device();
            total_dense += kv.second->numel() * sizeof(float);
        }

        printf("  Sparse: %.2f GB on GPU\n", total_sparse / 1e9);
        printf("  Dense: %.2f GB on GPU\n", total_dense / 1e9);
        printf("  Total: %.2f GB on GPU\n", (total_sparse + total_dense) / 1e9);
    }

    SparseTensor* get_sparse(const std::string& name) {
        auto it = sparse_tensors.find(name);
        return (it != sparse_tensors.end()) ? it->second : nullptr;
    }

    DenseTensor* get_dense(const std::string& name) {
        auto it = dense_tensors.find(name);
        return (it != dense_tensors.end()) ? it->second : nullptr;
    }
};

#endif // OB1_MODEL_LOADER_H
/**
 * CUDA Kernel Profiling Utilities
 * =================================
 * 
 * Provides timing and profiling hooks for ONETWO optimization analysis.
 * 
 * Usage:
 *   #include "ob1_profiling.h"
 *   
 *   PROFILE_KERNEL_START("kernel_name");
 *   kernel<<<...>>>(...);
 *   PROFILE_KERNEL_END("kernel_name");
 */

#ifndef OB1_PROFILING_H
#define OB1_PROFILING_H

#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include <map>
#include <string>
#include <chrono>
#include <iostream>
#include <sstream>
#include <mutex>

// =============================================================================
// PROFILING MODE
// =============================================================================

// Set to 1 to enable profiling, 0 to disable (zero overhead when disabled)
#define OB1_PROFILING_ENABLED 1

#if OB1_PROFILING_ENABLED

// =============================================================================
// KERNEL TIMING
// =============================================================================

struct KernelProfile {
    double total_time_ms;
    int call_count;
    double min_time_ms;
    double max_time_ms;
    
    KernelProfile() : total_time_ms(0.0), call_count(0), 
                     min_time_ms(1e10), max_time_ms(0.0) {}
};

// Global profile storage (host-side)
extern std::map<std::string, KernelProfile> g_kernel_profiles;
extern bool g_profiling_active;

// Helper function to update profile (defined in .cpp)
void update_kernel_profile(const std::string& name, float ms);

// Start profiling a kernel
#define PROFILE_KERNEL_START(name) \
    cudaEvent_t __profile_start_##name, __profile_stop_##name; \
    cudaEventCreate(&__profile_start_##name); \
    cudaEventCreate(&__profile_stop_##name); \
    cudaEventRecord(__profile_start_##name);

// End profiling a kernel
#define PROFILE_KERNEL_END(name) \
    cudaEventRecord(__profile_stop_##name); \
    cudaEventSynchronize(__profile_stop_##name); \
    float __profile_ms_##name; \
    cudaEventElapsedTime(&__profile_ms_##name, __profile_start_##name, __profile_stop_##name); \
    if (g_profiling_active) { \
        update_kernel_profile(std::string(#name), __profile_ms_##name); \
    } \
    cudaEventDestroy(__profile_start_##name); \
    cudaEventDestroy(__profile_stop_##name);

// Print profiling summary (defined in .cpp)
void print_profiling_summary();

// Reset profiling stats (defined in .cpp)
void reset_profiling();

// Enable/disable profiling (defined in .cpp)
void enable_profiling(bool enable);

#else

// Profiling disabled - zero overhead macros
#define PROFILE_KERNEL_START(name)
#define PROFILE_KERNEL_END(name)
#define print_profiling_summary() ((void)0)
#define reset_profiling() ((void)0)
#define enable_profiling(x) ((void)0)

#endif // OB1_PROFILING_ENABLED

// =============================================================================
// MEMORY PROFILING
// =============================================================================

struct MemoryProfile {
    size_t allocated_bytes;
    size_t peak_allocated_bytes;
    int allocation_count;
    
    MemoryProfile() : allocated_bytes(0), peak_allocated_bytes(0), allocation_count(0) {}
};

extern MemoryProfile g_memory_profile;

// Track memory allocation
inline void profile_malloc(size_t bytes) {
#if OB1_PROFILING_ENABLED
    if (g_profiling_active) {
        g_memory_profile.allocated_bytes += bytes;
        g_memory_profile.allocation_count++;
        if (g_memory_profile.allocated_bytes > g_memory_profile.peak_allocated_bytes) {
            g_memory_profile.peak_allocated_bytes = g_memory_profile.allocated_bytes;
        }
    }
#endif
}

// Track memory free
inline void profile_free(size_t bytes) {
#if OB1_PROFILING_ENABLED
    if (g_profiling_active) {
        if (g_memory_profile.allocated_bytes >= bytes) {
            g_memory_profile.allocated_bytes -= bytes;
        }
    }
#endif
}

// Print memory profile (defined in .cpp)
void print_memory_profile();

#endif // OB1_PROFILING_H
/**
 * Safetensors Direct Loader - NO Python!
 * Reads safetensors files and sends weights directly to CUDA server
 */

#ifndef OB1_SAFETENSORS_LOADER_H
#define OB1_SAFETENSORS_LOADER_H

#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <fstream>
#include <cstdint>

struct SafetensorMetadata {
    std::string name;
    std::vector<int64_t> shape;
    std::string dtype;
    std::pair<uint64_t, uint64_t> data_offsets;
};

class SafetensorsLoader {
public:
    static bool load_and_send_to_server(
        const std::string& safetensors_path,
        const std::string& server_url,
        const std::string& tensor_name_prefix = ""
    );
    
    static std::vector<SafetensorMetadata> parse_metadata(const std::string& safetensors_path);
    static bool send_tensor_to_server(
        const std::string& safetensors_path,
        const SafetensorMetadata& metadata,
        uint64_t header_size,
        const std::string& server_url
    );
    
private:
    static std::string read_json_header(std::ifstream& file);
    static std::vector<float> read_tensor_data(
        std::ifstream& file,
        const SafetensorMetadata& metadata,
        uint64_t data_offset
    );
};

#endif

// =============================================================================
// GLOBAL STATE

// Server State Container
struct ServerState {
    IronState* d_state = nullptr;
    IronState* h_state = nullptr;
    uint32_t* d_buffer = nullptr;
    uint32_t buffer_fill[2] = {0, 0};
    int current_buffer = 0;
    uint32_t buffer_size = 26214400;
    uint32_t num_nodes = 4096;
    uint32_t* d_batch_xor = nullptr;
    float* d_batch_pot = nullptr;
    uint32_t* d_feedback = nullptr;
};

ServerState state;
std::mutex state_mutex;
cudaStream_t stream;
OneTwoBuffers onetwo_bufs = {0};

// XYZT Bridge for /api/crystal
static IronXyztBridge g_bridge;

// CONFIG
bool use_fbc_mode = true;
bool is_pipe_mode = false;
bool use_ngram_mode = false;  // Use XOR mode for probe_vocab compatibility
bool inference_mode = false;
std::string model_path;
InferenceEngine* inference_engine = nullptr;
OB1Model* loaded_model = nullptr;

// N-GRAM STATE (NEW)
NGramState* d_ngram_state = nullptr;
NGramState h_ngram_state;
int ngram_window_size = 4;      // 4-byte n-grams (word-level)
int ngram_lock_threshold = 50;  // Lock after 50 repetitions

// IRON MULTI-PROJECTION FINGERPRINT
IronFingerprint* d_fingerprint = nullptr;
IronMultiScale* d_multiscale = nullptr;

// BUFFERS
uint32_t* d_e6_state = nullptr;
uint8_t* d_filter_mask = nullptr;
uint32_t* d_clean_tokens = nullptr;
uint32_t* d_clean_counter = nullptr;
float* d_probe_results = nullptr;

// HOST BUFFER FOR OUTPUT (Pipe Mode)
std::vector<uint8_t> host_mask_buffer;

struct is_kept {
    __host__ __device__
    bool operator()(uint8_t x) { return x != 0; }
};

// =============================================================================
// INIT FUNCTIONS
// =============================================================================

void init_iron_engine(ServerState& s, uint32_t num_nodes) {
    s.num_nodes = num_nodes;

    IronState h_iron;
    memset(&h_iron, 0, sizeof(IronState));
    h_iron.num_nodes = num_nodes;
    h_iron.max_connections = 16;
    h_iron.vocab_size = 256;

    // Original fields
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.xor_accumulators, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.node_potentials, num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.node_activations, num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.node_locked, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.previous_lattice_idx, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.connection_counts, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.lattice_connections, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.sector_assignment, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.discovery_candidates, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));

    // === ONETWO/XYZT UPGRADES (Feb 2026) ===
    // VALUE-AT-POSITION tracking (from onetwo_engine.py SpatialEngine)
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.predicted_value, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.value_confidence, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));

    // Per-position transitions
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.pos_transitions, num_nodes * 16 * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.trans_confidence, num_nodes * 16 * sizeof(uint8_t));

    // Flow maps
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.next_node_pred, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.next_node_hits, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.next_node_total, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Node coordinates (for auto-wiring)
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.node_coords, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Edge impedance
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.edge_delay, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.edge_consistent, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.edge_pending_val, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint8_t));

    // Saturation metrics (for benchmarking)
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.d_wire_overflow, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.d_flow_atomics, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.d_node_hit_count, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Self-observation loop (from One_Theorem paper)
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.node_z_level, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.err_stream, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.conf_stream, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.edge_weight, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint16_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.d_err_count, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.d_correct_count, sizeof(uint32_t));
    h_iron.self_obs_boost = 20;  // Default boost value from paper

    // Inline learning fields (XYZT-style)
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.edge_fire_agree, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.edge_fire_total, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint32_t));
    h_iron.inline_learning_enabled = 1;  // Enable by default
    h_iron.learn_rate = 20;              // Weight change per adaptation
    h_iron.atrophy_rate = 5;             // Slow decay for noise
    h_iron.inline_learning_period = 32;  // Update every 32 firings

    // Initialize original fields
    cudaMemset(h_iron.xor_accumulators, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.node_potentials, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.node_activations, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.node_locked, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.previous_lattice_idx, 0xFF, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.connection_counts, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.lattice_connections, 0, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.sector_assignment, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));

    // Initialize ONETWO fields
    cudaMemset(h_iron.predicted_value, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.value_confidence, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.pos_transitions, 0, num_nodes * 16 * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.trans_confidence, 0, num_nodes * 16 * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.next_node_pred, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.next_node_hits, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.next_node_total, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.edge_delay, 3, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint8_t)); // Default delay=3
    cudaMemset(h_iron.edge_consistent, 0, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.edge_pending_val, 0, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.d_wire_overflow, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.d_flow_atomics, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.d_node_hit_count, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Initialize self-observation fields
    cudaMemset(h_iron.node_z_level, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.err_stream, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.conf_stream, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(uint8_t));
    // Initialize edge weights to midpoint (32768 = 50% strength)
    uint16_t* h_edge_weights = (uint16_t*)malloc(num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint16_t));
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections; i++) {
        h_edge_weights[i] = 32768;
    }
    cudaMemcpy(h_iron.edge_weight, h_edge_weights, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint16_t), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);
    free(h_edge_weights);
    cudaMemset(h_iron.d_err_count, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.d_correct_count, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Initialize inline learning fields
    cudaMemset(h_iron.edge_fire_agree, 0, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.edge_fire_total, 0, num_nodes * h_iron.max_connections * sizeof(uint32_t));

    // === XYZT 3D FRESNEL WAVE STATE ===
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.xyzt_waves, num_nodes * NUM_DIRS * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.xyzt_waves_in, num_nodes * NUM_DIRS * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.xyzt_Z, num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.xyzt_energy, num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.d_xyzt_active_cells, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.d_xyzt_gain_events, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&h_iron.d_xyzt_gate_fires, sizeof(uint32_t));

    cudaMemset(h_iron.xyzt_waves, 0, num_nodes * NUM_DIRS * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.xyzt_waves_in, 0, num_nodes * NUM_DIRS * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.xyzt_energy, 0, num_nodes * sizeof(float));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.d_xyzt_active_cells, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.d_xyzt_gain_events, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(h_iron.d_xyzt_gate_fires, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Initialize impedance Z by depth: Z = 1.0 + z * 0.5 (deeper = higher impedance)
    {
        float* h_Z = (float*)malloc(num_nodes * sizeof(float));
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; i++) {
            int z = (i >> 8) & 0xF;
            h_Z[i] = 1.0f + z * 0.5f;
        }
        cudaMemcpy(h_iron.xyzt_Z, h_Z, num_nodes * sizeof(float), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);
        free(h_Z);
    }

    // XYZT defaults
    h_iron.xyzt_ticks_per_call = 1;
    h_iron.xyzt_noise_floor = 0.001f;
    h_iron.xyzt_hebbian_rate = 0.01f;

    // Initialize node coordinates in a 16x16x16 grid (XYZT-compatible)
    uint32_t* h_coords = (uint32_t*)malloc(num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t));
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; i++) {
        uint32_t x = i & 0xF;
        uint32_t y = (i >> 4) & 0xF;
        uint32_t z = (i >> 8) & 0xF;
        // Pack: X[31:22] Y[21:12] Z[11:4] flags[3:0]
        h_coords[i] = ((x & 0x3FF) << 22) | ((y & 0x3FF) << 12) | ((z & 0xFF) << 4);
    }
    cudaMemcpy(h_iron.node_coords, h_coords, num_nodes * sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);
    free(h_coords);

    // Allocate device state and copy
    cudaMalloc(&s.d_state, sizeof(IronState));
    h_iron.d_iron_state = s.d_state;  // IMPORTANT: Set device self-pointer BEFORE copying
    cudaMemcpy(s.d_state, &h_iron, sizeof(IronState), cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);

    s.h_state = new IronState(h_iron);

    cudaMalloc(&s.d_buffer, s.buffer_size * sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(s.d_buffer, 0, s.buffer_size * sizeof(uint32_t));

    cudaMalloc(&s.d_batch_xor, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMalloc(&s.d_batch_pot, sizeof(float));
    cudaMalloc(&s.d_feedback, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(s.d_batch_xor, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));
    cudaMemset(s.d_batch_pot, 0, sizeof(float));
    cudaMemset(s.d_feedback, 0, sizeof(uint32_t));
}

// =============================================================================
// THE CASCADE (FLUSH LOGIC)
// =============================================================================

void flush_buffer() {
    std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(state_mutex);
    uint32_t raw_count = state.buffer_fill[state.current_buffer];
    if (raw_count == 0) return;

    uint32_t* d_raw = state.d_buffer;
    size_t clean_count = 0;

    // --- N-GRAM MODE: BYPASS E6 FILTER ---
    // N-gram detection works on raw bytes, not filtered tokens
    // Using optimized shared-memory kernel (50-150 GB/s vs 1.26 GB/s)
    if (use_ngram_mode) {
        size_t byte_count = raw_count * 4;

        launch_ngram_shared_repetition(
            (const uint8_t*)d_raw,
            byte_count,
            ngram_window_size,
            d_ngram_state,
            ngram_lock_threshold,
            stream
        );

        cudaStreamSynchronize(stream);
        state.buffer_fill[state.current_buffer] = 0;
        return;  // Skip the rest of the cascade
    }

    // --- LAYER 1: SYNC (DISABLED - E6 stays at stable primes {5,7,11,13,17,19}) ---
    // static int sync_counter = 0;
    // if (sync_counter++ % 10 == 0) {
    //     ob1_project_e8_to_e6<<<1, 6, 0, stream>>>(state.d_state, d_e6_state);
    // }

    // --- LAYER 2: FILTER ---
    if (use_fbc_mode) {
        int threads = 256;
        int blocks = (raw_count + 255) / 256;

        // E6 Sieve (threshold 1000 = bypass, accept everything for testing)
        ob1_e6_filter_fbc<<<blocks, threads, 0, stream>>>(
            d_raw, raw_count, d_e6_state, d_filter_mask, 1000.0f
        );

        // Unpack Survivors
        cudaMemsetAsync(d_clean_counter, 0, sizeof(uint32_t), stream);
        ob1_fbc_unpack_survivors<<<blocks, threads, 0, stream>>>(
            d_raw, d_filter_mask, raw_count, d_clean_tokens, d_clean_counter, state.buffer_size
        );

        uint32_t h_count;
        cudaMemcpyAsync(&h_count, d_clean_counter, sizeof(uint32_t), cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost, stream);
        cudaStreamSynchronize(stream);
        clean_count = h_count;

    } else {
        int threads = 256;
        int blocks = (raw_count + 255) / 256;

        ob1_e6_filter<<<blocks, threads, 0, stream>>>(
            d_raw, raw_count, d_e6_state, d_filter_mask, 85.0f
        );

        thrust::device_ptr<uint32_t> raw_ptr(d_raw);
        thrust::device_ptr<uint8_t> mask_ptr(d_filter_mask);
        thrust::device_ptr<uint32_t> clean_ptr(d_clean_tokens);

        uint32_t* end_ptr = thrust::copy_if(
            thrust::cuda::par.on(stream),
            raw_ptr, raw_ptr + raw_count, mask_ptr, clean_ptr, is_kept()
        ).get();
        clean_count = end_ptr - d_clean_tokens;
    }

    // --- OUTPUT VERDICT (Gravity Mode Only) ---
    if (is_pipe_mode) {
        // We write the decision mask back to STDOUT
        // 1 bit/byte per word.
        // Sync is required here to maintain pipe order, but OS buffers help.

        if (host_mask_buffer.size() < raw_count) host_mask_buffer.resize(raw_count);

        cudaMemcpy(host_mask_buffer.data(), d_filter_mask, raw_count, cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);

        // Write to STDOUT (The Drain)
        // This is the "Sink" in Source -> Iron -> Sink
