 *   Layout: [w2_p137, w2_p251, w2_p397,
 *            w8_p137, w8_p251, w8_p397,
 *            w64_p137, w64_p251, w64_p397]
 *
 * Each float is fed through a transducer → bit in the corresponding BitStream.
 * The transducer self-calibrates after 16 samples.
 */
static inline void bridge_feed_chunk(IronXyztBridge *b, const float *floats) {
    for (int i = 0; i < BRIDGE_N_SENSORS; i++) {
        /* Scale float to int64 for transducer (preserve 3 decimal places) */
        int64_t val = (int64_t)(floats[i] * 1000.0f);
        int node_id = b->sensor_nodes[i];
        xdcr_feed(&b->xdcrs[i], &b->graph.nodes[node_id].bs, val);
    }
    b->n_chunks++;
}

/*
 * Auto-wire and collide.
 * Call AFTER feeding all chunks.
 *
 * Creates edges:
 *   Z=0 → Z=1: XOR between nearby sensors (same window, different primes)
 *   Z=1 → Z=2: AND between Z=1 outputs (find agreement across collisions)
 *
 * Returns number of edges created.
 */
static inline int bridge_collide(IronXyztBridge *b) {
    if (b->wired) return b->graph.n_edges;

    /*
     * Z=0 auto-wire: max_dist=2 connects same-window primes
     *   (0,0)↔(1,0), (1,0)↔(2,0), (0,0)↔(2,0) for window 0
     *   Same for windows 1 and 2
     *   Also connects adjacent windows at same prime
     *   Total: ~18 edges, all XOR (even Z → XOR)
     */
    int e0 = graph_autowire(&b->graph, 0, 2);

    /* Execute Z=0→Z=1 collisions */
    graph_tick(&b->graph);

    /*
     * Z=1 auto-wire: max_dist=4 connects Z=1 outputs
     *   These are AND (odd Z → AND) — find agreements
     *   Wider radius catches cross-window, cross-prime relationships
     */
    int e1 = graph_autowire(&b->graph, 1, 4);

    /* Execute Z=1→Z=2 collisions */
    graph_tick(&b->graph);

    b->wired = 1;
    return e0 + e1;
}

/*
 * Run Hebbian learning.
 * Correlated source streams → strengthen edge weight.
 * Uncorrelated → weaken.
 *
 * n_rounds: how many learning passes (10-50 typical)
 *
 * Returns total edges changed across all rounds.
 */
static inline int bridge_learn(IronXyztBridge *b, int n_rounds) {
    int total_changed = 0;
    for (int r = 0; r < n_rounds; r++) {
        /*
         * 60 = strengthen threshold (above 60% correlation → strengthen)
         * 45 = weaken threshold (below 45% correlation → weaken)
         * 15 = learning rate per round
         *
         * Binary baseline is 50%. So:
         *   >60% = meaningfully correlated → make stronger
         *   <45% = meaningfully anti-correlated or independent → make weaker
         */
        total_changed += graph_learn(&b->graph, 60, 45, 15);
    }
    return total_changed;
}

/*
 * Read the crystal: converged edge weights after learning.
 * The weight topology IS the structural signature.
 *
 * Strong edges = projections that consistently correlate for this input type.
 * Weak edges = projections that are independent for this input type.
 * The PATTERN of strong/weak IS classification — no labels needed.
 */
static inline CrystalResult bridge_read_crystal(IronXyztBridge *b) {
    CrystalResult r;
    memset(&r, 0, sizeof(r));
    r.n_learn_rounds = 0;  /* caller should set */
    r.n_chunks_fed = b->n_chunks;

    int ne = b->graph.n_edges;
    if (ne > BRIDGE_MAX_EDGES) ne = BRIDGE_MAX_EDGES;
    r.n_edges = ne;

    float sum_w = 0;
    int max_w = 0, min_w = 256;
    int max_i = 0, min_i = 0;

    for (int i = 0; i < ne; i++) {
        Edge *e = &b->graph.edges[i];
        r.edges[i].edge_idx = i;
        r.edges[i].src_a = e->src_a;
        r.edges[i].src_b = e->src_b;
        r.edges[i].jtype = e->jtype;
        r.edges[i].weight = e->weight;
        r.edges[i].corr_pct = analysis_corr_pct(
            &b->graph.nodes[e->src_a].bs,
            &b->graph.nodes[e->src_b].bs);

        snprintf(r.edges[i].label, 48, "%s %s %s",
                 b->graph.nodes[e->src_a].name,
                 e->jtype == JXN_XOR ? "XOR" :
                 e->jtype == JXN_AND ? "AND" :
                 e->jtype == JXN_OR  ? "OR"  : "NND",
                 b->graph.nodes[e->src_b].name);

        sum_w += e->weight;
        if (e->weight > max_w) { max_w = e->weight; max_i = i; }
        if (e->weight < min_w) { min_w = e->weight; min_i = i; }
        if (e->weight > 200) r.n_strong++;
        if (e->weight < 30)  r.n_weak++;
    }

    r.strongest_edge = max_i;
    r.weakest_edge = min_i;
    r.avg_weight = ne > 0 ? sum_w / ne : 0;

    return r;
}

/*
 * Compute distance between two crystals.
 * Uses L1 distance on edge weight vectors.
 * Two similar input types → similar crystal → small distance.
 * Two different types → different crystal → large distance.
 */
static inline float bridge_crystal_distance(const CrystalResult *a, const CrystalResult *b) {
    int n = a->n_edges < b->n_edges ? a->n_edges : b->n_edges;
    float dist = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        float d = (float)a->edges[i].weight - (float)b->edges[i].weight;
        dist += d < 0 ? -d : d;
    }
    return dist;
}

/*
 * Run-length analysis on a specific node's BitStream.
 * Returns run pattern as array of lengths + prediction.
 */
static inline Prediction bridge_predict_node(IronXyztBridge *b, int node_id) {
    if (node_id < 0 || node_id >= b->graph.n_nodes) {
        Prediction p = {0,0,0,0};
        return p;
    }
    return bs_predict(&b->graph.nodes[node_id].bs);
}

#ifdef __cplusplus
}
#endif

#endif /* IRON_XYZT_BRIDGE_H */
/*
 * xyzt_lib.h — XYZT Engine as linkable library
 *
 * Drop this + xyzt_lib.c into your IRON project.
 * Strips the main/tests from xyzt.c, exposes the engine API.
 *
 * Integration: #include "xyzt_lib.h" in your main.cu
 *              Link xyzt_lib.c alongside (compiled as C, not CUDA)
 */

#ifndef XYZT_LIB_H
#define XYZT_LIB_H

#include <stdint.h>

#ifdef __cplusplus
extern "C" {
#endif

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
   CORE TYPES
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

#define BS_WORDS    256     /* 16384 bits max per stream */
#define MAX_NODES   128
#define MAX_EDGES   512
#define MAX_RUNS    4096

typedef struct { uint64_t w[BS_WORDS]; int len; } BitStream;

typedef struct {
    int64_t prev, threshold;
    int count, calibrated;
    int64_t hist[64]; int hist_len;
} Transducer;

typedef uint32_t Coord;

typedef enum { JXN_XOR=0, JXN_AND, JXN_OR, JXN_NAND } JunctionType;

typedef struct {
    Coord       coord;
    char        name[24];
    BitStream   bs;
    Transducer *xdcr;
    int64_t   (*sensor_fn)(void);
    int         is_delta;
} Node;

typedef struct {
    uint8_t      src_a, src_b, dst;
    JunctionType jtype;
    uint8_t      weight;
    uint8_t      delay;
    int          pending_bit;
    int          consistent;
    int          last_output;
} Edge;

typedef struct {
    Node    nodes[MAX_NODES];
    int     n_nodes;
    Edge    edges[MAX_EDGES];
    int     n_edges;
    int64_t prev_sensor[MAX_NODES];
} Graph;

typedef struct {
    int lengths[MAX_RUNS];
    int types[MAX_RUNS];
    int count;
} RunList;

typedef struct { int predicted, valid, delta, accel; } Prediction;

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
   TIME
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

void     T_init(void);
uint64_t T_tick(void);
uint64_t T_ns(void);

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
   BITSTREAM
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

void bs_clear(BitStream *b);
void bs_init(BitStream *b);
void bs_push(BitStream *b, int bit);
int  bs_get(const BitStream *b, int t);
int  bs_popcount(const BitStream *b);
int  bs_activity_pct(const BitStream *b);
void bs_pattern(const BitStream *b, char *buf, int max);
void bs_runs(const BitStream *b, RunList *runs);
int  bs_decode_values(const BitStream *b, int *values, int max_values);
Prediction bs_predict(const BitStream *b);

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
   TRANSDUCER
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

void xdcr_init(Transducer *x);
int  xdcr_feed(Transducer *x, BitStream *out, int64_t val);

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
   COORDINATES
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

Coord coord_pack(int x, int y, int z);
int   coord_x(Coord c);
int   coord_y(Coord c);
int   coord_z(Coord c);
int   coord_manhattan(Coord a, Coord b);

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
   JUNCTIONS
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

void jxn_apply(const BitStream *a, const BitStream *b, BitStream *dst, JunctionType t);
int  jxn_newest_bit(const BitStream *a, const BitStream *b, JunctionType t);
int  analysis_corr_pct(const BitStream *a, const BitStream *b);

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
   GRAPH ENGINE
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

void graph_init(Graph *g);
int  graph_add_sensor(Graph *g, int x, int y, const char *name,
                      int64_t (*fn)(void), int is_delta);
int  graph_add_node(Graph *g, int x, int y, int z, const char *name);
int  graph_add_pattern(Graph *g, int x, int y, int z,
                       const char *name, const char *pat);
int  graph_wire(Graph *g, int a, int b, int dst, JunctionType jt,
                int weight, int delay);
int  graph_add_edge(Graph *g, int a, int b, int dst, JunctionType jt);
int  graph_autowire(Graph *g, int z_level, int max_dist);
void graph_tick(Graph *g);
void graph_tick_z_ordered(Graph *g);
void graph_run(Graph *g, int n, int interval_us);
int  graph_learn(Graph *g, int strengthen_thresh, int weaken_thresh, int rate);

/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
   REPORTING (optional, for debug)
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

void graph_report(Graph *g);
void graph_corr_matrix(Graph *g);

#ifdef __cplusplus
}
#endif

#endif /* XYZT_LIB_H */

gemini
Fixed Issues
Removed invalid Bash entries — All entries trying to match C++ code snippets (like Bash('namespace force {' )) were removed. Bash permissions are for shell commands, not code.
Kept valid entries:
Bash(gemini --version:*) — Valid command pattern
Bash(findstr:*) — Valid Windows command
Bash(printf:*) — Valid command
Bash(where:*) — Valid Windows command
Bash(cmd.exe /c:*) — Valid Windows command pattern
Bash(cmake --build:*) — Valid build command
MCP plugin permissions — Kept as-is
WebSearch — Kept as-is
Fixed syntax — All entries now use correct syntax:
Bash(command:*) for prefix matching
Bash("exact command") for exact commands
No empty parentheses
No mismatched parentheses
What Bash Permissions Are For
Bash permissions control which shell commands the AI can run, not code snippets. Examples:
Bash(npm:*) — Allows any npm command
Bash(git status) — Allows exact command
Bash — Allows all commands (not recommended)

cd '.\Dev\'
THE CLOSURE TEST
PhaseActionPurpose1Crystallize with signalCreate structure S2Extract signatureGet what defines S3Baseline: fresh signalHow fast does it normally lock?4Closure: feed signatureDoes it recognize itself?5Compare trajectoriesFaster = self-recognition6Cross-recognitionDifferent signal as control
THE THEORY
D(∅) = 1
If system has state S
And we feed S back as input O
Then: S ⊕ O → 0
Self-recognition = Void state = perfect closure
WHAT WE'RE MEASURING`
MetricMeaningSpeed ratioDoes it lock FASTER when fed its own state?Density ratioDoes it crystallize STRONGER with self-input?Lock advantageMore locks from self-recognition?`
`
POSSIBLE OUTCOMES`
SELF_RECOGNITION:  density_ratio > 2.0, locks faster`
                   → System sees itself`
                   → D(∅) = 1 confirmed`
                   `
NEEDS_STATE_ENDPOINT: Can't extract internal state
                      → Test is limited to proxy (same signal)
                      → Need /state API to test properly
NO_RECOGNITION:    No special resonance with self

@("C:\Users\Public\BlueStacks", "C:\Users\Default\.dotnet") | ForEach-Object {`
    $exists = Test-Path $_`
    Write-Host "  $_ - $(if ($exists) {'STILL EXISTS'} else {'deleted'})"`
}
